sections.title_en: Opening

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين بيار / اين سوم دفتر كه سنت شد سه بار
entries.english: O Light of the Truth, Husamu’ddin, bring this Third Book, for “three times” has become a sunna.

entries.farsi: بر گشا گنجينه‏ى اسرار را / در سوم دفتر بهل اعذار را
entries.english: Open the treasury of mysteries; in respect of the Third Book leave excuses alone.

entries.farsi: قوتت از قوت حق مى‏زهد / نه از عروقى كز حرارت مى‏جهد
entries.english: Your power flows from the power of God, not from the veins which throb because of heat.

entries.farsi: اين چراغ شمس كاو روشن بود / نه از فتيل و پنبه و روغن بود
entries.english: This lamp, the sun, which is bright— it is not by means of wick and cotton and oil.

entries.farsi: سقف گردون كاو چنين دايم بود / نه از طناب و استنى قايم بود
entries.english: The vault of heaven, which is so enduring, is not supported by any tent-rope or pillar.

entries.farsi: قوت جبريل از مطبخ نبود / بود از ديدار خالق وجود
entries.english: The power of Gabriel was not from the kitchen; it was from beholding the Creator of existence.

entries.farsi: همچنان اين قوت ابدال حق / ‏هم ز حق دان نه از طعام و از طبق
entries.english: Likewise, know this power of the Abdal of God to be from God, not from dishes and from trays.

entries.farsi: جسمشان را هم ز نور اسرشته‏اند / تا ز روح و از ملك بگذشته‏اند
entries.english: Their bodies too have been moulded of the Light, so that they have transcended the Spirit and the Angel.

entries.farsi: چون كه موصوفى به اوصاف جليل / ‏ز آتش امراض بگذر چون خليل
entries.english: Inasmuch as you are endowed with the qualities of the Almighty, pass beyond the fire of the maladies, like Khalil.

entries.farsi: هر مزاجى را عناصر مايه است / ‏وين مزاجت برتر از هر پايه است
entries.english: To you also the fire will become coolness and safety, O you to whose complexion the elements are slaves.

entries.farsi: گردد آتش بر تو هم برد و سالم / ‏اى عناصر مر مزاجت را غالم
entries.english: The elements are the substance of every complexion, but this complexion of yours is superior to every grade.

entries.farsi: اين مزاجت از جهان منبسط / وصف وحدت را كنون شد ملتقط
entries.english: This complexion of yours is of the simple world; it has now gathered up the attributes of Unity.

entries.farsi: اى دريغا عرصه‏ى افهام خلق / ‏سخت تنگ آمد ندارد خلق حلق
entries.english: Oh, alas, the area of the people’s understandings is exceeding narrow: the people have no throat.

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق به حذق راى تو / حلق بخشد سنگ را حلواى تو
entries.english: O Light of the Truths, through the keenness of your perception your sweetmeat bestows a throat on stone.

entries.farsi: كوه طور اندر تجلى حلق يافت / ‏تا كه مى‏نوشيد و مى را بر نتافت
entries.english: Mount Sinai in the epiphany gained a throat, so that it quaffed the wine; but it could not bear the wine.

entries.farsi: صار دكا منه و انشق الجبل / ‏هل رأيتم من جبل رقص الجمل
entries.english: Thereby the mountain was shattered and split asunder: have you seen a mountain amble like a camel?

entries.farsi: لقمه بخشى آيد از هر كس به كس / ‏حلق بخشى كار يزدان است و بس
entries.english: Bestowal of mouthfuls comes from everyone to someone; bestowal of a throat is the work of God alone.

entries.farsi: حلق بخشد جسم را و روح را / حلق بخشد بهر هر عضوت جدا
entries.english: He bestows a throat on the body and on the spirit; He bestows a separate throat for every part of you.

entries.farsi: اين گهى بخشد كه اجلالی شوى / ‏و ز دغا و از دغل خالى شوى
entries.english: This He bestows at the time when you become Majestic and become void of guile and deceit,

entries.farsi: تا نگويى سر سلطان را به كس / ‏تا نريزى قند را پيش مگس
entries.english: So that you will not tell the King’s secret to any one nor pour out sugar before flies.

entries.farsi: گوش آن كس نوشد اسرار جلال / ‏كاو چو سوسن صد زبان افتاد و الل
entries.english: The secrets of Majesty are drunk in by the ear of that one who, like the lily, has a hundred tongues and is dumb.

entries.farsi: حلق بخشد خاك را لطف خدا / تا خورد آب و برويد صد گيا
entries.english: The grace of God bestows a throat on the earth, to the end that it may drink water and make a hundred herbs grow.

entries.farsi: باز خاكى را ببخشد حلق و لب / ‏تا گياهش را خورد اندر طلب
entries.english: Again, He bestows on the creature of earth a throat and lip, in order that it may eat its herbage in desire.

entries.farsi: چون گياهش خورد حيوان گشت زفت / ‏گشت حيوان لقمه‏ى انسان و رفت
entries.english: When the animal has eaten its herbage, it becomes fat: the animal becomes a mouthful for Man and goes.

entries.farsi: باز خاك آمد شد اكال بشر / چون جدا شد از بشر روح و بصر
entries.english: In turn it becomes earth and becomes a devourer of Man, when the spirit and the sight are separated from Man.

entries.farsi: ذره‏ها ديدم دهانشان جمله باز / گر بگويم خوردشان گردد دراز
entries.english: I beheld the atoms with their mouths all open: if I should tell of their food, it would become long.

entries.farsi: برگها را برگ از انعام او / دايگان را دايه لطف عام او
entries.english: Provisions have provision from His bounty; His universal grace is the nourisher of them that nourish.

entries.farsi: رزقها را رزقها او مى‏دهد / ز انكه گندم بى‏غذايى چون زهد
entries.english: He bestows gifts on the gifts, for how should wheat spring forth without any sustenance?

entries.farsi: نيست شرح اين سخن را منتها / پاره‏اى گفتم بدانى پاره‏ها
entries.english: There is no end to the explanation of this matter. I have told a portion: you may know the portions.

entries.farsi: اين جهان و ساكنانش منتشر / و آن جهان و سالكانش مستمر
entries.english: Know that the entire world is eating and being eaten; know that those who have everlasting life are fortunate and accepted.

entries.farsi: جمله عالم آكل و مأكول دان / ‏باقيان را مقبل و مقبول دان
entries.english: This world and its inhabitants are dispersed; that world and its travellers are continuing.

entries.farsi: اين جهان و عاشقانش منقطع / ‏اهل آن عالم مخلد مجتمع
entries.english: This world and its lovers are cut off; the people of that world are eternalized and united.

entries.farsi: باقيات الصالحات آمد كريم / ‏رسته از صد آفت و اخطار و بيم
entries.english: The noble, then, is he that gives to himself the Water of Life that remains unto everlasting.

entries.farsi: پس كريم آن است كاو خود را دهد / آب حيوانى كه ماند تا ابد
entries.english: The noble one is the good works which endure: he has been freed from a hundred banes and perils and fears.

entries.farsi: گر هزارانند يك كس بيش نيست / ‏چون خيالات عدد انديش نيست
entries.english: If they (the noble) are thousands, there is no more than one: it is not like the fancies of him that thinks of number.

entries.farsi: آكل و مأكول را حلق است و ناى / ‏غالب و مغلوب را عقل است و راى
entries.english: The eater and the eaten have a throat and windpipe: the victor and the vanquished have understanding and mental perception.

entries.farsi: حلق بخشيد او عصاى عدل را / خورد آن چندان عصا و حبل را
entries.english: He bestowed a throat on the rod of justice; it devoured all those many rods and ropes;

entries.farsi: و اندر او افزون نشد ز آن جمله اكل / ‏ز انكه حيوانى نبودش اكل و شكل
entries.english: And in it was no increase from all that eating, because its eating and its form were not animal.

entries.farsi: مر يقين را چون عصا هم حلق داد / تا بخورد او هر خيالى را كه زاد
entries.english: To Faith also He gave a throat like the rod, so that it devoured every vain fancy that was born.

entries.farsi: پس معانى را چو اعيان حلقهاست / ‏رازق حلق معانى هم خداست
entries.english: Hence the spiritual and intelligible things, like the concrete things, have throats, and the giver of food to the throat of the spiritual and intelligible things is also God.

entries.farsi: پس ز مه تا ماهى ايچ از خلق نيست / ‏كه به جذب مايه او را حلق نيست
entries.english: Therefore from the Moon to the Fish there is nothing in creation that has not a throat in respect of its drawing sustenance.

entries.farsi: حلق جان از فكر تن خالى شود / آن گهان روزيش اجلالی شود
entries.english: The spirit’s throat is emptied of thought for the body, then its apportioned sustenance becomes Majestic.

entries.farsi: شرط تبديل مزاج آمد بدان / ‏كز مزاج بد بود مرگ بدان
entries.english: Know that the necessary condition is the transformation of the nature, for the death of evil men is from evil nature.

entries.farsi: چون مزاج آدمى گل خوار شد / زرد و بد رنگ و سقيم و خوار شد
entries.english: When it has become natural to a human being to eat clay, he grows pale and ill-complexioned and sickly and miserable;

entries.farsi: چون مزاج زشت او تبديل يافت / ‏رفت زشتى از رخش چون شمع تافت
entries.english: When his ugly nature has been transformed, the ugliness departs from his face, and he shines like a candle.

entries.farsi: دايه‏اى كو طفل شير آموز را / تا به نعمت خوش كند پدفوز را
entries.english: Where is a nurse for the suckling babe?— that with kindness she may sweeten the inner part of its mouth,

entries.farsi: ز انكه پستان شد حجاب آن ضعيف / ‏از هزاران نعمت و خوان و رعيف
entries.english: And, though she bar its way to her teat may open up for it the way to a hundred gardens?

entries.farsi: گر ببندد راه آن پستان بر او / بر گشايد راه صد بستان بر او
entries.english: Because the teat has become to that feeble a barrier from thousands of pleasures and dishes and loaves

entries.farsi: پس حيات ماست موقوف فطام / ‏اندك اندك جهد كن تم الكالم
entries.english: Our life, then, depends on weaning. Endeavour little by little. The discourse is complete.

entries.farsi: چون جنين بود آدمى بد خون غذا / از نجس پاكى برد مومن كذا
entries.english: When man was an embryo his nourishment was blood: in like fashion the true believer draws purity from filth.

entries.farsi: از فطام خون غذايش شير شد / وز فطام شير لقمه‏گير شد
entries.english: Through being weaned from blood, his nourishment became milk; and through being weaned from milk, he became a taker of food.

entries.farsi: و ز فطام لقمه لقمانى شود / طالب اشكار پنهانى شود
entries.english: And through being weaned from food he becomes like Luqman; he becomes a seeker of the hidden game.

entries.farsi: گر جنين را كس بگفتى در رحم / ‏هست بيرون عالمى بس منتظم
entries.english: If any one were to say to the embryo in the womb, “Outside is a world exceedingly well-ordered,

entries.farsi: يك زمين خرمى با عرض و طول / ‏اندر او صد نعمت و چندين اكول
entries.english: A pleasant earth, broad and long, wherein are a hundred delights and so many things to eat,

entries.farsi: كوهها و بحرها و دشتها / بوستان‏ها باغ‏ها و كشت‏ها
entries.english: Mountains and seas and plains, fragrant orchards, gardens and sown fields,

entries.farsi: آسمانى بس بلند و پر ضيا / آفتاب و ماهتاب و صد سها
entries.english: A sky very lofty and full of light, sun and moonbeams and a hundred stars.

entries.farsi: از جنوب و از شمال و از دبور / باغها دارد عروسيها و سور
entries.english: From the south-wind and from the north-wind and from the west-wind the gardens have wedding-feasts and banquets.

entries.farsi: در صفت نايد عجايبهاى آن / ‏تو در اين ظلمت چه‏اى در امتحان
entries.english: Its marvels come not into description: why are you in tribulation in this darkness?

entries.farsi: او به حكم حال خود منكر بدى / ‏زين رسالت معرض و كافر شدى
entries.english: Why do you drink blood on the gibbet of this narrow place in the midst of confinement and filth and pain?”

entries.farsi: خون خورى در چار ميخ تنگنا / در ميان جنس و انجاس و عنا
entries.english: It, in virtue of its present state, would be incredulous, and would turn away from this message and would disbelieve it,

entries.farsi: كاين محال است و فريب است و غرور / ز انكه تصويرى ندارد وهم كور
entries.english: Saying, “This is absurd and is a deceit and delusion,” because the judgement of the blind has no imagination.

entries.farsi: جنس چيزى چون نديد ادراك او / نشنود ادراك منكرناك او
entries.english: Inasmuch as its perception has not seen anything of the kind, its incredulous perception would not listen;

entries.farsi: همچنان كه خلق عام اندر جهان / ‏ز آن جهان ابدال مى‏گويندشان
entries.english: Just as in this world the Abdal speak of that world to the common folk,

entries.farsi: كاين جهان چاهى است بس تاريك و تنگ / ‏هست بيرون عالمى بى‏بو و رنگ
entries.english: Saying, “This world is an exceeding dark and narrow pit; outside is a world without scent or colour”:

entries.farsi: هيچ در گوش كسى ز ايشان نرفت / ‏كاين طمع آمد حجاب ژرف و زفت
entries.english: Naught entered into the ear of a single one of them, for this desire is a barrier huge and stout.

entries.farsi: گوش را بندد طمع از استماع / ‏چشم را بندد غرض از اطالع
entries.english: Desire closes the ear from hearing; self-interest closes the eye from beholding,

entries.farsi: همچنان كه آن جنين را طمع خون / ‏كان غذاى اوست در اوطان دون
entries.english: Even as, in the case of the embryo, desire for the blood which is its nourishment in the low abodes

entries.farsi: از حديث اين جهان محجوب كرد / غير خون او مى‏نداند چاشت خورد
entries.english: Debarred it from the news of this world: it knows no breakfast but blood

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى خورندگان پيل بچه از حرص و ترك نصيحت ناصح
sections.title_en: Story of those who ate the young elephant from greed and because they neglected the advice of the sincere counsellor

entries.farsi: آن شنيدى تو كه در هندوستان / ‏ديد دانايى گروهى دوستان
entries.english: Have you heard that in India a sage spied a party of friends?

entries.farsi: گرسنه مانده شده بى‏برگ و عور / مى‏رسيدند از سفر از راه دور
entries.english: Left hungry, lacking provisions, and naked, they were coming from travel on a far road.

entries.farsi: مهر داناييش جوشيد و بگفت / ‏خوش سالميشان و چون گلبن شكفت
entries.english: His wisdom’s love was stirred, and he gave them a fair greeting and blossomed like a rose-bush.

entries.farsi: گفت دانم كز تجوع و ز خال / جمع آمد رنجتان زين كربال
entries.english: “I know,” he said, “that anguish has gathered upon you from this Karbala in consequence of hunger and emptiness;

entries.farsi: ليك اللهَّ اهلل اى قوم جليل / ‏تا نباشد خوردتان فرزند پيل
entries.english: But, for God’s sake, for God’s sake, O illustrious company let not your food be the young of the elephant!

entries.farsi: پيل هست اين سو كه اكنون مى‏رويد / پيل زاده مشكنيد و بشنويد
entries.english: The elephant is in this direction that you are now going; do not tear in pieces the elephant’s offspring, but listen.

entries.farsi: پيل بچگانند اندر راهتان / ‏صيد ايشان هست بس دل خواهتان
entries.english: The young elephants are on your road: to hunt them down is what your hearts desire exceedingly.

entries.farsi: بس ضعيفند و لطيف و بس سمين / ‏ليك مادر هست طالب در كمين
entries.english: They are very weak and tender and very fat, but their mother is searching lying in wait.

entries.farsi: آتش و دود آيد از خرطوم او / الحذر ز آن كودك مرحوم او
entries.english: She will roam a hundred leagues’ distance in quest of her children, moaning and making lament.

entries.farsi: از پى فرزند صد فرسنگ راه / ‏او بگردد در حنين و آه آه
entries.english: Fire and smoke issue from her trunk: beware of those pitied children of hers !”

entries.farsi: اوليا اطفال حقند اى پسر / در حضور و غيبت ايشان با خبر
entries.english: O son, the saints are God’s children: in absence and presence well aware.

entries.farsi: غايبى منديش از نقصانشان / ‏كاو كشد كين از براى جانشان
entries.english: Do not deem absence to be the result of imperfection on their part, for He takes vengeance for the sake of their spirits.

entries.farsi: گفت اطفال منند اين اوليا / در غريبى فرد از كار و كيا
entries.english: He said, “These saints are My children in exile, sundered from dominion and glory;

entries.farsi: از براى امتحان خوار و يتيم / ‏ليك اندر سر منم يار و نديم
entries.english: Despised and orphaned for the sake of probation, but secretly I am their friend and intimate.

entries.farsi: پشت دار جمله عصمتهاى من / ‏گوييا هستند خود اجزاى من
entries.english: All of them are supported by My protections: you may say they are in truth parts of Me.

entries.farsi: هان و هان اين دلق پوشان منند / صد هزار اندر هزار و يك تنند
entries.english: Take heed! Take heed! These are My dervishes; they are a hundred thousand thousand and they are one body.”

entries.farsi: ور نه كى كردى به يك چوبى هنر / موسيى فرعون را زير و زبر
entries.english: Else, how should a Moses have overthrown Pharaoh by means of one goodly rod?

entries.farsi: ور نه كى كردى به يك نفرين بد / نوح شرق و غرب را غرقاب خود
entries.english: Else, how should Noah have submerged East and West in his Flood by means of one evil curse?

entries.farsi: بر نكندى يك دعاى لوط راد / جمله شهرستانشان را بى‏مراد
entries.english: One prayer of the generous Lot would not have razed their entire city in despair.

entries.farsi: گشت شهرستان چون فردوسشان / ‏دجله‏ى آب سيه رو بين نشان
entries.english: Their city, resembling Paradise, became a lake of black water: go, behold the sign!

entries.farsi: سوى شام است اين نشان و اين خبر / در ره قدسش ببينى در گذر
entries.english: This sign and this information lie in the direction of Syria: you will see it as you pass on the way to Jerusalem.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران ز انبياى حق پرست / ‏خود به هر قرنى سياستها بده ست
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of prophets who worshipped God truly there have been chastisements in every generation

entries.farsi: گر بگويم وين بيان افزون شود / خود جگر چه بود كه كه‏ها خون شود
entries.english: If I should tell on and if this narration should increase, not only hearts but the mountains would bleed.

entries.farsi: خون شود كه‏ها و باز آن بفسرد / تو نبينى خون شدن كورى و رد
entries.english: The mountains bleed and again become solid; you do not see them bleed: you are blind and reprobate.

entries.farsi: طرفه كورى دور بين تيز چشم / ‏ليك از اشتر نبيند غير پشم
entries.english: A marvellous blind man, far-sighted and keen-eyed, but sees nothing of the camel except the hair!

entries.farsi: مو به مو بيند ز صرفه‏ى حرص انس / ‏رقص بى‏مقصود دارد همچو خرس
entries.english: Man, from the frugality of greed, inspects hair by hair: like a bear, he keeps dancing to no purpose.

entries.farsi: رقص آن جا كن كه خود را بشكنى / ‏پنبه را از ريش شهوت بر كنى
entries.english: Dance where you break yourself and tear away the cotton from the sore of lust.

entries.farsi: رقص و جوالن بر سر ميدان كنند / رقص اندر خون خود مردان كنند
entries.english: Men dance and wheel on the battle-field: they dance in their own blood.

entries.farsi: چون رهند از دست خود دستى زنند / چون جهند از نقص خود رقصى‏كنند
entries.english: When they are freed from the hand of self, they clap a hand; when they escape from their own imperfection, they make a dance.

entries.farsi: مطربانشان از درون دف مى‏زنند / بحرها در شورشان كف مى‏زنند
entries.english: From within them musicians strike the tambourine; at their ecstasy the seas burst into foam.

entries.farsi: تو نبينى ليك بهر گوششان / ‏برگها بر شاخها هم كف‏زنان
entries.english: You see it not, but for their ears the leaves too on the boughs are clapping hands.

entries.farsi: تو نبينى برگها را كف زدن / ‏گوش دل بايد نه اين گوش بدن
entries.english: You do not see the clapping of the leaves: one must have the spiritual ear, not this ear of the body.

entries.farsi: سر كشد گوش محمد در سخن / ‏كش بگويد در نبى حق هُوَ أذن
entries.english: Close the ear of the head to jesting and lying, that you may see the resplendent city of the soul.

entries.farsi: گوش سر بر بند از هزل و دروغ / ‏تا ببينى شهر جان با فروغ
entries.english: The ear of Mohammed draws out the hidden meaning in the words, for God says of him in the Qur’an, “He is an ear.”

entries.farsi: سربه‏سر گوش است و چشم است اين نبى / ‏تازه زو ما مرضع است او ما صبى
entries.english: This Prophet is entirely ear and eye; we are refreshed by him: he is the breast and we the boy

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد باز ران / ‏سوى اهل پيل و بر آغاز ران
entries.english: This discourse has no end. Go back to those who had to do with the elephant, and start at the beginning.

sections.title_fa: ‏بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى متعرضان پيل بچگان
sections.title_en: The remainder of the Story of those who molested the young elephants

entries.farsi: هر دهان را پيل بويى مى‏كند / گرد معده‏ى هر بشر بر مى‏تند
entries.english: The elephant takes a sniff at every mouth and keeps poking round the belly of every man,

entries.farsi: تا كجا يابد كباب پور خويش / ‏تا نمايد انتقام و زور خويش
entries.english: To see where she will find the roasted flesh of her young, so that she may manifest her vengeance and strength.”

entries.farsi: هان كه بوياى دهانتان خالق است / ‏كى برد جان غير آن كاو صادق است
entries.english: You eat the flesh of God’s servants: you backbite them, you will suffer retribution.

entries.farsi: گوشتهاى بندگان حق خورى / ‏غيبت ايشان كنى كيفر برى
entries.english: Beware, for he that smells your mouths is the Creator: how shall any one save his life except him that is true?

entries.farsi: و ان آن افسوسيى كش بوى گير / باشد اندر گور منكر يا نكير
entries.english: Woe to the scoffer whose smell shall be tested in the grave by Munkar or Nakir!

entries.farsi: نى دهان دزديدن امكان ز آن مهان / ‏نه دهان خوش كردن از دارو دهان
entries.english: There is no possibility of withdrawing the mouth from those mighty ones, or of sweetening the mouth with medicinal ointments.

entries.farsi: آب و روغن نيست مر رو پوش را / راه حيلت نيست عقل و هوش را
entries.english: There is no water and oil to cover the face, there is no way of evasion to intelligence and sagacity.

entries.farsi: چند كوبد زخمهاى گرزشان / ‏بر سر هر ژاژخا و مرزشان
entries.english: How many a time will the blows of their maces beat upon the head and rump of every vain gabbler!

entries.farsi: گرز عزراييل را بنگر اثر / گر نبينى چوب و آهن در صور
entries.english: Look at the effect of the mace of ‘Azra’il, if you do not see the wood and iron in forms.

entries.farsi: هم به صورت مى‏نمايد گه‏گهى / ‏ز آن همان رنجور باشد آگهى
entries.english: Sometimes too they appear in form: the patient is aware thereof.

entries.farsi: گويد آن رنجور اى ياران من / ‏چيست اين شمشير بر ساران من
entries.english: The patient says, O my friends, what is this sword over my head? “

entries.farsi: ما نمى‏بينيم باشد اين خيال / ‏چه خيال است اين كه اين هست ارتحال
entries.english: “We do not see it; this must be imagined.” What fancy is this? For it is departure.

entries.farsi: چه خيال است اين كه اين چرخ نگون / ‏از نهيب اين خيالى شد كنون
entries.english: What fancy is this, from terror of which this inverted sphere has now become a phantom?

entries.farsi: گرزها و تيغها محسوس شد / پيش بيمار و سرش منكوس شد
entries.english: To the sick man the maces and swords became perceptible, and his head dropped down.

entries.farsi: او همى‏بيند كه آن از بهر اوست / ‏چشم دشمن بسته ز آن و چشم دوست
entries.english: He sees that that is for his sake: the eye of foe and friend is barred from it.

entries.farsi: حرص دنيا رفت و چشمش تيز شد / چشم او روشن گه خون‏ريز شد
entries.english: Worldly greed vanished, his eye became keen: his eye became illumined at the moment of bloodshed.

entries.farsi: مرغ بى‏هنگام شد آن چشم او / از نتيجه‏ى كبر او و خشم او
entries.english: That eye of his, from the result of his pride and his anger, became the cock that crows unseasonably.

entries.farsi: سر بريدن واجب آيد مرغ را / كاو به غير وقت جنباند درا
entries.english: It is necessary to cut off the head of the bird that rings the bell at the wrong time.

entries.farsi: هر زمان نزعى است جزو جانت را / بنگر اندر نزع جان ايمانت را
entries.english: At every moment your particular spirit is struggling with death: in your spirit’s death-struggle look to your faith!

entries.farsi: عمر تو مانند هميان زر است / ‏روز و شب مانند دينار اشمر است
entries.english: Your life is like a purse of gold: day and night are like him who counts the gold coins.

entries.farsi: مى‏شمارد مى‏دهد زر بى‏وقوف / ‏تا كه خالى گردد و آيد خسوف
entries.english: He counts and gives the gold without stopping, until it is emptied and there comes the eclipse.

entries.farsi: پس بنه بر جاى هر دم را عوض / ‏تا ز وَ اسْ جُدْ وَ اقْتَرِبْ يابى غرض
entries.english: If you take away from a mountain and do not put in the place, the mountain will be demolished by that giving.

entries.farsi: گر ز كه بستانى و ننهى به جاى / ‏اندر آيد كوه ز آن دادن ز پاى
entries.english: Therefore, for every breath, put an equivalent in its place, so that by and fall to worship and draw nigh you may gain your object.

entries.farsi: در تمامى كارها چندين مكوش / ‏جز به كارى كه بود در دين مكوش
entries.english: Do not strive so much to complete affairs: do not strive in any affair that is not religious.

entries.farsi: عاقبت تو رفت خواهى ناتمام / ‏كارهايت ابتر و نان تو خام
entries.english: at the end you will depart incomplete, your affairs marred and your bread unbaked.

entries.farsi: و آن عمارت كردن گور و لحد / نه به سنگ است و به چوب و نه لبد
entries.english: And the beautifying of your grave and sepulchre is not by means of stone and wood and plaster;

entries.farsi: بلكه خود را در صفا گورى كنى / ‏در منى او كنى دفن منى
entries.english: Nay, but by digging for yourself a grave in purity and burying egoism in His egoism,

entries.farsi: خاك او گردى و مدفون غمش / تا دمت يابد مددها از دمش
entries.english: And by becoming His dust and buried in love of Him, so that your breath may gain replenishments from His breath.

entries.farsi: گورخانه و قبه‏ها و كنگره / ‏نبود از اصحاب معنى آن سره
entries.english: A tomb with domes and turrets— that is not good on the part of the followers of Reality.

entries.farsi: بنگر اكنون زنده اطلس پوش را / هيچ اطلس دست‏گيرد هوش را
entries.english: Look now at a living person attired in satin: does the satin help his understanding at all?

entries.farsi: در عذاب منكر است آن جان او / كژدم غم در دل غمدان او
entries.english: His soul is in hateful torment, the scorpion of grief is in his grief-laden heart.

entries.farsi: از برون بر ظاهرش نقش و نگار / و ز درون ز انديشه‏ها او زار زار
entries.english: Outside, on his exterior, embroideries and decorations; but within he is sorely lamenting from thoughts,

entries.farsi: و آن يكى بينى در آن دلق كهن / ‏چون نبات انديشه و شكر سخن
entries.english: While you may see another in an old patched frock, his thoughts as the sugar-cane and his words (like) sugar.

sections.title_fa: باز گشتن به حكايت پيل
sections.title_en: Returning to the story of the elephant

entries.farsi: گفت ناصح بشنويد اين پند من / ‏تا دل و جانتان نگردد ممتحن
entries.english: Said the sincere adviser, “Listen to this counsel of mine, so that your hearts and souls may not be afflicted.

entries.farsi: با گياه و برگها قانع شويد / در شكار پيل بچگان كم رويد
entries.english: Be content with herbage and leaves; do not go in chase of the young elephants.

entries.farsi: من برون كردم ز گردون وام نصح / ‏جز سعادت كى بود انجام نصح
entries.english: I have put off from my neck the debt of admonition: how should the end of admonition be anything but felicity?

entries.farsi: اين بگفت و خير بادى كرد و رفت / ‏گشت قحط و جوعشان در راه زفت
entries.english: I came to deliver the message that I may save you from repentance.

entries.farsi: من به تبليغ رسالت آمدم / ‏تا رهانم مر شما را از ندم
entries.english: Beware! Let not greed waylay you, let not greed for food tear you up by the roots!”

entries.farsi: هين مبادا كه طمع رهتان زند / طمع برگ از بيخهاتان بر كند
entries.english: This he said, and gave a farewell and departed; their famine and hunger waxed great on the way.

entries.farsi: اندر افتادند چون گرگان مست / ‏پاك خوردندش فرو شستند دست
entries.english: Suddenly, in the direction of a highroad, they espied a fat young elephant, newly born.

entries.farsi: ناگهان ديدند سوى جاده‏اى / ‏پور پيلى فربهى نوزاده‏اى
entries.english: They fell upon it like furious wolves, ate it clean up, and washed their hands.

entries.farsi: آن يكى همره نخورد و پند داد / كه حديث آن فقيرش بود ياد
entries.english: One of the fellow-travellers did not eat and exhorted, for the sayings of that dervish were remembered by him.

entries.farsi: از كبابش مانع آمد آن سخن / ‏بخت نو بخشد ترا عقل كهن
entries.english: Those words hindered him from its roasted flesh : old intelligence bestows on you a new fortune.

entries.farsi: پس بيفتادند و خفتند آن همه / ‏و آن گرسنه چون شبان اندر رمه
entries.english: Then they all fell down and slept, but the hungry one like the shepherd in the flock.

entries.farsi: ديد پيلى سهمناكى مى‏رسيد / اوال آمد سوى حارس دويد
entries.english: He saw a frightful elephant approaching: first she came and ran towards him who was keeping guard.

entries.farsi: بوى مى‏كرد آن دهانش را سه بار / هيچ بويى زو نيامد ناگوار
entries.english: She smelt his mouth three times: no disagreeable smell came from it.

entries.farsi: چند بارى گرد او گشت و برفت / ‏مر و را نازرد آن شه پيل زفت
entries.english: She paced round him several times and went off: the huge queen-elephant did not harm him.

entries.farsi: مر لب هر خفته‏اى را بوى كرد / بوى مى‏آمد و را ز آن خفته مرد
entries.english: She smelt the lips of every sleeper, and the smell was coming to her from each of those slumbering men.

entries.farsi: از كباب پيل زاده خورده بود / بر درانيد و بكشتش پيل زود
entries.english: He had eaten of the roasted flesh of the young elephant: the elephant quickly tore him to pieces and killed him.

entries.farsi: بر هوا انداخت هر يك را گزاف / ‏تا همى‏زد بر زمين مى‏شد شكاف
entries.english: At once she set about rending the people of that company one by one, and she had no awe of it.

entries.farsi: در زمان او يك به يك را ز آن گروه / ‏مى‏درانيد و نبودش ز آن شكوه
entries.english: She tossed each one in the air recklessly, so that he dashed on the earth and was cloven asunder.

entries.farsi: اى خورنده‏ى خون خلق از راه برد / تا نيارد خون ايشانت نبرد
entries.english: O drinkers of the people’s blood, leave the way, lest their blood wage war against you.

entries.farsi: مال ايشان خون ايشان دان يقين / ‏ز انكه مال از زور آيد در يمين
entries.english: Know for sure that their property is their blood, because property comes into one’s hand by strength.

entries.farsi: مادر آن پيل بچگان كين كشد / پيل بچه خواره را كيفر كشد
entries.english: The mother of those young elephants will exact vengeance: retribution will slay him that eats the young elephant.

entries.farsi: پيل بچه مى‏خورى اى پاره خوار / هم بر آرد خصم پيل از تو دمار
entries.english: O eater of bribes, you eat the young elephant: from you too the Master of the elephant will wring the breath.

entries.farsi: آن كه يابد بوى حق را از يمن / ‏چون نيابد بوى باطل را ز من
entries.english: The smell put to shame the deviser of fraud: the elephant knows the smell of her child.

entries.farsi: بوى رسوا كرد مكر انديش را / پيل داند بوى طفل خويش را
entries.english: He that perceives the smell of God from Yemen, how should not he perceive the smell of falsehood from me?

entries.farsi: مصطفى چون برد بوى از راه دور / چون نيابد از دهان ما بخور
entries.english: Inasmuch as Mustafa smelt from far away, how should not he smell the odour from our mouths?

entries.farsi: هم بيابد ليك پوشاند ز ما / بوى نيك و بد بر آيد بر سما
entries.english: He does smell it, but he conceals from us: the good and bad smells go up to Heaven.

entries.farsi: تو همى‏خسبى و بوى آن حرام / ‏مى‏زند بر آسمان سبزفام
entries.english: You are sleeping, and the smell of that unlawful deed is beating on the azure sky.

entries.farsi: همره انفاس زشتت مى‏شود / تا به بوگيران گردون مى‏رود
entries.english: It accompanies your foul breaths; it ascends to the smellers in the celestial sphere.

entries.farsi: بوى كبر و بوى حرص و بوى آز / در سخن گفتن بيايد چون پياز
entries.english: The smell of pride and the smell of greed and the smell of lust will become, in speaking, like onions.

entries.farsi: گر خورى سوگند من كى خورده‏ام / ‏از پياز و سير تقوى كرده‏ام
entries.english: If you take oath, saying, “When have I eaten them? I have abstained from onions and garlic,”

entries.farsi: آن دم سوگند غمازى كند / بر دماغ همنشينان بر زند
entries.english: The breath of your oath will inform and will strike upon the noses of those who sit beside you.

entries.farsi: بس دعاها رد شود از بوى آن / ‏آن دل كژ مى‏نمايد در زبان
entries.english: Many prayers, then, are rejected because of the smell thereof: the corrupt heart shows in the tongue.

entries.farsi: اخْ سَؤُا آيد جواب آن دعا / چوب رد باشد جزاى هر دغا
entries.english: The answer to such a prayer is “Get you gone”: the requital for every knave is the cudgel of repulse.

entries.farsi: گر حديثت كج بود معنيت راست / ‏آن كجى لفظ مقبول خداست
entries.english: If your words be wrong and your meaning right, that wrongness of expression is acceptable to God.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه خطاى محبان بهتر از صواب بيگانگان است نزد محبوب
sections.title_en: Explaining that in the sight of the Beloved a fault committed by lovers is better than the correctness of strangers.

entries.farsi: آن بالل صدق در بانگ نماز / حى را هى همى‏خواند از نياز
entries.english: The veracious Bilal in the call to prayer used, from ardent feeling, to pronounce ђayya as hayya,

entries.farsi: تا بگفتند اى پيمبر راست نيست / ‏اين خطا اكنون كه آغاز بناست
entries.english: So that they said, “O Messenger, this fault is not right now when it is the beginning of the edifice.

entries.farsi: اى نبى و اى رسول كردگار / يك موذن كاو بود افصح بيار
entries.english: O Prophet and Messenger of the Creator, get a muezzin who speaks more correctly.

entries.farsi: عيب باشد اول دين و صالح / ‏لحن خواندن لفظ حى على الفالح
entries.english: At the commencement of religion and piety, it is a disgrace to mispronounce hayy ala’l-falah.”

entries.farsi: خشم پيغمبر بجوشيد و بگفت / ‏يك دو رمزى از عنايات نهفت
entries.english: The Prophet’s wrath boiled up, and he gave one or two indications of the hidden favours which God had bestowed upon Bilal,

entries.farsi: كاى خسان نزد خدا هى بالل / ‏بهتر از صد حى و خى و قيل و قال
entries.english: Saying, “ O base men, in God’s sight the hayy of Bilal is better than a hundred ha’s and kha’s and words and phrases.

entries.farsi: وامشورانيد تا من رازتان / ‏وانگويم آخر و آغازتان
entries.english: Do not stir me to anger, lest I divulge your secret— your end and your beginning.”

entries.farsi: گر ندارى تو دم خوش در دعا / رو دعا مى‏خواه ز اخوان صفا
entries.english: If you have not a sweet breath in prayer, go and beg a prayer from the pure.

sections.title_fa: امر حق تعالى به موسى عليه السلام كه مرا به دهانى خوان كه بدان دهان گناه نكرده‏اى
sections.title_en: How God most High commanded Moses, on whom be peace, saying, “Call unto Me with a mouth with which you have not sinned.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى ز من مى‏جو پناه / ‏با دهانى كه نكردى تو گناه
entries.english: He said, “O Moses, ask Me for protection with a mouth you have not sinned with.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى من ندارم آن دهان / ‏گفت ما را از دهان غير خوان
entries.english: Moses said, “I have not such a mouth.” God said, “Call unto Me by the mouth of others.”

entries.farsi: از دهان غير كى كردى گناه / ‏از دهان غير بر خوان كاى اله
entries.english: When did you sin by the mouth of others? Invoke by the mouth of others, crying, “O God!”

entries.farsi: آن چنان كن كه دهانها مر ترا / در شب و در روزها آرد دعا
entries.english: Act in such wise that mouths may pray for you in the nights and days.

entries.farsi: از دهانى كه نكرده‏ستى گناه / ‏و آن دهان غير باشد عذر خواه
entries.english: Ask pardon by a mouth with which you have committed no sin— and that will be the mouth of others—

entries.farsi: يا دهان خويشتن را پاك كن / ‏روح خود را چابك و چالاك كن
entries.english: Or make your own mouth pure; make your spirit alert and nimble.

entries.farsi: ذكر حق پاك است چون پاكى رسيد / رخت بر بندد برون آيد پليد
entries.english: Praise of God is pure: when purity has come, defilement packs and goes out.

entries.farsi: مى‏گريزد ضدها از ضدها / شب گريزد چون بر افروزد ضيا
entries.english: Contraries flee from contraries: night flees when the light shines forth.

entries.farsi: چون در آيد نام پاك اندر دهان / ‏نى پليدى ماند و نى اندهان
entries.english: When the pure Name comes into the mouth, neither impurity remains nor sorrows.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه اللهَّ گفتن نيازمند عين لبيك گفتن حق است
sections.title_en: Showing that the supplicant’s invocation of God is essentially the same thing as God’s response to him

entries.farsi: آن يكى اللهَّ مى‏گفتى شبى / ‏تا كه شيرين مى‏شد از ذكرش لبى
entries.english: One night a certain man was crying “Allah! “ till his lips were growing sweet with praise of Him.

entries.farsi: گفت شيطان آخر اى بسيار گو / اين همه اللهَّ را لبيك كو
entries.english: The Devil said, “Please, O garrulous one, where is the ‘Here am I’ to all this ‘Allah’?

entries.farsi: مى‏نيايد يك جواب از پيش تخت / ‏چند اللهَّ مى‏زنى با روى سخت
entries.english: Not a single response is coming from the Throne: how long will you cry ‘Allah’ with grim face?”

entries.farsi: او شكسته دل شد و بنهاد سر / ديد در خواب او خضر را در خضر
entries.english: He became broken-hearted and laid down his head: in a dream he saw Khadir amidst the verdure.

entries.farsi: گفت هين از ذكر چون وامانده‏اى / ‏چون پشيمانى از آن كش خوانده‏اى
entries.english: He said, “Listen, you have held back from praising God: how is it that you repent of having called unto Him?”

entries.farsi: گفت لبيكم نمى‏آيد جواب / ‏ز آن همى‏ترسم كه باشم رد باب
entries.english: He said, “No Here am I is coming to me in response, hence I fear that I may be driven away from the Door.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن اللهَّ تو لبيك ماست / ‏و آن نياز و درد و سوزت پيك ماست
entries.english: He said, “That ‘Allah’ of yours is My ‘Here am I,’ and that supplication and grief and ardour of yours is My messenger.

entries.farsi: حيله‏ها و چاره جوييهاى تو / جذب ما بود و گشاد اين پاى تو
entries.english: Your shifts and attempts to find a means were My drawing, and released your feet.

entries.farsi: ترس و عشق تو كمند لطف ماست / ‏زير هر يا رب تو لبيكهاست
entries.english: Your fear and love are the noose to catch My favour: beneath every ‘O Lord’ is many a ‘Here am I’.”

entries.farsi: جان جاهل زين دعا جز دور نيست / ‏ز انكه يا رب گفتنش دستور نيست
entries.english: Far from this prayer is the soul of the fool, because to him it is not permitted to cry “O Lord.”

entries.farsi: بر دهان و بر دلش قفل است و بند / تا ننالد با خدا وقت گزند
entries.english: On his mouth and heart are lock and bolt, to the end that he may not moan unto God in the hour of bale.

entries.farsi: داد مر فرعون را صد ملك و مال / ‏تا بكرد او دعوى عز و جلال
entries.english: He gave to Pharaoh hundredfold possessions and riches, so that he claimed might and majesty.

entries.farsi: داد او را جمله ملك اين جهان / ‏حق ندادش درد و رنج و اندهان
entries.english: In his whole life that man of evil nature felt no headache, lest he should moan unto God.

entries.farsi: در همه عمرش نديد او درد سر / تا ننالد سوى حق آن بد گهر
entries.english: God gave him all the empire of this world, He did not give him grief and pain and sorrows.

entries.farsi: خواندن بى‏درد از افسردگى است / ‏خواندن با درد از دل بردگى است
entries.english: Grief is better than the empire of the world, so that you may call unto God in secret.

entries.farsi: درد آمد بهتر از ملك جهان / ‏تا بخوانى مر خدا را در نهان
entries.english: The call of the griefless is from a frozen heart, the call of the grieving one is from rapture:

entries.farsi: آن شده آواز صافى و حزين / ‏اى خدا وى مستغاث و اى معين
entries.english: To withdraw the voice under the lips, to bear in mind origin and beginning;

entries.farsi: آن كشيدن زير لب آواز را / ياد كردن مبدا و آغاز را
entries.english: The voice become pure and sad, “O God!” and “O You whose help is sought!” and “O Helper!”

entries.farsi: ناله‏ى سگ در رهش بى‏جذبه نيست / ‏ز انكه هر راغب اسير ره زنى است
entries.english: The moan of a dog for His sake is not void of attraction, because everyone who desires is a brigand’s captive—

entries.farsi: چون سگ كهفى كه از مردار رست / ‏بر سر خوان شهنشاهان نشست
entries.english: As the dog of the Cave, this was freed from carrion and sat at the table of the emperors:

entries.farsi: تا قيامت مى‏خورد او پيش غار / آب رحمت عارفانه بى‏تغار
entries.english: Until the Resurrection, before the Cave it is drinking in Gnostic wise without pot the water of mercy.

entries.farsi: اى بسا سگ پوست كاو را نام نيست / ‏ليك اندر پرده بى‏آن جام نيست
entries.english: Oh, there is many a one in a dog’s skin, who has no name, yet is not without that cup in secret.

entries.farsi: جان بده از بهر اين جام اى پسر / بى‏جهاد و صبر كى باشد ظفر
entries.english: Give your life for this cup, O son: how may victory be without warfare and patience?

entries.farsi: صبر كردن بهر اين نبود حرج / ‏صبر كن كالصبر مفتاح الفرج
entries.english: To show patience for the sake of this is no hardship: show patience, for patience is the key to joy.

entries.farsi: زين كمين بى‏صبر و حزمى كس نجست / ‏حزم را خود صبر آمد پا و دست
entries.english: From this ambush none escaped without some patience and prudence: to prudence, indeed, patience is the foot and hand.

entries.farsi: حزم كن از خورد كاين زهرين گياست / ‏حزم كردن زور و نور انبياست
entries.english: Exercise prudence in eating, for this is poisonous herbage: to exercise prudence is the strength and light of the prophets.

entries.farsi: كاه باشد كاو به هر بادى جهد / كوه كى مر باد را وزنى نهد
entries.english: He that jumps at every breath of wind is straw, how should the mountain attach any weight to the wind?

entries.farsi: هر طرف غولى همى‏خواند ترا / كاى برادر راه خواهى هين بيا
entries.english: On every side a ghoul is calling you— “Hark, O brother, you wish the way, come.

entries.farsi: رهنمايم همرهت باشم رفيق / ‏من قالووزم در اين راه دقيق
entries.english: I will show the way, I will be your kind fellow-traveller, I am the guide on this intricate path.”

entries.farsi: نى قالووز است و نى ره داند او / يوسفا كم رو سوى آن گرگ خو
entries.english: She is not the guide, and she does not know the way. O Joseph, do not go towards that wolfish one!

entries.farsi: حزم اين باشد كه نفريبد ترا / چرب و نوش و دامهاى اين سرا
entries.english: Prudence is this, that you be not beguiled by the fat things and sweets and snares of the World;

entries.farsi: كه نه چربش دارد و نى نوش او / سحر خواند مى‏دمد در گوش او
entries.english: For she has neither fat nor sweet: she chants spells of magic and breathes into your ear,

entries.farsi: كه بيا مهمان ما اى روشنى / ‏خانه آن تست و تو آن منى
entries.english: Saying, “Come in as my guest, O light: the house is yours, and you are mine.”

entries.farsi: حزم آن باشد كه گويى تخمه‏ام / ‏يا سقيمم خسته‏ى اين دخمه‏ام
entries.english: Prudence is this, that you say, “I have indigestion” or “I am ill, I am a sick man in this charnel-house”;

entries.farsi: يا سرم درد است درد سر ببر / يا مرا خوانده ست آن خالو پسر
entries.english: Or “ My head aches: take away my headache,” or “the son of my maternal uncle has invited me”—

entries.farsi: ز انكه يك نوشت دهد با نيشها / كه بكارد در تو نوشش ريشها
entries.english: For she will give you honey with stings, so that her honey will plant in you sores.

entries.farsi: زر اگر پنجاه اگر شصتت دهد / ماهيا او گوشت در شستت دهد
entries.english: Whether she give you fifty or sixty gold, she gives you, O fish, flesh on a hook.

entries.farsi: گر دهد خود كى دهد آن پر حيل / ‏جوز پوسيده ست گفتار دغل
entries.english: If she gives, when does that deceitful one really give? The words of the swindler are rotten walnuts.

entries.farsi: ژغژغ آن عقل و مغزت را برد / صد هزاران عقل را يك نشمرد
entries.english: Their rattling robs you of understanding and brain and does not reckon myriads of understandings as one.

entries.farsi: يار تو خورجين تست و كيسه‏ات / ‏گر تو رامينى مجو جز ويسه‏ات
entries.english: Your bag and your purse are your friend,: if you are Ramin, seek none but your Wisa.

entries.farsi: ويسه و معشوق تو هم ذات تست / ‏وين برونيها همه آفات تست
entries.english: It is your essential self that is your Wisa and beloved, and all these external things are banes to you.

sections.title_en: 230 Prudence is this, that when they invite you,

entries.farsi: حزم آن باشد كه چون دعوت كنند / تو نگويى مست و خواهان منند
entries.english: you should not say, “They are enamored and fond of me.”

entries.farsi: دعوت ايشان صفير مرغ دان / ‏كه كند صياد در مكمن نهان
entries.english: Know that their invitation is the bird’s whistle which the fowler gives concealed in place of ambush:

entries.farsi: مرغ مرده پيش بنهاده كه اين / ‏مى‏كند اين بانگ و آواز و حنين
entries.english: He has put forward a dead bird, that this is making this plaintive noise and cry.

entries.farsi: مرغ پندارد كه جنس اوست او / جمع آيد بر دردشان پوست او
entries.english: The birds think he is one of their kind: they gather round, and he rends their skins

entries.farsi: جز مگر مرغى كه حزمش داد حق / ‏تا نگردد گيج آن دانه و ملق
entries.english: Except, no doubt, the bird on which God has bestowed prudence, so that it may not be fooled by that bait and enticement.

entries.farsi: هست بى‏حزمى پشيمانى يقين / ‏بشنو اين افسانه را در شرح اين
entries.english: Imprudence is assuredly repentance. Hear the following story in explanation of this.

sections.title_fa: فريفتن روستايى شهرى را و به دعوت خواندن به البه و الحاح بسيار
sections.title_en: How the countryman deceived the townsman and invited him with humble entreaties and great importunity.

entries.farsi: اى برادر بود اندر ما مضى / شهريى با روستايى آشنا
entries.english: In the past, O brother, there was a townsman intimate with a countryman.

entries.farsi: روستايى چون سوى شهر آمدى / ‏خرگه اندر كوى آن شهرى زدى
entries.english: Whenever the countryman came to town, he would pitch his tent in the street of the townsman.

entries.farsi: دو مه و سه ماه مهمانش بدى / ‏بر دكان او و بر خوانش بدى
entries.english: He would be his guest for two or three months, he would be in his shop and at his table,

entries.farsi: هر حوايج را كه بوديش آن زمان / ‏راست كردى مرد شهرى رايگان
entries.english: And the townsman would provide, free of cost, everything that he wanted during that time.

entries.farsi: رو به شهرى كرد و گفت اى خواجه تو / هيچ مى‏نايى سوى ده فرجه جو
entries.english: He turned to the townsman and said, “Sire, are you never coming to the country for a holiday?

entries.farsi: اللهَّ اللهَّ جمله فرزندان بيار / كاين زمان گلشن است و نو بهار
entries.english: Bring all your children, in God’s name, for this is the time of the rose-garden and the springtide;

entries.farsi: يا به تابستان بيا وقت ثمر / تا ببندم خدمتت را من كمر
entries.english: Or come in summer, in the fruit-season, that I may brace my belt to do you service.

entries.farsi: خيل و فرزندان و قومت را بيار / در ده ما باش سه ماه و چهار
entries.english: Bring your retinue and your children and kinsfolk, and stay in our village three or four months,

entries.farsi: كه بهاران خطه‏ى ده خوش بود / كشت زار و الله‏ى دل كش بود
entries.english: For in spring the countryside is pleasant; there are sown fields and lovely anemones.”

entries.farsi: وعده دادى شهرى او را دفع حال / ‏تا بر آمد بعد وعده هشت سال
entries.english: The townsman was putting him off with promises, until eight years had elapsed since the promise.

entries.farsi: او به هر سالى همى‏گفتى كه كى / ‏عزم خواهى كرد كامد ماه دى
entries.english: Every year he would say, “When will you set out on the journey?—for the month of December is come,”

entries.farsi: او بهانه ساختى كامسالمان / ‏از فالن خطه بيامد ميهمان
entries.english: And he would make an excuse, saying, “This year we have a guest who has come from such and such a district;

entries.farsi: سال ديگر گر توانم وارهيد / از مهمات آن طرف خواهم دويد
entries.english: Next year I will run to that part, if I can escape from the pressing affairs.”

entries.farsi: گفت هستند آن عيالم منتظر / بهر فرزندان تو اى اهل بر
entries.english: He said, “My family are expecting your children, O benefactor.”

entries.farsi: باز هر سالى چو لكلك آمدى / ‏تا مقيم قبه‏ى شهرى شدى
entries.english: Every year he was coming back, like the stork, to reside in the townsman’s pavilion,

entries.farsi: خواجه هر سالى ز زر و مال خويش / ‏خرج او كردى گشادى بال خويش
entries.english: And every year the Khwaja would spend his gold and wealth upon him and open his wings.

entries.farsi: آخرين كرت سه ماه آن پهلوان / ‏خوان نهادش بامدادان و شبان
entries.english: On the last occasion, that paladin set dishes before him at morn and eve for three months.

entries.farsi: از خجالت باز گفت او خواجه را / چند وعده چند بفريبى مرا
entries.english: From shame he again said to the Khwaja, “How long promises? How long will you deceive me?”

entries.farsi: گفت خواجه جسم و جانم وصل جوست / ‏ليك هر تحويل اندر حكم هوست
entries.english: The Khwaja said, “My body and soul are eager for the meeting, but every change depends on the decree of Him.

entries.farsi: آدمى چون كشتى است و بادبان / ‏تا كى آرد باد را آن باد ران
entries.english: Man is like a ship or sail: to see when the Driver of the wind shall send the breeze.”

entries.farsi: باز سوگندان بدادش كاى كريم / ‏گير فرزندان بيا بنگر نعيم
entries.english: Once more he adjured him, crying, “O generous man, take your children and come and behold the pleasures.”

entries.farsi: دست او بگرفت سه كرت به عهد / كاهلل اهلل زو بيا بنماى جهد
entries.english: He took his hand three times in covenant, saying, “In God’s name, come quickly, make the utmost effort!”

entries.farsi: بعد ده سال و به هر سالى چنين / ‏البه‏ها و وعده‏هاى شكرين
entries.english: After ten years—and every year the same sugared entreaties and promises—

entries.farsi: كودكان خواجه گفتند اى پدر / ماه و ابر و سايه هم دارد سفر
entries.english: The Khwaja’s children said, “O father, the moon and the clouds and the shadows too have their journeys.

entries.farsi: حقها بر وى تو ثابت كرده‏اى / ‏رنجها در كار او بس برده‏اى
entries.english: You have laid obligations on him; you have taken great pains on his account,

entries.farsi: او همى‏خواهد كه بعضى حق آن / ‏واگزارد چون شوى تو ميهمان
entries.english: And he wishes to repay some part of that obligation when you become his guest.

entries.farsi: بس وصيت كرد ما را او نهان / ‏كه كشيدش سوى ده البه‏كنان
entries.english: He gave us many injunctions in secret: ‘Bring him to the country,’ said he, ‘coaxing him!’”

entries.farsi: گفت حق است اين ولى اى سيبويه / ‏اتق من شر من أحسنت اليه
entries.english: He said, “This is true, but, O Sibawayh, be on your guard against the malice of him to whom you have shown kindness.

entries.farsi: دوستى تخم دم آخر بود / ترسم از وحشت كه آن فاسد شود
entries.english: Love is the seed of the last breath: I fear that it may be corrupted by estrangement.”

entries.farsi: صحبتى باشد چو شمشير قطوع / ‏همچو دى در بوستان و در زروع
entries.english: There is a friendship like a cutting sword, as December in the gardens and cornfields ;

entries.farsi: صحبتى باشد چو فصل نو بهار / زو عمارتها و دخل بى‏شمار
entries.english: There is a friendship like the season of spring, from which restorations and produce incalculable.

entries.farsi: حزم آن باشد كه ظن بد برى / ‏تا گريزى و شوى از بد برى
entries.english: Prudence is this; that you think evil, so that you may flee and become quit of evil.

entries.farsi: حزم سوء الظن گفته است آن رسول / ‏هر قدم را دام مى‏دان اى فضول
entries.english: The Prophet has said, “Prudence is thinking evil”: know that for every footstep there is a snare, O fool!

entries.farsi: روى صحرا هست هموار و فراخ / هر قدم دامى است كم ران اوستاخ
entries.english: The surface of the plateau is level and broad, every step there is a snare : do not advance boldly.

entries.farsi: آن بز كوهى دود كه دام كو / چون بتازد دامش افتد در گلو
entries.english: The mountain-goat runs on, saying; “Where is the snare?” As it speeds onward; the snare lights on its throat.

entries.farsi: آن كه مى‏گفتى كه كو اينك ببين / ‏دشت مى‏ديدى نمى‏ديدى كمين
entries.english: O you who said “Where?” look and see! You saw the plain; you did not see the ambush.

entries.farsi: بى‏كمين و دام و صياد اى عيار / دنبه كى باشد ميان كشت‏زار
entries.english: Without ambush and snare and hunter, O cunning one, how should there be a sheep’s tail amidst the wheat field?

entries.farsi: آن كه گستاخ آمدند اندر زمين / ‏استخوان و كله‏هاشان را ببين
entries.english: They that came along boldly on the earth— see their bones and skulls!

entries.farsi: چون به گورستان روى اى مرتضى / ‏استخوانشان را بپرس از ما مضى
entries.english: When you go to the graveyard, O you with whom God is pleased, ask their bones concerning that which is past,

entries.farsi: تا به ظاهر بينى آن مستان كور / چون فرو رفتند در چاه غرور
entries.english: That you may see clearly how those blind intoxicated men went down into the pit of delusion.

entries.farsi: چشم اگر دارى تو كورانه ميا / ور ندارى چشم دست آور عصا
entries.english: If you have eyes, do not walk blindly; and if you have not eyes, take a staff in your hand.

entries.farsi: آن عصاى حزم و استدالل را / چون ندارى ديد مى‏كن پيشوا
entries.english: When you have not the staff of prudence and judgement, make the eye your leader;

entries.farsi: ور عصاى حزم و استدالل نيست / ‏بى‏عصا كش بر سر هر ره مه‏ايست
entries.english: And if there is no staff of prudence and judgement, do not stand on every road without a guide.

entries.farsi: گام ز آن سان نه كه نابينا نهد / تا كه پا از چاه و از سگ وارهد
entries.english: Step in the same fashion as a blind man steps, in order that your foot may escape from the pit and the dog.

entries.farsi: لرزلرزان و به ترس و احتياط / مى‏نهد پا تا نيفتد در خباط
entries.english: He plants his foot tremblingly and with fear and precaution, so that he may not fall into derangement.

entries.farsi: اى زدودى جسته در نارى شده / ‏لقمه جسته لقمه‏ى مارى شده
entries.english: O you who have jumped away from some smoke and fallen into a fire, you who have sought a mouthful and become a mouthful for a snake,

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى اهل سبا و طاغى كردن نعمت ايشان را
sections.title_en: Story of the people of Saba and how prosperity made them obstinate

entries.farsi: از صدا آن كوه خود آگاه نيست / ‏سوى معنى هوش كه را راه نيست
entries.english: You have not read the story of the people of Saba. Or you have read it and seen nothing but the echo.

entries.farsi: تو نخواندى قصه‏ى اهل سبا / يا بخواندى و نديدى جز صدا
entries.english: The mountain itself is not aware of the echo: the mind of the mountain has no sense of the meaning.

entries.farsi: او همى بانگى كند بى‏گوش و هوش / ‏چون خمش كردى تو او هم شد خموش
entries.english: Without ear and mind, it goes on making a noise; when you are silent, it also becomes silent.

entries.farsi: داد حق اهل سبا را بس فراغ / ‏صد هزاران قصر و ايوانها و باغ
entries.english: God bestowed on the people of Saba much ease— myriads of castles and palaces and orchards.

entries.farsi: شكر آن نگزاردند آن بد رگان / ‏در وفا بودند كمتر از سگان
entries.english: Those ill-natured ones rendered no thanks for that: in fidelity they were less than dogs.

entries.farsi: مر سگى را لقمه‏ى نانى ز در / چون رسد بر در همى‏بندد كمر
entries.english: When to a dog there comes from the door a piece of bread, he will gird up his loins at the door.

entries.farsi: پاسبان و حارس در مى‏شود / گر چه بر وى جور و سختى مى‏رود
entries.english: He will become the watcher and guardian of the door, even though violence and hard treatment befall him.

entries.farsi: هم بر آن در باشدش باش و قرار / كفر دارد كرد غيرى اختيار
entries.english: Still will he stay and abide at that door: he will deem it ingratitude to prefer another.

entries.farsi: ور سگى آيد غريبى روز و شب / ‏آن سگانش مى‏كنند آن دم ادب
entries.english: And, if a strange dog comes by day or night, the dogs there will at once teach him a lesson,

entries.farsi: كه برو آن جا كه اول منزل است / ‏حق آن نعمت گروگان دل است
entries.english: Saying, “Go to the place that is your first lodging: obligation for that kindness is the heart’s pledge.”

entries.farsi: مى‏گزندش كه برو بر جاى خويش / ‏حق آن نعمت فرو مگذار بيش
entries.english: They will bite him, saying, “Go to your place, and do not any more leave the obligation for that kindness.”

entries.farsi: از در دل و اهل دل آب حيات / ‏چند نوشيدى و وا شد چشمهات
entries.english: From the door of the spirit and spiritual men how long did you drink the water of life, and yours eves were opened!

entries.farsi: بس غذاى سكر و وجد و بى‏خودى / ‏از در اهل دالن بر جان زدى
entries.english: Much food from the door of the spiritual, of intoxication and ecstasy and selflessness, did you cast upon your soul.

entries.farsi: باز اين در را رها كردى ز حرص / ‏گرد هر دكان همى‏گردى چو خرس
entries.english: Afterwards, through greed, you did abandon that door, and you are going round about every shop, like a bear.

entries.farsi: بر در آن منعمان چرب ديگ / ‏مى‏دوى بهر ثريد مرده‏ريگ
entries.english: For the sake of worthless crust of bread (tharid) you are running to the doors of those patrons whose pots are fat.

entries.farsi: چربش اينجا دان كه جان فربه شود / كار نااوميد اينجا به شود
entries.english: Know that here the “fat” is that the soul becomes fat, and here the plight of the desperate is made good.

sections.title_fa: جمع آمدن اهل آفت هر صباحى در صومعه‏ى عيسى عليه السلام جهت طلب شفا به دعاى او
sections.title_en: How the smitten would assemble every morning at the door of the cell of Jesus, on whom be peace, craving to be healed through his prayer.

entries.farsi: جمع گشتندى ز هر اطراف خلق / ‏از ضرير و لنگ و شل و اهل دلق
entries.english: The table of the spiritual is the cell of Jesus: O afflicted one, beware, beware! Do not forsake this door!

entries.farsi: صومعه‏ى عيساست خوان اهل دل / ‏هان و هان اى مبتال اين در مهل
entries.english: From all sides the people would gather— blind and lame and palsied and clothed in rags—

entries.farsi: او چو فارغ گشتى از اوراد خويش / ‏چاشتگه بيرون شدى آن خوب كيش
entries.english: At the door of the cell of Jesus in the morning, that he by his breath might deliver them from tribulation.

entries.farsi: بر در آن صومعه عيسى صباح / ‏تا به دم اوشان رهاند از جناح
entries.english: As soon as he finished his litanies, that man of goodly religion would go forth at morningtide,

entries.farsi: جوق جوقى مبتال ديدى نزار / شسته بر در در اميد و انتظار
entries.english: And would see troops of afflicted feeble folk seated at the door in hope and expectancy.

entries.farsi: گفتى اى اصحاب آفت از خدا / حاجت اين جملگانتان شد روا
entries.english: He would say, “O you that are smitten, the desires of all you here present have been granted by God.

entries.farsi: هين روان گرديد بى‏رنج و عنا / سوى غفارى و اكرام خدا
entries.english: Listen, set off and go without pain or trouble towards the forgiveness and kindness of God.”

entries.farsi: جملگان چون اشتران بسته پاى / ‏كه گشايى زانوى ايشان به راى
entries.english: All, like tethered camels whose knees you unbind with foresight,

entries.farsi: آزمودى تو بسى آفات خويش / ‏يافتى صحت از اين شاهان كيش
entries.english: At his prayer would begin to run on their feet, hastening gladly and joyously to their homes.

entries.farsi: خوش دوان و شادمان سوى خان / ‏از دعاى او شدندى پا دوان
entries.english: You have experienced many maladies in yourself, and have gained health from these kings of religion.

entries.farsi: اى مغفل رشته‏اى بر پاى بند / تا ز خود هم گم نگردى اى لوند
entries.english: How often has your limping been turned into a smooth gait, how often has your soul been made void of grief and pain !

entries.farsi: چند آن لنگى تو رهوار شد / چند جانت بى‏غم و آزار شد
entries.english: O heedless one, tie a string to your foot, that you may not become lost to yourself even, O sluggard !

entries.farsi: الجرم آن راه بر تو بسته شد / چون دل اهل دل از تو خسته شد
entries.english: Your ingratitude and forgetfulness did not call to mind your drinking of honey.

entries.farsi: ناسپاسى و فراموشى تو / ياد ناورد آن عسل نوشى تو
entries.english: Necessarily, that way became barred to you, since the hearts of the “men of heart” were made sore by you.

entries.farsi: زودشان درياب و استغفار كن / ‏همچو ابرى گريه‏هاى زار كن
entries.english: Quickly overtake them and ask pardon of God; weep lamentably like a cloud,

entries.farsi: تا گلستانشان سوى تو بشكفد / ميوه‏هاى پخته بر خود واكفد
entries.english: In order that their rose-garden may open its blossoms to you, and that the ripe fruits may burst and reveal themselves.

entries.farsi: هم بر آن در گرد كم از سگ مباش / ‏با سگ كهف ار شده‏ستى خواجه‏تاش
entries.english: Pace round that same door: do not be less than a dog, if you have become a fellow-servant with the dog of the Cave,

entries.farsi: آن در اول كه خوردى استخوان / ‏سخت گير و حق گزار آن را ممان
entries.english: Because even dogs admonish dogs, saying, “Fix your heart on your first home,

entries.farsi: چون سگان هم مر سگان را ناصحند / كه دل اندر خانه‏ى اول ببند
entries.english: Hold fast to the first door where you did eat bones, and fulfill your obligation : do not leave that.”

entries.farsi: مى‏گزندش كز ادب آن جا رود / وز مقام اولين مفلح شود
entries.english: They keep biting him, that from a sense of duty he may go thither and be prospered by his first abode.

entries.farsi: مى‏گزندش كاى سگ طاغى برو / با ولى نعمتت ياغى مشو
entries.english: They bite him, saying, “O naughty dog, Go! Do not become an enemy to your benefactor.

entries.farsi: بر همان در همچو حلقه بسته باش / ‏پاسبان و چابك و برجسته باش
entries.english: Be attached, like the door-ring, to that same door; keep watch and be nimble and ready to spring.

entries.farsi: صورت نقض وفاى ما مباش / ‏بى‏وفايى را مكن بى‏هوده فاش
entries.english: Do not be the type of our breaking faith; do not recklessly make disloyalty notorious.

entries.farsi: مر سگان را چون وفا آمد شعار / رو سگان را ننگ و بد نامى ميار
entries.english: Since fidelity is the badge by which the dogs are known, Go and do not bring disgrace and ill-fame upon the dogs.”

entries.farsi: بى‏وفايى چون سگان را عار بود / بى‏وفايى چون روا دارى نمود
entries.english: Inasmuch as unfaithfulness has been a disgrace to dogs, how should you deem it right to show unfaithfulness?

entries.farsi: بى‏وفايى دان وفا با رد حق / ‏بر حقوق حق ندارد كس سبق
entries.english: The high God has boasted of faithfulness: He has said, “Who but I am most faithful in keeping a promise?”

entries.farsi: حق تعالى فخر آورد از وفا / گفت من اوفى بعهد غيرنا
entries.english: Know that faithfulness with rejection of God is unfaithfulness: no one has precedence over God’s rights.

entries.farsi: حق مادر بعد از آن شد كان كريم / ‏كرد او را از جنين تو غريم
entries.english: Your mother’s right arose after that Bounteous One had made her indebted for your embryo.

entries.farsi: صورتى كردت درون جسم او / داد در حملش و را آرام و خو
entries.english: He bestowed on you a form within her body; He gave ease to her during pregnancy and accustomed her.

entries.farsi: همچو جزو متصل ديد او ترا / متصل را كرد تدبيرش جدا
entries.english: She deemed you as a part joined; His providence separated that which was joined.

entries.farsi: حق هزاران صنعت و فن ساخته ست / ‏تا كه مادر بر تو مهر انداخته ست
entries.english: God has prepared thousands of artifices and contrivances, so that your mother has thrown love upon you.

entries.farsi: آن كه مادر آفريد و ضرع و شير / با پدر كردش قرين آن خود مگير
entries.english: Therefore God’s right is prior to the mother: whoever does not recognise that right is an ass.

entries.farsi: پس حق حق سابق از مادر بود / هر كه آن حق را نداند خر بود
entries.english: Do not even admit that He created mother, breast, and milk, and united her with the father!

entries.farsi: اى خداوند قديم احسان تو / آن كه دانم و آن كه نه هم آن تو
entries.english: O Lord, O You whose beneficence is eternal, Yours is both that which I know and that which I know not.

entries.farsi: تو بفرمودى كه حق را ياد كن / ‏ز انكه حق من نمى‏گردد كهن
entries.english: You did command, saying, “Remember God, because My right shall never grow old.

entries.farsi: ياد كن لطفى كه كردم آن صبوح / ‏با شما از حفظ در كشتى نوح
entries.english: Remember the kindness which I did to you that morn by protecting in the ship of Noah.

entries.farsi: پيله بابايانتان را آن زمان / ‏دادم از طوفان و از موجش امان
entries.english: At that time I gave your fathers protection from the Flood and from its waves.

entries.farsi: آب آتش خو زمين بگرفته بود / موج او مر اوج كه را مى‏ربود
entries.english: Water, like fire in nature, had covered the earth: its waves were sweeping away the highest peaks of the mountains.

entries.farsi: حفظ كردم من نكردم ردتان / ‏در وجود جد جد جدتان
entries.english: I protected you; I did not spurn you, in the bodies of the ancestors of the ancestors of your ancestors.

entries.farsi: چون شدى سر پشت پايت چون زنم / ‏كارگاه خويش ضايع چون كنم
entries.english: Now that you have come to the head, how should I smite the sole of your foot? How should I let My workshop go to waste?

entries.farsi: چون فداى بى‏وفايان مى‏شوى / ‏از گمان بد بدان سو مى‏روى
entries.english: How are you becoming devoted to the unfaithful and going in that direction from ill thoughts?

entries.farsi: اين گمان بد بر آن جا بر كه تو / مى‏شوى در پيش همچون خود دو تو
entries.english: I am clear of negligence and infidelities, you come to Me and think evil.

entries.farsi: من ز سهو و بى‏وفاييها برى / ‏سوى من آيى گمان بد برى
entries.english: Think this evil thought against the place where you cringe before one like yourself.

entries.farsi: بس گرفتى يار و همراهان زفت / ‏گر ترا پرسم كه كو گويى كه رفت
entries.english: You got many powerful friends and companions: if I ask you, ‘Where?’ you will say, ‘He is gone.’

entries.farsi: يار نيكت رفت بر چرخ برين / ‏يار فسقت رفت در قعر زمين
entries.english: Your good friend is gone up to the highest Heaven; your wicked friend is gone to the bottom of the earth.

entries.farsi: تو بماندى در ميانه آن چنان / ‏بى‏مدد چون آتشى از كاروان
entries.english: You are left in the middle so helpless, like a fire from a caravan.”

entries.farsi: دامن او گير اى يار دلير / كاو منزه باشد از بالا و زير
entries.english: O valiant friend, lay hold of the skirt of Him who is exempt from “above” and “below.”

entries.farsi: نى چو عيسى سوى گردون بر شود / نى چو قارون در زمين اندر رود
entries.english: Neither does He ascend to Heaven, like Jesus, nor go into the earth, like Qarun.

entries.farsi: با تو باشد در مكان و بى‏مكان / ‏چون بمانى از سرا و از دكان
entries.english: He is with you in space and in the spaceless when you leave house and shop behind.

entries.farsi: او بر آرد از كدورتها صفا / مر جفاهاى ترا گيرد وفا
entries.english: He brings forth purity from defilements; He takes your acts of wrong as faithful performance.

entries.farsi: چون جفا آرى فرستد گوشمال / ‏تا ز نقصان واروى سوى كمال
entries.english: When you commit wrong, He sends chastisement, to the end that you may go back from imperfection towards perfection.

entries.farsi: چون تو وردى ترك كردى در روش / ‏بر تو قبضى آيد از رنج و تبش
entries.english: When you have neglected a part of your prayers in the Way, there comes over you a painful and hot feeling of contraction.

entries.farsi: آن ادب كردن بود يعنى مكن / ‏هيچ تحويلى از آن عهد كهن
entries.english: That is the corrective act, meaning, “Do not make any change in the ancient covenant

entries.farsi: پيش از آن كاين قبض زنجيرى شود / اين كه دل گيرى است پا گيرى شود
entries.english: Before this contraction shall become a chain, this which grips the heart shall become a fetter gripping the foot.”

entries.farsi: رنج معقولت شود محسوس و فاش / ‏تا نگيرى اين اشارت را به الش
entries.english: Your mental pain shall become perceptible to the senses and manifest. See that you do not hold this indication as naught.

entries.farsi: در معاصى قبضها دلگير شد / قبضها بعد از اجل زنجير شد
entries.english: The contractions in sins affect the heart; after death contractions become chains.

entries.farsi: دزد چون مال كسان را مى‏برد / قبض و دل تنگى دلش را مى‏خلد
entries.english: Whoever here shall turn his hack upon Our commemoration, We shall give him a straitened life and reward him with blindness.”

entries.farsi: نعط من أعرض هنا عن ذكرنا / ‏عيشه ضنكا و نجزي بالعمى
entries.english: When a thief is carrying off people’s property, contraction and tightening of heart prick his heart,

entries.farsi: او همى‏گويد عجب اين قبض چيست / ‏قبض آن مظلوم كز شرت گريست
entries.english: He says, “I wonder what this contraction is”: “The contraction of the injured person who wept at your wickedness.”

entries.farsi: قبض دل قبض عوان شد الجرم / ‏گشت محسوس آن معانى زد علم
entries.english: When he pays no regard to this contraction, the wind of perseverance blows its fire.

entries.farsi: چون بدين قبض التفاتى كم كند / باد اصرار آتشش را دم كند
entries.english: The contraction that grips the heart turns into the grip of the policeman: inevitably those ideas become sensible and display themselves.

entries.farsi: غصه‏ها زندان شده‏ست و چار ميخ / ‏غصه بيخ است و برويد شاخ بيخ
entries.english: The pangs have become prison and the cross: the pang is the root, and the root produces boughs.

entries.farsi: بيخ پنهان بود هم شد آشكار / قبض و بسط اندرون بيخى شمار
entries.english: The root was hidden, it is revealed. Consider inward contraction and expansion as a root.

entries.farsi: قبض ديدى چاره‏ى آن قبض كن / ‏ز انكه سرها جمله مى‏رويد ز بن
entries.english: When it is a bad root, strike it quickly, so that an ugly thorn may not grow in the garden.

entries.farsi: چون كه بيخ بد بود زودش بزن / ‏تا نرويد زشت خارى در چمن
entries.english: You have felt the contraction: seek a remedy for it, because all heads grow from the bottom.

entries.farsi: بسط ديدى بسط خود را آب ده / ‏چون بر آيد ميوه با اصحاب ده
entries.english: You have felt the expansion: water your expansion, and when the fruit appears, give it to your friends.

sections.title_fa: باقى قصه‏ى اهل سبا
sections.title_en: The remainder of the Story of the people of Saba

entries.farsi: آن سبا ز اهل صبا بودند و خام / ‏كارشان كفران نعمت با كرام
entries.english: Saba were folk given over to dalliance and foolishness; it was their practice to show ingratitude to the generous.

entries.farsi: باشد آن كفران نعمت در مثال / ‏كه كنى با محسن خود تو جدال
entries.english: By way of illustration, it would be ingratitude to dispute with your benefactor,

entries.farsi: كه نمى‏بايد مرا اين نيكوى / ‏من برنجم زين چه رنجه مى‏شوى
entries.english: Saying, “I do not want this kindness, I am annoyed by it: why are you troubling me?

entries.farsi: لطف كن اين نيكويى را دور كن / ‏من نخواهم چشم زودم كور كن
entries.english: Do a favour, take away this kindness; I do not desire an eye: blind me at once!”

entries.farsi: ما نمى‏خواهيم اين ايوان و باغ / ‏نه زنان خوب و نه امن و فراغ
entries.english: Hence the people of Saba said, “put a far distance between us: our blemish is better for us, take away our adornment.

entries.farsi: پس سبا گفتند باعد بيننا / شيننا خير لنا خذ زبننا
entries.english: We do not desire these palaces and orchards, nor fair women nor that safety and ease.

entries.farsi: شهرها نزديك همديگر بد است / ‏آن بيابان است خوش كانجا دد است
entries.english: Towns near to each other are bad; the desert, where the wild beasts are, is good.”

entries.farsi: يطلب اإلنسان في الصيف الشتا / فإذا جاء الشتاء أنكر ذا
entries.english: Man craves winter in summer, and when winter comes, he likes it not,

entries.farsi: فهو ال يرضى بحال أبدا / ال بضيق ال بعيش رغدا
entries.english: for he is never content with any state, neither with poverty nor with a life of plenty.

entries.farsi: قُتِلَ الإِْنْسانُ ما أكفره / ‏كلما نال هدى أنكره
entries.english: May Man be killed! How ungrateful he is! Whenever he obtains guidance, he spurns it.

entries.farsi: نفس زين سان است ز آن شد كشتنى / ‏اقتلوا أنفسكم گفت آن سنى
entries.english: The carnal soul is of this sort, hence it ought to be killed: that Exalted One has said, “Kill yourselves.”

entries.farsi: خار سه سويه است هر چون كش نهى / ‏در خلد وز زخم او تو كى جهى
entries.english: It is a three sided thorn: however you may place it, it will pierce, and how will you escape from its stab?

entries.farsi: آتش ترك هوا در خار زن / ‏دست اندر يار نيكو كار زن
entries.english: Set the thorn on fire with renunciation of sensual passion, and cling to the righteous friend.

entries.farsi: چون ز حد بردند اصحاب سبا / كه به پيش ما وبا به از صبا
entries.english: When the people of Saba carried beyond bounds, saving, “In our opinion, pestilence is better than the zephyr,”

entries.farsi: ناصحانشان در نصيحت آمدند / از فسوق و كفر مانع مى‏شدند
entries.english: Their counsellors began to admonish and restrain from impiety and ingratitude;

entries.farsi: قصد خون ناصحان مى‏داشتند / تخم فسق و كافرى مى‏كاشتند
entries.english: They sought to take the lives of their counsellors, and sowed the seed of impiety and ingratitude.

entries.farsi: چون قضا آيد شود تنگ اين جهان / ‏از قضا حلوا شود رنج دهان
entries.english: When the decree comes to pass, this world becomes cramped; by the decree sweetmeat becomes anguish to the mouth.

entries.farsi: گفت إِذا جاء القضاء ضاق الفضا / تحجب األبصار إِذا جاء القضاء
entries.english: He said, “When the Decree comes, the expanse is narrow; when the Decree comes, the eyes are veiled.”

entries.farsi: مكر آن فارس چو انگيزيد گرد / آن غبارت ز استغاثت دور كرد
entries.english: The eye is bandaged at the time of the Decree, so that the eye does not see the eye’s salve.

entries.farsi: چشم بسته مى‏شود وقت قضا / تا نبيند چشم كحل چشم را
entries.english: When the cunning of that Horseman has raised the dust, the dust keeps you off from calling for aid.

entries.farsi: سوى فارس رو مرو سوى غبار / ور نه بر تو كوبد آن مكر سوار
entries.english: Go towards the Horseman, not towards the dust; else the cunning of the Rider will beat upon you.

entries.farsi: گفت حق آن را كه اين گرگش بخورد / ديد گرد گرگ چون زارى نكرد
entries.english: God said, “He whom this wolf devoured, he saw the wolf ’s dust: how did not he make piteous moan?”

entries.farsi: او نمى‏دانست گرد گرگ را / با چنين دانش چرا كرد او چرا
entries.english: Did not he know the wolf ’s dust? With such knowledge, why did he graze?

entries.farsi: گوسفندان بوى گرگ با گزند / مى‏بدانند و به هر سو مى‏خزند
entries.english: Sheep know the smell of the harmful wolf and creep away in every direction.

entries.farsi: مغز حيوانات بوى شير را / مى‏بداند ترك مى‏گويد چرا
entries.english: The brain of animals knows the smell of the lion and bids farewell to grazing.

entries.farsi: بوى شير خشم ديدى باز گرد / با مناجات و حذر انباز گرد
entries.english: You have smelt the lion of wrath. Turn back! Consort with prayer and dread!

entries.farsi: وانگشتند آن گروه از گرد گرگ / ‏گرگ محنت بعد گرد آمد سترگ
entries.english: That multitude did not turn back from the wolf ’s dust, and after the dust the wolf of tribulation came on in his might.

entries.farsi: بر دريد آن گوسفندان را به خشم / ‏كه ز چوپان خرد بستند چشم
entries.english: In wrath he tore to pieces those sheep which shut their eyes to the shepherd, Wisdom.

entries.farsi: چند چوپانشان بخواند و نامدند / خاك غم در چشم چوپان مى‏زدند
entries.english: How oft did the shepherd call them! And they came not: they were throwing the dust of resentment in the eyes of the shepherd,

entries.farsi: كه برو ما از تو خود چوپان‏تريم / ‏چون تبع گرديم هر يك سروريم
entries.english: Saving, “ Go: we ourselves are better shepherds than you. How should we become: followers? We are chieftains, every one.

entries.farsi: طعمه‏ى گرگيم و آن يار نه / ‏هيزم ناريم و آن عار نه
entries.english: We are food for the wolf, and we are not for the Friend; we are fuel for the Fire, and we are not for dishonour.”

entries.farsi: حميتى بد جاهليت در دماغ / ‏بانگ شومى بر دمنشان كرد زاغ
entries.english: A heathen pride was in their brains: the raven croaked disaster over the traces of their habitation.

entries.farsi: بهر مظلومان همى‏كندند چاه / ‏در چه افتادند و مى‏گفتند آه
entries.english: They were digging a pit for the oppressed: they fell into the pit, crying “Alas !”

entries.farsi: پوستين يوسفان بشكافتند / آن چه مى‏كردند يك يك يافتند
entries.english: They tore the coats of the Josephs, and that which they gave they got, piece by piece.

entries.farsi: كيست آن يوسف دل حق جوى تو / چون اسيرى بسته اندر كوى تو
entries.english: Who is that Joseph? Your God-seeking heart, bound as a captive in your abode.

entries.farsi: جبرئيلى را بر استن بسته‏اى / ‏پر و بالش را به صد جا خسته‏اى
entries.english: You have bound a Gabriel on a pillar; you have wounded his wings and plumes in a hundred places.

entries.farsi: پيش او گوساله بريان آورى / ‏كه كشى او را به كهدان آورى
entries.english: You set before him a roasted calf, you fetch straw and bring him to the straw-barn,

entries.farsi: كه بخور اين است ما را لوت و پوت / ‏نيست او را جز لقاء اللهَّ قوت
entries.english: Saying, “Eat; this is a dainty meal for us,” for him there is no food but meeting God face to face.

entries.farsi: زين شكنجه و امتحان آن مبتال / مى‏كند از تو شكايت با خدا
entries.english: On account of this torment and tribulation that afflicted is complaining of you to God,

entries.farsi: كاى خدا افغان از اين گرگ كهن / ‏گويدش نك وقت آمد صبر كن
entries.english: Crying, “O God, deliver from this old wolf!” He says to it, “Lo, the hour is come: have patience.

entries.farsi: داد تو واخواهم از هر بى‏خبر / داد كه دهد جز خداى دادگر
entries.english: I will demand justice for you from every heedless one: who gives justice but God, the Dealer of justice?”

entries.farsi: او همى‏گويد كه صبر شد فنا / در فراق روى تو يا ربنا
entries.english: It says, “My patience is lost in separation from Your face, O Lord.

entries.farsi: احمدم درمانده در دست يهود / صالحم افتاده در حبس ثمود
entries.english: I am Ahmad left forsaken in the hands of the Jews, I am Salih fallen into prison of Thamud.

entries.farsi: اى سعادت بخش جان انبيا / يا بكش يا باز خوانم يا بيا
entries.english: O You that bestow felicity on the souls of the prophets either slay me or call me back or come.

entries.farsi: با فراقت كافران را نيست تاب / ‏مى‏گود يا ليتني كنت تراب
entries.english: The infidels cannot endure to be separated from You: he is saying, ‘O would that I had been dust!’

entries.farsi: حال او اين است كو خود ز آن سو است / ‏چون بود بى‏تو كسى كان تو است
entries.english: This is the state of him who in sooth belongs to that side: how should one that belongs to You be without You?”

entries.farsi: حق همى‏گويد كه آرى اى نزه / ‏ليك بشنو صبر آر و صبر به
entries.english: God says, “Yes, O pure; but listen and have patience, for patience is better.

entries.farsi: صبح نزديك است خامش كم خروش / ‏من همى‏كوشم پى تو تو مكوش
entries.english: The dawn is near. Hush, do not wail! I am striving for you, do not you strive.”

sections.title_fa: بقيه‏ى داستان رفتن خواجه به دعوت روستايى سوى ده
sections.title_en: The rest of the Story of the Khwaja’s going to the village on the invitation of the countryman.

entries.farsi: شد ز حد هين باز گرد اى يار گرد / روستايى خواجه را بين خانه برد
entries.english: It has passed beyond bounds: return, O valiant friend. The countryman, mark you, took the Khwaja to his house.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى اهل سبا يك گوشه نه / ‏آن بگو كان خواجه چون آمد به ده
entries.english: Put aside the story of the people of Saba: tell how the Khwaja came to the village.

entries.farsi: روستايى در تملق شيوه كرد / تا كه حزم خواجه را كاليوه كرد
entries.english: The countryman used blandishments in ingratiating himself, until he made the Khwaja’s prudence crazy.

entries.farsi: از پيام اندر پيام او خيره شد / تا زالل حزم خواجه تيره شد
entries.english: He was distracted by message upon message, till the clear water of his prudence became turbid.

entries.farsi: هم از اينجا كودكانش در پسند / نرتع و نلعب به شادى مى‏زدند
entries.english: On the same side his children in approval were joyously striking up “Let us frolic and play,”

entries.farsi: همچو يوسف كش ز تقدير عجب / ‏نرتع و نلعب ببرد از ظل اب
entries.english: Like Joseph, whom by the wondrous predestination “Let us frolic and play” carried off from his father’s shadow.

entries.farsi: آن نه بازى بلكه جان‏بازى است آن / ‏حيله و مكر و دغاسازى است آن
entries.english: That is not play; nay, it is play with one’s life, it is cunning and deceit and contrivance of fraud.

entries.farsi: هر چه از يارت جدا اندازد آن / ‏مشنو آن را كان زيان دارد زيان
entries.english: Whatever would fling you asunder from the Friend, do not listen to it, for it holds loss, loss.
