entries.farsi: گر بود آن سود صد در صد مگير / بهر زر مگسل ز گنجور اى فقير
entries.english: If the gain be a hundred hundredfold, do not accept it: do not, for the sake of the gold, break with the dervish, who is the treasurer!

entries.farsi: اين شنو كه چند يزدان زجر كرد / گفت اصحاب نبى را گرم و سرد
entries.english: Hear how many a rebuke, hot and cold, God addressed to the Companions of the Prophet,

entries.farsi: ز انكه بر بانگ دهل در سال تنگ / ‏جمعه را كردند باطل بى‏درنگ
entries.english: Because, in a year of distress, at the sound of the drum they without tarrying made void the Friday congregation,

entries.farsi: تا نبايد ديگران ارزان خرند / ز آن جلب صرفه ز ما ايشان برند
entries.english: “Lest” “others should buy cheap and get the advantage over us in respect of those imported goods.”

entries.farsi: ماند پيغمبر به خلوت در نماز / با دو سه درويش ثابت پر نياز
entries.english: The Prophet was left alone in prayer with two or three poor men firm and full of supplication.

entries.farsi: گفت طبل و لهو و بازرگانيى / ‏چونتان ببريد از ربانيى
entries.english: He said, “ How did the drum and the pastime and a trading affair sunder you from a man of God ?

entries.farsi: بهر گندم تخم باطل كاشتيد / و آن رسول حق را بگذاشتيد
entries.english: You have dispersed madly towards the wheat, and left a Prophet standing.

entries.farsi: قد فضضتم نحو قمح هائما / ثم خليتم نبيا قائما
entries.english: On account of the wheat you sowed the seed of vanity and forsook that Messenger of God.

entries.farsi: صحبت او خير من لهو است و مال / ‏بين كه را بگذاشتى چشمى بمال
entries.english: Companionship with him is better than pastime and riches: see whom you have forsaken, rub an eye!

entries.farsi: خود نشد حرص شما را اين يقين / ‏كه منم رزاق و خير الرازقين
entries.english: Truly, to your greed did not this become certain, that I am the Provider and the best of them that provide?”

entries.farsi: آن كه گندم را ز خود روزى دهد / كى توكلهات را ضايع نهد
entries.english: He that gives sustenance from Himself unto the wheat, how should He let your acts of trust be wasted?

entries.farsi: از پى گندم جدا گشتى از آن / ‏كى فرستاده‏ست گندم ز آسمان
entries.english: For the sake of wheat you have become parted from Him who has sent the wheat from Heaven.

sections.title_fa: دعوت باز بطان را از آب به صحرا
sections.title_en: How the falcon invited the ducks to come from the water to the plain

entries.farsi: باز گويد بط را كز آب خيز / تا ببينى دشتها را قند ريز
entries.english: Says the falcon to the duck, “Come out of the water, that you may see the plains diffusing sweetness,”

entries.farsi: بط عاقل گويدش اى باز دور / آب ما را حصن و امن است و سرور
entries.english: The wise duck says to him, “Away, O falcon! The water is our stronghold and safety and joy.”

entries.farsi: ديو چون باز آمد اى بطان شتاب / ‏هين به بيرون كم رويد از حصن آب
entries.english: The Devil is like the falcon. O ducks make haste! Beware; do not come out of your stronghold, the water.

entries.farsi: باز را گويند رو رو باز گرد / از سر ما دست‏دار اى پاى مرد
entries.english: They say to the falcon, “Go, Go! Turn back and keep your hand off our heads, O kind friend!

entries.farsi: ما برى از دعوتت دعوت ترا / ما ننوشيم اين دم تو كافرا
entries.english: We do not accept your invitation: keep the invitation for yourself: we will not listen to these words of yours, O infidel!

entries.farsi: حصن ما را قند و قندستان ترا / من نخواهم هديه‏ات بستان ترا
entries.english: The stronghold is for us: let the sugar and sugar-fields be yours! I do not desire your gift: take it for yourself!

entries.farsi: چون كه جان باشد نيايد لوت كم / ‏چون كه لشكر هست كم نايد علم
entries.english: While there is life, food will not fail; when there is an army, banners will not fail.”

entries.farsi: خواجه‏ى حازم بسى عذر آوريد / بس بهانه كرد با ديو مريد
entries.english: The prudent Khwaja offered many an excuse and made many a pretext to the obstinate Devil.

entries.farsi: شاه كارى ناز كم فرموده است / ‏ز انتظارم شاه شب نغنوده است
entries.english: “At this moment,” said he, “ I have serious matters; if I come, they will not be set in order.

entries.farsi: گفت اين دم كارها دارم مهم / ‏گر بيايم آن نگردد منتظم
entries.english: The King has charged me with a delicate affair, and because of expecting me he has not slept during the night.

entries.farsi: من نيازم ترك امر شاه كرد / من نتانم شد بر شه روى زرد
entries.english: I dare not neglect the King’s command; I cannot fall into disgrace with the King.

entries.farsi: هر صباح و هر مسا سرهنگ خاص / مى‏رسد از من همى‏جويد مناص
entries.english: Every morning and evening a special officer arrives and requests of me a means of escape.

entries.farsi: تو روا دارى كه آيم سوى ده / ‏تا در ابرو افكند سلطان گره
entries.english: Do you deem it right that I should go into the country, with the result that the King would knit his brows?

entries.farsi: بعد از آن درمان خشمش چون كنم / ‏زنده خود را زين مگر مدفون كنم
entries.english: How should I heal his anger after that? Surely, by this I should bury myself alive.”

entries.farsi: زين نمط او صد بهانه باز گفت / ‏حيله‏ها با حكم حق نفتاد جفت
entries.english: He related a hundred pretexts of this sort; expedients did not coincide with God’s decree.

entries.farsi: گر شود ذرات عالم حيله پيچ / ‏با قضاى آسمان هيچند هيچ
entries.english: If the atoms of the world contrive expedients, they are nothing, nothing, against the ordinance of Heaven.

entries.farsi: چون گريزد اين زمين از آسمان / ‏چون كند او خويش را از وى نهان
entries.english: How shall this earth flee from Heaven, how shall it conceal itself from it?

entries.farsi: هر چه آيد ز آسمان سوى زمين / ‏نى مفر دارد نه چاره نى كمين
entries.english: Whatever may come from Heaven to the earth, it has no refuge or device or hiding-place.

entries.farsi: آتش از خورشيد مى‏بارد بر او / او به پيش آتشش بنهاده رو
entries.english: Is fire from the sun raining upon it, it has laid its face before his fire;

entries.farsi: ور همى طوفان كند باران بر او / شهرها را مى‏كند ويران بر او
entries.english: And if the rain is making a flood upon it and devastating the cities upon it,

entries.farsi: او شده تسليم او ايوب‏وار / كه اسيرم هر چه مى‏خواهى بيار
entries.english: It has become resigned to it, like Job, saying, “I am captive: bring whatever you will.”

entries.farsi: اى كه جزو اين زمينى سر مكش / ‏چون كه بينى حكم يزدان در مكش
entries.english: O you, who are a part of this earth, do not lift up your head; when you see the decree of God, do not withdraw.

entries.farsi: چون خَلَقْناكُمْ شنودى مِنْ تراب / ‏خاك باشى جست از تو، رو متاب
entries.english: Since you have heard “We created you of dust,” He has required you to be dust: do not avert your face.

entries.farsi: بين كه اندر خاك تخمى كاشتم / ‏گرد خاكى و منش افراشتم
entries.english: “Mark how I have sown a seed in the earth: you are dust of the earth, and I have raised it aloft.

entries.farsi: حمله‏ى ديگر تو خاكى پيشه گير / تا كنم بر جمله ميرانت امير
entries.english: Once more adopt the practice of earthiness, that I may make you prince over all princes.”

entries.farsi: آب از بالا به پستى در رود / آن گه از پستى به بالا بر رود
entries.english: Water goes from above to below; then from below it goes up above.

entries.farsi: گندم از بالا به زير خاك شد / بعد از آن او خوشه و چالاك شد
entries.english: The wheat went beneath the earth from above; afterwards it became ears of corn and sprang up quickly.

entries.farsi: دانه‏ى هر ميوه آمد در زمين / ‏بعد از آن سرها بر آورد از دفين
entries.english: The seed of every fruit entered into the earth; afterwards it raised up heads from the buried.

entries.farsi: اصل نعمتها ز گردون تا به خاك / ‏زير آمد شد غذاى جان پاك
entries.english: The source of blessings descended from Heaven to the earth and became the nutriment of the pure spirit.

entries.farsi: از تواضع چون ز گردون شد به زير / گشت جزو آدمى حى دلير
entries.english: Forasmuch as it came down from Heaven on account of humility, it became part of the living and valiant man

entries.farsi: پس صفات آدمى شد آن جماد / بر فراز عرش پران گشت شاد
entries.english: Hence that inanimate matter was turned into human qualities and soared joyously above the empyrean,

entries.farsi: كز جهان زنده ز اول آمديم / ‏باز از پستى سوى بالا شديم
entries.english: Saying, “We came at first from the living world, and have gone back from below to above “

entries.farsi: جمله اجزا در تحرك در سكون / ‏ناطقان كإنا إليه راجعون
entries.english: All particles, in movement at rest, are speakers: “Truly, to Him we are returning.”

entries.farsi: ذكر و تسبيحات اجزاى نهان / ‏غلغلى افكند اندر آسمان
entries.english: The praises and glorifications of the hidden particles have filled Heaven with an uproar.

entries.farsi: چون قضا آهنگ نيرنجات كرد / روستايى شهريى را مات كرد
entries.english: When the Decree set out to enchantments, the countryman checkmated a townsman.

entries.farsi: با هزاران حزم خواجه مات شد / ز آن سفر در معرض آفات شد
entries.english: Notwithstanding thousands of resolutions, the Khwaja was checkmated, and from that journey he fell into the midst of calamities.

entries.farsi: اعتمادش بر ثبات خويش بود / گر چه كه بد نيم سيلش در ربود
entries.english: His reliance was upon his own firmness, though he was a mountain, a half-flood swept him away.

entries.farsi: چون قضا بيرون كند از چرخ سر / عاقالن گردند جمله كور و كر
entries.english: When the Decree puts forth its head from Heaven, all the intelligent become blind and deaf;

entries.farsi: ماهيان افتند از دريا برون / ‏دام گيرد مرغ پران را زبون
entries.english: Fishes are cast out of the sea; the snare catches miserably the flying bird.

entries.farsi: تا پرى و ديو درشيشه شود / بلكه هاروتى به بابل در رود
entries.english: Even genie and demon go into the bottle; nay, a Harut goes into Babylon.

entries.farsi: جز كسى كاندر قضاى حق گريخت / ‏خون او را هيچ تربيعى نريخت
entries.english: Except that one who has taken refuge with the Decree: his blood no astrological square shed.

entries.farsi: غير آن كه در گريزى در قضا / هيچ حيله ندهدت از وى رها
entries.english: Except that you take refuge with the Decree, no contrivance will give you release from it.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى اهل ضروان و حيلت كردن ايشان تا بى‏زحمت درويشان باغها را قطاف كنند
sections.title_en: The Story of the people of Zarwan and how they contrived that they should pick the fruit in their orchards without being troubled by the poor.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى اصحاب ضروان خوانده‏اى / ‏پس چرا در حيله جويى مانده‏اى
entries.english: You have read the story of the people of Zarwan: then why have you persisted in seeking expedients?

entries.farsi: حيله مى‏كردند كژدم نيش چند / كه برند از روزى درويش چند
entries.english: Several men who stung like scorpions were contriving that they might cut off part of the daily bread of some poor folk.

entries.farsi: شب همه شب مى‏سگاليدند مكر / روى در رو كرده چندين عمرو و بكر
entries.english: During the night, the whole night, they were devising a plot; many an Amr and Bakr had put their faces together.

entries.farsi: با گل انداينده اسگاليده گل / ‏دست كارى مى‏كند پنهان ز دل
entries.english: Those wicked men were speaking their inmost thoughts in secret, lest God should discover it.

entries.farsi: خفيه مى‏گفتند سرها آن بدان / ‏تا نبايد كه خدا در يابد آن
entries.english: Did the clay devise against the Plasterer? Is the hand doing any work that is hidden from the heart?

entries.farsi: گفت أ ال يعلم هواك من خلق / ‏إن في نجواك صدقا أم ملق
entries.english: He has said, “Does not He who created know your desire, whether in your secret conversation there is sincerity or cajolery?”

entries.farsi: كيف يغفل عن ظعين قد غدا / من يعاين اين مثواه غدا
entries.english: How should a traveller who has set out at morn be unheeded by One who sees plainly where shall be his lodging tomorrow?

entries.farsi: أينما قد هبطا أو صعدا / قد تواله و أحصى عددا
entries.english: Wherever he has descended or mounted, He has taken charge of it and reckoned up by number.

entries.farsi: گوش را اكنون ز غفلت پاك كن / ‏استماع هجر آن غمناك كن
entries.english: Now purge your ear of forgetfulness and listen to the separation of the sorrowful one.

entries.farsi: آن زكاتى دان كه غمگين را دهى / گوش را چون پيش دستانش نهى
entries.english: Know that when you set your ear to his tale, that is the alms which you give to the sad;

entries.farsi: بشنوى غمهاى رنجوران دل / ‏فاقه‏ى جان شريف از آب و گل
entries.english: You will hear the sorrows of the heart-sick— the starvation of the noble spirit by the water and clay.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى پر دود دارد پر فنى / ‏مر و را بگشا ز اصغا روزنى
entries.english: One filled with knowledge, it has a house filled with smoke: open a window for it by listening.

entries.farsi: گوش تو او را چو راه دم شود / دود تلخ از خانه‏ى او كم شود
entries.english: When your ear becomes a way of breath for it, the bitter smoke will decrease from its house.

entries.farsi: غم گسارى كن تو با ما اى روى / ‏گر به سوى رب اعلى مى‏روى
entries.english: Show sympathy with us, O well-watered one, if You are faring towards the most high Lord.

entries.farsi: اين تردد حبس و زندانى بود / كه بنگذارد كه جان سويى رود
entries.english: This vacillation is a prison and jail that will not let the soul go in any direction.

entries.farsi: اين بدين سو آن بدان سو مى‏كشد / هر يكى گويا منم راه رشد
entries.english: This draws in one direction, and that in another, each saying, “I am the right way.”

entries.farsi: اين تردد عقبه‏ى راه حق است / ‏اى خنك آن را كه پايش مطلق است
entries.english: This vacillation is a precipice on the Way to God: oh, blest is he whose feet are loosed.

entries.farsi: بى‏تردد مى‏رود در راه راست / ‏ره نمى‏دانى بجو گامش كجاست
entries.english: He fares on the right way without vacillation: you do not know the way, seek where his footprints are.

entries.farsi: گام آهو را بگير و رو معاف / ‏تا رسى از گام آهو تا به ناف
entries.english: Cleave to the footprints of the deer and advance safely, that from the deer’s footprints you may attain to the musk-gland.

entries.farsi: زين روش بر اوج انور مى‏روى / ‏اى برادر گر بر آذر مى‏روى
entries.english: By means of this wayfaring you will ascend to the most luminous zenith, O brother, if you will walk on the fire

entries.farsi: نى ز دريا ترس و نى از موج و كف / ‏چون شنيدى تو خطاب ال تخف
entries.english: No fear of sea or waves or foam, since you have heard the allocution, “Be not afraid.”

entries.farsi: ال تَخَفْ دان چون كه خوفت داد حق / ‏نان فرستد چون فرستادت طبق
entries.english: Know that it is Be not afraid, when God has given you the fear: He will send the bread, since He has sent the tray to you.

entries.farsi: خوف آن كس راست كاو را خوف نيست / ‏غصه‏ى آن كس را كش اينجا طوف نيست
entries.english: The fear is for that one who has no fear; the anguish for that one who does not frequent this place.

sections.title_fa: ‏روان شدن خواجه به سوى ده
sections.title_en: The Khwaja’s departure to the country

entries.farsi: خواجه در كار آمد و تجهيز ساخت / ‏مرغ عزمش سوى ده اشتاب تاخت
entries.english: The Khwaja got to work and made preparations: the bird, his resolve, sped rapidly towards the country.

entries.farsi: اهل و فرزندان سفر را ساختند / رخت را بر گاو عزم انداختند
entries.english: His kinsfolk and children made ready for the journey and threw the baggage upon the ox of departure,

entries.farsi: شادمانان و شتابان سوى ده / ‏كه برى خورديم از ده مژده ده
entries.english: Rejoicing and hastening towards the country, saying, “We have eaten some fruit: give the glad news of the country!

entries.farsi: مقصد ما را چراگاه خوش است / ‏يار ما آن جا كريم و دل كش است
entries.english: The place for which we are bound is a sweet pasturage, and our friend there is kind and charming.

entries.farsi: با هزاران آرزومان خوانده است / ‏بهر ما غرس كرم بنشانده است
entries.english: He has invited us with thousands of wishes; he has planted for us the shoot of kindness.

entries.farsi: ما ذخيره‏ى ده زمستان دراز / از بر او سوى شهر آريم باز
entries.english: From him we shall bring back to town the store of the countryside during the long winter.

entries.farsi: بلكه باغ ايثار راه ما كند / در ميان جان خودمان جا كند
entries.english: Nay, he will give up the orchard for our sake; he will make a place for us in the middle of his soul.

entries.farsi: عجلوا أصحابنا كي تربحوا / عقل مى‏گفت از درون ال تفرحوا
entries.english: Hasten, friends, that you may get gain!” Reason from within was saying, “Do not rejoice! ”

entries.farsi: من رباح اللهَّ كونوا رابحين / ‏إن ربي ال يُحِبُّ الفرحين
entries.english: Be gainers by the gain of God: lo, my Lord loves not them that rejoice.

entries.farsi: افرحوا هونا بما آتاكم / ‏كل آت مشغل ألهاكم
entries.english: Rejoice moderately on account of what He causes to come to you: everything that comes and is a source of preoccupation diverts you

entries.farsi: شاد از وى شو مشو از غير وى / ‏او بهار است و دگرها ماه دى
entries.english: Rejoice in Him, do not rejoice in anything except Him: He is the spring, and other things the month of December.

entries.farsi: هر چه غير اوست استدراج تست / ‏گر چه تخت و ملك تست و تاج تست
entries.english: Everything other than He is the leading you gradually to perdition, though it is your throne and kingdom and your crown.

entries.farsi: شاد از غم شو كه غم دام لقاست / ‏اندر اين ره سوى پستى ارتقاست
entries.english: Rejoice in sorrow, for sorrow is the snare of union: in this Way the ascent is downwards.

entries.farsi: غم يكى گنج است و رنج تو چو كان / ‏ليك كى درگيرد اين در كودكان
entries.english: Sorrow is a treasure, and your pain is as the mine, but how should this catch hold of children?

entries.farsi: كودكان چون نام بازى بشنوند / جمله با خر گور هم تگ مى‏دوند
entries.english: When children hear the name of “play,” they all run with the speed of a wild ass.

entries.farsi: اى خران كور اين سو دامهاست / در كمين اين سوى خون آشامهاست
entries.english: O blind asses, in this direction there are snares; in this direction there are bloodsheds in ambush.

entries.farsi: تيرها پران كمان پنهان ز غيب / ‏بر جوانى مى‏رسد صد تير شيب
entries.english: The arrows are flying, the bow is hidden: from the Unseen World come upon youth a hundred arrows of old one.

entries.farsi: گام در صحراى دل بايد نهاد / ز انكه در صحراى گل نبود گشاد
entries.english: You must set foot on the plain of the heart, because in the plain of clay there is no opening.

entries.farsi: ايمن آباد است دل اى دوستان / ‏چشمه‏ها و گلستان در گلستان
entries.english: The heart is the abode of security, O friends; f ountains and rose-gardens within rose-gardens.

entries.farsi: عج إلى القلب و سر يا ساريه / ‏فيه أشجار و عين جاريه
entries.english: Turn towards the heart and journey on, O night-travellers: therein are trees and a flowing spring.

entries.farsi: ده مرو ده مرد را احمق كند / عقل را بى‏نور و بى‏رونق كند
entries.english: Do not go to the country: the country makes a fool of a man; it makes the intellect void of light and splendour.

entries.farsi: قول پيغمبر شنو اى مجتبى / گور عقل آمد وطن در روستا
entries.english: O chosen one, listen to the Prophet’s saying: “To dwell in the country is the grave of the intellect.”

entries.farsi: هر كه در رستا بود روزى و شام / ‏تا به ماهى عقل او نبود تمام
entries.english: If anyone stays in the country a single day and evening, his intellect will not be fully restored for a month.

entries.farsi: تا به ماهى احمقى با او بود / از حشيش ده جز اينها چه درود
entries.english: For a month foolishness will abide with him: what but these things should he reap from the parched herbage of the country?

entries.farsi: و انكه ماهى باشد اندر روستا / روزگارى باشدش جهل و عما
entries.english: And he that stays a month in the country, ignorance and blindness will be his for a long time.

entries.farsi: ده چه باشد شيخ واصل ناشده / ‏دست در تقليد و حجت در زده
entries.english: What is “the country”: The Shaykh that has not been united, but has become addicted to conventionality and argument.

entries.farsi: پيش شهر عقل كلى اين حواس / ‏چون خران چشم بسته در خراس
entries.english: Compared with the town, Universal Reason, these senses are like asses in an ass-mill with their eyes bandaged.

entries.farsi: اين رها كن صورت افسانه گير / هل تو دردانه تو گندم دانه گير
entries.english: Leave this and take the outward form of the tale: let the pearlseed alone and take the wheatseed.

entries.farsi: گر به در ره نيست هين بر مى‏ستان / ‏گر بدان ره نيستت اين سو بران
entries.english: If there is no way to the pearl, come, take the wheat; if there is no way for you in that, push on in this direction.

entries.farsi: اول هر آدمى خود صورت است / ‏بعد از آن جان كاو جمال سيرت است
entries.english: Take its outward! Though the outward fly crookedly, the outward at last will lead to the inward.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش گير ار چه ظاهر كج بود / عاقبت ظاهر سوى باطن رود
entries.english: In truth, the first of every human being is the form; after that the spirit, which is beauty of disposition.

entries.farsi: اول هر ميوه جز صورت كى است / ‏بعد از آن لذت كه معناى وى است
entries.english: How is the first of every fruit anything but the form? After that the delicious taste which is its real meaning.

entries.farsi: اوال خرگاه سازند و خرند / ترك را ز آن پس به مهمان آورند
entries.english: First they make or buy a tent; afterwards they bring the Turcoman as a guest.

entries.farsi: صورتت خرگاه دان معنيت ترك / ‏معنيت مالح دان صورت چو فلك
entries.english: Deem your form to be the tent, your real essence the Turcoman; regard your essence as the sailor, your form as the ship.

entries.farsi: بهر حق اين را رها كن يك نفس / خواجه و بچگان جهازى ساختند
entries.english: For God’s sake, quit this for a moment, so that the Khwaja’s ass may shake its bell.

sections.title_en: How the Khwaja and his family went to the country.

entries.farsi: بر ستوران جانب ده تاختند / شادمانه سوى صحرا راندند
entries.english: The Khwaja and his children prepared an outfit and galloped on their beasts towards the country.

entries.farsi: بى‏سفرها ماه كى خسرو شود / از سفر بيدق شود فرزين راد
entries.english: Merrily they rode afield; they chanted, “Travel, that you may gain advantage”;

entries.farsi: سافروا كى تغنموا بر خواندند / كز سفرها ماه كيخسرو شود
entries.english: For by travelling the moon becomes Kay Khusraw: how should it become an emperor (khusraw) without travelling?

entries.farsi: وز سفر يابيد يوسف صد مراد / روز روى از آفتابى سوختند
entries.english: Through travel the pawn becomes a noble queen, and through travel Joseph gained a hundred objects of desire.

entries.farsi: شب ز اختر راه مى‏آموختند / خوب گشته پيش ايشان راه زشت
entries.english: By day they scorched their faces in the sun, by night they were learning the way from the stars.

entries.farsi: ‏از نشاط ده شده ره چون بهشت / تلخ از شيرين لبان خوش مى‏شود
entries.english: The bad road to them seemed good: from delight in the country the road seemed like Paradise.

entries.farsi: خار از گلزار دل كش مى‏شود / حنظل از معشوق خرما مى‏شود
entries.english: From sweet-lipped ones bitterness becomes sweet; from the rose-garden thorns become charming.

entries.farsi: خانه از هم خانه صحرا مى‏شود / اى بسا از نازنينان خار كش
entries.english: Colocynth turns into dates from the beloved; the house is made spacious fields by the housemate.

entries.farsi: ‏بر اميد گل‏عذار ماه‏وش / اى بسا حمال گشته پشت ريش
entries.english: Oh, many a dainty youth that suffers thorns in the hope of a rose-cheeked moon-like.

entries.farsi: ‏از براى دل بر مه روى خويش / كرده آهنگر جمال خود سياه
entries.english: Oh, many a porter, his back torn with wounds for the sake of the moon-faced one to whom he has lost his heart.

entries.farsi: ‏تا كه شب آيد ببوسد روى ماه / خواجه تا شب بر دكانى چار ميخ
entries.english: The ironsmith has blackened his beauty, that night comes he may kiss the face of the moon.

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه سروى در دلش كردست بيخ / تاجرى دريا و خشكى مى‏رود
entries.english: The merchant, racked, on a bench till nightfall, because a cypress has taken root in his heart

entries.farsi: آن به مهر خانه‏شينى مى‏دود / هر كه را با مرده سودايى بود
entries.english: A trader is faring over sea and land: he runs for love of one who sits at home.

entries.farsi: بر اميد زنده سيمايى بود / آن دروگر روى آورده به چوب
entries.english: Whoever has a passion for that which is dead; it is in hope of one who has the features of the living.

entries.farsi: ‏بر اميد خدمت مه روى خوب / بر اميد زنده‏اى كن اجتهاد
entries.english: The carpenter turns his face to wood, in the hope of rendering service to a fair one whose face is like the moon.

entries.farsi: كاو نگردد بعد روزى دو جماد / مونسى مگزين خسى را از خسى
entries.english: Do you exert yourself in hope of the Living One who does not become lifeless after a day or two!

entries.farsi: ‏عاريت باشد در او آن مونسى / انس تو با مادر و بابا كجاست
entries.english: Do not from meanness choose a mean person as your friend: that friendship in him is borrowed.

entries.farsi: ‏گر بجز حق مونسانت را وفاست / انس تو با دايه و الال چه شد
entries.english: If your friends other than God possess constancy, where is your friendship with your mother and father?

entries.farsi: گر كسى شايد به غير حق عضد / انس تو با شير و با پستان نماند
entries.english: If anyone but God is worthy to be relied upon, what has become of your friendship with your nurse and tutor?

entries.farsi: نفرت تو از دبيرستان نماند / آن شعاعى بود بر ديوارشان
entries.english: Your friendship with the milk and the breast did not endure; your shyness of school did not endure.

entries.farsi: ‏جانب خورشيد وارفت آن نشان / بر هر آن چيزى كه افتد آن شعاع
entries.english: That was radiance upon their wall: that sign went back towards the Sun.

entries.farsi: ‏تو بر آن هم عاشق آيى اى شجاع / عشق تو بر هر چه آن موجود بود
entries.english: On whatever thing that radiance may fall, you become in love with that, O brave man.

entries.farsi: آن ز وصف حق زر اندود بود / چون زرى با اصل رفت و مس بماند
entries.english: On whatever existent thing your love, that is gilded with Divine qualities.

entries.farsi: طبع سير آمد طالق او براند / از زر اندود صفاتش پا بكش
entries.english: When the goldenness has gone to its original source and the copper remains, nature is surfeited and proceeds to divorce it.

entries.farsi: ‏از جهالت قلب را كم گوى خوش / كان خوشى در قلبها عاريتى است
entries.english: Withdraw your foot from that which is gilded by His qualities, do not from ignorance call the base alloy beautiful;

entries.farsi: ‏زير زينت مايه‏ى بى‏زينتى است / زر ز روى قلب در كان مى‏رود
entries.english: For in base coin the beauty is borrowed: beneath the comeliness is the substance uncomely.

entries.farsi: سوى آن كان رو تو هم كان مى‏رود / نور از ديوار تا خور مى‏رود
entries.english: The gold is going from the face of the false coin into the mine: do you too go towards the Mine to which it is going.

entries.farsi: تو بدان خور رو كه در خور مى‏رود / زين سپس بستان تو آب از آسمان
entries.english: The light is going from the wall up to the sun: do you go to that Sun which ever goes in proportion.

entries.farsi: ‏چون نديدى تو وفا در ناودان / معدن دنبه نباشد دام گرگ
entries.english: Henceforth take the water from Heaven, forasmuch as you have not found faithfulness in the aqueduct.

entries.farsi: ‏كى شناسد معدن آن گرگ سترگ / زر گمان بردند بسته در گره
entries.english: The lure to catch the wolf is not the place where the sheep’s tail came from: how should that fierce wolf know the place of provenance?

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏شتابيدند مغروران به ده / همچنين خندان و رقصان مى‏شدند
entries.english: They imagined gold tied in knots; the deluded were making haste to the countryside.

entries.farsi: سوى آن دوالب چرخى مى‏زدند / چون همى‏ديدند مرغى مى‏پريد
entries.english: Thus were they going along, laughing and dancing and meandering towards the water-wheel.

entries.farsi: جانب ده صبر جامه مى‏دريد / هر كه مى‏آمد ز ده از سوى او
entries.english: Whenever they saw a bird flying in the direction of the country, patience rent its garments;

entries.farsi: بوسه مى‏دادند خوش بر روى او / كه تو روى يار ما را ديده‏اى
entries.english: They would kiss joyfully the face of anyone who came from the country, from his neighbourhood,

entries.farsi: ‏پس تو جان را جان و ما را ديده‏اى / همچو مجنون كاو سگى را مى‏نواخت
entries.english: Saying, “You have seen the face of our friend, therefore to the Soul you are the soul, and to us the eye.”

sections.title_fa: نواختن مجنون آن سگ را كه مقيم كوى ليلى بود
sections.title_en: How Majnun petted the dog that lived in Layla’s abode.

entries.farsi: ‏بوسه‏اش مى‏داد و پيشش مى‏گداخت / گرد او مى‏گشت خاضع در طواف
entries.english: Like Majnun, who was petting a dog and kissing it and melting before it:

entries.farsi: ‏هم جالب شكرش مى‏داد صاف / بو الفضولى گفت اى مجنون خام
entries.english: He was pacing round it, stooping humbly in circumambulation; he was also giving it pure sugar-julep.

entries.farsi: ‏اين چه شيد است اين كه مى‏آرى مدام / پوز سگ دايم پليدى مى‏خورد
entries.english: An idle talker said, “O half-baked Majnun, what hypocrisy is this that you are always displaying?

entries.farsi: مقعد خود را به لب مى‏استرد / عيبهاى سگ بسى او بر شمرد
entries.english: A dog’s muzzle is ever eating filth; a dog scrapes its ass with its lips.”

entries.farsi: عيب دان از غيب دان بويى نبرد / گفت مجنون تو همه نقشى و تن
entries.english: He recounted the dog’s faults at some length: no one who perceives faults (‘aybdan) has got a scent of him that knows the things unseen (ghaybdan).

entries.farsi: ‏اندر آ و بنگرش از چشم من / كاين طلسم بسته مولى است اين
entries.english: Majnun said, “You are entirely form and body: come within, and view it through my eyes;

entries.farsi: پاسبان كوچه‏ى ليلى است اين / همتش بين و دل و جان و شناخت
entries.english: For this is a talisman sealed by the Lord: this is the guardian of the abode of Layla.

entries.farsi: ‏كاو كجا بگزيد و مسكن گاه ساخت / او سگ فرخ رخ كهف من است
entries.english: Look at its high aspiration and its heart and soul and knowledge; where it chose and made its dwelling-place.

entries.farsi: من به شيران كى دهم يك موى او / اى كه شيران مر سگانش را غالم
entries.english: It is the dog of blessed countenance, of my Cave; no, it is the sharer of my grief and woe.

entries.farsi: ‏بلكه او هم درد و هم لهف من است / آن سگى كه باشد اندر كوى او
entries.english: The dog that stays in her abode, how should I give a single hair of it to the lions?

entries.farsi: ‏گفت امكان نيست خامش و السلام / گر ز صورت بگذريد اى دوستان
entries.english: Oh, since to her dogs the lions are (devoted) slaves, there is no possibility of speaking. Silence, and farewell!”

entries.farsi: ‏جنت است و گلستان در گلستان / صورت خود چون شكستى سوختى
entries.english: If you pass beyond form, O friends, it is Paradise and rose-gardens within rose-gardens.

entries.farsi: ‏صورت كل را شكست آموختى / بعد از آن هر صورتى را بشكنى
entries.english: When you have broken and destroyed your own form, you have learned to break every form.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو حيدر باب خيبر بر كنى / سغبه‏ى صورت شد آن خواجه‏ى سليم
entries.english: After that, you will break every form: like Haydar, you will uproot the gate of Khaybar.

entries.farsi: ‏كه به ده مى‏شد به گفتارى سقيم / سوى دام آن تملق شادمان
entries.english: That simple Khwaja was duped by form, for he was going to the country on infirm words.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو مرغى سوى دانه‏ى امتحان / از كرم دانست مرغ آن دانه را
entries.english: Joyously towards the snare of that flattery, as a bird towards the bait of tribulation

entries.farsi: غايت حرص است نى جود آن عطا / مرغكان در طمع دانه شادمان
entries.english: The bird deemed the bait a mark of kindness, that gift is the extreme of cupidity and is not munificence;

entries.farsi: ‏سوى آن تزوير پران و دوان / گر ز شادى خواجه آگاهت كنم
entries.english: In desire for the bait the little birds are merrily flying and running towards that imposture.

entries.farsi: ‏ترسم اى رهرو كه بى‏گاهت كنم / مختصر كردم چو آمد ده پديد
entries.english: If I acquaint you with the joy of the Khwaja, I fear, O wayfarer, lest I make you late.

entries.farsi: خود نبود آن ده ره ديگر گزيد / قرب ماهى ده به ده مى‏تاختند
entries.english: I will abridge. When the village came in sight, it was not in truth that village, he chose another road.

entries.farsi: ز انكه راه ده نكو نشناختند / هر كه در ره بى‏قالووزى رود
entries.english: For about a month they were hurrying from village to village, because they did not well know the way to the village.

entries.farsi: هر دو روزه راه صد ساله شود / هر كه تازد سوى كعبه بى‏دليل
entries.english: If anybody goes on the way without a leader, every two days’ journey becomes one of a hundred years.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو اين سر گشتگان گردد ذليل / هر كه گيرد پيشه‏ى بى‏اوستا
entries.english: Whoever speeds towards the Ka`ba without a guide becomes contemptible, like these bewildered men.

entries.farsi: ريش‏خندى شد به شهر و روستا / جز كه نادر باشد اندر خافقين
entries.english: Whoever takes up a trade without a teacher becomes a laughing-stock in town and country.

entries.farsi: ‏آدمى سر بر زند بى‏والدين / مال او يابد كه كسبى مى‏كند
entries.english: Except it be singular, between East and West does a descendant of Adam put forth his head without parents?

entries.farsi: نادرى باشد كه بر گنجى زند / مصطفايى كو كه جسمش جان بود
entries.english: He gains wealth who earns something; it is an extraordinary event when one hits upon a treasure.

entries.farsi: تا كه رحمن علم القرآن بود / اهل تن را جمله عَلَّمَ بالقلم
entries.english: Where is a Mustafa? Whose body is spirit, so that the Merciful should teach the Qur’an?

entries.farsi: ‏واسطه افراشت در بذل كرم / هر حريصى هست محروم اى پسر
entries.english: For all those who are attached to the body He, in profusion of bounty, raised “He taught by the pen” as the means.

entries.farsi: چون حريصان تك مرو آهسته‏تر / اندر آن ره رنجها ديدند و تاب
entries.english: O son, every greedy person is deprived: do not you run like the greedy, more slowly.

entries.farsi: ‏چون عذاب مرغ خاكى در عذاب / سير گشته از ده و از روستا
entries.english: On that journey they suffered pains and anguish like the torment of a land-bird in fresh water.

entries.farsi: وز شكر ريز چنان نااوستا / بعد ماهى چون رسيدند آن طرف
entries.english: They became sick of the village and the country and of the sugared expressions of such an uninstructed boor.

sections.title_fa: رسيدن خواجه و قومش به ده و ناديده و ناشناخته آوردن روستايى ايشان را
sections.title_en: How the Khwaja and his kinsfolk arrived at the village, and how the countryman pretended not to see or recognise them.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏نوا ايشان ستوران بى‏علف / روستايى بين كه از بد نيتى
entries.english: When, after a month, they arrived in that quarter, themselves without provisions and their beasts without fodder,

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏كند بعد اللتيا و التي / روى پنهان مى‏كند ز ايشان به روز
entries.english: See how the countryman, from evil intent, still inflict calamities small and great,

entries.farsi: تا سوى باغش بنگشايند پوز / آن چنان رو كه همه زرق و شر است
entries.english: And keeps his face hidden from them by day, lest they should open their mouths in the direction of his orchard

entries.farsi: ‏از مسلمانان نهان اوليتر است / رويها باشد كه ديوان چون مگس
entries.english: It is better that a face like that, which is wholly hypocrisy and malice, should he hidden from Moslems.

entries.farsi: ‏بر سرش بنشسته باشد چون حرس / چون ببينى روى او در توفتند
entries.english: There are faces on which demons are settled like gnats, as guardsmen.

entries.farsi: يا مبين آن رو چو ديدى خوش مخند / در چنان روى خبيث عاصيه
entries.english: When you behold his face, they fall upon you: Either do not behold that face, when you have beheld, do not laugh pleasantly.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت يزدان نسفعا بالناصيه / چون بپرسيدند و خانه‏ش يافتند
entries.english: Concerning such a wicked, sinful face God has said, “Verily, We will drag by the forelock.”

entries.farsi: همچو خويشان سوى در بشتافتند / در فرو بستند اهل خانه‏اش
entries.english: When they had made enquiry and found his house, they hurried like kinsfolk to the door.

entries.farsi: ‏خواجه شد زين كژ روى ديوانه‏وش / ليك هنگام درشتى هم نبود
entries.english: The people in his house bolted the door. At this perverseness, the Khwaja became mad-like,

entries.farsi: چون در افتادى به چه تيزى چه سود / بر درش ماندند ايشان پنج روز
entries.english: But indeed it was no time for harshness: when you have fallen into the pit, what is the use of being enraged?

entries.farsi: شب به سرما روز خود خورشيد سوز / نى ز غفلت بود ماندن نى خرى
entries.english: Five days they remained at his door: the night in the cold, the day itself in the blaze of the sun.

entries.farsi: ‏بلكه بود از اضطرار و بى‏خورى / با لئيمان بسته نيكان ز اضطرار
entries.english: Their remaining was not from heedlessness or asininity; no, it was from necessity and want of an ass.

entries.farsi: شير مردارى خورد از جوع زار / او همى‏ديدش همى‏كردش سالم
entries.english: From necessity, the good are bound to the vile: from sore hunger the lion will eat a putrid carcass.

entries.farsi: ‏كه فالنم من مرا اين است نام / گفت باشد من چه دانم تو كى‏اى
entries.english: He would see him and salute him, saving, “I am so-and-so, and this is my name.”

entries.farsi: ‏يا پليدى يا قرين پاكى‏اى / گفت اين دم با قيامت شد شبيه
entries.english: “Maybe,” he said; “ how should I know who you are, whether you are a dirty fellow or an honest gentleman?”

entries.farsi: ‏تا برادر شد يفر من اخيه / شرح مى‏كردش كه من آنم كه تو
entries.english: “This moment,” said he, “resembles the Resurrection, since a brother has come to flee from his brother.”

entries.farsi: لوتها خوردى ز خوان من دو تو / آن فالن روزت خريدم آن متاع
entries.english: He would explain to him, saying, “I am he from whose table you did eat many delicacies.

entries.farsi: ‏كل سر جاوز الاثنين شاع / سر مهر ما شنيدستند خلق
entries.english: On such and such a day I bought that merchandise for you: every secret that goes beyond the two is published.

entries.farsi: ‏شرم دارد رو چو نعمت خورد حلق / او همى‏گفتش چه گويى ترهات
entries.english: The people heard the secret of our affection; when the gullet has received bounty, the face has bashfulness.”

entries.farsi: ‏نى ترا دانم نه نام تو نه جات / پنجمين شب ابر و بارانى گرفت
entries.english: He would say to him, “do you talk nonsense? I know neither you nor your name nor your dwelling-place.”

entries.farsi: ‏كاسمان از بارشش دارد شگفت / چون رسيد آن كارد اندر استخوان
entries.english: On the fifth night there began such a cloud and rain that the sky might be astonished at its raining.

entries.farsi: حلقه زد خواجه كه مهتر را بخوان / چون به صد الحاح آمد سوى در
entries.english: When the knife reached the bone, the Khwaja knocked at the door, crying, “Call the master!”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر چيست اى جان پدر / گفت من آن حق‏ها بگذاشتم
entries.english: When, in response to a hundred urgent entreaties, he came to the door, he said, “Why, what is it, my dear sir?”

entries.farsi: جان مسكينم در اين گرما و سوز / يك جفا از خويش و از يار و تبار
entries.english: He replied, “ I abandon those claims, I renounce that which I was fancying.

entries.farsi: ‏ترك كردم آن چه مى‏پنداشتم / پنج ساله رنج ديدم پنج روز
entries.english: I have suffered five years’ pain: five days my miserable soul amidst this heat and blaze.”

entries.farsi: در گرانى هست چون سيصد هزار / ز انكه دل ننهاد بر جور و جفاش
entries.english: One injustice from kindred and friends and family is in heaviness as three hundred thousand,

entries.farsi: ‏جانش خوگر بود با لطف و وفاش / هر چه بر مردم بال و شدت است
entries.english: Because he did not set his mind on his cruelty and injustice: his soul was accustomed to kindness and faithfulness from him.

entries.farsi: ‏اين يقين دان كز خالف عادت است / گفت اى خورشيد مهرت در زوال
entries.english: Whatever is tribulation and sore grief to men, know for sure that this is in consequence of its being contrary to habit.

entries.farsi: ‏گر تو خونم ريختى كردم حالل / امشب باران به ما ده گوشه‏اى
entries.english: He said, “O you the sun of whose love is in decline, if you have shed my blood, I acquit you.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بيابى در قيامت توشه‏اى / گفت يك گوشه است آن باغبان
entries.english: On this night of rain give us a nook, so that at the Resurrection you may obtain a heavenly reward.”

entries.farsi: ‏هست اينجا گرگ را او پاسبان / در كفش تير و كمان از بهر گرگ
entries.english: “‘There is a nook,” he replied, “ belonging to the keeper of the vineyard: he keeps watch there against the wolf,

entries.farsi: ‏تا زند گر آيد آن گرگ سترگ / گر تو آن خدمت كنى جا آن تست
entries.english: Bow and arrow in his hand on account of the wolf, so that he may shoot if the fierce wolf should come.

entries.farsi: ‏ور نه جاى ديگرى فرماى جست / گفت صد خدمت كنم تو جاى ده
entries.english: If you will do that service, the place is yours; and if not, have the kindness to seek another place.”

entries.farsi: ‏آن كمان و تير در كفم بنه / من نخسبم حارسى رز كنم
entries.english: He said, “I will do a hundred services, give you the place, and put that bow and arrow in my hand.

entries.farsi: گر بر آرد گرگ سر تيرش زنم / بهر حق مگذارم امشب اى دو دل
entries.english: I will not sleep, I will guard the vines; if the wolf raises his head, I will shoot the arrow at him.

entries.farsi: ‏آب باران بر سر و در زير گل / گوشه‏اى خالى شد و او با عيال
entries.english: For God’s sake do not leave me to-night, O double-hearted, the rain-water overhead and the mud underneath!”

entries.farsi: رفت آن جا جاى تنگ و بى‏مجال / چون ملخ بر همدگر گشته سوار
entries.english: A nook was cleared, and he with his family went there: a narrow place and without room to turn.

entries.farsi: از نهيب سيل اندر كنج غار / شب همه شب جمله گويان اى خدا
entries.english: Mounted upon one another, like locusts, from terror of the flood into the corner of the cavern,

entries.farsi: اين سزاى ما سزاى ما سزا / اين سزاى آن كه شد يار خسان
entries.english: During the night, the whole night, they all crying, “O God, this serves us right, serves us right, serves us right.”

entries.farsi: ‏يا كسى كرد از براى ناكسان / اين سزاى آن كه اندر طمع خام
entries.english: This is what is deserved by him that consorted with the vile, or showed worthiness for the sake of the unworthy.

entries.farsi: ‏ترك گويد خدمت خاك كرام / خاك پاكان ليسى و ديوارشان
entries.english: This is what is deserved by him that in vain desire gives up paying homage to the dust of the noble.

entries.farsi: ‏بهتر از عام و رز و گلزارشان / بنده‏ى يك مرد روشن دل شوى
entries.english: That you lick the dust and the wall of the pure is better than the vulgar and their vines and rose-gardens.

entries.farsi: ‏به كه بر فرق سر شاهان روى / از ملوك خاك جز بانگ دهل
entries.english: That you become a slave to a man of enlightened heart is better than that you should walk upon the crown of the head of kings.

entries.farsi: ‏تو نخواهى يافت اى پيك سبل / شهريان خود ره زنان نسبت به روح
entries.english: From the kings of earth you will get nothing but the noise of a drum, O courier of roads.

entries.farsi: ‏روستايى كيست گيج و بى‏فتوح / اين سزاى آن كه بى‏تدبير عقل
entries.english: Even the townsmen are brigands in comparison with the Spirit. Who is the countryman? The fool that is without spiritual gifts

entries.farsi: ‏بانگ غولى آمدش بگزيد نقل / چون پشيمانى ز دل شد تا شغاف
entries.english: This is what is deserved by him who, the cry of a ghoul came to him, without rational foresight chose to move.

entries.farsi: ‏ز آن سپس سودى ندارد اعتراف / آن كمان و تير اندر دست او
entries.english: When repentance has gone from the heart to the pericardium after that it is of no use to acknowledge.

entries.farsi: گرگ را جويان همه شب سو به سو / گرگ بروى خود مسلط چون شرر
entries.english: The bow and arrow in his hand, he seeking the wolf all night to and fro.

entries.farsi: گرگ جويان و ز گرگ او بى‏خبر / هر پشه هر كيك چون گرگى شده
entries.english: The wolf, in truth, was given power over him, like sparks of fire: seeking the wolf, and unaware of the wolf.

entries.farsi: ‏اندر آن ويرانه‏شان زخمى زده / فرصت آن پشه راندن هم نبود
entries.english: Every gnat, every flea, had become as a wolf and inflicted a wound upon them in that ruined place

entries.farsi: از نهيب حمله‏ى گرگ عنود / تا نبايد گرگ آسيبى زند
entries.english: There was no opportunity even of driving away those gnats, because of dread of an attack by the rebellious wolf,

entries.farsi: روستايى ريش خواجه بر كند / اين چنين دندان كنان تا نيم شب
entries.english: Lest the wolf should inflict some damage, the countryman would tear out the Khwaja’s beard.

entries.farsi: ‏جانشان از ناف مى‏آمد به لب / ناگهان تمثال گرگ هشته‏اى
entries.english: In this wise gnashing their teeth till midnight: their souls were coming from the navel to the lip.

entries.farsi: ‏سر بر آورد از فراز پشته‏اى / تير را بگشاد آن خواجه ز شست
entries.english: Suddenly the figure of a deserted wolf raised its head from the top of a hillock.

entries.farsi: ‏زد بر آن حيوان كه تا افتاد پست / اندر افتادن ز حيوان باد جست
entries.english: The Khwaja loosed the arrow from the thumbstall and shot at the animal, so that it fell to the ground.

entries.farsi: ‏روستايى هاى كرد و كوفت دست / ناجوانمردا كه خر كره‏ى من است
entries.english: In falling, wind escaped from the animal: the countryman uttered a wail and heat his hands,

entries.farsi: ‏گفت نى اين گرگ چون آهرمن است / اندر او اشكال گرگى ظاهر است
entries.english: “O ungenerous, it is my ass-colt!” “No,” said he, “this is the devilish wolf,

entries.farsi: ‏شكل او از گرگى او مخبر است / گفت نى بادى كه جست از فرج وى
entries.english: The features of wolfishness are apparent in it; its form makes acquainted with its wolfishness.”

entries.farsi: مى‏شناسم همچنانك آبى ز مى / كشته‏اى خر كره‏ام را در رياض
entries.english: “No,” he said, “I know the wind that escaped from its arse as well as water from wine.

entries.farsi: ‏كه مبادت بسط هرگز ز انقباض / گفت نيكوتر تفحص كن شب است
entries.english: You have killed my ass-colt in the meadows— may you never be released from anguish!”

entries.farsi: ‏شخصها در شب ز ناظر محجب است / شب غلط بنمايد و مبدل بسى
entries.english: “Make a better investigation,” he replied; “it is night, and at night material objects are screened from the beholder.

entries.farsi: ‏ديد صايب شب ندارد هر كسى / هم شب و هم ابر و هم باران ژرف
entries.english: Night causes many a thing to appear wrong and changed: not everyone has the seeing correctly by night.

entries.farsi: ‏اين سه تاريكى غلط آرد شگرف / گفت آن بر من چو روز روشن است
entries.english: Both night and clouds and heavy rain: these three types of darkness produce great error.”

entries.farsi: مى‏شناسم چون مسافر زاد را / خواجه بر جست و بيامد ناشكفت
entries.english: He said, “To me it is as bright day: I know, it is the wind of my ass-colt.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏شناسم باد خر كره‏ى من است / در ميان بيست باد آن باد را
entries.english: Amongst twenty winds I know that wind as the traveller his provisions for the journey.”

entries.farsi: ‏روستايى را گريبانش گرفت / كابله طرار شيد آورده‏اى
entries.english: The Khwaja sprang up, and losing patience he seized the countryman by his collar,

entries.farsi: ‏بنگ و افيون هر دو با هم خورده‏اى / در سه تاريكى شناسى باد خر
entries.english: Crying, “O fool and pick pocket, you have shown hypocrisy: you have eaten both dung and opium together.

entries.farsi: چون ندانى مر مرا اى خيره‏سر / آن كه داند نيم شب گوساله را
entries.english: In the midst of triple darkness you know the wind of the ass: how do not you know me, O giddy-head?

entries.farsi: چون نداند همره ده ساله را / خويشتن را واله و عارف كنى
entries.english: He that knows a colt at midnight, how should he not know his own friend of ten years

entries.farsi: ‏خاك در چشم مروت مى‏زنى / كه مرا از خويش هم آگاه نيست
entries.english: You are feigning to be distraught and a gnostic: you are throwing dust in the eyes of generosity,

entries.farsi: ‏در دلم گنجاى جز اهلل نيست / آن چه دى خوردم از آنم ياد نيست
entries.english: Saying, “ I have no consciousness even of myself: in my heart there is no room for anything but God.

entries.farsi: در چنين بى‏خويشيم معذور دار / آن كه مردارى خورد يعنى نبيذ
entries.english: I have no recollection of what I ate yesterday: this heart takes joy in nothing except bewilderment.

entries.farsi: شرع او را سوى معذوران كشيد / مست و بنگى را طالق و بيع نيست
entries.english: I am sane and maddened by God: remember, and in such a state of selflessness, hold me excusable.

entries.farsi: ‏اين دل از غير تحير شاد نيست / عاقل و مجنون حقم ياد آر
entries.english: He that eats carrion, that is to say, date-wine— the Law enrolls him amongst those who are excused.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو طفل است او معاف و معتفى است / مستيى كايد ز بوى شاه فرد
entries.english: The drunkard and shit eater has not divorce or barter; he is even as a child: he is a person absolved and emancipated.

entries.farsi: صد خم مى در سر و مغز آن نكرد / پس بر او تكليف چون باشد روا
entries.english: The intoxication that arises from the scent of the unique King— a hundred vats of wine never wrought that in head and brain.

entries.farsi: اسب ساقط گشت و شد بى‏دست و پا / بار كه نهد در جهان خر كره را
entries.english: To him, then, how should the obligation be applicable? The horse is fallen and has become unable to move.

entries.farsi: درس كه دهد پارسى بو مره را / بار بر گيرند چون آمد عرج
entries.english: Who in the world would lay a load upon the ass-colt? Who would give lessons in Persian to Bu Murra?

entries.farsi: ‏گفت حق لَيْسَ عَلَى الأَْعْ مى‏ حرج / سوى خود اعمى شدم از حق بصير
entries.english: When lameness comes, the load is taken off: God has said, it is no sin in the blind.

entries.farsi: پس معافم از قليل و از كثير / الف درويشى زنى و بى‏خودى
entries.english: I have become blind in regard to myself, seeing by God: therefore I am absolved from the small and from the great.”

entries.farsi: ‏هاى و هوى مستيان ايزدى / كه زمين را من ندانم ز آسمان
entries.english: You brag of your dervishhood and selflessness, the wailful cries of those intoxicated with God,

entries.farsi: ‏امتحانت كرد غيرت امتحان / باد خر كره چنين رسوات كرد
entries.english: Saying, “I know not earth from heaven.” The jealousy has tried you, tried you.

entries.farsi: هستى نفى ترا اثبات كرد / اين چنين رسوا كند حق شيد را
entries.english: Thus has the wind of your ass-colt put you to shame, thus has it affirmed the existence of your self-negation.

entries.farsi: اين چنين گيرد رميده صيد را / صد هزاران امتحان است اى پسر
entries.english: In this wise God exposes hypocrisy, in this wise does He catch the quarry that has started away.

entries.farsi: هر كه گويد من شدم سرهنگ در / گر نداند عامه او را ز امتحان
entries.english: There are hundreds of thousands of trials, O son, for anyone who says, “I am the captain of the Gate.”

entries.farsi: ‏پختگان راه جويندش نشان / چون كند دعوى خياطى خسى
entries.english: If the common do not know him by the trial, the adepts of the Way will demand from him the token.

entries.farsi: ‏افكند در پيش او شه اطلسى / كه ببر اين را بغلطاق فراخ
entries.english: When a churl pretends to be a tailor, the king will throw down a piece of satin in front of him,

entries.farsi: ‏ز امتحان پيدا شود او را دو شاخ / گر نبودى امتحان هر بدى
entries.english: Saying “Cut this into a wide undervest (baghaltaq)”: from the trial there appear two horns on him.

entries.farsi: چون ببيند زخم گردد چون اسير / مست حق هشيار چون شد از دبور
entries.english: Were there not a testing of every vicious person, every effeminate would be a Rustam in the fray.

entries.farsi: ‏هر مخنث در وغا رستم بدى / خود مخنث را زره پوشيده گير
entries.english: Even suppose that the effeminate has put on a coat of mail: as soon as he feels the blow, he will become as a captive.

entries.farsi: مست حق نايد به خود از نفخ صور / باده‏ى حق راست باشد نى دروغ
entries.english: How will he that is intoxicated with God be restored to his senses by the west-wind? The God-intoxicated man will not come to himself at the blast of the trumpet.

entries.farsi: ‏دوغ خوردى دوغ خوردى دوغ دوغ / ساختى خود را جنيد و بايزيد
entries.english: The wine of God is true, not false: you have drunk buttermilk, you have drunk buttermilk, buttermilk, buttermilk !

entries.farsi: رو كه نشناسم تبر را از كليد / بد رگى و منبلى و حرص و آز
entries.english: You have made yourself out to be a Junayd or a Bayazid, “Go, for I do not know a hatchet from a key.”

entries.farsi: چون كنى پنهان به شيد اى مكر ساز / خويش را منصور حالجى كنى
entries.english: How by means of hypocrisy, O contriver of fraud, will you conceal depravity of nature and sloth and greed and concupiscence?

entries.farsi: ‏آتشى در پنبه‏ى ياران زنى / كه بنشناسم عمر از بو لهب
entries.english: You make yourself a Mansur-i Hallaj and set fire to the cotton of your friends,

entries.farsi: ‏باد كره‏ى خود شناسم نيم شب / اى خرى كاين از تو خر باور كند
entries.english: Saying, “I do not know Umar from Bu Lahab; I know the wind of my ass-colt at midnight.”

entries.farsi: خويش را بهر تو كور و كر كند / خويش را از ره‏روان كمتر شمر
entries.english: Oh, the ass that would believe this from an ass like you, and would make himself blind and deaf for your sake!

entries.farsi: تو حريف ره‏ريانى گه مخور / باز پر از شيد سوى عقل تاز
entries.english: Do not count yourself one of the travellers on the Way; you are a comrade of them that defile the Way: do not eat shit!

entries.farsi: كى پرد بر آسمان پر مجاز / خويشتن را عاشق حق ساختى
entries.english: Fly back from hypocrisy, hasten towards Reason: how shall the wing of the phenomenal soar to Heaven?

entries.farsi: ‏عشق با ديو سياهى باختى / عاشق و معشوق را در رستخيز
entries.english: You have feigned to be a lover of God; you have played the game of love with a black devil.

entries.farsi: دو بدو بندند و پيش آرند تيز / تو چه خود را گيج و بى‏خود كرده‏اى
entries.english: At the Resurrection lover and beloved shall be tied in couples and quickly brought forward.

entries.farsi: ‏خون رز كو خون ما را خورده‏اى / رو كه نشناسم ترا از من بجه
entries.english: Why have you made yourself crazy and senseless? Where is the blood of the vine’? You have drunk our blood,

entries.farsi: ‏عارف بى‏خويشم و بهلول ده / تو توهم مى‏كنى از قرب حق
entries.english: “Go, I do not know you: spring away from me. I am a gnostic who is beside himself and the Buhlul of the village.”

entries.farsi: ‏كه طبق گر دور نبود از طبق / اين نمى‏بينى كه قرب اوليا
entries.english: You art conceiving a false opinion of your nearness to God, thinking that the Tray-maker is not far from the tray;

entries.farsi: صد كرامت دارد و كار و كيا / آهن از داود مومى مى‏شود
entries.english: You do not see this, that the nearness of the saints has a hundred miracles, displays and powers.

entries.farsi: موم در دستت چو آهن مى‏بود / قرب خلق و رزق بر جمله ست عام
entries.english: By David iron is made a piece of wax; in your hand wax is as iron.

entries.farsi: ‏قرب وحى عشق دارند اين كرام / قرب بر انواع باشد اى پدر
entries.english: Nearness in respect of creating and sustaining is common to all; these noble ones possess the nearness of the inspiration of Love.

entries.farsi: مى‏زند خورشيد بر كهسار و زر / ليك قربى هست با زر شيد را
entries.english: Nearness is of various kinds, O father: the sun strikes on the mountains and on the gold;

entries.farsi: كه از آن آگه نباشد بيد را / شاخ خشك و تر قريب آفتاب
entries.english: But between the sun and the gold there is a nearness of which the bid-tree has no knowledge.

entries.farsi: ‏آفتاب از هر دو كى دارد حجاب / ليك كو آن قربت شاخ طرى
entries.english: The dry and fresh bough is near to the sun: how should the sun be screened off from either?

entries.farsi: ‏غير زو تر خشك گشتن گو بياب / آن چنان مستى مباش اى بى‏خرد
entries.english: But where is the nearness of the sappy bough, from which you eat ripe fruit?

entries.farsi: ‏كه ثمار پخته از وى مى‏خورى / شاخ خشك از قربت آن آفتاب
entries.english: From nearness to the sun let the dry bough get anything besides withering sooner!

entries.farsi: كه به عقل آيد پشيمانى خورد / بلك از آن مستان كه چون مى مى‏خورند
entries.english: O man without wisdom, do not be an inebriate of the sort that he comes to his wits he feels sorry;

entries.farsi: عقلهاى پخته حسرت مى‏برند / اى گرفته همچو گربه موش پير
entries.english: Nay, be one of those inebriates on account of whom, while they are drinking the wine, mature intellects suffer regret.

entries.farsi: گر از آن مى شير گيرى شير گير / اى بخورده از خيالى جام هيچ
entries.english: O you, who, like a cat, have caught an old mouse, if you are so brave from that wine, catch the Lion!

entries.farsi: ‏همچو مستان حقايق بر مپيچ / مى‏فتى اين سو و آن سو مست‏وار
entries.english: O you who have drunk quaffed the cup of Nothingness from a phantom do not reel like them that are intoxicated with the realities.

entries.farsi: اى تو اين سو نيستت ز آن سو گذار / گر بدان سو راه يابى بعد از آن
entries.english: You are falling to this side and that, like the drunken: O you on this side, there is no passage for you on that side.

entries.farsi: ‏گه بدين سو گه بدان سو سر فشان / جمله اين سويى از آن سو گپ مزن
entries.english: If you find the way to that side, henceforth toss your head now to this side, now to that!

entries.farsi: ‏چون ندارى مرگ هرزه جان مكن / آن خضر جان كز اجل نهراسد او
entries.english: You are all on this side, do not idly boast of that side: since you have not the death, do not agonise yourself in vain.

entries.farsi: شايد ار مخلوق را نشناسد او / كام از ذوق توهم خوش كنى
entries.english: He with the soul of Khadir, that does not shrink from death— if he know not the created, it is fitting.

entries.farsi: ‏در دمى در خيك خود پرش كنى / پس به يك سوزن تهى گردى ز باد
entries.english: You sweeten your palate with the savour of false imagination; you blow into the bag of selfhood and fill it:

entries.farsi: اين چنين فربه تن عاقل مباد / كوزه‏ها سازى ز برف اندر شتا
entries.english: Then, at one prick of a needle you are emptied of wind— may no intelligent man’s body be fat like this!

entries.farsi: كى كند چون آب بيند آن وفا / آن شغالى رفت اندر خم رنگ
entries.english: You make pots of snow in winter: when they see the water how shall they maintain that constancy?

sections.title_fa: افتادن شغال در خم رنگ و رنگين شدن و دعوى طاوسى كردن ميان شغالان
sections.title_en: How the jackal fell into the dyeing-vat and was dyed with many colours and pretended amongst the jackals that he was a peacock.

entries.farsi: ‏اندر آن خم كرد يك ساعت درنگ / پس بر آمد پوستش رنگين شده
entries.english: A certain jackal went into the dyeing-vat, stayed in the vat for a while,

entries.farsi: ‏كه منم طاوس عليين شده / پشم رنگين رونق خوش يافته
entries.english: And then arose, his skin having become multi-coloured, saying, ‘I have become the Peacock of ‘Illiyyin.”

entries.farsi: ‏آفتاب آن رنگها بر تافته / ديد خود را سبز و سرخ و فور و زرد
entries.english: His coloured fur had gained a charming brilliance, and the sun shone upon those colours.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را بر شغالان عرضه كرد / جمله گفتند اى شغالك حال چيست
entries.english: He beheld himself green and red and roan and yellow, he presented himself to the jackals.

entries.farsi: ‏كه ترا در سر نشاط ملتويست / از نشاط از ما كرانه كرده‏اى
entries.english: They all said, “O little jackal, what is the matter, that you have in your head manifold exultation?

entries.farsi: ‏اين تكبر از كجا آورده‏اى / يك شغالى پيش او شد كاى فالن
entries.english: Because of exultation you have turned aside from us: whence have you brought this arrogance?”

entries.farsi: ‏شيد كردى يا شدى از خوش دالن / شيد كردى تا بمنبر بر جهى
entries.english: One of the jackals went to him and said, “O so-and-so, have you acted deceitfully or have you become one of those whose hearts rejoice?

entries.farsi: ‏تا ز الف اين خلق را حسرت دهى / بس بكوشيدى نديدى گرميى
entries.english: You have acted deceitfully to the end that you may jump on to the pulpit and by your idle chatter give this folk regret.

entries.farsi: كه خوشيم و از درون بس ناخوشند
entries.english: You have striven much, you have not felt any ardour; hence from deceit you have exhibited a piece of impudence.”

entries.farsi: ‏باز بى‏شرمى پناه هر دغاست / كه التفات خلق سوى خود كشند
entries.english: Ardour belongs to the saints and prophets; on the other hand, impudence is the refuge of every impostor;

entries.farsi: ‏پس ز شيد آورده‏اى بى‏شرميى / گرمى آن اوليا و انبياست
entries.english: For they draw the people’s attention to themselves, saying, “We are happy,” though within they are exceedingly unhappy.

sections.title_fa: چرب كردن مرد الفى لب و سبلت خود را هر بامداد به پوست دنبه و بيرون آمدن ميان حريفان كه من چنين خورده‏ام و چنان
sections.title_en: How a braggart greased his lips and moustache every morning with the skin of a fat sheep’s tail and came amongst his companions, saying,” I have eaten such and such.”

entries.farsi: پوست دنبه يافت شخصى مستهان / ‏هر صباحى چرب كردى سبلتان
entries.english: A person, who was lightly esteemed, used to grease his moustaches every morning

entries.farsi: در ميان منعمان رفتى كه من / ‏لوت چربى خورده‏ام در انجمن
entries.english: And go amongst the munificent, saying, “I have eaten some well-greased food at the party.”

entries.farsi: دست بر سبلت نهادى در نويد / رمز يعنى سوى سبلت بنگريد
entries.english: He would gaily put his hand on his moustache as a sign, meaning, “Look at my moustache!

entries.farsi: كاين گواه صدق گفتار من است / ‏وين نشان چرب و شيرين خوردن است
entries.english: For this is the witness to the truth of my words, and this is the token of my eating greasy and delicious food.”

entries.farsi: اشكمش گفتى جواب بى‏طنين / ‏كه أباد اللهَّ كيد الكاذبين
entries.english: His belly would say in soundless response, “May God destroy the plots of the liars!

entries.farsi: الف تو ما را بر آتش بر نهاد / كان سبيل چرب تو بركنده باد
entries.english: Your boasting has set me on fire: may that greasy moustache of yours be torn out!

entries.farsi: گر نبودى الف زشتت اى گدا / يك كريمى رحم افكندى به ما
entries.english: Were it not for your foul boasting, O beggar, some generous man would have taken pity on me;

entries.farsi: ور نمودى عيب و كژ كم باختى / ‏يك طبيبى داروى او ساختى
entries.english: And if you had shown the ailment and had not played false, some physician would have prepared a remedy for it.”

entries.farsi: گفت حق كه كژ مجنبان گوش و دم / ‏ينفعن الصادقين صدقهم
entries.english: God has said, “Do not move ear or tail crookedly: their veracity shall profit the veracious.”

entries.farsi: كهف اندر كژ مخسب اى محتلم / ‏آن چه دارى وانما و فاستقم
entries.english: Ne recurvatus in antro dormiveris, O to qui passus es nocturnam pol­ lutionem: reveal that which you have, and act straight

entries.farsi: ور نگويى عيب خود بارى خمش / ‏از نمايش وز دغل خود را مكش
entries.english: Or if you tell not your fault, at least refrain from talk: do not kill yourself by ostentation and trickery.

entries.farsi: گر تو نقدى يافتى مگشا دهان / ‏هست در ره سنگهاى امتحان
entries.english: If you have got any money, do not open your mouth: there are touchstones on the Way,

entries.farsi: سنگهاى امتحان را نيز پيش / ‏امتحانها هست در احوال خويش
entries.english: And for the touchstones too there are tests concerning their own states.

entries.farsi: امتحان بر امتحان است اى پدر / هين به كمتر امتحان خود را مخر
entries.english: God has said, “From birth unto death they are tried every year twice.

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان از والدت تا به حين / ‏يفتنون كل عام مرتين
entries.english: There is test upon test, O father: beware, do not buy yourself at the smallest test.

sections.title_fa: ايمن بودن بلعم باعور كه امتحانها كرد حضرت او را و از آنها روى سپيد آمده بود
sections.title_en: How Bal am the son of Ba`ur was secure, because the Lord had made tests and he had come through them honourably.

entries.farsi: بلعم باعور و ابليس لعين / ز امتحان آخرين گشته مهين
entries.english: Bal’am the son of Ba’ur and the accursed Iblis were disgraced at the ultimate test.

entries.farsi: او به دعوى ميل دولت مى‏كند / معده‏اش نفرين سبلت مى‏كند
entries.english: He, by his pretension, desires to be rich; his belly is execrating his moustache,

entries.farsi: كانچه پنهان مى‏كند پيداش كن / ‏سوخت ما را اى خدا رسواش كن
entries.english: Crying, “Reveal what he is hiding! He has consumed me: O God, expose him!”

entries.farsi: جمله اجزاى تنش خصم وى‏اند / كز بهارى الفد ايشان در دى‏اند
entries.english: All the members of his body are his adversaries, for he chatters of spring they are in December.

entries.farsi: الف وا داد كرمها مى‏كند / شاخ رحمت را ز بن بر مى‏كند
entries.english: Vain talk repels acts of kindness and tears off the bough of pity from the trunk of the tree.

entries.farsi: راستى پيش آر يا خاموش كن / ‏و آن گهان رحمت ببين و نوش كن
entries.english: Bring forward honesty, or else be silent, and then behold pity and enjoy it.

entries.farsi: آن شكم خصم سبيل او شده / ‏دست پنهان در دعا اندر زده
entries.english: That belly became the adversary of his moustache and secretly had recourse to prayer,

entries.farsi: كاى خدا رسوا كن اين الف لئام / ‏تا بجنبد سوى ما رحم كرام
entries.english: Crying, “O God, expose this idle brag of the base, in order that the pity of the noble may be moved towards me.”

entries.farsi: مستجاب آمد دعاى آن شكم / ‏سوزش حاجت بزد بيرون علم
entries.english: The belly’s prayer was answered: the ardency of need put out a flag.

entries.farsi: گفت حق گر فاسقى و اهل صنم / ‏چون مرا خوانى اجابتها كنم
entries.english: God has said, “Though you be a profligate and idolater, I will answer when you call Me.”

entries.farsi: تو دعا را سخت گير و مى‏شخول / ‏عاقبت برهاندت از دست غول
entries.english: Cleave fast unto prayer and ever cry out: in the end it will deliver you from the hands of the ghoul.

entries.farsi: چون شكم خود را به حضرت در سپرد / گربه آمد پوست آن دنبه ببرد
entries.english: When the belly committed itself to God, the cat came and carried off the skin of that sheep’s tail.

entries.farsi: از پس گربه دويدند او گريخت / ‏كودك از ترس عتابش رنگ ريخت
entries.english: They ran after the cat, she fled. The child, from fear of his scolding, changed colour.

entries.farsi: آمد اندر انجمن آن طفل خرد / آب روى مرد الفى را ببرد
entries.english: The little child came into the company and took away the prestige of the boastful man.

entries.farsi: گفت آن دنبه كه هر صبحى بدان / چرب مى‏كردى لبان و سبلتان
entries.english: It said, “The sheep’s tail with which every morning you greased your lips and moustaches—

entries.farsi: گربه آمد ناگهانش در ربود / بس دويديم و نكرد آن جهد سود
entries.english: The cat came and suddenly snatched it away: I ran hard, but the effort was of no use.”

entries.farsi: خنده آمد حاضران را از شگفت / ‏رحمهاشان باز جنبيدن گرفت
entries.english: Those who were present laughed from astonishment, and their feelings of pity began to be moved again.

entries.farsi: دعوتش كردند و سيرش داشتند / تخم رحمت در زمينش كاشتند
entries.english: They invited him and kept him well-fed; they sowed the seed of pity in his soil.

entries.farsi: او چو ذوق راستى ديد از كرام / ‏بى‏تكبر راستى را شد غالم
entries.english: When he had tasted honesty from the noble, he without arrogance became devoted to honesty.

sections.title_fa: دعوى طاوسى كردن آن شغال كه در خم صباغ افتاد
sections.title_en: How the jackal which had fallen into the dyer’s vat pretended to be a peacock.

entries.farsi: آن شغال رنگ رنگ آمد نهفت / ‏بر بنا گوش مالمت‏گر بگفت
entries.english: That multi-coloured jackal came secretly and tapped on the lobe of the rebuker’s ear.

entries.farsi: بنگر آخر در من و در رنگ من / ‏يك صنم چون من ندارد خود شمن
entries.english: Please look at me and at my colour: truly the idolater possesses no idol like me.

entries.farsi: چون گلستان گشته‏ام صد رنگ و خوش / ‏مر مرا سجده كن از من سر مكش
entries.english: Like the flower-garden I have become many-hued and lovely: bow in homage to me, do not withdraw from me.

entries.farsi: كر و فر و آب و تاب و رنگ بين / ‏فخر دنيا خوان مرا و ركن دين
entries.english: Behold my glory and splendour and sheen and radiance and colour! Call me the Pride of the World and the Pillar of the Religion!

entries.farsi: مظهر لطف خدايى گشته‏ام / ‏لوح شرح كبريايى گشته‏ام
entries.english: I have become the theatre of the Divine Grace; I have become the tablet on which the Divine Majesty is unfolded.

entries.farsi: آن شغالان آمدند آن جا به جمع / ‏همچو پروانه به گرداگرد شمع
entries.english: O jackals take heed; do not call me a jackal: how should a jackal have so much beauty?”

entries.farsi: اى شغالان هين مخوانيدم شغال / ‏كى شغالى را بود چندين جمال
entries.english: Those jackals came here en masse, like moths around the candle.

entries.farsi: پس چه خوانيمت بگو اى جوهرى / ‏گفت طاوس نر چون مشترى
entries.english: “Say then, what shall we call you, O creature of substance?” He replied, “A peacock as Jupiter.”

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندش كه طاوسان جان / ‏جلوه‏ها دارند اندر گلستان
entries.english: Then they said to him, “The spiritual peacocks have displays in the Rose-garden:

entries.farsi: بانگ طاوسان كنى گفتا كه ال / پس نه‏اى طاوس خواجه بو العال
entries.english: Do you display yourself like that?” “No,” said he: “not having gone into the desert, how should I tread Mina?”

entries.farsi: تو چنان جلوه كنى گفتا كه نى / ‏باديه نارفته چون كوبم منى
entries.english: “Do you utter the cry of peacocks?” “Nay,” said he. “Then, Master Bu ’l-‘Ala, you are not a peacock.

entries.farsi: خلعت طاوس آيد ز آسمان / ‏كى رسى از رنگ و دعويها بدان
entries.english: The peacock’s garment of honour comes from Heaven: how will you attain thereto by means of colours and pretences?”

sections.title_fa: تشبيه فرعون و دعوى الوهيت او بدان شغال كه دعوى طاوسى مى‏كرد
sections.title_en: Comparison of Pharaoh and his pretence of divinity to the jackal which pretended to be a peacock

entries.farsi: همچو فرعونى مرصع كرده ريش / ‏برتر از عيسى پريده از خريش
entries.english: You are even as Pharaoh, who bejewelled his beard and in his asinine folly soared higher than Jesus.

entries.farsi: او هم از نسل شغال ماده زاد / هر كه ديد آن جاه و مالش سجده كرد
entries.english: He too was born of the generation of the she-jackal and fell into a vat of riches and power.

entries.farsi: سجده‏ى افسوسيان را او بخورد / گشت مستك آن گداى ژنده دلق
entries.english: Everyone who beheld his power and riches bowed down to him in worship: he swallowed the worship of the idle mockers.

entries.farsi: ‏از سجود و از تحيرهاى خلق / مال مار آمد كه در او زهرهاست
entries.english: That beggar in tattered cloak became miserably drunk with the people’s worship and feelings of amazement.

entries.farsi: ‏و آن قبول و سجده‏ى خلق اژدهاست / هاى اى فرعون ناموسى مكن
entries.english: Riches are a snake, for therein are poisons; and popular favour and worship is a dragon.

entries.farsi: ‏تو شغالى هيچ طاوسى مكن / سوى طاوسان اگر پيدا شوى
entries.english: Ah, do not assume a virtue, O Pharaoh: you are a jackal, do not in any wise behave as a peacock.

entries.farsi: ‏عاجزى از جلوه و رسوا شوى / موسى و هارون چو طاوسان بدند
entries.english: If you appear in the direction of the peacocks, you are incapable of display and you will he put to shame.

entries.farsi: پر جلوه بر سر و رويت زدند / زشتى‏ات پيدا شد و رسوايى‏ات
entries.english: Moses and Aaron were as peacocks: they flapped the wings of display upon your head and face.

entries.farsi: ‏سر نگون افتادى از بالايى‏ات / چون محك ديدى سيه گشتى چو قلب
entries.english: Your foulness and disgrace were exposed; you did fall headlong from your height.

entries.farsi: ‏نقش شيرى رفت و پيدا گشت كلب / اى سگ گرگين زشت از حرص و جوش
entries.english: When you saw the touchstone, you became black, like adulterated coin: the leonine figure vanished, and the dog was revealed.

entries.farsi: پوستين شير را بر خود مپوش / غره‏ى شيرت بخواهد امتحان
entries.english: O foul mangy dog, through greed and exuberant insolence do not clothe yourself in the lion’s skin.

entries.farsi: ‏نقش شير و آن گه اخالق سگان
entries.english: The roar of the lion will demand from you the test. The figure of a lion, and then the dispositions of dogs!

sections.title_fa: ِتفسير وَ لَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِي لَحْ نِ الْقَوْل
sections.title_en: Explanation of, and you will surely know them in the perversion of their speech.

entries.farsi: گر منافق زفت باشد نغز و هول / ‏واشناسى مر و را در لحن و قول
entries.english: God said to the Prophet in the course, “One sign of the hypocritical is easier:

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان مر نبى را در مساق / ‏يك نشانى سهلتر ز اهل نفاق
entries.english: Though the hypocrite is big, handsome, and terrible, you will recognise him in his perverse enunciation and speech.”

entries.farsi: چون سفالين كوزه‏ها را مى‏خرى / ‏امتحانى مى‏كنى اى مشترى
entries.english: When you are buying earthenware pots, you make a trial, O purchaser.

entries.farsi: بانگ اشكسته دگرگون مى‏بود / بانگ چاووش است پيشش مى‏رود
entries.english: You give the pot a tap with your hand: why? In order that you may know the cracked one by the sound

entries.farsi: مى‏زنى دستى بر آن كوزه چرا / تا شناسى از طنين اشكسته را
entries.english: The voice of the cracked one is different; the voice is a chawush: it goes in front of it.

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏آيد كه تعريفش كند / همچو مصدر فعل تصريفش كند
entries.english: The voice comes in order to make it known: it determines it, as the verb the masdar.

entries.farsi: چون حديث امتحان رويى نمود / يادم آمد قصه‏ى هاروت زود
entries.english: When the subject of probation cropped up, the story of Harut at once came into my memory.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى هاروت و ماروت و دليرى ايشان بر امتحان حق تعالى
sections.title_en: The Story of Harut and Marut and their boldness in encountering the probations of God most High

entries.farsi: پيش از اين ز آن گفته بوديم اندكى / ‏خود چه گوييم از هزارانش يكى
entries.english: Before this, we had told a little of it: what, indeed, should we tell? We can tell only one of its thousands.

entries.farsi: حمله‏ى ديگر ز بسيارش قليل / ‏گفته آيد شرح يك عضوى ز پيل
entries.english: I wished to speak of the truths in it, till now they have remained on account of hindrances.

entries.farsi: خواستم گفتن در آن تحقيقها / تا كنون واماند از تعويقها
entries.english: Once again a little of its much shall be told— the description of a single limb of the elephant.

entries.farsi: گوش كن هاروت را ماروت را / اى غالم و چاكران ماروت را
entries.english: Listen to Harut and Marut, O you to whose face we are slaves and servants.

entries.farsi: مست بودند از تماشاى اله / ‏و ز عجايبهاى استدراج شاه
entries.english: They were intoxicated with the spectacle of God and with the marvels of the King’s gradual temptation.

entries.farsi: اين چنين مستى است ز استدراج حق / ‏تا چه مستيها كند معراج حق
entries.english: Such intoxication arises from God’s gradual temptation, so that what intoxications are wrought by the ascension to God.

entries.farsi: دانه‏ى دامش چنين مستى نمود / خوان انعامش چها داند گشود
entries.english: The bait in His snare produced intoxication like this: what things, can the table of His bounty reveal !

entries.farsi: مست بودند و رهيده از كمند / هاى و هوى عاشقانه مى‏زدند
entries.english: They were drunken and freed from the noose: they were uttering rapturous cries in the fashion of lovers;

entries.farsi: يك كمين و امتحان در راه بود / صرصرش چون كاه كه را مى‏ربود
entries.english: In their road there was one ambush and trial: its mighty wind would sweep the mountain away like straw.

entries.farsi: امتحان مى‏كردشان زير و زبر / كى بود سر مست را ز اينها خبر
entries.english: The trial was turning them upside down, how should one that is drunk have consciousness of these things?

entries.farsi: خندق و ميدان به پيش او يكى است / ‏چاه و خندق پيش او خوش مسلكى است
entries.english: To him pit and open field are one; to him dungeon and pit are a pleasant path to tread.

entries.farsi: آن بز كوهى بر آن كوه بلند / بر دود از بهر خوردى بى‏گزند
entries.english: The mountain-goat runs up that high mountain for the sake of some harmless food.

entries.farsi: تا علف چيند ببيند ناگهان / ‏بازيى ديگر ز حكم آسمان
entries.english: While he browses, suddenly he sees another trick played by the ordinance of Heaven.

entries.farsi: بر كهى ديگر بر اندازد نظر / ماده بز بيند بر آن كوه دگر
entries.english: He casts his gaze upon another mountain: on that other mountain he espies a she-goat.

entries.farsi: چشم او تاريك گردد در زمان / ‏بر جهد سر مست زين كه تا بدان
entries.english: Straightway his eye is darkened: he leaps madly from this mountain to that.

entries.farsi: آن چنان نزديك بنمايد و را / كه دويدن گرد بالوعه سرا
entries.english: To him it seems as near as to run round the sink of a house.

entries.farsi: آن هزاران گز دو گز بنمايدش / ‏تا ز مستى ميل جستن آيدش
entries.english: Those thousands of ells appear to him two ells, in order that from mad infatuation the impulse to leap may come to him.

entries.farsi: او ز صيادان به كه بگريخته / ‏خود پناهش خون او را ريخته
entries.english: As soon as he leaps, he falls midway between the two pitiless mountains.

entries.farsi: چون كه بجهد در فتد اندر ميان / ‏در ميان هر دو كوه بى‏امان
entries.english: He had fled to the mountain from the hunters: his very refuge shed his blood.

entries.farsi: باشد اغلب صيد اين بز همچنين / ‏ور نه چالاك است و چست و خصم بين
entries.english: The hunters are seated between the two mountains in expectation of this awesome decree.

entries.farsi: شسته صيادان ميان آن دو كوه / ‏انتظار اين قضاى باشكوه
entries.english: The capture of this goat is, for the most part, in this manner; else he is agile and nimble and quick to see the enemy.

entries.farsi: رستم ار چه با سر و سبلت بود / دام پا گيرش يقين شهوت بود
entries.english: Though Rustam have head and moustache, lust will certainly be the snare to catch his feet.

entries.farsi: همچو من از مستى شهوت ببر / مستى شهوت ببين اندر شتر
entries.english: Be cut off, like me, from the intoxication of lust: look at the intoxication of lust in the camel!

entries.farsi: باز اين مستى شهوت در جهان / ‏پيش مستى ملك دان مستهان
entries.english: Know, again, that this intoxication of lust in the world is deemed of small account beside the intoxication of the angels.

entries.farsi: آب شيرين تا نخوردى، آب شور / خوش بود خوش چون درون ديده نور
entries.english: The intoxication of that one breaks the intoxication of this one: how should he show any propensity to lust?

entries.farsi: مستى آن مستى اين بشكند / او به شهوت التفاتى كى كند
entries.english: Until you have drunk sweet water, briny water is sweet, sweet as the light in the eye;

entries.farsi: قطره‏اى از باده‏هاى آسمان / ‏بر كند جان را ز مى و ز ساقيان
entries.english: A single drop of the wines of Heaven causes the soul to be rapt away from the wine and cupbearers —

entries.farsi: تا چه مستيها بود امالك را / و ز جلالت روحهاى پاك را
entries.english: So that what intoxications befall the angels and the spirits purified by the Divine glory,

entries.farsi: كه به بويى دل در آن مى‏بسته‏اند / خم باده‏ى اين جهان بشكسته‏اند
entries.english: Who have set their hearts on that wine at one smell, and have broken the jar of this world’s wine;—

entries.farsi: جز مگر آنها كه نوميدند و دور / همچو كفارى نهفته در قبور
entries.english: Except, maybe, them that are in despair and far, like infidels hidden in graves,

entries.farsi: نااميد از هر دو عالم گشته‏اند / خارهاى بى‏نهايت كشته‏اند
entries.english: Have lost all hope of both worlds and have sown thorns without end.

entries.farsi: پس ز مستيها بگفتند اى دريغ / ‏بر زمين باران بداديمى چو ميغ
entries.english: Therefore they, because of their feelings of intoxication, said, “Alas, we would rain upon the earth, like clouds;

entries.farsi: گستريديمى در اين بى‏داد جا / عدل و انصاف و عبادات و وفا
entries.english: We would spread in this place of injustice justice and equity and devotions and faithfulness.”

entries.farsi: اين بگفتند و قضا مى‏گفت بيست / ‏پيش پاتان دام ناپيدا بسى است
entries.english: This they said, and the Divine decree was saying, “Stop! Before your feet there is many an unseen pitfall.”

entries.farsi: هين مدو گستاخ در دشت بال / هين مران كورانه اندر كربال
entries.english: Beware; do not run boldly into the desert of woe! Beware; do not push on blindly into the Karbala,

entries.farsi: كه ز موى و استخوان هالكان / ‏مى‏نيابد راه پاى سالكان
entries.english: For because of the hair and bones of the perished the travellers’ feet find no way.
