entries.farsi: جمله‏ى راه استخوان و موى و پى / ‏بس كه تيغ قهر ال شى كرد شى
entries.english: The whole way is bones and hair and sinews: many is the thing that the sword of Vengeance has made nothing.

entries.farsi: گفت حق كه بندگان جفت عون / ‏بر زمين آهسته مى‏رانند و هون
entries.english: God has said that servants attended by help walk on the earth quietly and meekly.

entries.farsi: پا برهنه چون رود در خارزار / جز به وقفه و فكرت و پرهيزكار
entries.english: How should a bare-footed man go into the thorn-thicket save with halting and reflection and cautiously?

entries.farsi: اين قضا مى‏گفت ليكن گوششان / ‏بسته بود اندر حجاب جوششان
entries.english: The Decree was saying this, but their ears were closed in the veil of their hotheadedness.

entries.farsi: چشمها و گوشها را بسته‏اند / جز مر آنها را كه از خود رسته‏اند
entries.english: Eyes and ears have been closed, except for them that have escaped from themselves.

entries.farsi: جز عنايت كى گشايد چشم را / جز محبت كى نشاند خشم را
entries.english: Who but Grace shall open the eyes? Who but Love shall allay the Wrath?

entries.farsi: جهد بى‏توفيق خود كس را مباد / در جهان و اهلل أعلم بالسداد
entries.english: Truly, may no one in the world have toil without prospering! And God best knows the right course.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى خواب ديدن فرعون آمدن موسى را عليه السلام و تدارك انديشيدن
sections.title_en: The Story of Pharaoh’s dream of the coming of Moses, on whom be peace, and how he took thought to relieve himself.

entries.farsi: جهد فرعونى چو بى‏توفيق بود / هر چه او مى‏دوخت آن تفتيق بود
entries.english: Inasmuch as Pharaoh’s toil was not blessed, whatever he would stitch, that was ripping asunder.

entries.farsi: از منجم بود در حكمش هزار / وز معبر نيز و ساحر بى‏شمار
entries.english: He had a thousand astrologers at his beck, and also a countless multitude of dream-interpreters and magicians.

entries.farsi: مقدم موسى نمودندش به خواب / ‏كه كند فرعون و ملكش را خراب
entries.english: There was shown to him in a dream the coming of Moses, who would destroy Pharaoh and his kingdom.

entries.farsi: با معبر گفت و با اهل نجوم / ‏چون بود دفع خيال و خواب شوم
entries.english: He said to the interpreters and astrologers, “How may the ill-boding phantasm and dream be warded off?”

entries.farsi: جمله گفتندش كه تدبيرى كنيم / ‏راه زادن را چو ره زن مى‏زنيم
entries.english: They all said to him, “We will contrive something, we will waylay the birth, like brigands.”

entries.farsi: تا رسيد آن شب كه مولد بود آن / ‏راى اين ديدند آن فرعونيان
entries.english: Untill the night arrived on which the begetting took place; those Pharaoh’s men deemed it advisable,

entries.farsi: كه برون آرند آن روز از پگاه / ‏سوى ميدان بزم و تخت پادشاه
entries.english: Early on that day, to bring forth the King’s banquet and throne towards the maydan,

entries.farsi: الصال اى جمله اسرائيليان / ‏شاه مى‏خواند شما را ز آن مكان
entries.english: “Welcome, O all you Israelites! The King calls you from that place,

entries.farsi: تا شما را رو نمايد بى‏نقاب / ‏بر شما احسان كند بهر ثواب
entries.english: That he may show unto you his face unveiled, and do kindness unto you for the sake of the recompense”;

entries.farsi: كان اسيران را بجز دورى نبود / ديدن فرعون دستورى نبود
entries.english: For to those captives there was naught but distance: the sight of Pharaoh was not permitted.

entries.farsi: گر فتادندى به ره در پيش او / بهر آن ياسه بخفتندى به رو
entries.english: If they fell in with him on the road, they would lie on their faces on account of the law.

entries.farsi: ياسه اين بد كه نبيند هيچ اسير / درگه و بى‏گه لقاى آن امير
entries.english: The law was this: no captive in or out of season shall behold the countenance of that Prince,

entries.farsi: بانگ چاووشان چو در ره بشنود / تا نبيند رو به ديوارى كند
entries.english: And whenever on the road he hears the shout of the beadles, he shall turn his face towards a wall, that he may not see;

entries.farsi: ور ببيند روى او مجرم بود / آن چه بدتر بر سر او آن رود
entries.english: And if he sees his face, he shall be guilty of a crime, and the worst punishment shall befall him.

entries.farsi: بودشان حرص لقاى ممتنع / ‏چون حريص است آدمى فيما منع
entries.english: They had greed for the inaccessible countenance, since Man is greedy for that which has been forbidden.

sections.title_fa: ‏به ميدان خواندن بنى اسرائيل را براى حيلت منع والدت موسى عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How they summoned the Israelites to the maydan, as a device to prevent the begetting of Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: اى اسيران سوى ميدانگه رويد / كز شهنشه ديدن و جود است اميد
entries.english: “O captives, go you to the maydan, for there is hope of seeing and munificence from the King of kings.”

entries.farsi: چون شنيدند مژده اسرائيليان / ‏تشنگان بودند و بس مشتاق آن
entries.english: When the Israelites heard the glad news, they were thirsting and longing exceedingly for that.

entries.farsi: حيله را خوردند و آن سو تاختند / خويشتن را بهر جلوه ساختند
entries.english: They swallowed the trick and hastened in that direction and made themselves ready for the unveiling.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت
sections.title_en: Story.

entries.farsi: همچنان كاينجا مغول حيله‏دان / ‏گفت مى‏جويم كسى از مصريان
entries.english: Even as here the crafty Moghul said, “I am seeking a certain one of the Egyptians.

entries.farsi: مصريان را جمع آريد اين طرف / ‏تا در آيد آن كه مى‏بايد به كف
entries.english: Bring the Egyptians together on this side, in order that he who is wanted may come to hand.”

entries.farsi: هر كه مى‏آمد بگفتا نيست اين / ‏هين در آ خواجه در آن گوشه نشين
entries.english: Whenever anyone came, he said, “it is not this one: oh, come in, sir, and sit in that corner,”

entries.farsi: تا بدين شيوه همه جمع آمدند / گردن ايشان بدين حيلت زدند
entries.english: Till in this fashion they all were assembled, and they beheaded them by means of this trick.

entries.farsi: دعوت مكارشان اندر كشيد / الحذر از مكر شيطان اى رشيد
entries.english: The ill-starredness of the fact that they would not obey God’s summoner towards the call to prayer,

entries.farsi: شومى آن كه سوى بانگ نماز / داعى اللهَّ را نبردندى نياز
entries.english: The invitation of the deceiver inveigled them. O righteous man, beware of the deceit of the Devil!

entries.farsi: بانگ درويشان و محتاجان بنوش / ‏تا نگيرد بانگ محتاليت گوش
entries.english: Listen to the cry of the poor and needy; lest yours ear receive the cry of a cunning rogue.

entries.farsi: گر گدايان طامعند و زشت خو / در شكم خواران تو صاحب دل بجو
entries.english: If the beggars are covetous and depraved, seek the man of heart amongst the gluttons.

entries.farsi: در تگ دريا گهر با سنگهاست / ‏فخرها اندر ميان ننگهاست
entries.english: At the bottom of the sea there are pearls with pebbles: glories are amidst shames.

entries.farsi: پس بجوشيدند اسرائيليان / ‏از پگه تا جانب ميدان دوان
entries.english: The Israelites, then, bestirred themselves mightily, running betimes towards the maydan.

entries.farsi: چون به حيلتشان بميدان برد او / روى خود بنمودشان بس تازه رو
entries.english: When he by cunning had brought them into the maydan, he displayed his face to them, looking very fresh.

entries.farsi: كرد دل دارى و بخششها بداد / هم عطا هم وعده‏ها كرد آن قباد
entries.english: He showed fondness and gave presents: that Emperor bestowed both gifts and promises.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفت از براى جانتان / ‏جمله در ميدان بخسبيد امشبان
entries.english: After that, he said: “For your lives’ sake, do you all sleep in the maydan to-night!”

entries.farsi: پاسخش دادند كه خدمت كنيم / ‏گر تو خواهى يك مه اينجا ساكنيم
entries.english: They answered him, saying, “ We will do service: if you desire, we will dwell here a month.”

sections.title_fa: ‏باز گشتن فرعون از ميدان به شهر شاد به تفريق بنى اسرائيل از زنانشان در شب حمل
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh returned from the maydan to the city, glad at having parted the Israelites from their wives on the night of the conception.

entries.farsi: شه شبانگه باز آمد شادمان / ‏كامشبان حمل است و دورند از زنان
entries.english: At nightfall the King came back, rejoicing and saying, “The conception is tonight, and they are far from their wives.”

entries.farsi: خازنش عمران هم اندر خدمتش / ‏هم به شهر آمد قرين صحبتش
entries.english: ‘Imran, his treasurer, also came to the city in attendance upon him as his companion.

entries.farsi: گفت اى عمران بر اين در خسب تو / هين مرو سوى زن و صحبت مجو
entries.english: He said, “O ‘Imran, do you sleep at this door. Beware! Go not to your wife or seek to lie with her.”

entries.farsi: گفت خسبم هم بر اين درگاه تو / هيچ ننديشم بجز دل خواه تو
entries.english: He replied, “I will sleep at this portal of yours; I will think of nothing but your pleasure.”

entries.farsi: بود عمران هم ز اسرائيليان / ‏ليك مر فرعون را دل بود و جان
entries.english: ‘Imran, too, was one of the Israelites, but he was heart and soul to Pharaoh.

entries.farsi: كى گمان بردى كه او عصيان كند / آن كه خوف جان فرعون آن كند
entries.english: How should he have thought that he would disobey and do that which the dread of Pharaoh’s soul?

sections.title_fa: ‏جمع آمدن عمران با مادر موسى و حامله شدن مادر موسى عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How Imran lay with the mother of Moses and how the mother of Moses, on him be peace, became pregnant.

entries.farsi: زن بر او افتاد و بوسيد آن لبش / ‏بر جهانيدش ز خواب اندر شبش
entries.english: The King departed, and he slept at the door; at midnight his wife came to see him.

entries.farsi: شه برفت و او بر آن درگاه خفت / ‏نيم شب آمد پى ديدنش جفت
entries.english: The wife fell upon him and kissed his lips: she roused him from his slumber in the night.

entries.farsi: گشت بيدار او و زن را ديد خوش / ‏بوسه باران كرده از لب بر لبش
entries.english: He awoke and saw that his wife was fair and that she rained kisses from her lips upon his.

entries.farsi: گفت عمران اين زمان چون آمدى / ‏گفت از شوق و قضاى ايزدى
entries.english: ‘Imran said, “How did you come at this time?” She said, “From desire and from the Divine ordinance.”

entries.farsi: در كشيدش در كنار از مهر مرد / بر نيامد با خود آن دم در نبرد
entries.english: The man drew her lovingly into his arms; at that moment he did not rise to battle with himself.

entries.farsi: جفت شد با او امانت را سپرد / پس بگفت اى زن نه اين كارى است خرد
entries.english: Concubuit cum ea et depositum (semen) tradidit; then he said, “O wife, this is not a small matter.

entries.farsi: آهنى بر سنگ زد زاد آتشى / ‏آتشى از شاه و ملكش كين كشى
entries.english: A steel struck upon the stone, and a fire was born— a fire that shall take vengeance on the King and his empire.

entries.farsi: من چو ابرم تو زمين موسى نبات / ‏حق شه شطرنج و ما ماتيم مات
entries.english: I am as the cloud, you the earth, and Moses the plant. God is the king on the chessboard, and we are checkmated, checkmated.

entries.farsi: آن چه اين فرعون مى‏ترسد از او / هست شد اين دم كه گشتم جفت تو
entries.english: Deem checkmate and victory from the King, O spouse: do not deem them to be from us, do not jeer at us.

entries.farsi: مات و برد از شاه مى‏دان اى عروس / ‏آن مدان از ما مكن بر ما فسوس
entries.english: That of which this Pharaoh is afraid came into being at the moment when I lay with you.

sections.title_fa: وصيت كردن عمران جفت را بعد از مجامعت كه مرا نديده باشى
sections.title_en: How after having lain with her Imran charged his wife to pretend that she had not visited him.

entries.farsi: وامگردان هيچ از اينها دم مزن / ‏تا نيايد بر من و تو صد حزن
entries.english: Do not reveal any of these things; do not breathe a word, lest there come upon me and you a hundred sorrows.

entries.farsi: در زمان از سوى ميدان نعره‏ها / مى‏رسيد از خلق و پر مى‏شد هوا
entries.english: In the end the effects of this will be made manifest, forasmuch as the signs have appeared, O beloved.”

entries.farsi: عاقبت پيدا شود آثار اين / ‏چون عالمتها رسيد اى نازنين
entries.english: Forthwith from the direction of the maydan loud cries were coming from the people, and the air was filled.

entries.farsi: شاه از آن هيبت برون جست آن زمان / ‏پا برهنه كاين چه غلغلهاست هان
entries.english: Thereupon the King, in terror, sprang forth bare-footed, saying, “Listen, what are these tumults?

entries.farsi: از سوى ميدان چه بانگ است و غريو / كز نهيبش مى‏رمد جنى و ديو
entries.english: What is the noise and uproar from the direction of the maydan, in fear whereof genie and demon are fleeing in dismay?”

entries.farsi: از عطاى شاه شادى مى‏كنند / رقص مى‏آرند و كفها مى‏زنند
entries.english: ‘Imran said, “May our King live! The people of Israel are rejoicing on account of you.

entries.farsi: گفت عمران شاه ما را عمر باد / قوم اسرائيليان‏اند از تو شاد
entries.english: Because of the bounty of the King they are making merry and dancing and clapping their hands.”

entries.farsi: گفت باشد كاين بود اما و ليك / ‏وهم و انديشه مرا پر كرد نيك
entries.english: He said, “Maybe it is this, but it makes me very suspicious and anxious.

sections.title_fa: ‏ترسيدن فرعون از آن بانگ
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh was frightened by the noise.

entries.farsi: اين صدا جان مرا تغيير كرد / از غم و اندوه تلخم پير كرد
entries.english: This sound has marred my soul and aged me with bitter pain and grief.”

entries.farsi: پيش مى‏آمد سپس مى‏رفت شه / ‏جمله شب او همچو حامل وقت زه
entries.english: The King was pacing to and fro, all night he was even as a woman in the hour of childbirth.

entries.farsi: هر زمان مى‏گفت اى عمران مرا / سخت از جا برده است اين نعره‏ها
entries.english: Every moment he would say, “O ‘Imran, these clamours have upset me mightily.”

entries.farsi: زهره نى عمران مسكين را كه تا / باز گويد اختالط جفت را
entries.english: Poor ‘Imran had not the courage to relate his intercourse with his wife,

entries.farsi: كه زن عمران به عمران در خزيد / تا كه شد استاره‏ى موسى پديد
entries.english: How the wife of Imran had stolen to his side, so that the star of Moses appeared.

entries.farsi: هر پيمبر كه در آيد در رحم / ‏نجم او بر چرخ گردد منتجم
entries.english: Whenever any prophet enters into the womb, his star becomes conspicuous in the sky.

sections.title_fa: ‏پيدا شدن ستاره‏ى موسى عليه السلام بر آسمان و غريو منجمان در ميدان
sections.title_en: The appearance of the star of Moses, on whom be peace, in the sky and the outcry of the astrologers in the maydan.

entries.farsi: بر فلك پيدا شد آن استاره‏اش / ‏كورى فرعون و مكر و چاره‏اش
entries.english: His star appeared in the sky, to the confusion of Pharaoh and his plots and devices.

entries.farsi: راند عمران جانب ميدان و گفت / ‏اين چه غلغل بود شاهنشه نخفت
entries.english: Day broke: he said to him, “O ‘Imran, go, inform yourself concerning that uproar and noise.”

entries.farsi: روز شد گفتش كه اى عمران برو / واقف آن غلغل و آن بانگ شو
entries.english: ‘Imran rode to the maydan and said, “What uproar was this? The King of kings has not slept.”

entries.farsi: هر منجم سر برهنه جامه چاك / ‏همچو اصحاب عزا بوسيد خاك
entries.english: Every astrologer, with head bare and garment rent, kissed the earth, like mourners.

entries.farsi: ريش و مو بر كنده رو بدريدگان / ‏خاك بر سر كرده پر خون ديده‏گان
entries.english: Their voices were choked with lamentation, like mourners, and their guise.

entries.farsi: همچو اصحاب عزا آوازشان / ‏بد گرفته از فغان و سازشان
entries.english: They had plucked out their beards and hair; their faces were torn; they had cast earth on their heads, and their eyes were filled with blood.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت خير است اين چه آشوب است و حال / ‏بد نشانى مى‏دهد منحوس سال
entries.english: He said, “Is it well? What is this perturbation and emotion? Does the unlucky year give an evil sign?”

entries.farsi: عذر آوردند و گفتند اى امير / كرد ما را دست تقديرش اسير
entries.english: They offered excuses and said, “O Amir, the hand of His predestination has made us captive.

entries.farsi: اين همه كرديم و دولت تيره شد / دشمن شه هست گشت و چيره شد
entries.english: We have done all this, and Fortune is darkened: the King’s enemy has come into being and has prevailed.

entries.farsi: شب ستاره‏ى آن پسر آمد عيان / ‏كورى ما بر جبين آسمان
entries.english: During the night the star of that boy became clearly visible, to our confusion, on the front of heaven.

entries.farsi: زد ستاره‏ى آن پيمبر بر سما / ما ستاره بار گشتيم از بكا
entries.english: The star of that prophet shot up in the sky: we, from weeping, began to shed stars.”

entries.farsi: با دل خوش شاد عمران و ز نفاق / ‏دست بر سر مى‏برد كاه الفراق
entries.english: ‘Imran, with a right glad heart and from hypocrisy, was beating his hands on his head and crying, “Alas, all is lost.”

entries.farsi: كرد عمران خويش پر خشم و ترش / ‏رفت چون ديوانگان بى‏عقل و هش
entries.english: ‘Imran feigned to be wrathful and grim; he went senseless and witless, like madmen.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را اعجمى كرد و براند / گفتهاى بس خشن بر جمع خواند
entries.english: He feigned to be ignorant and pushed forward and addressed to the company words exceeding rough.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را ترش و غمگين ساخت او / نردهاى باژگونه باخت او
entries.english: He made himself out to be bitterly annoyed and grieved; he played reversed dice.

entries.farsi: گفتشان شاه مرا بفريفتيد / از خيانت و ز طمع نشكيفتيد
entries.english: He said to them, “You have deceived my King; you have not refrained from treachery and covetousness.

entries.farsi: سوى ميدان شاه را انگيختيد / آب روى شاه ما را ريختيد
entries.english: You roused the King towards the maydan; you let our King’s honour go to waste.

entries.farsi: دست بر سينه زديد اندر ضمان / ‏شاه را ما فارغ آريم از غمان
entries.english: You put your hands on your breasts in warrant, saying, ‘We will set the King free from cares.’”

entries.farsi: شاه هم بشنيد و گفت اى خاينان / ‏من بر آويزم شما را بى‏امان
entries.english: The King too heard and said, “O traitors, I will hang you up without quarter.

entries.farsi: خويش را در مضحكه انداختم / ‏مالها با دشمنان درباختم
entries.english: I exposed myself to derision; I squandered riches on my enemies,

entries.farsi: تا كه امشب جمله اسرائيليان / ‏دور ماندند از مالقات زنان
entries.english: To the end that to-night all the Israelites might remain far away from meeting with their wives.

entries.farsi: مال رفت و آب رو و كار خام / ‏اين بود يارى و افعال كرام
entries.english: Wealth and honour are gone, and all is done in vain: is this friendship and the deeds of the noble?

entries.farsi: سالها ادرار و خلعت مى‏بريد / مملكتها را مسلم مى‏خوريد
entries.english: For years you have been taking stipends and robes of honour and devouring kingdoms as you pleased.

entries.farsi: رايتان اين بود و فرهنگ و نجوم / ‏طبل خوارانيد و مكاريد و شوم
entries.english: Was this your judgement and wisdom and astrology? You are sycophants and deceivers and ill-omened.

entries.farsi: من شما را بر درم و آتش زنم / ‏بينى و گوش و لبانتان بر كنم
entries.english: I will rend you to pieces and set you ablaze, I will tear off your noses and ears and lips.

entries.farsi: من شما را هيزم آتش كنم / ‏عيش رفته بر شما ناخوش كنم
entries.english: I will make you fuel for the fire, I will make your past pleasure sour to you.”

entries.farsi: سجده كردند و بگفتند اى خديو / گر يكى كرت ز ما چربيد ديو
entries.english: They prostrated themselves and said, “O Khedive, if one time the Devil has prevailed against us,

entries.farsi: سالها دفع بالها كرده‏ايم / ‏وهم حيران ز آن چه ماها كرده‏ايم
entries.english: For years we have warded off afflictions: the imagination is dumbfounded by that which we have done.

entries.farsi: فوت شد از ما و حملش شد پديد / نطفه‏اش جست و رحم اندر خزيد
entries.english: It has eluded us, and his conception has occurred: his semen came out and entered the womb;

entries.farsi: ليك استغفار اين روز والد / ما نگه داريم اى شاه و قباد
entries.english: But pardon for this, we shall watch the day of birth, O King and Sovereign.

entries.farsi: روز ميالدش رصد بنديم ما / تا نگردد فوت و نجهد اين قضا
entries.english: We shall observe the day of his nativity that this event may not escape and evade us.

entries.farsi: گر نداريم اين نگه ما را بكش / ‏اى غالم راى تو افكار و هش
entries.english: If we do not keep watch for this, kill us, O you to whose judgement thoughts and intelligence are slaves.”

entries.farsi: تا به نه مه مى‏شمرد او روز روز / تا نپرد تير حكم خصم دوز
entries.english: For nine months he was counting day after day, lest the arrow of the Decree that transfixes its enemy should fly.

entries.farsi: بر قضا هرك او شبيخون آورد / سر نگون آيد ز خون خود خورد
entries.english: Anyone who makes a night-attack upon Doom falls headlong and drinks of his own blood.

entries.farsi: چون زمين با آسمان خصمى كند / شوره گردد سر ز مرگى بر زند
entries.english: When the earth shows enmity to the sky, it becomes salty and presents a spectacle of death.

entries.farsi: نقش با نقاش پنجه مى‏زند / سبلتان و ريش خود بر مى‏كند
entries.english: The picture struggles hand to hand with the Painter, it tears out its own moustaches and beard.

sections.title_fa: خواندن فرعون زنان نوزاده را سوى ميدان هم جهت مكر
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh summoned the women who had new-born children to the maydan, also for the sake of his plot.

entries.farsi: بعد نه مه شه برون آورد تخت / ‏سوى ميدان و منادى كرد سخت
entries.english: After nine months the King brought out his throne to the maydan and made a strict proclamation.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه پار مردان را رسيد / خلعت و هر كس از ايشان زر كشيد
entries.english: “O women, go with your babes to the maydan; go forth, all you of Israel.

entries.farsi: كاى زنان با طفلكان ميدان رويد / جمله اسرائيليان بيرون شويد
entries.english: Just as last year robes of honour were bestowed on the men, and every one of them bore away gold,

entries.farsi: هين زنان امسال اقبال شماست / ‏تا بيابد هر كسى چيزى كه خواست
entries.english: Listen, O women, this year it is your fortune, so that each one may obtain the thing she desires.

entries.farsi: مر زنان را خلعت و صلت دهد / كودكان را هم كاله زر نهد
entries.english: He will give the women robes of honour and donations; on the children too he will put miters of gold.

entries.farsi: هر كه او اين ماه زاييده ست هين / ‏گنجها گيريد از شاه مكين
entries.english: Take heed! Every one of you that has borne a child during this month shall receive treasures from the mighty King.”

entries.farsi: آن زنان با طفلكان بيرون شدند / شادمان تا خيمه‏ى شه آمدند
entries.english: The women went forth with their babes: they came joyfully to the King’s tent.

entries.farsi: هر زن نو زاده بيرون شد ز شهر / سوى ميدان غافل از دستان و قهر
entries.english: Every woman that had newly given birth went forth from the city to the maydan, unsuspicious of guile and vengeance.

entries.farsi: چون زنان جمله بدو گرد آمدند / هر چه بود آن نر ز مادر بستدند
entries.english: When all the women were gathered around him, they took away from the mothers whatever was male,

entries.farsi: سر بريدندش كه اين است احتياط / تا نرويد خصم و نفزايد خباط
entries.english: And cut off its head, saying, “This is a precaution that the enemy may not grow up and that disorder may not increase.”

sections.title_fa: به وجود آمدن موسى و آمدن عوانان به خانه‏ى عمران و وحى آمدن به مادر موسى كه موسى را در آتش انداز
sections.title_en: How Moses was born and how the officers came to Imran’s house and how it was divinely revealed
to the mother of Moses that she should cast Moses into the fire.

entries.farsi: خود زن عمران كه موسى برده بود / دامن اندرچيد از آن آشوب و دود
entries.english: Imran’s wife herself, who had brought Moses, kept aloof from that turmoil and fume.

entries.farsi: آن زنان قابله در خانه‏ها / بهر جاسوسى فرستاد آن دغا
entries.english: That villain sent the midwives into the houses for the purpose of spying.

entries.farsi: غمز كردندش كه اينجا كودكى است / ‏نامد او ميدان كه در وهم و شكى است
entries.english: They gave information of her, saying, “Here is a child: she did not come to the maydan: for she is under suspicion and doubt.

entries.farsi: اندر اين كوچه يكى زيبا زنى است / ‏كودكى دارد و ليكن پر فنى است
entries.english: In this street there is a comely woman: she has a child, but she is an artful one.”

entries.farsi: پس عوانان آمدند او طفل را / در تنور انداخت از امر خدا
entries.english: Then the officers came: she, by the command of God, cast the child into the stove.

entries.farsi: وحى آمد سوى زن ز آن با خبر / كه ز اصل آن خليل است اين پسر
entries.english: From that omniscient One revelation came to the woman that this boy is of the stock of the Friend,

entries.farsi: عصمت يا نار كونى باردا / ال تكون النار حرا شاردا
entries.english: Through the protection of, “O fire, be cool,” the fire will not be hot and untamed.

entries.farsi: زن به وحى انداخت او را در شرر / بر تن موسى نكرد آتش اثر
entries.english: In consequence of the revelation the woman cast him amidst the sparks: the fire produced no effect on the body of Moses.

entries.farsi: پس عوانان بى‏مراد آن سو شدند / باز غمازان كز آن واقف بدند
entries.english: Then the officers went away without having attained their object, again the informers, who were aware of it,

entries.farsi: با عوانان ماجرا برداشتند / پيش فرعون از براى دانگ چند
entries.english: Raised an altercation with the officers before Pharaoh for the sake of some petty coins,

entries.farsi: كاى عوانان باز گرديد آن طرف / ‏نيك نيكو بنگريد اندر غرف
entries.english: Saying, “O officers, go back thither, and look very carefully in the upper rooms.”

sections.title_fa: ‏وحى آمدن به مادر موسى كه موسى را در آب افكن
sections.title_en: How it was divinely revealed to the mother of Moses that she should throw Moses into the water.

entries.farsi: باز وحى آمد كه در آبش فگن / ‏روى در اوميد دار و مو مكن
entries.english: Once more the revelation came: “Throw him into the water; keep your face in hope and do not tear your hair.

entries.farsi: در فگن در نيلش و كن اعتماد / من ترا با وى رسانم رو سپيد
entries.english: Throw him into the Nile and put trust: I will bring you to him happily.”

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد مكرهاش / ‏جمله مى‏پيچيد هم در ساق و پاش
entries.english: This discourse has no end. All his plots entangled his legs and feet.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران طفل مى‏كشت او برون / ‏موسى اندر صدر خانه در درون
entries.english: He was killing hundreds of thousands of children outside; Moses remained indoors in the upper part of the house.

entries.farsi: از جنون مى‏كشت هر جا بد جنين / ‏از حيل آن كور چشم دور بين
entries.english: Wherever were embryos, in his frenzy that far-seeing blind man was killing them by cunning devices.

entries.farsi: اژدها بد مكر فرعون عنود / مكر شاهان جهان را خورده بود
entries.english: The craft of the iniquitous Pharaoh was a dragon: it had devoured the craft of the kings of the world;

entries.farsi: ليك از او فرعون‏تر آمد پديد / هم و را هم مكر او را در كشيد
entries.english: But one that was a greater Pharaoh than it came into sight and swallowed both him and his craft.

entries.farsi: اژدها بود و عصا شد اژدها / اين بخورد آن را به توفيق خدا
entries.english: It was a dragon: the rod became a dragon, and this devoured that by the aid of God.

entries.farsi: دست شد بالاى دست اين تا كجا / ‏تا به يزدان كه إليه المنتهى
entries.english: Hand is above hand: how far is this? Up to God, for unto Him is the end.

entries.farsi: كان يكى درياست بى‏غور و كران / ‏جمله درياها چو سيلى پيش آن
entries.english: For that is a sea without bottom or shore: beside it all the seas together are as a torrent.

entries.farsi: حيله‏ها و چاره‏ها گر اژدهاست / ‏پيش إال اللهَّ آنها جمله الست
entries.english: If devices and expedients are a dragon, beside except Allah they all are nothing.

entries.farsi: چون رسيد اينجا بيانم سر نهاد / محو شد و اهلل اعلم بالرشاد
entries.english: Now that my exposition has reached this point, it lays down its head and expires; and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: آن چه در فرعون بود آن در تو هست / ‏ليك اژدرهات محبوس چه است
entries.english: That which was in Pharaoh, the same is in you, but your dragon is confined in the pit.

entries.farsi: اى دريغ اين جمله احوال تو است / ‏تو بر آن فرعون بر خواهيش بست
entries.english: Alas, all this is what passes in you: you would fain fasten it on Pharaoh.

entries.farsi: چه خرابت مى‏كند نفس لعين / ‏دور مى‏اندازدت سخت اين قرين
entries.english: If they say it of you, there arises in you a feeling of estrangement; and of another, it seems to you a fable.

entries.farsi: گر ز تو گويند وحشت زايدت / ‏ور ز ديگر آن فسانه آيدت
entries.english: What ruin is wrought in you by the accursed sensual soul! This familiar casts you exceeding far.

entries.farsi: آتشت را هيزم فرعون نيست / ‏ور نه چون فرعون او شعله زنى است
entries.english: Your fire has not Pharaoh’s fuel; otherwise, it is one that throws out flames like Pharaoh.

sections.title_fa: حكايت مارگير كه اژدهاى فسرده را مرده پنداشت و در ريسمانهاش پيچيد و آورد به بغداد
sections.title_en: Story of the snake-catcher who thought the frozen serpent was dead and wound it in ropes and brought it to Baghdad.

entries.farsi: يك حكايت بشنو از تاريخ گوى / ‏تا برى زين راز سر پوشيده بوى
entries.english: Listen to a tale of the chronicler, in order that you may get an inkling of this veiled mystery.

entries.farsi: مارگيرى رفت سوى كوهسار / تا بگيرد او به افسونهاش مار
entries.english: A snake-catcher went to the mountains to catch a snake by his incantations.

entries.farsi: گر گران و گر شتابنده بود / آن كه جوينده ست يابنده بود
entries.english: Whether one be slow or speedy, he that is a seeker will be a finder.

entries.farsi: در طلب زن دايما تو هر دو دست / ‏كه طلب در راه نيكو رهبر است
entries.english: Always apply yourself with both hands to seeking, for search is an excellent guide on the way.

entries.farsi: لنگ و لوك و خفته شكل و بى‏ادب / ‏سوى او مى‏غيژ و او را مى‏طلب
entries.english: Lame and limping and bent in figure and unmannerly, ever creep towards Him and be in quest of Him.

entries.farsi: گه بگفت و گه به خاموشى و گه / ‏بوى كردن گير هر سو بوى شه
entries.english: Now by speech and now by silence and now by smelling, catch in every quarter the scent of the King.

entries.farsi: گفت آن يعقوب با اوالد خويش / ‏جستن يوسف كنيد از حد بيش
entries.english: Jacob said to his sons, “Make search for Joseph beyond bounds.

entries.farsi: هر حس خود را در اين جستن به جد / هر طرف رانيد شكل مستعد
entries.english: In this search earnestly direct every sense towards every side, like one that is ready.”

entries.farsi: گفت از روح خدا ال تَيْأَسُوا / همچو گم كرده پسر رو سو به سو
entries.english: He said, “Do not despair of God’s breath”; go to and fro as one that has lost his son.

entries.farsi: از ره حس دهان پرسان شويد / گوش را بر چار راه آن نهيد
entries.english: Inquire by means of the sense of the mouth, and lay your ears on the four roads of that (which you seek).

entries.farsi: هر كجا بوى خوش آيد بو بريد / سوى آن سر كاشناى آن سريد
entries.english: Whenever a sweet scent comes, smell in that direction, for you are acquainted with that direction.

entries.farsi: هر كجا لطفى ببينى از كسى / ‏سوى اصل لطف ره يابى عسى
entries.english: Whenever you are aware of a kindness from any one, it is possible you may find the way to the source of the kindness.

entries.farsi: اين همه جوها ز دريايى است ژرف / ‏جزو را بگذار و بر كل دار طرف
entries.english: All these lovely things are from a deep Sea: leave the part and keep your eye upon the Whole.

entries.farsi: جنگهاى خلق بهر خوبى است / ‏برگ بى‏برگى نشان طوبى است
entries.english: The wars of mankind are for the sake of Beauty; the garniture of ungarnishedness is the sign of the Tuba tree.

entries.farsi: خشمهاى خلق بهر آشتى است / ‏دام راحت دايما بى‏راحتى است
entries.english: The angers of mankind are for the sake of Peace; restlessness is ever the snare for Rest.

entries.farsi: هر زدن بهر نوازش را بود / هر گله از شكر آگه مى‏كند
entries.english: Every blow is for the sake of fondness; every complaint makes aware of gratitude.

entries.farsi: بوى بر از جزو تا كل اى كريم / ‏بوى بر از ضد تا ضد اى حكيم
entries.english: Smell from the part to the Whole, O noble one; smell from opposite to opposite, O wise one.

entries.farsi: جنگها مى‏آشتى آرد درست / ‏مارگير از بهر يارى مار جست
entries.english: Assuredly wars bring peace; the snake-catcher sought the snake for the purpose of friendship.

entries.farsi: بهر يارى مار جويد آدمى / ‏غم خورد بهر حريف بى‏غمى
entries.english: Man seeks a snake for the purpose of friendship and cares for one that is without care.

entries.farsi: او همى‏جستى يكى مارى شگرف / ‏گرد كوهستان و در ايام برف
entries.english: He was searching round about the mountains for a big snake in the days of snow.

entries.farsi: اژدهايى مرده ديد آن جا عظيم / ‏كه دلش از شكل او شد پر ز بيم
entries.english: He espied there a huge dead dragon, at the aspect whereof his heart was filled with fear.

entries.farsi: مارگير اندر زمستان شديد / مار مى‏جست اژدهايى مرده ديد
entries.english: The snake-catcher was looking for snakes in the hard winter, he espied a dead dragon.

entries.farsi: مارگير از بهر حيرانى خلق / ‏مار گيرد اينت نادانى خلق
entries.english: The snake-catcher catches snakes in order to amaze the people— behold the foolishness of the people!

entries.farsi: آدمى كوهى است چون مفتون شود / كوه اندر مار حيران چون شود
entries.english: Man is a mountain: how should he be led into temptation? How should a mountain become amazed at a snake?

entries.farsi: خويشتن را آدمى ارزان فروخت / ‏بود اطلس خويش بر دلقى بدوخت
entries.english: Wretched Man does not know himself: he has come from a high estate and fallen into a low one.

entries.farsi: خويشتن نشناخت مسكين آدمى / ‏از فزونى آمد و شد در كمى
entries.english: Man has sold himself cheaply: he was satin; he has sewn himself on to a tattered cloak.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مار و كه حيران اوست / ‏او چرا حيران شده ست و مار دوست
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of snakes and mountains are amazed at him: why has he become amazed and fond of a snake?

entries.farsi: مارگير آن اژدها را بر گرفت / ‏سوى بغداد آمد از بهر شگفت
entries.english: The snake-catcher took up that snake and came to Baghdad for the sake of astonishment.

entries.farsi: اژدهايى چون ستون خانه‏اى / ‏مى‏كشيدش از پى دانگانه‏اى
entries.english: In quest of a paltry fee he carried along a dragon like the pillar of a house,

entries.farsi: كاژدهاى مرده‏اى آورده‏ام / ‏در شكارش من جگرها خورده‏ام
entries.english: Saying, “I have brought a dead dragon: I have suffered agonies in hunting it.”

entries.farsi: او همى مرده گمان بردش و ليك / ‏زنده بود و او نديدش نيك نيك
entries.english: He thought it was dead, but it was living, and he did not see it very well.

entries.farsi: او ز سرماها و برف افسرده بود / زنده بود و شكل مرده مى‏نمود
entries.english: It was frozen by frosts and snow: it was alive, but it presented the appearance of the dead.

entries.farsi: عالم افسرده ست و نام او جماد / جامد افسرده بود اى اوستاد
entries.english: The world is frozen: its name is jamád (inanimate): jámid is “frozen,” O master.

entries.farsi: باش تا خورشيد حشر آيد عيان / ‏تا ببينى جنبش جسم جهان
entries.english: Wait till the sun of the Resurrection shall become manifest, that you may see the movement of the world’s body.

entries.farsi: چون عصاى موسى اينجا مار شد / عقل را از ساكنان اخبار شد
entries.english: When here the rod of Moses became a snake, information was given to the intellect concerning motionless beings.

entries.farsi: پاره‏ى خاك ترا چون مرد ساخت / ‏خاكها را جملگى شايد شناخت
entries.english: Since He (God) made your piece of earth a man, you should recognise the entire sum of the particles of earth:

entries.farsi: مرده زين سويند وز آن سو زنده‏اند / خامش اينجا و آن طرف گوينده‏اند
entries.english: From this standpoint they are dead and from that standpoint they are living; silent here and speaking yonder.

entries.farsi: چون از آن سوشان فرستد سوى ما / آن عصا گردد سوى ما اژدها
entries.english: When He sends them from that quarter towards us, the rod becomes a dragon in relation to us.

entries.farsi: كوهها هم لحن داودى كند / جوهر آهن به كف مومى بود
entries.english: The mountains too make a song like that of David, and the substance of iron is wax in the hand.

entries.farsi: باد حمال سليمانى شود / بحر با موسى سخن دانى شود
entries.english: The wind becomes a bearer for Solomon; the sea becomes capable of understanding words in regard to Moses.

entries.farsi: ماه با احمد اشارت بين شود / نار ابراهيم را نسرين شود
entries.english: The moon becomes able to see the sign in obedience to Ahmad; the fire becomes wild-roses for Abraham.

entries.farsi: خاك قارون را چو مارى در كشد / استن حنانه آيد در رشد
entries.english: The earth swallows Qárún like a snake; the Moaning Pillar comes into righteousness.

entries.farsi: سنگ بر احمد سالمى مى‏كند / كوه يحيى را پيامى مى‏كند
entries.english: The stone salaams to Ahmad; the mountain sends a message to Yahya.

entries.farsi: ما سميعيم و بصيريم و خوشيم / ‏با شما نامحرمان ما خامشيم
entries.english: “We have hearing and sight and are happy, with you, the uninitiated, we are mute.”

entries.farsi: چون شما سوى جمادى مى‏رويد / محرم جان جمادان چون شويد
entries.english: Forasmuch as you are going towards inanimateness, how shall you become familiar with the spiritual life of inanimate beings?

entries.farsi: از جمادى عالم جانها رويد / غلغل اجزاى عالم بشنويد
entries.english: Go from inanimateness into the world of spirits; listen to the loud noise of the particles of the world.

entries.farsi: فاش تسبيح جمادات آيدت / ‏وسوسه‏ى تاويلها نربايدت
entries.english: The glorification of God by inanimate beings will become evident to you; the doubts suggested by interpretations will not carry you away.

entries.farsi: چون ندارد جان تو قنديلها / بهر بينش كرده اى تاويلها
entries.english: Since your soul has not the lamps for seeing, you have made interpretations,

entries.farsi: بلكه مر بيننده را ديدار آن / ‏وقت عبرت مى‏كند تسبيح خوان
entries.english: Saying, “How should visible glorification be the meaning intended? The claim to see is an erroneous fancy.

entries.farsi: كه غرض تسبيح ظاهر كى بود / دعوى ديدن خيال غى بود
entries.english: No, the sight of that causes him that sees it to glorify God at the time when he regards its significance.

entries.farsi: پس چو از تسبيح يادت مى‏دهد / آن داللت همچو گفتن مى‏بود
entries.english: Therefore, inasmuch as it reminds you of glorification, that indication is even as uttering”

entries.farsi: اين بود تاويل اهل اعتزال / ‏و آن آن كس كاو ندارد نور حال
entries.english: This is the interpretation of the Mu‘tazilites and of those who do not possess the light of immediate intuition.

entries.farsi: چون ز حس بيرون نيامد آدمى / ‏باشد از تصوير غيبى اعجمى
entries.english: When a man has not escaped from sense-perception, he will be a stranger to the ideas of the unseen world.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد مارگير / مى‏كشيد آن مار را با صد زحير
entries.english: This discourse has no end. The snake-catcher, with a hundred pains, was bringing the snake along,

entries.farsi: تا به بغداد آمد آن هنگامه جو / تا نهد هنگامه‏اى بر چار سو
entries.english: Till (at last) the would-be showman arrived at Baghdad that he might set up a public show at the cross-roads.

entries.farsi: بر لب شط مرد هنگامه نهاد / غلغله در شهر بغداد اوفتاد
entries.english: The man set up a show on the bank of the Tigris, and a hubbub arose in the city of Baghdad—

entries.farsi: مارگيرى اژدها آورده است / ‏بو العجب نادر شكارى كرده است
entries.english: “A snake-catcher has brought a dragon: he has captured a marvellous rare beast.”

entries.farsi: جمع آمد صد هزاران خام ريش / ‏صيد او گشته چو او از ابلهيش
entries.english: Myriads of simpletons assembled, who had become a prey to him as he in his folly.

entries.farsi: منتظر ايشان و هم او منتظر / تا كه جمع آيند خلق منتشر
entries.english: They were waiting, and he too was waiting for the scattered people to assemble.

entries.farsi: جمع آمد صد هزاران ژاژخا / حلقه كرده پشت پا بر پشت پا
entries.english: The greater the crowd, the better goes the begging and contributing.

entries.farsi: مرد را از زن خبر نى ز ازدحام / ‏رفته در هم چون قيامت خاص و عام
entries.english: Myriads of idle babblers assembled, forming a ring, sole against sole.

entries.farsi: مردم هنگامه افزون‏تر شود / كديه و توزيع نيكوتر رود
entries.english: Man took no heed of woman: on account of the throng they were mingled together like nobles and common folk at the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: چون همى حراقه جنبانيد او / مى‏كشيدند اهل هنگامه گلو
entries.english: When he began to move the cloth, the people in the crowd strained their throats,

entries.farsi: و اژدها كز زمهرير افسرده بود / زير صد گونه پالس و پرده بود
entries.english: And the dragon, which had been frozen by intense cold, was underneath a hundred kinds of coarse woollen cloths and coverlets.

entries.farsi: بسته بودش با رسنهاى غليظ / احتياطى كرده بودش آن حفيظ
entries.english: He had bound it with thick ropes: that careful keeper had taken great precaution for it.

entries.farsi: در درنگ انتظار و اتفاق / ‏تافت بر آن مار خورشيد عراق
entries.english: During the delay of expectation and coming together, the sun of ‘Iraq shone upon the snake

entries.farsi: آفتاب گرم سيرش گرم كرد / رفت از اعضاى او اخالط سرد
entries.english: The sun of the hot country warmed it; the cold humours went out of its limbs.

entries.farsi: مرده بود و زنده گشت او از شگفت / ‏اژدها بر خويش جنبيدن گرفت
entries.english: It had been dead, and it revived: from astonishment the dragon began to uncoil itself.

entries.farsi: خلق را از جنبش آن مرده مار / گشتشان آن يك تحير صد هزار
entries.english: By the stirring of that dead serpent the people’s amazement was multiplied a hundred thousand fold.

entries.farsi: با تحير نعره‏ها انگيختند / جملگان از جنبشش بگريختند
entries.english: With amazement they started shrieking and fled en masse from its motion.

entries.farsi: مى‏گسست او بند وز آن بانگ بلند / هر طرف مى‏رفت چاقاچاق بند
entries.english: It set about bursting the bonds, and at that loud outcry the bonds on every side went crack, crack.

entries.farsi: بندها بگسست و بيرون شد ز زير / اژدهايى زشت غران همچو شير
entries.english: It burst the bonds and glided out from beneath— a hideous dragon roaring like a lion.

entries.farsi: در هزيمت بس خاليق كشته شد / از فتاده كشتگان صد پشته شد
entries.english: Many people were killed in the rout: a hundred heaps were made of the fallen slain.

entries.farsi: مارگير از ترس بر جا خشك گشت / ‏كه چه آوردم من از كهسار و دشت
entries.english: The snake-catcher became paralysed with fear on the spot, crying, “What have I brought from the mountains and the desert?”

entries.farsi: گرگ را بيدار كرد آن كور ميش / رفت نادان سوى عزراييل خويش
entries.english: The blind sheep awakened the wolf: unwittingly it went towards its ‘Azrá’íl.

entries.farsi: اژدها يك لقمه كرد آن گيج را / سهل باشد خون خورى حجاج را
entries.english: The dragon made one mouthful of that dolt: blood-drinking is easy for Hajjáj.

entries.farsi: خويش را بر استنى پيچيد و بست / ‏استخوان خورده را در هم شكست
entries.english: It wound and fastened itself on a pillar and crunched the bones of the devoured man.

entries.farsi: نفست اژدرهاست او كى مرده است / ‏از غم بى‏آلتى افسرده است
entries.english: The dragon is your sensual soul: how is it dead? It is frozen by grief and lack of means.

entries.farsi: گر بيابد آلت فرعون او / كه به امر او همى‏رفت آب جو
entries.english: If it obtains the means of Pharaoh, by whose command the water of the river would flow,

entries.farsi: آن گه او بنياد فرعونى كند / راه صد موسى و صد هارون زند
entries.english: Then it will begin to act like Pharaoh and will waylay a hundred Moses and Aaron.

entries.farsi: كرمك است آن اژدها از دست فقر / پشه اى گردد ز جاه و مال صقر
entries.english: That dragon, under stress of poverty, is a little worm; a gnat is made a falcon by power and riches.

entries.farsi: اژدها را دار در برف فراق / ‏هين مكش او را به خورشيد عراق
entries.english: Keep the dragon in the snow of separation; beware, do not carry it into the sun of Iraq.

entries.farsi: تا فسرده مى‏بود آن اژدهات / ‏لقمه‏ى اويى چو او يابد نجات
entries.english: As long as that dragon of your remains frozen; you are a mouthful for it, when it gains release.

entries.farsi: مات كن او را و ايمن شو ز مات / ‏رحم كم كن نيست او ز اهل صالت
entries.english: Mortify it and become safe from death; have no mercy: it is not one of them that deserve favours;

entries.farsi: كان تف خورشيد شهوت بر زند / آن خفاش مرده‏ريگت پر زند
entries.english: For the heat of the sun of lust strikes upon it, that vile bat of yours flaps its wings.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشانش در جهاد و در قتال / ‏مردوار اللهَّ يجزيك الوصال
entries.english: Lead it manfully to the warfare and battle: God will reward you with access.

entries.farsi: چون كه آن مرد اژدها را آوريد / در هواى گرم و خوش شد آن مريد
entries.english: When that man brought the dragon into the hot air and the insolent brute became well,

entries.farsi: الجرم آن فتنه‏ها كرد اى عزيز / بيست همچندان كه ما گفتيم نيز
entries.english: Inevitably it wrought that mischief, my dear friend, too, twenty times as many as we have told.

entries.farsi: تو طمع دارى كه او را بى‏جفا / بسته دارى در وقار و در وفا
entries.english: Do you hope, without using violence, to keep it bound in quiet and faithfulness?

entries.farsi: هر خسى را اين تمنا كى رسد / موسيى بايد كه اژدرها كشد
entries.english: How should this wish be fulfilled for any worthless one? It needs a Moses to kill the dragon.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران خلق ز اژدرهاى او / در هزيمت كشته شد از راى او
entries.english: By his dragon hundreds of thousands of people were killed in the rout, as he had designed.

sections.title_fa: ‏تهديد كردن فرعون موسى را عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh threatened Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: گفت فرعونش چرا تو اى كليم / ‏خلق را كشتى و افكندى تو بيم
entries.english: Pharaoh said to him, “Why did you, O Kalím, kill the people and cause fear to fall?

entries.farsi: در هزيمت از تو افتادند خلق / ‏در هزيمت كشته شد مردم ز زلق
entries.english: The people were put to flight and rout by you; in the rout the folk were killed through slipping.

entries.farsi: الجرم مردم ترا دشمن گرفت / ‏كين تو در سينه مرد و زن گرفت
entries.english: Necessarily, the folk have come to regard you as their enemy; men and women have conceived hatred of you in their breasts.

entries.farsi: خلق را مى‏خواندى بر عكس شد / از خالفت مردمان را نيست بد
entries.english: You were calling the people to you, it has turned out contrariwise: the folk cannot but resist you.

entries.farsi: من هم از شرت اگر پس مى‏خزم / ‏در مكافات تو ديگى مى‏پزم
entries.english: I too, though I am shrinking back from your malice, am concocting a plan to requite you.

entries.farsi: دل از اين بر كن كه بفريبى مرا / يا بجز فى پس روى گردد ترا
entries.english: Put away from your heart the thought that you will deceive me or that you will get any follower but your shadow.

entries.farsi: تو بدان غره مشو كش ساختى / ‏در دل خلقان هراس انداختى
entries.english: Be not deluded by that which you have contrived: you have cast terror into the hearts of the people.

entries.farsi: صد چنين آرى و هم رسوا شوى / ‏خوار گردى ضحكه‏ى غوغا شوى
entries.english: You may bring a hundred such, and you will be exposed in the same way; you will become despicable and the laughing-stock of the mob.

entries.farsi: همچو تو سالوس بسياران بدند / عاقبت در مصر ما رسوا شدند
entries.english: Many have been impostors like you, in our Egypt they have been brought to disgrace in the end.”

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب موسى فرعون را در تهديدى كه مى‏كردش
sections.title_en: The answer of Moses to Pharaoh concerning the threats which he made against him

entries.farsi: گفت با امر حقم اشراك نيست / ‏گر بريزد خونم امرش باك نيست
entries.english: He said, “I admit nothing as co-partner with the command of God: if His command shall shed my blood, there is no fear.

entries.farsi: راضيم من شاكرم من اى حريف / ‏اين طرف رسوا و پيش حق شريف
entries.english: I am content, I am thankful, O adversary: here disgraced, but with God honoured.

entries.farsi: پيش خلقان خوار و زار و ريش‏خند / پيش حق محبوب و مطلوب و پسند
entries.english: In the sight of the people contemptible and wretched and a laughingstock: in God’s sight (I am) loved and sought and approved.

entries.farsi: از سخن مى‏گويم اين ور نى خدا / از سيه رويان كند فردا ترا
entries.english: I say this of words; otherwise, to-morrow God will make you one of the black-faced.

entries.farsi: عزت آن اوست و آن بندگانش / ‏ز آدم و ابليس برمى‏خوان نشانش
entries.english: Glory belongs to Him and to His servants: recite the sign thereof through Adam and Iblis.

entries.farsi: شرح حق پايان ندارد همچو حق / ‏هين دهان بر بند و بر گردان ورق
entries.english: The explanation of God, like God, has no limit. Take heed, close your mouth and turn over a leaf.”

sections.title_fa: ‏پاسخ فرعون موسى را عليه السلام
sections.title_en: The reply of Pharaoh to Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: گفت فرعونش ورق در حكم ماست / ‏دفتر و ديوان حكم اين دم مراست
entries.english: Pharaoh said to him, “The leaf is under my authority; the book and register of authority is mine at this moment.

entries.farsi: مر مرا بخريده‏اند اهل جهان / ‏از همه عاقلترى تو اى فالن
entries.english: The people of the world have chosen me: art you wiser than all, O fellow?

entries.farsi: موسيا خود را خريدى هين برو / خويشتن كم بين به خود غره مشو
entries.english: O Moses, you have vaunted yourself. Listen, go! Have less regard for yourself, be not self-deluded.

entries.farsi: جمع آرم ساحران دهر را / تا كه جهل تو نمايم شهر را
entries.english: I will assemble the magicians of the world, that I may exhibit your foolishness to the city.

entries.farsi: اين نخواهد شد به روزى و دو روز / مهلتم ده تا چهل روز تموز
entries.english: This will not be done in a day or two: give me time till the forty days of Tamúz.”

sections.title_fa: جواب موسى عليه السلام فرعون را
sections.title_en: The answer of Moses, on whom be peace, to Pharaoh.

entries.farsi: گفت موسى اين مرا دستور نيست / ‏بنده‏ام امهال تو مأمور نيست
entries.english: Moses said, “This is not permitted to me: I am the slave: the giving of time to you is not commanded.

entries.farsi: مى‏زنم با تو به جد تا زنده‏ام / ‏من چه كاره‏ى نصرتم من بنده‏ام
entries.english: If you are powerful and I in sooth have no ally, I am subject to His command: I have nothing to do with that.

entries.farsi: گر تو چيرى و مرا خود يار نيست / ‏بنده فرمانم بدانم كار نيست
entries.english: I will combat you with all my might so long as I live; what have I to do with helping? I am a slave.

entries.farsi: مى‏زنم تا در رسد حكم خدا / او كند هر خصم از خصمى جدا
entries.english: I will fight till the decision of God comes to pass: He separates every adversary from an adversary.”

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب فرعون موسى را و وحى آمدن موسى را عليه السلام
sections.title_en: The reply of Pharaoh to Moses, and the coming of a Divine revelation to Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: گفت نى نى مهلتى بايد نهاد / عشوه‏ها كم ده تو كم پيماى باد
entries.english: He said, “No, no, you must appoint a certain respite: do not give cajoleries, do not talk vain things.”

entries.farsi: حق تعالى وحى كردش در زمان / ‏مهلتش ده متسع مهراس از آن
entries.english: At once the high God made a revelation to him, saying, “Give him an ample respite: be not afraid of that.

entries.farsi: اين چهل روزش بده مهلت به طوع / ‏تا سگالد مكرها او نوع نوع
entries.english: Willingly give him these forty days that he may think of diverse plots.

entries.farsi: تا بكوشد او كه نه من خفته‏ام / ‏تيز رو گو پيش ره بگرفته‏ام
entries.english: Let him endeavor, for I am not asleep; bid him advance quickly, I have barred the way in front.

entries.farsi: حيله‏هاشان را همه بر هم زنم / ‏و آن چه افزايند من بر كم زنم
entries.english: I will confound all their devices, and I will reduce to little that which they increase.

entries.farsi: آب را آرند و من آتش كنم / ‏نوش و خوش گيرند و من ناخوش كنم
entries.english: Let them fetch water, and I will make fire; let them get honey and sweets and I will make bitter.

entries.farsi: تو مترس و مهلتش ده دم دراز / گو سپه گرد آر و صد حيلت بساز
entries.english: Let them join in a bond of love, and I will destroy it; I will do that which they conceive not.

entries.farsi: مهر پيوندند و من ويران كنم / ‏آن كه اندر وهم نارند آن كنم
entries.english: Have no fear, and give him a lengthy respite; bid him bring together his host and prepare a hundred devices.”

sections.title_fa: مهلت دادن موسى عليه السلام فرعون را تا ساحران را جمع كند از مداين
sections.title_en: How Moses, on whom be peace, gave Pharaoh a respite, that he might assemble the magicians from the cities.

entries.farsi: گفت امر آمد برو مهلت ترا / من بجاى خود شدم رستى ز ما
entries.english: He said, “The command has come. Go, the respite is to you. I depart to my dwelling-place: you are delivered from me.”

entries.farsi: او همى‏شد و اژدها اندر عقب / ‏چون سگ صياد دانا و محب
entries.english: He was going, and at his heels the dragon wise and loving, like the hunter’s dog.

entries.farsi: چون سگ صياد جنبان كرده دم / ‏سنگ را مى‏كرد ريگ او زير سم
entries.english: Like the hunter’s dog, wagging its tail: it made the stones sand beneath its hoof.

entries.farsi: در هوا مى‏كرد خود بالاى برج / ‏كه هزيمت مى‏شد از وى روم و گرج
entries.english: With its breath it drew in stone and iron and visibly chewed the iron into small fragments.

entries.farsi: سنگ و آهن را به دم درمى‏كشيد / خرد مى‏خاييد آهن را پديد
entries.english: In the air it was making itself above the zodiac, so that Greeks and Georgians would flee from it in panic.

entries.farsi: كفك مى‏انداخت چون اشتر ز كام / ‏قطره‏اى بر هر كه زد مى‏شد جذام
entries.english: From its palate it cast out foam, like a camel: whomsoever a drop hit, he was smitten with tubercular leprosy.

entries.farsi: ژغ‏ژغ دندان او دل مى‏شكست / ‏جان شيران سيه مى‏شد ز دست
entries.english: The gnashing of its teeth would break the heart; the souls of black lions would be distraught.

entries.farsi: چون به قوم خود رسيد آن مجتبى / شدق او بگرفت باز او شد عصا
entries.english: When that chosen one reached his kinsfolk, he took hold of the corner of its mouth, and it became again a staff.

entries.farsi: تكيه بر وى كرد و مى‏گفت اى عجب / ‏پيش ما خورشيد و پيش خصم شب
entries.english: He leaned upon it, saying, “O wonder! To me the sun, to my enemy night.

entries.farsi: اى عجب چون مى‏نبيند اين سپاه / ‏عالمى پر آفتاب چاشتگاه
entries.english: O wonder! How does this host not see a whole world filled with the sun at morning tide?

entries.farsi: چشم باز و گوش باز و اين ذكا / خيره‏ام در چشم بندى خدا
entries.english: Eyes open, and ears open, and this sun! I am amazed at God’s eye-bandaging.

entries.farsi: من از ايشان خيره ايشان هم ز من / ‏از بهارى خار ايشان من سمن
entries.english: I am amazed at them, and they too at me: from one springtime, they are thorns and I am jasmine.

entries.farsi: پيششان بردم بسى جام رحيق / ‏سنگ شد آبش به پيش اين فريق
entries.english: I bore to them many a cup of pure wine: its juice turned to stone before this company.

entries.farsi: دسته‏ى گل بستم و بردم به پيش / ‏هر گلى چون خار گشت و نوش نيش
entries.english: I twined a handful of roses and carried it to them: every rose became as a thorn, and the honey turned to poison.

entries.farsi: آن نصيب جان بى‏خويشان بود / چون كه با خويشند پيدا كى شود
entries.english: That is the portion allotted to the selfless: since they are with themselves, how should it be shown?

entries.farsi: خفته‏ى بيدار بايد پيش ما / تا به بيدارى ببيند خوابها
entries.english: With us, one must need be a waking sleeper, that in the state of wakefulness he may dream dreams.”

entries.farsi: دشمن اين خواب خوش شد فكر خلق / ‏تا نخسبد فكرتش بسته ست حلق
entries.english: Thought of created things is an enemy to this sweet sleep: until his thought is asleep, his throat is shut.

entries.farsi: حيرتى بايد كه روبد فكر را / خورده حيرت فكر را و ذكر را
entries.english: Bewilderment is needed to sweep thought away: bewilderment devours thought and recollection.

entries.farsi: هر كه كاملتر بود او در هنر / او به معنى پس به صورت پيشتر
entries.english: The more perfect he is in science, the more backward he is in reality and the more forward in appearance.

entries.farsi: راجعون گفت و رجوع اين‏سان بود / كه گله واگردد و خانه رود
entries.english: He has said, “Truly, to Him we are returning”; and the return is in the same wise as a herd turns back and goes home.

entries.farsi: چون كه واگرديد گله از ورود / پس فتد آن بز كه پيش آهنگ بود
entries.english: When the herd has turned back from going down to water, the goat that was the leader falls behind.

entries.farsi: پيش افتد آن بز لنگ پسين / ‏أضحك الرجعى وجوه العابسين
entries.english: And the lame hindmost goat is now in front: the return caused the faces to laugh of them that were frowning.

entries.farsi: از گزافه كى شدند اين قوم لنگ / ‏فخر را دادند و بخريدند ننگ
entries.english: How did this party become lame and give up glory and purchase ignominy in vain?

entries.farsi: پا شكسته مى‏روند اين قوم حج / ‏از حرج راهى است پنهان تا فرج
entries.english: This party goes on the pilgrimage with broken legs; there is a secret way from difficulty to ease.

entries.farsi: دانشى بايد كه اصلش ز آن سر است / ‏ز انكه هر فرعى به اصلش رهبر است
entries.english: This company washed their hearts of kinds of knowledge, because this knowledge does not know this Way.

entries.farsi: دل ز دانشها بشستند اين فريق / ‏ز انكه اين دانش نداند آن طريق
entries.english: One needs a knowledge whereof the root is yonder, inasmuch as every branch is a guide to its root.

entries.farsi: هر پرى بر عرض دريا كى پرد / تا لدن علم لدنى مى‏برد
entries.english: How should every wing fly across the breadth of the Sea? The esoteric knowledge will bear to the Presence.

entries.farsi: پس چرا علمى بياموزى به مرد / كش ببايد سينه را ز آن پاك كرد
entries.english: Why, then, should you teach a man the knowledge of which it benefits him to purify his breast?

entries.farsi: پس مجو پيشى از اين سر لنگ باش / ‏وقت واگشتن تو پيش آهنگ باش
entries.english: Therefore do not seek to be in front: be lame on this side, and be the leader at the moment of return.

entries.farsi: آخرون السابقون باش اى ظريف / ‏بر شجر سابق بود ميوه‏ى طريف
entries.english: O clever one, be the hindmost and the foremost”: the fresh fruit is prior to the tree.

entries.farsi: گر چه ميوه آخر آيد در وجود / اول است او ز انكه او مقصود بود
entries.english: Although the fruit comes last into being, it is the first, because it was the object.

entries.farsi: چون ماليك گوى ال عِلْمَ لنا / تا بگيرد دست تو علمتنا
entries.english: Say, like the angels, “We have no knowledge,” to the end that “You have taught us” may take your hand.

entries.farsi: گر درين مكتب ندانى تو هجا / ‏همچو احمد پرى از نور حجى
entries.english: If in this school you do not know the alphabet, you are filled, like Ahmad, with the light of Reason.

entries.farsi: گر نباشى نامدار اندر بالد / كم نه‏اى و اللهَّ أعلم بالعباد
entries.english: If you are not famous in the world, you are not deficient: God knows best concerning His servants.

entries.farsi: اندر آن ويران كه آن معروف نيست / ‏از براى حفظ گنجينه‏ى زرى است
entries.english: A treasure of gold is, for safety’s sake, in a desolate spot that is not well-known.

entries.farsi: موضع معروف كى بنهند گنج / ‏زين قبل آمد فرج در زير رنج
entries.english: How should they deposit the treasure in a well-known place? On this account it is said, “Joy is beneath sorrow.”

entries.farsi: خاطر آرد بس شكال اينجا و ليك / ‏بسكلد اشكال را استور نيك
entries.english: Here the mind may bring many difficulties, but a good beast will break the tether.

entries.farsi: هست عشقش آتشى اشكال سوز / هر خيالى را بروبد نور روز
entries.english: His love is a fire that consumes difficulties: the daylight sweeps away every phantom.

entries.farsi: هم از آن سو جو جواب اى مرتضى / كاين سؤال آمد از آن سو مر ترا
entries.english: O you, with whom He is pleased, seek the answer from the same quarter from which this question came to you.

entries.farsi: گوشه‏ى بى‏گوشه‏ى دل شه رهى است / ‏تاب ال شرقى و ال غرب از مهى است
entries.english: The cornerless corner of the heart is a King’s highway: the radiance that is neither of the east nor of the west is from a Moon.

entries.farsi: تو از اين سو و از آن سو چون گدا / اى كه معنى چه مى‏جويى صدا
entries.english: Why on this side and on that, like a beggar, O mountain of Reality, are you seeking the echo?

entries.farsi: هم از آن سو جو كه وقت درد تو / مى‏شوى در ذكر يا ربى دو تو
entries.english: Seek from the same quarter to which, in the hour of pain, you bend low, crying repeatedly, “O my Lord!

entries.farsi: وقت درد و مرگ از آن سو مى‏نمى / ‏چون كه دردت رفت چونى اعجمى
entries.english: In the hour of pain and death you turn in that direction: how, when your pain is gone, are you ignorant?

entries.farsi: وقت محنت گشته‏اى اهلل گو / چون كه محنت رفت گويى راه كو
entries.english: At the time of tribulation you have called unto God, when the tribulation is gone, you say, “Where is the way?”

entries.farsi: اين از آن آمد كه حق را بى‏گمان / ‏هر كه بشناسد بود دايم بر آن
entries.english: This is because: every one that knows God without uncertainty is constantly engaged in that,

entries.farsi: و انكه در عقل و گمان هستش حجاب / ‏گاه پوشيده ست و گه بدريده جيب
entries.english: While he that is veiled in intellect and uncertainty is sometimes covered and sometimes with his collar torn.

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى گاه چيره گه نگون / ‏عقل كلى ايمن از ريب المنون
entries.english: The particular intellect is sometimes dominant, sometimes overthrown; the Universal Intellect is secure from the hazards of Time.

entries.farsi: عقل بفروش و هنر، حيرت بخر / رو به خوارى نه بخارا اى پسر
entries.english: Sell intellect and talent and buy bewilderment: take yourself to lowliness, O son, not to Bukhara!

entries.farsi: ما چه خود را در سخن آغشته‏ايم / ‏كز حكايت ما حكايت گشته‏ايم
entries.english: Why have I steeped myself in the discourse, so that from story-telling I have become a story?

entries.farsi: اين حكايت نيست پيش مرد كار / وصف حال است و حضور يار غار
entries.english: I become nothing and a fable in making moan, in order that I may gain influence over them that prostrate themselves in prayer.

entries.farsi: من عدم و افسانه گردم در حنين / ‏تا تقلب يابم اندر ساجدين
entries.english: This is not a story in the eyes of the man of experience: it is a description of an actual state, and it is the presence of the Friend of the Cave.

entries.farsi: آن اساطير اولين كه گفت عاق / ‏حرف قرآن را بد آثار نفاق
entries.english: That “stories of the ancients,” which the disobedient applied to the words of the Qur’an, was a mark of hypocrisy.

entries.farsi: المكانى كه در او نور خداست / ‏ماضى و مستقبل و حال از كجاست
entries.english: The man transcending space, in whom is the Light of God— where is the past, the future, or the present?

entries.farsi: ماضى و مستقبلش نسبت به توست / ‏هر دو يك چيزند پندارى كه دوست
entries.english: His being past or future is in relation to you: both are one thing, and you think they are two.

entries.farsi: نسبت زير و زبر شد ز آن دو كس / ‏سقف سوى خويش يك چيز است و بس
entries.english: One individual is to him father and to us son: the roof is below Zayd and above ‘Amr.

entries.farsi: يك تنى او را پدر ما را پسر / بام زير زيد و بر عمرو آن زبر
entries.english: The relation of “below” and “above” arises from those two persons: as regards itself, the roof is one thing only.

entries.farsi: نيست مثل آن مثال است اين سخن / ‏قاصر از معناى نو حرف كهن
entries.english: These expressions are not similar to that: they are a comparison: the old words fall short of the new meaning.

entries.farsi: چون لب جو نيست مشكا لب ببند / بى‏لب و ساحل بده‏ست اين بحر قند
entries.english: Since there is no river’s edge, close your lips, O water skin: this Sea of candy has been without edge or shore.

sections.title_fa: ‏فرستادن فرعون به مداين در طلب ساحران
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh sent to the cities in search of the magicians

entries.farsi: چون كه موسى باز گشت و او بماند / اهل راى و مشورت را پيش خواند
entries.english: When Moses had returned and he remained, he called his advisers and counsellors to his presence.

entries.farsi: آن چنان ديدند كز اطراف مصر / جمع آردشان شه و صراف مصر
entries.english: They deemed it right that the King and Ruler of Egypt should assemble them from all parts of Egypt.

entries.farsi: او بسى مردم فرستاد آن زمان / ‏هر نواحى بهر جمع جادوان
entries.english: Thereupon he sent many men in every direction to collect the sorcerers.

entries.farsi: هر طرف كه ساحرى بد نامدار / كرد پران سوى او ده پيك كار
entries.english: In whatsoever region there was a renowned magician, he set flying towards him ten active couriers.

entries.farsi: دو جوان بودند ساحر مشتهر / سحر ايشان در دل مه مستمر
entries.english: There were two youths, famous magicians: their magic penetrated into the heart of the moon.

entries.farsi: شير دوشيده ز مه فاش آشكار / در سفرها رفته بر خمى سوار
entries.english: They milked the moon publicly and openly; in their journeys they went mounted on a wine-jar.

entries.farsi: شكل كرباسى نموده ماهتاب / ‏آن بپيموده فروشيده شتاب
entries.english: They caused the moonshine to appear like a piece of linen: they measured and sold it speedily,

entries.farsi: سيم برده مشترى آگه شده / ‏دست از حسرت به رخها بر زده
entries.english: And took the silver away: the purchaser, on becoming aware, would smite his hand upon his cheeks in grief.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران همچنين در جادوى / ‏بوده منشى و نبوده چون روى
entries.english: They were the inventors of a hundred thousand such in sorcery, and were not like the rhyme-letter.

entries.farsi: چون بديشان آمد آن پيغام شاه / ‏كز شما شاه است اكنون چاره خواه
entries.english: When the King’s message reached them, “The king now desires help from you,

entries.farsi: از پى آن كه دو درويش آمدند / بر شه و بر قصر او موكب زدند
entries.english: Because two dervishes have come and marched in force against the King and his palace

entries.farsi: نيست با ايشان بغير يك عصا / كه همى‏گردد به امرش اژدها
entries.english: They have nothing with them except one rod, which becomes a dragon at his command.

entries.farsi: شاه و لشكر جمله بى‏چاره شدند / زين دو كس جمله به افغان آمدند
entries.english: The King and the whole army are helpless: all have been brought to lamentation by these two persons.

entries.farsi: چاره‏اى مى‏بايد اندر ساحرى / ‏تا بود كه زين دو ساحر جان برى
entries.english: A remedy must be sought in magic, that maybe you will save lives from these two enchanters—”

entries.farsi: آن دو ساحر را چو اين پيغام داد / ترس و مهرى در دل هر دو فتاد
entries.english: When he gave the message to those two magicians, a fear and love descended on the hearts of them both.

entries.farsi: عرق جنسيت چو جنبيدن گرفت / ‏سر به زانو بر نهادند از شگفت
entries.english: When the vein of homogeneity began to throb, they laid their heads upon their knees in astonishment.

entries.farsi: چون دبيرستان صوفى زانو است / ‏حل مشكل را دو زانو جادو است
entries.english: Inasmuch as the knee is the Sufi’s school, the two knees are sorcerers for solving a difficulty.

sections.title_fa: خواندن آن دو ساحر پدر را از گور و پرسيدن از روان پدر حقيقت موسى عليه السلام را
sections.title_en: How those two magicians summoned their father from the grave and questioned their father’s spirit concerning the real nature of Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: بردشان بر گور او بنمود راه / ‏پس سه روزه داشتند از بهر شاه
entries.english: Afterwards they said, “Come, O mother, where is our father’s grave? Show us the way.”

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتند اى مادر بيا / گور بابا كو تو ما را ره نما
entries.english: She took them and showed the way to his grave: then they fasted for three days for the sake of the King.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتند اى بابا بما / شاه پيغامى فرستاد از وجا
entries.english: After that they said, “O father, the King in consternation has sent us a message

entries.farsi: كه دو مرد او را به تنگ آورده‏اند / آب رويش پيش لشكر برده‏اند
entries.english: Those two men have brought him to sore straits and have destroyed his prestige with the army.

entries.farsi: نيست با ايشان سالح و لشكرى / ‏جز عصا و در عصا شور و شرى
entries.english: There is not with them any weapons or soldiers; nothing but a rod, and in the rod is a calamity and bane.

entries.farsi: تو جهان راستان در رفته‏اى / ‏گر چه در صورت به خاكى خفته‏اى
entries.english: You are gone into the world of the righteous; though to outward seeming you lie in a tomb. If that is magic, inform us; and if it be divine, O spirit of our father,

entries.farsi: آن اگر سحر است ما را ده خبر / ور خدايى باشد اى جان پدر
entries.english: Also inform us, so that we may bow down and bring ourselves in touch with an elixir.

entries.farsi: هم خبر ده تا كه ما سجده كنيم / ‏خويشتن بر كيميايى بر زنيم
entries.english: We are despairing, and a hope has come; we are banished, and Mercy has drawn us.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن ساحر مرده با فرزندان خود
sections.title_en: How the dead magician answered his sons.

entries.farsi: نااميدانيم و اوميدى رسيد / راندگانيم و كرم ما را كشيد
entries.english: He cried, “O my dearest sons, it rests to declare this plainly.

entries.farsi: بانگ زد كاى جان و فرزندان من / ‏هست پيدا گفتن اين را مرتهن
entries.english: It is not permitted to me to speak openly and freely, yet the mystery is not far from mine eye.

entries.farsi: فاش و مطلق گفتنم دستور نيست / ليك راز از پيش چشمم دور نيست
entries.english: But I will show unto you a sign that this hidden thing may be made manifest to you.

entries.farsi: ليك بنمايم نشانى با شما / تا شود پيدا شما را اين خفا
entries.english: O light of mine eyes, when you go thither, become acquainted with the place where he sleeps,

entries.farsi: نور چشمانم چو آن جا گه رويد / از مقام خفتنش آگه شويد
entries.english: And at the time when that Sage is asleep, make for the rod, abandon fear.

entries.farsi: ‏چاره‏ى ساحر بر تو حاضر است / ور نتانى هان و هان آن ايزدى است
entries.english: If you shall steal it and are able, he is a magician; the means of dealing with a magician is present with you;

entries.farsi: ‏او رسول ذو الجلال و مهتدى است / گر جهان فرعون گيرد شرق و غرب
entries.english: But if you cannot, beware and beware! That is of God: he is the messenger of the Glorious and is guided.

entries.farsi: ‏سر نگون آيد خدا را گاه حرب / اين نشان راست دادم جان باب
entries.english: Though Pharaoh occupies the world, east and west, he will fall headlong: God and then war!

entries.farsi: ‏بر نويس اللهَّ اعلم بالصواب / جان بابا چون بخسبد ساحرى
entries.english: I give this true sign, O soul of your father: inscribe it: God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: ‏سحر و مكرش را نباشد رهبرى / چون كه چوپان خفت گرگ ايمن شود
entries.english: O soul of your father, when a magician sleeps, there is none to direct his magic and craft.

entries.farsi: چون كه خفت آن جهد او ساكن شود / ليك حيوانى كه چوپانش خداست
entries.english: When the shepherd has gone to sleep, the wolf becomes unafraid: when he sleeps, his exertion ceases;

entries.farsi: ‏گرگ را آن جا اميد و ره كجاست / جادويى كه حق كند حق است و راست
entries.english: But the animal whose shepherd is God— how has the wolf hope or way there?

entries.farsi: ‏جادويى خواندن مر آن حق را خطاست / جان بابا اين نشان قاطع است
entries.english: The sorcery which God practices is real and true: it is wrong to call that real thing sorcery.

entries.farsi: ‏گر بميرد نيز حقش رافع است
entries.english: O soul of your father, this is the decisive sign: even if he dies, God exalts him.”

sections.title_fa: تشبيه كردن قرآن مجيد را به عصاى موسى و وفات مصطفى عليه السلام را نمودن به خواب موسى و قاصدان تغيير قرآن را به آن دو ساحر بچه كه قصد بردن عصا كردند چون موسى را خفته يافتند

sections.title_en: Comparison of the sublime Qur’an to the rod of Moses, and the death of Mustafa, on whom be peace, to the sleep of Moses, and those who seek to alter the Qur’an to the two young magicians who attempted to carry off the rod of Moses when they found him asleep.

entries.farsi: مصطفى را وعده كرد الطاف حق / ‏گر بميرى تو نميرد اين سبق
entries.english: The Mercy of God made a promise to Mustafa, saying, “If you shall die, this Lesson shall not die.

entries.farsi: من كتاب و معجزه‏ات را رافعم / ‏بيش و كم كن را ز قرآن مانعم
entries.english: I am exalting your Book and Miracle; I am defending the Qur’an from those who would make it more or less.

entries.farsi: من تو را اندر دو عالم حافظم / ‏طاعنان را از حديثت رافضم
entries.english: I am exalting you in both worlds; I am driving away the scoffers from your Tidings.

entries.farsi: كس نتاند بيش و كم كردن در او / تو به از من حافظى ديگر مجو
entries.english: None shall be able to make additions or omissions therein. Do not you seek another protector better than Me.

entries.farsi: رونقت را روز روز افزون كنم / ‏نام تو بر زر و بر نقره زنم
entries.english: Day by day I will increase your splendour; I will strike your name on gold and on silver.

entries.farsi: منبر و محراب سازم بهر تو / در محبت قهر من شد قهر تو
entries.english: For your sake I will prepare pulpit and prayer-niche: in love your vengeance has become My vengeance.

entries.farsi: نام تو از ترس پنهان مى‏گوند / چون نماز آرند پنهان مى‏شوند
entries.english: They, from fear, are uttering your name covertly and hiding when they perform their prayers;

entries.farsi: از هراس و ترس كفار لعين / ‏دينت پنهان مى‏شود زير زمين
entries.english: From terror and dread of the accursed infidels your Religion is being hidden underground;

entries.farsi: من مناره پر كنم آفاق را / كور گردانم دو چشم عاق را
entries.english: I will fill the world, from end to end, with minarets; I will make blind the eyes of the recalcitrant.

entries.farsi: چاكرانت شهرها گيرند و جاه / ‏دين تو گيرد ز ماهى تا به ماه
entries.english: Your servants will occupy cities and power: your Religion will extend from the Fish to the Moon.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت باقيش داريم ما / تو مترس از نسخ دين اى مصطفا
entries.english: We shall keep it living until the Resurrection: be not you afraid of the annulment of the Religion, O Mustafa.

entries.farsi: اى رسول ما تو جادو نيستى / ‏صادقى هم خرقه‏ى موسيستى
entries.english: O My Messenger, you are not a sorcerer: you are truthful; you wear the mantle of Moses.

entries.farsi: تو اگر در زير خاكى خفته‏اى / ‏چون عصايش دان تو آن چه گفته‏اى
entries.english: To you the Qur’an is even as the rod: it swallows up infidelities, like a dragon.

entries.farsi: هست قرآن مر ترا همچون عصا / كفرها را در كشد چون اژدها
entries.english: If you sleep beneath a sod, deem as his rod that which you have spoken.

entries.farsi: قاصدان را بر عصايت دست نى / ‏تو بخسب اى شه مبارك خفتنى
entries.english: Assailants have no power over his rod. Sleep, O King, a blessed sleep!

entries.farsi: تن بخفته نور تو بر آسمان / ‏بهر پيكار تو زه كرده كمان
entries.english: Your body is asleep; your Light in Heaven has strung a bow for your war.

entries.farsi: فلسفى و آن چه پوزش مى‏كند / قوس نورت تير دوزش مى‏كند
entries.english: The philosopher and that which his mouth does— the bow of your Light is piercing him with arrows”

entries.farsi: آن چنان كرد و از آن افزون كه گفت / ‏او بخفت و بخت و اقبالش نخفت
entries.english: Thus He did, and more than He said: he slept, but his fortune and prosperity slumbered not.

entries.farsi: جان بابا چون كه ساحر خواب شد / كار او بى‏رونق و بى‏تاب شد
entries.english: “O soul of your father, when a magician goes to sleep, his work becomes tarnished and dim.”

entries.farsi: هر دو بوسيدند گورش را و رفت / ‏تا به مصر از بهر اين پيكار زفت
entries.english: Both kissed his grave and went away to Egypt for the purpose of this mighty struggle.

entries.farsi: چون به مصر از بهر آن كار آمدند / طالب موسى و خانه‏ى او شدند
entries.english: When they came to Egypt for the sake of that enterprise, they sought after Moses and his house.

entries.farsi: اتفاق افتاد كان روز ورود / موسى اندر زير نخلى خفته بود
entries.english: It chanced that on the day of their arrival Moses was asleep under a palm-tree,

entries.farsi: پس نشان دادندشان مردم بدو / كه برو آن سوى نخلستان بجو
entries.english: So the folk gave them a clue to him, saying, “Go, and seek over there in the direction of the palm-grove.”

entries.farsi: چون بيامد ديد در خرما بنان / ‏خفته‏اى كه بود بيدار جهان
entries.english: When he came, he espied amongst the date trees a sleeper who was the wake fullest man in the world.

entries.farsi: بهر نازش بسته او دو چشم سر / عرش و فرشش جمله در زير نظر
entries.english: For pleasure’s sake he had shut the two eyes of his head, all Heaven and Earth were under his gaze.

entries.farsi: اى بسا بيدار چشم خفته دل / ‏خود چه بيند ديد اهل آب و گل
entries.english: Oh, many a one whose eye is awake and whose heart is asleep: what, in truth, should be seen by the eyes of creatures of water and clay?

entries.farsi: آن كه دل بيدار دارد، چشم سر / گر بخسبد بر گشايد صد بصر
entries.english: He that keeps his heart awake— though the eye of his head may sleep, it will open a hundred eyes.

entries.farsi: ور دلت بيدار شد مى‏خسب خوش / ‏نيست غايب ناظرت از هفت و شش
entries.english: If you are not one of heart, be awake, be a seeker of the heart, and be in strife;

entries.farsi: گر تو اهل دل نه اى بيدار باش / ‏طالب دل باش و در پيكار باش
entries.english: But if your heart has been awakened, sleep sound: your eye is not absent from the seven heavens and the six directions.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه خسبد چشم من / ‏ليك كى خسبد دلم اندر وسن
entries.english: The Prophet said, “My eye slumbers, but when does my heart slumber in drowsiness?”

entries.farsi: شاه بيدار است حارس خفته‏گير / جان فداى خفتگان دل بصير
entries.english: The King is awake: suppose the guardsman is asleep; may soul be sacrificed to the sleepers whose hearts are seeing!

entries.farsi: وصف بيدارى دل اى معنوى / ‏در نگنجد در هزاران مثنوى
entries.english: The description of the heart’s wakefulness, O spiritual man, would not be contained in thousands of rhymed couplets.

entries.farsi: چون بديدندش كه خفته ست او دراز / بهر دزدى عصا كردند ساز
entries.english: When they saw that he was sleeping outstretched, they made preparations for stealing the rod.

entries.farsi: ساحران قصد عصا كردند زود / كز پسش بايد شدن وانگه ربود
entries.english: The magicians quickly approached the rod, saying, “We must go behind him and then snatch it.”

entries.farsi: آن چنان بر خود بلرزيد آن عصا / كان دو بر جا خشك گشتند از وجا
entries.english: When they prepared a little nearer, the rod began to shake.

entries.farsi: اندكى چون پيشتر كردند ساز / اندر آمد آن عصا در اهتزاز
entries.english: The rod quivered upon itself in such wise that both on the spot became petrified by the shock.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن شد اژدها و حمله كرد / هر دوان بگريختند و روى زرد
entries.english: After that, it turned into a dragon and made a rush: both fled, and pale of countenance

entries.farsi: رو در افتادن گرفتند از نهيب / ‏غلط غلطان منهزم در هر نشيب
entries.english: Began to fall on their faces from affright, tumbling panic-stricken down every slope

entries.farsi: بعد از آن اطالق و تبشان شد پديد / كارشان تا نزع و جان كندن رسيد
entries.english: Then to them it became certain that he was from Heaven, since they were seeing the limit of magicians.

entries.farsi: پس يقين شان شد كه هست از آسمان / ‏ز انكه مى‏ديدند حد ساحران
entries.english: Afterwards diarrhea and fever appeared in them, and their case reached the last gasp and the death-agony.

entries.farsi: پس فرستادند مردى در زمان / ‏سوى موسى از براى عذر آن
entries.english: Then at once they sent a man to Moses to excuse what they had done,

entries.farsi: كه امتحان كرديم و ما را كى رسد / امتحان تو اگر نبود حسد
entries.english: Saying, “We have tested you, and how should testing you occur to us unless there be envy?

entries.farsi: عفو كرد و در زمان نيكو شدند / پيش موسى بر زمين سر مى‏زدند
entries.english: We are sinners against the King: do you crave pardon for us, O you that are the elect of the elect of the Court of God.”

entries.farsi: مجرم شاهيم ما را عفو خواه / ‏اى تو خاص الخاص درگاه اله
entries.english: He pardoned, and at once they became well; they were striking their heads upon the earth in the presence of Moses.

entries.farsi: گفت موسى عفو كردم اى كرام / ‏گشت بر دوزخ تن و جانتان حرام
entries.english: Moses said, “I pardon, O nobles: your bodies and souls have become unlawful to Hell.

entries.farsi: من شما را خود نديدم اى دو يار / اعجمى سازيد خود را ز اعتذار
