entries.english: Truly I did not see you; O two friends, make yourselves strangers to exculpation.

entries.farsi: همچنان بيگانه شكل و آشنا / در نبرد آييد بهر پادشا
entries.english: Come, even as you are, alien in appearance familiar, to combat for the King.”

entries.farsi: پس زمين را بوسه دادند و شدند / انتظار وقت و فرصت مى‏بدند
entries.english: Then they kissed the earth and departed: they were waiting in expectation of the time and opportunity.

sections.title_fa: جمع آمدن ساحران از مداين پيش فرعون و تشريفها يافتن و دست بر سينه زدن در قهر خصم او كه اين بر ما نويس
sections.title_en: How the magicians from the cities assembled before Pharaoh and received robes of honour and laid their hands upon their breasts, to subdue his enemy, and saying,
“Write this down against us.”

entries.farsi: تا به فرعون آمدند آن ساحران / ‏دادشان تشريفهاى بس گران
entries.english: Those magicians came unto Pharaoh, and he gave them robes of honour exceedingly precious.

entries.farsi: وعده‏هاشان كرد و پيشين هم بداد / بندگان و اسبان و نقد و جنس و زاد
entries.english: He made promises to them, and also gave them in advance slaves and horses and money and goods and provisions.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن مى‏گفت هين اى سابقان / ‏گر فزون آييد اندر امتحان
entries.english: After that, he was saying, “Listen, O you that are foremost, if you prove superior in the trial,

entries.farsi: بر فشانم بر شما چندان عطا / كه بدرد پرده‏ى جود و سخا
entries.english: I will scatter over you so many gifts that the veil of bounty and munificence will be rent.”

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندش به اقبال تو شاه / ‏غالب آييم و شود كارش تباه
entries.english: Then they said to him, “Through your fortune, O King, we shall prevail, and his cause shall be ruined.

entries.farsi: ذكر موسى بند خاطرها شده‏ست / ‏كاين حكايتهاست كه پيشين بده‏ست
entries.english: We are heroes and champions in this art: no one in the world can resist us.”

entries.farsi: ما در اين فن صفدريم و پهلوان / ‏كس ندارد پاى ما اندر جهان
entries.english: The mention of Moses has become a chain to the thoughts, that these are stories which happened long ago.

entries.farsi: ذكر موسى بهر رو پوش است ليك / ‏نور موسى نقد تست اى مرد نيك
entries.english: The mention of Moses serves for a mask, but the Light of Moses is your actual concern, O good man.

entries.farsi: موسى و فرعون در هستى تست / ‏بايد اين دو خصم را در خويش جست
entries.english: Moses and Pharaoh are in your being: you must seek these two adversaries in yourself.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت هست از موسى نتاج / ‏نور ديگر نيست ديگر شد سراج
entries.english: The generation from Moses is till the Resurrection: the Light is not different, the lamp has become different.

entries.farsi: اين سفال و اين پليته ديگر است / ‏ليك نورش نيست ديگر ز آن سر است
entries.english: This earthenware lamp and this wick are different, but its light is not different: it is from yonder.

entries.farsi: گر نظر در شيشه دارى گم شوى / ‏ز انكه از شيشه است اعداد دوى
entries.english: If you keep looking at the glass, you will be lost, because from the glass arise the numbers of dualism;

entries.farsi: ور نظر بر نور دارى وارهى / ‏از دوى و اعداد جسم منتهى
entries.english: But if you keep your gaze upon the Light, you will be delivered from dualism and the numbers of the finite body.

entries.farsi: از نظرگاه است اى مغز وجود / اختالف مومن و گبر و جهود
entries.english: From the place of view, O kernel of Existence, there arises the difference between the true believer and the Zoroastrian and the Jew.

sections.title_fa: ‏اختالف كردن در چگونگى و شكل پيل
sections.title_en: The disagreement as to the description and shape of the elephant

entries.farsi: پيل اندر خانه‏ى تاريك بود / عرضه را آورده بودندش هنود
entries.english: The elephant was in a dark house: some Hindus had brought it for exhibition.

entries.farsi: از براى ديدنش مردم بسى / ‏اندر آن ظلمت همى‏شد هر كسى
entries.english: In order to see it, many people were going, every one, into that darkness.

entries.farsi: آن يكى را كف به خرطوم اوفتاد / گفت همچون ناودان است اين نهاد
entries.english: As seeing it with the eye was impossible, was feeling it in the dark with the palm of his hand.

entries.farsi: ديدنش با چشم چون ممكن نبود / اندر آن تاريكى‏اش كف مى‏بسود
entries.english: The hand of one fell on its trunk: he said, “This creature is like a water-pipe.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى را دست بر گوشش رسيد / آن بر او چون باد بيزن شد پديد
entries.english: The hand of another touched its ear: to him it appeared to be like a fan.

entries.farsi: آن يكى را كف چو بر پايش بسود / گفت شكل پيل ديدم چون عمود
entries.english: Since another handled its leg, he said, “I found the elephant’s shape to be like a pillar.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى بر پشت او بنهاد دست / ‏گفت خود اين پيل چون تختى بده ست
entries.english: Another laid his hand on its back: he said, “Truly, this elephant was like a throne.”

entries.farsi: همچنين هر يك به جزوى كه رسيد / فهم آن مى‏كرد هر جا مى‏شنيد
entries.english: Similarly, whenever anyone heard, he understood the part that he had touched.

entries.farsi: از نظرگه گفتشان شد مختلف / ‏آن يكى دالش لقب داد اين الف
entries.english: On account of the place of view, their statements differed: one man entitled it “dál,” another “alif.”

entries.farsi: در كف هر كس اگر شمعى بدى / ‏اختالف از گفتشان بيرون شدى
entries.english: If there had been a candle in each one’s hand, the difference would have gone out of their words.

entries.farsi: چشم حس همچون كف دست است و بس / ‏نيست كف را بر همه‏ى او دست‏رس
entries.english: The eye of sense-perception is only like the palm of the hand: the palm has not power to reach the whole of him.

entries.farsi: جنبش كفها ز دريا روز و شب / ‏كف همى‏بينى و دريا نى عجب
entries.english: The eye of the Sea is one thing and the foam another: leave the foam and look with the eye of the Sea.

entries.farsi: چشم دريا ديگر است و كف دگر / كف بهل وز ديده‏ى دريا نگر
entries.english: Day and night the movement of foam-flecks from the Sea: you behold the foam, but not the Sea. Marvellous!

entries.farsi: ما چو كشتيها بهم بر مى‏زنيم / ‏تيره چشميم و در آب روشنيم
entries.english: We are dashing against each other, like boats: our eyes are darkened, though we are in the clear water.

entries.farsi: آب را آبى است كاو مى‏راندش / ‏روح را روحى است كاو مى‏خواندش
entries.english: O you that have gone to sleep in the body’s boat, you have seen the water, look on the Water of the water.

entries.farsi: اى تو در كشتى تن رفته به خواب / ‏آب را ديدى نگر در آب آب
entries.english: The water has Water that is driving it; the spirit has a Spirit that is calling it.

entries.farsi: موسى و عيسى كجا بد كافتاب / ‏كشت موجودات را مى‏داد آب
entries.english: Where were Moses and Jesus when the Sun was giving water to the sown field of existent things?

entries.farsi: آدم و حوا كجا بود آن زمان / ‏كه خدا افكند اين زه در كمان
entries.english: Where were Adam and Eve at the time when God fitted this string to the bow?

entries.farsi: اين سخن هم ناقص است و ابتر است / ‏آن سخن كه نيست ناقص آن سر است
entries.english: This speech, too, is imperfect and maimed; the speech that is not imperfect is yonder.

entries.farsi: گر بگويد ز آن بلغزد پاى تو / ور نگويد هيچ از آن اى واى تو
entries.english: If he speaks from that, your foot will stumble; and if he speak nothing of that, oh, alas for you!

entries.farsi: ور بگويد در مثال صورتى / ‏بر همان صورت بچسبى اى فتى
entries.english: And if he speak in the likeness of a form, you will stick to that form, O youth.

entries.farsi: بسته پايى چون گيا اندر زمين / ‏سر بجنبانى به بادى بى‏يقين
entries.english: You are foot-bound on the earth, like grass: you nod your head at a wind, without certainty.

entries.farsi: ليك پايت نيست تا نقلى كنى / ‏يا مگر پا را از اين گل بر كنى
entries.english: But you have no foot that you should make a departure or perchance drag your foot out of this mud.

entries.farsi: چون كنى پا را حياتت زين گل است / ‏اين حياتت را روش بس مشكل است
entries.english: How should you drag your foot away? Your life is from this mud: it is mighty hard for this life of yours to go.

entries.farsi: چون حيات از حق بگيرى اى روى / ‏پس شوى مستغنى از گل مى‏روى
entries.english: When you receive life from God, O dependent one, then you will become independent of the mud and will go.

entries.farsi: بسته‏ى شير زمينى چون حبوب / ‏جو فطام خويش از قوت القلوب
entries.english: When the sucking is separated from its nurse, it becomes an eater of morsels and abandons her.

entries.farsi: شير خواره چون ز دايه بگسلد / لوت‏خواره شد مر او را مى‏هلد
entries.english: You, like seeds, are in bondage to the milk of earth: seek to wean yourself by the spiritual food.

entries.farsi: حرف حكمت خور كه شد نور ستير / اى تو نور بى‏حجب را ناپذير
entries.english: Drink the word of Wisdom, for it has become a hidden light, O you who are unable to receive the unveiled Light,

entries.farsi: تا پذيرا گردى اى جان نور را / تا ببينى بى‏حجب مستور را
entries.english: To the end that you may become able, O Soul, to receive the Light, and that you may behold without veils that which is hidden,

entries.farsi: چون ستاره سير بر گردون كنى / ‏بلكه بى‏گردون سفر بى‏چون كنى
entries.english: And traverse the sky like a star; no, journey unconditioned, without sky.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كز نيست در هست آمدى / ‏هين بگو چون آمدى مست آمدى
entries.english: Thus you came into being from non-existence. Say now, how did you come? You arrived drunken.

entries.farsi: راههاى آمدن يادت نماند / ليك رمزى بر تو بر خواهيم خواند
entries.english: The ways of your coming are not remembered by you, but we will recite to you a hint.

entries.farsi: هوش را بگذار و آن گه هوش دار / گوش را بر بند و آن گه گوش دار
entries.english: Let your mind go, and then be mindful! Close your ear, and then listen!

entries.farsi: نى نگويم ز انكه خامى تو هنوز / در بهارى تو نديدستى تموز
entries.english: No, I will not tell, because you still art unripe: you art in springtime, you have not seen Tamúz.

entries.farsi: اين جهان همچون درخت است اى كرام / ‏ما بر او چون ميوه‏هاى نيم خام
entries.english: This world is even as the tree, O noble ones: we are like the half-ripened fruit upon it.

entries.farsi: سخت گيرد خامها مر شاخ را / ز انكه در خامى نشايد كاخ را
entries.english: The unripe cling fast to the bough, because during immaturity they are not fit for the palace.

entries.farsi: چون بپخت و گشت شيرين لب‏گزان / ‏سست گيرد شاخها را بعد از آن
entries.english: When they have ripened and have become sweet— after that, biting their lips, they take a feeble hold of the boughs.

entries.farsi: چون از آن اقبال شيرين شد دهان / ‏سرد شد بر آدمى ملك جهان
entries.english: When the mouth has been sweetened by that felicity, the kingdom of the world becomes cold to Man.

entries.farsi: سخت‏گيرى و تعصب خامى است / ‏تا جنينى كار خون آشامى است
entries.english: To take a tight hold and to attach one’s self strongly is rawness: so long as you are an embryo, your occupation is blood-drinking.

entries.farsi: نى تو گويى هم بگوش خويشتن / ‏نه من و نه غير من اى هم تو من
entries.english: Another thing remains, but the Holy Spirit will tell you the tale of it, without me.

entries.farsi: چيز ديگر ماند اما گفتنش / ‏با تو روح القدس گويد بى‏منش
entries.english: No, you will tell it even to your own ear— neither I nor another than I, O you that are even I—

entries.farsi: همچو آن وقتى كه خواب اندر روى / ‏تو ز پيش خود به پيش خود شوى
entries.english: Just as, when you fall asleep, you go from the presence of yourself into the presence of yourself:

entries.farsi: بشنوى از خويش و پندارى فالن / ‏با تو اندر خواب گفته ست آن نهان
entries.english: You hear from yourself, and deem that such or such a one has secretly told you in the dream that.

entries.farsi: آن تو زفتت كه آن نه صد تو است / ‏قلزم است و غرقه‏گاه صد تو است
entries.english: You are not a single “you,” O good comrade; no, you are the sky and the deep sea.

entries.farsi: تو يكى تو نيستى اى خوش رفيق / ‏بلكه گردونى و درياى عميق
entries.english: Your mighty “You,” which is nine hundredfold, is the ocean and the drowning-place of a hundred “you’s.”

entries.farsi: خود چه جاى حد بيدارى است و خواب / دم مزن و اللهَّ أعلم بالصواب‏
entries.english: Indeed, what occasion for the terms wakefulness and sleep? Do not speak, for God knows best what is right.

entries.farsi: دم مزن تا بشنوى از دم زنان / ‏آن چه نامد در زبان و در بيان
entries.english: Do not speak, so that you may hear from the Speakers that which came not into utterance or into explanation.

entries.farsi: دم مزن تا بشنوى ز آن آفتاب / ‏آن چه نامد در كتاب و در خطاب
entries.english: Do not speak, so that you may hear from the Sun that which came not into book or into allocution.

entries.farsi: دم مزن تا دم زند بهر تو روح / ‏آشنا بگذار در كشتى نوح
entries.english: Do not speak, so that the Spirit may speak for you: in the ark of Noah leave off swimming!

entries.farsi: همچو كنعان كاشنا مى‏كرد او / كه نخواهم كشتى نوح عدو
entries.english: Like Canaan, who was swimming and saying, “I do not want the ark of Noah, my enemy.”

entries.farsi: هى بيا در كشتى بابا نشين / ‏تا نگردى غرق طوفان اى مهين
entries.english: “Hey, come and sit in your father’s ark, that you may not be drowned in the Flood, O despicable one!”

entries.farsi: گفت نى من آشنا آموختم / ‏من بجز شمع تو شمع افروختم
entries.english: He answered, “No, I have learned to swim: I have lighted a candle other than your candle.”

entries.farsi: باد قهر است و بالى شمع كش / ‏جز كه شمع حق نمى‏پايد خمش
entries.english: “Beware! Do it not, for these are the waves of the Flood of tribulation; to-day hand, foot and swimming are nothing.

entries.farsi: هين مكن كاين موج طوفان بالست / ‏دست و پا و آشنا امروز الست
entries.english: It is the wind of vengeance and the woe that extinguishes the candle. No candle but God’s is enduring. Be silent!”

entries.farsi: گفت نى رفتم بر آن كوه بلند / عاصم است آن كه مرا از هر گزند
entries.english: He said, “No, I will go up that high mountain: that mountain will protect me from every hurt.”

entries.farsi: هين مكن كه كوه كاه است اين زمان / ‏جز حبيب خويش را ندهد امان
entries.english: “Beware! Do it not, for at this time the mountain is a straw. He gives safety to none except His beloved.”

entries.farsi: گفت من كى پند تو بشنوده‏ام / ‏كه طمع كردى كه من زين دوده‏ام
entries.english: He answered, “When have I listened to your advice that you should hope that I am of this family?

entries.farsi: خوش نيامد گفت تو هرگز مرا / من برى‏ام از تو در هر دو سرا
entries.english: Your words were never pleasing to me: I am quit of you in both worlds.”

entries.farsi: هين مكن بابا كه روز ناز نيست / ‏مر خدا را خويشى و انباز نيست
entries.english: “Beware, baba, don’t do it, for is not the day for disdain. God has no kinship or partner.

entries.farsi: تا كنون كردى و اين دم نازكى است / ‏اندر اين درگاه گيرا ناز كيست
entries.english: Until now you have shown, and at this moment there is disdain: whose disdain is of any effect in this Court?

entries.farsi: لم يلد لم يولد است او از قدم / ‏نى پدر دارد نه فرزند و نه عم
entries.english: From eternity He is begets not, nor is He begotten: He has neither father nor son nor uncle.

entries.farsi: ناز فرزندان كجا خواهد كشيد / ناز بابايان كجا خواهد شنيد
entries.english: How will He suffer the disdain of sons? How will He listen to the disdain of fathers?

entries.farsi: نيستم مولود پيرا كم بناز / نيستم والد جوانا كم گراز
entries.english: ‘I am not begotten: O old man, do not be proud. I am not a begetter: O youth, do not strut.

entries.farsi: نيستم شوهر نيم من شهوتى / ‏ناز را بگذار اينجا اى ستى
entries.english: I am not a husband, I am not connected with lust: here, O lady, leave off being disdainful.’

entries.farsi: جز خضوع و بندگى و اضطرار / اندر اين حضرت ندارد اعتبار
entries.english: Excepting humility and slavishness and utter helplessness, nothing has consideration in this Presence.”

entries.farsi: گفت بابا سالها اين گفته‏اى / ‏باز مى‏گويى به جهل آشفته‏اى
entries.english: He said, “Father, for years you have said this; you are saying again: you art deranged with folly.

entries.farsi: چند از اينها گفته‏اى با هر كسى / ‏تا جواب سرد بشنودى بسى
entries.english: How many of these things have you said to everyone, so that oftentimes you have heard a cold answer!

entries.farsi: اين دم سرد تو در گوشم نرفت / ‏خاصه اكنون كه شدم دانا و زفت
entries.english: This cold breath of yours did not enter my ear, especially now when I have become wise and strong.”

entries.farsi: همچنين مى‏گفت او پند لطيف / ‏همچنان مى‏گفت او دفع عنيف
entries.english: He said, “Baba, what harm will it do if you listen once to the advice of your father?”

entries.farsi: گفت بابا چه زيان دارد اگر / بشنوى يك بار تو پند پدر
entries.english: On this wise was he speaking kindly counsel, and on that wise was he uttering harsh refusal.

entries.farsi: اندر اين گفتن بدند و موج تيز / بر سر كنعان زد و شد ريز ريز
entries.english: Neither did the father become weary of admonishing Canaan, nor did a single breath enter the ear of that graceless man.

entries.farsi: نه پدر از نصح كنعان سير شد / نه دمى در گوش آن ادبير شد
entries.english: They were in this talk when a fierce billow dashed upon Canaan’s head, and he was shivered to fragments.

entries.farsi: نوح گفت اى پادشاه بردبار / مر مرا خر مرد و سيلت برد بار
entries.english: Noah said, “O long-suffering King, my ass is dead, and Your Flood has carried away the load.

entries.farsi: وعده كردى مر مرا تو بارها / كه بيابد اهلت از طوفان رها
entries.english: Many times You did promise me, saying, ‘Your family shall be saved from the Deluge.’

entries.farsi: دل نهادم بر اميدت من سليم / ‏پس چرا بربود سيل از من گليم
entries.english: I simple fixed my heart on hope of You: why, then, has the Flood swept my garment away from me?”

entries.farsi: گفت او از اهل و خويشانت نبود / خود نديدى تو سپيدى او كبود
entries.english: He said, “He was not of your family and kinsfolk: did not you yourself see you are white, he blue?”

entries.farsi: تا كه باقى تن نگردد زار از او / گر چه بود آن تو شو بيزار از او
entries.english: When the worm has fallen upon your tooth, it is not a tooth: tear it out, O master.

entries.farsi: چون كه دندان تو كرمش در فتاد / نيست دندان بركنش اى اوستاد
entries.english: In order that the rest of your body may not be made miserable by it, become quit of it, although it was yours.

entries.farsi: گفت بيزارم ز غير ذات تو / غير نبود آن كه او شد مات تو
entries.english: He said, “I am quit of anything other than Your Essence; he that has died in You is not other.

entries.farsi: تو همى‏دانى كه چونم با تو من / ‏بيست چندانم كه با باران چمن
entries.english: You know how I am to You: I am as the orchard to the rain, and twenty times as much—

entries.farsi: زنده از تو شاد از تو عايلى / ‏مغتذى بى‏واسطه و بى‏حايلى
entries.english: Living by You, rejoicing because of You, a pauper receiving sustenance without any medium or intervention;

entries.farsi: متصل نه منفصل نه اى كمال / ‏بلكه بى‏چون و چگونه و اعتالل
entries.english: Not united, not separated, O Perfection; no, devoid of quality or description or causation.

entries.farsi: ماهيانيم و تو درياى حيات / ‏زنده‏ايم از لطفت اى نيكو صفات
entries.english: We are the fishes, and You the Sea of Life: we live by Your favour, O You whose attributes are excellent.

entries.farsi: تو نگنجى در كنار فكرتى / ‏نه به معلولى قرين چون علتى
entries.english: You are not contained in the bosom of any thought, nor are You joined with the effect, as a cause.

entries.farsi: پيش از اين طوفان و بعد از اين مرا / تو مخاطب بوده‏اى در ماجرا
entries.english: Before this Flood and after it, You have been the object of my address in colloquy.

entries.farsi: با تو مى‏گفتم نه با ايشان سخن / ‏اى سخن بخش نو و آن كهن
entries.english: I was speaking with You, not with them, O You that are the Giver of speech newly and of old.

entries.farsi: نى كه عاشق روز و شب گويد سخن / ‏گاه با اطالل و گاهى با دمن
entries.english: Is it not the case that the lover, day and night, converses now with the ruins, now with the traces?

entries.farsi: روى با اطالل كرده ظاهرا / او كه را مى‏گويد آن مدحت كه را
entries.english: To outward seeming, he has turned his face towards the ruins, to whom is he saying that song of praise, to whom?

entries.farsi: شكر طوفان را كنون بگماشتى / ‏واسطه‏ى اطالل را برداشتى
entries.english: Thanks! Now You have let loose the Flood and removed the ruins which stood between.

entries.farsi: ز انكه اطالل لئيم و بد بدند / نه ندايى نه صدايى مى‏زدند
entries.english: Because they were vile and evil ruins, uttering neither a cry nor an echo

entries.farsi: من چنان اطالل خواهم در خطاب / ‏كز صدا چون كوه واگويد جواب
entries.english: I desire such ruins to speak with as answer back, like the mountain, by an echo,

entries.farsi: تا مثنا بشنوم من نام تو / عاشقم بر نام جان آرام تو
entries.english: So that I may hear Your name redoubled, I am in love with Your soul soothing name.

entries.farsi: آن كه پست مثال سنگالخ / ‏موش را شايد نه ما را در مناخ
entries.english: That is why every prophet holds the mountains dear: that he may hear Your name redoubled.

entries.farsi: هر نبى ز آن دوست دارد كوه را / تا مثنا بشنود نام ترا
entries.english: That low mountain, resembling stony ground, is suitable for a mouse, not for us, as a resting-place.

entries.farsi: من بگويم او نگردد يار من / ‏بى‏صدا ماند دم گفتار من
entries.english: I speak, it does not join with me: the breath of my speech remains without echo.

entries.farsi: با زمين آن به كه هموارش كنى / ‏نيست هم دم با قدم يارش كنى
entries.english: It is better that you level it with the earth; it is not in accord with your breath: you should join it with your foot.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى نوح ار تو خواهى جمله را / حشر گردانم بر آرم از ثرا
entries.english: He said, “O Noah, if you desire, I will assemble them all and raise them from the earth.

entries.farsi: بهر كنعانى دل تو نشكنم / ‏ليك از احوال آگه مى‏كنم
entries.english: I will not break your heart for the sake of a Canaan, but I am acquainting with states.”

entries.farsi: گفت نى نى راضى‏ام كه تو مرا / هم كنى غرقه اگر بايد ترا
entries.english: He said, “No, no, I am content that You should drown me too, if it suits You.

entries.farsi: هر زمانم غرقه مى‏كن من خوشم / ‏حكم تو جان است چون جان مى‏كشم
entries.english: Keep drowning me every instant, I am pleased: Your ordinance is my soul, I bear it as my soul.

entries.farsi: ننگرم كس را و گر هم بنگرم / ‏او بهانه باشد و تو منظرم
entries.english: I do not look at any one, and even if I do look at, he is a pretext, and You are the object of my regard.

entries.farsi: عاشق صنع توام در شكر و صبر / عاشق مصنوع كى باشم چو گبر
entries.english: I am in love with Your making in thanksgiving and patience; how should I be in love, like the infidel, with that which You have made?”

entries.farsi: عاشق صنع خدا با فر بود / عاشق مصنوع او كافر بود
entries.english: He that loves God’s making is glorious; he that loves what He has made is an unbeliever.

sections.title_fa: ‏توفيق ميان اين دو حديث كه الرضا بالكفر كفر و حديث ديگر من لم يرض بقضائي فليطلب ربا سوائي
sections.title_en: Reconciliation of these two Traditions: “To be satisfied with infidelity is an act of infidelity,” and “If anyone is not satisfied with My ordainment, let him seek a lord other than Me.”

entries.farsi: دى سؤالى كرد سائل مر مرا / ز انكه عاشق بود او بر ماجرا
entries.english: Yesterday an inquirer put a question to me, because he was fond of disputation.

entries.farsi: گفت نكته‏ى الرضا بالكفر كفر / اين پيمبر گفت و گفت اوست مهر
entries.english: He said, “This Prophet uttered the deep saying, ‘To be satisfied with infidelity is an act of infidelity’; his words are a seal.

entries.farsi: باز فرمود او كه اندر هر قضا / مر مسلمان را رضا بايد رضا
entries.english: Again, he said that the Moslem must be satisfied in every ordainment, must be satisfied.

entries.farsi: نه قضاى حق بود كفر و نفاق / ‏گر بدين راضى شوم باشد شقاق
entries.english: Is not infidelity and hypocrisy the ordainment of God? If I become satisfied with this, it will be opposition,

entries.farsi: ور نيم راضى بود آن هم زيان / ‏پس چه چاره باشدم اندر ميان
entries.english: And if I am not satisfied, that too will be detrimental: between, then, what means is there for me?”

entries.farsi: گفتمش اين كفر مقضى نه قضاست / ‏هست آثار قضا اين كفر راست
entries.english: I said to him, “This infidelity is the thing ordained, it is not the ordainment; this infidelity is truly the effects of the ordainment.

entries.farsi: پس قضا را خواجه از مقضى بدان / ‏تا شكالت دفع گردد در زمان
entries.english: Therefore know, sire, the ordainment from the thing ordained, so that your difficulty may be removed at once.

entries.farsi: راضيم در كفر ز آن رو كه قضاست / ‏نه از اين رو كه نزاع و خبث ماست
entries.english: I acquiesce in infidelity in that respect that it is the ordainment, not in this respect that it is our contentiousness and wickedness.

entries.farsi: كفر از روى قضا هم كفر نيست / ‏حق را كافر مخوان اينجا مه ايست
entries.english: In respect of the ordainment, infidelity indeed is not infidelity. Do not call God ‘infidel,’ do not stand here.

entries.farsi: كفر جهل است و قضاى كفر علم / ‏هر دو كى يك باشد آخر حلم و خلم
entries.english: Infidelity is ignorance, and the ordainment of infidelity is knowledge: how, pray, should hilm (forbearance) and khilm (anger) both be one?

entries.farsi: زشتى خط زشتى نقاش نيست / ‏بلكه از وى زشت را بنمودنى است
entries.english: The ugliness of the script is not the ugliness of the artist; no, it is an exhibition of the ugly by him.

entries.farsi: قوت نقاش باشد آن كه او / هم تواند زشت كردن هم نكو
entries.english: The power of the artist is that he can make both the ugly and the beautiful.”

entries.farsi: گر گشايم بحث اين را من به ساز / تا سؤال و تا جواب آيد دراز
entries.english: If I develop the investigation of this methodically, so that question and answer become lengthy,

entries.farsi: ذوق نكته‏ى عشق از من مى‏رود / نقش خدمت نقش ديگر مى‏شود
entries.english: The savour of Love’s mystery will go from me, the form of piety will be deformed.

sections.title_fa: ‏مثل در بيان آن كه حيرت مانع بحث و فكرت است
sections.title_en: A parable illustrating the fact that bewilderment prevents investigation and consideration.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مرد دو مو آمد شتاب / ‏پيش يك آيينه‏دار مستطاب
entries.english: A certain man, whose hair was of two colours, came hastily to a highly esteemed barber.

entries.farsi: گفت از ريشم سپيدى كن جدا / ‏كه عروس نو گزيدم اى فتى
entries.english: He said, “Remove the hoariness from my beard, for I have chosen a new bride, O young man.”

entries.farsi: ريش او ببريد و كل پيشش نهاد / گفت تو بگزين مرا كارى فتاد
entries.english: He cut off his beard and laid the whole of it before him, and said, “Do you pick out, and for it happens that I have some important business.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى زد سيليى مر زيد را / حمله كرد او هم براى كيد را
entries.english: That “pick out” is dialectic, for religious emotion has no care for these things.

entries.farsi: اين سؤال و آن جواب است آن گزين / ‏كه سر اينها ندارد درد دين
entries.english: A certain man slapped Zayd on the neck; he at once rushed at him with warlike purpose.

entries.farsi: گفت سيلى زن سؤالت مى‏كنم / ‏پس جوابم گوى و آن گه مى‏زنم
entries.english: The assailant said, “I will ask you a question so answer me and then strike me.

entries.farsi: بر قفاى تو زدم آمد طراق / ‏يك سؤالى دارم اينجا در وفاق
entries.english: I struck the nape of your neck, and there was the sound of a slap: at this point I have a question in concord:

entries.farsi: اين طراق از دست من بوده‏ست يا / از قفا گاه تو اى فخر كيا
entries.english: Was this sound caused by my hand or by the nape of your neck, O pride of the noble?”

entries.farsi: تو كه بى‏دردى همى‏انديش اين / ‏نيست صاحب درد را اين فكر هين
entries.english: He said, “On account of the pain I have no leisure to stop in this reflection and consideration.

entries.farsi: گفت از درد اين فراغت نيستم / ‏كه در اين فكر و تفكر بيستم
entries.english: Do you, who art without pain, ponder on this; he that feels the pain has no such thought. Take heed!”

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت
sections.title_en: Story.

entries.farsi: در صحابه كم بدى حافظ كسى / ‏گر چه شوقى بود جانشان را بسى
entries.english: Amongst the Companions there was scarcely any one that knew the Qur’an by heart, though their souls had a great desire,

entries.farsi: ز انكه چون مغزش در آگند و رسيد / پوستها شد بس رقيق و واكفيد
entries.english: Because, inasmuch as its kernel had filled and had reached maturity, the rinds became very thin and burst.

entries.farsi: قشر جوز و فستق و بادام هم / ‏مغز چون آگندشان شد پوست كم
entries.english: Similarly, the shells of the walnut and the pistachio-nut and the almond— when the kernel has filled them, the rind decreases.

entries.farsi: مغز علم افزود كم شد پوستش / ‏ز انكه عاشق را بسوزد دوستش
entries.english: The kernel, knowledge, increases, its rind decreases, because the lover is consumed by his beloved.

entries.farsi: وصف مطلوبى چو ضد طالبى است / ‏وحى و برق نور سوزنده‏ى نبى است
entries.english: Since the quality of being sought is the opposite of seeking, the Revelation and the flashing of the Light consume the prophet with burning.

entries.farsi: چون تجلى كرد اوصاف قديم / ‏پس بسوزد وصف حادث را گليم
entries.english: When the Attributes of the Eternal have shone forth, then the mantle of temporality is burned.

entries.farsi: جمع صورت با چنين معنى ژرف / ‏نيست ممكن جز ز سلطانى شگرف
entries.english: Every one that knew a quarter of the Qur’an by heart was hearing from the Companions, “Great is he amongst us.”

entries.farsi: ربع قرآن هر كه را محفوظ بود / جل فينا از صحابه مى‏شنود
entries.english: To combine the form with such a deep meaning is not possible, except on the part of a mighty king.

entries.farsi: در چنين مستى مراعات ادب / ‏خود نباشد ور بود باشد عجب
entries.english: In such intoxication the observance of due respect will not be there at all; or if it be, it is a wonder.

entries.farsi: اندر استغنا مراعات نياز / جمع ضدين است چون گرد و دراز
entries.english: To observe humility in independence is to combine two opposites, like “round” and “long.”

entries.farsi: خود عصا معشوق عميان مى‏بود / كور خود صندوق قرآن مى‏بود
entries.english: Truly the staff is loved by the blind; the blind man himself is a coffer of the Qur’an.

entries.farsi: باز صندوقى پر از قرآن به است / ‏ز آن كه صندوقى بود خالى به دست
entries.english: He said, “In sooth the blind are coffers full of the words of the Qur’an and commemoration and warning.”

entries.farsi: گفت كوران خود صناديقند پر / از حروف مصحف و ذكر و نذر
entries.english: Again, a coffer full of the Qur’an is better than he that is an empty coffer in the hand.

entries.farsi: باز صندوقى كه خالى شد ز بار / به ز صندوقى كه پر موش است و مار
entries.english: Yet again, the coffer that is empty of load is better than the coffer that is full of mice and snakes.

entries.farsi: حاصل اندر وصل چون افتاد مرد / گشت دالله به پيش مرد سرد
entries.english: The sum: when a man has attained to union, the go between becomes worthless to him.

entries.farsi: چون به مطلوبت رسيدى اى مليح / ‏شد طلب كارى علم اكنون قبيح
entries.english: Since you have reached the object of your search, O elegant one, the search for knowledge has now become evil.

entries.farsi: چون شدى بر بامهاى آسمان / ‏سرد باشد جست و جوى نردبان
entries.english: Since you have mounted to the roofs of Heaven, it would be futile to seek a ladder.

entries.farsi: جز براى يارى و تعليم غير / سرد باشد راه خير از بعد خير
entries.english: After felicity, the way to felicity is worthless except for the sake of helping and teaching others.

entries.farsi: آينه‏ى روشن كه شد صاف و جلى / ‏جهل باشد بر نهادن صيقلى
entries.english: The shining mirror, which has become clear and perfect— it would be folly to apply a polisher (to it).

entries.farsi: پيش سلطان خوش نشسته در قبول / ‏زشت باشد جستن نامه و رسول
entries.english: Seated happily beside the Sultan in favour—it would be disgraceful to seek letter and messenger

sections.title_fa: داستان مشغول شدن عاشقى به عشق نامه خواندن و مطالعه كردن عشق نامه در حضور معشوق خويش و معشوق آن را ناپسند داشتن كه طلب الدليل عند ‏حضور المدلول قبيح و الاشتغال بالعلم بعد الوصول الى المعلوم مذموم
sections.title_en: Story of a lover’s being engrossed in reading and perusing a love-letter in the presence of his beloved,

and how the beloved was displeased thereat. It is shameful to seek the proof in the presence of that which is proved, and blameworthy to occupy one’s self with knowledge after having attained to that which is known.

entries.farsi: آن يكى را يار پيش خود نشاند / نامه بيرون كرد و پيش يار خواند
entries.english: A certain man, his beloved let sit beside her, produced a letter and read it to her.

entries.farsi: بيتها در نامه و مدح و ثنا / زارى و مسكينى و بس البه‏ها
entries.english: In the letter were verses and praise and laud, lamentation and wretchedness and many humble entreaties.

entries.farsi: گفت معشوق اين اگر بهر من است / ‏گاه وصل اين عمر ضايع كردن است
entries.english: The beloved said, “If this is for my sake, this at the time of meeting is to waste one’s life.

entries.farsi: آن چه مى‏ديدم ز تو پارينه سال / ‏نيست اين دم گر چه مى‏بينم وصال
entries.english: I am here beside you, and you reading a letter! This, at any rate, is not the mark of lovers.”

entries.farsi: من به پيشت حاضر و تو نامه خوان / ‏نيست اين بارى نشان عاشقان
entries.english: He replied, “You are present here, but I am not gaining my pleasure well.

entries.farsi: گفت اينجا حاضرى اما و ليك / ‏من نمى‏يابم نصيب خويش نيك
entries.english: That which I felt last year on account of you is non-existent at this moment, though I am experiencing union.

entries.farsi: من از اين چشمه زاللى خورده‏ام / ‏ديده و دل ز آب تازه كرده‏ام
entries.english: I have drunk cool water from this fountain; I have refreshed eye and heart with its water.

entries.farsi: چشمه مى‏بينم و ليكن آب نى / ‏راه آبم را مگر زد ره زنى
entries.english: I am seeing the fountain, but the water is not there: maybe some brigand has waylaid my water.”

entries.farsi: گفت پس من نيستم معشوق تو / من به بلغار و مرادت در قتو
entries.english: She said, “Then I am not your beloved: I am in Bulghar, and the object of your desire is in Qutú

entries.farsi: عاشقى تو بر من و بر حالتى / ‏حالت اندر دست نبود يا فتى
entries.english: You are in love with me and with a state of feeling; the state of feeling is not in your hand, O youth.

entries.farsi: پس نيم كلى مطلوب تو من / ‏جزو مقصودم ترا اندر زمن
entries.english: Therefore I am not the whole of that which is sought by you;

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى معشوقه‏ام معشوق نى / ‏عشق بر نقد است بر صندوق نى
entries.english: I am part of the object of your quest at the present time.

entries.farsi: هست معشوق آن كه او يك تو بود / مبتدا و منتهايت او بود
entries.english: I am the house of your beloved, not the beloved; love is for the cash, not for the coffer.”

entries.farsi: چون بيابى‏اش نمانى منتظر / هم هويدا او بود هم نيز سر
entries.english: The beloved is that one who is single, who is your beginning and end.

entries.farsi: مير احوال است نه موقوف حال / ‏بنده‏ى آن ماه باشد ماه و سال
entries.english: When you find him, you will not remain in expectation: he is both the manifest and also the mystery,

entries.farsi: چون بگويد حال را فرمان كند / چون بخواهد جسمها را جان كند
entries.english: He is the lord of states of feeling, not dependent on any state: month and year are slaves of that Moon.

entries.farsi: منتها نبود كه موقوف است او / منتظر بنشسته باشد حال جو
entries.english: When he bids the “state,” it does his behest; when he wills, he makes bodies spirit.

entries.farsi: آن كه او موقوف حال است آدمى است / گه به حال افزون و گاهى در كمى است
entries.english: One that is stopped is not the end; he will be seated, waiting and seeking the “state.”

entries.farsi: كيمياى حال باشد دست او / دست جنباند شود مس مست او
entries.english: His hand is the elixir that transmutes the “state”: he moves his hand, the copper becomes intoxicated with him.

entries.farsi: گر بخواهد مرگ هم شيرين شود / خار و نشتر نرگس و نسرين شود
entries.english: If he will, even death becomes sweet; thorns and stings become narcissus and wild-rose.

entries.farsi: صوفى ابن الوقت باشد در مثال / ‏ليك صافى فارغ است از وقت و حال
entries.english: He that is dependent on the “state” is a human being: at one moment he is greater by the “state,” at another moment he is in decrease.

entries.farsi: حالها موقوف عزم و راى او / زنده از نفخ مسيح آساى او
entries.english: In similitude the Sufi is “the son of the time,” but the pure one (şáfí) is unconcerned with “time” and “state.”

entries.farsi: عاشق حالى نه عاشق بر منى / ‏بر اميد حال بر من مى‏تنى
entries.english: “States” are dependent on his decision and judgement; vivified by his Messiah-like breath.

entries.farsi: آن كه يك دم كم دمى كامل بود / نيست معبود خليل آفل بود
entries.english: “You art in love with your ‘state,’ you are not in love with me; you are attached to me in the hope of the ‘state.’”

entries.farsi: و انكه آفل باشد و گه آن و اين / ‏نيست دل بر ال أُحِبُّ اآلفلين
entries.english: He that at one moment is deficient and at another moment perfect is not He that was worshipped by Khalíl: he is one that sinks;

entries.farsi: آن كه او گاهى خوش و گه ناخوش است / ‏يك زمانى آب و يك دم آتش است
entries.english: And he that is liable to sink and is now that and this is not the beloved: “I love not them that sink.”

entries.farsi: برج مه باشد و ليكن ماه نى / ‏نقش بت باشد ولى آگاه نى
entries.english: He that is now pleasing and now unpleasing, at one moment water and at one moment fire,

entries.farsi: هست صوفى صفا جو ابن وقت / ‏وقت را همچون پدر بگرفته سخت
entries.english: May be the mansion of the Moon, but he is not the Moon; he may be the picture of the Adored One, but he is not conscious.
The Sufi that seeks purity is ‘the son of the time’: he has clasped the ‘time’ tightly as his father.

entries.farsi: هست صافى غرق نور ذو الجلال / ‏ابن كس نى فارغ از اوقات و حال
entries.english: The pure one (şáfí) is plunged in the Light of the Glorious; he is not the son of any one, free from ‘times’ and ‘states’—

entries.farsi: غرقه‏ى نورى كه او لَمْ يولد است / ‏لَمْ يَلِدْ لَمْ يُولَدْ آن ايزد است
entries.english: Plunged in the Light which is unbegotten: He neither begets nor is He begotten belongs to God.

entries.farsi: رو چنين عشقى بجو گر زنده‏اى / ‏ور نه وقت مختلف را بنده‏اى
entries.english: Go, seek a love like this, if you are alive; otherwise, you are a slave to the changing “time.”

entries.farsi: منگر اندر نقش زشت و خوب خويش / ‏بنگر اندر عشق و در مطلوب خويش
entries.english: Do not regard your ugly or beauteous form; regard Love and the object of your search.

entries.farsi: منگر آن كه تو حقيرى يا ضعيف / ‏بنگر اندر همت خود اى شريف
entries.english: Do not regard the fact that you are despicable or infirm; look upon your aspiration, O noble one.

entries.farsi: تو به هر حالى كه باشى مى‏طلب / ‏آب مى‏جو دايما اى خشك لب
entries.english: In whatsoever state you be, keep searching; O you with dry lip, always be seeking the water,

entries.farsi: خشكى لب هست پيغامى ز آب / ‏كه به مات آرد يقين اين اضطراب
entries.english: For that dry lip of yours gives evidence that at last it will reach the springhead.

entries.farsi: كان لب خشكت گواهى مى‏دهد / كاو به آخر بر سر منبع رسد
entries.english: Dryness of lip is a message from the water that this agitation will certainly bring you to the water,

entries.farsi: كاين طلب‏كارى مبارك جنبشى است / ‏اين طلب در راه حق مانع‏كشى است
entries.english: For this seeking is a blessed motion; this search is a killer of obstacles on the Way to God.

entries.farsi: اين طلب مفتاح مطلوبات تست / ‏اين سپاه و نصرت رايات تست
entries.english: This search is the key to the things sought by you; this is your army and the victory of your banners.

entries.farsi: اين طلب همچون خروسى در صياح / ‏مى‏زند نعره كه مى‏آيد صباح
entries.english: This search is like chanticleer crowing and proclaiming that the dawn is at hand.

entries.farsi: گر چه آلت نيستت تو مى‏طلب / ‏نيست آلت حاجت اندر راه رب
entries.english: Although you have no equipment, be ever seeking: equipment is not necessary on the Way of the Lord.

entries.farsi: كز جوار طالبان طالب شوى / ‏و ز ظالل غالبان غالب شوى
entries.english: Whomever you see engaged in search, O son, become his friend and cast your head before him,

entries.farsi: هر كه را بينى طلب‏كار اى پسر / يار او شو پيش او انداز سر
entries.english: For through being the neighbour of the seekers you will become a seeker, and from the shadows of the conquerors you will become a conqueror.

entries.farsi: گر يكى مورى سليمانى بجست / ‏منگر اندر جستن او سست سست
entries.english: If an ant has sought the rank of Solomon, do not look languidly on its quest.

entries.farsi: هر چه دارى تو ز مال و پيشه‏اى / ‏نه طلب بود اول و انديشه‏اى
entries.english: Everything that you have of wealth and a handicraft— was it not at first a quest and a thought?

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت آن شخص كه در عهد داود عليه السلام شب و روز دعا مى‏كرد كه مرا روزى حالل ده بى‏رنج
sections.title_en: Story of the person who in the time of David, on whom be peace, used to pray night and day, crying, “Give me a lawful livelihood without trouble.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى در عهد داود نبى / ‏نزد هر دانا و پيش هر غبى
entries.english: In the time of the prophet David a certain man, beside every sage and before every simpleton,

entries.farsi: اين دعا مى‏كرد دايم كاى خدا / ثروتى بى‏رنج روزى كن مرا
entries.english: used to always utter this prayer: “O God, bestow on me riches without trouble!

entries.farsi: چون مرا تو آفريدى كاهلى / ‏زخم خوارى سست جنبى منبلى
entries.english: Since You have created me a lazybones, a receiver of blows, a slow mover, a sluggard,

entries.farsi: بر خران پشت ريش بى‏مراد / بار اسبان و استران نتوان نهاد
entries.english: One cannot place on sore-backed luckless asses the load carried by horses and mules.

entries.farsi: كاهلم من سايه خسبم در وجود / خفتم اندر سايه‏ى اين فضل و جود
entries.english: Inasmuch as You, O perfect One, have created me lazy, do You accordingly give me the daily bread by the way of laziness.

entries.farsi: كاهلم چون آفريدى اى ملى / ‏روزيم ده هم ز راه كاهلى
entries.english: I am lazy and sleeping in the shade in existence: I sleep in the shade of this Bounty and Munificence.

entries.farsi: كاهالن و سايه خسبان را مگر / روزيى بنوشته‏اى نوعى دگر
entries.english: Surely for them that are lazy and sleeping in the shade You have prescribed a livelihood in another fashion.

entries.farsi: هر كه را پاى است جويد روزيى / ‏هر كه را پا نيست كن دل سوزيى
entries.english: Every one that has a foot seeks a livelihood: show some pity towards every one that has no foot.

entries.farsi: رزق را مى‏ران به سوى آن حزين / ‏ابر را مى‏كش به سوى هر زمين
entries.english: Send the daily bread to that sorrowful one: waft the rain-clouds towards every land.

entries.farsi: چون زمين را پا نباشد جود تو / ابر را راند به سوى او دو تو
entries.english: Since the land has no foot, Your munificence drives the clouds doubly towards it.

entries.farsi: روزيى خواهم به ناگه بى‏تعب / ‏كه ندارم من ز كوشش جز طلب
entries.english: Since the babe has no foot, its mother comes and pours the ration upon it.

entries.farsi: طفل را چون پا نباشد مادرش / ‏آيد و ريزد وظيفه بر سرش
entries.english: I crave a daily portion suddenly without fatigue, for I have nothing of endeavour except the seeking.”

entries.farsi: مدت بسيار مى‏كرد اين دعا / ‏روز تا شب شب همه شب تا ضحى
entries.english: Thus was he praying for a long time, day until night and all night until morning.

entries.farsi: خلق مى‏خنديد بر گفتار او / بر طمع خامى و بر پيكار او
entries.english: The people were laughing at his words, at the folly of his hope, and at his contention,

entries.farsi: كه چه مى‏گويد عجب اين سست ريش / ‏يا كسى داده‏ست بنگ بى‏هشيش
entries.english: Saying; “Marvellous! What is he saying—this idiot? Or has some one given him beng of senselessness?

entries.farsi: راه روزى كسب و رنج است و تعب / ‏هر كسى را پيشه‏اى داد و طلب
entries.english: The way of daily bread is work and trouble and fatigue; He has given every one a handicraft and seeking.

entries.farsi: اطلبوا األرزاق في أسبابها / ادخلوا األوطان من أبوابها
entries.english: ‘Seek your daily portions in the means thereof: enter your dwellings by their doors.’

entries.farsi: شاه و سلطان و رسول حق كنون / ‏هست داود نبى ذو فنون
entries.english: At present the King and ruler and messenger of God is the prophet David, one endowed with many accomplishments.

entries.farsi: با چنان عزى و نازى كاندر اوست / ‏كه گزيدستش عنايتهاى دوست
entries.english: Notwithstanding such glory and pride as is in him, forasmuch as the favours of the Friend have chosen him out—

entries.farsi: معجزاتش بى‏شمار و بى‏عدد / موج بخشايش مدد اندر مدد
entries.english: His miracles are countless and innumerable, the waves of his bounty are tide upon tide:

entries.farsi: هيچ كس را خود ز آدم تا كنون / ‏كى بده‏ست آواز صد چون ارغنون
entries.english: When has anyone, even from Adam till now, had a voice like an organ?

entries.farsi: كه به هر وعظى بميراند دويست / ‏آدمى را صوت خويش كرد نيست
entries.english: Which at every preaching causes to die? His beautiful voice made two hundred human beings non-existent.

entries.farsi: شير و آهو جمع گردد آن زمان / ‏سوى تذكيرش مغفل اين از آن
entries.english: At that time the lion and the deer unite towards his exhortation, the one oblivious of the other;

entries.farsi: كوه و مرغان هم رسايل با دمش / ‏هر دو اندر وقت دعوت محرمش
entries.english: The mountains and the birds are accompanying his breath; both are his confidants in the hour of his calling;

entries.farsi: اين و صد چندين مر او را معجزات / ‏نور رويش بى‏جهات و در جهات
entries.english: These and a hundred times as many miracles are to him; the light of his countenance is transcendent and immanent—

entries.farsi: با همه تمكين خدا روزى او / كرده باشد بسته اندر جستجو
entries.english: Notwithstanding all majesty, God must have made his livelihood to be bound up with seeking and endeavour.

entries.farsi: اين چنين مخذول واپس مانده‏اى / ‏خانه كنده‏ى دون و گردون رانده‏اى
entries.english: Without weaving coats of mail and some trouble, his livelihood is not coming, notwithstanding all his winning.

entries.farsi: بى‏زره بافى و رنجى روزى‏اش / ‏مى‏نيايد با همه پيروزى‏اش
entries.english: A God-forsaken abandoned one like this, a low scoundrel and outcast from Heaven,

entries.farsi: اين چنين مدبر همى‏خواهد كه زود / بى‏تجارت پر كند دامن ز سود
entries.english: A backslider of this sort, desires, without trading, at once to fill his skirt with gain!

entries.farsi: اين چنين گيجى بيامد در ميان / ‏كه بر آيم بر فلك بى‏نردبان
entries.english: Such a crazy fellow has come forward, saying, ‘I will climb up to the sky without a ladder.’”

entries.farsi: اين همى‏گفتش به تسخر رو بگير / كه رسيدت روزى و آمد بشير
entries.english: This one would say to him derisively, “Go and receive, for your daily portion has arrived and the messenger has come with the good news”;

entries.farsi: و آن همى‏خنديد ما را هم بده / ‏ز انچه يابى هديه‏اى سالار ده
entries.english: And that one would laugh, “Give us too of what you get as a gift, O headman of the village.”

entries.farsi: او از اين تشنيع مردم وين فسوس / ‏كم نمى‏كرد از دعا و چاپلوس
entries.english: He was not diminishing his prayers and wheedling entreaties because of this abuse and ridicule from the people,

entries.farsi: تا كه شد در شهر معروف و شهير / كاو ز انبان تهى جويد پنير
entries.english: that he became well-known and celebrated in the town as one who seeks cheese from an empty wallet.

entries.farsi: شد مثل در خام طبعى آن گدا / او از اين خواهش نمى‏آمد جدا
entries.english: That beggar became a proverb for foolishness; he would not desist from this petitioning.

sections.title_fa: دويدن گاو در خانه‏ى آن دعاكننده به الحاح، قال النَّبىّ عليه السلام إن اللهَّ يحب الملحين في الدعاء زيرا عين خواست از حق تعالى و الحاح خواهنده را به است از آن چه مى‏خواهد آن را از او

sections.title_en: How a cow ran into the house of him that was praying importunately. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, has said, “God loves them that are persistent in prayer,” because the actual asking from God most High and the importunity is better for the petitioner than the thing which he is asking of Him.

entries.farsi: تا كه روزى ناگهان در چاشت‏گاه / ‏اين دعا مى‏كرد با زارى و آه
entries.english: Until suddenly one day, he was uttering this prayer with moaning and sighs at morningtide,

entries.farsi: ناگهان در خانه‏اش گاوى دويد / شاخ زد بشكست در بند و كليد
entries.english: Suddenly a cow ran into his house; she butted with her horns and broke the bolt and key.

entries.farsi: گاو گستاخ اندر آن خانه بجست / ‏مرد در جست و قوايمهاش بست
entries.english: The cow boldly jumped into the house; the man sprang forward and bound her legs.

entries.farsi: پس گلوى گاو ببريد آن زمان / ‏بى‏توقف بى‏تامل بى‏امان
entries.english: Then he at once cut the throat of the cow without pause, without consideration, and without mercy.

entries.farsi: چون سرش ببريد شد سوى قصاب / ‏تا اهابش بر كند در دم شتاب
entries.english: After he had cut off her head, he went to the butcher, in order that he might quickly rip off her hide forthwith.

sections.title_fa: ‏عذر گفتن نظم كننده و مدد خواستن
sections.title_en: The Poet’s excusing himself and asking help.

entries.farsi: اى تقاضاگر درون همچون جنين / ‏چون تقاضا مى‏كنى اتمام اين
entries.english: O You that make demands within, like the embryo— since You are making a demand, make easy

entries.farsi: سهل گردان ره نما توفيق ده / ‏يا تقاضا را بهل بر ما منه
entries.english: The fulfillment of this, shows the way, give guidance, or relinquish the demand and do not lay upon me!

entries.farsi: چون ز مفلس زر تقاضا مى‏كنى / ‏زر ببخشش در سر اى شاه غنى
entries.english: Since You are demanding gold from an insolvent, give him gold in secret, O rich King!

entries.farsi: بى‏تو نظم و قافيه شام و سحر / زهره كى دارد كه آيد در نظر
entries.english: Without You, how should poesy and rhyme dare to come into sight at eve or morn?

entries.farsi: نظم و تجنيس و قوافى اى عليم / ‏بنده‏ى امر تواند از ترس و بيم
entries.english: Poesy and homonymy and rhymes, O Knowing One, are the slaves of Your Command from fear and dread,

entries.farsi: هر يكى تسبيح بر نوعى دگر / گويد و از حال آن اين بى‏خبر
entries.english: Inasmuch as You have made everything a glorifier — the undiscerning entity and the discerning.

entries.farsi: چون مسبح كرده‏اى هر چيز را / ذات بى‏تمييز و با تمييز را
entries.english: Each glorifies in a different fashion, and that one is unaware of the state of this one.

entries.farsi: آدمى منكر ز تسبيح جماد / و آن جماد اندر عبادت اوستاد
entries.english: Man disbelieves in the glorification uttered by inanimate things, but those inanimate things are masters in worship.

entries.farsi: بلكه هفتاد و دو ملت هر يكى / ‏بى‏خبر از يكدگر و اندر شكى
entries.english: No, the two-and-seventy sects, every one, are unaware of each other and in a doubt.

entries.farsi: چون دو ناطق را ز حال همدگر / نيست آگه چون بود ديوار و در
entries.english: Since two speakers have no knowledge of each other’s state, how will be wall and door?

entries.farsi: چون من از تسبيح ناطق غافلم / ‏چون بداند سبحه‏ى صامت دلم
entries.english: Since I am heedless of the glorification uttered by one who speaks, how should my heart know the glorification performed by that which is mute?

entries.farsi: سنى از تسبيح جبرى بى‏خبر / جبرى از تسبيح سنى بى‏اثر
entries.english: The Sunni is unaware of the Jabri’s glorification; the Jabri is unaffected by the Sunni’s glorification.

entries.farsi: هست سنى را يكى تسبيح خاص / ‏هست جبرى را ضد آن در مناص
entries.english: The Sunni has a particular glorification; the Jabri has the opposite thereof in refuge.

entries.farsi: اين همى‏گويد كه آن ضالست و گم / ‏بى‏خبر از حال او وز امر قم
entries.english: This one says, “He is astray and lost,” unaware of his state and of the command, “Arise!”

entries.farsi: و آن همى‏گويد كه اين را چه خبر / جنگشان افكند يزدان از قدر
entries.english: And that one says, “What awareness has this one?” God, by fore-ordainment, has cast them into strife.

entries.farsi: گوهر هر يك هويدا مى‏كند / جنس از ناجنس پيدا مى‏كند
entries.english: He makes manifest the real nature of each; He displays the congener by the uncongenial.

entries.farsi: قهر را از لطف داند هر كسى / ‏خواه دانا خواه نادان يا خسى
entries.english: Everyone knows mercy from vengeance, whether he be wise or ignorant or vile,

entries.farsi: ليك لطفى قهر در پنهان شده / ‏يا كه قهرى در دل لطف آمده
entries.english: But a mercy that has become hidden in vengeance, or a vengeance that has sunk into the heart of mercy,

entries.farsi: باقيان زين دو گمانى مى‏برند / سوى النه‏ى خود به يك پر مى‏پرند
entries.english: No one knows except the divine man in whose heart is a spiritual touchstone.

entries.farsi: كم كسى داند مگر ربانيى / كش بود در دل محك جانيى
entries.english: The rest hold an opinion of these two: they fly to their nest with a single wing.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه علم را دو پر است و گمان را يك پر است، ناقص آمد ظن به پرواز ‏ ابتر است، و مثال ظن و يقين در علم
sections.title_en: Explaining that Knowledge has two wings, and Opinion one: “Opinion is defective and curtailed in flight”; and a comparison illustrating opinion and certainty in knowledge.

entries.farsi: علم را دو پر گمان را يك پر است / ‏ناقص آمد ظن به پرواز ابتر است
entries.english: Knowledge has two wings, Opinion one wing: Opinion is defective and curtailed in flight.

entries.farsi: مرغ يك پر زود افتد سر نگون / ‏باز بر پرد دو گامى يا فزون
entries.english: The one-winged bird soon falls headlong; then again it flies up some two paces or more.

entries.farsi: افت و خيزان مى‏رود مرغ گمان / ‏با يكى پر بر اميد آشيان
entries.english: The bird, Opinion, falling and rising, goes on with one wing in hope of the nest.

entries.farsi: چون ز ظن وارست علمش رو نمود / شد دو پر آن مرغ يك پر پر گشود
entries.english: When he has been delivered from Opinion, Knowledge shows its face to him: that one-winged bird becomes two-winged and spreads his wings.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن يمشى سويا مستقيم / ‏نى على وجهه مكبا او سقيم
entries.english: After that, he walks erect and straight, not falling flat on his face or ailing.

entries.farsi: با دو پر بر مى‏پرد چون جبرئيل / ‏بى‏گمان و بى‏مگر بى‏قال و قيل
entries.english: He flies aloft with two wings, like Gabriel, without opinion and without peradventure and without disputation.

entries.farsi: او نگردد گرم‏تر از گفتشان / ‏جان طاق او نگردد جفتشان
entries.english: If the entire world should say to him, “You are on the Way of God and the right religion,”

entries.farsi: گر همه‏ى عالم بگويندش توى / ‏بر ره يزدان و دين مستوى
entries.english: He will not be made hotter by their words: his lonely soul will not mate with them;

entries.farsi: او نيفتد در گمان از طعنشان / ‏او نگردد دردمند از ظعنشان
entries.english: And if they all should say to him, “You are astray: you think a mountain, and you are a blade of straw,”

entries.farsi: بلكه گر دريا و كوه آيد به گفت / ‏گويدش با گمرهى گشتى تو جفت
entries.english: He will not fall into opinion because of their taunts; he will not be grieved by their departure.

entries.farsi: ور همه گويند او را گمرهى / ‏كوه پندارى و تو برگ كهى
entries.english: No, if seas and mountains should come to speech and should say to him, “You are wedded to perdition,”

entries.farsi: هيچ يك ذره نيفتد در خيال / ‏يا به طعن طاعنان رنجور حال
entries.english: Not the least jot will he fall into phantasy or sickness on account of the taunts of the scoffers.

sections.title_fa: ‏مثال رنجور شدن آدمى به وهم تعظيم خلق و رغبت مشتريان به وى و حكايت معلم
sections.title_en: Parable of a man’s being made ill by vain conceit of the veneration in which he is held by the people and of the supplication addressed to him by those seeking his favour;
and the story of the Teacher.

entries.farsi: كودكان مكتبى از اوستاد / رنج ديدند از مالل و اجتهاد
entries.english: The boys in a certain school suffered at the hands of their master from weariness and toil.

entries.farsi: مشورت كردند در تعويق كار / تا معلم در فتد در اضطرار
entries.english: They consulted about stopping work, so that the teacher should be reduced to the necessity,

entries.farsi: تا رهيم از حبس و تنگى و ز كار / هست او چون سنگ خارا برقرار
entries.english: “Since no illness befalls him, which would cause him to take absence for several days,

entries.farsi: چون نمى‏آيد و را رنجوريى / ‏كه بگيرد چند روز او دوريى
entries.english: So that we might escape from imprisonment and confinement and work. He is fixed, like a solid rock.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى زيركتر اين تدبير كرد / كه بگويد اوستا چونى تو زرد
entries.english: One, the cleverest, planned that he should say, “Master, how are you pale?

entries.farsi: خير باشد رنگ تو بر جاى نيست / ‏اين اثر يا از هوا يا از تبى است
entries.english: May it be well! Your colour is changed: this is the effect either of air or of a fever.”

entries.farsi: اندكى اندر خيال افتد از اين / ‏تو برادر هم مدد كن اين چنين
entries.english: “At this he will begin to fancy a little: do you too, brother, help in like manner.

entries.farsi: آن خيالش اندكى افزون شود / كز خيالى عاقلى مجنون شود
entries.english: When you come in through the door of the school, say, ‘Master, is your state good?’

entries.farsi: چون در آيى از در مكتب بگو / خير باشد اوستا احوال تو
entries.english: That fancy of his will increase a little, for by a fancy a sensible man is driven mad.

entries.farsi: آن سوم و آن چارم و پنجم چنين / ‏در پى ما غم نمايند و حنين
entries.english: After us let the third and the fourth and the fifth show sympathy and sorrow likewise,

entries.farsi: تا چو سى كودك تواتر اين خبر / متفق گويند يابد مستقر
entries.english: So that, when with one consent thirty boys successively tell this story, it may find lodging.”

entries.farsi: متفق گشتند در عهد وثيق / ‏كه نگرداند سخن را يك رفيق
entries.english: Each said to him, “Bravo, O sagacious one! May your fortune rest on the favour!”

entries.farsi: هر يكى گفتش كه شاباش اى ذكى / ‏باد بختت بر عنايت متكى
entries.english: They agreed, in firm covenant, that no fellow should alter the words;

entries.farsi: بعد از آن سوگند داد او جمله را / تا كه غمازى نگويد ماجرا
entries.english: And afterwards he administered an oath to them all, lest any tell-tale should reveal the plot.

entries.farsi: راى آن كودك بچربيد از همه / ‏عقل او در پيش مى‏رفت از رمه
entries.english: The counsel of that boy prevailed over all; his intellect was going in front of the flock.

entries.farsi: آن تفاوت هست در عقل بشر / كه ميان شاهدان اندر صور
entries.english: There is the same difference in human intellect as amongst loved ones in forms.

entries.farsi: زين قبل فرمود احمد در مقال / ‏در زبان پنهان بود حسن رجال
entries.english: From this point of view, Ahmad said in talk, “The excellence of men is hidden in the tongue.”

sections.title_fa: عقول خلق متفاوت است در اصل فطرت و نزد معتزله متساوى است و تفاوت عقول از تحصيل علم است
sections.title_en: People’s intellects differ in their original nature, according to the Mu‘tazilites they are equal and the difference in intellects arises from the acquisition of knowledge.

entries.farsi: اختالف عقلها در اصل بود / بر وفاق سنيان بايد شنود
entries.english: You must hear in accordance with the Sunnis the difference in intellects was original,

entries.farsi: بر خالف قول اهل اعتزال / ‏كه عقول از اصل دارند اعتدال
entries.english: In contradiction to the doctrine of the Mu‘tazilites, who hold that intellects were originally equal,

entries.farsi: تجربه و تعليم بيش و كم كند / تا يكى را از يكى اعلم كند
entries.english: Experience and teaching makes them more or less, so that it makes one person more knowing than another.

entries.farsi: باطل است اين ز انكه راى كودكى / ‏كه ندارد تجربه در مسلكى
entries.english: This is false, because the counsel of a boy who has not experience in any course of action—

entries.farsi: بردميد انديشه‏اى ز آن طفل خرد / پير با صد تجربه بويى نبرد
entries.english: From that small child sprang up a thought the old man with a hundred experiences did not sense at all.

entries.farsi: خود فزون آن به كه آن از فطرت است / ‏تا ز افزونى كه جهد و فكرت است
entries.english: Truly, the superiority that is from nature is even better than the superiority that is endeavour and reflection.

entries.farsi: تو بگو داده‏ى خدا بهتر بود / يا كه لنگى راهوارانه رود
entries.english: Tell, is the gift of God better, or that a lame person should walk smoothly?

sections.title_fa: در وهم افگندن كودكان استاد را
sections.title_en: How the boys made the teacher imagine.

entries.farsi: روز گشت و آمدند آن كودكان / ‏بر همين فكرت ز خانه تا دكان
entries.english: Day broke, and those boys, on this thought, came from their homes to the shop.

entries.farsi: جمله استادند بيرون منتظر / تا در آيد اول آن يار مصر
entries.english: They all stood outside, waiting for that resolute fellow to go in first,

entries.farsi: ز انكه منبع او بده‏ست اين راى را / سر امام آيد هميشه پاى را
entries.english: Because he was the source of this plan: the head is always an Imam to the foot.

entries.farsi: او در آمد گفت استا را سالم / ‏خير باشد رنگ رويت زردفام
entries.english: O imitator, do not you seek precedence over one who is a source of the heavenly light.

entries.farsi: اى مقلد تو مجو پيشى بر آن / ‏كاو بود منبع ز نور آسمان
entries.english: He came in and said to the master “Salaam! I hope you are well. Your face is yellow in colour.”

entries.farsi: گفت استا نيست رنجى مر مرا / تو برو بنشين مگو ياوه هال
entries.english: The master said, “I have no ailment. Go and sit down and don’t talk nonsense, hey!”

entries.farsi: نفى كرد اما غبار وهم بد / اندكى اندر دلش ناگاه زد
entries.english: He denied, but the dust of evil imagination suddenly struck a little upon his heart.

entries.farsi: اندر آمد ديگرى گفت اين چنين / ‏اندكى آن وهم افزون شد بدين
entries.english: Another came in and said the like: by this that imagination was a little increased.

entries.farsi: همچنين تا وهم او قوت گرفت / ‏ماند اندر حال خود بس در شگفت
entries.english: In like manner, until his imagination gained strength and he was left marvelling exceedingly as to his state health.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيمار شدن فرعون هم به وهم از تعظيم خلقان
sections.title_en: How Pharaoh was made ill by vain imagination arising from the people’s reverence.

entries.farsi: سجده‏ى خلق از زن و از طفل و مرد / زد دل فرعون را رنجور كرد
entries.english: The people are prostrating themselves—women, children, and men— smote the heart of Pharaoh and made him ill.

entries.farsi: گفتن هر يك خداوند و ملك / ‏آن چنان كردش ز وهمى منهتك
entries.english: Every one’s calling him lord and king made him so tattered from a vain imagination,

entries.farsi: كه بدعوى الهى شد دلير / اژدها گشت و نمى‏شد هيچ سير
entries.english: That he dared to pretend to divinity: he became a dragon and would never be sated.

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى آفتش وهم است و ظن / ‏ز انكه در ظلمات شد او را وطن
entries.english: Imagination and opinion are the bane of the particular reason, because its dwelling-place is in the darkness.

entries.farsi: بر زمين گر نيم گز راهى بود / آدمى بى‏وهم ايمن مى‏رود
entries.english: If there be a path half an ell wide on the ground, a man will walk safely without imagining;

entries.farsi: بر سر ديوار عالى گر روى / ‏گر دو گز عرضش بود كج مى‏شوى
entries.english: If you walk on the top of a high wall, you will stagger even if its width be two ells;

entries.farsi: بلكه مى‏افتى ز لرزه‏ى دل به وهم / ‏ترس وهمى را نكو بنگر بفهم
entries.english: No, through imagination and from trembling of heart, you will be falling. Consider well and understand the fear that is due to imagination.

sections.title_fa: ‏رنجور شدن استاد به وهم
sections.title_en: How the teacher was made ill by imagination.

entries.farsi: گشت استا سست از وهم و ز بيم / ‏بر جهيد و مى‏كشانيد او گليم
entries.english: The master became unnerved by imagination and dread; he sprang up and began to drag his cloak along,

entries.farsi: خشمگين با زن كه مهر اوست سست / ‏من بدين حالم نپرسيد و نجست
entries.english: Angry with his wife and saying, “Her love is weak: I am in this state, and she did not ask and inquire.

entries.farsi: خود مرا آگه نكرد از رنگ من / ‏قصد دارد تا رهد از ننگ من
entries.english: She did not even inform me about my colour: she intends to be freed from my disgrace.

entries.farsi: او به حسن و جلوه‏ى خود مست گشت / ‏بى‏خبر كز بام افتادم چو طشت
entries.english: She has become intoxicated with her beauty and the display and is unaware that I have fallen from the roof, like a bowl.”

entries.farsi: آمد و در را به تندى واگشاد / كودكان اندر پى آن اوستاد
entries.english: He came and fiercely opened the door— the boys at the master’s heels.

entries.farsi: گفت زن خير است چون زود آمدى / ‏كه مبادا ذات نيكت را بدى
entries.english: His wife said, “Are you well? How have you come soon? May no evil happen to your goodly person!”

entries.farsi: گفت كورى رنگ و حال من ببين / ‏از غمم بيگانگان اندر حنين
entries.english: He said, “Are you blind? Look at my colour and appearance: strangers are lamenting my affliction,

entries.farsi: تو درون خانه از بغض و نفاق / ‏مى‏نبينى حال من در احتراق
entries.english: You, at home, from hatred and hypocrisy do not see the state of anguish I am in.”

entries.farsi: گفت زن اى خواجه عيبى نيستت / ‏وهم و ظن الش بى‏معنى‏ستت
entries.english: His wife said, “O sir, there is nothing wrong with you: it is your vain unreal imagination and opinion.”

entries.farsi: گفتش اى غر تو هنوزى در لجاج / ‏مى‏نبينى اين تغير و ارتجاج
entries.english: He said to her, “O strumpet, are you still obstinately disputing? Don’t you see this change and tremor?

entries.farsi: گر تو كور و كر شدى ما را چه جرم / ‏ما در اين رنجيم و در اندوه و گرم
entries.english: If you have become blind and deaf, what fault of mine is it? I am in this pain and grief and woe.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى خواجه بيارم آينه / ‏تا بدانى كه ندارم من گنه
entries.english: She said, “O sir, I will bring the mirror, in order that you may know that I am innocent.”

entries.farsi: گفت رو نه تو رهى نه آينه‏ت / ‏دايما در بغض و كينى و عنت
entries.english: “Go,” said he; “may neither you nor your mirror be saved! You are always in hatred and malice and sin.

entries.farsi: جامه‏ى خواب مرا رو گستران / ‏تا بخسبم كه سر من شد گران
entries.english: Make my bed at once, that I may lie down, for my head is sore.”

entries.farsi: زن توقف كرد مردش بانگ زد / كاى عدو زوتر ترا اين مى‏سزد
entries.english: The wife lingered; the man shouted at her, saying, “O hateful one, quicker! This is worthy of you.”

sections.title_fa: ‏در جامه‏ى خواب افتادن استاد و ناليدن او از وهم رنجورى
sections.title_en: How the master went to bed and moaned, imagining himself to be ill.

entries.farsi: جامه خواب آورد و گسترد آن عجوز / گفت امكان نى و باطن پر ز سوز
entries.english: The old woman brought the bed-clothes and spread them. She said, “There is no possibility, and my heart is filled with burning.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم متهم دارد مرا / ور نگويم جد شود اين ماجرا
entries.english: If I speak, he will hold me suspect; and if I say nothing, this affair will become serious.”

entries.farsi: فال بد رنجور گرداند همى / ‏آدمى را كه نبودستش غمى
entries.english: A man who has not suffered any pain is made ill by a bad omen.

entries.farsi: قول پيغمبر قبوله يفرض / ان تمارضتم لدينا تمرضوا
entries.english: It is obligatory to accept the saying of the Prophet, “If you pretend to be sick beside me, you will become sick.”

entries.farsi: گر بگويم او خيالى بر زند / فعل دارد زن كه خلوت مى‏كند
entries.english: “If I tell him, he will cast up a vain fancy; my wife has a design, for she is making arrangements to be alone.

entries.farsi: مر مرا از خانه بيرون مى‏كند / بهر فسقى فعل و افسون مى‏كند
entries.english: She is getting me out of the house, she is plotting and cajoling for the purpose of some wickedness.’”

entries.farsi: جامه خوابش كرد و استاد اوفتاد / آه آه و ناله از وى مى‏بزاد
entries.english: She prepared his bed, and the master fell down: sighs and moans were arising from him.

entries.farsi: كودكان آن جا نشستند و نهان / ‏درس مى‏خواندند با صد اندهان
entries.english: The boys sat there, reciting their lesson with a hundred sorrows in secret,

entries.farsi: كاين همه كرديم و ما زندانييم / ‏بد بنايى بود ما بد بانييم
entries.english: Thinking, “We have done all this and we are prisoners: it was a bad building, and we are bad builders.”

sections.title_fa: دوم بار در وهم افگندن كودكان استاد را كه او را از قرآن خواندن ما درد سر افزايد
sections.title_en: How for the second time the boys made the master imagine, saying that their recitation of the Qur’an would increase his headache.

entries.farsi: گفت آن زيرك كه اى قوم پسند / درس خوانيد و كنيد آوا بلند
entries.english: The clever boy said, “O good fellows, recite the lesson and make your voices loud.”

entries.farsi: درد سر افزايد استا را ز بانگ / ‏ارزد اين كاو درد يابد بهر دانگ
entries.english: When they were reciting, he said, “Boys, the noise we are making will do the master harm.

entries.farsi: چون همى‏خواندند گفت اى كودكان / ‏بانگ ما استاد را دارد زيان
entries.english: The master’s headache will be increased by the noise: is it worthwhile that he should suffer pain for the sake of pence?”

entries.farsi: گفت استا راست مى‏گويد رويد / درد سر افزون شدم بيرون شويد
entries.english: The master said, “He is speaking the truth: depart. My headache is worse: go out!”

sections.title_fa: خالص يافتن كودكان از مكتب بدين مكر
sections.title_en: How the boys escaped from school by this trick.

entries.farsi: سجده كردند و بگفتند اى كريم / ‏دور بادا از تو رنجورى و بيم
entries.english: They bowed and said, “O honoured sir, may illness and danger be far from you!”

entries.farsi: پس برون جستند سوى خانه‏ها / همچو مرغان در هواى دانه‏ها
entries.english: Then they bounded off to their homes, like birds in desire of grain.

entries.farsi: عذر آوردند كاى مادر تو بيست / ‏اين گناه از ما و از تقصير نيست
entries.english: Their mothers became angry with them and said, “A school-day and you at play!”

entries.farsi: مادرانشان خشمگين گشتند و گفت / ‏روز كتاب و شما با لهو جفت
entries.english: They offered excuses, saying, “Stop, mother! This sin does not proceed from us and is not caused by our fault.

entries.farsi: از قضاى آسمان استاد ما / گشت رنجور و سقيم و مبتال
entries.english: By the destiny of Heaven our master has become ill and sick and afflicted.”

entries.farsi: مادران گفتند مكر است و دروغ / ‏صد دروغ آريد بهر طمع دوغ
entries.english: The mothers said, “It is a trick and a lie: you bring forward a hundred lies because of your greed for buttermilk.

entries.farsi: كودكان گفتند بسم اللهَّ رويد / بر دروغ و صدق ما واقف شويد
entries.english: In the morning we will come to the master, that we may see the bottom of this trick of yours.”

entries.farsi: ما صباح آييم پيش اوستا / تا ببينيم اصل اين مكر شما
entries.english: “Go in God’s name,” said the boys; “inform yourselves as to our lying or telling the truth.”

sections.title_fa: رفتن مادران كودكان به عيادت اوستاد
sections.title_en: How the mothers of the boys went to visit the sick master.

entries.farsi: بامدادان آمدند آن مادران / ‏خفته استا همچو بيمار گران
entries.english: At morning those mothers came; the master in bed like one who is gravely ill,

entries.farsi: هم عرق كرده ز بسيارى لحاف / ‏سر ببسته رو كشيده در سجاف
entries.english: Perspiring on account of the great number of coverlets, his head bandaged and his face enveloped in the quilt.

entries.farsi: آه آهى مى‏كند آهسته او / جملگان گشتند هم الحول گو
entries.english: He was moaning softly: they too all began to cry “La hawl.”

entries.farsi: خير باشد اوستاد اين درد سر / جان تو ما را نبوده زين خبر
entries.english: They said, “Master, we hope all will be well. This headache— by your soul, we were not aware of it.”

entries.farsi: گفت من هم بى‏خبر بودم از اين / ‏آگهم مادر غران كردند هين
entries.english: He replied, “I also was not aware of it; the whoresons made me aware, mark you.

entries.farsi: من بدم غافل به شغل قال و قيل / ‏بود در باطن چنين رنجى ثقيل
entries.english: I did not notice, through being busy with discourse, within there was such a severe malady.”

entries.farsi: از زنان مصر يوسف شد سمر / كه ز مشغولى بشد ز ايشان خبر
entries.english: When a man is busy in earnest, he is blind to the sight of his pain.

entries.farsi: چون به جد مشغول باشد آدمى / ‏او ز ديد رنج خود باشد عمى
entries.english: It has become an oft-told tale concerning the women of Joseph’s Egypt that consciousness departed from them on account of their pre-occupation.

entries.farsi: پاره پاره كرده ساعدهاى خويش / ‏روح واله كه نه پس بيند نه پيش
entries.english: They cut their fore-arms to pieces: the spirit is distraught, so that it looks neither behind nor before.

entries.farsi: اى بسا مرد شجاع اندر حراب / ‏كه ببرد دست يا پايش ضراب
entries.english: Oh, many a brave man in battle whose hand or foot is cut by blows,

entries.farsi: او همان دست آورد در گيرودار / بر گمان آن كه هست او برقرار
entries.english: And he bears that same hand into the combat, thinking that it remains firm.

entries.farsi: خود ببيند دست رفته در ضرر / خون از او بسيار رفته بى‏خبر
entries.english: Indeed he will see that his hand has been injured much blood has gone from him unawares.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه تن روح را چون لباسى است ‏ و اين دست آستين دست روح است و اين پاى موزه‏ى پاى روح است
sections.title_en: Explaining that the body is as a garment to the spirit and that this hand is the sleeve of the spirit’s hand, and that this foot is the shoe of the spirit’s foot.

entries.farsi: تا بدانى كه تن آمد چون لباس / ‏رو بجو البس لباسى را مليس
entries.english: That you may know that the body is like a garment. Go, seek the wearer of the garment, and do not lick a garment.

entries.farsi: روح را توحيد اللهَّ خوشتر است / ‏غير ظاهر دست و پاى ديگر است
entries.english: To the spirit the knowledge of the Unity is sweeter: it has a hand and foot different from those which are visible.

entries.farsi: دست و پا در خواب بينى و ائتالف / ‏آن حقيقت دان مدانش از گزاف
entries.english: You may behold in dream the hand and foot and their connection: deem that a reality, deem it not to be in vain.

entries.farsi: آن تويى كه بى‏بدن دارى بدن / ‏پس مترس از جسم و جان بيرون شدن
entries.english: You are such that without the body you have a body: do not, then, dread the going forth of the soul from the body.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن درويش كه در كوه خلوت كرده بود و بيان حالوت انقطاع و خلوت و داخل شدن در اين ‏منقبت كه أنا جليس من ذكرني و أنيس من استأنس بي ‏گر با همه‏اى چو بى‏منى بى‏همه‏اى ور بى‏همه‏اى چو با منى با همه‏اى

sections.title_en: Story of the dervish who had secluded himself in the mountains, with an account of the sweetness of severance and seclusion and of entering upon this path, for, “I am the companion of them that commemorate Me and the friend of them that take Me as their friend. If you are with all, you art without all when you art without Me; And if you are without all, you are with all when you are with Me.”

entries.farsi: بود درويشى به كهسارى مقيم / ‏خلوت او را بود هم خواب و نديم
entries.english: There was a dervish dwelling in a mountainous place: solitude was his bedfellow and boon-companion.

entries.farsi: چون ز خالق مى‏رسيد او را شمول / ‏بود از انفاس مرد و زن ملول
entries.english: Since the refreshing breeze was coming for him from the Creator, he was weary of the breaths of man and woman.

entries.farsi: همچنان كه سهل شد ما را حضر / سهل شد هم قوم ديگر را سفر
entries.english: Just as staying at home is easy to us, so travelling is easy to another class of people.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه عاشقى بر سرورى / ‏عاشق است آن خواجه بر آهنگرى
entries.english: In the same way as you art in love with dominion, that worthy man is in love with the ironsmith’s handicraft.

entries.farsi: دست و پا بى‏ميل جنبان كى شود / خار و خس بى‏آب و بادى كى رود
entries.english: Everyone has been made for some particular work, and the desire for that has been put into his heart.

entries.farsi: هر كسى را بهر كارى ساختند / ميل آن را در دلش انداختند
entries.english: How should hand and foot be set in motion without desire? How should sticks and straws go without any water or wind?

entries.farsi: ور ببينى ميل خود سوى زمين / ‏نوحه مى‏كن هيچ منشين از حنين
entries.english: If you see your desire towards Heaven, unfold the wings of empire, like the Huma;

entries.farsi: گر ببينى ميل خود سوى سما / پر دولت بر گشا همچون هما
entries.english: But if you see your desire towards the earth, keep lamenting, cease not at all from moaning.

entries.farsi: عاقالن خود نوحه‏ها پيشين كنند / جاهالن آخر به سر بر مى‏زنند
entries.english: The wise, indeed, make lamentations at first; the foolish beat their heads at the last.

entries.farsi: ز ابتداى كار آخر را ببين / ‏تا نباشى تو پشيمان يوم دين
entries.english: From the beginning of the affair discern the end, so that you may not be repenting on the Day of Judgement.

sections.title_fa: ديدن زرگر عاقبت كار را و سخن بر وفق عاقبت گفتن با مستعير ترازو
sections.title_en: How a goldsmith discerned the end of the affair and spoke in accordance with the end to one who wished to borrow his scales.

entries.farsi: آن يكى آمد به پيش زرگرى / ‏كه ترازو ده كه بر سنجم زرى
entries.english: A certain man came to a goldsmith, saying, “Give me the scales, that I may weigh some gold.”

entries.farsi: گفت خواجه رو مرا غربال نيست / ‏گفت ميزان ده بدين تسخر مه‏ايست
entries.english: The master said, “Go, I have no sieve.” “Give me the scales,” he replied, “and don’t stop to jest like this.”

entries.farsi: گفت جاروبى ندارم در دكان / ‏گفت بس بس اين مضاحك را بمان
entries.english: He said, “I have no broom in the shop.” “Enough, enough!” cried the other; “leave these jokes.

entries.farsi: من ترازويى كه مى‏خواهم بده / ‏خويشتن را كر مكن هر سو مجه
entries.english: Give the scales which I am asking for; don’t make yourself out to be deaf, don’t jump in every direction.”

entries.farsi: اين شنيدم ليك پيرى مرتعش / ‏دست لرزان جسم تو نامنتعش
entries.english: He said, “I heard what you said, I am not deaf; you must not think that I am nonsensical.

entries.farsi: گفت بشنيدم سخن كر نيستم / ‏تا نپندارى كه بى‏معنيستم
entries.english: I heard this, but you are a shaky old man: your hand is trembling and your body is not erect;

entries.farsi: و آن زر تو هم قراضه‏ى خرد و مرد / دست لرزد پس بريزد زر خرد
entries.english: And moreover that gold of yours consists of little tiny filings: your hand trembles, so the fragments of gold will drop;

entries.farsi: پس بگويى خواجه جاروبى بيار / تا بجويم زر خود را در غبار
entries.english: Then you will say, ‘Sir, fetch a broom that I may look in the dust for my gold.’

entries.farsi: چون بروبى خاك را جمع آورى / ‏گوييم غلبير خواهم اى جرى
entries.english: When you sweep, you will gather dust; you will say to me, ‘I want the sieve, O gallant man.’

entries.farsi: من ز اول ديدم آخر را تمام / ‏جاى ديگر رو از اينجا و السلام
entries.english: I from the beginning discerned the end complete. Go from here to some other place, and farewell!”

sections.title_fa: بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى آن زاهد كوهى كه نذر كرده بود كه ميوه‏ى كوهى از درخت باز نكنم و درخت نفشانم و كسى را نگويم صريح و كنايت كه بيفشان آن خورم كه باد افكنده باشد از درخت
sections.title_en: The rest of the Story of the ascetic of the mountain who had made a vow that he would not pluck any mountain fruit from the trees or shake the trees or tell anyone to shake them, either plainly or in veiled terms, and that he would only eat what the wind might cause to fall from the trees.

entries.farsi: اندر آن كه بود اشجار و ثمار / بس مرود كوهى آن جا بى‏شمار
entries.english: On that mountain were trees and fruits; there were many mountain-pears- numberless.

entries.farsi: گفت آن درويش يا رب با تو من / ‏عهد كردم زين نچينم در زمن
entries.english: The dervish said, “O Lord, I make a covenant with You. I will not pluck any of these during the time.

entries.farsi: جز از آن ميوه كه باد انداختش / ‏من نچينم از درخت منتعش
entries.english: I will not pluck from the raised-up trees but the fruit that the wind has caused to fall.”

entries.farsi: مدتى بر نذر خود بودش وفا / تا در آمد امتحانات قضا
entries.english: For a while he kept his vow faithfully: till the tribulations of Destiny came on.

entries.farsi: زين سبب فرمود استثنا كنيد / گر خدا خواهد به پيمان بر زنيد
