entries.english: On this account He has commanded, saying, “Make the exception: attach ‘if God will’ to your promise.

entries.farsi: هر زمان دل را دگر ميلى دهم / ‏هر نفس بر دل دگر داغى نهم
entries.english: Every instant I give to the heart a different desire, every moment I lay upon the heart a different brand.

entries.farsi: كل اصباح لنا شأن جديد / كل شي‏ء عن مرادي ال يحيد
entries.english: At every dawn I have a new employment: nothing turns aside from that which I have willed.”

entries.farsi: در حديث آمد كه دل همچون پرى است / ‏در بيابانى اسير صرصرى است
entries.english: It has come down in the Traditions that the heart is like a feather in a desert, the captive of a violent blast.

entries.farsi: باد پر را هر طرف راند گزاف / ‏گه چپ و گه راست با صد اختالف
entries.english: The wind drives the feather recklessly in every direction, now left, now right, with a hundred diversities.

entries.farsi: در حديث ديگر اين دل دان چنان / ‏كآب جوشان ز آتش اندر قازغان
entries.english: In another Tradition, “Deem this heart to be as water boiling in a cauldron from fire.”

entries.farsi: هر زمان دل را دگر رايى بود / آن نه از وى ليك از جايى بود
entries.english: At every time the heart has a different resolution: that is not from it, but from a certain place.

entries.farsi: پس چرا ايمن شوى بر راى دل / ‏عهد بندى تا شوى آخر خجل
entries.english: Why, then, will you trust in the heart’s resolution and make a covenant, that in the end you should be shamed?

entries.farsi: اين هم از تاثير حكم است و قدر / چاه مى‏بينى و نتوانى حذر
entries.english: This too is from the effect of the ordinance and decree; you see the pit and cannot take precaution.

entries.farsi: اين عجب كه دام بيند هم وتد / گر بخواهد ور نخواهد مى‏فتد
entries.english: It is no wonder, indeed, for the flying bird not to see the snare fall into destruction;

entries.farsi: نيست خود از مرغ پران اين عجب / ‏كه نبيند دام و افتد در عطب
entries.english: The wonder is that it should see both the snare and the net-pin and fall willy-nilly.

entries.farsi: چشم باز و گوش باز و دام پيش / ‏سوى دامى مى‏پرد با پر خويش
entries.english: Eye open and ear open and the snare in front, it is flying towards a snare with its own wings.

sections.title_fa: تشبيه بند و دام قضا به صورت پنهان به اثر پيدا
sections.title_en: A comparison the bonds and snares of Destiny, though outwardly invisible, are manifest in their effects.

entries.farsi: بينى اندر دلق مهتر زاده‏اى / ‏سر برهنه در بال افتاده‏اى
entries.english: You may see a nobleman’s son in a tattered cloak, bareheaded, fallen into affliction.

entries.farsi: در هواى نابكارى سوخته / ‏اقمشه و امالك خود بفروخته
entries.english: Consumed with passion for some ne’er-do-well, sold his furniture and properties

entries.farsi: خان و مان رفته شده بد نام و خوار / كام دشمن مى‏رود ادباروار
entries.english: His household gone, become ill-famed and despised; he walks along like misfortune, to the joy of his foes.

entries.farsi: زاهدى بيند بگويد اى كيا / همتى مى‏دار از بهر خدا
entries.english: He sees an ascetic, he will say, “O venerable sir, bestow on me a benediction for God’s sake,

entries.farsi: كاندر اين ادبار زشت افتاده‏ام / ‏مال و زر و نعمت از كف داده‏ام
entries.english: For I have fallen into this ugly misfortune and have let wealth and gold and happiness go from my hand

entries.farsi: همتى تا بو كه من زين وارهم / ‏زين گل تيره بود كه بر جهم
entries.english: A benediction, so that maybe I shall be delivered from this and maybe escape from this dark clay.

entries.farsi: اين دعا مى‏خواهد او از عام و خاص / ‏كالخالص و الخالص و الخالص
entries.english: He is begging this prayer of high and low, crying, “Release and release and release!”

entries.farsi: از كدامين بند مى‏جويى خالص / ‏و از كدامين حبس مى‏جويى مناص
entries.english: His hand is free and his foot free and there is no chain, no custodian over him, and no iron.

entries.farsi: دست باز و پاى باز و بند نى / ‏نى موكل بر سرش نى آهنى
entries.english: From what chain are you seeking release, and from what imprisonment art you seeking to escape?

entries.farsi: بند تقدير و قضاى مختفى / ‏كه نبيند آن بجز جان صفى
entries.english: The hidden chain of fore-ordainment and destiny, which none but the elect spirit may behold

entries.farsi: ز انكه آهنگر مر آن را بشكند / حفره‏گر هم خشت زندان بر كند
entries.english: Though it is not visible, it is in ambush; it is worse that prison and chains of iron,

entries.farsi: گر چه پيدا نيست آن در مكمن است / ‏بدتر از زندان و بند آهن است
entries.english: Because that the ironsmith may break, and the excavator may even dig up the bricks of the prison;

entries.farsi: اى عجب اين بند پنهان گران / ‏عاجز از تكسير آن آهنگران
entries.english: O wonder, this heavy hidden chain the ironsmiths are powerless to shatter.

entries.farsi: ديدن آن بند احمد را رسد / بر گلوى بسته حَبْلٌ مِنْ مسد
entries.english: Vision of that chain belongs to Ahmad: on the throat bound with a cord of palm-fibres.

entries.farsi: ديد بر پشت عيال بو لهب / ‏تنگ هيزم گفت حماله‏ى حطب
entries.english: He saw a load of firewood on the back of Abu Lahab’s wife and said the carrier of faggots.

entries.farsi: حبل و هيزم را جز او چشمى نديد / كه پديد آيد بر او هر ناپديد
entries.english: The cord and the firewood no eye beheld but his, for to him every unseen thing becomes visible.

entries.farsi: باقيانش جمله تاويلى كنند / كاين ز بى‏هوشى است و ايشان هوشمند
entries.english: All the rest interpret it, for this arises from senselessness, and they are sensible—

entries.farsi: ليك از تاثير آن پشتش دو تو / گشته و نالان شده او پيش تو
entries.english: But from the effect of that his back has been bent double, and he is moaning before you,

entries.farsi: كه دعايى همتى تا وارهم / ‏تا از اين بند نهان بيرون جهم
entries.english: A prayer! A benediction! That I may be delivered and that I may escape from this hidden chain.”

entries.farsi: آن كه بيند اين عالمتها پديد / چون نداند او شقى را از سعيد
entries.english: He who sees these signs clearly, how should not he know the damned from the blest?

entries.farsi: داند و پوشد به امر ذو الجلال / ‏كه نباشد كشف راز حق حالل
entries.english: He knows, and by command of the Almighty he conceals, for it would not be lawful to divulge the secret of God.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد آن فقير / از مجاعت شد زبون و تن اسير
entries.english: This discourse has no end. That dervish, through hunger, became feeble and his body a prisoner.

sections.title_fa: ‏مضطر شدن فقير نذر كرده به كندن امرود از درخت و گوشمال حق رسيدن بى‏مهلت
sections.title_en: How the dervish who had made the vow was reduced to plucking the pears from the tree, and how God’s chastisement came without delay.

entries.farsi: پنج روز آن باد امرودى نريخت / ‏ز آتش جوعش صبورى مى‏گريخت
entries.english: For five days the wind did not cause a single pear to drop, and on account of the fire of hunger his patience was fleeing.

entries.farsi: بر سر شاخى مرودى چند ديد / باز صبرى كرد و خود را واكشيد
entries.english: He espied several pears on a bough, once more he acted with patience and restrained himself.

entries.farsi: باد آمد شاخ را سر زير كرد / طبع را بر خوردن آن چير كرد
entries.english: The wind came and lowered the end of the bough and caused his carnal nature to prevail for the eating of that.

entries.farsi: جوع و ضعف و قوت جذب قضا / كرد زاهد را ز نذرش بى‏وفا
entries.english: Hunger and weakness and the strength of Destiny’s pull made the ascetic unfaithful to his vow.

entries.farsi: چون كه از امرودبن ميوه سكست / ‏گشت اندر نذر و عهد خويش سست
entries.english: When he had plucked fruit from the pear-tree, he became frail in his vow and promise.

entries.farsi: هم در آن دم گوشمال حق رسيد / چشم او بگشاد و گوش او كشيد
entries.english: At the same instant God’s chastisement arrived: it opened his eye and pulled his ear.

sections.title_fa: متهم كردن آن شيخ را با دزدان و بريدن دستش را
sections.title_en: How the Shaykh was suspected of being in company with thieves and had his hand cut off.

entries.farsi: بيست از دزدان بدند آن جا و بيش / ‏بخش مى‏كردند مسروقات خويش
entries.english: In that place there were twenty thieves and more, dividing the things they had stolen.

entries.farsi: شحنه را غماز آگه كرده بود / مردم شحنه بر افتادند زود
entries.english: The perfect had been informed by an informer: the prefect’s men quickly fell upon them.

entries.farsi: هم بدان جا پاى چپ و دست راست / ‏جمله را ببريد و غوغايى بخاست
entries.english: He cut off on the spot the left feet and right hands of them all, and a great hubbub arose.

entries.farsi: دست زاهد هم بريده شد غلط / پاش را مى‏خواست هم كردن سقط
entries.english: The ascetic’s hand too was cut off by mistake; he was about to make his foot also fall,

entries.farsi: در زمان آمد سوارى بس گزين / ‏بانگ بر زد بر عوان كاى سگ ببين
entries.english: Just in time, a very elect cavalier came up and shouted at the officer, “Look out, O dog!

entries.farsi: اين فالن شيخ است و ابدال خدا / دست او را تو چرا كردى جدا
entries.english: This is such-and-such a Shaykh and Abdal of God: why have you severed his hand?”

entries.farsi: آن عوان بدريد جامه تيز رفت / ‏پيش شحنه داد آگاهيش تفت
entries.english: The officer rent his garment and went speedily to the prefect and gave him the information at once.

entries.farsi: شحنه آمد پا برهنه عذر خواه / ‏كه ندانستم خدا بر من گواه
entries.english: The prefect came bare-footed, begging pardon. “I did not know,” he said; “God will bear me witness.

entries.farsi: هين بحل كن مر مرا زين كار زشت / ‏اى كريم و سرور اهل بهشت
entries.english: Pray now absolve me from this foul deed, O generous man and chief of the inhabitants of Paradise!”

entries.farsi: گفت مى‏دانم سبب اين نيش را / مى‏شناسم من گناه خويش را
entries.english: He said, “I know the cause of this knife: I recognise my sin.

entries.farsi: من شكستم حرمت ايمان او / پس يمينم برد دادستان او
entries.english: I violated the sanctity of His oaths: therefore His judgement took my right hand away.

entries.farsi: من شكستم عهد و دانستم بد است / ‏تا رسيد آن شومى جرات به دست
entries.english: I broke my covenant and knew it was evil, so that that ill-omened audacity reached my hand.

entries.farsi: دست ما و پاى ما و مغز و پوست / ‏باد اى والى فداى حكم دوست
entries.english: May my hand and my foot and brain and skin be offered in sacrifice, O governor, to the decree of the Beloved!

entries.farsi: قسم من بود اين ترا كردم حالل / ‏تو ندانستى ترا نبود وبال
entries.english: It was my lot. I absolve you from this. You did not know: you have no guilt.

entries.farsi: و انكه او دانست او فرمان رواست / ‏با خدا سامان پيچيدن كجاست
entries.english: And He that knew He is the One whose command is carried into execution: where is the power of struggling with God?”

entries.farsi: اى بسا مرغى پريده دانه جو / كه بريده حلق او هم حلق او
entries.english: Oh, many the bird flying in search of grain whose gullet was cut by its gullet!

entries.farsi: اى بسا ماهى در آب دور دست / ‏گشته از حرص گلو مأخوذ شست
entries.english: Oh, many the bird that, through its belly and pangs of hunger, was made captive in a cage on the edge of a terrace!

entries.farsi: اى بسا مرغى ز معده و ز مغص / ‏بر كنار بام محبوس قفص
entries.english: Oh, many the fish that, because of its gullet’s greed, was caught by a hook in water hard to reach!

entries.farsi: اى بسا مستور در پرده بده / ‏شومى فرج و گلو رسوا شده
entries.english: Oh, many the chaste woman in a curtained bower that was brought to open shame by the misfortune of lust and gluttony!

entries.farsi: اى بسا قاضى حبر نيك خو / از گلو و رشوتى او زرد رو
entries.english: Oh, many the learned and honest judge that was disgraced by greed and bribery!

entries.farsi: بلكه در هاروت و ماروت آن شراب / ‏از عروج چرخشان شد سد باب
entries.english: No, in the case of Harut and Marut that wine debarred them from ascending to Heaven

entries.farsi: بايزيد از بهر اين كرد احتراز / ديد در خود كاهلى اندر نماز
entries.english: On this account Bayazid took precaution: he observed in himself remissness in the ritual prayer.

entries.farsi: از سبب انديشه كرد آن ذو لباب / ‏ديد علت خوردن بسيار از آب
entries.english: That possessor of the marrow meditated concerning the cause, he perceived that the cause was much water-drinking.

entries.farsi: گفت تا سالى نخواهم خورد آب / ‏آن چنان كرد و خدايش داد تاب
entries.english: He said, “For a year I will not drink water.” He acted accordingly, and God bestowed on him the power.

entries.farsi: اين كمينه جهد او بد بهر دين / ‏گشت او سلطان و قطب العارفين
entries.english: This was his least penance for the Religion’s sake: he became a sultan and the Pole of the Gnostics.

entries.farsi: چون بريده شد براى حلق دست / ‏مرد زاهد را در شكوى ببست
entries.english: Since the ascetic’s hand had been cut off by reason of his gullet, he closed the door of complaint.

entries.farsi: شيخ اقطع گشت نامش پيش خلق / در عريش او را يكى زاير بيافت
entries.english: His name amongst the people came to be Shaykh Aqta: the calamities of his gullet made him well-known by this.

sections.title_fa: ‏كرامات شيخ اقطع و زنبيل بافتن او به دو دست
sections.title_en: The miraculous gifts of Shaykh Aqta, and how he used to weave palm-leaf baskets with both hands

entries.farsi: ‏كاو به هر دو دست مى زنبيل بافت / گفت او را اى عدوى جان خويش
entries.english: A visitor found him in his hut, that he was weaving a basket with both hands.

entries.farsi: ‏در عريشم آمدى سر كرده پيش / اين چرا كردى شتاب اندر سباق
entries.english: He said to him, “O enemy of your own life, you have come putting your head into my hut.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت از افراط مهر و اشتياق / پس تبسم كرد و گفت اكنون بيا
entries.english: Why have you made such hot haste?” He replied, “From excess of love and longing.”

entries.farsi: ليك مخفى دار اين را اى كيا / تا نميرم من مگو اين با كسى
entries.english: Then he (the Shaykh) smiled and said, “Now come in, but keep this secret, O noble sir.

entries.farsi: ‏نه قرينى نه حبيبى نه خسى / بعد از آن قومى دگر از روزنش
entries.english: Till I die, do not tell this to any one, neither to a comrade nor to a beloved nor to a worthless fellow.”

entries.farsi: ‏مطلع گشتند بر بافيدنش / گفت حكمت را تو دانى كردگار
entries.english: Afterwards other folk, through his window, became acquainted with his weaving.

entries.farsi: من كنم پنهان تو كردى آشكار / آمد الهامش كه يك چندى بدند
entries.english: He said, “O Creator, You know the wisdom. I conceal, You have revealed it.”

entries.farsi: كه در اين غم بر تو منكر مى‏شدند / كه مگر سالوس بود او در طريق
entries.english: The Divine inspiration came to him: “There were a number of people who were beginning to disbelieve in you in this affliction,

entries.farsi: ‏كه خدا رسواش كرد اندر فريق / من نخواهم كان رمه كافر شوند
entries.english: Saying, maybe he was a hypocrite in the Way, so that God made him infamous among humankind.’

entries.farsi: در ضاللت در گمان بد روند / اين كرامت را بكرديم آشكار
entries.english: I do not wish that that party should become infidels and in thinking evil fall into perdition;

entries.farsi: كه دهيمت دست اندر وقت كار / تا كه آن بى‏چارگان بد گمان
entries.english: We divulged this miracle—, that We give you a hand in your working-time—

entries.farsi: ‏رد نگردند از جناب آسمان / من ترا بى‏اين كرامتها ز پيش
entries.english: To the end that these wretched evil-thinking men may not be turned back from the Lord of Heaven

entries.farsi: ‏خود تسلى دادمى از ذات خويش / اين كرامت بهر ايشان دادمت
entries.english: Formerly, indeed, without these miracles I was giving you consolation from My Person;

entries.farsi: ‏وين چراغ از بهر آن بنهادمت / تو از آن بگذشته‏اى كز مرگ تن
entries.english: This miracle I have given you for their sake, and on that account have I bestowed on you this lamp.

entries.farsi: ‏ترسى و تفريق اجزاى بدن / وهم تفريق سر و پا از تو رفت
entries.english: You are past being afraid of bodily death and dismemberment of the limbs.

entries.farsi: ‏دفع وهم اسپر رسيدت نيك زفت / ساحران را نه كه فرعون لعين
entries.english: Vain imagination concerning the dismemberment of head and foot has gone from you: there has come to you, for a defense against imagi­ nation, a shield exceeding strong.”

sections.title_fa: سبب جرات ساحران فرعون بر قطع دست و پا
sections.title_en: The reason why the magicians of Pharaoh had courage to suffer the amputation of their hands and feet

entries.farsi: ‏كرد تهديد سياست بر زمين / كه ببرم دست و پاتان از خالف
entries.english: Is it not that the accursed Pharaoh threatened punishment on the earth,

entries.farsi: ‏وهم و تخويفند و وسواس و گمان / كه بودشان لرزه و تخويف و ترس
entries.english: Saying, “I will cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then I will hang you up: I will not hold you exempt”?

entries.farsi: ‏پس در آويزم ندارمتان معاف / او همى‏پنداشت كايشان در همان
entries.english: He thought that they were in the same imagination and terror and distraction and doubt,

entries.farsi: بر دريچه‏ى نور دل بنشسته‏اند / سايه‏ى خود را ز خود دانسته‏اند
entries.english: So that they would be trembling and terrified and affrighted by the vain imaginings and threats of the carnal soul

entries.farsi: ‏از توهمها و تهديدات نفس / او نمى‏دانست كايشان رسته‏اند
entries.english: He did not know that they had been delivered and were seated at the window of the light of the heart;

entries.farsi: چابك و چست و گش و برجسته‏اند / هاون گردون اگر صد بارشان
entries.english: They had recognised their shadows from their selves, and were brisk and alert and happy and exulting;

entries.farsi: ‏خرد كوبد اندر اين گلزارشان / اصل اين تركيب را چون ديده‏اند
entries.english: If the mortar of the Sky should pound them small a hundred times in this miry place,

entries.farsi: از فروع وهم كم ترسيده‏اند / اين جهان خواب است اندر ظن مه‏ايست
entries.english: Since they had seen the origin of this composition, they were not afraid of the derivatives of imagination.

entries.farsi: ‏گر رود در خواب دستى باك نيست / گر به خواب اندر سرت ببريد گاز
entries.english: This world is a dream—do not rest in opinion; if in dream a hand go, it is no harm.

entries.farsi: هم سرت بر جاست هم عمرت دراز / گر ببينى خواب در خود را دو نيم
entries.english: If in dream a pruning-fork has cut off your head, not only is your head in its place but your life is prolonged.

entries.farsi: ‏تن درستى چون بخيزى نى سقيم / حاصل اندر خواب نقصان بدن
entries.english: If in dream you see yourself in two halves, you are sound in body when you rise, not sick.

entries.farsi: ‏نيست باك و نى دو صد پاره شدن / اين جهان را كه به صورت قائم است
entries.english: The sum: in dreams it is no harm for the body to be maimed or to be torn into two hundred pieces.

entries.farsi: ‏سالكان اين ديده پيدا بى‏رسول / روز در خوابى مگو كاين خواب نيست
entries.english: The Prophet said of this world, which is substantial in appearance, that it is the sleeper’s dream.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت پيغمبر كه حلم نائم است / از ره تقليد تو كردى قبول
entries.english: You have accepted this conventionally; the travellers have beheld this clairvoyantly, without the Prophet.

entries.farsi: ‏سايه فرع است اصل جز مهتاب نيست / خواب و بيداريت آن دان اى عضد
entries.english: You are asleep in the daytime: do not say that this is not sleep. The shadow is derivative; the origin is nothing but the moonlight.

entries.farsi: كه ببيند خفته كاو در خواب شد / او گمان برده كه اين دم خفته‏ام
entries.english: Know, O comrade that your sleep and waking is as though a sleeper should dream that he has gone to sleep.

entries.farsi: چون بخواهد باز خود قايم كند / كور را هر گام باشد ترس چاه
entries.english: He thinks, “Now I am asleep,” unaware that he is in the second sleep.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏خبر ز آن كاوست در خواب دوم / كوزه‏گر گر كوزه‏اى را بشكند
entries.english: If the potter breaks a pot, he himself will restore it when he wishes.

entries.farsi: پس بداند او مغاك و چاه را / پا و زانويش نلرزد هر دمى
entries.english: The blind man at every step is afraid of the pit: he walks on the road with a thousand fears;

entries.farsi: ‏با هزاران ترس مى‏آيد به راه / مرد بينا ديد عرض راه را
entries.english: The seeing man has seen the width of the road, so he knows the hole and the pit;

entries.farsi: ‏رو ترش كى دارد او از هر غمى / خيز فرعونا كه ما آن نيستيم
entries.english: His legs and knees do not tremble at any time: how should he look sour because of any affliction?

entries.farsi: ‏كه به هر بانگى و غولى بيستيم / خرقه‏ى ما را بدر دوزنده هست
entries.english: “Arise, O Pharaoh! For we are not such as to stop at every cry and ghoul.

entries.farsi: خوش در آريم اى عدوى نابكار / خوشتر از تجريد از تن و ز مزاج
entries.english: Rend our mantle! There is One who will sew; and if not, truly the more naked we are, the better for us.

entries.farsi: ‏ور نه خود ما را برهنه‏تر به است / بى‏لباس اين خوب را اندر كنار
entries.english: Without raiment we would fain clasp this Beauteous One to our bosoms, O enemy good-for-nothing!

entries.farsi: ‏نيست اى فرعون بى‏الهام گيج / گفت استر با شتر كاى خوش رفيق
entries.english: There is nothing sweeter than to be stripped of the body and the temperament, O stupid uninspired Pharaoh!”

sections.title_fa: شكايت استر پيش شتر كه من بسيار در رو مى‏افتم و تو نمى‏افتى الا به نادر
sections.title_en: How the mule complained to the camel, saying, “I am often falling on my face, while you fall but seldom.”

entries.farsi: ‏در فراز و شيب و در راه دقيق / تو نيايى در سر و خوش مى‏روى
entries.english: Said the mule to the camel, “O good friend, in hill and dale and in the obscure track

entries.farsi: ‏خواه در خشكى و خواه اندر نمى / اين سبب را باز گو با من كه چيست
entries.english: You do not tumble on your head but go happily along, while I am tumbling on my head, like one who has lost his way.

entries.farsi: ‏من همى‏آيم به سر در چون غوى / من همى‏افتم به رو در هر دمى
entries.english: At every moment I am falling on my face, whether in a dry place or a wet.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بدانم من كه چون بايد بزيست / گفت چشم من ز تو روشن‏تر است
entries.english: Declare to me what is the cause of this that I may know how I must live.”

entries.farsi: ‏بعد از آن هم از بلندى ناظر است / چون بر آيم بر سر كوهى بلند
entries.english: He said, “My eye is clearer than yours; furthermore, it is also looking from on high:

entries.farsi: آخر عقبه ببينم هوشمند / پس همه پستى و بالايى راه
entries.english: When I come up to the top of a high hill, I regard attentively the end of the pass;

entries.farsi: ‏ديده‏ام را وانمايد هم اله / هر قدم را از سر بينش نهم
entries.english: Then too God reveals to my eye all the lowness and loftiness of the way,

entries.farsi: ‏از عثار و اوفتادن وارهم / تو نبينى پيش خود يك دو سه گام
entries.english: I take every step with sight and am delivered from stumbling and falling,

entries.farsi: ‏دانه بينى و نبينى رنج دام / يستوي األعمى لديكم و البصير
entries.english: You do not see two or three steps in front of you: you see the bait, but you do not see the pain of the snare.

entries.farsi: في المقام و النزول و المسير / چون جنين را در شكم حق جان دهد
entries.english: Are the blind and the seeing equal before you in their abiding and their alighting and their journeying?”

entries.farsi: جذب اجزا در مزاج او نهد / از خورش او جذب اجزا مى‏كند
entries.english: When God gives a spirit to the embryo in the womb, He implants in its temperament drawing particles together.

entries.farsi: تار و پود جسم خود را مى‏تند / تا چهل سالش به جذب جزوها
entries.english: By means of food it draws the particles together and weaves the warp and woof of its body:

entries.farsi: حق حريصش كرده باشد در نما / جذب اجزا روح را تعليم كرد
entries.english: Till forty years, God will have made it desirous of drawing particles together in growth.

entries.farsi: چون نداند جذب اجزا شاه فرد / جامع اين ذره‏ها خورشيد بود
entries.english: The incomparable King taught the spirit to draw particles together: how should He not know how to draw particles together?

entries.farsi: بى‏غذا اجزات را داند ربود / آن زمانى كه در آيى تو ز خواب
entries.english: The assembler of these motes was the Sun: He knows how to seize your particles without nutrition.

entries.farsi: ‏هوش و حس رفته را خواند شتاب / تا بدانى كان از او غايب نشد
entries.english: At the moment when you emerge from sleep, He quickly recalls the departed consciousness and sensation.

entries.farsi: باز آيد چون بفرمايد كه عد
entries.english: To the end that you may know that those have not become absent from Him, they come back when He commands them to return.

sections.title_fa: اجتماع اجزاى خر عزير عليه السلام بعد از پوسيدن باذن اللهَّ و در هم مركب شدن پيش چشم عزير
sections.title_en: How by permission of God the particles of the ass of ‘Uzayr were assembled
after putrefaction and recompounded before the eyes of ‘Uzay

entries.farsi: هين عزيرا در نگر اندر خرت / ‏كه بپوسيده ست و ريزيده برت
entries.english: “Hey, ‘Uzayr, look upon your ass which has rotted and crumbled beside you

entries.farsi: پيش تو گردآوريم اجزاش را / آن سر و دم و دو گوش و پاش را
entries.english: We will collect its parts in your presence— its head and tail and ears and legs.”

entries.farsi: درنگر در صنعت پاره زنى / ‏كاو همى‏دوزد كهن بى‏سوزنى
entries.english: There is no hand, and He is putting the parts together and giving a unity to the pieces.

entries.farsi: دست نى و جزو بر هم مى‏نهد / پاره‏ها را اجتماعى مى‏دهد
entries.english: Consider the art of a Tailor who sews old rags without a needle:

entries.farsi: ريسمان و سوزنى نى وقت خرز / آن چنان دوزد كه پيدا نيست درز
entries.english: No thread or needle at the time of sewing; He sews in such wise that no seam is visible.

entries.farsi: چشم بگشا حشر را پيدا ببين / ‏تا نماند شبهه‏ات در يوم دين
entries.english: “Open your eyes and behold the resurrection plainly, that there may not remain in you doubt concerning the Day of Judgement,

entries.farsi: تا ببينى جامعى‏ام را تمام / ‏تا نلرزى وقت مردن ز اهتمام
entries.english: And that you may behold My unitive power entire, so that at the time of death you will not tremble with anxiety,

entries.farsi: همچنان كه وقت خفتن ايمنى / ‏از فوات جمله حسهاى تنى
entries.english: Even as at the time of sleep you are secure from the passing of all the bodily senses:

entries.farsi: بر حواس خود نلرزى وقت خواب / ‏گر چه مى‏گردد پريشان و خراب
entries.english: At the time of sleep you do not tremble for your senses, though they become scattered and ruined.”

sections.title_fa: ‏جزع ناكردن شيخى بر مرگ فرزندان خويش
sections.title_en: How a certain Shaykh showed no grief at the death of his sons.

entries.farsi: بود شيخى رهنمايى پيش از اين / ‏آسمانى شمع بر روى زمين
entries.english: Formerly there was a Shaykh, a Director, a heavenly Candle on the face of the earth,

entries.farsi: چون پيمبر در ميان امتان / ‏در گشاى روضه‏ى دار الجنان
entries.english: One like a prophet amongst religious communities, an opener of the door of the garden of Paradise

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه شيخ رفته پيش / ‏چون نبى باشد ميان قوم خويش
entries.english: The Prophet said that a Shaykh who has gone forward is like a prophet amidst his people.

entries.farsi: يك صباحى گفتش اهل بيت او / سخت دل چونى بگو اى نيك خو
entries.english: One morning his family said to him, “Tell us, O man of good disposition; how are you hard-hearted?

entries.farsi: ما ز مرگ و هجر فرزندان تو / نوحه مى‏داريم با پشت دو تو
entries.english: We with backs double are mourning for the death and loss of your sons:

entries.farsi: تو نمى‏گريى نمى‏زارى چرا / يا كه رحمت نيست اندر دل ترا
entries.english: Why are not you weeping and lamenting? Or have you no pity in your heart?

entries.farsi: چون ترا رحمى نباشد در درون / ‏پس چه اوميدستمان از تو كنون
entries.english: Since you have no pity within, what hope for us is there now from you?

entries.farsi: ما به اوميد توايم اى پيشوا / كه بنگذارى تو ما را در فنا
entries.english: We are in hope of you, O guide that you will not leave us to perish.

entries.farsi: چون بيارايند روز حشر تخت / ‏خود شفيع ما تويى آن روز سخت
entries.english: When the throne is set up on the Day of Resurrection, it is you that are our intercessor on that grievous day.

entries.farsi: در چنان روز و شب بى‏زينهار / ما به اكرام توايم اوميدوار
entries.english: On such a merciless day and night we are hopeful of your kindness.

entries.farsi: دست ما و دامن تست آن زمان / ‏كه نماند هيچ مجرم را امان
entries.english: Our hands will cling to your skirt at that moment when security remains not to any sinner.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه روز رستخيز / كى گذارم مجرمان را اشك ريز
entries.english: The Prophet has said, “On the Day of Resurrection how should I leave the sinners to shed tears?

entries.farsi: من شفيع عاصيان باشم به جان / ‏تا رهانمشان ز اشكنجه‏ى گران
entries.english: I will intercede with my soul for the disobedient, that I may deliver them from the heavy torment.

entries.farsi: عاصيان و اهل كباير را به جهد / وا رهانم از عتاب نقض عهد
entries.english: I will deliver by my efforts the disobedient and those who have committed capital sins from punishment for breaking their covenant.

entries.farsi: صالحان امتم خود فارغند / از شفاعتهاى من روز گزند
entries.english: The righteous of my community are, in truth, free from my intercessions on the Day of Woe;

entries.farsi: بلكه ايشان را شفاعتها بود / گفتشان چون حكم نافذ مى‏رود
entries.english: No, they have intercessions, and their words go like an effective decree.

entries.farsi: هيچ وازر وزر غيرى بر نداشت / ‏من نيم وازر خدايم بر فراشت
entries.english: No burdened one shall bear another’s burden, I am not burdened: God has exalted me.”

entries.farsi: آن كه بى‏وزر است شيخ است اى جوان / ‏در قبول حق چو اندر كف كمان
entries.english: O youth, the Shaykh is he that is without a burden and is like a bow in the hand in receiving God.

entries.farsi: شيخ كه بود پير يعنى مو سپيد / معنى اين مو بدان اى بى‏اميد
entries.english: Who is a “Shaykh”? An old man that is, white-haired. Do you apprehend the meaning of this “hair,” O hopeless one?

entries.farsi: هست آن موى سيه هستى او / تا ز هستى‏اش نماند تاى مو
entries.english: The black hair is his self-existence: till not a single hair of his self-existence remains.

entries.farsi: چون كه هستى‏اش نماند پير اوست / ‏گر سيه مو باشد او يا خود دو موست
entries.english: When his self-existence has ceased, he is “old”, whether he be black-haired or grizzled.

entries.farsi: هست آن موى سيه وصف بشر / نيست آن مو موى ريش و موى سر
entries.english: That “black hair” is the attribute of men; that “hair” is not the hair of the beard or the hair of the head.

entries.farsi: عيسى اندر مهد بر دارد نفير / كه جوان ناگشته ما شيخيم و پير
entries.english: Jesus in the cradle raises a cry, saying, “Without having become a youth, I am a Shaykh and a Pir.”

entries.farsi: گر رهيد از بعض اوصاف بشر / شيخ نبود كهل باشد اى پسر
entries.english: If he has been delivered from a part of the attributes of men, he is not a Shaykh; he is grey, O son.

entries.farsi: چون يكى موى سيه كان وصف ماست / ‏نيست بر وى شيخ و مقبول خداست
entries.english: When there is not on him a single black hair which is our attribute, he is a Shaykh and accepted of God;

entries.farsi: چون بود مويش سپيد ار با خود است / ‏او نه پير است و نه خاص ايزد است
entries.english: If, when his hair is white, he is with himself, he is not a Pir and is not the elect of God;

entries.farsi: ور سر مويى ز وصفش باقى است / ‏او نه از عرش است او آفاقى است
entries.english: And if a single hair-tip of his attributes is surviving, he is not of heaven: he belongs to the world.

sections.title_fa: عذر گفتن شيخ بهر ناگريستن بر مرگ فرزندان خود
sections.title_en: How the Shaykh excused himself for not weeping on the death of his sons.

entries.farsi: بر همه‏ى كفار ما را رحمت است / ‏گر چه جان جمله كافر نعمت است
entries.english: The Shaykh said to her, “Do not think, O gracious one, that I have not pity and affection and a compassionate heart.

entries.farsi: شيخ گفت او را مپندار اى رفيق / ‏كه ندارم رحم و مهر و دل شفيق
entries.english: I have pity for all the unbelievers, though the souls of them all are ungrateful.

entries.farsi: بر سگانم رحمت و بخشايش است / ‏كه چرا از سنگهاشان مالش است
entries.english: I have pity and forgiveness for dogs, saying, ‘Why do they suffer chastisement from the stones?

entries.farsi: آن سگى كه مى‏گزد گويم دعا / كه از اين خو وارهانش اى خدا
entries.english: I utter a prayer for the dog that bites, crying, ‘O God, deliver him from this disposition!

entries.farsi: اين سگان را هم در آن انديشه دار / كه نباشند از خاليق سنگسار
entries.english: Keep also these dogs in that thought, so that they may not be stoned by the people.’”

entries.farsi: خلق را خواند سوى درگاه خاص / ‏حق را خواند كه وافر كن خالص
entries.english: He brought the saints on to the earth, in order that He might make them a mercy to created beings.

entries.farsi: ز آن بياورد اوليا را بر زمين / ‏تا كندشان رَحْ مَةً للعالمين
entries.english: He calls the people to the Portal of Grace; he calls unto God, saying, “Give release in full!”

entries.farsi: جهد بنمايد از اين سو بهر پند / چون نشد گويد خدايا در مبند
entries.english: He earnestly strives to admonish them in regard to this, and when it does not succeed, he says, “O God, do not shut the door!”

entries.farsi: رحمت جزوى بود مر عام را / رحمت كلى بود همام را
entries.english: To the common belongs the particular mercy; the universal mercy belongs to the hero.

entries.farsi: رحمت جزوش قرين گشته به كل / ‏رحمت دريا بود هادى سبل
entries.english: His particular mercy has been united with the universal: the mercy of the Sea is the guide on the ways.

entries.farsi: رحمت جزوى به كل پيوسته شو / رحمت كل را تو هادى بين و رو
entries.english: O particular mercy, become joined to the universal: deem the universal mercy the true guide, and go.

entries.farsi: تا كه جزو است او نداند راه بحر / هر غديرى را كند ز اشباه بحر
entries.english: So long as he is a part, he does not know the way to the Sea: he makes out every pool to be like unto the Sea.

entries.farsi: متصل گردد به بحر آن گاه او / ره برد تا بحر همچون سيل و جو
entries.english: Inasmuch as he does not know the way to the Sea, how should he act as a guide? How should he lead the people towards the Sea?

entries.farsi: چون نداند راه يم كى ره برد / سوى دريا خلق را چون آورد
entries.english: He becomes united with the Sea, then he guides to the Sea, like a torrent or river.

entries.farsi: ور كند دعوت به تقليدى بود / نه از عيان و وحى و تاييدى بود
entries.english: And if he calls, it is in a conventional fashion; it is not from vision and the revelation of any aid.

entries.farsi: گفت پس چون رحم دارى بر همه / ‏همچو چوپانى به گرد اين رمه
entries.english: She said, “Then, since you have pity on all, and are like the shepherd around this flock,

entries.farsi: چون ندارى نوحه بر فرزند خويش / ‏چون كه فصاد اجلشان زد به نيش
entries.english: How mourn you not for your own sons, when Death, the Bleeder, has pierced them with his lancet?

entries.farsi: چون گواه رحم اشك ديده‏هاست / ‏ديده‏ى تو بى‏نم و گريه چراست
entries.english: Since the evidence of pity is tears in the eyes, why are your eyes without moisture and tearless?”

entries.farsi: رو به زن كرد و بگفتش اى عجوز / خود نباشد فصل دى همچون تموز
entries.english: He turned towards his wife and said to her, “Old woman, verily the season of December is not like July.

entries.farsi: جمله گر مردند ايشان گر حى‏اند / غايب و پنهان ز چشم دل كى‏اند
entries.english: Whether they all are dead or living, when are they absent and hid from the eye of the heart?

entries.farsi: من چو بينمشان معين پيش خويش / ‏از چه رو رو را كنم همچون تو ريش
entries.english: Inasmuch as I see them distinct before me, for what reason should I tear my face as you do?

entries.farsi: گر چه بيرونند از دور زمان / ‏با من‏اند و گرد من بازى‏كنان
entries.english: Although they are outside of Time’s revolution, they are with me and playing around me.

entries.farsi: گريه از هجران بود يا از فراق / ‏با عزيزانم وصال است و عناق
entries.english: Weeping is caused by severance or by parting; I am united with my dear ones and embracing them.

entries.farsi: خلق اندر خواب مى‏بينندشان / ‏من به بيدارى همى‏بينم عيان
entries.english: People see them in sleep; I see them plainly in waking state.

entries.farsi: زين جهان خود را دمى پنهان كنم / ‏برگ حس را از درخت افشان كنم
entries.english: I hide myself for a moment from this world; I shake the leaves of sense perception from the tree.”

entries.farsi: حس اسير عقل باشد اى فالن / ‏عقل اسير روح باشد هم بدان
entries.english: Sense-perception is captive to the intellect, O reader; know also that the intellect is captive to the spirit.

entries.farsi: دست بسته‏ى عقل را جان باز كرد / كارهاى بسته را هم ساز كرد
entries.english: The spirit sets free the chained hand of the intellect and brings its embarrassed affairs into harmony.

entries.farsi: حسها و انديشه بر آب صفا / همچو خس بگرفته روى آب را
entries.english: The senses and thoughts are like weeds on the clear water— covering the surface of the water.

entries.farsi: دست عقل آن خس به يك سو مى‏برد / آب پيدا مى‏شود پيش خرد
entries.english: The hand of the intellect sweeps those weeds aside; the water is revealed to the intellect.

entries.farsi: خس بس انبه بود بر جو چون حباب / ‏خس چو يك سو رفت پيدا گشت آب
entries.english: The weeds lay very thick on the stream, like bubbles; when the weeds went aside, the water was revealed.

entries.farsi: چون كه دست عقل نگشايد خدا / خس فزايد از هوا بر آب ما
entries.english: Unless God looses the hand of the intellect, the weeds on our water are increased by sensual desire.

entries.farsi: آب را هر دم كند پوشيده او / آن هوا خندان و گريان عقل تو
entries.english: Every moment they cover the water: that desire is laughing, and your intellect is weeping;

entries.farsi: چون كه تقوى بست دو دست هوا / حق گشايد هر دو دست عقل را
entries.english: When piety has chained the hands of desire, God looses the hands of the intellect.

entries.farsi: پس حواس چيره محكوم تو شد / چون خرد سالار و مخدوم تو شد
entries.english: So, when the intellect becomes your captain and master, the dominant senses become subject to you.

entries.farsi: حس را بى‏خواب خواب اندر كند / تا كه غيبها ز جان سر بر زند
entries.english: He, without being asleep, puts his senses to sleep, so that the unseen things may emerge from the Soul.

entries.farsi: هم به بيدارى ببيند خوابها / هم ز گردون بر گشايد بابها
entries.english: Even in your waking state he dreams dreams and the gates of Heaven will open to you.

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى خواندن شيخ ضرير مصحف را در رو و بينا شدن وقت قرائت
sections.title_en: Story of the blind old man’s reading the Qur’an in front and regaining his sight when he read

entries.farsi: ديد در ايام آن شيخ فقير / مصحفى در خانه‏ى پيرى ضرير
entries.english: Once upon a time a dervish Shaykh saw a Qur’an in the house of a blind old man.

entries.farsi: پيش او مهمان شد او وقت تموز / هر دو زاهد جمع گشته چند روز
entries.english: He became his guest in Tamúz: the two ascetics were together for several days.

entries.farsi: گفت اينجا اى عجب مصحف چراست / ‏چون كه نابيناست اين درويش راست
entries.english: He said, “Oh, I wonder what the Book is for, as this righteous dervish is blind.”

entries.farsi: اندر اين انديشه تشويشش فزود / كه جز او را نيست اينجا باش و بود
entries.english: In this reflection, his perplexity increased: “No one lives here except him.

entries.farsi: اوست تنها مصحفى آويخته / ‏من نيم گستاخ يا آميخته
entries.english: He is alone, he has hung a Book. I am not unmannerly or muddled

entries.farsi: تا بپرسم نى خمش صبرى كنم / ‏تا به صبرى بر مرادى بر زنم
entries.english: As to ask. Nay, hush! I will be patient, in order that by patience I may gain my object.”

entries.farsi: صبر كرد و بود چندى در حرج / ‏كشف شد كالصبر مفتاح الفرج
entries.english: He showed patience and was in a quandary for some time, it was disclosed, for patience is the key to joy.

sections.title_fa: صبر كردن لقمان چون ديد كه داود عليه السلام حلقه‏ها مى‏ساخت از سؤال كردن با اين نيت كه صبر از سؤال موجب فرج باشد
sections.title_en: How Luqman, when he saw David, on whom be peace, making rings, refrained from questioning him, with the intention that this act of self-control should be the cause of relief.

entries.farsi: رفت لقمان سوى داود صفا / ديد كاو مى‏كرد ز آهن حلقه‏ها
entries.english: Luqman went to David, the pure of heart, and observed that he was making rings of iron,

entries.farsi: جمله را با هم دگر در مى‏فگند / ز آهن پوالد آن شاه بلند
entries.english: That the exalted King was casting all the steel rings into each other.

entries.farsi: صنعت زراد او كم ديده بود / در عجب مى‏ماند و وسواسش فزود
entries.english: He had not seen the armourer’s handicraft, he remained in astonishment and his curiosity increased—

entries.farsi: كاين چه شايد بود واپرسم از او / كه چه مى‏سازى ز حلقه‏ى تو به تو
entries.english: “What can this be? I will ask him what he is making with the interfolded rings.”

entries.farsi: باز با خود گفت صبر اوليتر است / ‏صبر تا مقصود زوتر رهبر است
entries.english: Again he said to himself, “Patience is better: patience is the quickest guide to the object of one’s quest.”

entries.farsi: چون نپرسى زودتر كشفت شود / مرغ صبر از جمله پران‏تر بود
entries.english: When you ask no question, the sooner will it be disclosed to you: the bird, patience, flies faster than all.

entries.farsi: ور بپرسى ديرتر حاصل شود / سهل از بى‏صبرى‏ات مشكل شود
entries.english: And if you ask, the more slowly will your object be gained: what is easy will be made difficult by your impatience.

entries.farsi: چون كه لقمان تن بزد هم در زمان / ‏شد تمام از صنعت داود آن
entries.english: When Luqman kept silence; straightway that was finished by David’s craftsmanship.

entries.farsi: پس زره سازيد و در پوشيد او / پيش لقمان كريم صبر خو
entries.english: Then he fashioned a coat of mail and put it on in the presence of the noble and patient Luqman.

entries.farsi: گفت اين نيكو لباس است اى فتى / در مصاف و جنگ دفع زخم را
entries.english: “This,” he said, “is an excellent garment, O young man, for warding off blows on the battle-field and in war.”

entries.farsi: گفت لقمان صبر هم نيكو دمى است / ‏كه پناه و دافع هر جا غمى است
entries.english: Luqman said, “Patience too is of good effect, for it is the protection and defence against pain everywhere.”

entries.farsi: صبر را با حق قرين كرد اى فالن / ‏آخر و العصر را آگه بخوان
entries.english: He has joined sabr with haqq; O reader, recite attentively the end of Wa’l-‘asr.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران كيميا حق آفريد / كيميايى همچو صبر آدم نديد
entries.english: God created hundreds of thousands of elixirs; Man has not seen an elixir like patience.

sections.title_fa: بقيه‏ى حكايت نابينا و مصحف خواندن او
sections.title_en: The remainder of the story of the blind man and his reading the Qur’an

entries.farsi: مرد مهمان صبر كرد و ناگهان / ‏كشف گشتش حال مشكل در زمان
entries.english: The guest showed patience, and of a sudden the difficult case was unveiled to him all at once.

entries.farsi: نيم شب آواز قرآن را شنيد / جست از خواب آن عجايب را بديد
entries.english: At midnight he heard the sound of the Qur’an; he sprang up from sleep and beheld a marvel—

entries.farsi: كه ز مصحف كور مى‏خواندى درست / ‏گشت بى‏صبر و از او آن حال جست
entries.english: That the blind man was reading correctly from the Qur’an. He became impatient and sought from him that matter.

entries.farsi: گفت آيا اى عجب با چشم كور / چون همى‏خوانى همى‏بينى سطور
entries.english: “Oh, wonderful!” he cried. “You with sightless eyes, how are you reading, seeing the lines?

entries.farsi: آن چه مى‏خوانى بر آن افتاده‏اى / ‏دست را بر حرف آن بنهاده‏اى
entries.english: You have touched that which you are reading: you have laid your hand upon the words of that.

entries.farsi: اصبعت در سير پيدا مى‏كند / كه نظر بر حرف دارى مستند
entries.english: Your finger, in motion, makes it evident that you have your eye resting on the words.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى گشته ز جهل تن جدا / اين عجب مى‏دارى از صنع خدا
entries.english: He replied, “O you who have been separated from the body’s ignorance, do you feel this wonder at the work of God?

entries.farsi: من ز حق در خواستم كاى مستعان / ‏بر قرائت من حريصم همچو جان
entries.english: I begged of God, crying, ‘O You whose help is sought, I am covetous of reading the Book as of life.

entries.farsi: نيستم حافظ مرا نورى بده / ‏در دو ديده وقت خواندن بى‏گره
entries.english: I do not know it by heart: at the time of reading it, bestow on my two eyes an untroubled light.

entries.farsi: باز ده دو ديده‏ام را آن زمان / ‏كه بگيرم مصحف و خوانم عيان
entries.english: Give me back my eyes at that moment, so that I may take the Book and read it plain.’

entries.farsi: آمد از حضرت ندا كاى مرد كار / اى به هر رنجى به ما اوميدوار
entries.english: From the Divine Presence came the cry: ‘O man of work, O you that have hope of Me in every grief,

entries.farsi: حسن ظن است و اميدى خوش ترا / كه ترا گويد به هر دم برتر آ
entries.english: You have the good thought and the fair hope that at each moment bids you mount higher.

entries.farsi: هر زمان كه قصد خواندن باشدت / ‏يا ز مصحفها قرائت بايدت
entries.english: Whenever you intend to read the Qur’an or want the lection from copies,

entries.farsi: من در آن دم وادهم چشم ترا / تا فرو خوانى معظم جوهرا
entries.english: At that moment I will restore your eye, in order that you may read, O venerable being.’

entries.farsi: همچنان كرد و هر آن گاهى كه من / ‏واگشايم مصحف اندر خواندن
entries.english: Even so He did, and whenever I open the Book to read,

entries.farsi: آن خبيرى كه نشد غافل ز كار / آن گرامى پادشاه و كردگار
entries.english: That all-knowing One who never becomes forgetful of His work, that honoured Sovereign and Maker,

entries.farsi: باز بخشد بينشم آن شاه فرد / در زمان همچون چراغ شب نورد
entries.english: That incomparable King at once gives my sight back to me, like a lamp that makes an end of the night.”

entries.farsi: زين سبب نبود ولى را اعتراض / ‏هر چه بستاند فرستد اعتياض
entries.english: On this account the saint has no objection: whatever He takes away, He sends compensation.

entries.farsi: گر بسوزد باغت انگورت دهد / در ميان ماتمى سورت دهد
entries.english: If He burns your vineyard, He will give you grapes; in the midst of mourning He will give you festivity.

entries.farsi: آن شل بى‏دست را دستى دهد / كان غمها را دل مستى دهد
entries.english: To the handless paralytic He gives a hand, to the mine of grief He gives the heart of an intoxicated one.

entries.farsi: ال نسلم و اعتراض از ما برفت / ‏چون عوض مى‏آيد از مفقود زفت
entries.english: “We will not submit” and objection have gone from us, since there is coming a great recompense for what has been lost.

entries.farsi: بى‏چراغى چون دهد او روشنى / ‏گر چراغت شد چه افغان مى‏كنى
entries.english: Inasmuch as heat comes to me without fire, I am content if his fire kills me.

entries.farsi: چون كه بى‏آتش مرا گرمى رسد / راضيم گر آتش ما را كشد
entries.english: Inasmuch as He gives light without, any lamp— if your lamp is gone, why are you lamenting?

sections.title_fa: ‏صفت بعضى از اوليا كه راضى‏اند به احكام و دعا و البه نكنند كه اين حكم را بگردان
sections.title_en: Description of some saints who are content with the ordainments and do not beseech to change this decree.

entries.farsi: بشنو اكنون قصه‏ى آن رهروان / ‏كه ندارند اعتراضى در جهان
entries.english: Now listen to a story of those travellers on the Way who have no objection in the world.

entries.farsi: ز اوليا اهل دعا خود ديگرند / كه گهى دوزند و گاهى مى‏درند
entries.english: Those of the saints who make invocation are in sooth different: s ometimes they sew and sometimes they tear.

entries.farsi: قوم ديگر مى‏شناسم ز اوليا / كه دهانشان بسته باشد از دعا
entries.english: I know another class of saints whose mouths are closed to invocation.

entries.farsi: از رضا كه هست رام آن كرام / ‏جستن دفع قضاشان شد حرام
entries.english: Because of the content that is subservient to those noble ones, it has become unlawful for them to seek to avert Destiny.

entries.farsi: در قضا ذوقى همى‏بينند خاص / ‏كفرشان آيد طلب كردن خالص
entries.english: In Destiny they experience a peculiar delight: it would be infidelity for them to crave release.

entries.farsi: حسن ظنى بر دل ايشان گشود / كه نپوشند از غمى جامه‏ى كبود
entries.english: He has revealed to their hearts such a good opinion that they do not put on the blue garb on account of any sorrow.

sections.title_fa: سؤال كردن بهلول آن درويش را
sections.title_en: How Buhlul questioned a certain dervish.

entries.farsi: گفت بهلول آن يكى درويش را / چونى اى درويش واقف كن مرا
entries.english: Buhlul said to a certain dervish, “How are you, O dervish? Inform me.”

entries.farsi: سيل و جوها بر مراد او روند / اختران ز آن سان كه خواهد آن شوند
entries.english: He said, “How should that one be, according to whose desire the work of the world goes on?—

entries.farsi: گفت چون باشد كسى كه جاودان / ‏بر مراد او رود كار جهان
entries.english: According to whose desire the torrents and rivers flow, and the stars move in such wise as he wills;

entries.farsi: زندگى و مرگ، سرهنگان او / بر مراد او روانه كو به كو
entries.english: And Life and Death are his officers, going to and fro according to his desire.

entries.farsi: هر كجا خواهد فرستد تعزيت / ‏هر كجا خواهد ببخشد تهنيت
entries.english: He sends condolence wherever he will; he bestows felicitation wherever he will.

entries.farsi: سالكان راه هم بر كام او / ماندگان از راه هم در دام او
entries.english: The travellers on the Way according to his pleasure; they that have lost the Way in his snare.

entries.farsi: هيچ دندانى نخندد در جهان / ‏بى‏رضا و امر آن فرمان‏روان
entries.english: No tooth flashes with laughter in the world without the approval and command of that imperial personage.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه راست گفتى همچنين / ‏در فر و سيماى تو پيداست اين
entries.english: He said, “O King, you have spoken truly: it is even so: this is manifest in your radiance and aspect.

entries.farsi: اين و صد چندينى اى صادق و ليك / ‏شرح كن اين را بيان كن نيك نيك
entries.english: You are this and a hundred times as much, O veracious one; but ex­ pound this and explain it very well,

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه فاضل و مرد فضول / ‏چون به گوش او رسد آرد قبول
entries.english: In such fashion that the virtuous and the man given to vanity may assent when it comes to their ears

entries.farsi: آن چنانش شرح كن اندر كالم / ‏كه از آن بهره بيابد عقل عام
entries.english: Expound it in your discourse in such a way that the understanding of the common may profit thereby.”

entries.farsi: ناطق كامل چو خوان باشى بود / خوانش پر هر گونه‏ى آشى بود
entries.english: The perfect speaker is like one who distributes trays of delicacies, and whose table is every sort of food,

entries.farsi: كه نماند هيچ مهمان بى‏نوا / هر كسى يابد غذاى خود جدا
entries.english: So that no guest remains without provisions, each one gets his nourishment separately:

entries.farsi: همچو قرآن كه به معنى هفت توست / ‏خاص را و عام را مطعم در اوست
entries.english: Like the Qur’an which is sevenfold in meaning, and in this there is food for the elect and for the vulgar.

entries.farsi: گفت اين بارى يقين شد پيش عام / ‏كه جهان در امر يزدان است رام
entries.english: He said, “This at least is evident to the vulgar, that the world is subject to the command of God.

entries.farsi: هيچ برگى در نيفتد از درخت / ‏بى‏قضا و حكم آن سلطان بخت
entries.english: No leaf drops from a tree without the predestination and ordainment of that Ruler of Fortune.

entries.farsi: از دهان لقمه نشد سوى گلو / تا نگويد لقمه را حق كه ادخلوا
entries.english: No morsel goes from the mouth towards the stomach till God says to that morsel, ‘Enter!’

entries.farsi: ميل و رغبت كان زمام آدمى است / ‏جنبش آن رام امر آن غنى است
entries.english: The inclination and desire which is Man’s nose-rein— its movement is subject to the command of that Self-sufficient One.

entries.farsi: جز به فرمان قديم نافذش / ‏شرح نتوان كرد و جلدى نيست خوش
entries.english: In the earth and heavens not an atom moves a wing, not a straw turns,

entries.farsi: در زمينها و آسمانها ذره‏اى / ‏پر نجنباند نگردد پره‏اى
entries.english: Save by His eternal and effectual command. To expound is impossible, and presumption is not good.

entries.farsi: كه شمرد برگ درختان را تمام / ‏بى‏نهايت كى شود در نطق رام
entries.english: Who may number all the leaves of the trees? How may the Infinite become amenable to speech?

entries.farsi: اين قدر بشنو كه چون كلى كار / مى‏نگردد جز به امر كردگار
entries.english: Hear this much,: since all action only comes to pass by the command of the Maker,

entries.farsi: چون قضاى حق رضاى بنده شد / حكم او را بنده‏اى خواهنده شد
entries.english: When the predestination of God becomes the pleasure of His servant, he becomes a willing slave to His decree,

entries.farsi: نى تكلف نه پى مزد و ثواب / بلكه طبع او چنين شد مستطاب
entries.english: Without tasking himself, and not on account of the reward and recompense; nay, his nature has become so goodly.

entries.farsi: زندگى خود نخواهد بهر خود / نى پى ذوق حيات مستلذ
entries.english: He does not desire his life for himself or to the end that he may enjoy the life that is found sweet.

entries.farsi: هر كجا امر قدم را مسلكى است / ‏زندگى و مردگى پيشش يكى است
entries.english: Wherever the Eternal Command takes its course, living and dying are one to him.

entries.farsi: بهر يزدان مى‏زيد نى بهر گنج / ‏بهر يزدان مى‏مرد نه از خوف و رنج
entries.english: He lives for God’s sake, not for riches; he dies for God’s sake, not from fear of pain.

entries.farsi: هست ايمانش براى خواست او / نه براى جنت و اشجار و جو
entries.english: His faith is for the sake of His will, not for the sake of Paradise and its trees and streams.

entries.farsi: اين چنين آمد ز اصل آن خوى او / نه رياضت نه به جست و جوى او
entries.english: His abandonment of infidelity is also for God’s sake, not for fear lest he go into the Fire.

entries.farsi: ترك كفرش هم براى حق بود / نه ز بيم آن كه در آتش رود
entries.english: That disposition of his is like this originally: it is not discipline or by his effort and endeavour.

entries.farsi: آن گهان خندد كه او بيند رضا / همچو حلواى شكر او را قضا
entries.english: He laughs at the moment when he sees pleasure: to him Destiny is even as sugared sweetmeat.”

entries.farsi: بنده‏اى كش خوى و خلقت اين بود / نه جهان بر امر و فرمانش رود
entries.english: The servant whose disposition and character is this— does not the world move according to his command and behest?

entries.farsi: پس چرا البه كند او يا دعا / كه بگردان اى خداوند اين قضا
entries.english: Then why should he make entreaty and cry in prayer, “O God, avert this destiny”?

entries.farsi: مرگ او و مرگ فرزندان او / بهر حق پيشش چو حلوا در گلو
entries.english: For God’s sake his death and the death of his children are to him like sweetmeat in the gullet.

entries.farsi: نزع فرزندان بر آن با وفا / چون قطايف پيش شيخ بى‏نوا
entries.english: To that loyal one the death-agony of his children is like honey cakes to a destitute old man.

entries.farsi: پس چرا گويد دعا الا مگر / در دعا بيند رضاى دادگر
entries.english: Why, then, should he invoke, unless perchance he see the pleasure of the Judge in invocation?

entries.farsi: آن شفاعت و آن دعا نه از رحم خود / مى‏كند آن بنده‏ى صاحب رشد
entries.english: That righteous servant does not make that intercession and invocation from his own mercifulness.

entries.farsi: دوزخ اوصاف او عشق است و او / سوخت مر اوصاف خود را مو به مو
entries.english: He has burned up his own mercifulness at the moment when he has lighted the lamp of love of God.

entries.farsi: رحم خود را او همان دم سوخته است / ‏كه چراغ عشق حق افروخته ست
entries.english: Love is the Hell-fire of his attributes, and it has burnt up the attributes of self, hair by hair.

entries.farsi: هر طروقى اين فروقى كى شناخت / ‏جز دقوقى تا در اين دولت بتاخت
entries.english: When did any night-traveller understand this distinction except Daquqi? So that he sped into this empire.

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى دقوقى و كراماتش
sections.title_en: The story of Daquqi and his miraculous gifts

entries.farsi: آن دقوقى داشت خوش ديباجه‏اى / ‏عاشق و صاحب كرامت خواجه‏اى
entries.english: That Daquqi had a fair front; he was a lord who loved and possessed miraculous gifts.

entries.farsi: بر زمين مى‏شد چو مه بر آسمان / ‏شب روان را گشته زو روشن روان
entries.english: He walked on earth as the moon in heaven: by him the spirits of the night-travellers became illumined.

entries.farsi: در مقامى مسكنى كم ساختى / ‏كم دو روز اندر دهى انداختى
entries.english: He would not make his abode in any one place; he would not spend two days in a village.

entries.farsi: گفت در يك خانه گر باشم دو روز / عشق آن مسكن كند در من فروز
entries.english: He said, “If I stay two days in one house, love of that dwelling-place is kindled in me.

entries.farsi: ال أعود خلق قلبي بالمكان / ‏كي يكون خالصا في الامتحان
entries.english: I am afraid of being beguiled by the dwelling-place: migrate, O my soul, and travel to independence.

entries.farsi: روز اندر سير بد شب در نماز / چشم اندر شاه باز او همچو باز
entries.english: I will not accustom my heart’s nature to locality, in order that it may be pure in the trial.”

entries.farsi: غرة المسكن أحاذره أنا / انقلي يا نفس سافر للغنا
entries.english: During the day he was in travel, during the night in ritual prayer: his eye open on the King, and he like the falcon.

entries.farsi: مشفقى بر خلق و نافع همچو آب / ‏خوش شفيعى و دعايش مستجاب
entries.english: Severed from the creatures, not on account of ill nature; isolated from man and woman, not because of dualism.

entries.farsi: منقطع از خلق نه از بد خويى / ‏منفرد از مرد و زن نى از دويى
entries.english: A compassionate man to the creatures and beneficial as water; a goodly intercessor, and his prayers were answered

entries.farsi: نيك و بد را مهربان و مستقر / بهتر از مادر شهى‏تر از پدر
entries.english: Kind to the good and the bad, and a sure refuge; better than a mother, dearer than a father

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر شما را اى مهان / ‏چون پدر هستم شفيق و مهربان
entries.english: The Prophet said, “O sirs, to you I am compassionate and kind as a father,

entries.farsi: ز آن سبب كه جمله اجزاى منيد / جزو را از كل چرا بر مى‏كنيد
entries.english: Because you all are parts of me.” Why will ye tear the part away from the whole?

entries.farsi: جزو از كل قطع شد بى‏كار شد / عضو از تن قطع شد مردار شد
entries.english: The part is severed from the whole, it becomes useless; the limb is severed from the body, it becomes carrion.

entries.farsi: تا نپيوندد به كل بار دگر / مرده باشد نبودش از جان خبر
entries.english: Till it is joined once more to the whole, it is dead: it has no consciousness of life;

entries.farsi: جزو ازين كل گر برد يك سو رود / اين نه آن كل است كاو ناقص شود
entries.english: And if it moves, yet it has no support: the newly severed limb also moves.

entries.farsi: ور بجنبد نيست آن را خود سند / عضو نو ببريده هم جنبش كند
entries.english: If the part be severed and fall asunder from this whole, this is not the whole that is liable to defect.

entries.farsi: قطع و وصل او نيايد در مقال / مر على را در مثالى شير خواند
entries.english: Separation from it and conjunction with it are not predicable; the defective thing has been mentioned for the sake of comparison.

sections.title_fa: ‏باز گشتن به قصه‏ى دقوقى
sections.title_en: Return to the story of Daquqi.

entries.farsi: شير مثل او نباشد گر چه راند / از مثال و مثل و فرق آن بران
entries.english: He once compared Ali to a lion, the lion is not like him, though he used.

entries.farsi: گوى تقوى از فرشته مى‏ربود / آن كه اندر سير مه را مات كرد
entries.english: From comparison (mithál) and likeness (mithl) and the difference between those push on, O youth, towards the story of Daquqi:

entries.farsi: ‏جانب قصه‏ى دقوقى اى جوان / آن كه در فتوى امام خلق بود
entries.english: That one who in giving legal judgments was the Imam of the people and in piety bore away the ball from the angels;

entries.farsi: هم ز دين دارى او دين رشك خورد / با چنين تقوى و اوراد و قيام
entries.english: That one who checkmated the moon in wayfaring, while the Religion was jealous of his religiousness

entries.farsi: ‏طالب خاصان حق بودى مدام / در سفر معظم مرادش آن بدى
entries.english: Notwithstanding such piety and devotions and performance of the ritual prayer, he was always seeking the elect of God.

entries.farsi: ‏كه دمى بر بنده‏ى خاصى زدى / اين همى‏گفتى چو مى‏رفتى به راه
entries.english: In travel his chief object was that he might come in touch for a moment with an elect servant.

entries.farsi: ‏كن قرين خاصگانم اى اله / يا رب آنها را كه بشناسد دلم
entries.english: Whilst he was going along the road, he would be saying, “O God, make me a companion of the elect.

entries.farsi: ‏بنده و بسته ميان و مجملم / و انكه نشناسم تو اى يزدان جان
entries.english: O Lord, to those whom my heart knows I am a slave and one who has girt his loins and is ready to do good service;

entries.farsi: ‏بر من محجوبشان كن مهربان / حضرتش گفتى كه اى صدر مهين
entries.english: And those whom I know not, do You, O God of the soul, make them kindly disposed to me who am debarred.”

entries.farsi: ‏اين چه عشق است و چه استسقاست اين / مهر من دارى چه مى‏جويى دگر
entries.english: The Lord would say to him, “O most noble prince, what passion is this and what unquenchable thirst is this?

entries.farsi: چون خدا با تست چون جويى بشر / او بگفتى يا رب اى داناى راز
entries.english: You have My love: why are you seeking other? When God is with you, how do you seek man?”

entries.farsi: تو گشودى در دلم راه نياز / در ميان بحر اگر بنشسته‏ام
entries.english: He would answer, “O Lord, O Knower of the secret, You have opened in my heart the way of supplication.

entries.farsi: ‏طمع در آب سبو هم بسته‏ام / همچو داودم نود نعجه مراست
entries.english: If I am seated in the midst of the Sea, yet I have set my desire on the water in the jug.

entries.farsi: ‏طمع در نعجه‏ى حريفم هم بجاست / حرص اندر عشق تو فخر است و جاه
entries.english: I am like David: I have ninety ewes, and yet desire for my rival’s ewe has arisen in me.

entries.farsi: ‏حرص اندر غير تو ننگ و تباه / شهوت و حرص نران پيشى بود
entries.english: Greed for Your love is glorious and grand; greed for any besides You is shameful and corrupt.”

entries.farsi: و آن هيزان ننگ و بد كيشى بود / حرص مردان از ره پيشى بود
entries.english: The lust and greed of the manly is advancement, while that of the effeminate is disgrace and irreligion.

entries.farsi: در مخنث حرص سوى پس رود / آن يكى حرص از كمال مردى است
entries.english: The greed of men is by the forward way, greed in the effeminate goes backward.

entries.farsi: ‏و آن دگر حرص افتضاح و سردى است / آه سرى هست اينجا بس نهان
entries.english: The one greed belongs to the perfection of manliness, while the other greed is a disgrace and disgusting.

entries.farsi: ‏كه سوى خضرى شود موسى دوان / همچو مستسقى كز آبش سير نيست
entries.english: Ah, there is a very occult mystery here that Moses sets out to go towards a Khizr.

entries.farsi: ‏بر هر آن چه يافتى باهلل مايست / بى‏نهايت حضرت است اين بارگاه
entries.english: By God, do not tarry in anything that you have gained, like one suffering from dropsy who is never sated with water.

entries.farsi: ‏صدر را بگذار صدر تست راه
entries.english: This court is the Infinite Plane. Leave the seat of honour behind: the Way is your seat of honour.

sections.title_fa: ‏سر طلب كردن موسى خضر را عليهما السلام با كمال نبوت و قربت
sections.title_en: The mystery of Moses seeking Khizr, notwithstanding his perfection as a prophet and as one nigh unto God

entries.farsi: از كليم حق بياموز اى كريم / ‏بين چه مى‏گويد ز مشتاقى كليم
entries.english: Learn from him with whom God spoke, O noble sir! See what Kalim says in his longing!

entries.farsi: با چنين جاه و چنين پيغمبرى / ‏طالب خضرم ز خود بينى برى
entries.english: “Notwithstanding such a dignity and such a prophetic office, I am a seeker of Khizr, quit of self-regard.”

entries.farsi: موسيا تو قوم خود را هشته‏اى / ‏در پى نيكو پيى سر گشته‏اى
entries.english: “O Moses, you have forsaken your people; you have wandered distraught in search of a blessed man.

entries.farsi: كيقبادى رسته از خوف و رجا / چند گردى چند جويى تا كجا
entries.english: You are an emperor delivered from fear and hope: how long will you wander? How long will you seek? To what destination?

entries.farsi: آن تو با تست و تو واقف بر اين / ‏آسمانا چند پيمايى زمين
entries.english: Yours is with you, and you are conscious of this. O sky, how long will you traverse the earth?”

entries.farsi: مى‏روم تا مجمع البحرين من / ‏تا شوم مصحوب سلطان زمن
entries.english: Moses said, “Do not make this reproach, and do not waylay the Sun and the Moon.

entries.farsi: گفت موسى اين مالمت كم كنيد / آفتاب و ماه را كم ره زنيد
entries.english: I will fare as far as the meeting-place of the two seas, that I may be accompanied by the Sovereign of the time.

entries.farsi: اجعل الخضر ألمري سببا / ذاك أو أمضي و أسري حقبا
entries.english: I will make Khizr a means to my purpose: that or I will go onward and journey by night a long while.

entries.farsi: سالها پرم به پر و بالها / سالها چه بود هزاران سالها
entries.english: I will fly with wings and pinions for years: what are years? For thousands of years.”

entries.farsi: مى‏روم يعنى نمى‏ارزد بدان / ‏عشق جانان كم مدان از عشق نان
entries.english: “I will fare,” meaning, “Is it not worth that? Do not deem the passion for the Beloved to be less than the passion for bread.”

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد اى عمو / آن دقوقى رحمة اللهَّ عليه
entries.english: This discourse has no end, O uncle. Tell the story of Daquqi.

sections.title_fa: ‏باز گشتن به قصه‏ى دقوقى
sections.title_en: Resuming the story of Daquqi

entries.farsi: ‏گفت سافرت مدى في خافقيه / سال و مه رفتم سفر از عشق ماه
entries.english: That Daquqi, God have mercy on him, said: “I travelled a long time between His two horizons.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏خبر از راه حيران در اله / پا برهنه مى‏روى بر خار و سنگ
entries.english: Years and months I went on my journey for love of the Moon, unconscious of the way, lost in God.”

entries.farsi: ‏گفت من حيرانم و بى‏خويش و دنگ / تو مبين اين پايها را بر زمين
entries.english: “Do you go bare-foot over thorns and stones?” He said, “I am bewildered and beside myself and crazed.”

entries.farsi: دل چه داند اوست مست دلنواز / آن دراز و كوته اوصاف تن است
entries.english: Do not regard these feet on the earth, for assuredly the lover walks on his heart;

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه بر دل مى‏رود عاشق يقين / از ره و منزل ز كوتاه و دراز
entries.english: The heart that is intoxicated with the Sweetheart, what should it know of road and stage or of short and long?

entries.farsi: ‏رفتن ارواح ديگر رفتن است / تو سفر كردى ز نطفه تا به عقل
entries.english: That “long” and “short” are attributes of the body: the faring of spirits is another faring.

entries.farsi: نى به گامى بود نى منزل نه نقل‏ / سير جان بى‏چون بود در دور و دير
entries.english: You have journeyed from the seed to rationality: it was not by a step or stage or moving from one place to another.

entries.farsi: جسم ما از جان بياموزيد سير / سير جسمانه رها كرد او كنون
entries.english: The journey of the spirit is unconditioned in respect of Time and Space: our body learned from the spirit how to journey.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏رود بى‏چون نهان در شكل چون / گفت روزى مى‏شدم مشتاق‏وار
entries.english: Now it has relinquished the bodily manner of journeying: it moves unconditioned, masked in the form of conditionedness.

entries.farsi: تا ببينم در بشر انوار يار / تا ببينم قلزمى در قطره‏اى
entries.english: He said, “One day I was going along like him that yearns, that I might behold in man the radiance of the Beloved,

entries.farsi: ‏آفتابى درج اندر ذره‏اى / چون رسيدم سوى يك ساحل به گام
entries.english: That I might behold an ocean in a drop of water, a sun enclosed in a speck.

entries.farsi: ‏بود بى‏گه گشته روز و وقت شام / هفت شمع از دور ديدم ناگهان
entries.english: When I came on foot to a certain shore, the day had turned late, and it was eventide.

sections.title_fa: ‏نمودن مثال هفت شمع سوى ساحل
sections.title_en: The apparition of what seemed like seven candles in the direction of the shore.

entries.farsi: ‏اندر آن ساحل شتابيدم بدان / نور شعله‏ى هر يكى شمعى از آن
entries.english: Of a sudden I saw from afar seven candles and hastened along the shore towards them.

entries.farsi: ‏بر شده خوش تا عنان آسمان / خيره گشتم خيرگى هم خيره گشت
entries.english: The light of the flame of each candle thereof ascended beauteously to the loft of the sky.

entries.farsi: ‏موج حيرت عقل را از سر گذشت / اين چگونه شمعها افروخته ست
entries.english: I became amazed, even amazement became amazed: the waves of bewilderment passed over the head of my understanding.

entries.farsi: ‏كاين دو ديده‏ى خلق از اينها دوخته ست / خلق جويان چراغى گشته بود
entries.english: ‘What kind of candles are these He has lighted, so that the eyes of His creatures are screened from them?’

entries.farsi: پيش آن شمعى كه بر مه مى‏فزود / چشم بندى بد عجب بر ديده‏ها
entries.english: The people had gone to seek a lamp in the presence of that candle which was surpassing the moon.

entries.farsi: بندشان مى‏كرد يَهْدِي مَنْ يشاء / باز مى‏ديدم كه مى‏شد هفت يك
entries.english: Wonderful! There was a bandage over their eyes: they were bound by He guides aright those whom He will.

sections.title_fa: شدن آن هفت شمع بر مثال يك شمع
sections.title_en: How the seven candles became what seemed like one candle.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏شكافد نور او جيب فلك / باز آن يك بار ديگر هفت شد
entries.english: Then I saw the seven become one, its light cleaving the bosom of the sky.

entries.farsi: كه نيايد بر زبان و گفت ما / آن كه يك ديدن كند ادراك آن
entries.english: Then again that one became seven once more: my intoxication and bewilderment waxed mighty.

entries.farsi: مستى و حيرانى من زفت شد / اتصالاتى ميان شمعها
entries.english: Such connections between the candles as may not come on my tongue and my speech.

entries.farsi: ‏سالها نتوان نمودن از زبان / آن كه يك دم بيندش ادراك هوش
entries.english: That which one look perceives, it is impossible during Years to show it forth by the tongue.

entries.farsi: ‏سالها نتوان شنودن آن بگوش / چون كه پايانى ندارد رو اليك
entries.english: That which intellectual apprehension sees in one moment, it is impossible during Years to hear it by the ear.

entries.farsi: تا چه چيز است از نشان كبريا / مى‏شدم بى‏خويش و مدهوش و خراب
entries.english: Since it has no end, go to yourself, for, I cannot reckon any praise of You.

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه ال أحصي ثناء ما عليك / پيشتر رفتم دوان كان شمعها
entries.english: I advanced farther, running what thing those candles are of the signs of the Divine Majesty.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بيفتادم ز تعجيل و شتاب / ساعتى بى‏هوش و بى‏عقل اندر اين
entries.english: I was going, beside myself and dumbfounded and deranged, till I fell down from making haste and speed.

entries.farsi: ‏اوفتادم بر سر خاك زمين / باز با هوش آمدم برخاستم
entries.english: In this, senseless and witless, I lay fallen awhile upon the dust of the earth.

entries.farsi: ‏در روش گويى نه سر نى پاستم
entries.english: Then I came back to my senses and rose up: you would say that in my faring I had neither head nor foot.

sections.title_fa: نمودن آن شمعها در نظر هفت مرد
sections.title_en: How those candles appeared to the eye as seven men.

entries.farsi: هفت شمع اندر نظر شد هفت مرد / نورشان مى‏شد به سقف الجورد
entries.english: The seven candles appeared to the eye as seven men: their light was mounting to the azure vault.

entries.farsi: پيش آن انوار نور روز درد / از صالبت نورها را مى‏سترد
entries.english: Beside those lights the daylight was dregs: by their intensity they were obliterating lights.

sections.title_fa: ‏باز شدن آن شمعها هفت درخت
sections.title_en: How those candles now became seven trees.

entries.farsi: باز هر يك مرد شد شكل درخت / ‏چشمم از سبزى ايشان نيك بخت
entries.english: Then each man assumed the shape of a tree: my eye was happy in their greenery.

entries.farsi: ز انبهى برگ پيدا نيست شاخ / ‏برگ هم گم گشته از ميوه‏ى فراخ
entries.english: On account of the denseness of the leaves no boughs were visible; the leaves too had become scant on account of the plenteous fruit.

entries.farsi: هر درختى شاخ بر سدره زده / ‏سدره چه بود از خال بيرون شده
entries.english: Every tree had thrown its boughs above the Sidra: what of the Sidra? They had reached beyond the Void.

entries.farsi: بيخ هر يك رفته در قعر زمين / ‏زيرتر از گاو و ماهى بد يقين
entries.english: The root of each had gone into the bottom of the earth: assuredly it was lower than the Ox and the Fish.

entries.farsi: بيخشان از شاخ خندان‏روى‏تر / عقل از آن اشكالشان زير و زبر
entries.english: Their roots were more smiling of face than the boughs: the intellect upside down by their shapes.

entries.farsi: ميوه‏اى كه بر شكافيدى ز زور / همچو آب از ميوه جستى برق نور
entries.english: From the fruit that was bursting forcibly flashes of light would spurt forth, like juice.

sections.title_fa: مخفى بودن آن درختان از چشم خلق
sections.title_en: How those trees were invisible to the people.

entries.farsi: اين عجب‏تر كه بر ايشان مى‏گذشت / ‏صد هزاران خلق از صحرا و دشت
entries.english: More wondrous was this that hundreds of thousands of people were passing through the desert and plain beside them

entries.farsi: ز آرزوى سايه جان مى‏باختند / از گليمى سايه‏بان مى‏ساختند
entries.english: Hazarding their lives (ready to sacrifice everything) in desire for shade, and making a parasol out of a woollen garment,

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى آن را نمى‏ديدند هيچ / ‏صد تفو بر ديده‏هاى پيچ پيچ
entries.english: And not seeing the shade of those at all. A hundred spittings on distorted eyes!

entries.farsi: ختم كرده قهر حق بر ديده‏ها / كه نبيند ماه را بيند سها
entries.english: The wrath of God had sealed their eyes, so that he should not see the moon, should see Suha.

entries.farsi: ذره‏اى را بيند و خورشيد نه / ‏ليك از لطف و كرم نوميد نه
entries.english: He sees a mote, not the sun; yet he is not despairing of the grace and loving kindness of God.

entries.farsi: كاروانها بى‏نوا و اين ميوه‏ها / پخته مى‏ريزد چه سحر است اى خدا
entries.english: The caravans are without food, and these fruits are dropping ripe: O God, what magic is this?

entries.farsi: سيب پوسيده هى چيدند خلق / ‏درهم‏افتاده به يغما خشك حلق
entries.english: With parched throats the people, having fallen pell-mell to plunder, were gathering the rotten apples,

entries.farsi: گفته هر برگ و شكوفه‏ى آن غصون / ‏دم‏به‏دم يا لَيْتَ قَوْمِي يعلمون
entries.english: Every leaf and bud of those boughs said continually, ‘Oh, would that my people knew!’

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏آمد ز سوى هر درخت / ‏سوى ما آييد خلق شور بخت
entries.english: From the direction of every tree was coming the cry, ‘Come towards us, O ye folk of evil fortune,’

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏آمد ز غيرت بر شجر / چشمشان بستيم كَلاَّ ال وزر
entries.english: From jealousy there was coming to the trees the cry, ‘We have bandaged their eyes; nay, there is no refuge.’

entries.farsi: گر كسى مى‏گفتشان كاين سو رويد / تا از اين اشجار مستسعد شويد
entries.english: If anyone had said to them ‘Go in this direction, that you may be made happy by these trees,’

entries.farsi: جمله مى‏گفتند كاين مسكين مست / ‏از قضاء اللهَّ ديوانه شده‏ست
entries.english: They all would have said, ‘By Divine destiny this poor intoxicated wretch has become mad:

entries.farsi: مغز اين مسكين ز سوداى دراز / وز رياضت گشت فاسد چون پياز
entries.english: Through long melancholy and through austerities the brain of this poor wretch has turned putrid, like an onion.’

entries.farsi: او عجب مى‏ماند يا رب حال چيست / ‏خلق را اين پرده و اضالل چيست
entries.english: He would have remained in astonishment, saying, ‘O Lord, what is the matter? What is this veil and misguidance that is upon the people?’

entries.farsi: خلق گوناگون با صد راى و عقل / ‏يك قدم آن سو نمى‏آرند نقل
entries.english: The people of every sort, with manifold discernment and understanding, do not move a foot in that direction.

entries.farsi: عاقالن و زيركانشان ز اتفاق / ‏گشته منكر زين چنين باغى و عاق
entries.english: By one consent the intelligent and acute amongst them have become incredulous of such a garden as this and undutiful.

entries.farsi: يا منم ديوانه و خيره شده / ‏ديو چيزى مر مرا بر سر زده
entries.english: Or have I become mad and crazy? Has the Devil cast something upon my head?

entries.farsi: چشم مى‏مالم به هر لحظه كه من / ‏خواب مى‏بينم خيال اندر زمن
entries.english: At every moment I rub my eyes, whether I am dreaming and beholding a phantom in time.

entries.farsi: خواب چه بود بر درختان مى‏روم / ‏ميوه‏هاشان مى‏خورم چون نگروم
entries.english: How can it be a dream? I go up the trees, I eat their fruit: how should I not believe?

entries.farsi: باز چون من بنگرم در منكران / ‏كه همى‏گيرند زين بستان كران
entries.english: Again, when I look at the incredulous ones who turn aside from this orchard,

entries.farsi: با كمال احتياج و افتقار / ز آرزوى نيم غوره جان سپار
entries.english: Devoting their lives with the utmost indigence and penury because of their desire for half an unripe grape;

entries.farsi: ز اشتياق و حرص يك برگ درخت / ‏مى‏زنند اين بى‏نوايان آه سخت
entries.english: These destitute folk uttering grievous lamentation in their longing and greed for a single leaf,

entries.farsi: در هزيمت زين درخت و زين ثمار / اين خاليق صد هزار اندر هزار
entries.english: These hundred thousand on thousands of people fleeing from this tree and these fruits—

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گويم عجب من بى‏خودم / ‏دست در شاخ خيالى در زدم
entries.english: Once more I say, ‘Marvellous! Am I beside myself? Have I laid hold of a bough of phantasy?’”

entries.farsi: حتى إِذ ما اسْ تَيْأَسَ الرُّسُلُ بگو / تا يظنوا أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ كذبوا
entries.english: Repeat until when the Messengers despaired down to they thought they had been belied (kudhibú).

entries.farsi: اين قرائت خوان كه تخفيف كذب / ‏اين بود كه خويش بيند محتجب
entries.english: Recite with this reading (kudhibú), for the omission of the tashdíd in kudhibú signifies that he deems himself debarred.

entries.farsi: در گمان افتاد جان انبيا / ز اتفاق منكرى اشقيا
entries.english: The souls of the prophets fell into misgiving through the concurrence of disbelief of the wicked;

entries.farsi: جاءهم بعد التشكك نصرنا / تركشان گو بر درخت جان بر آ
entries.english: Our aid came to them after doubting. Take leave of them and climb the tree of the spirit.

entries.farsi: خلق گويان اى عجب اين بانگ چيست / ‏چون كه صحرا از درخت و بر تهى است
entries.english: Eat and give it to everyone that has an allotted portion: at each moment and each instant there are lessons in magic.

entries.farsi: مى‏خور و مى‏ده بدان كش روزى است / ‏هر دم و هر لحظه سحر آموزى است
entries.english: “The people are saying, ‘Oh, how wonderful! What is this cry?— since the wilderness is devoid of trees and fruit.

entries.farsi: گيج گشتيم از دم سوداييان / ‏كه به نزديك شما باغ است و خوان
entries.english: We have been fooled by the words of the madmen that beside us there are gardens and trays.

entries.farsi: اى عجب چندين دراز اين گفت‏وگو / چون بود بى‏هوده ور خود هست كو
entries.english: We rub our eyes, no garden is here; it is either a desert or a difficult road.

entries.farsi: چشم مى‏ماليم اينجا باغ نيست / ‏يا بيابان است يا مشكل رهى است
entries.english: Oh, how wonderful! This tale is so long: how should it be vain? And if it really is, where?

entries.farsi: من همى‏گويم چو ايشان اى عجب / ‏اين چنين مهرى چرا زد صنع رب
entries.english: I, like them, am saying, ‘Oh, how wonderful! Why has the action of the Lord put such a seal?’”

entries.farsi: زين تنازعها محمد در عجب / ‏در تعجب نيز مانده بو لهب
entries.english: By these contentions Mohammed was astonished; Abu Lahab also remained in astonishment.

entries.farsi: زين عجب تا آن عجب فرقى است ژرف / ‏تا چه خواهد كرد سلطان شگرف
entries.english: Between this astonishment and that astonishment there is a profound difference. What the Almighty King will do.

entries.farsi: اى دقوقى تيزتر ران هين خموش / ‏چند گويى چند چون قحط است گوش
entries.english: O Daquqi, advance more quickly. Listen, be silent! Inasmuch as there is a dearth of ears, how long will you speak, how long?

sections.title_fa: ‏يك درخت شدن آن هفت درخت
sections.title_en: How the seven trees became one.

entries.farsi: گفت راندم پيشتر من نيك بخت / ‏باز شد آن هفت جمله يك درخت
entries.english: He said, “I, the fortunate one, pushed forward; again all the seven became one tree.

entries.farsi: هفت مى‏شد فرد مى‏شد هر دمى / ‏من چسان مى‏گشتم از حيرت همى
