entries.english: At every moment they were becoming seven and a single one: what I was becoming like, through bewilderment.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن ديدم درختان در نماز / صف كشيده چون جماعت كرده ساز
entries.english: After that, I beheld the trees in the ritual prayer, drawn up in line and arranged like the congregation:

entries.farsi: يك درخت از پيش مانند امام / ‏ديگران اندر پس او در قيام
entries.english: One tree in front like the Imam, the others standing behind it.

entries.farsi: آن قيام و آن ركوع و آن سجود / از درختان بس شگفتم مى‏نمود
entries.english: That standing and kneeling and bowing low on the part of the trees seemed to me very marvellous.

entries.farsi: ياد كردم قول حق را آن زمان / ‏گفت النجم و شجر را يسجدان
entries.english: Then I called to mind the word of God: He said, concerning the stalkless plants and the trees, ‘they bow down.’

entries.farsi: اين درختان را نه زانو نه ميان / ‏اين چه ترتيب نماز است آن چنان
entries.english: Those trees had neither knee nor waist: what is such a regulation of the ritual prayer!

entries.farsi: آمد الهام خدا كاى با فروز / مى عجب دارى ز كار ما هنوز
entries.english: The Divine inspiration came, saying, ‘O illustrious one, are you still wondering at Our action?’

sections.title_fa: ‏هفت مرد شدن آن هفت درخت
sections.title_en: How the seven trees became seven men.

entries.farsi: بعد ديرى گشت آنها هفت مرد / جمله در قعده پى يزدان فرد
entries.english: After a long while those became seven men, all seated for the sake of God who is single.

entries.farsi: چشم مى‏مالم كه آن هفت ارسالن / ‏تا كيانند و چه دارند از جهان
entries.english: I keep rubbing my eyes who are those seven heroes and what they have of this world.

entries.farsi: چون به نزديكى رسيدم من ز راه / ‏كردم ايشان را سالم از انتباه
entries.english: When by the road I came near, I saluted them alertly.

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندم جواب آن سالم / ‏اى دقوقى مفخر و تاج كرام
entries.english: The company answered that salutation, saying, ‘O Daquqi, glory and crown of the noble!’

entries.farsi: گفتم آخر چون مرا بشناختند / پيش از اين بر من نظر ننداختند
entries.english: ‘Why,’ said I, ‘how did they recognise me? They never set eyes on me before this.’

entries.farsi: از ضمير من بدانستند زود / يكدگر را بنگريدند از فرود
entries.english: At once they knew of my unspoken thought, and looked covertly at one another,

entries.farsi: پاسخم دادند خندان كاى عزيز / اين بپوشيده ست اكنون بر تو نيز
entries.english: And smilingly answered, ‘O honoured one is this hidden from you even now?

entries.farsi: بر دلى كاو در تحير با خداست / ‏كى شود پوشيده راز چپ و راست
entries.english: How should the mystery of left and right be hidden from the heart that is in bewilderment with God?’

entries.farsi: گفتم ار سوى حقايق بشكفند / چون ز اسم حرف رسمى واقفند
entries.english: I said, ‘If they are open to the realities, how are they acquainted with names of letters attached to the form?’

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتند ما را آرزوست / ‏اقتدا كردن به تو اى پاك دوست
entries.english: He said, ‘If a name vanishes from a saint, know that that is from absorption, not from ignorance.’

entries.farsi: گفت اگر اسمى شود غيب از ولى / ‏آن ز استغراق دان نز جاهلى
entries.english: Afterwards they said, ‘We desire to follow your leadership, O holy friend.’

entries.farsi: تا شود آن حل به صحبتهاى پاك / ‏كه به صحبت رويد انگورى ز خاك
entries.english: ‘Yes,’ said I, but awhile— for I have certain difficulties from the revolution of Time—

entries.farsi: گفتم آرى ليك يك ساعت كه من / ‏مشكالتى دارم از دور زمن
entries.english: In order that they may be solved by means of holy companionships; for through companionship a grape grows from the earth.

entries.farsi: دانه‏ى پر مغز با خاك دژم / ‏خلوتى و صحبتى كرد از كرم
entries.english: A seed graciously consorted in solitary intercourse with the dark earth;

entries.farsi: خويشتن در خاك كلى محو كرد / تا نماندش رنگ و بو و سرخ و زرد
entries.english: It effaced itself entirely in the earth, so that no colour or scent or red or yellow remained to it.

entries.farsi: از پس آن محو قبض او نماند / پر گشاد و بسط شد مركب براند
entries.english: After that effacement its constriction ceased: it opened its wings and expanded and sped on its way.

entries.farsi: پيش اصل خويش چون بى‏خويش شد / رفت صورت جلوه‏ى معنيش شد
entries.english: Inasmuch as it became selfless in the presence of its origin, the form departed and its real essence was displayed.’

entries.farsi: سر چنين كردند هين فرمان تراست / ‏تف دل از سر چنين كردن بخاست
entries.english: They nodded so, ‘Listen, ’it is for you to command,’ and from their nodding so a flame arose in my heart.

entries.farsi: ساعتى با آن گروه مجتبى / چون مراقب گشتم و از خود جدا
entries.english: When for a while I had taken part with that elect company in contemplation and had been separated from myself,

entries.farsi: جمله تلوينها ز ساعت خاسته ست / ‏رست از تلوين كه از ساعت برست
entries.english: At that very hour my spirit was freed from hours; because hours make the young old.”

entries.farsi: هم در آن ساعت ز ساعت رست جان / ‏ز انكه ساعت پير گرداند جوان
entries.english: All changes have arisen from the hours: he that is freed from the hours is freed from change.

entries.farsi: چون ز ساعت ساعتى بيرون شوى / ‏چون نماند محرم بى‏چون شوى
entries.english: When for an hour you escape from the hours, relation abides not: you become familiar with that which is without relation.

entries.farsi: ساعت از بى‏ساعتى آگاه نيست / ‏ز آن كس آن سو جز تحير راه نيست
entries.english: The hours are not acquainted with timelessness, because for him there is no way there except bewilderment.

entries.farsi: هر نفر را بر طويله‏ى خاص او / بسته‏اند اندر جهان جستجو
entries.english: In this world of search and seeking every set of people have been tied in the stable peculiar to them,

entries.farsi: منتصب بر هر طويله رايضى / ‏جز به دستورى نيايد رافضى
entries.english: And over each stable a trainer has been appointed; save by permission no recalcitrant comes.

entries.farsi: از هوس گر از طويله بگسلد / در طويله‏ى ديگران سر در كند
entries.english: If, from vain desire, he should break away from the stable and intrude into the stable of others,

entries.farsi: در زمان آخورچيان چست خوش / ‏گوشه‏ى افسار او گيرند و كش
entries.english: At once the nimble and goodly stablemen seize the corner of his halter and drag.

entries.farsi: حافظان را گر نبينى اى عيار / اختيارت را ببين بى‏اختيار
entries.english: O cunning one, if you behold not your keepers, behold your choice involuntary.

entries.farsi: اختيارى مى‏كنى و دست و پا / بر گشاده ستت چرا حبسى چرا
entries.english: You are making a choice, and your hands and feet are loosed: why are you imprisoned, why?

entries.farsi: روى در انكار حافظ برده‏اى / ‏نام تهديدات نفسش كرده‏اى
entries.english: You have betaken yourself to denying the keeper: you have called it ‘threats of the fleshly soul.’

sections.title_fa: ‏پيش رفتن دقوقى به امامت
sections.title_en: How Daquqi went forward to act as Imam.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد تيز دو / هين نماز آمد دقوقى پيش رو
entries.english: This discourse has no end. “Run quickly! Listen, the prayer is come. Go forward, O Daquqi!

entries.farsi: اين يگانه هين دوگانه برگزار / تا مزين گردد از تو روزگار
entries.english: O unique one, come; perform the twofold kneeling, that Time may be adorned by you.

entries.farsi: اى امام چشم روشن در صال / چشم روشن بايد اندر پيشوا
entries.english: O clear-sighted Imam, the Imam must always be clear sighted.”

entries.farsi: در شريعت هست مكروه اى كيا / در امامت پيش كردن كور را
entries.english: According to the religious Law it is objectionable, O worthy, to put forward a blind man in the office of Imam.

entries.farsi: گر چه حافظ باشد و چست و فقيه / ‏چشم روشن به و گر باشد سفيه
entries.english: Though he knows the Qur’an by heart and be quick and learned in divinity, the clear-sighted man is superior, even if he be a fool.

entries.farsi: كور را پرهيز نبود از قذر / چشم باشد اصل پرهيز و حذر
entries.english: The blind man has no abstention from filth: the eye is the source of abstention and precaution.

entries.farsi: او پليدى را نبيند در عبور / هيچ مومن را مبادا چشم كور
entries.english: He does not see the dirt in passing by. May no true believer have blind eyes!

entries.farsi: كور ظاهر در نجاسه‏ى ظاهر است / ‏كور باطن در نجاسات سر است
entries.english: The man outwardly blind is in outward filthiness; the man inwardly blind is in inward filthiness.

entries.farsi: اين نجاسه‏ى ظاهر از آبى رود / آن نجاسه‏ى باطن افزون مى‏شود
entries.english: This outward filthiness may be removed by some water; that inward filthiness increases.

entries.farsi: جز به آب چشم نتوان شستن آن / ‏چون نجاسات بواطن شد عيان
entries.english: It cannot be washed away save by water of the eye, when the inward filthinesses have become manifest.

entries.farsi: چون نجس خوانده ست كافر را خدا / آن نجاست نيست بر ظاهر و را
entries.english: Since God has called the infidel “filth,” that filthiness is not on his outward part.

entries.farsi: ظاهر كافر ملوث نيست زين / ‏آن نجاست هست در اخالق و دين
entries.english: The infidel’s outward part is not defiled by this; that filthiness is in disposition and religion.

entries.farsi: اين نجاست بويش آيد بيست گام / ‏و آن نجاست بويش از رى تا به شام
entries.english: The smell of this outward filth comes twenty paces; but the smell of that filth from Rayy to Damascus;

entries.farsi: بلكه بويش آسمانها بر رود / بر دماغ حور و رضوان بر شود
entries.english: No, its smell goes up to the heavens and mounts to the brain of the houris and Rizwán.

entries.farsi: اين چه مى‏گويم به قدر فهم تست / ‏مردم اندر حسرت فهم درست
entries.english: What I am saying is according to the measure of your understanding: I die in grief for a sound understanding.

entries.farsi: فهم آب است و وجود تن سبو / چون سبو بشكست ريزد آب از او
entries.english: The understanding is the water, and the bodily existence the jug: when the jug is cracked, the water spills from it.

entries.farsi: اين سبو را پنج سوراخ است ژرف / ‏اندر او نه آب ماند خود نه برف
entries.english: This jug has five deep holes: neither water nor snow will stay in it.

entries.farsi: أمر غضوا غضه أبصاركم / ‏هم شنيدى راست ننهادى تو سم
entries.english: You have heard, too, the command, “Close your eyes tightly”; you have not walked aright.

entries.farsi: از دهانت نطق فهمت را برد / گوش چون ريگ است فهمت را خورد
entries.english: Your speech bears away your understanding by the mouth; your ear is like sand: it drinks your understanding.

entries.farsi: همچنين سوراخهاى ديگرت / ‏مى‏كشاند آب فهم مضمرت
entries.english: Similarly, your other holes are drawing the hidden water of your understanding.

entries.farsi: گر ز دريا آب را بيرون كنى / ‏بى‏عوض آن بحر را هامون كنى
entries.english: If you expel the water from the sea without compensation, you will make the sea a desert.

entries.farsi: بى‏گه است ار نه بگويم حال را / مدخل اعواض را و ابدال را
entries.english: It is late; otherwise, I would declare the state of the case the entrance of compensations and substitutes,

entries.farsi: كان عوضها و بدلها بحر را / از كجا آيد ز بعد خرجها
entries.english: Where comes to the sea those compensations and substitutes after expenditures

entries.farsi: صد هزاران جانور زو مى‏خورند / ابرها هم از برونش مى‏برند
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of animals drink of it; from outside also the clouds take it away;

entries.farsi: باز دريا آن عوضها مى‏كشد / از كجا، دانند اصحاب رشد
entries.english: Again the sea draws those compensations— whence is known to the righteous.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ها آغاز كرديم از شتاب / ‏ماند بى‏مخلص درون اين كتاب
entries.english: We began the stories in haste; in this Masnavi they are left without the issue.

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام الدين راد / كه فلك و اركان چو تو شاهى نزاد
entries.english: O Light of God, noble Husamu’ddin, a king whose like the sky and the elements have never brought to birth,

entries.farsi: تو به نادر آمدى در جان و دل / ‏اى دل و جان از قدوم تو خجل
entries.english: Seldom have you come into soul and heart, O you at whose advent heart and soul are abashed.

entries.farsi: چند كردم مدح قوم ما مضى / قصد من ز آنها تو بودى ز اقتضا
entries.english: How often have I praised the people of the past! Of necessity, you were my quest in them.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى خود را شناسد خود دعا / تو به نام هر كه خواهى كن ثنا
entries.english: Truly the invocation knows its own house: attach the praise to the name of whomsoever you will.

entries.farsi: بهر كتمان مديح از نامحل / ‏حق نهاده ست اين حكايات و مثل
entries.english: God has set down these tales and parables for the purpose of concealing the praise from the unworthy.

entries.farsi: حق پذيرد كسره اى دارد معاف / ‏كز دو ديده‏ى كور دو قطره كفاف
entries.english: Even if that praise is abashed before you, yet God accepts the exertion of one that has little.

entries.farsi: گر چه آن مدح از تو هم آمد خجل / ‏ليك بپذيرد خدا جهد المقل
entries.english: God accepts a crust and absolves, for from the eyes of a blind man two drops are enough.

entries.farsi: مرغ و ماهى داند آن ابهام را / كه ستودم مجمل اين خوش نام را
entries.english: Birds and fishes know the ambiguous style, in which I have praised compendiously this person of goodly name,

entries.farsi: تا بر او آه حسودان كم وزد / تا خيالش را به دندان كم گزد
entries.english: To the end that the sighs of the envious may not blow upon him, and that he may not bite the idea of Husamu’ddin with the teeth.

entries.farsi: خود خيالش را كجا يابد حسود / در وثاق موش طوطى كى غنود
entries.english: Where should the envious man find even the idea of him? When did a parrot rest in the abode of a mouse?

entries.farsi: آن خيال او بود از احتيال / ‏موى ابروى وى است آن نى هالل
entries.english: That idea of Husamu’ddin arises from cunning practice: it is the hair of his eyebrow, not the new moon.

entries.farsi: مدح تو گويم برون از پنج و هفت / ‏بر نويس اكنون دقوقى پيش رفت
entries.english: I sing your praise outside of the five and the seven. Now write “Daquqi went forward.”

sections.title_fa: ‏پيش رفتن دقوقى به امامت آن قوم
sections.title_en: How Daquqi went forward to lead that company.

entries.farsi: در تحيات و سالم الصالحين / ‏مدح جمله‏ى انبيا آمد عجين
entries.english: In the salutations and benedictions addressed to the righteous praise of all the prophets is blended.

entries.farsi: مدحها شد جملگى آميخته / ‏كوزه‏ها در يك لگن در ريخته
entries.english: The praises are all commingled: the jugs are poured into one basin.

entries.farsi: ز انكه خود ممدوح جز يك بيش نيست / ‏كيشها زين روى جز يك كيش نيست
entries.english: Inasmuch as the object of praise Himself is not more than One, from this point of view religions are but one religion.

entries.farsi: دان كه هر مدحى به نور حق رود / بر صور و اشخاص عاريت بود
entries.english: Know that every praise goes to the Light of God and is lent to forms and persons.

entries.farsi: مدحها جز مستحق را كى كنند / ليك بر پنداشت گمره مى‏شوند
entries.english: How should folk praise except Him who has the right?— but they go astray on a vain fancy.

entries.farsi: الجرم چون سايه سوى اصل راند / ضال مه گم كرد و ز استايش بماند
entries.english: The Light of God in relation to phenomena is as a light shining upon a wall— the wall is a link for these splendours:

entries.farsi: همچو نورى تافته بر حايطى / ‏حايط آن انوار را چون رابطى
entries.english: Necessarily, when the reflection moved towards its source, he who had gone astray lost the moon and ceased from praise;

entries.farsi: يا ز چاهى عكس ماهى وانمود / سر به چه در كرد و آن را مى‏ستود
entries.english: Or a reflection of the moon appeared from a well, and he put his head into the well and was praising that same:

entries.farsi: در حقيقت مادح ماه است او / گر چه جهل او به عكسش كرد رو
entries.english: In truth he is a praiser of the moon, although his ignorance has turned its face towards its reflection.

entries.farsi: مدح او مه راست نى آن عكس را / كفر شد آن چون غلط شد ماجرا
entries.english: His praise belongs to the moon, not to that reflection, that becomes infidelity when the matter is misapprehended;

entries.farsi: كز شقاوت گشت گمره آن دلير / مه به بالا بود و او پنداشت زير
entries.english: For that bold man was led astray by perdition: the moon was above, while he fancied it was below.

entries.farsi: زين بتان خلقان پريشان مى‏شوند / شهوت رانده پشيمان مى‏شوند
entries.english: The people are distracted by these idols, and they repent of the lust which they have indulged,

entries.farsi: ز انكه شهوت با خيالى رانده است / ‏وز حقيقت دورتر وامانده است
entries.english: Because he has indulged his lust with a phantom and has remained farther away from the Reality.

entries.farsi: با خيالى ميل تو چون پر بود / تا بدان پر بر حقيقت بر شود
entries.english: Your desire for a phantom is like a wing, so that by means of that wing he may ascend to the Reality.

entries.farsi: چون براندى شهوتى پرت بريخت / ‏لنگ گشتى و آن خيال از تو گريخت
entries.english: When you have indulged a lust, your wing drops off; you become lame, and that phantom flees from you.

entries.farsi: پر نگه دار و چنين شهوت مران / ‏تا پر ميلت برد سوى جنان
entries.english: Preserve the wing and do not indulge such lust, to the end that the wing of desire may bear you to Paradise.

entries.farsi: خلق پندارند عشرت مى‏كنند / بر خيالى پر خود بر مى‏كنند
entries.english: The people fancy they are enjoying themselves: they are tearing out their wings for the sake of a phantom.

entries.farsi: وام دار شرح اين نكته شدم / ‏مهلتم ده معسرم ز آن تن زدم
entries.english: I have become a debtor for the explanation of this topic. Give me time, I am destitute; on that account I keep silence.

sections.title_fa: ‏اقتدا كردن قوم از پس دقوقى
sections.title_en: How the company followed the leadership of Daquqi.

entries.farsi: پيش در شد آن دقوقى در نماز / قوم همچون اطلس آمد او طراز
entries.english: Daquqi advanced to perform the prayer: the company was the satin robe and he the embroidered border.

entries.farsi: اقتدا كردند آن شاهان قطار / در پى آن مقتداى نامدار
entries.english: Those kings followed his leadership, in a row behind that renowned exemplar.

entries.farsi: چون كه با تكبيرها مقرون شدند / همچو قربان از جهان بيرون شدند
entries.english: When they pronounced the takbirs, they went forth from this world, like a sacrifice.

entries.farsi: معنى تكبير اين است اى امام / ‏كاى خدا پيش تو ما قربان شديم
entries.english: O Imam, the meaning of the takbirs is this: “We have become a sacrifice, O God, before You.”

entries.farsi: وقت ذبح اللهَّ اكبر مى‏كنى / ‏همچنين در ذبح نفس كشتنى
entries.english: At the moment of slaughtering you say Allah Akbar: even so in slaughtering the fleshly soul which ought to be killed.

entries.farsi: تن چو اسماعيل و جان همچون خليل / ‏كرد جان تكبير بر جسم نبيل
entries.english: The body is like Ishmael, and the spirit like Abraham: the spirit has pronounced the takbir over the noble body.

entries.farsi: گشت كشته تن ز شهوتها و آز / شد به بسم اللهَّ بسمل در نماز
entries.english: By lusts and desires the body was killed, by bismillah in the ritual prayer it was sacrificed.

entries.farsi: چون قيامت پيش حق صفها زده / ‏در حساب و در مناجات آمده
entries.english: Whilst performing the prayer drawn up in ranks before God, as at the Resurrection, and engaged in self-examination and prayers,

entries.farsi: ايستاده پيش يزدان اشك ريز / بر مثال راست خيز رستخيز
entries.english: Standing in God’s presence and shedding tears, like one who rises erect on rising from the dead.

entries.farsi: حق همى‏گويد چه آوردى مرا / اندر اين مهلت كه دادم من ترا
entries.english: God will say, “What have you produced for Me during this term of respite which I gave you?

entries.farsi: عمر خود را در چه پايان برده‏اى / ‏قوت و قوت در چه فانى كرده‏اى
entries.english: In what have you brought your life to its end? In what have you consumed your food and strength?

entries.farsi: گوهر ديده كجا فرسوده‏اى / پنج حس را در كجا پالوده‏اى
entries.english: Where have you dimmed the lustre of your eye? Where have you dissipated your five senses?

entries.farsi: چشم و گوش و هوش و گوهرهاى عرش / ‏خرج كردى چه خريدى تو ز فرش
entries.english: You have expended eyes and ears and intellect and the pure celestial substances: what have you purchased from the earth?

entries.farsi: دست و پا دادمت چون بيل و كلند / من ببخشيدم ز خود آن كى شدند
entries.english: I gave you hands and feet as spade and mattock. When did those become of themselves?”

entries.farsi: همچنين پيغامهاى دردگين / ‏صد هزاران آيد از حضرت چنين
entries.english: Even so hundreds of thousands of such sorrowful messages come from the Lord.

entries.farsi: در قيام اين گفتها دارد رجوع / ‏و ز خجالت شد دو تا او در ركوع
entries.english: At the time of standing (in prayer) these words return, and from shame he is bent double in the genuflection.

entries.farsi: قوت استادن از خجلت نماند / در ركوع از شرم تسبيحى بخواند
entries.english: From shame the power of standing remains not, and from abashment he recites a litany of glorification while his knees are bowed.

entries.farsi: باز فرمان مى‏رسد بردار سر / از ركوع و پاسخ حق بر شمر
entries.english: Then comes the command, “Lift up your head from the genuflection and tell over answer to God.”

entries.farsi: سر بر آرد از ركوع آن شرمسار / باز اندر رو فتد آن خام كار
entries.english: The shamefaced one lifts up his head from the genuflection; then that man whose works are unripe falls on his face.

entries.farsi: باز فرمان آيدش بردار سر / از سجود و واده از كرده خبر
entries.english: Again the command comes to him, “Lift up your head from the prostration and give an account of your deeds.”

entries.farsi: سر بر آرد او دگر ره شرمسار / اندر افتد باز در رو همچو مار
entries.english: Once more the shamefaced one lifts up his head, and falls again on his face, as a snake.

entries.farsi: باز گويد سر بر آر و باز گو / كه بخواهم جست از تو مو به مو
entries.english: Again He says, “Lift up your head and relate, for I will inquire of you, hair by hair.”

entries.farsi: قوت پا ايستادن نبودش / ‏كه خطاب هيبتى بر جان زدش
entries.english: He has no power to stand on foot, since the words of awe addressed to him have smitten his soul;

entries.farsi: پس نشيند قعده ز آن بار گران / ‏حضرتش گويد سخن گو با بيان
entries.english: So he sits down because of that heavy burden. The Lord says to him, “Speak plainly!

entries.farsi: نعمتت دادم بگو شكرت چه بود / دادمت سرمايه هين بنماى سود
entries.english: I gave you bounty: tell, what were your thanks? I gave you capital: come, show the interest.”

entries.farsi: رو به دست راست آرد در سالم / ‏سوى جان انبيا و آن كرام
entries.english: He turns his face to the right hand in the salutation— towards the spirits of the prophets and those of the noble,

entries.farsi: يعنى اى شاهان شفاعت كاين لئيم / ‏سخت در گل ماندش پاى و گليم
entries.english: Meaning to say, “O kings, intercession, for this vile one’s feet and mantle are stuck fast in the mire”

sections.title_fa: بيان اشارت سالم سوى دست راست در قيامت از هيبت محاسبه‏ى حق و از انبيا استعانت و شفاعت خواستن
sections.title_en: Explaining that the salutation towards the right hand at the Resurrection indicates dread of being examined by God and seeking help and intercession from the prophets

entries.farsi: انبيا گويند روز چاره رفت / ‏چاره آن جا بود و دست‏افزار زفت
entries.english: The prophets say, “The day for remedy is past; the remedy and the strong implement were there.

entries.farsi: رو بگرداند به سوى دست چپ / ‏در تبار و خويش گويندش كه خپ
entries.english: You are an untimely bird. Leave, O miserable one, go, and do not wade in our blood.”

entries.farsi: مرغ بى‏هنگامى اى بد بخت رو / ترك ما گو خون ما اندر مشو
entries.english: He turns his face to the left hand towards his family and kinsfolk: they say to him, “Be silent!

entries.farsi: هين جواب خويش گو با كردگار / ما كه‏ايم اى خواجه دست از ما بدار
entries.english: Listen, answer for yourself to the Creator. Who are we? Sire, keep your hands off us!”

entries.farsi: نه ازين سو نه از آن سو چاره شد / جان آن بى‏چاره دل صد پاره شد
entries.english: No succour comes either from this side or from that: the soul of this desperate man is a hundred pieces.

entries.farsi: از همه نوميد شد مسكين كيا / پس بر آرد هر دو دست اندر دعا
entries.english: The wretched personage loses hope of all; then he lifts up both hands in supplication,

entries.farsi: كز همه نوميد گشتم اى خدا / اول و آخر تويى و منتها
entries.english: Crying, “O God, I have lost hope of all: You are the First and the Last and the ultimate Bourn.”

entries.farsi: بچه بيرون آر از بيضه‏ى نماز / سر مزن چو مرغ بى‏تعظيم و ساز
entries.english: Behold in the ritual prayer these goodly indications, in order that you may know these will certainly come to pass.

entries.farsi: در نماز اين خوش اشارتها ببين / ‏تا بدانى كاين بخواهد شد يقين
entries.english: From the ritual prayer, which is the egg, hatch the chick; do not peck like a bird without reverence or propriety.

sections.title_fa: ‏شنيدن دقوقى در ميان نماز افغان آن كشتى كه غرق خواست شدن
sections.title_en: How during the ritual prayer Daquqi heard cries of distress from a ship that was about to sink

entries.farsi: آن دقوقى در امامت كرد ساز / اندر آن ساحل در آمد در نماز
entries.english: Daquqi made ready to act as Imam: he began to perform the ritual prayer on the shore,

entries.farsi: ناگهان چشمش سوى دريا فتاد / چون شنيد از سوى دريا داد داد
entries.english: While that company stood up behind him. Look you, a goodly company, and an elect Imam!

entries.farsi: و آن جماعت در پى او در قيام / ‏اينت زيبا قوم و بگزيده امام
entries.english: Of a sudden his eye turned towards the sea, because he heard “Help! Help!” from the direction of the sea.

entries.farsi: در ميان موج ديد او كشتيى / ‏در قضا و در بال و زشتيى
entries.english: He saw amidst the waves a ship in fate, and in tribulation and an evil plight.

entries.farsi: هم شب و هم ابر و هم موج عظيم / ‏اين سه تاريكى و از غرقاب بيم
entries.english: Night, clouds and huge waves: these three types of darkness, and fear of the whirlpool.

entries.farsi: تند بادى همچو عزراييل خاست / ‏موجها آشوفت اندر چپ و راست
entries.english: A fierce wind, like Azra’il, arose; the waves tossed on left and right.

entries.farsi: اهل كشتى از مهابت كاسته / ‏نعره‏ى وا ويلها برخاسته
entries.english: The people in the ship were faint with terror: cries of woe had arisen,

entries.farsi: دستها در نوحه بر سر مى‏زدند / كافر و ملحد همه مخلص شدند
entries.english: And in lamentation they were beating their heads with their hands: infidel and deist—they all had become sincere,

entries.farsi: با خدا با صد تضرع آن زمان / ‏عهدها و نذرها كرده به جان
entries.english: Making heartfelt promises and vows to God with a hundred humble entreaties in that hour

entries.farsi: سر برهنه در سجود آنها كه هيچ / ‏رويشان قبله نديد از پيچ پيچ
entries.english: Bare-headed in the prostrate attitude were those whose faces, because of perversity, had never seen the qibla at all.

entries.farsi: گفته كه بى‏فايده ست اين بندگى / ‏آن زمان ديده در آن صد زندگى
entries.english: They said, “This worship of God is useless”; in that hour they saw a hundred lives therein.

entries.farsi: از همه اوميد ببريده تمام / ‏دوستان و خال و عم بابا و مام
entries.english: They had entirely abandoned hope of all— of friends and maternal and paternal uncles and father and mother.

entries.farsi: زاهد و فاسق شد آن دم متقى / ‏همچو در هنگام جان كندن شقى
entries.english: At that moment ascetic and reprobate had become God-fearing as a wicked man at the time of the death-agony.

entries.farsi: نى ز چپشان چاره بود و نى ز راست / ‏حيله‏ها چون مرد هنگام دعاست
entries.english: Neither on the left nor on the right was there any help for them: when expedients are dead, is the time to invoke God.

entries.farsi: در دعا ايشان و در زارى و آه / ‏بر فلك ز ايشان شده دود سياه
entries.english: They were in invocation and lament and moaning: a black smoke went up from them to heaven.

entries.farsi: ديو آن دم از عداوت بين بين / ‏بانگ زد كاى سگ پرستان علتين
entries.english: Then the Devil cried in enmity, “Away! Away! O dog-worshippers, two maladies.

entries.farsi: مرگ و جسك اى اهل انكار و نفاق / ‏عاقبت خواهد بدن اين اتفاق
entries.english: Death and woe! O unbelievers and hypocrites, this will befall in the end,

entries.farsi: چشمتان تر باشد از بعد خالص / ‏كه شويد از بهر شهوت ديو خاص
entries.english: After deliverance you will rejoice to become peculiar devils for the sake of your lust,

entries.farsi: يادتان نايد كه روزى در خطر / دستتان بگرفت يزدان از قدر
entries.english: And will not remember that in the day of peril God took your hands from His decree.”

entries.farsi: اين همى‏آمد ندا از ديو ليك / ‏اين سخن را نشنود جز گوش نيك
entries.english: This cry was coming from the Devil; but these words are unheard except by a good ear.

entries.farsi: راست فرموده ست با ما مصطفى / قطب و شاهنشاه و درياى صفا
entries.english: Mustafa, the Pole and the Emperor and the Sea of Purity, has told us truly,

entries.farsi: كانچه جاهل ديد خواهد عاقبت / ‏عاقالن بينند ز اول مرتبت
entries.english: That what the ignorant will see in the end the wise see from the first step

entries.farsi: كارها ز آغاز اگر غيب است و سر / عاقل اول ديد و آخر آن مصر
entries.english: If matters are hidden and secret at the beginning, the wise man sees at first, while that obstinate one at last.

entries.farsi: اولش پوشيده باشد و آخر آن / عاقل و جاهل ببيند در عيان‏
entries.english: The beginning thereof is concealed, and both the wise man and the ignorant will see the end in manifestation;

entries.farsi: گر نبينى واقعه‏ى غيب اى عنود / حزم را سيالب كى اندر ربود
entries.english: If you, O rebellious one, do not see the hidden event— when did the torrent sweep away your prudence?

entries.farsi: حزم چه بود بد گمانى بر جهان / دم‏به‏دم بيند بالى ناگهان‏
entries.english: What is prudence? To think ill of this world. He at every moment will see a sudden calamity.

sections.title_fa: ‏تصورات مرد حازم
sections.title_en: The ideas of the prudent man

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه ناگهان شيرى رسيد / مرد را بربود و در بيشه كشيد
entries.english: It is as when a lion has suddenly come up and seized a man and dragged him into the jungle.

entries.farsi: او چه انديشد در آن بردن ببين / تو همان انديش اى استاد دين‏
entries.english: At that carrying off, what will he think of? Consider, and think of the same thing, O you who are learned in the Religion.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشد شير قضا در بيشه‏ها / جان ما مشغول كار و پيشه‏ها
entries.english: The lion, Destiny, is dragging into the jungles our souls which are preoccupied with business and trades.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كز فقر مى‏ترسند خلق / زير آب شور رفته تا به حلق‏
entries.english: That is like that the people have fear of poverty, plunged up to their throats in the briny water.

entries.farsi: گر بترسندى از آن فقر آفرين / گنجهاشان كشف گشتى در زمني‏
entries.english: If they should fear the Creator of poverty, treasures would be opened to them on the earth.

entries.farsi: جمله‏شان از خوف غم در عين غم / در پى هستى فتاده در عدم‏
entries.english: Through fear of affliction they all are in the very essence of affliction: in their quest for existence they have fallen into non-existence.

sections.title_fa: ‏دعا و شفاعت دقوقى در خالص كشتى
sections.title_en: Daquqi’s entreaty and intercession for the deliverance of the ship

entries.farsi: چون دقوقى آن قيامت را بديد / رحم او جوشيد و اشك او دويد
entries.english: When Daquqi beheld that turmoil, his pity was stirred and his tears flowed fast.

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب منگر اندر فعلشان / دستشان گير اى شه نيكو نشان‏
entries.english: He said, “O Lord, do not look at their deeds! Take their hands, O auspicious King!

entries.farsi: خوش سالمتشان به ساحل باز بر / اى رسيده دست تو در بحر و بر
entries.english: Bring them back well and safe to the shore, O You whose hand reaches sea and land!

entries.farsi: اى كريم و اى رحيم سرمدى / در گذار از بد سگالان اين بدى‏
entries.english: O Gracious One, O Merciful and Everlasting One, pass over this wickedness committed by devisers of evil!

entries.farsi: اى بداده رايگان صد چشم و گوش / ‏بى‏ز رشوت بخش كرده عقل و هوش
entries.english: O You who have given, free of cost, a hundred eyes and ears, and, without bribe, have dispensed intellect and understanding;

entries.farsi: پيش از استحقاق بخشيده عطا / ديده از ما جمله كفران و خطا
entries.english: Who have bestowed the gift before the merit, having suffered from us the whole of ingratitude and transgression:

entries.farsi: اى عظيم از ما گناهان عظيم / ‏تو توانى عفو كردن در حريم
entries.english: O Almighty One, You are able to pardon our great sins in privacy.

entries.farsi: ما ز آز و حرص خود را سوختيم / ‏وين دعا را هم ز تو آموختيم
entries.english: We have burnt ourselves from lust and greed, and even this invocation we have learned from You.

entries.farsi: حرمت آن كه دعا آموختى / ‏در چنين ظلمت چراغ افروختى
entries.english: In reverence for Your having taught to invoke and for having lighted the lamp amidst darkness like this.”

entries.farsi: همچنين مى‏رفت بر لفظش دعا / آن زمان چون مادران با وفا
entries.english: Thus was the invocation running on his tongue at that time, like faithful mothers.

entries.farsi: اشك مى‏رفت از دو چشمش و آن دعا / بى‏خود از وى مى‏برآمد بر سما
entries.english: The tears were flowing from his eyes, and that invocation was going up to Heaven from him beside himself.

entries.farsi: آن دعاى بى‏خود آن خود ديگر است / ‏آن دعا ز او نيست گفت داور است
entries.english: That unconscious invocation is, in truth, different: that invocation is not from him, it is spoken by the Judge.

entries.farsi: آن دعا حق مى‏كند چون او فناست / ‏آن دعا و آن اجابت از خداست
entries.english: God is making that invocation, since he is non-existent (fana): the invocation and the answer are from God.

entries.farsi: واسطه‏ى مخلوق نى اندر ميان / ‏بى‏خبر ز آن البه كردن جسم و جان
entries.english: There is not present the medium, namely, the created person: body and spirit are unaware of making that supplication.

entries.farsi: بندگان حق رحيم و بردبار / خوى حق دارند در اصالح كار
entries.english: The servants of God are merciful and long-suffering: they possess the disposition of God in regard to putting things right.

entries.farsi: مهربان بى‏رشوتان ياريگران / ‏در مقام سخت و در روز گران
entries.english: They are kind and honest ones, helpers in the hard plight and the heavy day.

entries.farsi: هين بجو اين قوم را اى مبتال / هين غنيمت دارشان پيش از بال
entries.english: Listen; seek this company, O afflicted one! Listen; hold them a prize before the affliction.

entries.farsi: رست كشتى از دم آن پهلوان / ‏و اهل كشتى را به جهد خود گمان
entries.english: Through the breath of that hero the ship was saved, while the people in the ship thought by their own efforts,

entries.farsi: كه مگر بازوى ايشان در حذر / بر هدف انداخت تيرى از هنر
entries.english: That maybe in dread their arm had skillfully shot an arrow at the target.

entries.farsi: پا رهاند روبهان را در شكار / و آن ز دم دانند روباهان غرار
entries.english: Foxes, in the chase, are saved by their legs, but the foxes inconsiderately deem that from their tails.

entries.farsi: عشقها با دم خود بازند كاين / ‏مى‏رهاند جان ما را در كمين
entries.english: They play fondly with their tails, thinking, “These save our lives in the ambuscade.”

entries.farsi: روبها پا را نگه دار از كلوخ / ‏پا چو نبود دم چه سود اى چشم شوخ
entries.english: O fox, preserve your legs from brickbats; when you have no legs, what use is your tail, O bold-eyed one?

entries.farsi: ما چو روباهيم و پاى ما كرام / ‏مى‏رهاندمان ز صد گون انتقام
entries.english: We are like foxes, and the noble are our legs: they save us from a hundred kinds of vengeance.

entries.farsi: حيله‏ى باريك ما چون دم ماست / ‏عشقها بازيم با دم چپ و راست
entries.english: Our subtle contrivance is as our tails: we play fondly with our tails, left and right.

entries.farsi: دم بجنبانيم ز استدالل و مكر / تا كه حيران ماند از ما زيد و بكر
entries.english: We wag our tails in argumentation and cunning, in order that Zayd and Bakr may remain amazed at us.

entries.farsi: طالب حيرانى خلقان شديم / ‏دست طمع اندر الوهيت زديم
entries.english: We have sought to excite the amazement of the people; we have eagerly grasped at Divinity,

entries.farsi: تا به افسون مالك دلها شويم / ‏اين نمى‏بينيم ما كاندر گويم
entries.english: That by means of guile we may gain possession of hearts; we do not see that we are in a ditch

entries.farsi: در گوى و در چهى اى قلتبان / ‏دست وادار از سبال ديگران
entries.english: You are in the ditch and in the pit, O scoundrel: keep your hands off the moustache of others!

entries.farsi: چون به بستانى رسى زيبا و خوش / ‏بعد از آن دامان خلقان گير و كش
entries.english: When you arrive at a fair and beauteous garden, after that lay hold of the people’s skirts and lead them.

entries.farsi: اى مقيم حبس چار و پنج و شش / ‏نغز جايى ديگران را هم بكش
entries.english: O you, who dwell in the prison of the four and the five and the six, lead others also to a goodly place!

entries.farsi: اى چو خربنده حريف كون خر / بوسه‏گاهى يافتى ما را ببر
entries.english: O you who, like an ass-servant, are the comrade of the ass’s rump, you have found a spot to kiss: take us!

entries.farsi: چون ندادت بندگى دوست دست / ‏ميل شاهى از كجايت خاسته‏ست
entries.english: Since servitude to the Beloved has not been granted you, from where has arisen in you the wish for sovereignty?

entries.farsi: در هواى آن كه گويندت زهى / ‏بسته‏اى در گردن جانت زهى
entries.english: In your desire that they should say to you “Bravo!” you have tied a bowstring on the neck of your soul.

entries.farsi: روبها اين دم حيلت را بهل / ‏وقف كن دل بر خداوندان دل
entries.english: O fox, abandon this tail, contrivance, and devote your heart to the lords of the heart.

entries.farsi: تو دال منظور حق آن گه شوى / ‏كه چو جزوى سوى كل خود روى
entries.english: Under the protection of the lion, roast-meat will not fail; O fox, do not hasten towards the carcass.

entries.farsi: حق همى‏گويد نظرمان بر دل است / ‏نيست بر صورت كه آن آب و گل است
entries.english: O heart, you will be regarded by God at the moment when, like a part, you go towards your Whole.

entries.farsi: در پناه شير كم نايد كباب / ‏روبها تو سوى جيفه كم شتاب
entries.english: God says, “Our regard is on the heart; it is not on the external form, which is water and earth.”

entries.farsi: تو همى‏گويى مرا دل نيز هست / ‏دل فراز عرش باشد نى به پست
entries.english: You say, “I too have a heart”; the heart is above the empyrean, it is not below.

entries.farsi: در گل تيره يقين هم آب هست / ‏ليك ز آن آبت نشايد آب دست
entries.english: Certainly in the dark earth also there is water, but it is not proper for you to wash your hands with that water,

entries.farsi: ز انكه گر آب است مغلوب گل است / ‏پس دل خود را مگو كاين هم دل است
entries.english: Because, though it is water, it is overcome by the earth. Do not, then, say of your heart, “This too is a heart.”

entries.farsi: آن دلى كز آسمانها برتر است / ‏آن دل ابدال يا پيغمبر است
entries.english: The heart that is higher than the heavens is the heart of the saint or the prophet.

entries.farsi: آب ما محبوس گل مانده ست هين / ‏بحر رحمت جذب كن ما را ز طين
entries.english: That has become cleansed of earth and purified; it has come to growth and has been made complete.

entries.farsi: ترك گل كرده سوى بحر آمده / ‏رسته از زندان گل بحرى شده
entries.english: It has taken leave of earth and has come to the Sea; it has escaped from the prison of earth and has become of the Sea.

entries.farsi: پاك گشته آن ز گل صافى شده / در فزونى آمده وافى شده
entries.english: Our water has remained imprisoned in earth. Listen, O Sea of Mercy, draw us out of the clay!

entries.farsi: بحر گويد من ترا در خود كشم / ‏ليك مى‏الفى كه من آب خوشم
entries.english: The Sea says, “I draw you into myself, but you art vainly pretending to be the sweet water.

entries.farsi: الف تو محروم مى‏دارد ترا / ترك آن پنداشت كن در من در آ
entries.english: Your vain pretence is keeping you deprived of fortune: abandon that fancy and enter into me.”

entries.farsi: آب گل خواهد كه در دريا رود / گل گرفته پاى آب و مى‏كشد
entries.english: The water in the earth desires to go into the Sea; the earth has seized the water’s foot and is dragging.

entries.farsi: گر رهاند پاى خود از دست گل / ‏گل بماند خشك و او شد مستقل
entries.english: If it releases its foot from the hand of the earth, the earth will be left dry, and it becomes absolutely free.

entries.farsi: آن كشيدن چيست از گل آب را / جذب تو نقل و شراب ناب را
entries.english: What is that drawing back of the water by the earth? Your drawing the dessert and unmixed wine.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر شهوتى اندر جهان / ‏خواه مال و خواه جان و خواه نان
entries.english: Even so every lust in the world, whether it be riches or power or bread—

entries.farsi: اين خمار غم دليل آن شده ست / ‏كه بد آن مفقود مستى‏ات بده ست
entries.english: Each of these things produces intoxication in you, and when you gain it not, it inflicts a headache upon you.

entries.farsi: هر يكى زينها ترا مستى كند / چون نيابى آن خمارت مى‏زند
entries.english: This headache of grief has become a proof that your intoxication was caused by that missed object.

entries.farsi: جز به اندازه‏ى ضرورت زين مگير / تا نگردد غالب و بر تو امير
entries.english: Do not partake of these but according to the measure of necessity, lest they grow predominant and become rulers over you.

entries.farsi: سر كشيدى تو كه من صاحب دلم / ‏حاجت غيرى ندارم واصلم
entries.english: You scornfully refused, saying, “I am the owner of a heart: I have no need of anyone else, I am united.”

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه آب در گل سر كشد / كه منم آب و چرا جويم مدد
entries.english: That is as though the water in the earth should scornfully refuse, saying, “I am the water, and why should I seek aid?”

entries.farsi: دل تو اين آلوده را پنداشتى / ‏الجرم دل ز اهل دل برداشتى
entries.english: You fancied this polluted was the heart; consequently you averted your heart from those possessed of hearts.

entries.farsi: خود روا دارى كه آن دل باشد اين / ‏كاو بود در عشق شير و انگبين
entries.english: Do you indeed think it possible that this heart which is in love with milk and honey should be that heart?

entries.farsi: لطف شير و انگبين عكس دل است / ‏هر خوشى را آن خوش از دل حاصل است
entries.english: The deliciousness of milk and honey is the reflection of the heart: from that heart the sweetness of every sweet thing is derived.

entries.farsi: پس بود دل جوهر و عالم عرض / ‏سايه‏ى دل چون بود دل را غرض
entries.english: Hence the heart is the substance, and the world is the form: how should the heart’s shadow be the object of the heart’s desire?

entries.farsi: آن دلى كاو عاشق مال است و جاه / ‏يا زبون اين گل و آب سياه
entries.english: Is that the heart that is enamoured of riches and power, or is submissive to this black earth and water,

entries.farsi: يا خيالاتى كه در ظلمات او / مى‏پرستدشان براى گفت‏وگو
entries.english: Or to vain fancies which it worships in the darkness for the sake of fame?

entries.farsi: دل نباشد غير آن درياى نور / دل نظر گاه خدا و آن گاه كور
entries.english: The heart is nothing but the Sea of Light: is the heart the place for vision of God—and then blind?

entries.farsi: نى دل اندر صد هزاران خاص و عام / ‏در يكى باشد كدام است آن كدام
entries.english: The heart is not in hundreds of thousands noble or common; it is in a single one: which is he? Which?

entries.farsi: ريزه‏ى دل را بهل دل را بجو / تا شود آن ريزه چون كوهى از او
entries.english: Leave a fragment of the heart and seek the heart, in order that by means of it that fragment may become as a mountain.

entries.farsi: دل محيط است اندر اين خطه‏ى وجود / زر همى‏افشاند از احسان و جود
entries.english: The heart is encompassing this realm of existence and scattering gold in beneficence and bounty.

entries.farsi: از سالم حق سالمتها نثار / مى‏كند بر اهل عالم ز اختيار
entries.english: It chooses to lavish blessings derived from the Blessedness of God upon the people of the world.

entries.farsi: دامن تو آن نياز است و حضور / هين منه در دامن آن سنگ فجور
entries.english: Whoever’s skirt is right and ready, the largesse of the heart comes to that person.

entries.farsi: هر كه را دامن درست است و معد / آن نثار دل بدان كس مى‏رسد
entries.english: Your skirt is supplication and presence: beware, do not put in your skirt the stone of iniquity,

entries.farsi: تا ندرد دامنت ز آن سنگها / تا بدانى نقد را از رنگها
entries.english: In order that your skirt may not be torn by those stones and that you may distinguish the sterling coin from the colours.

entries.farsi: سنگ پر كردى تو دامن از جهان / ‏هم ز سنگ سيم و زر چون كودكان
entries.english: You have filled your skirt with stones from this world, and also with stones of silver and gold, as children.

entries.farsi: از خيال سيم و زر چون زر نبود / دامن صدقت دريد و غم فزود
entries.english: Inasmuch as from that fancy of silver and gold there was no gold, the skirt of your sincerity was rent and your sorrow increased.

entries.farsi: كى نمايد كودكان را سنگ سنگ / ‏تا نگيرد عقل دامنشان به چنگ
entries.english: How should the stone appear to the children as stone, till Reason lays hold of their skirts?

entries.farsi: پير عقل آمد نه آن موى سپيد / مو نمى‏گنجد در اين بخت و اميد
entries.english: The Pir is Reason, not that white hair: hair is not contained in this fortune and hope.

sections.title_fa: انكار كردن آن جماعت بر دعا و شفاعت دقوقى و پريدن ايشان و ناپيدا شدن در پرده‏ى غيب و حيران شدن دقوقى كه بر هوا رفتند يا بر زمين
sections.title_en: How the company took offence at Daquqi’s invocation and intercession, and flew away and disappeared in the Veil of the Unseen World; and how Daquqi was bewildered
whether they had gone into the air or on the earth.

entries.farsi: چون رهيد آن كشتى و آمد به كام / ‏شد نماز آن جماعت هم تمام
entries.english: “When the ship was saved and attained to their desire, simultaneously the prayer of that company was finished.

entries.farsi: فجفجى افتادشان با همدگر / كاين فضولى كيست از ما اى پدر
entries.english: They began to murmur to one another, saying, ‘O father, which of us is this busybody?’

entries.farsi: هر يكى با آن دگر گفتند سر / از پس پشت دقوقى مستتر
entries.english: Each one spoke in secret to the other, concealed behind Daquqi’s back,

entries.farsi: گفت هر يك من نكردستم كنون / ‏اين دعا نى از برون نى از درون
entries.english: And each one said, ‘I did not make this invocation just now, either externally or internally.’

entries.farsi: گفت مانا كاين امام ما ز درد / بو الفضوالنه مناجاتى بكرد
entries.english: He said, ‘It would seem that this Imam of ours, by grief, has meddled in offering a prayer.’

entries.farsi: گفت آن ديگر كه اى يار يقين / ‏مر مرا هم مى‏نمايد اين چنين
entries.english: Said the other, ‘O you who are familiar with certainty, so it appears to me too.

entries.farsi: او فضولى بوده است از انقباض / ‏كرد بر مختار مطلق اعتراض
entries.english: He has been meddlesome: by distress he has interfered with Him who chooses, the Absolute One.’

entries.farsi: چون نگه كردم سپس تا بنگرم / ‏كه چه مى‏گويند آن اهل كرم
entries.english: When I looked behind to see what those noble ones were saying,

entries.farsi: يك از ايشان را نديدم در مقام / ‏رفته بودند از مقام خود تمام
entries.english: I did not see one of them in their place: they had all gone from their place.

entries.farsi: نى بچپ نى راست نى بالا نه زير / چشم تيز من نشد بر قوم چير
entries.english: Neither on the left nor on the right nor above nor below: my keen eye was unable to find the company.

entries.farsi: درها بودند گويى آب گشت / ‏نى نشان پا و نى گردى به دشت
entries.english: You might say that they were pearls had become water: there was neither footprint nor any dust in the desert.

entries.farsi: در قباب حق شدند آن دم همه / ‏در كدامين روضه رفتند آن رمه
entries.english: At that moment they all entered into the tents of God: into what garden had the troop gone?

entries.farsi: آن چنان پنهان شدند از چشم او / مثل غوطه‏ى ماهيان در آب جو
entries.english: I remained in amazement as to how God caused this company to be concealed from mine eye.”

entries.farsi: در تحير ماندم كاين قوم را / چون بپوشانيد حق بر چشم ما
entries.english: In such wise did they vanish from his eye, like the plunge of fishes into the water of a stream.

entries.farsi: سالها در حسرت ايشان بماند / عمرها در شوق ايشان اشك راند
entries.english: During Years he continued to grieve for them; during ages he shed tears in longing for them.

entries.farsi: تو بگويى مرد حق اندر نظر / كى در آرد با خدا ذكر بشر
entries.english: You may say, “How should a man of God bring into view the thought of human beings beside God?”

entries.farsi: خر از اين مى‏خسبد اين جا اى فالن / ‏كه بشر ديدى تو ايشان را نه جان
entries.english: You are in a hole here, O so-and-so, because you have regarded them as flesh, not as spirit.

entries.farsi: كار از اين ويران شده ست اى مرد خام / ‏كه بشر ديدى مر اينها را چو عام
entries.english: You have come to ruin, O foolish man, because like the vulgar you regarded them for these human beings.

entries.farsi: تو همان ديدى كه ابليس لعين / ‏گفت من از آتشم آدم ز طين
entries.english: You have regarded in the same way as the accursed Iblis: he said, “I am of fire, Adam is of earth.”

entries.farsi: چشم ابليسانه را يك دم ببند / چند بينى صورت آخر چند چند
entries.english: Bandage your satanic eye for one moment: how long, pray, will you regard the form? How long, how long?

entries.farsi: اى دقوقى با دو چشم همچو جو / هين مبر اوميد ايشان را بجو
entries.english: O Daquqi with streaming eyes, come, do not abandon hope: seek them!

entries.farsi: هين بجو كه ركن دولت جستن است / ‏هر گشادى در دل اندر بستن است
entries.english: Come; seek, for search is the pillar of fortune: every success consists in fixing the heart.

entries.farsi: از همه‏ى كار جهان پرداخته / ‏كو و كو مى‏گو به جان چون فاخته
entries.english: Unconcerned with all the business of the world, keep saying with your soul ku, ku, like the dove.

entries.farsi: نيك بنگر اندر اين اى محتجب / ‏كه دعا را بست حق بر أستجب
entries.english: Consider this well, O you who are veiled, that God has tied “invocation” to “I will answer.”

entries.farsi: هر كه را دل پاك شد از اعتالل / ‏آن دعايش مى‏رود تا ذو الجلال
entries.english: Whoever’s heart is purged of infirmity; his invocation will go unto the Lord of glory.

sections.title_fa: باز شرح كردن حكايت آن طالب روزى حالل بى‏كسب و رنج در عهد داود عليه السلام و مستجاب شدن دعاى او
sections.title_en: Explaining further the story of him who in the time of David, on whom be peace, sought to receive lawful means of livelihood without working or taking trouble,
and how his prayer was answered favourably.

entries.farsi: يادم آمد آن حكايت كان فقير / روز و شب مى‏كرد افغان و نفير
entries.english: The story has come into my mind how that poor man used to moan and lament day and night,

entries.farsi: وز خدا مى‏خواست روزى حالل / ‏بى‏شكار و رنج و كسب و انتقال
entries.english: And beg of God a lawful means of livelihood without pursuit and trouble and work and movement.

entries.farsi: هم بگوييمش كجا خواهد گريخت / ‏چون ز ابر فضل حق حكمت بريخت
entries.english: We have formerly related a part of what happened to him, but hin­ drance intervened and became fivefold.

entries.farsi: پيش از اين گفتيم بعضى حال او / ليك تعويق آمد و شد پنج تو
entries.english: Too we shall tell it. Where will it flee, since wisdom has poured from the clouds of God’s bounty?

entries.farsi: صاحب گاوش بديد و گفت هين / ‏اى به ظلمت گاو من گشته رهين
entries.english: The owner of the cow espied him and said “Hey, O you to whose unrighteousness my cow has fallen a prey,

entries.farsi: هين چرا كشتى بگو گاو مرا / ابله طرار انصاف اندر آ
entries.english: Hey, tell why did you kill my cow? Fool! Pickpocket! Deal fairly.”

entries.farsi: آن دعاى كهنه‏ام شد مستجاب / ‏روزى من بود كشتم نك جواب
entries.english: He said, “I was begging God for daily bread and preparing a qibla of supplication.

entries.farsi: گفت من روزى ز حق مى‏خواستم / ‏قبله را از البه مى‏آراستم
entries.english: That ancient prayer of mine was answered. She was my portion of daily bread: I killed her. Behold the answer!”

entries.farsi: او ز خشم آمد گريبانش گرفت / ‏چند مشتى زد به رويش ناشكفت
entries.english: He came angrily and seized his collar; having lost patience, he struck him in the face with his fist several times.

sections.title_fa: ‏رفتن هر دو خصم نزد داود پيغامبر عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How both the adversaries went to David, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: اين چه مى‏گويى دعا چه بود مخند / بر سر و ريش من و خويش اى لوند
entries.english: He led him to the Prophet David, saying, “Come, O you crazy fool and criminal!

entries.farsi: حجت بارد رها كن اى دغا / عقل در تن آور و با خويش آ
entries.english: Drop silly argument, O impostor; let intelligence into your body and come to your senses!

entries.farsi: مى‏كشيدش تا به داود نبى / كه بيا اى ظالم گيج غبى
entries.english: What is this that you are saying? What is the prayer? Do not laugh at my head and beard and your own, O scoundrel!”

entries.farsi: گفت من با حق دعاها كرده‏ام / ‏اندر اين البه بسى خون خورده‏ام
entries.english: He said, “I have offered prayers to God, I have borne much toil and pain in this supplication.

entries.farsi: من يقين دارم دعا شد مستجاب / ‏سر بزن بر سنگ اى منكر خطاب
entries.english: I possess the certainty the prayer has been answered. Dash your head against the stones, O foul-spoken one!”

entries.farsi: گفت گرد آييد هين يا مسلمين / ‏ژاژ بينيد و فشار اين مهين
entries.english: He cried, “Hey, gather round, O Moslems! Behold the drivel and raving of this imbecile!

entries.farsi: اى مسلمانان دعا مال مرا / چون از آن او كند بهر خدا
entries.english: O Moslems, for God’s sake, how should prayer make my property belong to him?

entries.farsi: گر چنين بودى همه عالم بدين / ‏يك دعا امالك بردندى به كين
entries.english: If it were so, by means of a single prayer of this kind the whole world would carry off possessions by force.

entries.farsi: روز و شب اندر دعايند و ثنا / البه‏گويان كه تومان ده اى خدا
entries.english: If it were so, the blind beggars would have become grandees and princes;

entries.farsi: گر چنين بودى گدايان ضرير / محتشم گشته بدندى و امير
entries.english: They are day and night in invocation and praise, uttering entreaties and crying, ‘O God, give unto us!

entries.farsi: تا تو ندهى هيچ كس ندهد يقين / ‏اى گشاينده تو بگشا بند اين
entries.english: Unless You give, assuredly no one will give: O Opener, do You open the lock of this!’

entries.farsi: مكسب كوران بود البه و دعا / جز لب نانى نيابند از عطا
entries.english: Supplication and prayer is the means whereby the blind earn their livelihood, they get no gift but a crust of bread.”

entries.farsi: خلق گفتند اين مسلمان راست گوست / ‏وين فروشنده‏ى دعاها ظلم جوست
entries.english: The people said, “This Moslem speaks the truth, and this prayer-monger is one who seeks to act unjustly.

entries.farsi: اين دعا كى باشد از اسباب ملك / ‏كى كشيد اين را شريعت خود به سلك
entries.english: How should this prayer be a means of acquiring property? When forsooth did the religious Law enter this on the roll?

entries.farsi: بيع و بخشش يا وصيت يا عطا / يا ز جنس اين شود ملكى ترا
entries.english: A thing becomes your property by sale and donation or by bequest and gift or by some means of this kind.

entries.farsi: در كدامين دفتر است اين شرع نو / گاو را تو باز ده يا حبس رو
entries.english: In what book is this new statute? Give back the cow or go to prison!”

entries.farsi: او به سوى آسمان مى‏كرد رو / واقعه‏ى ما را نداند غير تو
entries.english: He was turning his face to Heaven, “No one knows my experience save You.

entries.farsi: در دل من آن دعا انداختى / ‏صد اميد اندر دلم افراختى
entries.english: You put that prayer into my heart, You raised dreams.”

entries.farsi: من نمى‏كردم گزافه آن دعا / همچو يوسف ديده بودم خوابها
entries.english: Not idly was I uttering that prayer: like Joseph, I had dreamed dreams.”

entries.farsi: ديد يوسف آفتاب و اختران / ‏پيش او سجده كنان چون چاكران
entries.english: Joseph saw the sun and the stars bowing low before him, like servants.

entries.farsi: اعتمادش بود بر خواب درست / ‏در چه و زندان جز آن را مى‏نجست
entries.english: He relied upon the true dream: in the dungeon and prison he sought nothing but that.

entries.farsi: ز اعتماد آن نبودش هيچ غم / ‏از غالمى و ز مالم و بيش و كم
entries.english: Because of his reliance upon that, he cared nothing for servitude or reproach or more or less.

entries.farsi: اعتمادى داشت او بر خواب خويش / ‏كه چو شمعى مى‏فروزيدش ز پيش
entries.english: He relied upon his dream which was shining in front of him like a candle.

entries.farsi: چون در افكندند يوسف را به چاه / ‏بانگ آمد سمع او را از اله
entries.english: When they cast Joseph into the well, there came to his ear a cry from God—

entries.farsi: كه تو روزى شه شوى اى پهلوان / ‏تا بمالى اين جفا در رويشان
entries.english: “O paladin, one day you will become king, so that you may rub this wrong upon their faces.”

entries.farsi: قايل اين بانگ نايد در نظر / ليك دل بشناخت قايل را ز اثر
entries.english: He who utters this cry is not visible, but the heart recognized the Speaker from the effect

entries.farsi: قوتى و راحتى و مسندى / ‏در ميان جان فتادش ز آن ندى
entries.english: From that allocution a strength and peace and support fell into the midst of his soul.

entries.farsi: چاه شد بر وى بدان بانگ جليل / ‏گلشن و بزمى چو آتش بر خليل
entries.english: Through that majestic cry the well became to him a rose-garden and banquet, as the fire to Abraham.

entries.farsi: هر جفا كه بعد از آتش مى‏رسيد / او بدان قوت به شادى مى‏كشيد
entries.english: By means of that strength he cheerfully endured every affliction that came to him afterwards—

entries.farsi: ُهمچنان كه ذوق آن بانگ أَ لَسْ ت / ‏در دل هر مومنى تا حشر هست
entries.english: Even as the delicious savour of the cry Am not I (your Lord)? subsists in the heart of every true believer till the Resurrection,

entries.farsi: تا نباشد بر بالشان اعتراض / ‏نى ز امر و نهى حقشان انقباض
entries.english: So that they do not rebel in tribulation or shrink from the commands and prohibitions of God.

entries.farsi: لقمه‏ى حكمى كه تلخى مى‏نهد / گل شكر آن را گوارش مى‏دهد
entries.english: The rose-jam digests the morsel, that is, the decree, which bestows bitterness;

entries.farsi: گل شكر آن را كه نبود مستند / لقمه را ز انكار او قى مى‏كند
entries.english: He that does not rely upon the rose-jam vomits the morsel in disgust.

entries.farsi: ُهر كه خوابى ديد از روز أَ لَسْ ت / ‏مست باشد در ره طاعات مست
entries.english: Anyone who has dreamed of the Day of Alast is drunk in the path of devotional works, drunken:

entries.farsi: مى‏كشد چون اشتر مست اين جوال / ‏بى‏فتور و بى‏گمان و بى‏مالل
entries.english: Like a drunken camel, he is bearing this sack without flagging and without questioning and without fatigue;

entries.farsi: كفك تصديقش به گرد پوز او / شد گواه مستى و دل سوز او
entries.english: The froth round his muzzle, namely, his confession of faith, has become a witness to his intoxication and heart-burning.

entries.farsi: اشتر از قوت چو شير نر شده / ‏زير ثقل بار اندك خور شده
entries.english: Through the strength the camel becomes like a fierce lion; beneath the heavy burden he eats little.

entries.farsi: ز آرزوى ناقه صد فاقه بر او / مى‏نمايد كوه پيشش تار مو
entries.english: In longing for the she-camel a hundred starvations on him; the mountain seems to him a strand of hair.

entries.farsi: در أَ لَسْ تُ آن كاو چنين خوابى نديد / اندر اين دنيا نشد بنده و مريد
entries.english: He who has not dreamed such a dream in Alast does not become a servant and seeker in this world;

entries.farsi: ور بشد اندر تردد صد دله / ‏يك زمان شكر استش و سالى گله
entries.english: Or if he does become, always changing and shifting in vacillation: he gives thanks for one moment and utters complaints for a Year.

entries.farsi: پاى پيش و پاى پس در راه دين / ‏مى‏نهد با صد تردد بى‏يقين
entries.english: He steps forward and backwards in the Way of the Religion with a hundred vacillations and without certainty.

entries.farsi: وام دار شرح اينم نك گرو / ور شتاب استت ز أَ لَمْ نَشْ رَحْ شنو
entries.english: I owe the exposition of this. Lo, the pledge; and if you are in haste, hear from Have not We opened….?

entries.farsi: چون ندارد شرح اين معنى كران / ‏خر به سوى مدعى گاو ران
entries.english: Since the explanation of this subject has no end, proceed to the litigant of the cow.

entries.farsi: گفت كورم خواند زين جرم آن دغا / بس بليسانه قياس است اى خدا
entries.english: He said, “The impostor has called me blind because of this crime: O God, ‘it is a very satanic inference.

entries.farsi: كور از خلقان طمع دارد ز جهل / ‏من ز تو كز تست هر دشوار سهل
entries.english: When have I been praying in the fashion of the blind? When have I begged of anyone except the Creator?

entries.farsi: من دعا كورانه كى مى‏كرده‏ام / ‏جز به خالق كديه كى آورده‏ام
entries.english: The blind man in his ignorance has hope of creatures; I of You, by whom every difficult thing is easy.

entries.farsi: آن يكى كورم ز كوران بشمريد / او نياز جان و اخالصم نديد
entries.english: That blind fellow has reckoned me amongst the blind: he has not seen my soul’s humble supplication and my entire devotion.

entries.farsi: كورى عشق است اين كورى من / ‏حب يعمى و يصم است اى حسن
entries.english: This blindness of mine is the blindness of love: it is Love makes one blind and deaf, O Hasan.

entries.farsi: كورم از غير خدا بينا بدو / مقتضاى عشق اين باشد بگو
entries.english: I am blind to aught other than God; I am seeing by Him: this is what Love demands, it is well. Say.

entries.farsi: تو كه بينايى ز كورانم مدار / دايرم بر گرد لطفت اى مدار
entries.english: Do not You, who are seeing, deem me to be one of the blind: I am revolving round Your grace, O Axis.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه يوسف صديق را / خواب بنمودى و گشتش متكا
entries.english: Just as You did show a dream to the veracious Joseph, and it became a support to him,

entries.farsi: مر مرا لطف تو هم خوابى نمود / آن دعاى بى‏حدم بازى نبود
entries.english: To me too Your grace showed a dream: that endless prayer of mine was not an idle play.

entries.farsi: مى‏نداند خلق اسرار مرا / ژاژ مى‏دانند گفتار مرا
entries.english: Creatures do not understand my hidden thoughts and they regard my words as drivel.

entries.farsi: حقشان است و كه داند راز غيب / ‏غير عالم سر و ستار عيب
entries.english: They have the right, for who knows the mystery of the Unseen save the Knower of secrets and the Coverer of faults?”

entries.farsi: خصم گفتش رو به من كن حق بگو / رو چه سوى آسمان كردى عمو
entries.english: His adversary said to him, “Turn your face to me! Tell the truth! Why have you turned your face towards Heaven, uncle?

entries.farsi: شيد مى‏آرى غلط مى‏افگنى / ‏الف عشق و الف قربت مى‏زنى
entries.english: You are employing fraud, you are casting error: you are prating of love and nearness.

entries.farsi: با كدامين روى چون دل مرده‏اى / ‏روى سوى آسمانها كرده‏اى
entries.english: Inasmuch as you are spiritually dead, with what face have you turned your face towards the heavens?”

entries.farsi: غلغلى در شهر افتاده از اين / ‏آن مسلمان مى‏نهد رو بر زمين
entries.english: An uproar arose on this account in the city, that Moslem laid his face on the ground.

entries.farsi: تو همى‏دانى و شبهاى دراز / كه همى‏خواندم تو را با صد نياز
entries.english: Crying, “O God, do not put this servant to shame: if I am wicked, yet do not divulge my secret.

entries.farsi: كاى خدا اين بنده را رسوا مكن / ‏گر بدم هم سر من پيدا مكن
entries.english: You know, and the long nights during which I was calling unto You with a hundred supplications.

entries.farsi: پيش خلق اين را اگر خود قدر نيست / ‏پيش تو همچون چراغ روشنى است
entries.english: Albeit this has no worth in the sight of the people, in Your sight it is like a shining lamp.”

sections.title_fa: ‏شنيدن داود عليه السلام سخن هر دو خصم و سؤال كردن از مدعى عليه
sections.title_en: How David, on whom be peace, heard what both the litigants had to say, and interrogated the defendant

entries.farsi: چون كه داود نبى آمد برون / ‏گفت هين چون است اين احوال چون
entries.english: When the prophet David came forth, he said, “Hey, what is this about? What is it?”

entries.farsi: كشت گاوم را بپرسش كه چرا / گاو من كشت او بيان كن ماجرا
entries.english: The plaintiff said, “O prophet of God, justice! My cow strayed into his house.

entries.farsi: مدعى گفت اى نبى اللهَّ داد / گاو من در خانه‏ى او در فتاد
entries.english: He killed my cow. Ask him why he killed my cow, and explain what happened.”

entries.farsi: گفت داودش بگو اى بو الكرم / ‏چون تلف كردى تو ملك محترم
entries.english: David said to him, “Speak, O noble one! How did you destroy the property of this honourable person?

entries.farsi: هين پراكنده مگو حجت بيار / تا به يك سو گردد اين دعوى و كار
entries.english: Take care! Do not talk incoherently, bring forward plea, in order that this claim and cause may be laid aside.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى داود بودم هفت سال / ‏روز و شب اندر دعا و در سؤال
entries.english: He said, “O David, for seven Years I was day and night in invocation and entreaty.

entries.farsi: اين همى‏جستم ز يزدان كاى خدا / روزيى خواهم حالل و بى‏عنا
entries.english: This I was seeking from God: ‘O God, I want a means of livelihood lawful and without trouble.’

entries.farsi: مرد و زن بر ناله‏ى من واقف‏اند / كودكان اين ماجرا را واصف‏اند
entries.english: Men and women are acquainted with my lamentation; the children describe this happening.

entries.farsi: تو بپرس از هر كه خواهى اين خبر / تا بگويد بى‏شكنجه بى‏ضرر
entries.english: Ask whomsoever you will for information about this, so that he may tell without torture and without harm.

entries.farsi: هم هويدا پرس و هم پنهان ز خلق / ‏كه چه مى‏گفت اين گداى ژنده دلق
entries.english: Inquire of the people both and secretly what this beggar with the tattered cloak used to say.

entries.farsi: بعد اين جمله‏ى دعا و اين فغان / ‏گاوى اندر خانه ديدم ناگهان
entries.english: After all this invocation and outcry, suddenly I saw a cow in my house.

entries.farsi: كشتم آن را تا دهم در شكر آن / ‏كه دعاى من شنود آن غيب دان
entries.english: My eye became dim, not on account of the food, for joy that the supplication had been accepted.

entries.farsi: چشم من تاريك شد نى بهر لوت / ‏شادى آن كه قبول آمد قنوت
entries.english: I killed her that I might give (alms) in thankfulness that He who knows things unseen had hearkened to my prayer.”

sections.title_fa: حكم كردن داود عليه السلام بر كشنده‏ى گاو
sections.title_en: How David, on whom be peace, gave judgment against the slayer of the cow.

entries.farsi: گفت داود اين سخنها را بشو / حجت شرعى در اين دعوى بگو
entries.english: David said, “Wipe out these words and declare a legal plea in this dispute.

entries.farsi: تو روا دارى كه من بى‏حجتى / ‏بنهم اندر شهر باطل سنتى
entries.english: Do you deem it allowable that, without any plea, I should establish a wrong ordinance in the city?

entries.farsi: اين كه بخشيدت خريدى وارثى / ‏ريع را چون مى‏ستانى حارثى
entries.english: Who gave you this? Did you buy or inherit her? How will you take the crop? Are you the farmer?

entries.farsi: كانچه كارى بدروى آن آن تست / ‏ور نه اين بى‏داد بر تو شد درست
entries.english: Know, uncle that the acquisition is like agriculture: unless you sow, the produce does not belong to you;

entries.farsi: كسب را همچون زراعت دان عمو / تا نكارى دخل نبود ز آن تو
entries.english: For you reap what you sow: that is yours. Otherwise, this act of injustice is proved against you.

entries.farsi: رو بده مال مسلمان كژ مگو / رو بجو وام و بده باطل مجو
entries.english: Go, pay the Moslem’s money, and don’t speak falsely. Go, try to borrow, and pay, and don’t seek wrong.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه تو همين مى‏گويى‏ام / ‏كه همى‏گويند اصحاب ستم
entries.english: “O King,” he replied, “you are saying to me the same thing as the oppressors say.”

sections.title_fa: ‏تضرع آن شخص از داورى داود عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How that person earnestly appealed against the judgment of David, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: سجده كرد و گفت كاى داناى سوز / در دل داود انداز آن فروز
entries.english: He prostrated himself and said, “O You who knows ardour, cast that flame into the heart of David!

entries.farsi: در دلش نه آن چه تو اندر دلم / ‏اندر افكندى به راز اى مفضلم
entries.english: Put in his heart that which You have secretly let fall into mine, O my Benefactor!”

entries.farsi: اين بگفت و گريه در شد هاى هاى / ‏تا دل داود بيرون شد ز جاى
entries.english: He said this and began to weep with loud cries of lamentation, so that David’s heart was moved exceedingly’.

entries.farsi: گفت هين امروز اى خواهان گاو / مهلتم ده وين دعاوى را مكاو
entries.english: “Listen,” said he, “O demander of the cow, give me a respite to-day and do not search into these matters of dispute,

entries.farsi: تا روم من سوى خلوت در نماز / پرسم اين احوال از داناى راز
entries.english: So that I may go to a solitary place and ask the Knower of mysteries about these matters, in prayer.

entries.farsi: خوى دارم در نماز اين التفات / ‏معنى قرة عيني في الصالت
entries.english: During prayer I am accustomed to turn thus: the meaning of, ‘the delight I feel in the ritual prayer’.

entries.farsi: روزن جانم گشاده ست از صفا / مى‏رسد بى‏واسطه نامه‏ى خدا
entries.english: The window of my soul is opened, and from the purity the Book of God comes without intermediary.

entries.farsi: نامه و باران و نور از روزنم / ‏مى‏فتد در خانه‏ام از معدنم
entries.english: The Book and the rain and the Light are falling through my window into my house from my source.’’

entries.farsi: دوزخ است آن خانه كان بى‏روزن است / ‏اصل دين اى بنده روزن كردن است
entries.english: The house that is without a window is Hell: to make a window, O servant, is the foundation of the Religion.

entries.farsi: تيشه‏ى هر بيشه‏اى كم زن بيا / تيشه زن در كندن روزن هال
entries.english: Do not ply the axe on every thicket: oh, come and ply the axe in excavating a window.

entries.farsi: يا نمى‏دانى كه نور آفتاب / ‏عكس خورشيد برون است از حجاب
entries.english: Or do not you know that the light of the sun is the reflection of the Sun beyond the veil?

entries.farsi: نور اين دانى كه حيوان ديد هم / ‏پس چه كَرَّمْنا بود بر آدمم
entries.english: You know that the animals have seen the light of this: what, then, is “I bestowed hounour on My Adam?”

entries.farsi: من چو خورشيدم درون نور غرق / ‏مى‏ندانم كرد خويش از نور فرق
entries.english: “I am plunged in the Light, like the sun; I cannot distinguish myself from the Light

entries.farsi: رفتنم سوى نماز و آن خال / بهر تعليم است ره مر خلق را
entries.english: My going to prayer and to that solitude is for the purpose of teaching the people the Way.

entries.farsi: كژ نهم تا راست گردد اين جهان / ‏حرب و خدعه اين بود اى پهلوان
entries.english: I put crooked in order that this world may become straight”: this is “War is deceit,” O brave knight.

entries.farsi: نيست دستورى و گر نه ريختى گرد از / ‏درياى راز انگيختى
entries.english: There is not permission; otherwise, he would have poured out and would have raised dust from the sea of the mystery.

entries.farsi: همچنين مى‏گفت داود اين نسق / ‏خواست گشتن عقل خلقان محترق
entries.english: David went on speaking in this tenor; the understanding of the people was on the point of being burned up.

entries.farsi: پس گريبانش كشيد از پس يكى / ‏كه ندارم در يكى‏اش من شكى
entries.english: Then someone pulled his collar from behind, saying, “I have not any doubt as to His unity.”

entries.farsi: با خود آمد گفت را كوتاه كرد / لب ببست و عزم خلوتگاه كرد
entries.english: He came to himself, cut short his discourse, closed his lips, and set out for the place where he was alone.

sections.title_fa: در خلوت رفتن داود تا آن چه حق است پيدا شود
sections.title_en: How David went into seclusion in order that the truth might be made manifest.

entries.farsi: در فرو بست و برفت آن گه شتاب / ‏سوى محراب و دعاى مستجاب
entries.english: He shut the door, and then went quickly to the prayer-niche and the invocation that is answered.

entries.farsi: حق نمودش آن چه بنمودش تمام / ‏گشت واقف بر سزاى انتقام
entries.english: God revealed the entire matter to him and he became aware of him that was deserving of punishment.

entries.farsi: روز ديگر جمله خصمان آمدند / پيش داود پيمبر صف زدند
entries.english: Next day all the litigants came and formed ranks before David.

entries.farsi: همچنان آن ماجراها باز رفت / ‏زود زد آن مدعى تشنيع زفت
entries.english: Thus the questions in dispute came up again: the plaintiff at once uttered violent reproaches.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكم كردن داود بر صاحب گاو كه از سر گاو برخيز و تشنيع صاحب گاو بر داود عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How David gave judgment against the owner of the cow, bidding him withdraw from the case concerning the cow; and how the owner of the cow reproached David, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: گفت داودش خمش كن رو بهل / ‏اين مسلمان را ز گاوت كن بحل
entries.english: David said to him, “Be silent! Go, abandon, and acquit this Moslem of your cow.

entries.farsi: چون خدا پوشيد بر تو اى جوان / ‏رو خمش كن حق ستارى بدان
entries.english: Inasmuch as God has thrown a veil over you, O youth, depart and keep silence and acknowledge the obligation of concealment.”

entries.farsi: گفت وا ويال چه حكم است اين چه داد / از پى من شرع نو خواهى نهاد
entries.english: He cried, “Oh, woe is me! What judgment is this, what justice? Will you establish a new law on my account?

entries.farsi: بر سگان كور اين استم نرفت / ‏زين تعدى سنگ و كه بشكافت تفت
entries.english: The fame of your justice has gone so far that earth and heaven have become fragrant.

entries.farsi: رفته است آوازه‏ى عدلت چنان / ‏كه معطر شد زمين و آسمان
entries.english: This wrong has never been done to blind dogs; rock and mountain are burst asunder of a sudden by this iniquity.”

entries.farsi: همچنين تشنيع مى‏زد بر مال / كالصال هنگام ظلم است الصال
entries.english: In such fashion was he uttering reproaches publicly, crying, “Listen, it is the time of injustice, Listen!”

sections.title_fa: ‏حكم كردن داود بر صاحب گاو كه جمله‏ى مال خود را به وى ده
sections.title_en: How David pronounced sentence against the owner of the cow, saying, “Give him the whole of your property.”

entries.farsi: بعد از آن داود گفتش كاى عنود / جمله مال خويش او را بخش زود
entries.english: After that, David said to him, “O rebellious man, give the whole of your wealth to him immediately;

entries.farsi: ور نه كارت سخت گردد گفتمت / ‏تا نگردد ظاهر از وى استمت
entries.english: Otherwise, your plight will become grievous; I tell you in order that your crime may not be made manifest through him.”

entries.farsi: خاك بر سر كرد و جامه بر دريد / كه به هر دم مى‏كنى ظلمى مزيد
entries.english: He put dust on his head and tore his raiment, crying, “At every instant you are adding an injury.”

entries.farsi: يك دمى ديگر بر اين تشنيع راند / باز داودش به پيش خويش خواند
entries.english: Once more he went on in this reproach; then David called him to his presence,

entries.farsi: گفت چون بختت نبود اى بخت كور / ظلمت آمد اندك اندك در ظهور
entries.english: And said, “Since it was not your fortune, O you whose fortune is blind, little by little your wickedness has come to light.

entries.farsi: ريده‏اى آن گاه صدر و پيشگاه / ‏اى دريغ از چون تو خر خاشاك و كاه
entries.english: Cacavisti, then the high seat and the place of honour. Oh, may sticks and straw be withheld from such an ass as you are!

entries.farsi: رو كه فرزندان تو با جفت تو / بندگان او شدند افزون مگو
entries.english: Go! Your children and your wife have become his slaves. Say no more!”

entries.farsi: سنگ بر سينه همى‏زد با دو دست / ‏مى‏دويد از جهل خود بالا و پست
entries.english: He was dashing stones against his breast with both hands and running up and down in his folly.

entries.farsi: خلق هم اندر مالمت آمدند / كز ضمير كار او غافل بدند
entries.english: The people too began to blame, for they were unaware of the hidden of his action.

entries.farsi: ظالم از مظلوم كى داند كسى / كاو بود سخره‏ى هوا همچون خسى
entries.english: How should one that is subject, like a straw, to sensuality know the oppressor from the oppressed?

entries.farsi: ظالم از مظلوم آن كس پى برد / كاو سر نفس ظلوم خود برد
entries.english: He that cuts off the head of his wicked self— he finds the way to the oppressor from the oppressed.

entries.farsi: ور نه آن ظالم كه نفس است از درون / ‏خصم مظلومان بود او از جنون
entries.english: Otherwise, that oppressor, which is the fleshly soul within, by frenzy, is the adversary of every oppressed person.

entries.farsi: سگ هماره حمله بر مسكين كند / تا تواند زخم بر مسكين زند
entries.english: A dog always attacks the poor; so far as it can, it inflicts wounds upon the poor.

entries.farsi: شرم شيران راست نى سگ را بدان / ‏كه نگيرد صيد از همسايگان
entries.english: Know that lions feel shame, not dogs, because he does not prey on his neighbours.

entries.farsi: عامه‏ى مظلوم كش ظالم پرست / از كمين سگسان سوى داود جست
entries.english: The mob, which slays the oppressed and worships the oppressor— their dog sprang forth from ambush towards David.

entries.farsi: روى در داود كردند آن فريق / ‏كاى نبى مجتبى بر ما شفيق
entries.english: That party turned their faces to David, saying, “O chosen prophet, who have compassion on us,

entries.farsi: اين نشايد از تو كاين ظلمى است فاش / ‏قهر كردى بى‏گناهى را به الش
entries.english: This is unworthy of you, for this is a manifest injustice: you have abased an innocent man for nothing.”

sections.title_fa: عزم كردن داود عليه السلام به خواندن خلق بدان صحرا كه راز آشكارا كند و حجتها همه قطع كند
sections.title_en: How David, on whom be peace, resolved to summon the people to a certain plain, in older that he might disclose the mystery and make an end of all arguments

entries.farsi: جمله برخيزيد تا بيرون رويم / ‏تا بر آن سر نهان واقف شويم
entries.english: He said, “O friends, the time has come that his hidden secret should he displayed.

entries.farsi: گفت اى ياران زمان آن رسيد / كان سر مكتوم او گردد پديد
entries.english: Arise, all, that we may go forth, so that we may become acquainted with that hidden secret.

entries.farsi: در فالن صحرا درختى هست زفت / ‏شاخهايش انبه و بسيار و چفت
entries.english: In such and such a plain there is a huge tree, its boughs dense and numerous and curved.

entries.farsi: سخت راسخ خيمه گاه و ميخ او / بوى خون مى‏آيدم از بيخ او
entries.english: Its tent and tent-pegs are very firm; from its roots the smell of blood is coming to me.

entries.farsi: خون شده ست اندر بن آن خوش درخت / ‏خواجه را كشته ست اين منحوس بخت
entries.english: Murder has been done at the bottom of that goodly tree: this man of sinister fortune has killed his master.

entries.farsi: تا كنون حلم خدا پوشيد آن / ‏آخر از ناشكرى آن قلتبان
entries.english: The clemency, of God has concealed that till now, at last; through the ingratitude of that scoundrel,

entries.farsi: كه عيال خواجه را روزى نديد / نى به نوروز و نه موسمهاى عيد
entries.english: Who never a single day looked upon his master’s family, not at Nawroz and seasonal festivals,

entries.farsi: بى‏نوايان را به يك لقمه نجست / ‏ياد ناورد او ز حقهاى نخست
entries.english: And never searched after the destitute with a single morsel of food, or bethought him of the former obligations,

entries.farsi: تا كنون از بهر يك گاو اين لعين / ‏مى‏زند فرزند او را بر زمين
entries.english: Untill for the sake of a cow this accursed wretch is now felling his son to the earth.

entries.farsi: او به خود برداشت پرده از گناه / ‏ور نه مى‏پوشيد جرمش را اله
entries.english: He, of himself, has lifted the veil from his crime; else God would have concealed his sin.”

entries.farsi: كافر و فاسق در اين دور گزند / پرده‏ى خود را به خود بر مى‏درند
entries.english: In this cycle of woe the infidel and the profligate rend their veils of their own accord.

entries.farsi: ظلم مستور است در اسرار جان / ‏مى‏نهد ظالم به پيش مردمان
entries.english: Wrong is covered in the inmost thoughts of the soul: the wrong-doer exposes it to men,

entries.farsi: كه ببينيدم كه دارم شاخها / گاو دوزخ را ببينيد از مال
entries.english: Saying, “Behold me! I have horns! Behold the cow of Hell in full view!”

sections.title_fa: گواهى دادن دست و پا و زبان بر سر ظالم هم در دنيا
sections.title_en: How hands and feet and tongue give evidence concerning the secret of the wicked,

entries.farsi: پس هم اينجا دست و پايت در گزند / بر ضمير تو گواهى مى‏دهند
entries.english: even in this world.

entries.farsi: چون موكل مى‏شود بر تو ضمير / كه بگو تو اعتقادت وامگير
