entries.english: Even here, then, your hands and feet, in harm, bear witness to your conscience.

entries.farsi: خاصه در هنگام خشم و گفت‏وگو / مى‏كند ظاهر سرت را مو به مو
entries.english: Since your conscience becomes an overseer to you and says, “Speak! Do not keep back your belief,”

entries.farsi: چون موكل مى‏شود ظلم و جفا / كه هويدا كن مرا اى دست و پا
entries.english: And, especially at times of anger and quarrelling, makes manifest your secret thought, hair by hair;

entries.farsi: چون همى‏گيرد گواه سر لگام / ‏خاصه وقت جوش و خشم و انتقام
entries.english: Since wrong and injustice become your overseer and say, “Display me, O hands and feet,”

entries.farsi: پس همان كس كاين موكل مى‏كند / تا لواى راز بر صحرا زند
entries.english: And since the witness to the secret thought seizes the reins— in particular at times of emotion and anger and revenge—

entries.farsi: اى به ده دست آمده در ظلم و كين / ‏گوهرت پيداست حاجت نيست اين
entries.english: That One, then, who appoints this as overseer, that it may unfurl the banner of the secret on the field—

entries.farsi: پس موكلهاى ديگر روز حشر / هم تواند آفريد از بهر نشر
entries.english: Then, He can also create, on the Day of Judgment, other overseers for the purpose of unfolding.

entries.farsi: نيست حاجت شهره گشتن در گزند / بر ضمير آتشينت واقفند
entries.english: O you who have entered most recklessly upon injustice and malice, your true nature is evident: this is not needed.

entries.farsi: جزو نارم سوى كل خود روم / ‏من نه نورم كه سوى حضرت شوم
entries.english: It is not necessary to become celebrated for harm: they are acquainted with your fiery conscience.

entries.farsi: نفس تو هر دم بر آرد صد شرار / كه ببينيدم منم ز اصحاب نار
entries.english: Your fleshly soul every moment emits a hundred sparks, saying, “Behold me! I am of the people of the Fire.

entries.farsi: او از او صد گاو برد و صد شتر / نفس اين است اى پدر از وى ببر
entries.english: I am a part of the Fire: I go to my whole; I am not light, so that I should go to the Lord”—

entries.farsi: همچنان كاين ظالم حق ناشناس / ‏بهر گاوى كرد چندين التباس
entries.english: Even as this unjust and ungrateful man wrought so much confusion for the sake of a cow.

entries.farsi: نيز روزى با خدا زارى نكرد / يا ربى نامد از او روزى به درد
entries.english: He carried off from him a hundred cows and a hundred camels: this is the fleshly soul: O father, cut yourself asunder from it.

entries.farsi: كاى خدا خصم مرا خشنود كن / ‏گر منش كردم زيان تو سود كن
entries.english: Besides, never once did he make humble supplication to God: never once did a cry of “O Lord!” come from him in sorrow—

entries.farsi: گر خطا كشتم ديت بر عاقله است / ‏ُعاقله‏ى جانم تو بودى از أَ لَسْ ت
entries.english: “O God, content my adversary: if I have inflicted loss upon him do You bestow profit!

entries.farsi: سنگ مى‏ندهد به استغفار در / اين بود انصاف نفس اى جان حر
entries.english: If I killed him by mistake, the blood-price falls on my kin: You have been my spirit’s kin from Alast.”
He does not give stones in return for the pearls of contrition, this, O noble spirit, is the justice of the fleshly soul.

sections.title_fa: ‏برون رفتن خلق به سوى آن درخت
sections.title_en: How the people went forth to that tree.

entries.farsi: چون برون رفتند سوى آن درخت / ‏گفت دستش را سپس بنديد سخت
entries.english: When they went forth to that tree, he said, “Tie his hands fast behind him,

entries.farsi: تا گناه و جرم او پيدا كنم / ‏تا لواى عدل بر صحرا زنم
entries.english: In order that I may bring to light his sin and crime, and may plant the banner of justice on the field

entries.farsi: گفت اى سگ جد او را كشته‏اى / ‏تو غالمى خواجه زين رو گشته‏اى
entries.english: O dog,” said he, “you have killed this man’s grandfather. You are a slave: by this means you have become a lord.

entries.farsi: آن زنت او را كنيزك بوده است / ‏با همين خواجه جفا بنموده است
entries.english: You killed your master and carried off his property: God has made manifest what happened to him.

entries.farsi: خواجه را كشتى و بردى مال او / كرد يزدان آشكارا حال او
entries.english: Your wife was his handmaid; she has acted unjustly towards this same master.

entries.farsi: تو غالمى كسب و كارت ملك اوست / ‏شرع جستى شرع بستان رو نكوست
entries.english: Whatever she bore to him, female or male— all of them from beginning to end are the property of the heir.

entries.farsi: هر چه زو زاييد ماده يا كه نر / ملك وارث باشد آنها سربسر
entries.english: You are a slave: your gains and goods are his property. You demanded the Law: take the Law and go: it is well.

entries.farsi: خواجه را كشتى به استم زار زار / هم بر اينجا خواجه گويان زينهار
entries.english: You killed your master miserably by violence, your master was crying for mercy on this very spot.

entries.farsi: كارد از اشتاب كردى زير خاك / ‏از خيالى كه بديدى سهمناك
entries.english: In your haste you hid the knife under the soil, because of the terrible apparition which you beheld.

entries.farsi: نك سرش با كارد در زير زمين / ‏باز كاويد اين زمين را همچنين
entries.english: Lo, his head together with the knife is under the soil! Dig you back the soil, thus!

entries.farsi: نام اين سگ هم نبشته كارد بر / كرد با خواجه چنين مكر و ضرر
entries.english: On the knife, too, the name of this dog is written, dealt with his master so deceitfully and injuriously”

entries.farsi: همچنان كردند چون بشكافتند / در زمين آن كارد و سر را يافتند
entries.english: They did even so, and when they cleft they found in the soil the knife and the head.

entries.farsi: ولوله در خلق افتاد آن زمان / ‏هر يكى زنار ببريد از ميان
entries.english: Thereupon tumultuous lamentation arose amongst the people: every one severed the girdle from his waist.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش بيا اى داد خواه / داد خود بستان بدان روى سياه
entries.english: After that, he said to him, “Come, O demander of justice, with that black face receive the justice due to you!’

sections.title_fa: قصاص فرمودن داود عليه السلام خونى را بعد از الزام حجت بر او
sections.title_en: How David, on whom be peace, ordered that retaliation should be taken on the murderer after his conviction.

entries.farsi: هم بدان تيغش بفرمود او قصاص / ‏كى كند مكرش ز علم حق خالص
entries.english: He ordered retaliation with that same knife: how should contrivance deliver him from the knowledge of God?

entries.farsi: حلم حق گر چه مواساها كند / ليك چون از حد بشد پيدا كند
entries.english: Although God’s clemency bestows kindnesses, yet when he has gone beyond bounds, He exposes.

entries.farsi: خون نخسبد در فتد در هر دلى / ‏ميل جست و جوى كشف مشكلى
entries.english: Blood does not sleep: the desire to investigate and lay bare a difficulty falls into every heart.

entries.farsi: اقتضاى داورى رب دين / ‏سر بر آرد از ضمير آن و اين
entries.english: The craving prompted by the ordainment of the Lord of the Judgment springs up in the conscience of all and sundry

entries.farsi: كان فالن چون شد چه شد حالش چه گشت / ‏همچنان كه جوشد از گلزار كشت
entries.english: “How was it with such-and-such? What happened to him? What became of him? “Just as the sown seed shoots up from the loam

entries.farsi: جوشش خون باشد آن واجستها / خارش دلها و بحث و ماجرا
entries.english: Those inquiries, the pricking of beans and the investigation and discussion, are the stirring of the blood.

entries.farsi: چون كه پيدا گشت سر كار او / معجزه‏ى داود شد فاش و دو تو
entries.english: When the mystery of his case had been divulged, David’s miracles became doubly manifest

entries.farsi: خلق جمله سر برهنه آمدند / سر به سجده بر زمينها مى‏زدند
entries.english: All the people came bare-headed and cast their heads in prostration on the earth,

entries.farsi: ما همه كوران اصلى بوده‏ايم / ‏از تو ما صد گون عجايب ديده‏ايم
entries.english: “We all have been blind from birth, we have seen from you marvels of a hundred kinds.

entries.farsi: تو به سه سنگ و فالخن آمدى / ‏صد هزاران مرد را برهم زدى
entries.english: The stone came to speech with you overtly, and said, ‘Take me for Saul’s expedition’;

entries.farsi: سنگ با تو در سخن آمد شهير / كز براى غزو طالوتم بگير
entries.english: You came with three pebbles and a sling and did rout a hundred thousand men:

entries.farsi: سنگهايت صد هزاران پاره شد / هر يكى هر خصم را خون‏خواره شد
entries.english: Your pebbles broke into a hundred thousand pieces, and each one drank the blood of an enemy.

entries.farsi: آهن اندر دست تو چون موم شد / چون زره سازى تو را معلوم شد
entries.english: Iron became as wax in your hand when the fashioning coats of mail was made known to you.

entries.farsi: كوهها با تو رسائل شد شكور / با تو مى‏خوانند چون مقرى زبور
entries.english: The mountains became your thankful accompanists: they chant the psalms with you, as one who teaches the recitation of the Qur’an.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران چشم دل بگشاده شد / از دم تو غيب را آماده شد
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of spiritual eyes were opened and through your breath were made ready for the Unseen;

entries.farsi: و آن قوى‏تر ز آن همه كاين دايم است / ‏زندگى بخشى كه سرمد قايم است
entries.english: And that is stronger than all those, for this one is lasting: you bestow the life that endures forever.’!

entries.farsi: جان جمله‏ى معجزات اين است خود / كاو ببخشد مرده را جان ابد
entries.english: This indeed is the soul of all miracles, that it should bestow everlasting life on the dead.

entries.farsi: كشته شد ظالم جهانى زنده شد / هر يكى از نو خدا را بنده شد
entries.english: The wicked man was killed and a whole world were quickened with life: every one became anew a servant to God.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه نفس آدمى به جاى آن خونى است كه مدعى گاو گشته بود و آن گاو كشنده عقل است و داود حق است يا شيخ كه نايب حق است كه به قوت و يارى او تواند ظالم را كشتن و توانگر شدن ‏روزى بى‏كسب و بى‏حساب
sections.title_en: Explaining that Man’s fleshly soul is in the position of the murderer who had become a claimant

on account of the cow, and that the slayer of the cow is the intellect, and that David is God or the Shaykh who is God’s vicar, by means, of whose strength and support it is possible to kill the wicked and be enriched with daily bread that is not earned by labour and for which, there is no reckoning.

entries.farsi: نفس خود را كش جهان را زنده كن / ‏خواجه را كشته ست او را بنده كن
entries.english: Kill your fleshly soul and make the world alive; it has killed its master: make it slave.

entries.farsi: مدعى گاو نفس تست هين / ‏خويشتن را خواجه كرده ست و مهين
entries.english: Listen! Your fleshly soul is the claimant for the cow: it has made itself a master and thief.

entries.farsi: آن كشنده‏ى گاو عقل تست رو / بر كشنده‏ى گاو تن منكر مشو
entries.english: The slayer of the cow is your intellect: go, do not be offended with the slayer of the cow, your body.

entries.farsi: عقل اسير است و همى‏خواهد ز حق / ‏روزى بى‏رنج و نعمت بر طبق
entries.english: The intellect is a captive and craves of God daily bread without toil, and bounty on a tray.

entries.farsi: روزى بى‏رنج او موقوف چيست / ‏آن كه بكشد گاو را كاصل بدى است
entries.english: Upon what does its daily bread without toil depend? Upon its killing the cow is the origin of evil

entries.farsi: خواجه زاده‏ى عقل مانده بى‏نوا / نفس خونى خواجه گشت و پيشوا
entries.english: The fleshly soul says, “How should you kill my ‘cow’?— because the “cow” of the fleshly soul is the form of the body.

entries.farsi: روزى بى‏رنج مى‏دانى كه چيست / ‏قوت ارواح است و ارزاق نبى است
entries.english: The intellect, the master’s son, is left destitute, the fleshly soul, the murderer, has become a master and leader.

entries.farsi: نفس گويد چون كشى تو گاو من / ‏ز انكه گاو نفس باشد نقش تن
entries.english: Do you know what the daily bread without toil is? It is the food of spirits and the daily bread of the prophet.

entries.farsi: ليك موقوف است بر قربان گاو / گنج اندر گاو دان اى كنج كاو
entries.english: But it depends upon sacrificing the cow: know the treasure in the cow, O you who dig in corners!

entries.farsi: دوش چيزى خورده‏ام ور نى تمام / ‏دادمى در دست فهم تو زمام
entries.english: Yesternight I ate something; otherwise, I would have given the reins entirely into the hand of your understanding.

entries.farsi: دوش چيزى خورده‏ام افسانه است / ‏هر چه مى‏آيد ز پنهان خانه است
entries.english: Yesternight I ate something,” is an idle tale: whatsoever comes is from the secret chamber.

entries.farsi: چشم بر اسباب از چه دوختيم / ‏گر ز خوش چشمان كرشم آموختيم
entries.english: Why have we fixed our eyes on causes, if we have learned from those with beauteous eyes how to glance amorously?

entries.farsi: هست بر اسباب اسبابى دگر / در سبب منگر در آن افكن نظر
entries.english: Over the causes there are other causes: do not look at the cause; let your gaze fall on the primary cause.

entries.farsi: انبيا در قطع اسباب آمدند / معجزات خويش بر كيوان زدند
entries.english: The prophets came in order to cut causes: they flung their miracles at Saturn.

entries.farsi: بى‏سبب مر بحر را بشكافتند / بى‏زراعت چاش گندم يافتند
entries.english: Without cause they split the sea asunder; without sowing they found heaps of wheat.

entries.farsi: ريگها هم آرد شد از سعيشان / ‏پشم بز ابريشم آمد كش كشان
entries.english: Sand, too, was turned into flour by their work; goat’s hair became silk as it was pulled.

entries.farsi: جمله قرآن هست در قطع سبب / ‏عز درويش و هالك بو لهب
entries.english: The whole of the Qur’an consists in the cutting off of causes: the glory of the poor and the destruction of Abu Lahab.

entries.farsi: مرغ بابيلى دو سه سنگ افكند / لشكر زفت حبش را بشكند
entries.english: A swallow drops two or three pebbles and shatters the mighty host of Abyssinia:

entries.farsi: پيل را سوراخ سوراخ افكند / سنگ مرغى كاو به بالا پر زند
entries.english: The pebble of a bird that flies aloft lays low the elephant, riddled with holes.

entries.farsi: حلق ببريده جهد از جاى خويش / ‏خون خود جويد ز خون پالاى خويش
entries.english: “Inflict the tail of the killed cow upon the murdered man, in order that at the same moment he may come to life in the winding-sheet,

entries.farsi: دم گاو كشته بر مقتول زن / ‏تا شود زنده همان دم در كفن
entries.english: He whose throat was cut may spring up from his place and seek his blood from the shedder of his blood

entries.farsi: همچنين ز آغاز قرآن تا تمام / ‏رفض اسباب است و علت و السلام
entries.english: In like manner, from the beginning of the Qur’an to the end, it is the abandonment of causes and means. Farewell.

entries.farsi: كشف اين نه از عقل كار افزا بود / بندگى كن تا ترا پيدا شود
entries.english: The explanation thereof is not by the meddlesome intellect: do service, in order that it may become clear to you.

entries.farsi: بند معقوالت آمد فلسفى / ‏شهسوار عقل عقل آمد صفى
entries.english: The philosopher is in bondage to things perceived by the intellect the pure is he that rides as a prince on the Intellect of intellect

entries.farsi: عقل عقلت مغز و عقل تست پوست / ‏معده‏ى حيوان هميشه پوست جوست
entries.english: The Intellect of intellect is your kernel; your intellect is the husk: the belly of animals is ever seeking husks.

entries.farsi: مغز جوى از پوست دارد صد مالل / ‏مغز نغزان را حالل آمد حالل
entries.english: He that seeks the kernel has a great loathing for the husk: to the goodly the kernel is lawful, lawful.

entries.farsi: چون كه قشر عقل صد برهان دهد / عقل كل كى گام بى‏ايقان نهد
entries.english: When the intellect, the husk, offers a hundred evidences, how should the Universal Intellect take a step without having cer­ tainty? The intellect makes books entirely black; the Intellect of intellect keeps the horizons filled (with light) from the Moon.

entries.farsi: از سياهى وز سپيدى فارغ است / ‏نور ماهش بر دل و جان بازغ است
entries.english: It is free from blackness and whiteness: the light of its moon rises upon heart and soul.

entries.farsi: عقل دفترها كند يك سره سياه / ‏عقل عقل آفاق دارد پر ز ماه
entries.english: If this black and white has gotten power, it is from the Night of Power that shone forth like a star.

entries.farsi: قيمت هميان و كيسه از زر است / ‏بى‏زر آن هميان و كيسه ابتر است
entries.english: The value of scrip and purse is from the gold: without the gold, scrip and purse are docked

entries.farsi: همچنان كه قدر تن از جان بود / قدر جان از پرتو جانان بود
entries.english: Even as the worth of the body is from the soul, the worth of the soul is from the radiance of the Soul of souls.

entries.farsi: گر بدى جان زنده بى‏پرتو كنون / ‏هيچ گفتى كافران را ميتون
entries.english: If the soul were now alive without radiance, would He ever have called the infidels “dead?”

entries.farsi: هين بگو كه ناطقه جو مى‏كند / تا به قرنى بعد ما آبى رسد
entries.english: Come; speak! For the Logos is digging a channel to the end that some water may reach a generation after us.

entries.farsi: گر چه هر قرنى سخن آرى بود / ليك گفت سالفان يارى بود
entries.english: Although every generation there is one who brings the word, yet the sayings of them that have gone before are helpful

entries.farsi: نى كه هم تورات و انجيل و زبور / شد گواه صدق قرآن اى شكور
entries.english: Is it not that the Pentateuch and the Gospel and the Psalms have borne witness to the truth of the Qur’an, O thankful one?

entries.farsi: روزى بى‏رنج جو و بى‏حسيب / ‏كز بهشتت آورد جبريل سيب
entries.english: Seek a livelihood without toil and without reckoning, so that Gabriel may bring you apples from Paradise;

entries.farsi: بلكه رزقى از خداوند بهشت / ‏بى‏صداع باغبان بى‏رنج كشت
entries.english: No, a livelihood from the Lord of Paradise, without headache on the part of the gardener and without the toil of sowing

entries.farsi: ز انكه نفع نان در آن نان داد اوست / ‏بدهدت آن نفع بى‏توسيط پوست
entries.english: Inasmuch as in that bread the benefit of the bread is His gift, He gives you that benefit, without making the husk a means.

entries.farsi: ذوق پنهان نقش نان چون سفره‏اى است / ‏نان بى‏سفره ولى را بهره‏اى است
entries.english: The savour is hidden; the outward form of the bread is like a table-cloth: the bread that is without table-cloth is a portion for the saint.

entries.farsi: رزق جانى كى برى با سعى و جست / جز به عدل شيخ كاو داود تست
entries.english: How will you, notwithstanding endeavour and search, gain the spiritual livelihood except through the justice of the Shaykh who is your David?

entries.farsi: نفس چون با شيخ بيند گام تو / از بن دندان شود او رام تو
entries.english: When the fleshly soul sees your steps with the Shaykh, willy-nilly it becomes submissive to you.

entries.farsi: صاحب آن گاو رام آن گاه شد / كز دم داود او آگاه شد
entries.english: Then did the owner of the cow become submissive, when he was made aware of the words of David.

entries.farsi: عقل گاهى غالب آيد در شكار / بر سگ نفست كه باشد شيخ يار
entries.english: The intellect, in chase, prevails over your currish fleshly soul at the time when the Shaykh is its helper.

entries.farsi: نفس اژدرهاست با صد زور و فن / ‏روى شيخ او را زمرد ديده كن
entries.english: The fleshly soul is a dragon with hundredfold strength and cunning: the face of the Shaykh is the emerald that plucks out its eye

entries.farsi: گر تو صاحب گاو را خواهى زبون / ‏چون خران سيخش كن آن سو اى حرون
entries.english: If you wish the owner of the cows to be abased, goad him in that direction as asses, O contumacious man!

entries.farsi: چون به نزديك ولى اهلل شود / آن زبان صد گزش كوته شود
entries.english: When he approaches him who is nigh unto God, his tongue, a hundred ells long, is shortened.

entries.farsi: صد زبان و هر زبانش صد لغت / ‏زرق و دستانش نيايد در صفت
entries.english: A hundred tongues, and each tongue of him a hundred languages: his fraud and guile come not into description.

entries.farsi: مدعى گاو نفس آمد فصيح / ‏صد هزاران حجت آرد ناصحيح
entries.english: The claimant for the cow, the fleshly soul, is eloquent and brings forward hundreds of thousands of unsound pleas.

entries.farsi: شهر را بفريبد الا شاه را / ره نتاند زد شه آگاه را
entries.english: He deceives the city except the king: he cannot waylay the sagacious king.

entries.farsi: مصحف و سالوس او باور مكن / ‏خويش با او همسر و همسر مكن
entries.english: The fleshly soul has glorification of God, and the Qur’an in its right hand; in its sleeve dagger and sword.

entries.farsi: نفس را تسبيح و مصحف در يمين / ‏خنجر و شمشير اندر آستين
entries.english: Do not believe its Qur’an and hypocritical ostentation; do not make yourself its confidant and comrade;

entries.farsi: سوى حوضت آورد بهر وضو / و اندر اندازد ترا در قعر او
entries.english: It will take you to the tank to perform the ritual ablution, and will cast you to the bottom thereof.

entries.farsi: عقل نورانى و نيكو طالب است / ‏نفس ظلمانى بر او چون غالب است
entries.english: The intellect is luminous and a seeker of good: how does the dark fleshly soul prevail over it?

entries.farsi: ز انكه او در خانه عقل تو غريب / ‏بر در خود سگ بود شير مهيب
entries.english: Because it is at home, your intellect is a stranger: the dog at his own door is a terrible lion.

entries.farsi: باش تا شيران سوى بيشه روند / وين سگان كور آن جا بگروند
entries.english: Wait till the lions go to the jungle and these blind dogs will believe there.

entries.farsi: مكر نفس و تن نداند عام شهر / او نگردد جز به وحى القلب قهر
entries.english: The common folk of the city do not know the deceit of the fleshly soul and of the body: it is not subdued save by inspiration in the heart.

entries.farsi: هر كه جنس اوست يار او شود / جز مگر داود كاو شيخت بود
entries.english: Every one that is its congener becomes its friend, except, to be sure, the David who is your Shaykh;

entries.farsi: كاو مبدل گشت و جنس تن نماند / هر كه را حق در مقام دل نشاند
entries.english: For he has been transmuted, and whomsoever God has seated in the abode of the heart, he is no more the body’s congener.

entries.farsi: خلق جمله علتى‏اند از كمين / ‏يار علت مى‏شود علت بقين
entries.english: All the people are infirm by ambush: it is certain that infirmity associates with infirmity.

entries.farsi: هر خسى دعوى داودى كند / هر كه بى‏تمييز كف در وى زند
entries.english: Every worthless fellow pretends to be David; everyone who lacks discernment lays hold of him:

entries.farsi: از صيادى بشنود آواز طير / مرغ ابله مى‏كند آن سوى سير
entries.english: He hears the bird’s note from a fowler and, foolish bird; he keeps going in that direction.

entries.farsi: نقد را از نقل نشناسد غوى است / ‏هين از او بگريز اگر چه معنوى است
entries.english: He does not distinguish fact from fiction: he is misguided. Come; flee from him, even if he is spiritual.

entries.farsi: رسته و بر بسته پيش او يكى است / ‏گر يقين دعوى كند او در شكى است
entries.english: What has grown and what has been tied on is one to him: though he may claim intuitive certainty, he is in a (great) doubt.

entries.farsi: اين چنين كس گر ذكى مطلق است / ‏چونش اين تمييز نبود احمق است
entries.english: If such a one is absolutely keen-witted, when he has not this discernment, he is a fool.

entries.farsi: هين از او بگريز چون آهو ز شير / سوى او مشتاب اى دانا دلير
entries.english: Listen; flee from him as the deer from the lion: do not hasten boldly towards him, O wise man!

sections.title_fa: ‏گريختن عيسى عليه السلام فراز كوه از احمقان
sections.title_en: How Jesus, on whom be peace, fled to the top of a mountain from the fools.

entries.farsi: عيسى مريم به كوهى مى‏گريخت / ‏شير گويى خون او مى‏خواست ريخت
entries.english: Jesus, son of Mary, was fleeing to a mountain:you would say that a lion wished to shed his blood.

entries.farsi: آن يكى در پى دويد و گفت خير / در پيت كس نيست چه گريزى چو طير
entries.english: A certain man ran after him and said, “Are you well? There is no one in pursuit of you: why do you flee, like a bird?”

entries.farsi: با شتاب او آن چنان مى‏تاخت جفت / ‏كز شتاب خود جواب او نگفت
entries.english: He still kept running with haste so that on account of his haste he did not answer him.

entries.farsi: يك دو ميدان در پى عيسى براند / پس به جد جد عيسى را بخواند
entries.english: He pushed on in pursuit of Jesus for the distance of one or two fields, and then invoked Jesus with the utmost earnestness,

entries.farsi: از كه اين سو مى‏گريزى اى كريم / ‏نه پيت شير و نه خصم و خوف و بيم
entries.english: Saying, “For the sake of pleasing God, stop one moment, for I have a difficulty concerning your flight.

entries.farsi: كز پى مرضات حق يك لحظه بيست / ‏كه مرا اندر گريزت مشكلى است
entries.english: From whom art you fleeing in this direction, O noble one? There is no lion pursuing you, no enemy, and there is no fear or danger.”

entries.farsi: گفت از احمق گريزانم برو / مى‏رهانم خويش را بندم مشو
entries.english: He said, “I am fleeing from the fool. Leave! I am saving myself. Do not stop me!”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر آن مسيحا نى توى / ‏كه شود كور و كر از تو مستوى
entries.english: “Why,” said he, “are not you the Messiah by whom the blind and the deaf are restored?”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى گفت آن شه نيستى / ‏كه فسون غيب را ماويستى
entries.english: He said, “Yes.” Said the other, “Are not you the King in whom the spells of the Unseen World have their abode?—

entries.farsi: چون بخوانى آن فسون بر مرده‏اى / ‏بر جهد چون شير صيد آورده‏اى
entries.english: When you chant those spells over a dead man, he springs up like a lion that has caught his prey.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى آن منم گفتا كه تو / نى ز گل مرغان كنى اى خوب رو
entries.english: He said, “Yes, I am he.” Said the other, “Don’t you make birds out of clay, O beauteous one?”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى گفت پس اى روح پاك / ‏هر چه خواهى مى‏كنى از كيست باك
entries.english: He said, “Yes.” Said the other, “Then, O pure Spirit, you do whatsoever you will: Whom do you fear?

entries.farsi: با چنين برهان كه باشد در جهان / ‏كه نباشد مر ترا از بندگان
entries.english: With such evidence, who is there in the world that would not be one of the slaves to you?”

entries.farsi: حرمت ذات و صفات پاك او / كه بود گردون گريبان چاك او
entries.english: Jesus said, “By the holy Essence of God, the Maker of the body and the Creator of the soul in eternity;

entries.farsi: كان فسون و اسم اعظم را كه من / ‏بر كر و بر كور خواندم شد حسن
entries.english: By the sanctity of the pure Essence and Attributes of Him, for whose sake the collar of Heaven is rent,

entries.farsi: گفت عيسى كه به ذات پاك حق / ‏مبدع تن خالق جان در سبق
entries.english: That the spells and the Most Great Name which I pronounced over the deaf and the blind were good.

entries.farsi: بر كه سنگين بخواندم شد شكاف / ‏خرقه را بدريد بر خود تا بناف
entries.english: I pronounced over the stony mountain: it was split and tore upon itself its mantle down to the navel.

entries.farsi: بر تن مرده بخواندم گشت حى / ‏بر سر ال شى بخواندم گشت شى
entries.english: I pronounced over the corpse: it came to life. I pronounced over nonentity: it became entity.

entries.farsi: خواندم آن را بر دل احمق به ود / صد هزاران بار و درمانى نشد
entries.english: I pronounced them lovingly over the heart of the fool hundreds of thousands of times, and it was no cure.

entries.farsi: سنگ خارا گشت و ز آن خو بر نگشت / ‏ريگ شد كز وى نرويد هيچ كشت
entries.english: He became hard rock and changed not from that disposition; he became sand from which no produce grows.”

entries.farsi: گفت حكمت چيست كانجا اسم حق / ‏سود كرد اينجا نبود آن را سبق
entries.english: Said the other, “What is the reason that the Name of God availed there, it had no advantage here?

entries.farsi: آن همان رنج است و اين رنجى، چرا / او نشد اين را و آن را شد دوا
entries.english: That is disease too, and this is a disease: why did it not become a cure for this, since it cured that?”

entries.farsi: گفت رنج احمقى قهر خداست / ‏رنج و كورى نيست قهر، آن ابتالست
entries.english: He said, “The disease of folly is the wrath of God; disease and blindness are not wrath: they are probation.”

entries.farsi: ابتال رنجى است كان رحم آورد / احمقى رنجى است كان زخم آورد
entries.english: Probation is a disease that brings mercy; folly is a disease that brings rejection.

entries.farsi: آن چه داغ اوست مهر او كرده است / ‏چاره‏اى بر وى نيارد برد دست
entries.english: That which is branded on him He has sealed: no hand can apply a remedy to it.

entries.farsi: ز احمقان بگريز چون عيسى گريخت / ‏صحبت احمق بسى خونها بريخت
entries.english: Flee from the foolish, seeing that Jesus fled: how much blood has been shed by companionship with fools!

entries.farsi: اندك اندك آب را دزدد هوا / دين چنين دزدد هم احمق از شما
entries.english: The air steals away water little by little: so too does the fool steal away religion from you.

entries.farsi: گرمى‏ات را دزدد و سردى دهد / همچو آن كاو زير كون سنگى نهد
entries.english: He steals away your heat and gives you cold, like one who puts a stone under your rump.

entries.farsi: آن گريز عيسيى نه از بيم بود / ايمن است او آن پى تعليم بود
entries.english: The flight of Jesus was not caused by fear, he is safe: it was for the purpose of teaching.

entries.farsi: زمهرير ار پر كند آفاق را / چه غم آن خورشيد با اشراق را
entries.english: Though intense frost covers the world from end to end, what harm to the radiant sun?

sections.title_fa: ‏قصه‏ى اهل سبا و حماقت ايشان و اثر ناكردن نصيحت انبيا در احمقان
sections.title_en: The story of the people of Saba and their folly, and how the admonition of the prophets produces no effect upon the foolish

entries.farsi: يادم آمد قصه‏ى اهل سبا / كز دم احمق صباشان شد وبا
entries.english: I am reminded of the story of the people of Saba—how their zephyr (Saba) was turned into pestilence (waba) by the words of the foolish.

entries.farsi: آن سبا ماند به شهر بس كالن / كودكان افسانه‏ها مى‏آورند
entries.english: That Saba resembles the great big city you may hear of from children in tales.

entries.farsi: درج در افسانه‏شان بس سر و پند / هزلها گويند در افسانه‏ها
entries.english: The children relate tales, in their tales there is enfolded many a mystery and lesson.

entries.farsi: گنج مى‏جو در همه ويرانه‏ها / بود شهرى بس عظيم و مه ولى
entries.english: In tales they say ridiculous things, in all ruined places do you ever seek the treasure.

entries.farsi: ‏قدر او قدر سكره بيش نى / بس عظيم و بس فراخ و بس دراز
entries.english: There was a city very huge and great, but its size was the size of a saucer, no more.

entries.farsi: سخت زفت و تو بتو همچون پياز / مردم ده شهر مجموع اندر او
entries.english: Very huge and very broad and very long, ever so big, as big as an onion.

entries.farsi: ليك جمله سه تن ناشسته رو / اندر او خلق و خاليق بى‏شمار
entries.english: The people of ten cities were assembled within it, but the whole three fellows with unwashed faces.

entries.farsi: ليك آن جمله سه خام پخته خوار / جان ناكرده به جانان تاختن
entries.english: Within it there were numberless people and folk, but the whole of them three beggarly fools.

entries.farsi: ‏گر هزاران است باشد نيم تن / آن يكى بس دور بين و ديده كور
entries.english: The soul that has not made haste towards the Beloved— if it is thousands, it is half a body.

entries.farsi: از سليمان كور و ديده پاى مور / و آن دگر بس تيز گوش و سخت كر
entries.english: One was very far-sighted and blind-eyed— blind to Solomon and seeing the leg of the ant;

entries.farsi: گنج در وى نيست يك جو سنگ زر / و آن دگر عور و برهنه‏ى الشه باز
entries.english: And the second was very sharp of hearing and extremely deaf— a treasure in which there is not a barleycorn’s weight of gold;

entries.farsi: ليك دامنهاى جامه‏ى او دراز / گفت كور اينك سپاهى مى‏رسند
entries.english: And the other was naked and bare, sexual organs patefacto, but the skirts of his raiment were long.

entries.farsi: من همى‏بينم كه چه قومند و چند / گفت كر آرى شنودم بانگشان
entries.english: The blind man said, “Look, an army is approaching: I see what people they are and how many.”

entries.farsi: ‏كه چه مى‏گويند پيدا و نهان / آن برهنه گفت ترسان زين منم
entries.english: The deaf man said, “Yes; I heard their voices what they are saying openly and secretly.”

entries.farsi: ‏كه ببرند از درازى دامنم / كور گفت اينك به نزديك آمدند
entries.english: The naked man said, “I am afraid they will cut off from the length of my skirt.”

entries.farsi: خيز بگريزيم پيش از زخم و بند / كر همى‏گويد كه آرى مشغله
entries.english: The blind man said, “Look, they have come near! Arise and let us flee before blows and chains.”

entries.farsi: ‏از طمع برند و من ناايمنم / شهر را هشتند و بيرون آمدند
entries.english: “Yes,” says the deaf man, “the noise is getting nearer. Come on, my friends!”

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏شود نزديكتر ياران هله / آن برهنه گفت آوه دامنم
entries.english: The naked man said, “Alas, from covetousness they will cut off my skirt, and I am unprotected.”

entries.farsi: در هزيمت در دهى اندر شدند / اندر آن ده مرغ فربه يافتند
entries.english: They left the city and came forth and in their flight entered a village.

entries.farsi: ليك ذره‏ى گوشت بر وى نه نژند / مرغ مرده‏ى خشك و ز زخم كالغ
entries.english: In that village they found a fat fowl, but not a mite of flesh on it: abject—

entries.farsi: ‏استخوانها زار گشته چون بناغ / ز آن همى‏خوردند چون از صيد شير
entries.english: A dried-up dead fowl, and its bones through being pecked at by crows had become bare like threads.

entries.farsi: هر يكى از خوردنش چون پيل سير / هر سه ز آن خوردند و بس فربه شدند
entries.english: They were eating thereof as a lion of his prey: each of them surfeited, like an elephant, with eating it.

entries.farsi: چون سه پيل بس بزرگ و مه شدند / آن چنان كز فربهى هر يك جوان
entries.english: All the three ate thereof and grew exceedingly fat: they became like three very great and big elephants,

entries.farsi: ‏در نگنجيدى ز زفتى در جهان / با چنين گبزى و هفت اندام زفت
entries.english: In such wise that each young man, because of fatness, was too stout to be contained in the world

entries.farsi: ‏از شكاف در برون جستند و رفت / راه مرگ خلق ناپيدا رهى است
entries.english: Notwithstanding such bigness and seven stout limbs, they sprang forth through a chink in the door and departed.

entries.farsi: ‏در نظر نايد كه آن بى‏جا رهى است / نك پياپى كاروانها مقتفى
entries.english: The way of creaturely death is an invisible way: it comes not into sight: for it is a way with location.

entries.farsi: ‏زين شكاف در كه هست آن مختفى / بر در ار جويى نيابى آن شكاف
entries.english: Lo, the caravans are following one after another through this chink which is hidden in the door.

entries.farsi: ‏سخت ناپيدا و ز او چندين زفاف
entries.english: If you look on the door for that chink, you will not find it: extremely unapparent, though so many processions through it.

sections.title_fa: شرح آن كور دور بين و آن كر تيز شنو و آن برهنه‏ى دراز دامن
sections.title_en: Explaining the far-sighted blind man, the deaf man who is sharp of hearing, and the naked man with the long skirts

entries.farsi: كر امل را دان كه مرگ ما شنيد / مرگ خود نشنيد و نقل خود نديد
entries.english: Know that Hope is the deaf man who has heard of our dying, has not heard of his own death or regarded his own decease.

entries.farsi: حرص نابيناست بيند مو به مو / عيب خلقان و بگويد كو به كو
entries.english: The blind man is Greed: he sees other people’s faults, hair by hair, and tells them from street to street,

entries.farsi: عيب خود يك ذره چشم كور او / مى‏نبيند گر چه هست او عيب جو
entries.english: His blind eyes do not perceive one mote of his own faults, although he is a fault-finder.

entries.farsi: عور مى‏ترسد كه دامانش برند / دامن مرد برهنه كى درند
entries.english: The naked man is afraid that his skirt will be cut off: how should they cut off the skirt of a naked man?

entries.farsi: مرد دنيا مفلس است و ترس‏ناك / ‏هيچ او را نيست وز دزدانش باك
entries.english: The worldly man is destitute and terrified: he possesses nothing, he has dread of thieves.

entries.farsi: او برهنه آمد و عريان رود / وز غم دزدش جگر خون مى‏شود
entries.english: Bare he came and naked he goes, and his heart is bleeding with anxiety on account of the thief

entries.farsi: وقت مرگش كه بود صد نوحه پيش / ‏خنده آيد جانش را زين ترس خويش
entries.english: At the hour of death when a hundred lamentations are beside him, his spirit begins to laugh at its own fear.

entries.farsi: آن زمان داند غنى كش نيست زر / هم ذكى داند كه بود او بى‏هنر
entries.english: At that moment the rich man knows that he has no gold; the keen-witted man, too, knows that he is devoid of talent.

entries.farsi: چون كنار كودكى پر از سفال / ‏كاو بر آن لرزان بود چون رب مال
entries.english: Like a child’s lap filled with potsherds, for he is trembling for them, like the owner of riches.

entries.farsi: گر ستانى پاره‏اى گريان شود / پاره گر بازش دهى خندان شود
entries.english: If you take a piece away, he begins to weep; and if you give the piece back to him, he begins to laugh.

entries.farsi: محتشم چون عاريت را ملك ديد / پس بر آن مال دروغين مى‏طپيد
entries.english: Since the child is not endued with knowledge, his weeping and laughter have no importance.

entries.farsi: چون نباشد طفل را دانش دثار / گريه و خنده‏ش ندارد اعتبار
entries.english: Inasmuch as the magnate regarded that which is a loan as property, he was quivering for that false wealth.

entries.farsi: خواب مى‏بيند كه او را هست مال / ‏ترسد از دزدى كه بربايد جوال
entries.english: He dreams that he has wealth and is afraid of the thief who may carry off his sack.

entries.farsi: چون ز خوابش بر جهاند گوش كش / ‏پس ز ترس خويش تسخر آيدش
entries.english: When Death pulls his ear and makes him start up from slumber, then he falls to mocking at his fears.

entries.farsi: همچنان لرزانى اين عالمان / ‏كه بودشان عقل و علم اين جهان
entries.english: Even such the trembling of these learned scholars who have the intelligence and knowledge of this world.

entries.farsi: از پى اين عاقالن ذو فنون / ‏گفت ايزد در نبى ال يعلمون
entries.english: On account of these accomplished intelligent men, God said in the Qur’an, They do not know.

entries.farsi: هر يكى ترسان ز دزدى كسى / ‏خويشتن را علم پندارد بسى
entries.english: Each is afraid of some one’s stealing: he fancies that he possesses a great deal of knowledge.

entries.farsi: گويد از كارم بر آوردند خلق / ‏غرق بى‏كارى است جانش تا به حلق
entries.english: He says, “They are wasting my time,” in truth he has no time that is profitable.

entries.farsi: گويد او كه روزگارم مى‏برند / خود ندارد روزگار سودمند
entries.english: He says, “The people have taken me away from my work,” his soul is plunged in idleness up to the throat.

entries.farsi: عور ترسان كه منم دامن كشان / ‏چون رهانم دامن‏از چنگالشان
entries.english: The naked man is frightened and says, “I am trailing a skirt: how shall I save my skirt from their clutches?”

entries.farsi: صد هزاران فصل داند از علوم / ‏جان خود را مى‏نداند آن ظلوم
entries.english: He knows a hundred thousand superfluous matters connected with the sciences that unjust man does not know his own soul.

entries.farsi: داند او خاصيت هر جوهرى / ‏در بيان جوهر خود چون خرى
entries.english: He knows the special properties of every substance, in elucidating his own substance he is as an ass,

entries.farsi: كه همى‏دانم يجوز و ال يجوز / خود ندانى تو يجوزى يا عجوز
entries.english: Saying, “I know permissible and not permissible.” You know not whether you yourself are permissible or an old woman.

entries.farsi: اين روا و آن ناروا دانى و ليك / ‏تو روا يا ناروايى بين تو نيك
entries.english: You know this licit and that illicit, but are you licit or illicit? Consider well!

entries.farsi: قيمت هر كاله مى‏دانى كه چيست / ‏قيمت خود را ندانى احمقى است
entries.english: You know what the value of every article of merchandise is; you know not the value of yourself, it is folly.

entries.farsi: سعدها و نحسها دانسته‏اى / ‏ننگرى تو سعد يا ناشسته‏اى
entries.english: You have become acquainted with the fortunate and inauspicious stars; you do not look to see whether you are fortunate or unwashed.

entries.farsi: جان جمله علمها اين است اين / ‏كه بدانى من كى‏ام در يوم دين
entries.english: This, this, is the soul of all the sciences— that you should know who you shall be on the Day of Judgment.

entries.farsi: آن اصول دين بدانستى تو ليك / ‏بنگر اندر اصل خود گر هست نيك
entries.english: You art acquainted with the fundamentals (usul) of the Religion, but look upon your own fundamental (asl) and see whether it is good.

entries.farsi: از اصولينت اصول خويش به / ‏كه بدانى اصل خود اى مرد مه
entries.english: Your own fundamentals are better for you than the two fundamentals, so that you may know your own fundamental, O great man.

sections.title_fa: صفت خرمى شهر اهل سبا و ناشكرى ايشان
sections.title_en: Description of the luxuriance of the city of the Sabaeans and their ingratitude

entries.farsi: اصلشان بد بود آن اهل سبا / مى‏رميدندى ز اسباب لقا
entries.english: Their fundamental was bad: those inhabitants of Saba were recoiling from the means to meeting;

entries.farsi: دادشان چندان ضياع و باغ و راغ / ‏از چپ و از راست از بهر فراغ
entries.english: He gave them so many estates and orchards and meadowlands, on the left hand and the right, for leisure.

entries.farsi: بس كه مى‏افتاد از پرى ثمار / تنگ مى‏شد معبر ره بر گذار
entries.english: Inasmuch as the fruit was falling from abundance, there was no room for anyone to pass on the road,

entries.farsi: آن نثار ميوه ره را مى‏گرفت / ‏از پرى ميوه ره‏رو در شگفت
entries.english: The scattered largesse of fruit would block the way: the wayfarer in amazement at the plenty of the fruit.

entries.farsi: سله بر سر در درختستانشان / ‏پر شدى ناخواست از ميوه فشان
entries.english: In their groves, through the dropping of the fruit, a basket on the head would be filled involuntarily.

entries.farsi: باد آن ميوه فشاندى نه كسى / ‏پر شدى ز آن ميوه دامنها بسى
entries.english: The breeze would scatter the fruit, not any one: by that fruit a multitude of skirts would be filled.

entries.farsi: خوشه‏هاى زفت تا زير آمده / ‏بر سر و روى رونده مى‏زده
entries.english: Huge clusters, having come low down, would strike against the head and face of the wayfarer.

entries.farsi: مرد گلخن تاب از پرى زر / بسته بودى در ميان زرين كمر
entries.english: On account of the plenty of gold a bath-stoker might have tied a golden belt on his waist.

entries.farsi: سگ كليچه كوفتى در زير پا / تخمه بودى گرگ صحرا از نوا
entries.english: The dogs would trample buns underfoot; the desert wolf would have indigestion from the food.

entries.farsi: گشته ايمن شهر و ده از دزد و گرگ / ‏بز نترسيدى هم از گرگ سترگ
entries.english: Town and village had become safe from robbers and wolves; the goat was not afraid even of the fierce wolf.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم شرح نعمتهاى قوم / ‏كه زيادت مى‏شد آن يوما فيوم
entries.english: If I explain the blessings bestowed upon the people, which were increasing day by day,

entries.farsi: مانع آيد از سخنهاى مهم / ‏انبيا بردند امر فاستقم
entries.english: It will hinder from important matters. The prophets brought the command, namely, “Do you, therefore, be righteous.”

sections.title_fa: آمدن پيغامبران از حق به نصيحت اهل سبا
sections.title_en: How the prophets came from God to admonish the people of Saba.

entries.farsi: سيزده پيغمبر آن جا آمدند / گمرهان را جمله رهبر مى‏شدند
entries.english: Thirteen prophets came thither: all were ready to guide those who had lost the way,

entries.farsi: كه هله نعمت فزون شد شكر كو / مركب شكر ار بخسبد حركوا
entries.english: Saying, “Come, the benefit has increased: where is the thanksgiving? If the steed of thanksgiving lies down, set in motion.

entries.farsi: شكر منعم واجب آيد در خرد / ور نه بگشايد در خشم ابد
entries.english: In reason it is necessary to give thanks to the Benefactor; otherwise, the door of everlasting wrath will be opened.

entries.farsi: هين كرم بينيد و اين خود كس كند / كز چنين نعمت به شكرى بس كند
entries.english: Listen; behold the loving kindness! And in sooth would anyone do this— namely, be content with a single thanksgiving for such benefits?

entries.farsi: سر ببخشد، شكر خواهد سجده‏اى / ‏پا ببخشد، شكر خواهد قعده‏اى
entries.english: He bestows a head and asks as thanksgiving one act of bowing; He bestows feet and asks as thanksgiving one act of sitting.”

entries.farsi: قوم گفته شكر ما را برد غول / ‏ما شديم از شكر و از نعمت ملول
entries.english: The people said, “The ghoul has carried off our thanksgiving: we have become weary of giving thanks and receiving benefits.

entries.farsi: ما چنان پژمرده گشتيم از عطا / كه نه طاعتمان خوش آيد نه خطا
entries.english: We have become so disgusted with the bounty that neither piety nor sin pleases us.

entries.farsi: ما نمى‏خواهيم نعمتها و باغ / ‏ما نمى‏خواهيم اسباب و فراغ
entries.english: We do not desire benefits and orchards: we do not desire means and leisure.”

entries.farsi: انبيا گفتند در دل علتى است / ‏كه از آن در حق شناسى آفتى است
entries.english: The prophets said, “In your hearts is a malady whence there is a canker in the acknowledgment of obligations,

entries.farsi: نعمت از وى جملگى علت شود / طعمه در بيمار كى قوت شود
entries.english: And whereby the benefit is wholly turned into disease: how should food become (a source of) strength in the sick?

entries.farsi: چند خوش پيش تو آمد اى مصر / جمله ناخوش گشت و صاف او كدر
entries.english: How many a sweet thing came to you, O persistent (in sin), and they all grew sour, and their pure became turbid!

entries.farsi: تو عدوى اين خوشيها آمدى / ‏گشت ناخوش هر چه بر وى كف زدى
entries.english: You became a foe to that sweetness: on whatsoever thing you did lay your hand, it became sour.

entries.farsi: هر كه او بيگانه باشد با تو هم / ‏پيش تو او بس مه است و محترم
entries.english: Whoever became your familiar and friend became despicable and vile in your sight;

entries.farsi: هر كه او شد آشنا و يار تو / شد حقير و خوار در ديدار تو
entries.english: And every one, too, that would be alien to you is, in your opinion, very grand and venerable.

entries.farsi: اين هم از تاثير آن بيمارى است / ‏زهر او در جمله جفتان سارى است
entries.english: This also is from the effect produced by that sickness: its poison pervades all associated.

entries.farsi: دفع آن علت ببايد كرد زود / كه شكر با آن حدث خواهد نمود
entries.english: It behooves quickly to remove that malady, for with that sugar will seem filth;

entries.farsi: هر خوشى كايد به تو ناخوش شود / آب حيوان گر رسد آتش شود
entries.english: Every sweet thing that comes to you grows sour: if the Water of Life arrives, it turns into fire.

entries.farsi: كيمياى مرگ و جسك است آن صفت / ‏مرگ گردد ز آن حياتت عاقبت
entries.english: That quality is the elixir of death and woe: thereby your life is at last turned into death.

entries.farsi: بس عزيزى كه به ناز اشكار شد / چون شكارت شد بر تو خوار شد
entries.english: There was many a food by which your heart was revived: when it entered your body, it became stinking.

entries.farsi: بس غذايى كه ز وى دل زنده شد / چون بيامد در تن تو گنده شد
entries.english: There is many a dear one that was hunted with blandishments: when he became your prey, he became cheap in your eyes.

entries.farsi: آشنايى عقل با عقل از صفا / چون شود هر دم فزون باشد وال
entries.english: When from sincerity the friendship of intellect with intellect arises, every moment the devotion is increased;

entries.farsi: آشنايى نفس با هر نفس پست / ‏تو يقين مى‏دان كه دم دم كمتر است
entries.english: Know for sure, the friendship of the carnal soul with any base carnal soul is momently diminished,

entries.farsi: ز انكه نفسش گرد علت مى‏تند / معرفت را زود فاسد مى‏كند
entries.english: Because his carnal soul hovers round disease and soon corrupts the acquaintance.

entries.farsi: گر نخواهى دوست را فردا نفير / دوستى با عاقل و با عقل گير
entries.english: If you do not wish your friend to be averse on the morrow, choose friendship with the intelligent and with the intellect.

entries.farsi: از سموم نفس چون با علتى / ‏هر چه گيرى تو مرض را آلتى
entries.english: Inasmuch as you are sick from the simoom of the carnal soul, whatever you may take you are the instrument for disease.

entries.farsi: گر بگيرى گوهرى سنگى شود / ور بگيرى مهر دل جنگى شود
entries.english: If you take a jewel, it becomes a stone; and if you take kindness of heart, it becomes hatred;

entries.farsi: ور بگيرى نكته‏ى بكرى لطيف / ‏بعد دركت گشت بى‏ذوق و كثيف
entries.english: And if you take a fine original saying, after your apprehension it has become tasteless and gross—

entries.farsi: كه من اين را بس شنيدم كهنه شد / چيز ديگر گو بجز آن اى عضد
entries.english: ‘I have heard this many a time; it has become old: tell something else besides this, O trusty friend.’

entries.farsi: چيز ديگر تازه و نو گفته گير / باز فردا ز آن شوى سير و نفير
entries.english: Suppose that something else fresh and new has been said, again to-morrow you art surfeited with it and averse.

entries.farsi: دفع علت كن چو علت خو شود / هر حديثى كهنه پيشت نو شود
entries.english: Remove the disease: when the disease is eradicated, every old tale will become new to you,

entries.farsi: تا كه آن كهنه بر آرد برگ نو / بشكفاند كهنه صد خوشه ز گو
entries.english: So that the old will bring forth new leaves: the old will cause a hundred clusters to blossom from the ditch.

entries.farsi: آن طبيبان طبيعت ديگرند / كه به دل از راه نبضى بنگرند
entries.english: We are the physicians, the disciples of God: the Red Sea beheld us and was parted.

entries.farsi: ما طبيبانيم شاگردان حق / ‏بحر قلزم ديد ما را فانفلق
entries.english: Those natural physicians are different, for they look into the heart by means of a pulse.

entries.farsi: ما به دل بى‏واسطه خوش بنگريم / ‏كز فراست ما به عالى منظريم
entries.english: We look well into the heart without intermediary, for through clairvoyance we are in a high belvedere.

entries.farsi: آن طبيبان غذايند و ثمار / جان حيوانى بديشان استوار
entries.english: Those are physicians of food and fruit: by them the animal soul is strong.

entries.farsi: كاين چنين فعلى ترا نافع بود / و آن چنان فعلى ز ره قاطع بود
entries.english: We are physicians of deeds and words: the ray of the light of Majesty is our inspirer,

entries.farsi: ما طبيبان فعاليم و مقال / ‏ملهم ما پرتو نور جلال
entries.english: That a deed like this will be beneficial to you,while a deed like that will cut off from the Way;

entries.farsi: اين چنين قولى ترا پيش آورد / و آن چنان قولى ترا نيش آورد
entries.english: And that words like these will lead you on,while words like those will bring anguish to you.

entries.farsi: آن طبيبان را بود بولى دليل / ‏وين دليل ما بود وحى جليل
entries.english: To those physicians urine is evidence, whereas this evidence of ours is the inspiration of the Almighty.

entries.farsi: دست‏مزدى مى‏نخواهيم از كسى / ‏دست‏مزد ما رسد از حق بسى
entries.english: We do not desire a fee from any one: our fee comes many a time from a God.

entries.farsi: هين صال بيمارى ناسور را / داروى ما يك به يك رنجور را
entries.english: Listen; come hither for the incurable disease! We, one by one, are a medicine for the sick.”

sections.title_fa: ‏معجزه خواستن قوم از پيغمبران
sections.title_en: How the people demanded miracles from the prophets.

entries.farsi: قوم گفتند اى گروه مدعى / ‏كو گواه علم طب و نافعى
entries.english: The people said, “O you company of impostors, where is the evidence of knowledge of medicine and usefulness?

entries.farsi: چون شما بسته‏ى همين خواب و خوريد / همچو ما باشيد در ده مى‏چريد
entries.english: Since you are in bondage, like us, to this same sleep and food are pasturing in the country—

entries.farsi: چون شما در دام اين آب و گليد / كى شما صياد سيمرغ دليد
entries.english: Since you are entrapped by this water and earth, how are you hunters of the Simurgh the heart?

entries.farsi: حب جاه و سرورى دارد بر آن / كه شمارد خويش از پيغمبران
entries.english: Love of power and dominion induces to reckon him amongst the prophets.

entries.farsi: ما نخواهيم اين چنين الف و دروغ / ‏كردن اندر گوش و افتادن به دوغ
entries.english: We will not put in our ears such vain boasts and lies and fall into deception.”

entries.farsi: انبيا گفتند كاين ز آن علت است / ‏مايه‏ى كورى حجاب رويت است
entries.english: The prophets said, “This arises from that malady: the original blindness is the screen from seeing.

entries.farsi: دعوى ما را شنيديد و شما / مى‏نبينيد اين گهر در دست ما
entries.english: You have heard our call, and you do not see this jewel in our hands.

entries.farsi: امتحان است اين گهر مر خلق را / ماش گردانيم گرد چشمها
entries.english: This jewel is a test for the people: we turn it about round eyes.

entries.farsi: هر كه گويد كو گوا گفتش گواست / ‏كاو نمى‏بيند گهر حبس عماست
entries.english: Whosoever says, ‘Where is the evidence?’ his words are evidence that he does not see the jewel and is in thrall to blindness.”

entries.farsi: آفتابى در سخن آمد كه خيز / كه بر آمد روز برجه كم ستيز
entries.english: A sun has come to speech, “Arise! For the day has risen; jump up, do not dispute!”

entries.farsi: تو بگويى آفتابا كو گواه / ‏گويدت اى كور از حق ديده خواه
entries.english: You say, “O sun, where is the evidence?”— It will say to you, “O blind one, beg of God an eye.”

entries.farsi: روز روشن هر كه او جويد چراغ / ‏عين جستن كورى‏اش دارد بالغ
entries.english: If anyone seeks a lamp in bright daylight, the very fact of seeking announces his blindness.

entries.farsi: ور نمى‏بينى گمانى پرده‏اى / ‏كه صباح است و تو اندر پرده‏اى
entries.english: And if you do not see but have formed an opinion that it is the dawn and that you are in a veil,

entries.farsi: كورى خود را مكن زين گفت فاش / ‏خامش و در انتظار فضل باش
entries.english: Do not proclaim your blindness by these words; keep silence and be in expectation of the grace.

entries.farsi: در ميان روز گفتن روز كو / خويش رسوا كردن است اى روز جو
entries.english: To say in the midst of day “Where is the day?” is to expose yourself, O day seeker.

entries.farsi: صبر و خاموشى جذوب رحمت است / ‏وين نشان جستن نشان علت است
entries.english: Patience and silence attract the mercy, whereas to seek this sign is a sign of infirmity.

entries.farsi: أَنْصِ تُوا بپذير تا بر جان تو / آيد از جانان جزاى أنصتوا
entries.english: Accept, “Be silent,” in order that the recompense of “Be silent” may come to your soul from the Beloved.

entries.farsi: گر نخواهى نكس پيش اين طبيب / ‏بر زمين زن زر و سر را اى لبيب
entries.english: If you do not desire relapse in the presence of this Physician, dash to the ground your gold (zar) and your head (sar), O man of understanding.

entries.farsi: گفت افزون را تو بفروش و بخر / بذل جان و بذل جاه و بذل زر
entries.english: Sell your superfluous speech and buy sacrifice of life and sacrifice of position and sacrifice of gold,

entries.farsi: تا ثناى تو بگويد فضل هو / كه حسد آرد فلك بر جاه تو
entries.english: That the grace of Him (Hu)may utter praise of you, so that Heaven will be envious of your high estate

entries.farsi: چون طبيبان را نگه داريد دل / ‏خود ببينيد و شويد از خود خجل
entries.english: When you have regard for the hearts of the physicians, you will see yourselves and will become ashamed of yourselves.

entries.farsi: دفع اين كورى به دست خلق نيست / ‏ليك اكرام طبيبان از هدى است
entries.english: It is not in the power of created beings to remove this blindness, but the honouring of the physicians is from Divine guidance.

entries.farsi: اين طبيبان را به جان بنده شويد / تا به مشك و عنبر آگنده شويد
entries.english: Become devoted to these physicians with soul, that you may be filled with musk and ambergris.

sections.title_fa: متهم داشتن قوم انبيا را
sections.title_en: How the people suspected the prophets.

entries.farsi: قوم گفتند اين همه زرق است و مكر / كى خدا نايب كند از زيد و بكر
entries.english: The people said, “All this is fraud and deceit: how should God make a vicar of Zayd and Bakr?

entries.farsi: هر رسول شاه بايد جنس او / آب و گل كو خالق افلاک كو
entries.english: Every king’s messenger must be of his kind: where are water and clay in comparison with the Creator of the heavens?

entries.farsi: مغز خر خورديم تا ما چون شما / پشه را داريم هم راز هما
entries.english: Have we eaten ass’s brains that we, like you, should deem a gnat to be the confidant of the huma?

entries.farsi: كو هما كو پشه كو گل كو خدا / ز آفتاب چرخ چه بود ذره را
entries.english: Where is a gnat in comparison with the huma? Where is earth in comparison with God? What relation to the mote has the sun in the sky?

entries.farsi: اين چه نسبت اين چه پيوندى بود / تا كه در عقل و دماغى در رود
entries.english: What resemblance is this, and what connection is this, that it should enter into any mind and brain?

sections.title_fa: حكايت خرگوشان كه خرگوشى را به رسالت پيش پيل فرستادند كه بگو كه من رسول ماه آسمانم ‏پيش تو كه از اين چشمه‏ى آب حذر كن چنان كه در كتاب كليله تمام گفته است
sections.title_en: Story of the hares who sent a hare as ambassador to the elephant, bidding him say, ‘I come to you as the ambassador of the Moon in heaven to bid you beware of this water-spring,’
as is told in full in the Book of Kalíla and Dimna.

entries.farsi: اين بدان ماند كه خرگوشى بگفت / ‏من رسول ماهم و با ماه جفت
entries.english: This resembles the saying of a certain hare— ‘I am the ambassador of the Moon and companion to the Moon.’

entries.farsi: كز رمه‏ى پيالن بر آن چشمه‏ى زالل / ‏جمله نخجيران بدند اندر وبال
entries.english: For all the beasts of chase were in woe on account of a herd of elephants beside that limpid spring;

entries.farsi: جمله محروم و ز خوف از چشمه دور / حيله‏اى كردند چون كم بود زور
entries.english: All were deprived and far from the spring by dread: since strength was inferior, they made a plot.

entries.farsi: از سر كه بانگ زد خرگوش زال / ‏سوى پيالن در شب غره‏ى هالل
entries.english: From the mountain-top the old hare cried towards the elephants on the first night of the new-moon—

entries.farsi: شاه پيال من رسولم پيش بيست / ‏بر رسوالن بند و زجر و خشم نيست
entries.english: ‘Come on the fourteenth, O king elephant, which you may find within the spring the proof of this.

entries.farsi: كه بيا رابع عشر اى شاه پيل / ‏تا درون چشمه يابى اين دليل
entries.english: O king elephant, I am the ambassador in your presence. Stop! Ambassadors are not subjected to imprisonment and violence and wrath.

entries.farsi: ماه مى‏گويد كه اى پيالن رويد / چشمه آن ماست زين يك سو شويد
entries.english: The Moon says, “O elephants; depart! The spring is mine, turn aside from it;

entries.farsi: ور نه من تان كور گردانم ستم / ‏گفتم از گردن برون انداختم
entries.english: And if not, I will make you blind. I have declared the wrong and have thrown off my neck.

entries.farsi: ترك اين چشمه بگوييد و رويد / تا ز زخم تيغ مه ايمن شويد
entries.english: Depart from this spring and depart, that you may be safe from the blows of the Moon’s sword.”

entries.farsi: نك نشان آن است كاندر چشمه ماه / ‏مضطرب گردد ز پيل آب خواه
entries.english: Lo, the token is that the Moon in the spring will be disturbed by the water-craving elephant.

entries.farsi: آن فالن شب حاضر آ اى شاه پيل / ‏تا درون چشمه يابى زين دليل
entries.english: Come and be present on the such-and-such a night, O king elephant, in order that within the spring you may find the proof of this.’

entries.farsi: چون كه هفت و هشت از مه بگذريد / شاه پيل آمد ز چشمه مى‏چريد
entries.english: When seven and eight of the month had passed, the king elephant came to drink from the spring

entries.farsi: چون كه زد خرطوم پيل آن شب در آب / ‏مضطرب شد آب و مه كرد اضطراب
entries.english: When on that night the elephant put his trunk in the water, the water was disturbed, and the Moon showed disturbance.

entries.farsi: پيل باور كرد از وى آن خطاب / ‏چون درون چشمه مه كرد اضطراب
entries.english: The elephant believed that speech of his, when the Moon in the spring showed disturbance.

entries.farsi: ما نه ز آن پيالن گوليم اى گروه / ‏كه اضطراب ماه آردمان شكوه
entries.english: O company, we are not among those stupid elephants who are terrified by the disturbance of the Moon.”

entries.farsi: انبيا گفتند آوه پند جان / ‏سخت تر كرد اى سفيهان بندتان
entries.english: The prophets said, “Ah, spiritual admonition has made your bondage more grievous, O you fools!

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن انبيا طعن ايشان را و مثل زدن ايشان را
sections.title_en: How the prophets answered their sneers and uttered parables unto them.

entries.farsi: اى دريغا كه دوا در رنجتان / گشت زهر قهر جان آهنجتان
entries.english: Oh, alas that in your disease the remedy has become for you the poison of soul-wringing vengeance.

entries.farsi: ظلمت افزود اين چراغ آن چشم را / چون خدا بگماشت پرده‏ى خشم را
entries.english: This lamp has increased the darkness of that eye, since God has set the veil of wrath.

entries.farsi: چه رئيسى جست خواهيم از شما / كه رياستمان فزون است از سما
entries.english: What dominion shall we crave from you? For our dominion is greater than the sky.”

entries.farsi: چه شرف يابد ز كشتى بحر در / خاصه كشتى ز سرگين گشته پر
entries.english: What glory should the sea of pearls acquire from the ship— especially a ship that has been filled with shit?

entries.farsi: اى دريغ آن ديده‏ى كور و كبود / آفتابى اندر او ذره نمود
entries.english: Oh, alas for that eye blind and blear! Therein a sun seemed as a mote.

entries.farsi: ز آدمى كه بود بى‏مثل و نديد / ديده‏ى ابليس جز طينى نديد
entries.english: In an Adam who was without like or equal the eye of Iblis discerned nothing but a piece of clay.

entries.farsi: چشم ديوانه بهارش دى نمود / ز آن طرف جنبيد كاو را خانه بود
entries.english: The devilish eye showed his spring as winter: it moved in the direction where its home was.

entries.farsi: اى بسا دولت كه آيد گاه گاه / ‏پيش بى‏دولت بگردد او ز راه
entries.english: Oh, many a fortune that comes now and then to the unfortunate one, and he turns away!

entries.farsi: اى بسا معشوق كايد ناشناخت / ‏پيش بد بختى نداند عشق باخت
entries.english: Oh, many a beloved who comes unbeknown to an ill-starred one, and he knows not how to make love!

entries.farsi: اين غلط ده ديده را حرمان ماست / ‏وين مقلب قلب را سوء القضاست
entries.english: This that misleads the eye is our damnation, and this that turns the heart is evil destiny.

entries.farsi: چون بت سنگين شما را قبله شد / لعنت و كورى شما را ظله شد
entries.english: Since to you the idol of stone has become an object of adoration, the curse and blindness have overshadowed you.

entries.farsi: چون بشايد سنگتان انباز حق / ‏چون نشايد عقل و جان هم راز حق
entries.english: When your stone is a fitting partner for God, how are not intellect and spirit fitting confidants of God?

entries.farsi: پشه‏ى مرده هما را شد شريك / ‏چون نشايد زنده هم راز مليك
entries.english: The dead gnat has become the consort of the huma: how is the living one not fit to be the confidant of the King?

entries.farsi: يا مگر مرده تراشيده شماست / ‏پشه‏ى زنده تراشيده خداست
entries.english: Or, maybe, the dead one is fashioned by you, the living gnat is fashioned by God.

entries.farsi: عاشق خويشيد و صنعت كرد خويش / ‏دم ماران را سر مار است كيش
entries.english: You are in love with yourselves and the thing manufactured by yourselves: to serpents’ tails the serpent’s head is a law.

entries.farsi: نى در آن دم دولتى و نعمتى / ‏نى در آن سر راحتى و لذتى
entries.english: In that tail there is no fortune or happiness; in that head there is no pleasure or delight.

entries.farsi: گرد سر گردان بود آن دم مار / اليقند و در خورند آن هر دو يار
entries.english: That serpent’s tail is circling around the head: both those friends are fit and suited.

entries.farsi: آن چنان گويد حكيم غزنوى / ‏در الهى نامه گر خوش بشنوى
entries.english: So says the Sage of Ghazna in the Ilahí-náma, if you will listen well—

entries.farsi: كم فضولى كن تو در حكم قدر / در خور آمد شخص خر با گوش خر
entries.english: “Do not behave as a meddler in the decree of fore-ordainment: the ass’s shape is suited to the ass’s ear.”

entries.farsi: شد مناسب عضوها و ابدانها / شد مناسب وصفها با جانها
entries.english: Limbs and bodies are congruous; qualities are congruous with souls.

entries.farsi: وصف هر جانى تناسب باشدش / ‏بى‏گمان با جان كه حق بتراشدش
entries.english: Unquestionably the quality of every soul has congruity with the soul; for God fashions it.

entries.farsi: چون صفت با جان قرين كرده ست او / پس مناسب دانش همچون چشم و رو
entries.english: Inasmuch as He has joined the quality to the soul, know that it is congruous, like eyes and face.

entries.farsi: شد مناسب وصفها در خوب و زشت / ‏شد مناسب حرفها كه حق نبشت
entries.english: The qualities are congruous in the good and evil: congruous are the letters that God has written.

entries.farsi: اصبع لطف است و قهر و در ميان / كلك دل با قبض و بسطى زين بنان
entries.english: The eye and the heart are between two fingers like a pen in the hand of the writer, O Husayn.

entries.farsi: ديده و دل هست بين اصبعين / ‏چون قلم در دست كاتب اى حسين
entries.english: are the fingers of Grace and Wrath, and between them the pen, the heart, is in a state of distress or ease by these fingers.

entries.farsi: اى قلم بنگر گر اجلاليستى / ‏كه ميان اصبعين كيستى
entries.english: O pen, if you are one that magnifies, consider whose two fingers you are between.

entries.farsi: اين حروف حالهات از نسخ اوست / ‏عزم و فسخت هم ز عزم و فسخ اوست
entries.english: All your volition and movement are by this finger: your head is on the crossways of the assembly-place.

entries.farsi: جمله قصد و جنبشت زين اصبع است / ‏فرق تو بر چار راه مجمع است
entries.english: These letters your states are of His writing: your forming a purpose and changing it is just from His forming a purpose and changing it.

entries.farsi: اين قلم داند ولى بر قدر خود / قدر خود پيدا كند در نيك و بد
entries.english: There is no way but supplication and self-abasement: not every pen is conscious of this subjection to control.

entries.farsi: جز نياز و جز تضرع راه نيست / ‏زين تقلب هر قلم آگاه نيست
entries.english: The pen knows this but according to its measure: it manifests its measure in good and evil.

entries.farsi: آن چه در خرگوش و پيل آويختند / كى رسدتان اين مثلها ساختن
entries.english: That which they attached to the hare and the elephant, so that they confused eternity with tricks,

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه هر كس را نرسد مثل آوردن خاصه در كار الهى
sections.title_en: Explaining that it is not seemly for everyone to adduce parables, especially concerning Divine actions

entries.farsi: ‏سوى آن درگاه پاك انداختن / آن مثل آوردن آن حضرت است
entries.english: How is it seemly for you to make these similitudes and cast them at that holy Court?

entries.farsi: ‏كه به علم سر و جهر او آيت است / تو چه دانى سر چيزى تا تو كل
entries.english: That use of similitudes belongs to the Lord, for He is the authority for the knowledge of the hidden and the manifest.

entries.farsi: ‏يا به زلفى يا به رخ آرى مثل / موسيى آن را عصا ديد و نبود
entries.english: What do you know of the hidden nature of anything, that you, baldpate, should use a lock of hair or a cheek as similitudes?

entries.farsi: اژدها بد سر او لب مى‏گشود / چون چنان شاهى نداند سر چوب
entries.english: A Moses deemed that a rod, but it was not: it was a dragon: its hidden nature was opening its lips.

entries.farsi: ‏تو چه دانى سر اين دام و حبوب / چون غلط شد چشم موسى در مثل
entries.english: Inasmuch as such a king knows not the hidden nature of wood, how should you know the hidden nature of this snare and bait?

entries.farsi: ‏چون كند موشى فضولى مدخل / آن مثالت را چو اژدرها كند
entries.english: Since the eye of Moses was at fault in the similitude, how should a meddling mouse find an entrance.

entries.farsi: تا به پاسخ جزو جزوت بر كند / اين مثال آورد ابليس لعين
entries.english: He will make that comparison of yours a dragon, that in answer it may tear you to pieces.

entries.farsi: ‏تا كه شد ملعون حق تا يوم دين / اين مثال آورد قارون از لجاج
entries.english: The accursed Iblis used this comparison, so that he fell under God’s curse till the Day of Judgement.

entries.farsi: ‏تا فرو شد در زمين با تخت و تاج / اين مثالت را چو زاغ و بوم دان
entries.english: Qarun from rebelliousness used this comparison, so that he sank down into the earth with his throne and diadem.

entries.farsi: ‏كه از ايشان پست شد صد خاندان
entries.english: Know that this comparison of yours is like crows and owls by which a hundred households are laid low.

sections.title_fa: ‏مثلها زدن قوم نوح عليه السلام به استهزا در زمان كشتى ساختن
sections.title_en: How the people of Noah uttered similitudes derisively at the time of his building the Ark.

entries.farsi: نوح اندر باديه كشتى بساخت / ‏صد مثل گو از پى تسخر بتاخت
entries.english: Noah built an Ark in the desert: a hundred speakers of paables ran up to riducule.

entries.farsi: در بيابانى كه چاه آب نيست / ‏مى‏كند كشتى چه نادان ابلهى است
entries.english: “He is making a ship in the desert where no well of water exists: what an ignorant fool he is!”

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گفت اى كشتى بتاز / و آن يكى مى‏گفت پرش هم بساز
entries.english: One was saying, “O Ship, run!” while another said, “Make wings for it too!”

entries.farsi: او همى‏گفت اين به فرمان خداست / ‏اين به چربكها نخواهد گشت كاست
entries.english: He said. “This is by the command of God: this will not be defeated by jeers.”

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت آن دزد كه پرسيدندش چه مى‏كنى نيم شب در بن اين ديوار گفت دهل مى‏زنم
sections.title_en: Story of the thief who was asked, “What are you doing at the bottom of this wall at midnight?” and replied, “I am beating a drum.”

entries.farsi: اين مثل بشنو كه شب دزدى عنيد / در بن ديوار حفره مى‏بريد
entries.english: Hear this parable— how a wicked thief was cutting a hole at the bottom of a wall.

entries.farsi: نيم بيدارى كه او رنجور بود / طق طق آهسته‏اش را مى‏شنود
entries.english: Some one half-awake, who was ill, heard the soft tapping of his,

entries.farsi: رفت بر بام و فرو آويخت سر / گفت او را در چه كارى اى پدر
entries.english: And went on the roof and hung his head down and said to him, “What are you about, O father?

entries.farsi: خير باشد نيم شب چه مى‏كنى / ‏تو كيى گفتا دهل‏زن اى سنى
entries.english: All is well, I hope. What are you doing at midnight? Who are you?” He said, “A drummer, O honourable sir.”

entries.farsi: در چه كارى گفت مى‏كوبم دهل / ‏گفت كو بانگ دهل اى بو سبل
entries.english: “What are you about?” He said, “I am beating the drum.” He said, “Where is the noise of the drum, O artful one?”

entries.farsi: گفت فردا بشنوى اين بانگ را / نعره‏ى يا حسرتا وا ويلتا
entries.english: He said, “You will hear this noise to-morrow, cries of ‘Oh, alas!’ and ‘Oh, woe is me!’”

entries.farsi: آن دروغ است و كژ و بر ساخته / ‏سر آن كژ را تو هم نشناخته
entries.english: That is a lie and false and made up; moreover, you have not perceived the secret of that falsehood.

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب آن مثل كه منكران گفتند از رسالت خرگوش پيغام پيل را از ماه آسمان
sections.title_en: The answer to the parable which the unbelievers related concerning the hare’s being sent as ambassador with a message to the elephant from the Moon in heaven.

entries.farsi: تا كه نفس گول را محروم كرد / ز آب حيوانى كه از وى خضر خورد
entries.english: Know that the hidden nature of that hare is the insolent Devil, who came as an ambassador to your soul,

entries.farsi: سر آن خرگوش دان ديو فضول / ‏كه به پيش نفس تو آمد رسول
entries.english: In order that he might deprive your foolish soul of the Water of Life whence Khizr drank

entries.farsi: باژگونه كرده‏اى معنيش را / كفر گفتى مستعد شو نيش را
entries.english: You have perverted its meaning, you have uttered blasphemy: prepare yourself for the sting.

entries.farsi: اضطراب ماه گفتى در زالل / ‏كه بترسانيد پيالن را شغال
entries.english: You have spoken of the moon’s being disturbed in the limpid water, whereby the jackal (hare) frightened the elephants;

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى خرگوش و پيل آرى و آب / ‏خشيت پيالن ز مه در اضطراب
entries.english: You relate the story of the hare and the elephant and the water, and the elephant’s fear of the moon when it was disturbed:

entries.farsi: اين چه ماند آخر اى كوران خام / ‏با مهى كه شد زبونش خاص و عام
entries.english: O you half-baked blind men, pray, how does this bear any resemblance to the Moon to whom lords and commons are subject?

entries.farsi: چه مه و چه آفتاب و چه فلك / ‏چه عقول و چه نفوس و چه ملك
entries.english: What is the moon and what is the sun and what is the sky? What are intelligences and souls and angels?

entries.farsi: آفتاب آفتاب آفتاب / ‏اين چه مى‏گويم مگر هستم به خواب
entries.english: “The Sun of the sun of the sun”: what is this I am saying? Surely I am asleep.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران شهر را خشم شهان / ‏سر نگون كرده است اى بد گمرهان
entries.english: The wrath of the kings has overthrown hundreds of thousands of cities; O ye wicked who have lost the way.

entries.farsi: كوه بر خود مى‏شكافد صد شكاف / ‏آفتابى چون خر آسى در طواف
entries.english: At their beck the mountain splits on itself into a hundred fissures; a sun goes round like an ass-mill.

entries.farsi: خشم مردان خشك گرداند سحاب / ‏خشم دلها كرد عالمها خراب
entries.english: The wrath of men makes the clouds dry; the wrath of hearts has laid worlds waste.

entries.farsi: بنگريد اى مردگان بى‏حنوط / در سياست گاه شهرستان لوط
entries.english: Look, O you unembalmed dead, upon the place where the city of Lot suffered punishment!

entries.farsi: پيل خود چه بود كه سه مرغ پران / ‏كوفتند آن پيلكان را استخوان
entries.english: What is the elephant even? For three flying birds crushed the bones of those wretched elephants.

entries.farsi: اضعف مرغان ابابيل است و او / پيل را بدريد و نپذيرد رفو
entries.english: The ababíl is the weakest of birds, and it rent the elephant irreparably.

entries.farsi: كيست كاو نشنيد آن طوفان نوح / ‏يا مصاف لشكر فرعون و روح
entries.english: Who is there that has not heard of the Flood of Noah, or of the battle of Pharaoh’s host with the Spirit?

entries.farsi: روحشان بشكست و اندر آب ريخت / ذره ذره آبشان بر مى‏گسيخت
entries.english: The Spirit routed them and cast them pell-mell into the water: the water was shattering them to atoms.

entries.farsi: كيست كاو نشيند احوال ثمود / و انكه صرصر عاديان را مى‏ربود
entries.english: Who is there that has not heard what happened to Thamud and how the sarsar windswept the Adites away?

entries.farsi: آن چنان پيالن و شاهان ظلوم / ‏زير خشم دل هميشه در رجوم
entries.english: Open your eyes for once upon such elephants, who were killers of elephants in war.

entries.farsi: چشم بارى در چنان پيالن گشا / كه بدندى پيل كش اندر وغا
entries.english: Such elephants and unjust kings as those are always in excommunication under the wrath of the heart.

entries.farsi: تا ابد از ظلمتى در ظلمتى / ‏مى‏روند و نيست غوثى رحمتى
entries.english: Unto everlasting they go from darkness to darkness, and there is no relief, no mercy.

entries.farsi: نام نيك و بد مگر نشنيده‏ايد / جمله ديدند و شما ناديده‏ايد
entries.english: Maybe you have not heard the name of good and evil. All have seen, and you are seeing not.

entries.farsi: ديده را ناديده مى‏آريد ليك / ‏چشمتان را واگشايد مرگ نيك
entries.english: You feign not to see the visible, but death will open your eyes well.

entries.farsi: گير عالم پر بود خورشيد و نور / چون روى در ظلمتى مانند گور
entries.english: Suppose the world is full of sun and light: when you go into darkness like the grave,

entries.farsi: بى‏نصيب آيى از آن نور عظيم / ‏بسته روزن باشى از ماه كريم
entries.english: You become without share in that great light; you are window-shut to the bounteous moon.

entries.farsi: تو درون چاه رفته ستى ز كاخ / ‏چه گنه دارد جهانهاى فراخ
entries.english: You have gone from the belvedere into the pit: how are the spacious worlds to blame?

entries.farsi: جان كه اندر وصف گرگى ماند او / چون ببيند روى يوسف را بگو
entries.english: The soul that has remained in the quality of wolfishness, how should it behold the face of Joseph? Say!

entries.farsi: لحن داودى به سنگ و كه رسيد / گوش آن سنگين دالنش كم شنيد
entries.english: The music of David reached rock and mountain, the ears of those stony-hearted ones heard it not.

entries.farsi: آفرين بر عقل و بر انصاف باد / هر زمان و اهلل أعلم بالرشاد
entries.english: Blessing ever be on reason and justice!— and God best knows the right way.

entries.farsi: صدقوا رسال كراما يا سبا / صدقوا روحا سباها من سبا
entries.english: Believe noble Messengers, O Saba! Believe a spirit made captive by Him who captured it.

entries.farsi: صدقوهم هم شموس طالعه / ‏يؤمنوكم من مخازي القارعه
entries.english: Believe them—they are rising suns— and they will preserve you from the ignominies of al-Qari‘a.

entries.farsi: صدقوهم هم بدور زاهره / قبل أن يلقوكم بالساهرة
entries.english: Believe them—they are shining full-moons— ere they confront you with al- Sahira.

entries.farsi: صدقوهم هم مصابيح الدجى / أكرموهم هم مفاتيح الرجا
entries.english: Believe them—they are the lamps of darkness; honour them— they are the keys of hope.

entries.farsi: صدقوا من ليس يرجو خيركم / ‏ال تضلوا ال تصدوا غيركم
entries.english: Believe those who hope not for your bounty; do not go astray, do not turn others away.

entries.farsi: پارسى گوييم هين تازى بهل / ‏هندوى آن ترك باش اى آب و گل
entries.english: Let us speak Persian: come, abandon Arabic. Be the Hindu of that Turcoman, O water and clay.

entries.farsi: هين گواهيهاى شاهان بشنويد / بگرويدند آسمانها بگرويد
entries.english: Listen, listen to the testimonies of the kings; the heavens have believed: believe you!

sections.title_fa: ‏معنى حزم و مثال مرد حازم
sections.title_en: The meaning of prudence, and a parable of the prudent man

entries.farsi: حزم چه بود در دو تدبير احتياط / از دو آن گيرى كه دور است از خباط
entries.english: Either consider what happened to the former, or fly with prudence towards the latter end.

entries.farsi: يا بحال اولينان بنگريد / يا سوى آخر به حزمى در پريد
entries.english: What is prudence? Precaution in two plans: of the two you will take that one which is far from craziness.

entries.farsi: آن دگر گويد دروغ است اين بران / ‏كه به هر شب چشمه‏اى بينى روان
entries.english: One person may say, “On this road there is no water for seven days, and there is foot-scorching sand.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى گويد در اين ره هفت روز / نيست آب و هست ريگ پاى سوز
entries.english: Another may say, “This is false: push on, for you will find a running fountain every night.”

entries.farsi: حزم آن باشد كه برگيرى تو آب / ‏تا رهى از ترس و باشى بر صواب
entries.english: It is prudence that you take water, so that you may be saved from dread and may be on the right.

entries.farsi: گر بود در راه آب اين را بريز / ور نباشد واى بر مرد ستيز
entries.english: If there be water, spill this; and if there be none, alas for the obstinate man!

entries.farsi: اى خليفه زادگان دادى كنيد / حزم بهر روز ميعادى كنيد
entries.english: O children of the Vicegerent, deal justly: act with prudence for the sake of the Day of Tryst.

entries.farsi: آن عدويى كز پدرتان كين كشيد / سوى زندانش ز عليين كشيد
entries.english: That enemy who took vengeance upon your father and dragged him from Illiyyin to prison,

entries.farsi: آن شه شطرنج دل را مات كرد / از بهشتش سخره‏ى آفات كرد
entries.english: And checkmated that king of the spiritual chessboard and made him, from Paradise, a thrall to calamities—

entries.farsi: چند جا بندش گرفت اندر نبرد / تا به كشتى در فكندش روى زرد
entries.english: How often in combat did he seize him by sleight, that he might wrestle with him and throw him in disgrace!

entries.farsi: اين چنين كرده ست با آن پهلوان / ‏سست سستش منگريد اى ديگران
entries.english: Thus has he done to that paladin: do not regard him with contempt, O you others!

entries.farsi: مادر و باباى ما را آن حسود / تاج و پيرايه به چالاكى ربود
entries.english: That envious one nimbly snatched away our mother’s and father’s crown and ornament.

entries.farsi: كردشان آن جا برهنه و زار و خوار / سالها بگريست آدم زار زار
entries.english: There he made them naked and wretched and despicable: years did Adam weep bitterly,

entries.farsi: كه ز اشك چشم او روييد نبت / ‏كه چرا اندر جريده‏ى الست ثبت
entries.english: So that herbs grew from the tears of his eyes: why he was inscribed in the scroll of la.

entries.farsi: تو قياسى گير طراريش را / كه چنان سرور كند زو ريش را
entries.english: Judge you of his impudent cheating from the fact that on account of him such a prince rends his beard.

entries.farsi: الحذر اى گل پرستان از شرش / ‏تيغ ال حولى زنيد اندر سرش
entries.english: Beware, O clay-worshippers, of his malice: smite the sword of la hawl on his head!

entries.farsi: كاو همى‏بيند شما را از كمين / ‏كه شما او را نمى‏بينيد هين
entries.english: For he sees you from ambush, so that you see him not. Take care!

entries.farsi: دايما صياد ريزد دانه‏ها / دانه پيدا باشد و پنهان دغا
entries.english: The fowler scatters grain incessantly: the grain is visible, but the deceit is hidden.

entries.farsi: هر كجا دانه بديدى الحذر / تا نبندد دام بر تو بال و پر
