entries.farsi: عكس هر كس را بدان اى جان ببين / ‏پهلوى جنسى كه خواهى مى‏نشين
entries.english: Know every one’s reflection: see, O my soul; ever sit beside the similar one whom you desire.

sections.title_fa: ًوجه عبرت گرفتن از اين حكايت و يقين دانستن كه إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْ رِ يُسْ را
sections.title_en: The right way of taking a lesson from this story and knowing with certainty that “verily, together with hardship there is ease”

entries.farsi: عبرت است آن قصه‏اى جان مر ترا / تا كه راضى باشى از حكم خدا
entries.english: That tale is a lesson to you, O my soul, to the end that you may acquiesce in the decree of God;

entries.farsi: تا كه زيرك باشى و نيكو گمان / ‏چون ببينى واقعه‏ى بد ناگهان
entries.english: So that you will be quick to understand and will have good thoughts when you see a calamity of a sudden.

entries.farsi: ديگران گردند زرد از بيم آن / ‏تو چو گل خندان گه سود و زيان
entries.english: Others turn pale from dread of it, you laughing in the hour of gain or loss, like the rose.

entries.farsi: ز انكه گل گر برگ برگش مى‏كنى / ‏خنده نگذارد نگردد منثنى
entries.english: Because the rose, though you tear it petal by petal, does not leave off laughing and does not become bent

entries.farsi: گويد از خارى چرا افتم به غم / ‏خنده را من خود ز خار آورده‏ام
entries.english: “Why,” it says, “should I fall into grief on account of a thorn? Indeed I have brought laughter by means of the thorn.”

entries.farsi: هر چه از تو ياوه گردد از قضا / تو يقين دان كه خريدت از بال
entries.english: Whatever by destiny becomes lost to you, know for sure that it has redeemed you from affliction.

entries.farsi: آن عقابش را عقابى دان كه او / در ربود آن موزه را ز آن نيك خو
entries.english: “What is Sufism?” He said, “To feel joy in the heart at the coming of sorrow.”

entries.farsi: ما التصوف قال وجدان الفرح / ‏في الفؤاد عند إتيان الترح
entries.english: Regard His chastisement as the eagle which carried off the boot from that man of excellent disposition,

entries.farsi: تا رهاند پاش را از زخم مار / اى خنك عقلى كه باشد بى‏غبار
entries.english: That she might save his foot from the serpent’s bite. Oh, happy is the understanding that is undimmed.

entries.farsi: گفت ال تأسوا عَلى‏ ما فاتكم / ‏إن أتى السرحان و أردى شاتكم
entries.english: He has said, “Grieve not for that which escapes you,” if the wolf come and destroy your sheep,

entries.farsi: كان بال دفع بالهاى بزرگ / ‏و آن زيان منع زيانهاى سترگ
entries.english: For that affliction keeps off great afflictions, and that loss prevents huge losses.

sections.title_fa: استدعاى آن مرد از موسى زبان بهايم با طيور
sections.title_en: How a certain man demanded of Moses the language of the beasts and birds.

entries.farsi: تا بود كز بانگ حيوانات و دد / عبرتى حاصل كنم در دين خود
entries.english: A young man said to Moses, “Teach me the language of the animals,

entries.farsi: گفت موسى را يكى مرد جوان / ‏كه بياموزم زبان جانوران
entries.english: That perchance from the voice of animals and wild beasts I may get a lesson concerning my religion.

entries.farsi: چون زبانهاى بنى آدم همه / ‏در پى آب است و نان و دمدمه
entries.english: Since the languages of the sons of Adam are entirely for the sake of water and bread and renown,

entries.farsi: بلكه حيوانات را دردى دگر / باشد از تدبير هنگام گذر
entries.english: It may be that the animals have a different care, that of taking thought for the hour of passing away.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى رو گذر كن زين هوس / ‏كاين خطر دارد بسى در پيش و پس
entries.english: “Depart,” said Moses; “abandon this vain desire, for this holds much danger before and behind.

entries.farsi: عبرت و بيدارى از يزدان طلب / ‏نه از كتاب و از مقال و حرف و لب
entries.english: Seek the lesson and the wakefulness from God, not from books and speech and words and lips.”

entries.farsi: گرم‏تر شد مرد ز آن منعش كه كرد / گرم‏تر گردد همى از منع مرد
entries.english: The man became more eager in consequence of the refusal which he made to him: a man always becomes more eager from being refused.

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى چو نور تو بتافت / ‏هر چه چيزى بود چيزى از تو يافت
entries.english: He said, “O Moses, since your light has shone forth, whatever was a thing has gained being from you.

entries.farsi: اين زمان قايم مقام حق توى / ‏ياس باشد گر مرا مانع شوى
entries.english: It is not worthy of your bounty, O generous one, to disappoint me of this object of desire.

entries.farsi: مر مرا محروم كردن زين مراد / اليق لطفت نباشد اى جواد
entries.english: At this time you are the vicegerent of God: it will be despair if you prevent me.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى يا رب اين مرد سليم / ‏سخره كرده‏ستش مگر ديو رجيم
entries.english: Moses said, “O Lord, surely the stoned Devil has subdued this simple man.

entries.farsi: گر بياموزم زيان كارش بود / ور نياموزم دلش بد مى‏شود
entries.english: If I teach him, it will be harmful to him; and if I do not teach him, he will become faint-hearted.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى موسى بياموزش كه ما / رد نكرديم از كرم هرگز دعا
entries.english: He said, “Teach him, O Moses, for We in our loving-kindness never have rejected prayer.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب او پشيمانى خورد / دست خايد جامه‏ها را بر درد
entries.english: He said, “O Lord, he will feel repentance and gnaw his hands and rend his garments.

entries.farsi: نيست قدرت هر كسى را سازوار / عجز بهتر مايه‏ى پرهيزكار
entries.english: Power is not suitable to every one: weakness is the best stock-in-trade for the devout.”

entries.farsi: فقر از اين رو فخر آمد جاودان / ‏كه به تقوى ماند دست نارسان
entries.english: For this reason poverty is everlasting glory, since the hand that cannot reach is left with fear of God.

entries.farsi: ز آن غنا و ز آن غنى مردود شد / كه ز قدرت صبرها بدرود شد
entries.english: Riches and the rich are spurned because acts of self-denial are relinquished by power.

entries.farsi: آدمى را عجز و فقر آمد امان / ‏از بالى نفس پر حرص و غمان
entries.english: Weakness and poverty are security for a man against the tribulation of the covetous and anxious soul.

entries.farsi: آن غم آمد ز آرزوهاى فضول / ‏كه بدان خو كرده است آن صيد غول
entries.english: That anxiety arises from the wanton desires to which that prey to the ghoul has become habituated.

entries.farsi: آرزوى گل بود گل خواره را / گل شكر نگوارد آن بى‏چاره را
entries.english: The eater of clay has a desire for clay: rose-flavoured sugar is indigestible for that wretched man.

sections.title_fa: ‏وحى آمدن از حق تعالى به موسى كه بياموزش چيزى كه استدعا مى‏كند يا بعضى از آن
sections.title_en: How Revelation came from God most High to Moses that he should teach him the thing demanded by him, or part of it.

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان تو بده بايست او / بر گشا در اختيار آن دست او
entries.english: God said, “Do you grant his need: let him have a free hand to choose.”

entries.farsi: اختيار آمد عبادت را نمك / ‏ور نه مى‏گردد به ناخواه اين فلك
entries.english: Choice is the salt of devotion; otherwise: this celestial sphere revolves involuntarily;

entries.farsi: گردش او را نه اجر و نه عقاب / ‏كه اختيار آمد هنر وقت حساب
entries.english: Its revolution has neither reward nor punishment, for free-will is a merit at the time of the Reckoning.

entries.farsi: جمله عالم خود مسبح آمدند / نيست آن تسبيح جبرى مزدمند
entries.english: All created beings indeed are glorifiers, that compulsory glorification is not wage-earning.

entries.farsi: تيغ در دستش نه از عجزش بكن / تا كه غازى گردد او يا راه زن
entries.english: “Put a sword in his hand; pull him away from weakness, so that he may become a holy warrior or a brigand,

entries.farsi: ز انكه كَرَّمْنا شد آدم ز اختيار / نيم زنبور عسل شد نيم مار
entries.english: Because We have honoured Man by free-will: half is honeybee, half is snake.”

entries.farsi: مومنان كان عسل زنبوروار / كافران خود كان زهرى همچو مار
entries.english: The true believers are a store of honey, like the bee; the infidels, in truth, are a store of poison, like the snake,

entries.farsi: باز كافر خورد شربت از صديد / هم ز قوتش زهر شد در وى پديد
entries.english: Because the true believer ate choice herbs, so that, like a bee, his spittle became life;

entries.farsi: ز انكه مومن خورد بگزيده نبات / ‏تا چو نحلى گشت ريق او حيات
entries.english: Again, the infidel drank sherbet of filthy water: accordingly from his nourishment poison appeared in him.

entries.farsi: اهل الهام خدا عين الحيات / ‏اهل تسويل هوا سم الممات
entries.english: Those inspired by God are the fountain of life; those allured by the enticements of sensuality are the poison of death.

entries.farsi: در جهان اين مدح و شاباش و زهى / ‏ز اختيار است و حفاظ آگهى
entries.english: In the world this praise and “well done!” and “bravo!” are in virtue of free-will and watchful attention.

entries.farsi: جمله رندان چون كه در زندان بوند / متقى و زاهد و حق خوان شوند
entries.english: All profligates, when they are in prison, become devout and ascetic and invokers of God.

entries.farsi: چون كه قدرت رفت كاسد شد عمل / ‏هين كه تا سرمايه نستاند اجل
entries.english: When the power is gone, the work becomes unsalable. Take heed lest Doom seize the capital.

entries.farsi: قدرتت سرمايه‏ى سود است هين / ‏وقت قدرت را نگه دار و ببين
entries.english: The power is your profit-earning capital. Listen, watch over the moment of power and observe!

entries.farsi: آدمى بر خنگ كَرَّمْنا سوار / در كف دركش عنان اختيار
entries.english: Man rides on the steed of “We have honoured”: the reins of free-will are in the hand of his intelligence.

entries.farsi: باز موسى داد پند او را به مهر / كه مرادت زرد خواهد كرد چهر
entries.english: Once more did Moses admonish him kindly, saying, “The thing you desire will make your face pale.

entries.farsi: ترك اين سودا بگو و ز حق بترس / ‏ديو داده ستت براى مكر درس
entries.english: Abandon this vain passion and be afraid of God: the Devil has schooled you for the purpose of deception.”

sections.title_fa: ‏قانع شدن آن طالب به تعليم زبان مرغ خانگى و سگ و اجابت موسى عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How that seeker was content to be taught the language of domestic fowls and dogs, and how Moses, on whom be peace; complied with his request.

entries.farsi: گفت بارى نطق سگ كاو بر در است / ‏نطق مرغ خانگى كه اهل پر است
entries.english: He said, “At any rate the language of the dog which is at the door and the language of the domestic fowl which has wings.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى هين تو دانى رو رسيد / نطق اين هر دو شود بر تو پديد
entries.english: “Listen,” said Moses, “you know! Go, it has arrived: the language of both of these will be revealed to you.”

entries.farsi: بامدادان از براى امتحان / ‏ايستاد او منتظر بر آستان
entries.english: At daybreak, in order to make trial, he stood waiting on the threshold.

entries.farsi: خادمه سفره بيفشاند و فتاد / پاره‏اى نان بيات آثار زاد
entries.english: The maid-servant shook the table-cloth, and a piece of bread, the remnants of last night’s meal, fell out.

entries.farsi: در ربود آن را خروسى چون گرو / گفت سگ كردى تو بر ما ظلم رو
entries.english: A cock snatched it up as the stake. The dog said, “You have done injustice-to me. Leave!

entries.farsi: دانه‏ى گندم توانى خورد و من / ‏عاجزم در دانه خوردن در وطن
entries.english: You can eat a grain of corn, while I am unable to eat grains in my abode.

entries.farsi: گندم و جو را و باقى حبوب / ‏مى‏توانى خورد و من نه اى طروب
entries.english: You can eat corn and barley and the rest of the grains, while I cannot, O jubilant one.

entries.farsi: اين لب نانى كه قسم ماست نان / مى‏ربايى اين قدر را از سگا
entries.english: This crust of bread, the bread which is our portion— you are taking away from the dogs such a quantity as this!”

sections.title_fa: جواب خروس سگ را
sections.title_en: The cock’s answer to the dog

entries.farsi: پس خروسش گفت تن زن غم مخور / كه خدا بدهد عوض ز اينت دگر
entries.english: Then the cock said to him, “Be silent, do not grieve, for God will give you something else instead of this.

entries.farsi: اسب اين خواجه سقط خواهد شدن / ‏روز فردا سير خور كم كن حزن
entries.english: The horse of this Khwaja is about to die: to-morrow eat your fill and be not sorrowful.

entries.farsi: اسب را بفروخت چون بشنيد مرد / پيش سگ شد آن خروسش روى زرد
entries.english: The horse’s death will be a feast-day for the dogs: there will be abundant provender without toil or earning.”

entries.farsi: مر سگان را عيد باشد مرگ اسب / ‏روزى وافر بود بى‏جهد و كسب
entries.english: When the man heard, he sold the horse. That cock of his was disgraced in the eyes of the dog.

entries.farsi: روز ديگر همچنان نان را ربود / آن خروس و سگ بر او لب بر گشود
entries.english: Next day the cock carried off the bread in the same fashion, and the dog opened his lips at him,

entries.farsi: اسب كش گفتى سقط گردد كجاست / ‏كور اختر گوى و محرومى ز راست
entries.english: Saying, “O beguiling cock, how long these lies? You are unrighteous and false and without lustre.

entries.farsi: كاى خروس عشوه‏ده چند اين دروغ / ‏ظالمى و كاذبى و بى‏فروغ
entries.english: Where is the horse that you said would die? You are a blind man who tells of the stars and you are deprived of truth.”

entries.farsi: گفت او را آن خروس با خبر / كه سقط شد اسب او جاى دگر
entries.english: That knowing cock said to him, “His horse died in another place.

entries.farsi: اسب را بفروخت و جست او از زيان / ‏آن زيان انداخت او بر ديگران
entries.english: He sold the horse and escaped from loss: he cast the loss upon others;

entries.farsi: زود استر را فروشيد آن حريص / ‏يافت از غم و ز زيان آن دم محيص
entries.english: But to-morrow his mule will die: that will be good fortune for the dogs, no more.”

entries.farsi: ليك فردا استرش گردد سقط / مر سگان را باشد آن نعمت فقط
entries.english: The covetous man immediately sold the mule and at that instant obtained deliverance from grief and loss.

entries.farsi: روز ثالث گفت سگ با آن خروس / ‏اى امير كاذبان با طبل و كوس
entries.english: On the third day the dog said to the cock, “O prince of liars with your drums and kettledrums!”

entries.farsi: گفت او بفروخت استر را شتاب / ‏گفت فردايش غالم آيد مصاب
entries.english: He said, “He sold the mule in haste,” said he, “to-morrow his slave will be stricken down,

entries.farsi: اين شنيد و آن غالمش را فروخت / رست از خسران و رخ را بر فروخت
entries.english: And when his slave dies, the next of kin will scatter pieces of bread upon the dogs and beggars.”

entries.farsi: چون غالم او بميرد نانها / بر سگ و خواهنده ريزند اقربا
entries.english: He heard this and sold his slave: he was saved from loss and his face was lit up.

entries.farsi: شكرها مى‏كرد و شاديها كه من / ‏رستم از سه واقعه اندر زمن
entries.english: He was giving thanks and making merry, saying, “I have been saved from three calamities in the world.

entries.farsi: تا زبان مرغ و سگ آموختم / ‏ديده‏ى سوء القضاء را دوختم
entries.english: Since I learned the language of the fowl and the dog I have pierced the eye of evil destiny.”

entries.farsi: روز ديگر آن سگ محروم گفت / ‏كاى خروس ژاژخا كو طاق و جفت
entries.english: Next day the disappointed dog said, “O stupid cock, where are the sundries?

sections.title_fa: خجل گشتن خروس پيش سگ به سبب دروغ شدن در آن سه وعده
sections.title_en: How the cock became abashed before the dog on account of being false in those three promises.

entries.farsi: چند چند آخر دروغ و مكر تو / خود نپرد جز دروغ از وكر تو
entries.english: How long, pray, how long your falsehood and deceit? Truly, nothing but falsehood flies out of your nest.”

entries.farsi: گفت حاشا از من و از جنس من / ‏كه بگرديم از دروغى ممتحن
entries.english: He said, “Far be it from me and from my kind that we should become afflicted with falsehood.

entries.farsi: ما خروسان چون موذن راست گوى / ‏هم رقيب آفتاب و وقت جوى
entries.english: We cocks are veracious like the muezzin: we are observers of the sun as well as seekers of the time.

entries.farsi: پاسبان آفتابيم از درون / ‏گر كنى بالاى ما طشتى نگون
entries.english: We are watchers of the sun inwardly, though you may turn a basin upside down over us.”

entries.farsi: پاسبان آفتابند اوليا / در بشر واقف ز اسرار خدا
entries.english: The watchers of the Sun are the saints: in the flesh acquainted with the Divine mysteries.

entries.farsi: اصل ما را حق پى بانگ نماز / داد هديه آدمى را در جهاز
entries.english: “God gave our family as a gift to Man to call to the ritual prayer in preparation.

entries.farsi: گر به ناهنگام سهوى‏مان رود / در اذان آن مقتل ما مى‏شود
entries.english: If a mistake be committed by us in the call to prayer at the wrong time, it will become the cause of our being killed.

entries.farsi: گفت ناهنگام حى على الفالح / ‏خون ما را مى‏كند خوار و مباح
entries.english: To say at the wrong time ‘Come to welfare’ will make our blood of no account and licit.”

entries.farsi: آن كه معصوم آمد و پاك از غلط / آن خروس جان وحى آمد فقط
entries.english: It is only the cock, the Soul of inspiration that is protected and purged of error.

entries.farsi: آن غالمش مرد پيش مشترى / ‏شد زيان مشترى آن يك سرى
entries.english: His slave died in the house of the purchaser: that was the purchaser’s loss entirely.

entries.farsi: او گريزانيد مالش را و ليك / ‏خون خود را ريخت اندر ياب نيك
entries.english: He saved his money, but he shed his own blood. Understand well!

entries.farsi: يك زيان دفع زيانها مى‏شدى / ‏جسم و مال ماست جانها را فدى
entries.english: One loss would have prevented losses: our bodies and money are the ransom for our souls.

entries.farsi: پيش شاهان در سياست گسترى / ‏مى‏دهى تو مال و سر را مى‏خرى
entries.english: In the presence of kings, in dispensing punishment, you offer money and purchase your head:

entries.farsi: اعجمى چون گشته‏اى اندر قضا / مى‏گريزانى ز داور مال را
entries.english: How have you become, in destiny, a miser— withholding your money from the Judge?

sections.title_fa: ‏خبر كردن خروس از مرگ خواجه
sections.title_en: How the cock foretold the death of the Khwaja.

entries.farsi: ليك فردا خواهد او مردن يقين / گاو خواهد كشت وارث در حنين
entries.english: “But to-morrow he will certainly die: his heir, in mourning, will slaughter a cow.

entries.farsi: صاحب خانه بخواهد مرد و رفت / ‏روز فردا نك رسيدت لوت زفت
entries.english: The owner of the house will die depart: lo, to-morrow a great deal of food will reach you.

entries.farsi: پاره‏هاى نان و اللنگ و طعام / ‏در ميان كوى يابد خاص و عام
entries.english: High and low will get pieces of bread and dainty morsels and viands in the midst of the street.

entries.farsi: گاو قربانى و نانهاى تنك / ‏بر سگان و سايالن ريزد سبك
entries.english: The sacrificed cow and thin loaves of bread will be scattered quickly over the dogs and the beggars.”

entries.farsi: از زيان مال و درد آن گريخت / ‏مال افزون كرد و خون خويش ريخت
entries.english: The death of the horse and mule and the death of the slave were bringing round the doom of this foolish deluded man.

entries.farsi: مرگ اسب و استر و مرگ غالم / ‏بد قضا گردان اين مغرور خام
entries.english: He fled from the loss of wealth and from grief thereat: he increased his wealth and shed his own blood.

entries.farsi: اين رياضتهاى درويشان چراست / ‏كان بال بر تن بقاى جانهاست
entries.english: These austerities of dervishes—what are they for? That that tribulation on the body is the everlasting life of spirits

entries.farsi: تا بقاى خود نيابد سالكى / ‏چون كند تن را سقيم و هالكى
entries.english: Unless a traveller gains the everlasting life of his self, how should he make his body a sick and perishing?

entries.farsi: آن كه بدهد بى‏اميد سودها / آن خداى است آن خداى است آن خدا
entries.english: How should he move his hand to altruism and work unless he sees his soul in exchange for what is given (by him)?

entries.farsi: دست كى جنبد به ايثار و عمل / ‏تا نبيند داده را جانش بدل
entries.english: That one who gives without expectation of gains— that one is God, is God, is God,

entries.farsi: يا ولى حق كه خوى حق گرفت / ‏نور گشت و تابش مطلق گرفت
entries.english: Or the friend of God, who has assumed the nature of God and has become luminous and has received the Absolute Radiance;

entries.farsi: كاو غنى است و جز او جمله فقير / كى فقيرى بى‏عوض گويد كه گير
entries.english: For He is rich, while all except Him are poor: how should a poor man say “Take” without compensation?

entries.farsi: تا نبيند كودكى كه سيب هست / ‏او پياز گنده را ندهد ز دست
entries.english: Till a child sees that the apple is there, it will not give up from its hand the stinking onion.

entries.farsi: اين همه بازار بهر اين غرض / ‏بر دكانها شسته بر بوى عوض
entries.english: All these market-folk, for the sake of this object, are seated on the benches in the hope of compensation:

entries.farsi: صد متاع خوب عرضه مى‏كنند / و اندرون دل عوضها مى‏تنند
entries.english: They offer a hundred fine articles of merchandise, and within their hearts they are intent on compensations.

entries.farsi: يك سالمى نشنوى اى مرد دين / ‏كه نگيرد آخر آن آستين
entries.english: O man of the Religion, you will not hear a single salaam whereof the end will not pluck your sleeve.

entries.farsi: بى‏طمع نشنيده‏ام از خاص و عام / ‏من سالمى اى برادر و السلام
entries.english: I have never heard a disinterested salaam from high or low, O brother—and the salaam—

entries.farsi: جز سالم حق، هين آن را بجو / خانه خانه جا به جا و كو به كو
entries.english: Except the salaam of God. Come; seek that from house to house, from place to place, and from street to street!

entries.farsi: از دهان آدمى خوش مشام / ‏هم پيام حق شنودم هم سالم
entries.english: From the mouth of the man who has a good scent I heard both the message and the salaam of God;

entries.farsi: وين سالم باقيان بر بوى آن / ‏من همى‏نوشم به دل خوشتر ز جان
entries.english: And in the hope of that I am listening with my heart to the salaams of the rest sweeter than life.

entries.farsi: ز آن سالم او سالم حق شده ست / ‏كاتش اندر دودمان خود زده ست
entries.english: His salaam has become the salaam of God because he has set fire to the household of self.

entries.farsi: مرده است از خود شده زنده به رب / ‏ز آن بود اسرار حقش در دو لب
entries.english: He has died to self and become living through the Lord: hence the mysteries of God are on his lips.

entries.farsi: مردن تن در رياضت زندگى است / ‏رنج اين تن روح را پايندگى است
entries.english: The death of the body in self-discipline is life: the sufferings of this body are everlastingness to the spirit.

entries.farsi: گوش بنهاده بد آن مرد خبيث / ‏مى‏شنود او از خروسش آن حديث
entries.english: That wicked man had lent ear: he was hearing from his cock the news.

sections.title_fa: دويدن آن شخص به سوى موسى به زنهار چون از خروس خبر مرگ خود شنيد
sections.title_en: How that person ran to Moses for protection when he heard from the cock the announcement of his death.

entries.farsi: رو همى‏ماليد در خاك او ز بيم / ‏كه مرا فرياد رس زين اى كليم
entries.english: When he heard these things, he started running in hot haste: he went to the door of Moses, with whom God conversed.

entries.farsi: چون شنيد اينها دوان شد تيز و تفت / ‏بر در موسى كليم اللهَّ رفت
entries.english: He was rubbing his face in the dust from fear, saying, “Save me from this, O Kalím!”

entries.farsi: گفت رو بفروش خود را و بره / ‏چون كه استا گشته‏اى برجه ز چه
entries.english: He said, “Go, sell yourself and escape! Since you have become expert, jump out of the pit!

entries.farsi: بر مسلمانان زيان انداز تو / كيسه و هميانها را كن دو تو
entries.english: Throw the loss upon true believers! Make your purses and wallets double!

entries.farsi: من درون خشت ديدم اين قضا / كه در آيينه عيان شد مر ترا
entries.english: I beheld in the brick this destiny which to you became visible in the mirror.

entries.farsi: باز زارى كرد كاى نيكو خصال / ‏مر مرا در سر مزن در رو ممال
entries.english: The intelligent man sees with his heart the end at the first; he that is lacking in knowledge sees it at the end.”

entries.farsi: عاقل اول بيند آخر را به دل / ‏اندر آخر بيند از دانش مقل
entries.english: Once more he made lamentation, saying, “O you who have goodly qualities, do not beat me on the head, do not rub into my face.

entries.farsi: از من آن آمد كه بودم ناسزا / ناسزايم را تو ده حسن الجزا
entries.english: That issued from me because I was unworthy: do you give good recompense to my unworthy.”

entries.farsi: گفت تيرى جست از شست اى پسر / نيست سنت كايد آن واپس به سر
entries.english: He said, “An arrow sped from the thumb-stall, my lad: it is not the rule that it should come back to the source;

entries.farsi: ليك در خواهم ز نيكو داورى / ‏تا كه ايمان آن زمان با خود برى
entries.english: But I will crave of good dispensation that you may take the Faith away with you at that time.

entries.farsi: چون كه ايمان برده باشى زنده‏اى / ‏چون كه با ايمان روى پاينده‏اى
entries.english: When you have taken the Faith away, you are living: when you go with the Faith, you are enduring.”

entries.farsi: هم در آن دم حال بر خواجه بگشت / ‏تا دلش شوريد و آوردند طشت
entries.english: At the same instant the Khwaja became indisposed, so that he felt qualms, and they brought the basin.

entries.farsi: شورش مرگ است نه هيضه‏ى طعام / ‏قى چه سودت دارد اى بد بخت خام
entries.english: It is the qualms of death, not indigestion: how should vomiting avail you, O foolish ill-fortuned man?

entries.farsi: چار كس بردند تا سوى وثاق / ‏ساق مى‏ماليد او بر پشت ساق
entries.english: Four persons carried him to his house: he was rubbing leg on the back of leg.

entries.farsi: پند موسى نشنوى شوخى كنى / ‏خويشتن بر تيغ پوالدى زنى
entries.english: If you do not listen to Moses’s counsel and are disrespectful, you dash yourself against a sword of steel.

entries.farsi: شرم نايد تيغ را از جان تو / آن تست اين اى برادر آن تو
entries.english: The sword feels no shame from your life: this is your own, O brother, your own.

sections.title_fa: دعاكردن موسى آن شخص را تا به ايمان رود از دنيا
sections.title_en: How Moses prayed for that person, that he might depart from the world in the Faith.

entries.farsi: موسى آمد در مناجات آن سحر / كاى خدا ايمان از او مستان مبر
entries.english: At dawn Moses began praying, saying, “O God, do not take the Faith from him, do not carry it away!

entries.farsi: پادشاهى كن بر او بخشا كه او / سهو كرد و خيره رويى و غلو
entries.english: Act in royal fashion, forgive him, for he has erred and behaved with impudence and transgressed exceedingly.

entries.farsi: دست را بر اژدها آن كس زند / كه عصا را دستش اژدرها كند
entries.english: I said to him, ‘This knowledge is not meet for you, he deemed my words a thwarting and vain.”

entries.farsi: گفتمش اين علم نه در خورد تست / ‏دفع پنداريد گفتم را و سست
entries.english: That one lays hands on the dragon whose hand makes the rod a dragon.

entries.farsi: سر غيب آن را سزد آموختن / ‏كه ز گفتن لب تواند دوختن
entries.english: To learn the secret of the Unseen is fitting for him who can seal his lips from speech.

entries.farsi: او به دريا رفت و مرغ آبى نبود / گشت غرقه دست گيرش اى ودود
entries.english: None but the water-fowl is proper for the sea. Understand—and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: در خور دريا نشد جز مرغ آب / ‏فهم كن و اهلل أعلم بالصواب
entries.english: “He went into the sea, and he was not a water-fowl: he sank. Take his hand, O Loving One!”

sections.title_fa: ‏اجابت كردن حق تعالى دعاى موسى را عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How God most High answered favourably the prayer of Moses, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: گفت بخشيدم بدو ايمان نعم / ‏ور تو خواهى اين زمان زنده‏ش كنم
entries.english: He said, “Yes, I bestow the Faith upon him, and if you wish I will bring him to life at this moment;

entries.farsi: بلكه جمله‏ى مردگان خاك را / اين زمان زنده كنم بهر ترا
entries.english: No, I will at this moment bring to life all the dead in the earth for your sake.”

entries.farsi: گفت موسى اين جهان مردن است / ‏آن جهان انگيز كانجا روشن است
entries.english: Moses said, “This is the world of dying: raise that world, for that place is resplendent.

entries.farsi: اين فنا جا چون جهان بود نيست / ‏باز گشت عاريت بس سود نيست
entries.english: Inasmuch as this abode of mortality is not the world of Being, the return to a borrowed thing is not much gain.

entries.farsi: رحمتى افشان بر ايشان هم كنون / ‏در نهان خانه‏ى لَدَيْنا محضرون
entries.english: Strew a gift of mercy upon them even now in the secret chamber of assembled in Our presence.”

entries.farsi: تا بدانى كه زيان جسم و مال / ‏سود جان باشد رهاند از وبال
entries.english: That you may know that loss of the body and of wealth is gain to the spirit and delivers it from bane.

entries.farsi: پس رياضت را به جان شو مشترى / ‏چون سپردى تن به خدمت جان برى
entries.english: Therefore be a purchaser of discipline with your soul: you will save your soul when you have given up your body to service.

entries.farsi: ور رياضت آيدت بى‏اختيار / سر بنه شكرانه ده اى كاميار
entries.english: And if the discipline comes to you without free choice, bow your head and give thanks, O successful one.

entries.farsi: چون حقت داد آن رياضت شكر كن / ‏تو نكردى او كشيدت ز امر كن
entries.english: Since God has given you that discipline, render thanks: you have not done; He has drawn you by the command, “Be!”

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن زنى كه فرزندش نمى‏زيست بناليد جواب آمد كه آن عوض رياضت تست و به جاى جهاد مجاهدان است ترا
sections.title_en: Story of the woman whose children never lived, and how, when she made lamentation, the answer came—“That is instead of your ascetic discipline and is for you in lieu of the self-mortification of those who mortify themselves.”

entries.farsi: آن زنى هر سال زاييدى پسر / بيش از شش مه نبودى عمرور
entries.english: That woman used to bear a son every year; he never lived more than six months;

entries.farsi: يا سه مه يا چار مه گشتى تباه / ‏ناله كرد آن زن كه افغان اى اله
entries.english: Either three months or four months he would perish. The woman made lamentation, crying, “Alas, O God,

entries.farsi: نه مهم بار است و سه ماهم فرح / ‏نعمتم زوتر رو از قوس قزح
entries.english: For nine months I have the burden, and for three months I have joy: my happiness is fleeter than the rainbow.”

entries.farsi: پيش مردان خدا كردى نفير / زين شكايت آن زن از درد نذير
entries.english: That woman, because of the terrifying anguish, used to make this plaintive outcry before the men of God.

entries.farsi: بيست فرزند اين چنين در گور رفت / ‏آتشى در جانشان افتاد تفت
entries.english: In this way twenty children went into the grave: a fire fell swiftly upon their lives,

entries.farsi: تا شبى بنمود او را جنتى / ‏باقيى سبزى خوشى بى‏ضنتى
entries.english: One night, there was shown to her a garden everlasting, verdant, delectable, and ungrudged.

entries.farsi: باغ گفتم نعمت بى‏كيف را / كاصل نعمتهاست و مجمع باغها
entries.english: I have called the Unconditioned Bounty a garden, since it is the source of bounties and the assembly of gardens;

entries.farsi: ور نه ال عين رأت چه جاى باغ / ‏گفت نور غيب را يزدان چراغ
entries.english: Otherwise, no eye has beheld: what place is there for a garden? God has called the Light of the Unseen “a lamp.”

entries.farsi: حاصل آن زن ديد آن را مست شد / ز آن تجلى آن ضعيف از دست شد
entries.english: It’s not a comparison; it is a parable thereof, in order that he who is bewildered may get a scent.

entries.farsi: مثل نبود آن مثال آن بود / تا برد بوى آن كه او حيران بود
entries.english: In short, the woman saw that and became intoxicated: at that revelation the weak fell into an ecstasy.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتند كاين نعمت و راست / ‏كاو به جان بازى بجز صادق نخاست
entries.english: She saw her name written on a palace: she who was of goodly belief knew that it belonged to her.

entries.farsi: ديد در قصرى نوشته نام خويش / ‏آن خود دانستش آن محبوب كيش
entries.english: After that, they said, “This Bounty is for him who has risen up with constant sincerity in self-devotion.

entries.farsi: خدمت بسيار مى‏بايست كرد / مر ترا تا بر خورى زين چاشت خورد
entries.english: You must have done much service, in order that you might partake of this repast;

entries.farsi: چون تو كاهل بودى اندر التجا / آن مصيبتها عوض دادت خدا
entries.english: As you were remiss in taking refuge, God gave you those afflictions instead.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب تا به صد سال و فزون / ‏اين چنينم ده بريز از من تو خون
entries.english: “O Lord,” cried she, “give me such-like for a hundred years and more! Shed my blood!”

entries.farsi: اندر آن باغ او چو آمد پيش پيش / ‏ديد در وى جمله فرزندان خويش
entries.english: When she advanced into that garden, she saw there all her children.

entries.farsi: گفت از من گم شد از تو گم نشد / بى‏دو چشم غيب كس مردم نشد
entries.english: She said, “They were lost to me, they were not lost to You.” Without the two eyes of the Unseen, no one becomes the Man.

entries.farsi: تو نكردى قصد و از بينى دويد / خون افزون تا ز تب جانت رهيد
entries.english: You did not let blood, and the superfluous blood ran from your nose, to the end that your life might be saved from fever.

entries.farsi: مغز هر ميوه به است از پوستش / ‏پوست دان تن را و مغز آن دوستش
entries.english: The core of every fruit is better than its rind: deem the body to be the rind, and its friend to be the core.

entries.farsi: مغز نغزى دارد آخر آدمى / ‏يك دمى آن را طلب گر ز آن دمى
entries.english: After all, Man has a goodly core: seek it for one moment, if you are of that breath.

sections.title_fa: ‏در آمدن حمزه در جنگ بى‏زره
sections.title_en: How Hamza, May God be well-pleased with him, came to battle without a coat of mail.

entries.farsi: اندر آخر حمزه چون در صف شدى / ‏بى‏زره سر مست در غزو آمدى
entries.english: Whenever at the end Hamza went into the ranks, he would enter the fray intoxicated, without a coat of mail.

entries.farsi: سينه باز و تن برهنه پيش پيش / ‏در فكندى در صف شمشير خويش
entries.english: Advancing with open breast and naked body, he would throw himself into the sword-bearing ranks.

entries.farsi: خلق پرسيدند كاى عم رسول / ‏اى هژبر صف شكن شاه فحول
entries.english: The people asked him, saying, “O uncle of the Prophet, O Lion that breaks the ranks, O prince of the champions,

entries.farsi: نه تو ال تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إلى / تهلكه خواندى ز پيغام خدا
entries.english: Have not you read in the Message of God ‘Do not cast yourselves with your own hands into destruction’?

entries.farsi: پس چرا تو خويش را در تهلكه / ‏مى‏دراندازى چنين در معركه
entries.english: Then why are you casting yourself thus into destruction on the field of battle?

entries.farsi: چون جوان بودى و زفت و سخت زه / ‏تو نمى‏رفتى سوى صف بى‏زره
entries.english: When you were young and robust and strongly-knit, you did not go into the battle-line without a coat of mail.

entries.farsi: الابالى‏وار با تيغ و سنان / ‏مى‏نمايى دار و گير و امتحان
entries.english: Now that you have become old and infirm and bent, you are knocking at the curtains of recklessness,

entries.farsi: چون شدى پير و ضعيف و منحنى / ‏پرده‏هاى الابالى مى‏زنى
entries.english: And with sword and spear, like one who heeds nothing, you are grappling and struggling and making trial.

entries.farsi: تيغ حرمت مى‏ندارد پير را / كى بود تمييز تيغ و تير را
entries.english: The sword has no respect for the old: how should sword and arrow possess discernment?”

entries.farsi: زين نسق غم خوارگان بى‏خبر / پند مى‏دادند او را از غير
entries.english: In this manner were the ignorant sympathisers giving him counsel zealously.

sections.title_fa: جواب حمزه مر خلق را
sections.title_en: The reply of Hamza to the people

entries.farsi: گفت حمزه چون كه بودم من جوان / ‏مرگ مى‏ديدم وداع اين جهان
entries.english: Hamza said, “When I was young, I used to regard farewell to this world as death.

entries.farsi: سوى مردن كس به رغبت كى رود / پيش اژدرها برهنه كى شود
entries.english: How should any one go to death eagerly? How should he come naked to meet the dragon?

entries.farsi: ليك از نور محمد من كنون / ‏نيستم اين شهر فانى را زبون
entries.english: But now, through the Light of Mohammed, I am not subject to this city that is passing away.

entries.farsi: از برون حس لشكرگاه شاه / ‏پر همى‏بينم ز نور حق سپاه
entries.english: Beyond the senses, I behold the camp of the King thronged with the army of the Light of God,

entries.farsi: خيمه در خيمه طناب اندر طناب / ‏شكر آن كه كرد بيدارم ز خواب
entries.english: Tent on tent and tent-rope on tent-rope. Thanks be to Him who awakened me from slumber!”

entries.farsi: آن كه مردن پيش چشمش تهلكه ست / ‏امر ال تُلْقُوا بگيرد او به دست
entries.english: That one in whose eyes death is destruction— he takes hold of the command, “Do not cast”;

entries.farsi: و انكه مردن پيش او شد فتح باب / ‏سارِعُوا آيد مر او را در خطاب
entries.english: And that one to whom death is the opening of the gate— for him in the Allocution there is, “Vie ye with each other in hastening.”

entries.farsi: الحذر اى مرگ بينان بارعوا / العجل اى حشر بينان سارعوا
entries.english: Beware, O you who regard death! Surpass one another! Quick, O you who regard the Resurrection! Vie with each other in hastening!

entries.farsi: الصال اى لطف‏بينان افرحوا / البال اى قهر بينان اترحوا
entries.english: Welcome, O you who regard the grace! Rejoice! Woe, O you who regard the wrath! Be sorrowful!

entries.farsi: هر كه يوسف ديد جان كردش فدى / ‏هر كه گرگش ديد برگشت از هدى
entries.english: Whosoever deems death to be Joseph gives up his soul in ransom for it; whosoever deems it to be the wolf turns back from right guidance.

entries.farsi: مرگ هر يك اى پسر هم رنگ اوست / ‏پيش دشمن دشمن و بر دوست دوست
entries.english: Every one’s death is of the same quality as himself, my lad: to the enemy an enemy, and to the friend a friend.

entries.farsi: آن كه مى‏ترسى ز مرگ اندر فرار / آن ز خود ترسانى اى جان هوش دار
entries.english: In the eyes of the Turcoman the mirror has a fair colour; similarly in the eyes of the Ethiopian the mirror is an Ethiopian.

entries.farsi: پيش ترك آيينه را خوش رنگى است / ‏پيش زنگى آينه هم زنگى است
entries.english: Your fear of death in fleeing is your fear of yourself. Take heed, O soul!

entries.farsi: روى زشت تست نه رخسار مرگ / ‏جان تو همچون درخت و مرگ برگ
entries.english: It’s your ugly face, not the visage of Death: your spirit is like the tree and death the leaf.

entries.farsi: از تو رسته ست ار نكوى است ار بد است / ‏ناخوش و خوش هر ضميرت از خود است
entries.english: It has grown from you, whether it is good or evil: every hidden thought of yours, foul or fair, is from yourself.

entries.farsi: گر به خارى خسته‏اى خود كشته‏اى / ‏ور حرير و قز درى خود رشته‏اى
entries.english: If you are wounded by a thorn, you yourself have sown; and if you are in satin and silk, you yourself have spun.

entries.farsi: دان كه نبود فعل هم رنگ جزا / هيچ خدمت نيست هم رنگ عطا
entries.english: Know that the act is not of the same complexion as the requital: the service is nowise of the same complexion as the payment given.

entries.farsi: مزد مزدوران نمى‏ماند به كار / كان عرض وين جوهر است و پايدار
entries.english: The labourers’ wage does not resemble the work, inasmuch as the latter is the accident, while the former is the substance and permanent.

entries.farsi: آن همه سختى و زور است و عرق / ‏وين همه سيم است و زر است و طبق
entries.english: The former is wholly hardship and effort and sweat, while the latter is wholly silver and gold and trays.

entries.farsi: گر ترا آيد ز جايى تهمتى / ‏كرد مظلوميت دعا در محنتى
entries.english: If suspicion fall upon you from some quarter, the person whom you wronged has invoked God against you in an affliction

entries.farsi: تو همى‏گويى كه من آزاده‏ام / ‏بر كسى من تهمتى ننهاده‏ام
entries.english: You say, “I am free: I have not laid suspicion on any one.”

entries.farsi: تو گناهى كرده‏اى شكل دگر / دانه كشتى دانه كى ماند به بر
entries.english: You have committed another form of sin; you sowed the seed: how should the seed resemble the fruit?

entries.farsi: او زنا كرد و جزا صد چوب بود / گويد او من كى زدم كس را به عود
entries.english: The celibate committed adultery, and the penalty was a hundred blows with the stick. “When,” says he, “did I strike any one with wood?”

entries.farsi: نه جزاى آن زنا بود اين بال / چوب كى ماند ز نارا در خال
entries.english: Was not this infliction the penalty for that adultery? How should the stick resemble adultery in secret?

entries.farsi: مار كى ماند عصا را اى كليم / ‏درد كى ماند دوا را اى حكيم
entries.english: How should the serpent resemble the rod, O Moses? How should the pain resemble the remedy, O doctor?

entries.farsi: تو به جاى آن عصا آب منى / ‏چون بيفكندى شد آن شخص سنى
entries.english: When you, instead of the rod, ejected semen into the uterus that semen became a fine figure

entries.farsi: يار شد يا مار شد آن آب تو / ز آن عصا چون است اين اعجاب تو
entries.english: That semen of yours became a friend or a serpent: why is this astonishment at the rod on your part?

entries.farsi: هيچ ماند آب آن فرزند را / هيچ ماند نيشكر مر قند را
entries.english: Does the semen at all resemble that child? Does the sugar-cane at all resemble the candy?

entries.farsi: چون سجودى يا ركوعى مرد كشت / ‏شد در آن عالم سجود او بهشت
entries.english: When a man has sown a prostration or a genuflexion, in yonder world his prostration becomes Paradise.

entries.farsi: حمد و تسبيحت نماند مرغ را / گر چه نطفه‏ى مرغ باد است و هوا
entries.english: When praise of God has flown from his mouth, the Lord of the daybreak fashions it into a bird of Paradise.

entries.farsi: چون كه پريد از دهانش حمد حق / ‏مرغ جنت ساختش رب الفلق
entries.english: Your praise and glorification does not resemble the bird, though the bird’s semen is wind and air.

entries.farsi: چون ز دستت رست ايثار و زكات / ‏گشت اين دست آن طرف نخل و نبات
entries.english: When altruism and almsgiving have grown up from your hand, this hand becomes on yonder side date-palms and herbage.

entries.farsi: آب صبرت جوى آب خلد شد / جوى شير خلد مهر تست و ود
entries.english: The water, your renunciation, became a river of water in Paradise; your love and affection is a river of milk in Paradise.

entries.farsi: ذوق طاعت گشت جوى انگبين / ‏مستى و شوق تو جوى خمر بين
entries.english: Delight in devotion became a river of honey; behold your intoxication and longing as a river of wine.

entries.farsi: اين سببها آن اثرها را نماند / كس نداند چونش جاى آن نشاند
entries.english: These causes did not resemble those effects: none knows how He installed it in the place of that.

entries.farsi: اين سببها چون به فرمان تو بود / چار جو هم مر ترا فرمان نمود
entries.english: Since these causes obeyed your command, the four rivers likewise showed obedience to you.

entries.farsi: هر طرف خواهى روانش مى‏كنى / ‏آن صفت چون بد چنانش مى‏كنى
entries.english: You make them flow in whatever direction you wish: as that quality was, such do you make it to be.

entries.farsi: چون منى تو كه در فرمان تست / ‏نسل آن در امر تو آيند چست
entries.english: As your semen, which is at your command— the progeny thereof are ready to your command.

entries.farsi: مى‏دود بر امر تو فرزند نو / كه منم جزوت كه كردى‏اش گرو
entries.english: Your young son runs at your command, saying, “I am the part of you which you did deposit in my mother’s womb.”

entries.farsi: آن صفت در امر تو بود اين جهان / ‏هم در امر تست آن جوها روان
entries.english: That quality obeyed your command in this world: likewise those rivers flow at your command.

entries.farsi: آن درختان مر ترا فرمان برند / كان درختان از صفاتت با برند
entries.english: Those trees are obedient to you, because those trees are fruitful by your qualities.

entries.farsi: چون به امر تست اينجا اين صفات / ‏پس در امر تست آن جا آن جزات
entries.english: Since these qualities are to your command here, so your recompense is at your command there.

entries.farsi: چون ز دستت زخم بر مظلوم رست / ‏آن درختى گشت از او زقوم رست
entries.english: When blows proceeded from your hand against the victim of injustice, they became a tree: the Zaqqum grew from them.

entries.farsi: چون ز خشم آتش تو در دلها زدى / ‏مايه‏ى نار جهنم آمدى
entries.english: When in anger you threw fire into hearts, you became the source of Hell-fire.

entries.farsi: آتشت اينجا چو آدم سوز بود / آن چه از وى زاد مرد افروز بود
entries.english: Since here your fire was burning mankind, that which was born of it was kindling men.

entries.farsi: آتش تو قصد مردم مى‏كند / نار كز وى زاد بر مردم زند
entries.english: Your fire makes an attack on the people: the fire that sprang from it rushes against the people.

entries.farsi: آن سخنهاى چو مار و كژدمت / ‏مار و كژدم گشت و مى‏گيرد دمت
entries.english: Your words resembling snakes and scorpions have become snakes and scorpions and are seizing your tail.

entries.farsi: اوليا را داشتى در انتظار / انتظار رستخيزت گشت يار
entries.english: You kept the friends waiting: you will be kept waiting at the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: وعده‏ى فردا و پس فرداى تو / انتظار حشرت آمد واى تو
entries.english: Your promise, “To-morrow” and “the day after to-morrow,” has become your waiting on the Day of Congregation: alas for you!

entries.farsi: منتظر مانى در آن روز دراز / در حساب و آفتاب جان گداز
entries.english: You will remain waiting on that long Day, in rendering an account and in the soul-consuming sun,

entries.farsi: خشم تو تخم سعير دوزخ است / ‏هين بكش اين دوزخت را كاين فخ است
entries.english: Because you were wont to keep Heaven waiting and sow the seed of “I will go on the Way to-morrow.”

entries.farsi: كاسمان را منتظر مى‏داشتى / ‏تخم فردا ره روم مى‏كاشتى
entries.english: Your anger is the seed of Hell-fire: take heed, extinguish this Hell of yours, for this is a trap.

entries.farsi: كشتن اين نار نبود جز به نور / نورك أطفأ نارنا نحن الشكور
entries.english: The extinction of this fire is not save by the Light: “your light has put out our fire, we are the grateful.”

entries.farsi: گر تو بى‏نورى كنى حلمى به دست / ‏آتشت زنده ست و در خاكستر است
entries.english: If you are devoid of the Light and do an act of clemency, it is evil: your fire is alive and is in the embers.

entries.farsi: آن تكلف باشد و رو پوش هين / ‏نار را نكشد بغير نور دين
entries.english: Beware! That is ostentation and masking: nothing will extinguish the fire except the Light of Religion.

entries.farsi: تا نبينى نور دين ايمن مباش / ‏كاتش پنهان شود يك روز فاش
entries.english: Do not be secure till you behold the Light of Religion, for the hidden fire will one day become manifest.

entries.farsi: نور آبى دان و هم بر آب چفس / ‏چون كه دارى آب از آتش مترس
entries.english: Deem the Light to be water, and cleave to the water withal: when you have the water, be not afraid of the fire.

entries.farsi: آب آتش را كشد كاتش به خو / مى‏بسوزد نسل و فرزندان او
entries.english: The water will extinguish the fire, because the fire by its nature burns up its progeny and children.

entries.farsi: سوى آن مرغابيان رو روز چند / تا ترا در آب حيوانى كشند
entries.english: Go, for a while, to those water-birds, that they may lead you to the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: مرغ خاكى مرغ آبى هم تنند / ليك ضدانند آب و روغنند
entries.english: The land-bird and the water-bird have the same body, but they are opposites: they are water and oil.

entries.farsi: اند‏هر يكى مر اصل خود را بنده / احتياطى كن به هم ماننده‏اند
entries.english: Each is devoted to its own origin; have a care: they resemble each other,

entries.farsi: ُهمچنان كه وسوسه و وحى أَ لَسْ ت / ‏هر دو معقولند ليكن فرق هست
entries.english: Just as both suggestion and Divine inspiration are intelligible, and yet there is a difference.

entries.farsi: هر دو دالالن بازار ضمير / رختها را مى‏ستايند اى امير
entries.english: Both brokers in the market of Conscience extol their wares, O prince.

entries.farsi: گر تو صراف دلى فكرت شناس / ‏فرق كن سر دو فكرت چون نخاس
entries.english: If you are a spiritual money-changer, one who recognises thought, distinguish the real nature of the two thoughts which resemble slave-dealers;

entries.farsi: ور ندانى اين دو فكرت از گمان / ‏الخالبه گوى و مشتاب و مران
entries.english: And if from opinion you do not know these two thoughts, say, “No deception!” and be not in a hurry and do not push forward.

sections.title_fa: حيله‏ى دفع مغبون شدن در بيع و شرا
sections.title_en: The means of preventing one’s self from being swindled in sale and purchase.

entries.farsi: آن يكى يارى پيمبر را بگفت / ‏كه منم در بيعها با غبن جفت
entries.english: A certain friend said to the Prophet, “I am always being swindled in commerce.

entries.farsi: مكر هر كس كاو فرو شد يا خرد / همچو سحر است و ز راهم مى‏برد
entries.english: The deceit of everyone who sells or buys is like magic and leads me off the track.”

entries.farsi: گفت در بيعى كه ترسى از غرار / شرط كن سه روز خود را اختيار
entries.english: He said, “When you are afraid of being duped in a commercial transaction, stipulate for yourself three days to choose,

entries.farsi: كه تانى هست از رحمان يقين / ‏هست تعجيلت ز شيطان لعين
entries.english: For deliberation is assuredly from the Merciful; your haste is from the accursed Devil.”

entries.farsi: پيش سگ چون لقمه‏ى نان افكنى / ‏بو كند آن گه خورد اى معتنى
entries.english: When you throw a morsel of bread to a dog, he smells, then he eats, O careful one.

entries.farsi: او ببينى بو كند ما با خرد / هم ببوييمش به عقل منتقد
entries.english: He smells with the nose, we too with wisdom smell it with the purified intelligence.

entries.farsi: با تانى گشت موجود از خدا / تا به شش روز اين زمين و چرخها
entries.english: This earth and the spheres were brought into existence by God with deliberation to six days;

entries.farsi: ور نه قادر بود كز كُنْ فيكون / ‏صد زمين و چرخ آوردى برون
entries.english: Otherwise, He was able—“Be, and it is”— to bring forth a hundred earths and heavens.

entries.farsi: آدمى را اندك اندك آن همام / گر چه قادر بود كاندر يك نفس
entries.english: Little by little till forty years that Potentate makes the human being a complete man,

entries.farsi: ‏از عدم پران كند پنجاه كس / عيسى قادر بود كاو از يك دعا
entries.english: Although He was able in a single moment to set flying fifty persons from non-existence

entries.farsi: بى‏توقف بر جهاند مرده را / خالق عيسى بنتواندكه او
entries.english: Jesus by means of one prayer was able to make the dead spring up without delay:

entries.farsi: بى‏توقف مردم آرد تو به تو / اين تانى از پى تعليم تست
entries.english: Is the Creator of Jesus unable, without delay, to bring men in manifold succession?

entries.farsi: ‏كه طلب آهسته بايد بى‏سكست / جو يكى كوچك كه دايم مى‏رود
entries.english: This deliberation is for the purpose of teaching you that you must seek slowly without break.

entries.farsi: نه نجس گردد نه گنده مى‏شود / زين تأنى زايد اقبال و سرور
entries.english: A little rivulet which is moving continually does not become defiled or grow fetid.

entries.farsi: اين تأنى بيضه دولت چون طيور / مرغ كى ماند به بيضه اى عنيد
entries.english: From this deliberation are born felicity and joy: this deliberation is the egg, fortune is like the birds.

entries.farsi: گر چه از بيضه همى‏آيد پديد / باش تا اجزاى تو چون بيضه‏ها
entries.english: How should the bird resemble the egg, O obstinate one, though it is produced from the egg?

entries.farsi: مرغها زايند اندر انتها / بيضه‏ى مار ار چه ماند در شبه
entries.english: Wait till your limbs, like eggs, hatch birds ultimately!

entries.farsi: ‏بيضه‏ى گنجشك را دور است ره / دانه‏ى آبى به دانه‏ى سيب نيز
entries.english: Though the serpent’s egg resembles the sparrow’s egg in likeness, the distance is far.

entries.farsi: گر چه ماند فرق‏ها دان اى عزيز / برگها هم رنگ باشد در نظر
entries.english: Again, though the seed of the quince resembles the seed of the apple, recognise the differences, O honoured.

entries.farsi: ميوه‏ها هر يك بود نوعى دگر / برگهاى جسمها ماننده‏اند
entries.english: Leaves are of the same colour to look at, fruits, every one, are of a diverse sort.

entries.farsi: آن يكى در ذوق و ديگر دردمند / همچنان در مرگ يكسان مى‏رويم
entries.english: The leaves, the bodies, are similar, but every soul lives with a produce.

entries.farsi: ليك هر جانى به ريعى زنده‏اند / خلق در بازار يكسان مى‏روند
entries.english: In the bazaar the people go all alike; one is in glee and another sorrowful.

entries.farsi: ‏نيم در خسران و نيمى خسرويم / چون بالل از ضعف شد همچون هالل
entries.english: Even so in death: we go all alike, half of us are losers and half are emperors.

sections.title_fa: ‏وفات يافتن بالل با شادى
sections.title_en: How Bilal, May God be well-pleased with him, died rejoicing.

entries.farsi: ‏رنگ مرگ افتاد بر روى بالل / جفت او ديدش بگفتا وا حرب
entries.english: When Bilal from weakness became as the new-moon, the hue of death fell upon Bilal’s face.

entries.farsi: ‏پس باللش گفت نه نه وا طرب / تا كنون اندر حرب بودم ز زيست
entries.english: His wife saw him and cried, “Oh, sorrow!” Then Bilal said to her, “Nay, nay! ‘Oh, joy!’

entries.farsi: ‏تو چه دانى مرگ چون عيش است و چيست / اين همى‏گفت و رخش در عين گفت
entries.english: Until now I have been in sorrow from living: how should you know how delightful death is, and what it is?”

entries.farsi: ‏نرگس و گلبرگ و الله مى‏شكفت / تاب رو و چشم پر انوار او
entries.english: He was saying this, and at the very moment of saying it his countenance was blooming with narcissi, rose-leaves, and red anemones.

entries.farsi: مى گواهى داد بر گفتار او / هر سيه دل مى سيه ديدى و را
entries.english: The glow of his face and his eye full of radiance were giving testimony to his words.

entries.farsi: مردم ديده سياه آمد چرا / مردم ناديده باشد رو سياه
entries.english: Every black-hearted one was regarding him as black; why is the man of the eye black?

entries.farsi: ‏مردم ديده بود مرآت ماه / خود كه بيند مردم ديده‏ى ترا
entries.english: The man blind is black-faced; the Man of the eye is the mirror for the Moon.

entries.farsi: در جهان جز مردم ديده‏فزا / چون به غير مردم ديده‏ش نديد
entries.english: Who in the world, indeed, sees the man of your eye except the Man of piercing sight?

entries.farsi: در صفات مردم ديده‏ى بلند / گفت جفتش الفراق اى خوش خصال
entries.english: Since none but the Man of the eye beheld it, who, then, but he attained to its colour?

entries.farsi: پس به غير او كه در رنگش رسيد / پس جز او جمله مقلد آمدند
entries.english: Therefore all except him are imitators in regard to the attributes of the sublime man of the eye.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت نه نه الوصال است الوصال / گفت جفت امشب غريبى مى‏روى
entries.english: His wife said to him, the parting, O man of goodly qualities.” “Nay, nay,” said he, “’it is the union, the union.”

entries.farsi: ‏از تبار و خويش غايب مى‏شوى / گفت نه نه بلكه امشب جان من
entries.english: The wife said, “To-night you will go to a strange country, you will become absent from your family and kindred.”

entries.farsi: گفت اندر حلقه‏ى خاص خدا / حلقه‏ى خاصش به تو پيوسته است
entries.english: “Nay, nay,” he replied; “contrariwise, to-night in sooth from a strange country my spirit is coming home.”

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏رسد خود از غريبى در وطن / گفت رويت را كجا بينيم ما
entries.english: She said, “Where shall we behold your face?” He answered, “In God’s chosen circle.”

entries.farsi: ‏نور مى‏تابد چو در حلقه نگين / گفت ويران گشت اين خانه دريغ
entries.english: His chosen circle adjoins you, if you look upward, not downward.

entries.farsi: ‏گر نظر بالا كنى نه سوى پست / اندر آن حلقه ز رب العالمين
entries.english: In that circle the Light from the Lord of created beings is gleaming like the bezel in the circle.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت اندر مه نگر منگر به ميغ / كرد ويران تا كند معمورتر
entries.english: “Alas,” she said, “this house has been ruined.” “Look on the moon,” said he, “do not look on the cloud.

entries.farsi: قوم انبه بود و خانه مختصر / من چو آدم بودم اول حبس كرب
entries.english: He has ruined it in order that He may make it more flourishing: my kinsfolk were numerous and the house was small.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكمت ويران شدن تن به مرگ
sections.title_en: The wisdom in ruining the body by death

entries.farsi: ‏پر شد اكنون نسل جانم شرق و غرب / من گدا بودم در اين خانه‏ى چو چاه
entries.english: Formerly, like Adam; I was imprisoned in grief; now East and West are filled with my spirit’s progeny.

entries.farsi: ‏شاه گشتم قصر بايد بهر شاه / قصرها خود مر شهان را مانس است
entries.english: I was a beggar in this dungeon-like house; I have become a king: a palace is needed for a king.”

entries.farsi: ظاهرش زفت و به معنى تنگ بر / گر نبودى تنگ اين افغان ز چيست
entries.english: Truly, palaces are the place for kings to take their pleasure in; for him that is dead a grave is a sufficient house and dwelling.

entries.farsi: ‏مرده را خانه و مكان گورى بس است / انبيا را تنگ آمد اين جهان
entries.english: To the prophets this world seemed narrow: like kings, they went into spacelessness.

entries.farsi: ‏چون شهان رفتند اندر ال مكان / مردگان را اين جهان بنمود فر
entries.english: To the dead this world appears splendid: its external is large, but in reality it is narrow.

entries.farsi: ‏چون دو تا شد هر كه در وى بيش زيست / در زمان خواب چون آزاد شد
entries.english: If it were not narrow, for what reason is this lamentation? Why has every one become doubled the more he lived in it?

entries.farsi: ز آن مكان بنگر كه جان چون شاد شد / ظالم از ظلم طبيعت باز رست
entries.english: When during the time of sleep the spirit is freed, behold how it rejoices in that place!

entries.farsi: ‏خنده‏ى او گريه فخرش جمله ننگ
entries.english: The wicked man is delivered from the wickedness of his nature, the prisoner escapes from thoughts of confinement.

entries.farsi: ‏سخت تنگ آمد به هنگام مناخ / چشم بند آمد فراخ و سخت تنگ
entries.english: This very wide earth and heaven becomes exceedingly narrow at the time of lying down.

entries.farsi: ‏مرد زندانى ز فكر حبس جست / اين زمين و آسمان بس فراخ
entries.english: It is an eye-bandage: wide and mighty narrow: its laughter is weeping, its glory is entirely shame.

sections.title_fa: ‏تشبيه دنيا كه به ظاهر فراخ است و به معنى تنگ و تشبيه خواب كه خالص است از اين تنگى
sections.title_en: Comparisons of these worlds, which is wide in appearance and narrow in reality, to a bathroom, and comparison to sleep, which is the release from this narrowness.

entries.farsi: همچو گرمابه كه تفسيده بود / تنگ آيى جانت پخسيده شود
entries.english: Like a bath-room which is very hot, you are distressed and your soul is melted.

entries.farsi: گر چه گرمابه عريض است و طويل / ‏ز آن تبش تنگ آيدت جان و كليل
entries.english: Although the bath-room is broad and long, your soul is distressed and fatigued by the heat.

entries.farsi: تا برون نايى بنگشايد دلت / ‏پس چه سود آمد فراخى منزلت
entries.english: Your heart does not expand till you come out: what advantage, then, is the spaciousness of the room to you?

entries.farsi: يا كه كفش تنگ پوشى اى غوى / ‏در بيابان فراخى مى‏روى
entries.english: Or as though you should put on tight shoes, O misguided one, and go into a wide desert.

entries.farsi: آن فراخى بيابان تنگ گشت / ‏بر تو زندان آمد آن صحرا و دشت
entries.english: The spaciousness of the desert becomes narrow; that desert and plain becomes a prison to you.

entries.farsi: هر كه ديد او مر ترا از دور گفت / ‏كاو در آن صحرا چو الله‏ى تر شكفت
entries.english: Whoever sees you from afar says, “He blooms like a fresh anemone in that desert”;

entries.farsi: او نداند كه تو همچون ظالمان / ‏از برون در گلشنى جان در فغان
entries.english: He does not know that you, like the wicked, are outwardly in the rose-garden, your soul is in lamentation.

entries.farsi: اوليا را خواب ملك است اى فالن / ‏همچو آن اصحاب كهف اندر جهان
entries.english: Your sleep is to put those shoes off, for your soul is free from the body for a while.

entries.farsi: خواب تو آن كفش بيرون كردن است / ‏كه زمانى جانت آزاد از تن است
entries.english: To the saints, O reader, sleep is a kingdom, as the Men of the Cave in this world.

entries.farsi: خواب مى‏بينند و آن جا خواب نه / ‏در عدم در مى‏روند و باب نه
entries.english: They dream, and no sleep is there; they go into nonexistence, and no door.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى تنگ و در او جان چنگ لوك / ‏كرد ويران تا كند قصر ملوك
entries.english: “A narrow house and the soul within is cramped: He ruined it in order that He might make a royal palace.

entries.farsi: چنگ لوكم چون جنين اندر رحم / ‏نه مهه گشتم شد اين نقالن مهم
entries.english: I am cramped like the embryo in the womb: I have become nine months old: this migration has become urgent.

entries.farsi: گر نباشد درد زه بر مادرم / ‏من در اين زندان ميان آذرم
entries.english: Unless the throes of childbirth overtake my mother: in this prison I am amidst the fire.

entries.farsi: تا چرد آن بره در صحراى سبز / هين رحم بگشا كه گشت اين بره گبز
entries.english: My mother, namely, my nature, in consequence of its death-throes, is giving birth, to the end that the lamb may be released from the ewe,

entries.farsi: مادر طبعم ز درد مرگ خويش / ‏مى‏كند ره تا رهد بره ز ميش
entries.english: So that the lamb may graze in the green fields. Come, open your womb, for this lamb has grown big.”

entries.farsi: حامله گريان ز زه كاين المناص / ‏و آن چنين خندان كه پيش آمد خالص
entries.english: If the pain of childbirth is grievous to the pregnant, it is, for the embryo, the breaking of prison.

entries.farsi: درد زه گر رنج آبستان بود / بر جنين اشكستن زندان بود
entries.english: The pregnant woman weeps at childbirth, saying, “Where is the refuge?”— but the embryo laughs, saying, “Deliverance has appeared.”

entries.farsi: هر چه زير چرخ هستند امهات / ‏از جماد و از بهيمه و ز نبات
entries.english: Whatever mothers there are under the sky— mineral, animal, or vegetable—

entries.farsi: آن چه كوسه داند از خانه‏ى كسان / ‏بلمه از خانه‏ى خودش كى داند آن
entries.english: They are heedless, every one, of another’s pain, except those persons that are discerning and perfect.

entries.farsi: هر يكى از درد غيرى غافل‏اند / جز كسانى كه نبيه و كامل‏اند
entries.english: How should the man with a bushy beard know of his own house that which the man with a few hairs on his chin knows of people’s houses?

entries.farsi: آن چه صاحب دل بداند حال تو / تو ز حال خود ندانى اى عمو
entries.english: What the man of heart knows of your condition you do not know of your own condition, O uncle.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان آن كه هر چه غفلت و غم و كاهلى و تاريكى است همه از تن است كه ارضى است و سفلى
sections.title_en: Setting forth that whatever is heedlessness and anxiety and indolence and darkness is all from the body, which belongs to the earth and the lower world.

entries.farsi: غفلت از تن بود چون تن روح شد / بيند او اسرار را بى‏هيچ بد
entries.english: Heedlessness was from the body: when the body has become spirit, it inevitably beholds the mysteries.

entries.farsi: چون زمين برخاست از جو فلك / ‏نه شب و نه سايه باشد لى و لك
entries.english: When the earth is removed from the celestial atmosphere, there is neither night nor shade nor sunset.

entries.farsi: هر كجا سايه ست و شب يا سايه‏گه / ‏از زمين باشد نه از افلاک و مه
entries.english: Wherever shade and night or shadowy place exists, it is by the earth, not by the heavens and the moon.

entries.farsi: دود پيوسته هم از هيزم بود / نه از آتشهاى مستنجم بود
entries.english: Likewise, it is from the faggots that the smoke always arises, not from the resplendent fires.

entries.farsi: وهم افتد در خطا و در غلط / عقل باشد در اصابتها فقط
entries.english: The imagination falls into error and mistake; the intellect is only in acts of true perception.

entries.farsi: هر گرانى و كسل خود از تن است / ‏جان ز خفت جمله در پريدن است
entries.english: Every state of heaviness and indolence, indeed, is from the body; the spirit, from its lightness is all on the wing.

entries.farsi: روى سرخ از غلبه‏ى خونها بود / روى زرد از جنبش صفرا بود
entries.english: The face is red from the predominance of blood; the face is yellow from the movement of the yellow bile.

entries.farsi: رو سپيد از قوت بلغم بود / باشد از سودا كه رو ادهم بود
entries.english: The face is white from the power of the phlegm; it is from the black bile that the face is swarthy.

entries.farsi: در حقيقت خالق آثار اوست / ‏ليك جز علت نبيند اهل پوست
entries.english: In reality He is the creator of effects, but followers of the husk see nothing but the cause.

entries.farsi: مغز كاو از پوستها آواره نيست / ‏از طبيب و علت او را چاره نيست
entries.english: The kernel that is not separated from the husks has no means from doctor and disease;

entries.farsi: چون دوم بار آدمى زاده بزاد / پاى خود بر فرق علتها نهاد
entries.english: When a son of man is born twice, he plants his foot upon the head of causes:

entries.farsi: علت اولى نباشد دين او / علت جزوى ندارد كين او
entries.english: The First Cause is not his religion; the particular cause has no enmity against him.

entries.farsi: مى‏پرد چون آفتاب اندر افق / ‏با عروس صدق و صورت چون تتق
entries.english: He flies, like the sun, in the horizon with the bride, sincerity; and form as a veil.

entries.farsi: بلكه بيرون از افق وز چرخها / ‏بى‏مكان باشد چو ارواح و نهى
entries.english: Nay, beyond horizons and skies he is without locality, like spirits and intelligences.

entries.farsi: بل عقول ماست سايه‏هاى او / مى‏فتد چون سايه‏ها در پاى او
entries.english: Nay, our intellects are the shadows of him: they fall, like shadows, at his feet.

entries.farsi: مجتهد هر گه كه باشد نص شناس / ‏اندر آن صورت نينديشد قياس
entries.english: Whenever the mujtahid (legist) knows a Statute, in that case he will not think of analogy;

entries.farsi: چون نيابد نص اندر صورتى / ‏از قياس آن جا نمايد عبرتى
entries.english: In a case where he does not find a Statute, there he will produce an example from analogy.

sections.title_fa: ‏تشبيه نص با قياس
sections.title_en: Comparison between Statute and analogy

entries.farsi: نص وحى روح قدسى دان يقين / ‏و آن قياس عقل جزوى تحت اين
entries.english: Know for sure that Statute is the Revelation of the Holy Spirit and that the analogy made by the individual intellect is under this.

entries.farsi: عقل از جان گشت با ادراك و فر / روح او را كى شود زير نظر
entries.english: The intellect is endued with apprehension and enlightenment by the Spirit: how should the Spirit become subject to its supervision?

entries.farsi: ليك جان در عقل تاثيرى كند / ز آن اثر آن عقل تدبيرى كند
entries.english: But the Spirit makes an impression on the intellect, and in consequence of that impression the intellect exercises certain governance.

entries.farsi: نوح وار ار صدقى زد در تو روح / ‏كو يم و كشتى و كو طوفان نوح
entries.english: If the Spirit has declared a belief in you, as Noah, where is the Sea and the Ship and the Flood of Noah?

entries.farsi: عقل اثر را روح پندارد و ليك / ‏نور خور از قرص خور دوراست نيك
entries.english: The intellect deems the impression to be the Spirit, but the light of the sun is very far from the orb of the sun.

entries.farsi: ز آن به قرصى سالكى خرسند شد / تا ز نورش سوى قرص افكند شد
entries.english: Hence a pilgrim is content with a loaf of bread (qursí), in order that by its light he may be thrown towards the Orb (Qurs),

entries.farsi: ز انكه اين نورى كه اندر سافل است / ‏نيست دايم روز و شب او آفل است
entries.english: Because this light which is below is not lasting: it is sinking day and night,

entries.farsi: و انكه اندر قرص دارد باش و جا / غرقه‏ى آن نور باشد دايما
entries.english: While he that has his abode and dwelling-place in the Orb is plunged in that Light continually.

entries.farsi: نه سحابش ره زند خود نه غروب / ‏وارهيد او از فراق سينه كوب
entries.english: Neither does cloud waylay him nor setting: he is delivered from heart-wringing separation.

entries.farsi: اين چنين كس اصلش از افلاک بود / يا مبدل گشت گر از خاك بود
entries.english: Such a person’s origin was from the heavens, or if he was of the earth, he has been transmuted,

entries.farsi: ز انكه خاكى را نباشد تاب آن / ‏كه زند بر وى شعاعش جاودان
entries.english: Because a creature of earth cannot endure that its beams should strike upon it everlastingly.

entries.farsi: گر زند بر خاك دايم تاب خور / آن چنان سوزد كه نايد زو ثمر
entries.english: If the radiance of the sun strikes upon the earth continually, it will be burned in such wise that no fruits will come from it.

entries.farsi: دايم اندر آب كار ماهى است / ‏مار را با او كجا همراهى است
entries.english: The business of the fish is always in the water: how has a snake the power of accompanying it on its way?

entries.farsi: ليك در كه مارهاى پر فنند / اندر اين يم ماهيى‏ها مى‏كنند
entries.english: But in the mountain are artful snakes that perform the actions of fish in this Sea.

entries.farsi: مكرشان گر خلق را شيدا كند / هم ز دريا تاسه‏شان رسوا كند
entries.english: Though their cunning makes the people mad, still their aversion to the Sea exposes them;

entries.farsi: و اندر اين يم ماهيان پر فنند / مار را از سحر ماهى مى‏كنند
entries.english: And in this Sea are artful fish, by magic turn snakes into fishes—

entries.farsi: ماهيان قعر درياى جلال / ‏بحرشان آموخته سحر حالل
entries.english: The fish of the deepest depth of the Sea of Majesty: the Sea has taught them lawful magic;

entries.farsi: پس محال از تاب ايشان حال شد / نحس آن جا رفت و نيكو فال شد
entries.english: Therefore through their illumination the absurd became a fact: the ill-starred one went there and became auspicious.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت گر بگويم زين كالم / ‏صد قيامت بگذرد وين ناتمام
entries.english: Though I should speak on this topic till the Resurrection, a hundred Resurrections would pass, and this incomplete.

sections.title_fa: ‏آداب المستمعين و المريدين عند فيض الحكمة من لسان الشيخ
sections.title_en: The rules to be observed by listeners and disciples at the emanation of wisdom from the tongue of the Shaykh

entries.farsi: بر ملوالن اين مكرر كردن است / ‏نزد من عمر مكرر بردن است
entries.english: To the weary this is repetition, in my eyes it is the bringing of repeated life.

entries.farsi: شمع از برق مكرر بر شود / خاك از تاب مكرر زر شود
entries.english: The candle goes upward from repeated flashes; earth becomes gold in consequence of repeated heat.

entries.farsi: گر هزاران طالبند و يك ملول / ‏از رسالت باز مى‏ماند رسول
entries.english: If there are thousands of seekers and a single weary one, the Messenger will refrain from delivering his message.

entries.farsi: اين رسوالن ضمير رازگو / مستمع خواهند اسرافيل خو
entries.english: These mystery-telling Messengers of the hidden Mind require a hearer who has the nature of Israfil.

entries.farsi: نخوتى دارند و كبرى چون شهان / چاكرى خواهند از اهل جهان
entries.english: They have a haughtiness and pride like kings: they require service from the people of the world.

entries.farsi: تا ادبهاشان به جا گه ناورى / از رسالتشان چگونه بر خورى
entries.english: Until you perform the observances due to them, how will you gain profit from their message?

entries.farsi: كى رسانند آن امانت را به تو / تا نباشى پيششان راكع دو تو
entries.english: How will they deliver that deposit to you till you are bowed double before them?

entries.farsi: هر ادبشان كى همى‏آيد پسند / كامدند ايشان ز ايوان بلند
entries.english: How is every observance acceptable to them?— for they have come from the Sublime Palace.

entries.farsi: ليك با بى‏رغبتيها اى ضمير / صدقه‏ى سلطان بيفشان وامگير
entries.english: They are not beggars, that they should be grateful to you, O impostor, for every service.

entries.farsi: نه گدايانند كز هر خدمتى / ‏از تو دارند اى مزور منتى
entries.english: But, O inmost consciousness, notwithstanding lack of desire, scatter the Sultan’s charity: do not withhold it!

entries.farsi: اسب خود را اى رسول آسمان / ‏در ملوالن منگر و اندر جهان
entries.english: O heavenly Messenger, do not regard the disgusted ones and let your horse bound onward!

entries.farsi: فرخ آن تركى كه استيزه نهد / اسبش اندر خندق آتش جهد
entries.english: Blest is the Turcoman who lays contention aside and whose horse gallops into the moat of fire—

entries.farsi: چشم را از غير و غيرت دوخته / همچو آتش خشك و تر را سوخته
entries.english: Makes his horse so hot that it seeks to mount to the zenith of the sky;

entries.farsi: گرم گرداند فرس را آن چنان / ‏كه كند آهنگ اوج آسمان
entries.english: Has shut his eyes to other and to jealousy; like fire, has consumed dry and wet.

entries.farsi: خود پشيمانى نرويد از عدم / ‏چون ببيند گرمى صاحب قدم
entries.english: If repentance find fault with him, he first sets fire to repentance.

entries.farsi: گر پشيمانى بر او عيبى كند / آتش اول در پشيمانى زند
entries.english: Verily, repentance does not spring forth from non-existence, when it sees the ardour of him whose presence brings fortune.

sections.title_fa: شناختن هر حيوانى بوى عدوى خود را و حذر كردن و بطالت و خسارت آن كس كه عدوى كسى بود ‏كه از او حذر ممكن نيست و فرار ممكن نى و مقابله ممكن نى
sections.title_en: How every animal knows the smell of its enemy and takes precaution. The folly and perdition of him that is the enemy of that One against whom precaution is impossible,
and flight is impossible, and resistance is impossible.

entries.farsi: اسب داند بانگ و بوى شير را / گر چه حيوان است الا نادرا
entries.english: The horse, though it is an animal, knows the roar and smell of the lion except in rare instances;

entries.farsi: بل عدوى خويش را هر جانور / خود بداند از نشان و از اثر
entries.english: Nay, every animal indeed knows its own enemy by sign and mark.

entries.farsi: روز خفاشك نيارد بر پريد / شب برون آمد چو دزدان و چريد
entries.english: The little bat dare not fly in the daytime: it came out at night, like thieves, and pastured.

entries.farsi: از همه محروم‏تر خفاش بود / كه عدوى آفتاب فاش بود
entries.english: The bat was more damned than all, because he was the enemy of the manifest Sun.

entries.farsi: نه تواند در مصافش زخم خورد / نه به نفرين تاندش مهجور كرد
entries.english: He cannot be wounded in battle with him, nor can he drive him away by cursing.

entries.farsi: آفتابى كه بگرداند قفاش / ‏از براى غصه و قهر خفاش
entries.english: The Sun who turns his back on account of the rage and violence of the bat—

entries.farsi: غايت لطف و كمال او بود / گر نه خفاشش كجا مانع شود
entries.english: It is the extreme of kindness and perfection on his part; otherwise, how should the bat prevent him?

entries.farsi: دشمنى گيرى به حد خويش گير / تا بود ممكن كه گردانى اسير
entries.english: You take an enemy, take within your limit, so that it may be possible for you to make prisoner.

entries.farsi: قطره با قلزم چو استيزه كند / ابله است او ريش خود بر مى‏كند
entries.english: When a drop of water contends with the Ocean, he is a fool: he is tearing out his own beard.

entries.farsi: حيلت او از سبالش نگذرد / چنبره‏ى حجره‏ى قمر چون بر درد
entries.english: His cunning does not pass beyond his moustache: how should it penetrate the vaulted chamber of the Moon?

entries.farsi: با عدوى آفتاب اين بد عتاب / ‏اى عدوى آفتاب آفتاب
entries.english: This was a rebuke to the enemy of the Sun, O enemy of the Sun of the Sun.

entries.farsi: اى عدوى آفتابى كز فرش / ‏مى‏لرزد آفتاب و اخترش
entries.english: O enemy of the Sun at whose glory His sun and stars tremble,

entries.farsi: تو عدوى او نه‏اى خصم خودى / ‏چه غم آتش را كه تو هيزم شدى
entries.english: You are not His enemy, you are the adversary of yourself: what does the Fire care that you have become firewood?

entries.farsi: اى عجب از سوزشت او كم شود / يا ز درد سوزشت پر غم شود
entries.english: Oh, marvellous! Shall He suffer defect through your burning, or shall He become full of sorrow for the pain of your burning?

entries.farsi: رحمت مخلوق باشد غصه‏ناك / ‏رحمت حق از غم و غصه‏ست پاك
entries.english: His mercy is not the mercy of Adam, for sorrow is mingled with the mercy of Adam.

entries.farsi: رحمتش نه رحمت آدم بود / كه مزاج رحم آدم غم بود
entries.english: The mercy of the creature is anxious; the mercy of God is exempt from sorrow and anxiety.

entries.farsi: رحمت بى‏چون چنين دان اى پدر / نايد اندر وهم از وى جز اثر
entries.english: Know that the mercy of the Unconditioned is like this, O father; nothing but the effect thereof comes into the imagination.

sections.title_fa: فرق ميان دانستن چيزى به مثال و تقليد و ميان دانستن ماهيت آن چيز
sections.title_en: The difference between knowing a thing by comparison and convention and knowing the essence of that thing

entries.farsi: ظاهر است آثار و ميوه‏ى رحمتش / ‏ليك كى داند جز او ماهيتش
entries.english: The effects and fruit of His mercy are manifest, but how should anyone except Him know its essence?

entries.farsi: هيچ ماهيات اوصاف كمال / ‏كس نداند جز به آثار و مثال
entries.english: None knows the essences of the attributes of Perfection except through effects and by means of comparison.

entries.farsi: طفل ماهيت نداند طمث را / جز كه گويى هست چون حلوا ترا
entries.english: The child does not know the essence of sexual intercourse, except that you say, “It is like sweetmeat to you.”

entries.farsi: كى بود ماهيت ذوق جماع / ‏مثل ماهيات حلوا اى مطاع
entries.english: How should the essence of the pleasure of sexual intercourse be like the essences of sweetmeat, O master?

entries.farsi: ليك نسبت كرد از روى خوشى / ‏با تو آن عاقل چو تو كودك وشى
entries.english: But, since you are childish, that intelligent man offered you the analogy respecting the sweetness.

entries.farsi: تا بداند كودك آن را از مثال / ‏گر نداند ماهيت يا عين حال
entries.english: In order that the child might know it by comparison, though he does not know the essence or essence of the matter.

entries.farsi: پس اگر گويى بدانم دور نيست / ‏ور ندانم گفت كذب و زور نيست
entries.english: Therefore, if you say “I know,” it is not far; and if you say, “I do not know,” it is not a lie and a falsehood.

entries.farsi: گر كسى گويد كه دانى نوح را / آن رسول حق و نور روح را
entries.english: If someone says, “Do you know Noah, the Messenger of God and the Light of the spirit?”—

entries.farsi: گر بگويى چون ندانم كان قمر / هست از خورشيد و مه مشهورتر
entries.english: And if you reply, “How should not I know? For that Moon is more celebrated than the sun and moon:

entries.farsi: كودكان خرد در كتابها / و آن امامان جمله در محرابها
entries.english: The little children at school and all the Imams in the mosques

entries.farsi: نام او خوانند در قرآن صريح / ‏قصه‏اش گويند از ماضى فصيح
entries.english: Recite his name distinctly in the Quran and tell plainly his story from the past”—

entries.farsi: راستگو دانيش تو از روى وصف / ‏گر چه ماهيت نشد از نوح كشف
entries.english: You, veracious man, know him by way of description, though the essence of Noah has not been revealed.

entries.farsi: ور بگويى من چه دانم نوح را / ‏همچو اويى داند او را اى فتى
entries.english: And if you reply, “How should I know Noah? One like him can know him, O youth.

entries.farsi: مور لنگم من چه دانم فيل را / پشه‏اى كى داند اسرافيل را
entries.english: I am a lame ant. How should I know the elephant? How should a gnat know Israfil?”—

entries.farsi: اين سخن هم راست است از روى آن / كه به ماهيت ندانيش اى فالن
entries.english: This saying is also true in regard to the fact that you do not know him in his essence, O so-and-so.

entries.farsi: ز انكه ماهيات و سر سر آن / ‏پيش چشم كامالن باشد عيان
entries.english: To be unable to perceive the essence, uncle, is the condition of common men: do not say it absolutely,

entries.farsi: عجز از ادراك ماهيت عمو / حالت عامه بود مطلق مگو
entries.english: Inasmuch as essences and their inmost secret are clearly visible to the eyes of the Perfect.

entries.farsi: در وجود از سر حق و ذات او / دورتر از فهم و استبصار كو
entries.english: Where in existence is more remote from understanding and mental perception than the consciousness and essence of God?

entries.farsi: چون كه آن مخفى نماند از محرمان / ‏ذات و وصفى چيست كان ماند نهان
entries.english: Since that does not remain hidden from familiars, what is the essence and attribute that should remain concealed?

entries.farsi: عقل بحثى گويد اين دور است و گو / بى‏ز تاويلى محالى كم شنو
entries.english: The intellect of the scholastic theologian says, “This is far and deeply involved: do not listen to an absurdity without some explanation.”

entries.farsi: قطب گويد مر ترا اى سست حال / ‏آن چه فوق حال تست آيد محال
entries.english: The Qutb replies, “To you, O infirm one that which is above your state seems absurd.”

entries.farsi: واقعاتى كه كنونت بر گشود / نه كه اول هم محالت مى‏نمود
entries.english: The visions which are now revealed to you, is it not the case that at first they seemed absurd to you?

entries.farsi: چون رهانيدت زده زندان كرم / ‏تيه را بر خود مكن حبس ستم
entries.english: Inasmuch as the Bounty has released you from ten prisons, do not make the desert an oppressive prison to yourself.

sections.title_fa: ‏جمع و توفيق ميان نفى و اثبات يك چيز از روى نسبت و اختالف جهت
sections.title_en: How the negation and affirmation of one thing may be combined and reconciled from the standpoint of relativity and difference of aspect.
