entries.farsi: نفى آن يك چيز و اثباتش رواست / ‏چون جهت شد مختلف نسبت دوتاست
entries.english: It is possible to deny and affirm the same thing: when the point of view is different, the relation is twofold.

entries.farsi: ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ از نسبت است / ‏نفى و اثبات است و هر دو مثبت است
entries.english: you didst not throw when you threw is relative: it is negation and affirmation: both are authorised.

entries.farsi: آن تو افكندى چو بر دست تو بود / تو نه افكندى كه قوت حق نمود
entries.english: You threw that, since it was on your hand; you didst not throw, for God manifested power.

entries.farsi: زور آدم زاد را حدى بود / مشت خاك اشكست لشكر كى شود
entries.english: The strength of one born of Adam has a limit: how should a handful of earth become the rout of an army?

entries.farsi: مشت مشت تست و افكندن ز ماست / ‏زين دو نسبت نفى و اثباتش رواست
entries.english: “The handful is your handful, and the throwing is from Me”: on account of these two relations the denial and the affirmation of it are right.

entries.farsi: همچو فرزندان خود دانندشان / ‏منكران با صد دليل و صد نشان
entries.english: The prophets are known by their enemies, just as their children are not doubtful.

entries.farsi: يعرفون األنبيا أضدادهم / ‏مثل ما ال يشتبه أوالدهم
entries.english: The unbelievers know them as their children by a hundred indications and a hundred signs,

entries.farsi: ليك از رشك و حسد پنهان كنند / خويشتن را بر ندانم مى‏زنند
entries.english: But, from jealousy and envy, they conceal and attach themselves to “I do not know.”

entries.farsi: پس چو يعرف گفت چون جاى دگر / گفت ال يعرفهم غيرى فذر
entries.english: Then, since He has said, “He knows,” how has He said in another place?— “None knows them except Me, so leave off;

entries.farsi: إنهم تحت قبابي كامنون / ‏جز كه يزدانشان نداند ز آزمون
entries.english: Verily, they are hidden beneath My tents.” None knows them by experience except God.

entries.farsi: هم به نسبت گير اين مفتوح را / كه بدانى و ندانى نوح را
entries.english: Regard also by means of relation this which was opened, that you know and do not know Noah.

sections.title_fa: ‏مسئله فنا و بقاى درويش
sections.title_en: The question of the fana and baqa of the dervish.

entries.farsi: گفت قايل در جهان درويش نيست / ‏ور بود درويش آن درويش نيست
entries.english: The speaker said, “There is no dervish in the world; and if there be a dervish, that dervish is non-existent.”

entries.farsi: هست از روى بقاى ذات او / نيست گشته وصف او در وصف هو
entries.english: He exists in respect of the survival of his essence; his attributes have become non-existent in the attributes of Him.

entries.farsi: چون زبانه‏ى شمع پيش آفتاب / ‏نيست باشد هست باشد در حساب
entries.english: Like the flame of a candle in the presence of the sun, he is non-existent, existent in calculation.

entries.farsi: هست باشد ذات او تا تو اگر / بر نهى پنبه بسوزد ز آن شرر
entries.english: Its essence is existent, so that, if you put cotton upon it, it will be consumed by the sparks;

entries.farsi: در دو صد من شهد يك اوقيه خل / چون در افكندى و در وى گشت حل
entries.english: It is non-existent: it gives you no light: the sun will have negated it.

entries.farsi: نيست باشد روشنى ندهد ترا / كرده باشد آفتاب او را فنا
entries.english: When you have thrown an ounce of vinegar into two hundred kilos of sugar, and it has become dissolved therein,

entries.farsi: نيست باشد طعم خل چون مى‏چشى / ‏هست اوقيه فزون چون بر كشى
entries.english: The flavour of the vinegar, when you taste, is non-existent, the ounce exists surplus when you weigh.

entries.farsi: پيش شيرى آهويى بى‏هوش شد / هستى‏اش در هست او رو پوش شد
entries.english: In the presence of a lion a deer becomes senseless: her existence becomes a veil for his existence.

entries.farsi: اين قياس ناقصان بر كار رب / ‏جوشش عشق است نه از ترك ادب
entries.english: These analogies drawn by imperfect men concerning the action of the Lord are the emotion of love, not from irreverence.

entries.farsi: نبض عاشق بى‏ادب بر مى‏جهد / خويش را در كفه‏ى شه مى‏نهد
entries.english: The lover’s pulse bounds up without reverence, he lays himself on the scale of the King’s balance.

entries.farsi: بى‏ادب‏تر نيست كس زو در جهان / ‏با ادب‏تر نيست كس زو در نهان
entries.english: None is more irreverent than he in the world; none is more reverent than he in secret.

entries.farsi: بى‏ادب باشد چو ظاهر بنگرى / ‏كه بود دعوى عشقش هم سرى
entries.english: Know, O chosen one, that these two opposites also, “reverent” and “irreverent,” are reconciled by means of relation.

entries.farsi: هم به نسبت دان وفاق اى منتجب / ‏اين دو ضد با ادب يا بى‏ادب
entries.english: He is irreverent when you regard the outward aspect, for his claim of love is equality;

entries.farsi: چون به باطن بنگرى دعوى كجاست / ‏او و دعوى پيش آن سلطان فناست
entries.english: When you regard the inward aspect, where is the claim? He and claim are dissolved in the presence of that Sultan.

entries.farsi: او ز روى لفظ نحوى فاعل است / ‏ور نه او مفعول و موتش قاتل است
entries.english: Mata Zaydun: if Zayd is the agent, he is not the agent, for he is defunct.

entries.farsi: مات زيد زيد اگر فاعل بود / ليك فاعل نيست كاو عاطل بود
entries.english: He is the agent in respect of the grammatical expression; otherwise, he is the one acted upon, and Death is his slayer.

entries.farsi: فاعل چه كاو چنان مقهور شد / در بخارا بنده‏ى صدر جهان
entries.english: What agent, since he has been so overpowered and all the qualities of an agent have been removed from him?

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى وكيل صدر جهان كه متهم شد و از بخارا گريخت از بيم جان، باز عشقش كشيد روكشان، كه كار جان سهل باشد عاشقان را
sections.title_en: Story of the Sadr-i Jahan’s Wakíl, who fell under suspicion and fled from Bukhara in fear of his life;
then love drew him back irresistibly, for the matter of life is of small account to lovers.

entries.farsi: ‏متهم شد گشت از صدرش نهان / مدت ده سال سر گردان بگشت
entries.english: In Bukhara the servant of the Sadr-i Jahan incurred suspicion and hid from his Sadr.

entries.farsi: ‏گه خراسان گه كهستان گاه دشت / از پس ده سال او از اشتياق
entries.english: During ten years he roamed distractedly, now in Khurasan, now in the mountain-land, now in the desert.

entries.farsi: ‏گشت بى‏طاقت ز ايام فراق / گفت تاب فرقتم زين پس نماند
entries.english: After ten years, through longing he became unable to endure the days of separation.

entries.farsi: صبر كى داند خالعت را نشاند / از فراق اين خاكها شوره شود
entries.english: He said, “Henceforth I cannot bear to be parted any more: how can patience allay state of abandonment?”

entries.farsi: آب زرد و گنده و تيره شود / باد جان افزا وخم گردد وبا
entries.english: From separation these soils are nitrous, and water becomes yellow and stinking and dark;

entries.farsi: آتشى خاكسترى گردد هبا / باغ چون جنت شود دار المرض
entries.english: The life-increasing wind becomes unhealthy and pestilential; a fire turns to ashes and dust.

entries.farsi: ‏زرد و ريزان برگ او اندر حرض / عقل دراك از فراق دوستان
entries.english: The orchard which resembled Paradise becomes the abode of disease, its leaves yellow and dropping in decay.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو تير انداز اشكسته كمان / دوزخ از فرقت چنان سوزان شده‏ست
entries.english: The penetrating intellect, through separation from its friends, like an archer whose bow is broken

entries.farsi: ‏پير از فرقت چنان لرزان شده‏ست / گر بگويم از فراق چون شرار
entries.english: From separation Hell has become so burning; from separation the old man has become so trembling.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت يك بود از صد هزار / پس ز شرح سوز او كم زن نفس
entries.english: If I should speak of separation, like sparks of fire, till the Resurrection, it would be one out of a hundred thousand.

entries.farsi: ‏رب سلم رب سلم گوى و بس / هر چه از وى شاد گردى در جهان
entries.english: Therefore do not breathe in description of its burning: say only “Lord, save! Lord, save!”

entries.farsi: ‏از فراق او بينديش آن زمان / ز آن چه گشتى شاد، بس كس شاد شد
entries.english: Everything by which you are rejoiced in the world— think at that time of the parting from it

entries.farsi: آخر از وى جست و همچون باد شد / از تو هم بجهد تو دل بر وى منه
entries.english: Many a one has been gladdened by what made you glad: at last it escaped from him and became even as wind.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش از آن كاو بجهد از وى تو بجه / همچو مريم گوى پيش از فوت ملك
entries.english: It will escape from you also: set not your heart upon it. Do you yourself escape from it before it escapes.

sections.title_fa: ‏پيدا شدن روح القدس به صورت آدمى بر مريم به وقت برهنگى و غسل كردن و پناه گرفتن به حق تعالى
sections.title_en: The appearance of the Holy Spirit in the shape of a man to Mary when she was undressed and washing herself, and how she took refuge with God.

entries.farsi: نقش را كالعوذ بالرحمن منك / ديد مريم صورتى بس جان فزا
entries.english: Before the slipping away of your possessions, say to the form, like Mary, refuge from you with the Merciful.”

entries.farsi: جان فزايى دل ربايى در خال / پيش او بر رست از روى زمين
entries.english: Mary in her chamber saw a form that gave increase of life— a life-increasing, heart-ravishing one.

entries.farsi: ‏چون مه و خورشيد آن روح الامين / از زمين بر رست خوبى بى‏نقاب
entries.english: That trusted Spirit rose up before her from the face of the earth, like the moon and the sun.

entries.farsi: كاو برهنه بود و ترسيد از فساد / صورتى كه يوسف ار ديدى عيان
entries.english: Beauty unveiled rose up from the earth such as the sun rises from the East.

entries.farsi: ‏آن چنان كز شرق رويد آفتاب / لرزه بر اعضاى مريم اوفتاد
entries.english: A trembling came over Mary’s limbs, for she was undressed and was afraid of evil.

entries.farsi: ‏دست از حيرت بريدى چون زنان / همچو گل پيشش بروييد آن ز گل
entries.english: Such a form that if Joseph had beheld it plainly, he would have cut his hand in amazement, like the women.

entries.farsi: ‏چون خيالى كه بر آرد سر ز دل / گشت بى‏خود مريم و در بى‏خودى
entries.english: It blossomed from the earth like a rose before her— like a phantasy which lifts its head from the heart.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت بجهم در پناه ايزدى / ز انكه عادت كرده بود آن پاك جيب
entries.english: Mary became selfless, and in her selflessness she said, “I will leap into the Divine protection,”

entries.farsi: ‏در هزيمت رخت بردن سوى غيب / چون جهان را ديد ملكى بى‏قرار
entries.english: Because that pure-bosomed one had made a habit of betaking herself in flight to the Unseen

entries.farsi: حازمانه ساخت ز آن حضرت حصار / تا به گاه مرگ حصنى باشدش
entries.english: Since she deemed the world a kingdom without permanence, she prudently made a fortress of that Presence,

entries.farsi: يورتگه نزديك آن دژ بر گزيد / چون بديد آن غمزه‏هاى عقل سوز
entries.english: In order that in the hour of death she should have a stronghold which the Enemy would find no way to attack.

entries.farsi: ‏كه نيابد خصم راه مقصدش / از پناه حق حصارى به نديد
entries.english: She saw no better fortress than the protection of God: she chose her abiding place near to that castle.

entries.farsi: كه از او مى‏شد جگرها تير دوز / شاه و لشكر حلقه در گوشش شده
entries.english: When she beheld those amorous reason-destroying glances whereby hearts were ever being pierced by arrows—

entries.farsi: ‏خسروان هوش بى‏هوشش شده / صد هزاران شاه مملوكش به رق
entries.english: King and army are enthralled by Him, the sovereigns of wit are made witless by Him;

entries.farsi: ‏صد هزاران بدر را داده به دق / زهره نى مر زهره را تا دم زند
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of kings are held in servitude by Him; hundreds of thousands of full-moons He has given over to wasting fever;

entries.farsi: عقل كلش چون ببيند كم زند / من چه گويم كه مرا در دوخته ست
entries.english: Zuhra has not the courage to breathe; Universal Reason, when it sees Him, humbles itself.

entries.farsi: ‏دمگهم را دمگه او سوخته ست / دود آن نارم دليلم من بر او
entries.english: What shall I say? For He has sealed my lips: His furnace has consumed the place of my breath.

entries.farsi: دور از آن شه باطل ما عبروا / خود نباشد آفتابى را دليل
entries.english: “I am the smoke of that fire, I am the evidence for it”— far from that King be their false interpretation!

entries.farsi: اين بس استش كه ذليل او بود / اين جلالت در داللت صادق است
entries.english: Truly, there is no evidence for a sun except the light of the lofty sun.

entries.farsi: ‏جز كه نور آفتاب مستطيل / سايه كه بود تا دليل او بود
entries.english: Who is the shadow that it should be an evidence for Him? It is enough for it that it should be abased before Him.

entries.farsi: ‏جمله ادراكات پس او سابق است / جمله ادراكات بر خرهاى لنگ
entries.english: This majesty in evidence declares the truth: all perceptions are behind, He is outstripping.

entries.farsi: ‏او سوار باد پران چون خدنگ / گر گريزد كس نيابد گرد شه
entries.english: All perceptions are on lame asses; He is mounted on the wind that flies like an arrow.

entries.farsi: ‏ور گريزند او بگيرد پيش ره / جمله ادراكات را آرام نى
entries.english: If He flees, none finds the dust of the King; and if they flee, He bars the way in front.

entries.farsi: ‏وقت ميدان است وقت جام نى / آن يكى وهمى چو بازى مى‏پرد
entries.english: All the perceptions are unquiet: it is the time for battle, not the time for the cup.

entries.farsi: و آن دگر چون تير معبر مى‏درد / و آن دگر چون كشتى با بادبان
entries.english: One perceptive faculty is flying like a falcon, while another, as an arrow, is tearing its place of passage;

entries.farsi: ‏و آن دگر اندر تراجع هر زمان / چون شكارى مى‏نمايدشان ز دور
entries.english: And another is like a ship with sails, and another is turning back every moment.

entries.farsi: جمله حمله مى‏فزايند آن طيور / چون كه ناپيدا شود حيران شوند
entries.english: When an object of chase appears to them from afar, all those birds increase their onset.

entries.farsi: همچو جغدان سوى هر ويران شوند / منتظر چشمى بهم يك چشم باز
entries.english: When it vanishes from sight, they become lost: like owls, they go to every wilderness,

entries.farsi: تا كه پيدا گردد آن صيد بناز / چون بماند دير گويند از مالل
entries.english: Waiting, with one eye closed and one eye open, that the delectable prey may appear

entries.farsi: ‏صيد بود آن خود عجب يا خود خيال / مصلحت آن است تا يك ساعتى
entries.english: When it tarries long, they say, “We wonder whether it was a prey or a phantom.”

entries.farsi: خويشتن را سوختندى ز اهتزاز / از هوس و ز حرص سود اندوختن
entries.english: The right course is that, for a short while, they should gather come strength and vigour by a rest.

entries.farsi: ‏قوتى گيرند و زور از راحتى / گر نبودى شب همه خلقان ز آز
entries.english: If there were no night, on account of cupidity all people would consume themselves by the agitation.

entries.farsi: ‏هر كسى دادى بدن را سوختن / شب پديد آيد چو گنج رحمتى
entries.english: From desire and greed of amassing gain, everyone would give his body to be consumed.

entries.farsi: ‏تا رهند از حرص خود يك ساعتى / چون كه قبضى آيدت اى راه رو
entries.english: Night appears, like a treasure of mercy, that they may be delivered from their greed for a short while.

entries.farsi: آن صالح تست آتش دل مشو / ز انكه در خرجى در آن بسط و گشاد
entries.english: When a feeling of (spiritual) contraction comes over you, O traveller, it is your good: do not become afire in your heart,

entries.farsi: خرج را دخلى ببايد ز اعتداد / گر هماره فصل تابستان بدى
entries.english: For in that expansion and delight you are spending: the expenditure requires an income of preparation.

entries.farsi: ‏سوزش خورشيد در بستان شدى / منبتش را سوختى از بيخ و بن
entries.english: If it were always the season of summer, the blazing heat of the sun would penetrate the garden

entries.farsi: ‏كه دگر تازه نگشتى آن كهن / گر ترش روى است آن دى مشفق است
entries.english: And burn up from root and bottom the soil whence its plants grow, so that the old ones would never again become fresh.

entries.farsi: ‏صيف خندان است اما محرق است / چون كه قبض آيد تو در وى بسط بين
entries.english: If December is sour-faced, it is kind; summer is laughing, but it is burning.

entries.farsi: ‏تازه باش و چين ميفگن در جبين / كودكان خندان و دانايان ترش
entries.english: When contraction comes, behold expansion therein: be fresh and do not let wrinkles fall on your brow.

entries.farsi: ‏غم جگر را باشد و شادى ز شش / چشم كودك همچو خر در آخور است
entries.english: Children are laughing, and sages are sour: sorrow appertains to the liver, and joy arises from the lungs.

entries.farsi: ‏چشم عاقل در حساب آخر است / او در آخور چرب مى‏بيند علف
entries.english: The eye of the child, like the ass, is on the stall; the eye of the wise man is in reckoning the end.

entries.farsi: ‏وين ز قصاب آخرش بيند تلف / آن علف تلخ است كاين قصاب داد
entries.english: He sees the rich fodder in the stall, while this sees his ultimate end to be death by the Butcher.

entries.farsi: بهر لحم ما ترازويى نهاد / روز حكمت خور علف كان را خدا
entries.english: That fodder is bitter, for this Butcher gave it: He set up a pair of scales for our flesh.

entries.farsi: بى‏غرض داده‏ست از محض عطا / فهم نان كردى نه حكمت اى رهى
entries.english: Go, eat the fodder of wisdom which God has given disinterestedly from pure bounty.

entries.farsi: ‏ز آن چه حق گفتت كُلُوا مِنْ رزقه / رزق حق حكمت بود در مرتبت
entries.english: O slave, you have understood bread, not wisdom, in that which God has spoken unto you—Eat ye of His provision.

entries.farsi: ‏كان گلو گيرت نباشد عاقبت / اين دهان بستى دهانى باز شد
entries.english: God’s provision in the stage is wisdom that will not choke you at the last.

entries.farsi: كاو خورنده‏ى لقمه‏هاى راز شد / گر ز شير ديو تن را وابرى
entries.english: You have closed this mouth, another mouth is opened, which becomes an eater of the morsels of mysteries.

entries.farsi: ‏در فطام او بسى نعمت خورى / ترك جوشش شرح كردم نيم خام
entries.english: If you cut off your body from the Devil’s milk, by weaning it you will enjoy much felicity.

entries.farsi: ‏از حكيم غزنوى بشنو تمام / در الهى نامه گويد شرح اين
entries.english: I have given a half-raw explanation of it like the Turcomans’ poorly boiled meat: hear in full from the Sage of Ghazna.

entries.farsi: ‏آن حكيم غيب و فخر العارفين / غم خور و نان غم افزايان مخور
entries.english: In the ILahí-náma that Sage of the Unseen and Glory of them that know explains this.

entries.farsi: ز انكه عاقل غم خورد كودك شكر / قند شادى ميوه‏ى باغ غم است
entries.english: “Eat sorrow, and do not eat the bread of those who increase sorrow, for the wise man eats sorrow, the child sugar.”

entries.farsi: ‏اين فرح زخم است و آن غم مرهم است / غم چو بينى در كنارش كش به عشق
entries.english: The sugar of joy is the fruit of the garden of sorrow, this joy is the wound and that sorrow is the plaster.

entries.farsi: ‏از سر ربوه نظر كن در دمشق / عاقل از انگور مى‏بيند همى
entries.english: When you see sorrow, embrace it with passionate love: look on Damascus from the top of Rubwa.

entries.farsi: ‏عاشق از معدوم شى بيند همى / جنگ مى‏كردند حمالان پرير
entries.english: The wise man is seeing the wine in the grape, the lover is seeing the thing in the non-existent.

entries.farsi: تو مكش تا من كشم حملش چو شير / ز انكه ز آن رنجش همى‏ديدند سود
entries.english: The day before yesterday the porters were quarrelling, “Don’t you lift, let me lift his load like a lion!”

entries.farsi: حمل را هر يك ز ديگر مى‏ربود / مزد حق كو مزد آن بى‏مايه كو
entries.english: Since they were seeing profit in that toil, each one was snatching the load from the other.

entries.farsi: اين دهد گنجيت مزد و آن تسو / گنج زرى كه چو خسبى زير ريگ
entries.english: What comparison is there between God’s reward and the reward given by that worthless creature? The former gives you a treasure as your reward, and the latter a dime.

entries.farsi: مونس گور و غريبى مى‏شود / بهر روز مرگ اين دم مرده باش
entries.english: A golden treasure that remains with you when you lie under the sand and is not left as a heritage.

entries.farsi: ‏با تو باشد آن نباشد مرده‏ريگ / پيش پيش آن جنازه‏ت مى‏دود
entries.english: It runs before your hearse and becomes your companion in the tomb and in the state where all is strange.

entries.farsi: ‏تا شوى با عشق سرمد خواجه‏تاش / صبر مى‏بيند ز پرده‏ى اجتهاد
entries.english: For the sake of your death-day be dead, now, so that you may be with everlasting Love, O fellow-servant.

entries.farsi: روى چون گلنار و زلفين مراد / غم چو آيينه‏ست پيش مجتهد
entries.english: Through the curtain of the struggle renunciation sees the face like a pomegranate-flower and the two tresses of the Desired One.

entries.farsi: رو دهد يعنى گشاد و كر و فر / اين دو وصف از پنجه‏ى دستت ببين
entries.english: Sorrow is as a mirror before the struggler, for in this contrary there appears the face of the contrary.

entries.farsi: كاندر اين ضد مى‏نمايد روى ضد / بعد ضد رنج آن ضد دگر
entries.english: After the contrary, pain, the other contrary, that is, gladness and triumph, shows its face.

entries.farsi: ‏بعد قبض مشت بسط آيد يقين / پنجه را گر قبض باشد دايما
entries.english: Observe these two qualities in the fingers of your hand: assuredly after the closing of the fist comes the opening.

entries.farsi: يا همه بسط او بود چون مبتال / زين دو وصفش كار و مكسب منتظم
entries.english: If the fingers be always closed or entirely open, he is like an afflicted person.

entries.farsi: ‏چون پر مرغ اين دو حال او را مهم / چون كه مريم مضطرب شد يك زمان
entries.english: His work and action is regulated by these two qualities: these two conditions are important for him as the bird’s wings.

entries.farsi: ‏همچنان كه بر زمين آن ماهيان / بانگ بر وى زد نمودار كرم
entries.english: When Mary was all at once dismayed, like those fishes on land,

sections.title_fa: ‏گفتن روح القدس مريم را كه من رسول حقم به تو، آشفته مشو و پنهان مشو از من كه فرمان اين است
sections.title_en: How the Holy Spirit said to Mary, “I am sent to you by God: be not agitated and do not hide from me, for this is the command.”

entries.farsi: ‏كه امين حضرتم از من مرم / از سرافرازان عزت سر مكش
entries.english: The Exemplar of Bounty cried out to her, “I am the trusted of the Lord: be not afraid of me.

entries.farsi: ‏از چنين خوش محرمان خود در مكش / اين همى‏گفت و ذباله‏ى نور پاك
entries.english: Do not turn your head away from the exalted of Majesty, do not withdraw yourself from such goodly confidants.”

entries.farsi: ‏از لبش مى‏شد پياپى بر سماك / از وجودم مى‏گريزى در عدم
entries.english: He was saying this, and from his lips a wick of pure light was going up to Arcturus step by step.

entries.farsi: ‏در عدم من شاهم و صاحب علم / خود بن و بنگاه من در نيستى است
entries.english: “You are fleeing from my existence into non-existence: in non-existence I am a King and standard-bearer.

entries.farsi: ‏يك سواره نقش من پيش ستى است / مريما بنگر كه نقش مشكلم
entries.english: Truly, my home and dwelling-place is in non-existence: solely my form is before the Lady.

entries.farsi: ‏هم هاللم هم خيال اندر دلم / چون خيالى در دلت آمد نشست
entries.english: O Mary, look, for I am a difficult form: I am both a new moon and a phantasy in the heart.

entries.farsi: ‏هر كجا كه مى‏گريزى با تو است / جز خيالى عارضيى باطلى
entries.english: When a phantasy comes into your heart and settles, it is with you wherever you flee—

entries.farsi: ‏كاو بود چون صبح كاذب آفلى / من چو صبح صادقم از نور رب
entries.english: Except an unsubstantial and vain phantasy which is one that sinks like the false dawn.

entries.farsi: ‏كه نگردد گرد روزم هيچ شب / هين مكن الحول عمران زاده‏ام
entries.english: I am of the light of the Lord, like the true dawn, for no night prowls around my day.

entries.farsi: ‏كه ز ال حول اين طرف افتاده‏ام / مر مرا اصل و غذا الحول بود
entries.english: Listen, do not cry La hawl against me, O daughter of Imran, for I have descended hither from La hawl.

entries.farsi: نور الحولى كه پيش از قول بود / تو همى‏گيرى پناه از من به حق
entries.english: La hawl was my origin and sustenance— the light of that La hawl which was prior to the spoken word.

entries.farsi: ‏من نگاريده‏ى پناهم در سبق / آن پناهم من كه مخلصهات بوذ
entries.english: You are taking refuge from me with God: I am in eternity the image of the refuge.

entries.farsi: تو اعوذ آرى و من خود آن اعوذ / آفتى نبود بتر از ناشناخت
entries.english: I am the refuge that was often your deliverance. You take refuge, and I myself am that refuge.

entries.farsi: ‏تو بر يار و ندانى عشق باخت / يار را اغيار پندارى همى
entries.english: There is no bane worse than ignorance: you are with your Friend and do not know how to make love.

entries.farsi: ‏شاديى را نام بنهادى غمى / اين چنين نخلى كه لطف يار ماست
entries.english: You are deeming your Friend a stranger: upon a joy you have bestowed the name of a grief.”

entries.farsi: ‏چون كه ما دزديم نخلش دار ماست / اين چنين مشكين كه زلف مير ماست
entries.english: Such a date-palm, which is our Friend’s favour— since we are robbers, His date-palm is our gallows.

entries.farsi: ‏چون كه بى‏عقليم اين زنجير ماست / اين چنين لطفى چو نيلى مى‏رود
entries.english: Such a musky object, which is the tress of our Prince— since we are demented, this is our chain.

entries.farsi: چون كه فرعونيم چون خون مى‏شود / خون همى‏گويد من آبم هين مريز
entries.english: Such a grace is flowing like a Nile— since we are Pharaohs, it is becoming like blood.

entries.farsi: يوسفم گرگ از توام اى پر ستيز / تو نمى‏بينى كه يار بردبار
entries.english: The blood is saying, “I am water. Beware; do not spill! I am Joseph; you make me the wolf, O contentious man.”

entries.farsi: چون كه با او ضد شدى گردد چو مار / لحم او و شحم او ديگر نشد
entries.english: Don’t you see that a long-suffering friend becomes like a snake when you have grown hostile to him?

entries.farsi: او چنان بد جز كه از منظر نشد / شمع مريم را بهل افروخته
entries.english: His flesh and fat is unchanged: only in appearance he has become so evil.

sections.title_fa: عزم كردن آن وكيل از عشق كه رجوع كند به بخارا الابالى‏وار
sections.title_en: How that Wakil, by love, made up his mind to return to Bukhara recklessly.

entries.farsi: ‏كه بخارا مى‏رود آن سوخته / سخت بى‏صبر و در آتشدان تيز
entries.english: Leave the candle of Mary lighted, for that ardent lover is going to Bukhara,

entries.farsi: رو سوى صدر جهان مى‏كن گريز / اين بخارا منبع دانش بود
entries.english: Mightily impatient and in the blazing furnace. Go; make a transition to the Sadr-i Jahan.

entries.farsi: پس بخارايى است هرك آنش بود / پيش شيخى در بخارا اندرى
entries.english: This “Bukhara” is the source of knowledge; therefore everyone who has that is a native of “Bukhara.”

entries.farsi: ‏تا به خوارى در بخارا ننگرى / جز به خوارى در بخاراى دلش
entries.english: In the presence of a Shaykh you are in “Bukhara”: see that you do not look on “Bukhara” as lowly.

entries.farsi: ‏راه ندهد جزر و مد مشكلش / اى خنك آن را كه ذلت نفسه
entries.english: Save with lowliness, its difficult ebb and flow will not give entrance into the “Bukhara” of his heart.

entries.farsi: ‏واى آن كس را كه يردى رفسه / فرقت صدر جهان در جان او
entries.english: Oh, happy he whose carnal soul is abased! Alas for that one whose recalcitrance destroys!

entries.farsi: پاره پاره كرده بود اركان او / گفت برخيزم هم آن جا واروم
entries.english: Separation from the Sadr-i Jahan had shattered foundations to pieces in his soul.

entries.farsi: پيش آن صدر نكو انديش او / گويم افكندم به پيشت جان خويش
entries.english: He said, “I will rise up and go back there: if I have become an infidel, I will believe once more.

entries.farsi: ‏كافر ار گشتم دگر ره بگروم / واروم آن جا بيفتم پيش او
entries.english: I will go back there and fall before him— before its kindly-thinking Sadr

entries.farsi: ‏زنده كن يا سر ببر ما را چو ميش / كشته و مرده به پيشت اى قمر
entries.english: I will say, ‘I throw myself before you: revive or cut off my head, like a sheep!

entries.farsi: به كه شاه زندگان جاى دگر / آزمودم من هزاران بار بيش
entries.english: It is better to be slain and dead before you, O Moon, than to be the king of the living in another place.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏تو شيرين مى‏نبينم عيش خويش / غن لي يا منيتي لحن النشور
entries.english: I have put it to the test more than a thousand times: I do not deem my life sweet without you.

entries.farsi: ابركي يا ناقتي تم السرور / ابلعي يا أرض دمعي قد كفى
entries.english: Sing to me, O object of my desire, and the melody of resurrection! Kneel, O my she-camel! The joy is complete.

entries.farsi: اشربي يا نفس وردا قد صفا / عدت يا عيدى الينا مرحبا
entries.english: O earth, swallow my tears- surely they are enough. Drink, O my soul, a draught that is now pure!

entries.farsi: نعم ما روحت يا ريح الصبا / گفت اى ياران روان گشتم وداع
entries.english: You have returned to us, O my festival! Welcome! How goodly is the refreshment you have brought, O Zephyr!’”

entries.farsi: ‏سوى آن صدرى كه مير است و مطاع / دم‏به‏دم در سوز بريان مى‏شوم
entries.english: He said, “Farewell, my friends: I have set out towards the Sadr who commands and is obeyed.

entries.farsi: ‏هر چه بادا باد آن جا مى‏روم / گر چه دل چون سنگ خارا مى‏كند
entries.english: Moment by moment I am being roasted in the flames. I will go there, come what may!

entries.farsi: جان من عزم بخارا مى‏كند / مسكن يار است و شهر شاه من
entries.english: Although he is making his heart like a hard rock, my soul is bound for Bukhara.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش عاشق اين بود حب الوطن
entries.english: It is the abode of my Friend and the city of my King: in the lover’s eyes this is love of one’s native land.”

sections.title_fa: پرسيدن معشوقى از عاشق غريب خود كه از شهرها كدام شهر را خوشتر يافتى و انبوه‏تر و محتشم‏تر و پر نعمت‏تر و دل گشاتر
sections.title_en: How a loved one asked her lover who had travelled in foreign countries, “Which city did you find the fairest, busiest, most magnificent, richest and most charming?”

entries.farsi: پس كدامين شهر از آنها خوشتر است / ‏گفت آن شهرى كه در وى دل بر است
entries.english: A loved one said to her lover, “O youth, you have seen many cities abroad.

entries.farsi: گفت معشوقى به عاشق كاى فتى / تو به غربت ديده‏اى بس شهرها
entries.english: Which of them, then, is the fairest?” He replied, “The city where my sweetheart is.”

entries.farsi: هر كجا باشد شه ما را بساط / هست صحرا گر بود سم الخياط
entries.english: Wherever the carpet is for our King, is the plain, though it is the eye of a needle.

entries.farsi: هر كجا كه يوسفى باشد چو ماه / ‏جنت است ار چه كه باشد قعر چاه
entries.english: Wherever a Joseph as the moon may be, it is Paradise, even though it be the bottom of a well.

sections.title_fa: منع كردن دوستان او را از رجوع كردن به بخارا و تهديد كردن و الابالى گفتن او
sections.title_en: How his friends hindered him from returning to Bukhara and threatened him, and how he said, “I don’t care.”

entries.farsi: گفت او را ناصحى اى بى‏خبر / عاقبت انديش اگر دارى هنر
entries.english: A candid adviser said to him, “O imprudent man, think of the end, if you have skill.

entries.farsi: در نگر پس را به عقل و پيش را / همچو پروانه مسوزان خويش را
entries.english: Consider reasonably the future and the past: do not let yourself be burnt like a moth.

entries.farsi: چون بخارا مى‏روى ديوانه‏اى / ‏اليق زنجير و زندان خانه‏اى
entries.english: How are you going to Bukhara? You are mad; you are fit for chains and the prison-house.

entries.farsi: او ز تو آهن همى‏خايد ز خشم / ‏او همى‏جويد ترا با بيست چشم
entries.english: He is champing iron in his wrath against you; he is seeking you with twenty eyes.

entries.farsi: مى‏كند او تيز از بهر تو كارد / او سگ قحط است و تو انبان آرد
entries.english: He is sharpening the knife for you: he is the starving dog, and you the bag of flour.

entries.farsi: چون رهيدى و خدايت راه داد / سوى زندان مى‏روى چونت فتاد
entries.english: After you have escaped and God has given you the road, you are going to prison: what is the matter with you?

entries.farsi: بر تو گر ده گون موكل آمدى / ‏عقل بايستى كز ايشان كم زدى
entries.english: Had there been ten sorts of custodians over you, intelligence would have been needed in order that you might become quit of them.

entries.farsi: چون موكل نيست بر تو هيچ كس / ‏از چه بسته گشت بر تو پيش و پس
entries.english: Since no one is a custodian over you, why have the future and the past become sealed to you?”

entries.farsi: عشق پنهان كرده بود او را اسير / آن موكل را نمى‏ديد آن نذير
entries.english: Secret love had made him captive: the warner was not seeing that custodian.

entries.farsi: هر موكل را موكل مختفى است / ‏ور نه او در بند سگ طبعى ز چيست
entries.english: Every custodian’s custodian is hidden; else, why is he in thrall to dog like nature?

entries.farsi: خشم شاه عشق بر جانش نشست / ‏بر عوانى و سيه روييش بست
entries.english: The anger of Love, the King, settled upon his soul and chained him to the office of a myrmidon and to ignominy.

entries.farsi: مى‏زند او را كه هين او را بزن / ‏ز آن عوانان نهان افغان من
entries.english: It is striking him and saying, “Listen, strike him!” Woe is me on account of those hidden followers.

entries.farsi: هر كه بينى در زيانى مى‏رود / گر چه تنها با عوانى مى‏رود
entries.english: Whomever you see going in a detriment, he, though alone, is going along with a myrmidon.

entries.farsi: گر از او واقف بدى افغان زدى / ‏پيش آن سلطان سلطانان شدى
entries.english: If he were aware of him, he would cry out in distress and go into the presence of the King of kings,

entries.farsi: ريختى بر سر به پيش شاه خاك / ‏تا امان ديدى ز ديو سهمناك
entries.english: And scatter earth on his head before the King, that he might find security from the frightful Devil.

entries.farsi: مير ديدى خويش را اى كم ز مور / ز آن نديدى آن موكل را تو كور
entries.english: You, O less than an ant, deemed yourself a prince: hence, blind, you did not see that custodian.

entries.farsi: غره گشتى زين دروغين پر و بال / ‏پر و بالى كاو كشد سوى وبال
entries.english: You were deluded by these false wings and plumes— the wings and plumes that lead to woe.

entries.farsi: پر سبك دارد ره بالا كند / چون گل آلو شد گرانيها كند
entries.english: He keep his wings light, he journeys upward; when he becomes defiled with earth, he makes heavinesses.

sections.title_fa: ‏الابالى گفتن عاشق، ناصح و عاذل را از سر عشق
sections.title_en: How the lover, impelled by love, said “I don’t care” to the person who counselled and scolded him.

entries.farsi: گفت اى ناصح خمش كن چند چند / پند كم ده ز انكه بس سخت است بند
entries.english: He said, “O counsellor, be silent! How long, how long? Do not give advice, for the bonds are very grievous.

entries.farsi: سخت‏تر شد بند من از پند تو / عشق را نشناخت دانشمند تو
entries.english: My bonds are more grievous than your advice: your doctor was not acquainted with love.

entries.farsi: آن طرف كه عشق مى‏افزود درد / بو حنيفه و شافعى درسى نكرد
entries.english: In that quarter where love was increasing pain, Bu Hanífa and Sháfi‘í gave no instruction.

entries.farsi: تو مكن تهديد از كشتن كه من / ‏تشنه‏ى زارم به خون خويشتن
entries.english: Do not you threaten me with being killed, for I thirst lamentably for mine own blood.”

entries.farsi: او دو صد جان دارد از جان هدى / ‏و آن دو صد را مى‏كند هر دم فدى
entries.english: For lovers, there is a dying at every moment: truly, the dying of lovers is not of one sort.

entries.farsi: عاشقان را هر زمانى مردنى است / ‏مردن عشاق خود يك نوع نيست
entries.english: He has two hundred souls from the Soul of Guidance, and those two hundred he is sacrificing at every instant.

entries.farsi: هر يكى جان را ستاند ده بها / از نبى خوان عشرة أمثالها
entries.english: For each soul he receives ten as its price: read from the Qur’an “ten like unto them.”

entries.farsi: گر بريزد خون من آن دوست رو / پاى كوبان جان بر افشانم بر او
entries.english: If that One of friendly countenance shed my blood, dancing I will scatter my soul upon Him.

entries.farsi: آزمودم مرگ من در زندگى است / ‏چون رهم زين زندگى پايندگى است
entries.english: I have tried it: my death is in life: when I escape from this life, it is to endure forever.

entries.farsi: اقتلوني اقتلوني يا ثقات / ‏إن في قتلي حياتا في حيات
entries.english: “Kill me; kill me, O trusty friends! Lo, in my being killed is life on life.”

entries.farsi: يا منير الخد يا روح البقا / اجتذب روحي و جد لي باللقا
entries.english: O You that makes the cheek radiant, O Spirit of everlastingness, draw my spirit to Yourself and generously bestow on me the meeting.

entries.farsi: لي حبيب حبه يشوي الحشا / ‏لو يشا يمشي على عيني مشى
entries.english: I have a Beloved whose love roasts the bowels: if He wished to walk upon mine eye, He would walk.

entries.farsi: پارسى گو گر چه تازى خوشتر است / ‏عشق را خود صد زبان ديگر است
entries.english: Speak Persian, though Arabic is sweeter: Love indeed has a hundred other tongues.

entries.farsi: بوى آن دل بر چو پران مى‏شود / آن زبانها جمله حيران مى‏شود
entries.english: When the scent of that Charmer of hearts begins to fly, all those tongues become dumbfounded

entries.farsi: بس كنم دل بر در آمد در خطاب / ‏گوش شو و اهلل أعلم بالصواب
entries.english: I will cease: the Sweetheart has begun to speak, be ear— and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: چون كه عاشق توبه كرد اكنون بترس / ‏كاو چو عياران كند بر دار درس
entries.english: Since the lover has repented, now beware, for he will lecture, like the adepts, on the gallows.

entries.farsi: گر چه اين عاشق بخارا مى‏رود / نه به درس و نه به استا مى‏رود
entries.english: Although this lover is going to Bukhara, he is not going to lectures or to a teacher.

entries.farsi: عاشقان را شد مدرس حسن دوست / دفتر و درس و سبقشان روى اوست
entries.english: For lovers, the lecturer is the beauty of the Beloved, their book and lecture and lesson is His face.

entries.farsi: خامشند و نعره‏ى تكرارشان / ‏مى‏رود تا عرش و تخت يارشان
entries.english: They are silent, but the shrill noise of their repetition is going up to the throne and high-seat of their Friend.

entries.farsi: درسشان آشوب و چرخ و زلزله / ‏نه زيادات است و باب و سلسله
entries.english: Their lesson is enthusiasm and the whirling dance and quaking agitation; not the Ziyadat and the chapter on “the chain.”

entries.farsi: سلسله‏ى اين قوم جعد مشك‏بار / مسئله‏ى دور است ليكن دور يار
entries.english: The “chain” of these people is the musk-dropping curls; they have the question of “the circle,” but it is the “circle” of the Friend.

entries.farsi: مسئله‏ى كيس ار بپرسد كس ترا / گو نگنجد گنج حق در كيسه‏ها
entries.english: If anyone asks you about the question of “the purse,” tell that God’s treasure is not contained in purses.

entries.farsi: ذكر هر چيزى دهد خاصيتى / ‏ز انكه دارد هر صفت ماهيتى
entries.english: If talk of khul and mubara is going on, do not disapprove: mention is being made of “Bukhara.”

entries.farsi: گر دم خلع و مبارا مى‏رود / بد مبين ذكر بخارا مى‏رود
entries.english: The mention of anything produces a particular effect, inasmuch as every quality has an essence.

entries.farsi: در بخارا در هنرها بالغى / ‏چون به خوارى رو نهى ز آن فارغى
entries.english: In Bukhara you attain to the sciences: when you turn to lowliness, you are freed from them.

entries.farsi: آن بخارى غصه‏ى دانش نداشت / ‏چشم بر خورشيد بينش مى‏گماشت
entries.english: That man of Bukhara had not the vexation of knowledge: he was fixing his eyes on the sun of vision.

entries.farsi: هر كه در خلوت ببينش يافت راه / ‏او ز دانشها نجويد دستگاه
entries.english: No one who in solitude has found the way to vision will seek power by means of the kinds of knowledge.

entries.farsi: با جمال جان چو شد هم كاسه‏اى / ‏باشدش ز اخبار و دانش تاسه‏اى
entries.english: When he has become a boon-companion to the beauty of the Soul, he will have a disgust of traditional learning and knowledge.

entries.farsi: ديد بر دانش بود غالب فرا / ز آن همى دنيا بچربد عامه را
entries.english: Vision is superior to knowledge: hence the present world prevails in the view of the vulgar,

entries.farsi: ز انكه دنيا را همى‏ببينند عين / ‏و آن جهانى را همى‏دانند دين
entries.english: Because they regard this world as ready money, while they deem what concerns that world to be a debt.

sections.title_fa: رو نهادن آن بنده‏ى عاشق سوى بخارا
sections.title_en: How that loving servant turned his face towards Bukhara.

entries.farsi: رو نهاد آن عاشق خونابه‏ريز / دل طپان سوى بخارا گرم و تيز
entries.english: With throbbing heart the lover, who shed tears mingled with blood, set out for Bukhara in hot haste.

entries.farsi: ريگ آمون پيش او همچون حرير / آب جيحون پيش او چون آب گير
entries.english: The sands of Amun seemed to him like silk, the river Oxus seemed to him like a pond.

entries.farsi: آن بيابان پيش او چون گلستان / ‏مى‏فتاد از خنده او چون گل‏ستان
entries.english: To him that wilderness was like a rose-garden: he was falling on his back from laughter, like the rose.

entries.farsi: در سمرقند است قند اما لبش / ‏از بخارا يافت و آن شد مذهبش
entries.english: The candy is in Samarkand; but his lip got it from “Bukhara,” and that became his creed.

entries.farsi: اى بخارا عقل افزا بوده‏اى / ‏ليكن از من عقل و دين بربوده‏اى
entries.english: “O Bukhara, you have increased understanding but you have robbed me of understanding and religion.

entries.farsi: بدر مى‏جويم از آنم چون هالل / ‏صدر مى‏جويم در اين صف نعال
entries.english: I am seeking the Full Moon: hence I am as the new moon. I am seeking the Sadr in this ‘shoe-row’.”

entries.farsi: چون سواد آن بخارا را بديد / در سواد غم بياضى شد پديد
entries.english: When he described that “Bukhara” looming black, whiteness appeared in the blackness of his grief.

entries.farsi: ساعتى افتاد بى‏هوش و دراز / عقل او پريد در بستان راز
entries.english: He fell awhile senseless and outstretched: his reason flew into the garden of the mystery.

entries.farsi: بر سر و رويش گالبى مى‏زدند / از گالب عشق او غافل بدند
entries.english: They were sprinkling rose-water on his head and face; they were unaware of the rose-water of his love.

entries.farsi: او گلستانى نهانى ديده بود / غارت عشقش ز خود ببريده بود
entries.english: He had beheld a hidden rose-garden: the raiding foray of Love had cut him off from himself.

entries.farsi: تو فسرده در خور اين دم نه‏اى / ‏با شكر مقرون نه‏اى گر چه نى‏اى
entries.english: You, frozen, art not worthy of this breath: though you are a reed, you are not associated with sugar.

entries.farsi: رخت عقلت با تو است و عاقلى / ‏كز جُنُوداً لَمْ تَرَوْها غافلى
entries.english: The baggage of intellect is with you, and you are possessed of your wits, for you are unaware of armies which you did not see.

sections.title_fa: ‏در آمدن آن عاشق الابالى در بخارا و تحذير كردن دوستان او را از پيدا شدن
sections.title_en: How the reckless lover entered Bukhara, and how his friends deterred him from showing himself.

entries.farsi: اندر آمد در بخارا شادمان / پيش معشوق خود و دار الامان
entries.english: Joyously he entered Bukhara near his beloved and the abode of security,

entries.farsi: همچو آن مستى كه پرد بر اثير / مه كنارش گيرد و گويد كه گير
entries.english: Like the man intoxicated who flies to heaven: the Moon embraces him and says, “Embrace!”

entries.farsi: هر كه ديدش در بخارا گفت خيز / پيش از پيدا شدن منشين گريز
entries.english: Every one that saw him in Bukhara said, “Arise before showing yourself! Do not sit! Flee!

entries.farsi: كه ترا مى‏جويد آن شه خشمگين / ‏تا كشد از جان تو ده ساله كين
entries.english: For that Prince is seeking you in anger, that he may wreak a ten years’ vengeance on your life.

entries.farsi: اللهَّ اهلل در ميا در خون خويش / ‏تكيه كم كن بر دم و افسون خويش
entries.english: By God, by God, do not plunge in your own blood; do not rely on your artful words and wiles.

entries.farsi: شحنه‏ى صدر جهان بودى و راد / معتمد بودى مهندس اوستاد
entries.english: You were the Sadr-i Jahan’s constable and a noble; you were the trusted and master-engineer.

entries.farsi: غدر كردى و ز جزا بگريختى / ‏رسته بودى باز چون آويختى
entries.english: You did act treacherously and flee from punishment: you had escaped: how have you let yourself be caught again?

entries.farsi: از بال بگريختى با صد حيل / ‏ابلهى آوردت اينجا يا اجل
entries.english: With a hundred devices you did flee from tribulation: has folly brought you hither or fate?

entries.farsi: اى كه عقلت بر عطارد دق كند / عقل و عاقل را قضا احمق كند
entries.english: O you whose intellect jeers at Mercury, Destiny makes a fool of intellect and the intelligent.

entries.farsi: نحس خرگوشى كه باشد شير جو / زيركى و عقل و چالاكيت كو
entries.english: Luckless is the hare that seeks the lion: where are your cleverness, intelligence and quick-wittedness?

entries.farsi: هست صد چندين فسون‏هاى قضا / گفت إِذا جاء القضاء ضاق الفضا
entries.english: The wiles of Destiny are a hundred times as many: he has said, ‘When Destiny comes, the wide field is straitened.’

entries.farsi: صدره و مخلص بود از چپ و راست / ‏از قضا بسته شود كان اژدهاست
entries.english: There are a hundred ways and places of refuge on left and right, they are barred by Destiny, for it is a dragon.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن عاشق عاذالن را و تهديد كنندگان را
sections.title_en: How the lover answered those who scolded and threatened him.

entries.farsi: گفت من مستسقى‏ام آبم كشد / گر چه مى‏دانم كه هم آبم كشد
entries.english: He said, “I have edema: the water draws me, though I know that the water too will kill me.

entries.farsi: هيچ مستسقى بنگريزد ز آب / ‏گر دو صد بارش كند مات و خراب
entries.english: None afflicted with edema will flee from the water, even if it checkmate and ruin him two hundred times.

entries.farsi: گر بياماسد مرا دست و شكم / ‏عشق آب از من نخواهد گشت كم
entries.english: If my hands and belly become swollen, the passionate desire for the water will not abate from me.

entries.farsi: گويم آن گه كه بپرسند از بطون / ‏كاشكى بحرم روان بودى درون
entries.english: At the time when they ask me of my inward state, I say, ‘Would that the Sea was flowing within me!’

entries.farsi: خيك اشكم گو بدر از موج آب / ‏گر بميرم هست مرگم مستطاب
entries.english: Let the water-skin, my belly, be burst by the waves of the water: if I die, my death is acceptable.

entries.farsi: من به هر جايى كه بينم آب جو / رشكم آيد بودمى من جاى او
entries.english: Wherever I see the water of a stream, jealousy comes over me that I might be in its place.

entries.farsi: دست چون دف و شكم همچون دهل / ‏طبل عشق آب مى‏كوبم چو گل
entries.english: Hands like a tambourine and belly like a drum, I am beating the drum of my love for the water, as the rose.

entries.farsi: گر بريزد خونم آن روح الامين / ‏جرعه جرعه خون خورم همچون زمين
entries.english: If that Trusty Spirit spills my blood, I will drink draught on draught of blood, like the earth.

entries.farsi: چون زمين و چون جنين خون‏خواره‏ام / ‏تا كه عاشق گشته‏ام اين كاره‏ام
entries.english: I am a blood-drinker, like the earth and like the embryo: since I became a lover I am in this trade.

entries.farsi: شب همى‏جوشم در آتش همچو ديگ / ‏روز تا شب خون خورم مانند ريگ
entries.english: During the night I boil on the fire, like a kettle; day till nightfall I drink blood, like the sand.

entries.farsi: من پشيمانم كه مكر انگيختم / ‏از مراد خشم او بگريختم
entries.english: I repent that I set contrivance afoot and fled from that which his anger desired.

entries.farsi: گو بران بر جان مستم خشم خويش / ‏عيد قربان اوست و عاشق گاوميش
entries.english: Let him drive on his anger against my intoxicated soul: he is the Feast of the Sacrifice, and the lover is the buffalo.

entries.farsi: گاو اگر خسبد و گر چيزى خورد / بهر عيد و ذبح او مى‏پرورد
entries.english: Whether the buffalo sleep or whether it eats something, he nurtures it for the Feast and the slaughter.

entries.farsi: گاو موسى بود قربان گشته‏اى / ‏كمترين جزوش حيات كشته‏اى
entries.english: Deem me to be the cow of Moses that gave life: each limb of me is the raising from the dead every one that is free.

entries.farsi: گاو موسى دان مرا جان داده‏اى / ‏جزو جزوم حشر هر آزاده‏اى
entries.english: The cow of Moses was one offered in sacrifice: her smallest limb brought a murdered man to life.

entries.farsi: بر جهيد آن كشته ز آسيبش ز جا / در خطاب اضربوه بعضها
entries.english: At its touch the murdered man sprang up from his place— at the words spoken, Strike him with part of her.

entries.farsi: از جمادى مردم و نامى شدم / ‏و ز نما مردم به حيوان بر زدم
entries.english: O my noble friends, slaughter this cow, if you desire to raise to life the spirits of insight.

entries.farsi: يا كرامي اذبحوا هذا البقر / إن أردتم حشر أرواح النظر
entries.english: I died to the inorganic state and became endowed with growth, and I died to growth and attained to the animal.

entries.farsi: مردم از حيوانى و آدم شدم / ‏پس چه ترسم كى ز مردن كم شدم
entries.english: I died from animality and became Adam: why, then, should I fear? When have I become less by dying?

entries.farsi: حمله‏ى ديگر بميرم از بشر / تا بر آرم از ماليك بال و پر
entries.english: At the next remove I shall die to man, that I may soar and lift up my head amongst the angels;

entries.farsi: بار ديگر از ملك قربان شوم / ‏آن چه اندر وهم نايد آن شوم
entries.english: And I must escape even from the angel: everything is perishing except His Face.

entries.farsi: و ز ملك هم بايدم جستن ز جو / ‏كُلُّ شَيْ‏ءٍ هالِكٌ إِلاَّ وجهه
entries.english: Once more I shall be sacrificed and die to the angel: I shall become that which enters not into the imagination.

entries.farsi: مرگ دان آنك اتفاق امت است / ‏كآب حيوانى نهان در ظلمت است
entries.english: Then I shall become non-existence: non-existence says to me, as an organ, Truly, unto Him shall we return.

entries.farsi: پس عدم گردم عدم چون ارغنون / ‏گويدم كه إِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجعون
entries.english: Know death to be what the community are agreed upon, namely, that the Water of Life is hidden in the Darkness.

entries.farsi: همچو نيلوفر برو زين طرف جو / همچو مستسقى حريص و مرگ جو
entries.english: Grow from this river-bank, like the water-lily, greedy and craving for death as the sufferer from dropsy.

entries.farsi: مرگ او آب است و او جوياى آب / ‏مى‏خورد و اهلل أعلم بالصواب
entries.english: The water is death to him, and he is seeking the water and drinking it— and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: اى فسرده عاشق ننگين نمد / كاو ز بيم جان ز جانان مى‏رمد
entries.english: Oh, the cold lover, clad in the felt of shame, who from fear of his life is fleeing from the Beloved!

entries.farsi: سوى تيغ عشقش اى ننگ زنان / ‏صد هزاران جان نگر دستك‏زنان
entries.english: O you disgrace to women, behold hundreds of thousands of souls clapping their hands towards the sword of His love!

entries.farsi: جوى ديدى كوزه اندر جوى ريز / آب را از جوى كى باشد گريز
entries.english: You have seen the river: spill your jug in the river: how should the water take flight from the river?

entries.farsi: آب كوزه چون در آب جو شود / محو گردد در وى و جو او شود
entries.english: When the water in the jug goes into the river-water, it disappears in it, and it becomes the river.

entries.farsi: وصف او فانى شد و ذاتش بقا / زين سپس نه كم شود نه بد لقا
entries.english: His attributes have passed away, and his essence remains: after this, he does not dwindle or become ill-favoured.

entries.farsi: خويش را بر نخل او آويختم / ‏عذر آن را كه از او بگريختم
entries.english: I have hanged myself on His palm-tree in excuse for having fled from Him.”

sections.title_fa: ‏رسيدن آن عاشق به معشوق خويش چون دست از جان خود بشست
sections.title_en: How that lover reached his Beloved when he washed his hands of his life.

entries.farsi: همچو گويى سجده كن بر رو و سر / جانب آن صدر شد با چشم تر
entries.english: Prostrating himself on face and head, like a ball, he went with wet eyes towards the Sadr.

entries.farsi: جمله خلقان منتظر سر در هوا / كش بسوزد يا بر آويزد و را
entries.english: All the people were waiting, their heads in the air, whether he would burn or hang him.

entries.farsi: اين زمان اين احمق يك لخت را / آن نمايد كه زمان بد بخت را
entries.english: “Now” “he will show to this simpleton that which Time shows to the unfortunate.

entries.farsi: همچو پروانه شرر را نور ديد / احمقانه در فتاد از جان بريد
entries.english: Like the moth, he deemed the sparks to be the light: foolishly he fell in and was cut off from life.”

entries.farsi: ليك شمع عشق چون آن شمع نيست / ‏روشن اندر روشن اندر روشنى است
entries.english: But the candle of Love is not like that candle: it is radiance in radiance in radiance.

entries.farsi: او بعكس شمعهاى آتشى است / ‏مى‏نمايد آتش و جمله خوشى است
entries.english: It is the reverse of the fiery candles: it seems to be fire, while it is all sweetness.

sections.title_fa: صفت آن مسجد كه مهمان كش بود و آن عاشق مرگ جوى الابالى كه در او مهمان شد
sections.title_en: Description of the lover-killing mosque and of the death-seeking reckless lover

entries.farsi: يك حكايت گوش كن اى نيك پى / ‏مسجدى بد بر كنار شهر رى
entries.english: who became a guest there

entries.farsi: بس كه اندر وى غريب عور رفت / ‏صبحدم چون اختران در گور رفت
entries.english: Lend ear to a story, O well-conducted man! There was a mosque on the outskirts of the city of Rayy.

entries.farsi: هيچ كس در وى نخفتى شب ز بيم / ‏كه نه فرزندش شدى آن شب يتيم
entries.english: No one ever slept the night there but on the same night from terror his children became orphans.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را نيك از اين آگاه كن / ‏صبح آمد خواب را كوتاه كن
entries.english: Many naked strangers that went into it and went at dawn, like the stars, into the grave
Make yourself very attentive to this! The dawn is come, cut short your slumber!

entries.farsi: هر كسى گفتى كه پريانند تند / اندر او مهمان كشان با تيغ كند
entries.english: Every one used to say that in it there were fierce Jinnis who killed the guests with blunt swords.

entries.farsi: آن دگر گفتى كه سحر است و طلسم / ‏كاين رصد باشد عدوى جان و خصم
entries.english: Another would say, “It is the magic and talisman, for this enchantment is the foe and enemy of life.”

entries.farsi: آن دگر گفتى كه بر نه نقش فاش / بر درش كاى ميهمان اينجا مباش
entries.english: Another would say, “Put an inscription conspicuously on its door— ‘O guest, do not stay here.

entries.farsi: شب مخسب اينجا اگر جان بايدت / ‏ور نه مرگ اينجا كمين بگشايدت
entries.english: Do not sleep the night here, if you want to live; otherwise, death will unmask an ambush for you in this place.’”

entries.farsi: و آن يكى گفتى كه شب قفلى نهيد / غافلى كايد شما كم ره دهيد
entries.english: And another would say, “Bolt at night, a heedless person comes, do not admit him.”

sections.title_fa: مهمان آمدن در آن مسجد
sections.title_en: How the guest came into the mosque.

entries.farsi: تا يكى مهمان در آمد وقت شب / ‏كاو شنيده بود آن صيت عجب
entries.english: A guest arrived at nightfall who had heard that marvellous rumour.

entries.farsi: از براى آزمون مى‏آزمود / ز انكه بس مردانه و جان سير بود
entries.english: He was testing in order to put to the proof, for he was very valiant and surfeited with life.

entries.farsi: گفت كم گيرم سر و اشكمبه‏اى / ‏رفته گير از گنج جان يك حبه‏اى
entries.english: He said, “I take little account of a head and belly: suppose that one grain is gone from the spirit’s treasure,

entries.farsi: صورت تن گو برو من كيستم / ‏نقش كم نايد چو من باقيستم
entries.english: Let the bodily form go: who am I? Is not the figure of small account when I am enduring forever?

entries.farsi: تا نيفتد بانگ نفخش اين طرف / ‏تا رهد آن گوهر از تنگين صدف
entries.english: Since by the grace of God the spirit was breathed into me, I am the breath of God kept apart from the windpipe of the body,

entries.farsi: چون نفخت بودم از لطف خدا / نفخ حق باشم ز ناى تن جدا
entries.english: To the end that the sound of His breathing should not fall in this direction and that that pearl should escape from the narrow shell.

entries.farsi: چون تمنوا موت گفت اى صادقين / ‏صادقم جان را بر افشانم بر اين
entries.english: Since God said, ‘Desire death, O you that are sincere,’ I am sincere: I will lavish my soul upon this.”

sections.title_fa: مالمت كردن اهل مسجد آن مهمان عاشق را از شب خفتن در آن جا و تهديد كردن مر او را
sections.title_en: How the people of the mosque blamed the lover-guest for sleeping the night there

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندش كه هين اينجا مخسب / ‏تا نكوبد جان ستانت همچو كسب
entries.english: and threatened him.

entries.farsi: كه غريبى و نمى‏دانى ز حال / ‏كاندر اينجا هر كه خفت آمد زوال
entries.english: The people said to him, “Beware! Do not sleep here, lest the Taker of the soul pound you like the dregs of sesame-grain,
For you are a stranger and ignorant of the fact that anyone who sleeps in this place perishes.

entries.farsi: اتفاقى نيست اين ما بارها / ‏ديده‏ايم و جمله اصحاب نهى
entries.english: This is not an occurrence: we and all those possessed of intelligence have often witnessed this.

entries.farsi: هر كه آن مسجد شبى مسكن شدش / ‏نيم شب مرگ هالهل آمدش
entries.english: To whomever that mosque gave lodging for a single night, poisonous death came to him at midnight.

entries.farsi: از يكى ما تا به صد اين ديده‏ايم / ‏نه به تقليد از كسى بشنيده‏ايم
entries.english: We have seen this not once but a hundred times: we have not heard it at second-hand from any one.

entries.farsi: گفت الدين نصيحه آن رسول / ‏آن نصيحت در لغت ضد غلول
entries.english: The Prophet said, ‘The religion is sincerity (nasíhat)’: that nasíhat etymologically is the opposite of unfaithfulness (ghulúl).

entries.farsi: اين نصيحت راستى در دوستى / ‏در غلولى خاين و سگ پوستى
entries.english: This nasíhat is ‘to be true in friendship’: in an act of ghulúl you are treacherous and currish.

entries.farsi: بى‏خيانت اين نصيحت از وداد / مى‏نماييمت مگرد از عقل و داد
entries.english: We are showing this sincerity towards you, without treachery, from love: do not turn away from reason and justice!”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن عاشق عاذالن را
sections.title_en: The lover’s reply to those who chide him

entries.farsi: منبلى‏ام زخم جو و زخم خواه / ‏عافيت كم جوى از منبل به راه
entries.english: He said, “O sincere advisers, I have become unrepentantly weary of the world of life.

entries.farsi: گفت او اى ناصحان من بى‏ندم / ‏از جهان زندگى سير آمدم
entries.english: I am an idle vagabond, seeking blows and desiring blows: do not seek rectitude from the vagabond on the road.

entries.farsi: منبلى نى كاو بود خود برگ جو / منبلى‏ام الابالى مرگ جو
entries.english: I am not the vagabond who in truth is a seeker of provender: I am the reckless vagabond the seeker of death.

entries.farsi: آن نه كاو بر هر دكانى بر زند / بل جهد از كون و كانى بر زند
entries.english: I am not the vagabond who gets small money into his palm, the nimble vagabond who would cross this bridge—

entries.farsi: منبلى نى كاو به كف پول آورد / منبلى چستى كز اين پل بگذرد
entries.english: Not the one who cleaves to every shop; nay, but springs away from existence and strikes upon a mine.

entries.farsi: مرگ شيرين گشت و نقلم زين سرا / چون قفس هشتن پريدن مرغ را
entries.english: Death and migration from this abode has become as sweet to me as leaving the cage and flying to the bird—

entries.farsi: آن قفس كه هست عين باغ در / مرغ مى‏بيند گلستان و شجر
entries.english: The cage that is in the very midst of the garden, the bird beholds the rose-beds and the trees,

entries.farsi: جوق مرغان از برون گرد قفص / ‏خوش همى‏خوانند ز آزادى قصص
entries.english: Outside, round the cage, a multitude of birds is sweetly chanting tales of liberty:

entries.farsi: مرغ را اندر قفس ز آن سبزه‏زار / نه خورش مانده است و نه صبر و قرار
entries.english: At that verdant place neither food remains to the bird in the cage, nor patience and rest,

entries.farsi: سر ز هر سوراخ بيرون مى‏كند / تا بود كاين بند از پا بر كند
entries.english: It puts out its head through every hole that maybe it may tear off this fetter from its leg.

entries.farsi: چون دل و جانش چنين بيرون بود / آن قفس را در گشايى چون بود
entries.english: Since its heart and soul are outside like this, how will it be when you open the cage?”

entries.farsi: نه چنان مرغ قفس در اندهان / ‏گرد بر گردش به حلقه گربگان
entries.english: Not such is the bird caged amidst anxieties— cats round about it in a ring:

entries.farsi: كى بود او را در اين خوف و حزن / ‏آرزوى از قفس بيرون شدن
entries.english: How, in this dread and sorrow, should it have the desire to go out of the cage?

entries.farsi: او همى‏خواهد كز اين ناخوش حصص / صد قفس باشد به گرد اين قفص
entries.english: It wishes that, from this unwelcome plucking, there might be a hundred cages round about this cage.

sections.title_fa: عشق جالينوس بر اين حيات دنيا بود كه هنر او همين جا به كار مى‏آيد هنرى نورزيده است كه در آن بازار به كار آيد آن جا خود را به عوام يكسان مى‏بيند
sections.title_en: The love of Galen is for this present life, for only here does his art avail; he has not practiced any art that avails in yonder market: there he sees himself to be the same as the vulgar.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه گفت جالينوس راد / از هواى اين جهان و از مراد
entries.english: That is even as wise Galen said on account of passion for this world and because of what he desired—

entries.farsi: راضيم كز من بماند نيم جان / ‏كه ز كون استرى بينم جهان
entries.english: “I am content that half of my vital spirit should remain, so that I may see the world through the mule’s arse.”

entries.farsi: گربه مى‏بيند به گرد خود قطار / مرغش آيس گشته بوده‏ست از مطار
entries.english: He sees around him cats in troops: his bird has despaired of flying;

entries.farsi: يا عدم ديده‏ست غير اين جهان / ‏در عدم ناديده او حشرى نهان
entries.english: Or he has deemed all except this world to be non-existence and has not perceived in non-existence a hidden resurrection.

entries.farsi: چون جنين كش مى‏كشد بيرون كرم / ‏مى‏گريزد او سپس سوى شكم
entries.english: Like the embryo which Bounty is drawing forth: it is fleeing back towards the belly.

entries.farsi: لطف رويش سوى مصدر مى‏كند / او مقر در پشت مادر مى‏كند
entries.english: Grace is turning its face towards the place of exit; it is making its abode in the mother’s loins,

entries.farsi: كه اگر بيرون فتم زين شهر و كام / ‏اى عجب بينم به ديده اين مقام
entries.english: Saying, “Oh, I wonder, if I fall outside of this city and pleasure, shall I see with my eye this dwelling-place;

entries.farsi: يا درى بودى در آن شهر وخم / ‏كه نظاره كردمى اندر رحم
entries.english: Or would there be in that noisome city a door, so that I might gaze into the womb,

entries.farsi: يا چو چشمه‏ى سوزنى را هم بدى / ‏كه ز بيرونم رحم ديده شدى
entries.english: Or would there be for me a path, as the eye of a needle, so that the womb might become visible to me from outside?”

entries.farsi: آن جنين هم غافل است از عالمى / ‏همچو جالينوس او نامحرمى
entries.english: That embryo, too, is unaware of a world: it is one unfamiliar, like Galen.

entries.farsi: او نداند كان رطوباتى كه هست / ‏آن مدد از عالم بيرونى است
entries.english: It does not know that the humours which exist are supplied from the external world,

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه چار عنصر در جهان / ‏صد مدد آرد ز شهر المكان
entries.english: Even as the four elements in this world obtain a hundred supplies from the City beyond space

entries.farsi: آب و دانه در قفس گر يافته‏ست / ‏آن ز باغ و عرصه‏اى در تافته‏ست
entries.english: If it has found water and seeds in its cage, those have appeared from a Garden and Expanse.

entries.farsi: جانهاى انبيا بينند باغ / ‏زين قفس در وقت نقالن و فراغ
entries.english: The spirits of the prophets behold the Garden from this cage at the time of their being transported and freed;

entries.farsi: پس ز جالينوس و عالم فارغند / همچو ماه اندر فلك‏ها بازغند
entries.english: Hence they are free of Galen and the world: they are shining like the moon in the skies.

entries.farsi: ور ز جالينوس اين گفت افترى است / ‏پس جوابم بهر جالينوس نيست
entries.english: And if this saying from Galen is a fiction, then my answer is not for Galen,

entries.farsi: اين جواب آن كس آمد كاين بگفت / ‏كه نبودستش دل پر نور جفت
entries.english: This is the answer to the person who said it, for the luminous heart has not been his mate.

entries.farsi: مرغ جانش موش شد سوراخ جو / چون شنيد از گربگان او عرجوا
entries.english: The bird, his spirit, became a mouse seeking a hole, when it heard from the cats, “Halt!”

entries.farsi: ز آن سبب جانش وطن ديد و قرار / اندر اين سوراخ دنيا موش‏وار
entries.english: On that account his spirit, mouse-like, deemed its home and abode to be in this world-hole.

entries.farsi: هم در اين سوراخ بنايى گرفت / ‏در خور سوراخ دانايى گرفت
entries.english: In this hole, too, it began to build and acquired knowledge suitable to the hole;

entries.farsi: پيشه‏هايى كه مر او را در مزيد / كاندر اين سوراخ كار آيد گزيد
entries.english: It chose the trades advantageous to it, which would be of use in this hole.

entries.farsi: ز انكه دل بر كند از بيرون شدن / ‏بسته شد راه رهيدن از بدن
entries.english: Inasmuch as it turned its heart away from going forth, the way of deliverance from the body was barred.

entries.farsi: عنكبوت ار طبع عنقا داشتى / از لعابى خيمه كى افراشتى
entries.english: If the spider had the nature of the Anqa, how should it have reared a tent of some gossamer?

entries.farsi: گربه كرده چنگ خود اندر قفص / ‏نام چنگش درد و سرسام و مغص
entries.english: The cat has put its claws into the cage: the name of its claws is pain and delirium and gripes.

entries.farsi: گربه مرگ است و مرض چنگال او / مى‏زند بر مرغ و پر و بال او
entries.english: The cat is Death, and its claws are disease: it is striking at the bird and its plumage.

entries.farsi: گوشه گوشه مى‏جهد سوى دوا / مرگ چون قاضى است و رنجورى گوا
entries.english: He darts from corner to corner towards the remedy. Death is like the cadi, and the disease is the witness.

entries.farsi: چون پياده‏ى قاضى آمد اين گواه / ‏كه همى‏خواند ترا تا حكم گاه
entries.english: This witness comes, like the cadi’s footman, who summons you to the place of judgment.

entries.farsi: مهلتى مى‏خواهى از وى در گريز / گر پذيرد شد و گرنه گفت خيز
entries.english: You, in flight, beg him a respite: if he consents, it is granted; otherwise, he says, “Arise.”

entries.farsi: جستن مهلت دوا و چاره‏ها / كه زنى بر خرقه‏ى تن پاره‏ها
entries.english: The seeking of a respite consists in remedies and cures, that you may patch the tattered cloak, the body.

entries.farsi: عاقبت آيد صباحى خشم‏وار / چند باشد مهلت آخر شرم دار
entries.english: At last, one morning, he comes angrily, saying, “How long will the respite be? Now, please, be ashamed!”

entries.farsi: عذر خود از شه بخواه اى پر حسد / پيش از آن كه آن چنان روزى رسد
entries.english: O envious man, ask your pardon of the King ere such a day as that arrives.

entries.farsi: و آن كه در ظلمت براند بارگى / ‏بر كند ز آن نور دل يك بارگى
entries.english: And he who rides his horse into the darkness and altogether removes his heart from the Light

entries.farsi: مى‏گريزد از گواه و مقصدش / ‏كان گوا سوى قضا مى‏خواندش
entries.english: Is fleeing from the witness and his purpose; for that witness is calling him to judgment.

sections.title_fa: ديگر باره مالمت كردن اهل مسجد مهمان را از شب خفتن در آن مسجد
sections.title_en: How the people of the mosque blamed the guest once more for sleeping in the mosque by night.

entries.farsi: آن ز دور آسان نمايد به نگر / كه به آخر سخت باشد ره گذر
entries.english: The people said to him, “Do not act with foolhardiness, depart, lest your body and your soul end up in pawn.”

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندش مكن جلدى برو / تا نگردد جامه و جانت گرو
entries.english: From afar it seems easy, look well! For in the end the passage is grievous.

entries.farsi: خويشتن آويخت بس مرد و سكست / ‏وقت پيچا پيچ دست‏آويز جست
entries.english: Many a man hanged himself and broke and at the moment of agony sought something for his hand to cling to.

entries.farsi: پيشتر از واقعه آسان بود / در دل مردم خيال نيك و بد
entries.english: Before the battle, the fancy of good or evil is slight in a man’s heart;

entries.farsi: چون در آيد اندرون كارزار / آن زمان گردد بر آن كس كار زار
entries.english: When he enters into the fray, then to that person the matter becomes woeful.

entries.farsi: چون نه شيرى هين منه تو پاى پيش / ‏كان اجل گرگ است و جان تست ميش
entries.english: Since you are not a lion, beware, do not step forward, for that Doom is a wolf, and your soul is the sheep;

entries.farsi: كيست ابدال آن كه او مبدل شود / خمرش از تبديل يزدان خل شود
entries.english: But if you are one of the Abdal and your sheep has become a lion, come on securely, for your death has been over thrown.

entries.farsi: ور ز ابدالى و ميشت شير شد / ايمن آ كه مرگ تو سر زير شد
entries.english: Who is the Abdal? He that becomes transmuted, he whose wine is turned into vinegar by Divine transmutation

entries.farsi: ليك مستى شير گيرى و ز گمان / ‏شير پندارى تو خود را هين مران
entries.english: But you are drunk, pot and from opinion think yourself to be a lion: Beware, do not advance!

entries.farsi: در ميان همدگر مردانه‏اند / در غزا چون عورتان خانه‏اند
entries.english: God has said of the unrighteous Hypocrites, “Their valour amongst themselves is a great valour.

entries.farsi: گفت حق ز اهل نفاق ناسديد / بأسهم ما بينهم بأس شديد
entries.english: Amongst one another they are manly, in a warlike expedition they are as the women of the house.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر سپهدار غيوب / ‏ال شجاعة يا فتى قبل الحروب
entries.english: The Prophet; the commander-in-chief of the things unseen, said, “There is no bravery, O youth, before the battles.”

entries.farsi: وقت الف غزو مستان كف كنند / وقت جوش جنگ چون كف بى‏فنند
entries.english: The drunks make a froth when there is talk of war, when war is raging they are as unskilled as froth.

entries.farsi: وقت ذكر غزو شمشيرش دراز / وقت كر و فر تيغش چون پياز
entries.english: At the time when war is spoken of, his scimitar is long; at the time of combat his sword is like an onion.

entries.farsi: وقت انديشه دل او زخم جو / پس به يك سوزن تهى شد خيك او
entries.english: At the time of premeditation his heart is eager for wounds; then his bag is emptied by a single needle.

entries.farsi: من عجب دارم ز جوياى صفا / كاو رمد در وقت صيقل از جفا
entries.english: I marvel at the seeker of purity who at the time of polishing shrinks from being handled roughly.

entries.farsi: عشق چون دعوى جفا ديدن گواه / ‏چون گواهت نيست شد دعوى تباه
entries.english: Love is like the lawsuit; to suffer harsh treatment is the evidence: when you have no evidence, the lawsuit is lost.

entries.farsi: چون گواهت خواهد اين قاضى مرنج / ‏بوسه ده بر مار تا يابى تو گنج
entries.english: Do not be aggrieved when this Judge demands your evidence: kiss the snake in order that you may gain the treasure.

entries.farsi: آن جفا با تو نباشد اى پسر / بلكه با وصف بدى اندر تو در
entries.english: That harshness is not towards you, O son; no, towards the evil qualities within you

entries.farsi: بر نمد چوبى كه آن را مرد زد / بر نمد آن را نزد بر گرد زد
entries.english: The blows of the stick with which a man beats a rug he inflicts, not on the rug, but on the dust.

entries.farsi: گر بزد مر اسب را آن كينه كش / ‏آن نزد بر اسب زد بر سكسكش
entries.english: If that vindictive fellow lashes the horse, he directs the blows, not at the horse, but at its stumbling,

entries.farsi: تا ز سكسك وارهد خوش پى شود / شيره را زندان كنى تا مى‏شود
entries.english: In order that it may be delivered from stumbling and may move well: you imprison must in order that it may become wine.

entries.farsi: گفت او را كى زدم اى جان دوست / ‏من بر آن ديوى زدم كاو اندر اوست
entries.english: He said, “You have struck that little orphan so many blows: how were not you afraid of the Divine wrath?”

entries.farsi: گفت چندان آن يتيمك را زدى / ‏چون نترسيدى ز قهر ايزدى
entries.english: He said, “O soul and friend, when did I strike him? I struck at the devil that is in him,”

entries.farsi: مادر ار گويد ترا مرگ تو باد / مرگ آن خو خواهد و مرگ فساد
entries.english: If your mother says to you, “May you die!” she wishes the death of that nature and the death of iniquity.

entries.farsi: آن گروهى كز ادب بگريختند / آب مردى و آب مردان ريختند
entries.english: The folk who fled from correction dishonoured their manhood and men.

entries.farsi: عاذالنشان از وغا واراندند / تا چنين هيز و مخنث ماندند
entries.english: The jokers drove them back from the war, so that they remained so infamous and effeminate.

entries.farsi: الف و غره‏ى ژاژخا را كم شنو / با چنينها در صف هيجا مرو
entries.english: Do not you listen to the boasting and roaring of the driveller: do not go into the battle-line with such fellows.

entries.farsi: ز انكه زادوكم خبالا گفت حق / كز رفاق سست بر گردان ورق
entries.english: Since they would have added to you corruption, God said, “Turn the leaf from pusillanimous comrades,

entries.farsi: كه گر ايشان با شما همره شوند / غازيان بى‏مغز همچون كه شوند
entries.english: For if they go along with you, the warriors will become pith-less, like straw.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را با شما هم صف كنند / پس گريزند و دل صف بشكنند
entries.english: They put themselves in line with you; then they flee and break the heart of the line.

entries.farsi: هست بادام كم خوش بيخته / ‏به ز بسيارى به تلخ آميخته
entries.english: Therefore, better a little army without these persons than it should be mustered with the Hypocrites.”

entries.farsi: پس سپاهى اندكى بى‏اين نفر / به كه با اهل نفاق آيد حشر
entries.english: A few well-sifted almonds are better than a great many mixed with bitter.

entries.farsi: تلخ و شيرين در ژغاژغ يك شى‏اند / نقص از آن افتاد كه هم دل نى‏اند
entries.english: The bitter and the sweet are one thing in respect of rattling; the defect arises from their not being the same at heart.

entries.farsi: گبر ترسان دل بود كاو از گمان / ‏مى‏زيد در شك ز حال آن جهان
entries.english: The infidel is of timorous heart, for, from opinion, he lives in doubt as to the state of that world.

entries.farsi: مى‏رود در ره نداند منزلى / ‏گام ترسان مى‏نهد اعمى دلى
entries.english: He is going along the road; he does not know any stage: one blind in heart steps timidly.

entries.farsi: چون نداند ره مسافر چون رود / با ترددها و دل پر خون رود
entries.english: When the traveller does not know the way, how does he go? He goes with hesitations, while his heart is full of blood.

entries.farsi: هر كه گويد هاى اين سو راه نيست / ‏او كند از بيم آن جا وقف و ايست
entries.english: If anyone says, “Hey! This is not the way,” he will halt there and stand still terrified.

entries.farsi: ور بداند ره دل باهوش او / كى رود هر هاى و هو در گوش او
entries.english: But if his wise heart knows the way, how should every hey and ho go into his ear?

entries.farsi: پس مشو همراه اين اشتر دالن / ‏ز انكه وقت ضيق و بيمند آفالن
entries.english: Therefore do not journey with these camel-hearted ones, for in the hour of distress and danger they are the ones who sink;

entries.farsi: تو ز رعنايان مجو هين كارزار / تو ز طاوسان مجو صيد و شكار
entries.english: Then they flee and leave you alone, though in boasting they are the magic of Babylon.

entries.farsi: پس گريزند و ترا تنها هلند / گر چه اندر الف سحر بابلند
entries.english: Beware! Do not you request sybarites to fight; do not request peacocks to engage in the hunt and the chase.

entries.farsi: طبع طاوس است و وسواست كند / دم زند تا از مقامت بر كند
entries.english: The carnal nature is a peacock: it tempts you and talks idly, that it may remove you from your post.

sections.title_fa: گفتن شيطان قريش را كه به جنگ احمد آييد كه من ياريها كنم و قبيله‏ى خود را به يارى خوانم و ‏وقت مالقات صفين گريختن
sections.title_en: How Satan said to the Quraysh, “Go to war with Ahmad, for I will aid you and call my tribe to help”;
and how, when the two battle-lines confronted each other, he fled.

entries.farsi: همچو شيطان در سپه شد صد يكم / ‏خواند افسون كه إنني جار لكم
entries.english: As Satan became the hundred-and-first in the army and spoke beguiling words, saying, “Truly, I am a protector for you.”

entries.farsi: چون قريش از گفت او حاضر شدند / هر دو لشكر در مالقات آمدند
entries.english: When the Quraysh had assembled at his bidding and the two armies confronted each other,

entries.farsi: ديد شيطان از ماليك اسپهى / ‏سوى صف مومنان اندر رهى
entries.english: Satan espied a host of angels on a road beside the ranks of the Faithful.

entries.farsi: آن جُنُوداً لَمْ تَرَوْها صف زده / ‏گشت جان او ز بيم آتشكده
entries.english: Those troops that ye saw not, drawn up in ranks; and from terror his soul became a fire-temple.

entries.farsi: پاى خود وا پس كشيده مى‏گرفت / ‏كه همى‏بينم سپاهى من شگفت
entries.english: Turning on his heel, he began to retreat, saying, “I behold a marvellous host”—

entries.farsi: أَي أخاف اللهَّ ما لي منه عون / ‏اذهبوا إِنِّي أَرى‏ ما ال ترون
entries.english: That is, “I fear God: I have no help from Him. Go! Truly, I see what you do not.”

entries.farsi: گفت حارث اى سراقه شكل هين / ‏دى چرا تو مى‏نگفتى اين چنين
entries.english: Harith said, “Hey, O you that have the form of Suraqa, why were not you saying such-like words yesterday?”

entries.farsi: مى‏نبينى غير اين ليك اى تو ننگ / ‏آن زمان الف بود اين وقت جنگ
entries.english: He replied, “At this moment I see destruction.” He said, “You see the puniest of the Arabs.

entries.farsi: گفت اين دم من همى‏بينم حرب / ‏گفت مى‏بينى جعاشيش عرب
entries.english: You are seeing nothing but this; but, O you disgrace, that was the time of talk, and this is the time of battle.

entries.farsi: دى همى‏گفتى كه پايندان شدم / ‏كه بودتان فتح و نصرت دم‏به‏دم
entries.english: Yesterday you were saying, ‘I pledge myself that victory and Divine aid will always be yours.’

entries.farsi: دى زعيم الجيش بودى اى لعين / ‏وين زمان نامرد و ناچيز و مهين
entries.english: Yesterday you were the surety for the army, O accursed one, and now you art cowardly, good-for-nothing, and vile,

entries.farsi: تا بخورديم آن دم تو و آمديم / ‏تو به تون رفتى و ما هيزم شديم
entries.english: So that we swallowed those words of your and came, you have gone to the bath-stove and we have become the fuel.”

entries.farsi: چون كه حارث با سراقه گفت اين / ‏از عتابش خشمگين شد آن لعين
entries.english: When Harith said this to Suraqa, that accursed one was enraged at his reproaches.

entries.farsi: دست خود خشمين ز دست او كشيد / چون ز گفت اوش درد دل رسيد
entries.english: He angrily withdrew his hand from his hand, since his heart was pained by his words.

entries.farsi: سينه‏اش را كوفت شيطان و گريخت / ‏خون آن بى‏چارگان زين مكر ريخت
entries.english: Satan smote his breast and fled: by means of this plot he shed the blood of those wretched men.

entries.farsi: كوفت اندر سينه‏اش انداختش / ‏پس گريزان شد چو هيبت تاختش
entries.english: After he had ruined so great a multitude, he then said, “Lo, I am quit of you.”

entries.farsi: چون كه ويران كرد چندين عالم او / ‏پس بگفت إِنِّي بَرِي‏ءٌ منكم
entries.english: He smote him on the breast and overthrew him; then he turned to flee, since terror urged him on.

entries.farsi: نفس و شيطان هر دو يك تن بوده‏اند / در دو صورت خويش را بنموده‏اند
entries.english: The fleshly soul and the Devil have both been one person; they have manifested themselves in two forms,

entries.farsi: دشمنى دارى چنين در سر خويش / ‏مانع عقل است و خصم جان و كيش
entries.english: Like the angel and the intellect, which were one, became two forms for the sake of His wise purposes.

entries.farsi: چون فرشته و عقل كايشان يك بدند / بهر حكمتهاش دو صورت شدند
entries.english: You have such an enemy as this in your inward part: he is the preventer of the intellect, and the adversary of the spirit and of religion.

entries.farsi: يك نفس حمله كند چون سوسمار / پس به سوراخى گريزد در فرار
entries.english: At one moment he dashes forward like the Libyan lizard; then in flight he darts away into a hole.

entries.farsi: در دل او سوراخها دارد كنون / ‏سر ز هر سوراخ مى‏آرد برون
entries.english: Just now he has holes in the heart, and from every hole he is putting out his head.

entries.farsi: نام پنهان گشتن ديو از نفوس / ‏و اندر آن سوراخ رفتن شد خنوس
entries.english: The name that denotes the Devil’s becoming hidden from souls and going into that hole is khunús,

entries.farsi: كه خنوسش چون خنوس قنفذ است / ‏چون سر قنفذ و را آمد شد است
entries.english: For his khunús is like the khunús of the hedgehog: like the head of the hedgehog, he pops in and out;

entries.farsi: كه خدا آن ديو را خناس خواند / كاو سر آن خار پشتك را بماند
entries.english: For God has called the Devil Khannás, because he resembles the head of the little hedgehog.

entries.farsi: تا چو فرصت يافت سر آرد برون / ‏زين چنين مكرى شود مارش زبون
entries.english: The head of the hedgehog is continually being hidden because of its fear of the cruel hunter,

entries.farsi: مى نهان گردد سر آن خار پشت / ‏دم‏به‏دم از بيم صياد درشت
