entries.english: Until, when it has found an opportunity, it puts out its head: by such a stratagem the snake becomes its prey.

entries.farsi: گر نه نفس از اندرون راهت زدى / ‏ره زنان را بر تو دستى كى بدى
entries.english: If the fleshly soul had not waylaid you from within, how would the brigands have any power to lay a hand upon you?

entries.farsi: ز آن عوان مقتضى كه شهوت است / ‏دل اسير حرص و آز و آفت است
entries.english: On account of the urgent follower, who is Lust, the heart is captive to greed and cupidity and bane.

entries.farsi: ز آن عوان سر شدى دزد و تباه / ‏تا عوانان را به قهر تست راه
entries.english: On account of that inward follower you have become thievish and depraved, so that the way is for the followers to coerce you.

entries.farsi: در خبر بشنو تو اين پند نكو / بين جنبيكم لكم أعدى عدو
entries.english: Listen to this good counsel in the Traditions— “Your worst enemy is between your two sides.”

entries.farsi: طمطراق اين عدو مشنو گريز / كاو چو ابليس است در لج و ستيز
entries.english: Do not listen to the pompous talk of this enemy; flee, for she is like Iblis in obstinately wrangling and quarrelling.

entries.farsi: بر تو او از بهر دنيا و نبرد / آن عذاب سرمدى را سهل كرد
entries.english: For the sake of this world and for contention’s sake she has made the everlasting torment easy to you.

entries.farsi: چه عجب گر مرگ را آسان كند / او ز سحر خويش صد چندان كند
entries.english: What wonder, if she makes death easy? By her magic she does a hundred times as much (as this).

entries.farsi: سحر كاهى را به صنعت كه كند / باز كوهى را چو كاهى مى‏تند
entries.english: Magic makes a straw a mountain by artifice; again, it weaves a mountain like a straw.

entries.farsi: زشتها را نغز گرداند به فن / ‏نغزها را زشت گرداند به ظن
entries.english: It makes ugly things beautiful by means of sleight; it makes beautiful things ugly by means of opinion.

entries.farsi: كار سحر اين است كاو دم مى‏زند / هر نفس قلب حقايق مى‏كند
entries.english: The work of magic is this, that it breathes and at every breath transforms realities.

entries.farsi: آدمى را خر نمايد ساعتى / ‏آدمى سازد خرى را و آيتى
entries.english: At one time it shows a man in the guise of an ass, it makes an ass a man and a notable.

entries.farsi: اين چنين ساحر درون تست و سر / إن في الوسواس سحرا مستتر
entries.english: Such a magician is within you and latent: truly, there is a concealed magic in temptation;

entries.farsi: اندر آن عالم كه هست اين سحرها / ساحران هستند جادويى گشا
entries.english: In the world in which are these magic arts, there are magicians who defeat sorcery.

entries.farsi: اندر آن صحرا كه رست اين زهر تر / نيز روييده‏ست ترياق اى پسر
entries.english: In the plain where this fresh poison grew, there has also grown the antidote, O son.

entries.farsi: گويدت ترياق از من جو سپر / كه ز زهرم من به تو نزديكتر
entries.english: The antidote says to you “Seek from me a shield, for I am nearer than the poison to you.

entries.farsi: گفت او سحر است و ويرانى تو / گفت من سحر است و دفع سحر او
entries.english: Her words are magic and your ruin; my words are magic and the counter-charm to her magic.”

sections.title_fa: ‏مكرر كردن عاذالن پند را بر آن مهمان آن مسجد مهمان كش
sections.title_en: How the fault-finders repeated their advice to the guest of the guest-killing mosque.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه ان فى البيان / ‏سحرا و حق گفت آن خوش پهلوان
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Truly, there is a magic in eloquence”; and that goodly hero spoke the truth.

entries.farsi: هين مكن جلدى برو اى بو الكرم / ‏مسجد و ما را مكن زين متهم
entries.english: “Hey, do not commit a foolhardy act, depart, O generous man, and do not make the mosque and us suspected on this account;

entries.farsi: كه بگويد دشمنى از دشمنى / ‏آتشى در ما زند فردا دنى
entries.english: For an enemy will speak form enmity, and to-morrow the villain will rouse a fire against us,

entries.farsi: كه بتاسانيد او را ظالمى / ‏بر بهانه‏ى مسجد او بد سالمى
entries.english: Saying, ‘some wicked man strangled him, on the pretext of the mosque he was safe,

entries.farsi: تا بهانه‏ى قتل بر مسجد نهد / چون كه بد نام است مسجد او جهد
entries.english: So that he might impute the murder to the mosque and, since the mosque has a bad name, might escape.’

entries.farsi: تهمتى بر ما منه اى سخت جان / ‏كه نه‏ايم ايمن ز مكر دشمنان
entries.english: Do not lay any suspicion upon us, O man of valiant spirit, for we are not secure from the craft of enemies.

entries.farsi: هين برو جلدى مكن سودا مپز / كه نتان پيمود كيوان را به گز
entries.english: Come now, depart! Do not be foolhardy, do not cherish vain desire, for it is impossible to measure Saturn by the ell.

entries.farsi: چون تو بسياران بالفيده ز بخت / ‏ريش خود بر كنده يك يك لخت لخت
entries.english: Many like you have chattered of luck, they have torn out their beards, one by one, piecemeal.

entries.farsi: هين برو كوتاه كن اين قيل و قال / ‏خويش و ما را در ميفكن در وبال
entries.english: Hey, begone! Cut short this palaver! Do not cast yourself and us into woe”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن مهمان ايشان را و مثل آوردن به دفع‏كردن حارس كشت به بانگ دف از كشت شترى را ‏كه كوس محمودى بر پشت او زدندى
sections.title_en: How the guest answered them and adduced the parable of the guardian of the wheat field who, by
making a noise with the tom-tom, sought to drive away from the wheat-field a camel on whose back they were beating the big kettle-drum of Mahmud.

entries.farsi: كودكى كاو حارس كشتى بدى / ‏طبلكى در دفع مرغان مى‏زدى
entries.english: He said, “O friends, I am not one of the devils that my sinews should fail at a single la hawl.

entries.farsi: گفت اى ياران از آن ديوان نى‏ام / ‏كه ز ال حولى ضعيف آيد پيم
entries.english: A boy, who was the guardian of a wheat field, used to beat a tom-tom in order to keep off the birds,

entries.farsi: تا رميدى مرغ ز آن طبلك ز كشت / ‏كشت از مرغان بد بى‏خوف گشت
entries.english: So that the birds, at the tom-tom, were scared away from the field, and the field became safe from evil birds.

entries.farsi: چون كه سلطان شاه محمود كريم / ‏بر گذر زد آن طرف خيمه‏ى عظيم
entries.english: When the Sultan, the noble King Mahmud, pitched a great tent in that neighbourhood as he passed on the way

entries.farsi: با سپاهى همچو استاره‏ى اثير / انبه و پيروز و صفدر ملك گير
entries.english: With an army like the stars of heaven, numerous and victorious, one that pierces the ranks and takes possession of empire—

entries.farsi: اشترى بد كاو بدى حمال كوس / ‏بختيى بد پيش رو همچون خروس
entries.english: There was a camel that carried the kettle-drum: it was a Bactrian, going in front like a cock:

entries.farsi: بانگ كوس و طبل بر وى روز و شب / ‏مى‏زدى اندر رجوع و در طلب
entries.english: Day and night he used loudly to beat the big kettle-drum and the drum on its back in returning and in setting out.

entries.farsi: اندر آن مزرع در آمد آن شتر / كودك آن طبلك بزد در حفظ بر
entries.english: That camel entered the wheat field, and the boy beat his tom-tom to protect the wheat.

entries.farsi: عاقلى گفتش مزن طبلك كه او / پخته‏ى طبل است و با آتش است خو
entries.english: An intelligent man said to him, ‘Don’t beat the tom-tom, for he is well-seasoned by the drum; he is accustomed to it.

entries.farsi: پيش او چه بود تبوراك تو طفل / ‏كه كشد او طبل سلطان بيست كفل
entries.english: What is your little tom-tom, child, to him; since he carries the Sultan’s drum twenty times the size?’

entries.farsi: خود تبوراك است اين تهديدها / پيش آن چه ديده است اين ديدها
entries.english: I am a lover, one who has been sacrificed to Nothingness: my soul is the band-stand for the drum of tribulation.

entries.farsi: عاشقم من كشته‏ى قربان ال / جان من نوبتگه طبل بال
entries.english: Truly, these threats are a little tom-tom beside that which these eyes have seen.

entries.farsi: اى حريفان من از آنها نيستم / ‏كز خيالاتى در اين ره بيستم
entries.english: O comrades, I am not one of those, that because of idle fancies I should halt on the Way.

entries.farsi: من چو اسماعيليانم بى‏حذر / بل چو اسماعيل آزادم ز سر
entries.english: I am unafraid, like the Ismailis; nay, like Ishmael I am free from head.

entries.farsi: فارغم از طمطراق و از ريا / قل تعالوا گفت جانم را بيا
entries.english: I am done with pomp and ostentation. ‘Say, come ye ’: He said to my soul, ‘Come.’”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه جاد في السلف / ‏بالعطية من تيقن بالخلف
entries.english: The Prophet has said that one who feels sure of the recompense will give generously beforehand.

entries.farsi: هر كه بيند مر عطا را صد عوض / ‏زود در بازد عطا را زين غرض
entries.english: Whoever sees a hundred compensations for the gift will at once give away the gift with this object.

entries.farsi: جمله در بازار از آن گشتند بند / تا چو سود افتاد مال خود دهند
entries.english: All have become tied in the bazaar, to the end that when gain occurs they may give their money.

entries.farsi: زر در انبانها نشسته منتظر / تا كه سود آيد به بذل آيد مصر
entries.english: With gold in their money-bags, they are seated expectantly that the gain may come and that he who persists may begin to squander.

entries.farsi: چون ببيند كاله‏اى در ربح بيش / ‏سرد گردد عشقش از كالاى خويش
entries.english: When he sees a piece of merchandise exceeding in profit, his fondness for his own goods becomes chilled;

entries.farsi: گرم ز آن مانده است با آن كاو نديد / كاله‏هاى خويش را ربح و مزيد
entries.english: He has remained enamoured of those, because he perceived no profit and advantage superior to his own goods.

entries.farsi: همچنين علم و هنرها و حرف / ‏چون نديد افزون از آنها در شرف
entries.english: Similarly, knowledge and accomplishments and trades: since he has not seen superior to them in excellence.

entries.farsi: تا به از جان نيست جان باشد عزيز / چون به آمد نام جان شد چيز ليز
entries.english: While nothing is better than life, life is precious; when a better appears, the name of life becomes a slippery thing.

entries.farsi: اين تصور وين تخيل لعبت است / ‏تا تو طفلى پس بدانت حاجت است
entries.english: The lifeless doll is as life to the child until he has grown up to manhood.

entries.farsi: لعبت مرده بود جان طفل را / تا نگشت او در بزرگى طفل‏زا
entries.english: Imagination and fancy are the doll: so long as you are a child, you have need of them;

entries.farsi: چون ز طفلى رست جان شد در وصال / ‏فارغ از حس است و تصوير و خيال
entries.english: When the spirit has escaped from childishness, it is in union: it is done with sense-perception and imagination and fancy.

entries.farsi: نيست محرم تا بگويم بى‏نفاق / ‏تن زدم و اهلل أعلم بالوفاق
entries.english: There is no confidant that I should speak without insincerity. I will keep silence, and God best knows the accord.

entries.farsi: مال و تن برفند ريزان فنا / ‏حق خريدارش كه اللهَّ اشترى
entries.english: The goods and the body are snow melting away to naught; God is their buyer, for God has purchased.

entries.farsi: برفها ز آن از ثمن اولى‏ستت / ‏كه تويى در شك يقينى نيستت
entries.english: The snows seem to you better than the price, because you are in doubt: you have no certainty,

entries.farsi: وين عجب ظن است در تو اى مهين / ‏كه نمى‏پرد به بستان يقين
entries.english: And in you, O contemptible man, there is this marvellous opinion that does not fly to the garden of certainty.

entries.farsi: هر گمان تشنه‏ى يقين است اى پسر / مى‏زند اندر تزايد بال و پر
entries.english: O son, every opinion is thirsting for certainty and emulously flapping its wings.

entries.farsi: چون رسد در علم پس بر پا شود / مر يقين را علم او بويا شود
entries.english: When it attains to knowledge, then the wing becomes a foot, and its knowledge begins to scent certainty,

entries.farsi: ز انكه هست اندر طريق مفتتن / ‏علم كمتر از يقين و فوق ظن
entries.english: For in the tested Way knowledge is inferior to certainty, but above opinion.

entries.farsi: علم جوياى يقين باشد بدان / ‏و آن يقين جوياى ديد است و عيان
entries.english: Know that knowledge is a seeker of certainty, and certainty is a seeker of vision and intuition.

entries.farsi: اندر ألهيكم بجو اين را كنون / ‏از پس كَلاَّ پس لَوْ تعلمون
entries.english: Seek this now, in Alhakum, after kalla and after lau ta‘lamún.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشد دانش به بينش اى عليم / ‏گر يقين گشتى ببينندى جحيم
entries.english: Knowledge leads to vision, O knowing one: if it became certainty, they would see Hell.

entries.farsi: ديد زايد از يقين بى‏امتهال / ‏آن چنانك از ظن، مى‏زايد خيال
entries.english: Vision is immediately born of certainty, just as fancy is born of opinion.

entries.farsi: اندر ألهيكم بيان اين ببين / ‏كه شود عِلْمَ الْيَقِينِ عين اليقين
entries.english: See in Alhakum the explanation of this, that the knowledge of certainty becomes the intuition of certainty.

entries.farsi: از گمان و از يقين بالاترم / ‏و ز مالمت بر نمى‏گردد سرم
entries.english: “I am higher than opinion and certainty, and my head is not to be turned aside by blame.

entries.farsi: چون دهانم خورد از حلواى او / چشم روشن گشتم و بيناى او
entries.english: Since my mouth ate of His sweetmeat, I have become clear-eyed and a seer of Him.

entries.farsi: پا نهم گستاخ چون خانه روم / ‏پا نلرزانم نه كورانه روم
entries.english: I step boldly when I go home: I do not let my feet tremble; I do not walk like the blind.

entries.farsi: آن چه زد بر سرو و قدش راست كرد / و انچه از وى نرگس و نسرين بخورد
entries.english: That which God said to the rose, and caused it to laugh, He said to my heart, and made it a hundred times more (beautiful).

entries.farsi: آن چه گل را گفت حق خندانش كرد / با دل من گفت و صد چندانش كرد
entries.english: That which touched the cypress and made its stature straight, and that of which the narcissus and wild-rose partook;

entries.farsi: آن چه نى را كرد شيرين جان و دل / ‏و انچه خاكى يافت زو نقش چگل
entries.english: That which made sweet the soul and heart of the sugar-cane, and that from which the creature of earth gained the form of Chigil;

entries.farsi: آن چه ابرو را چنان طرار ساخت / ‏چهره را گلگونه و گلنار ساخت
entries.english: That which made the eyebrow so ravishing and made the face rose-coloured and the pomegranate-flower;

entries.farsi: مر زبان را داد صد افسون‏گرى / ‏و انكه كان را داد زر جعفرى
entries.english: Gave a hundred enchantments to the tongue, and that which gave the gold of Ja‘far to the mine.

entries.farsi: بر دلم زد تير و سوداييم كرد / عاشق شكر و شكر خاييم كرد
entries.english: When the door of the Armoury was opened, the amorous glances became archers,

entries.farsi: عاشق آنم كه هر آن آن اوست / ‏عقل و جان جاندار يك مرجان اوست
entries.english: And shot arrows at my heart and frenzied me and made me in love with thanksgiving and sugar-chewing.

entries.farsi: چون در زرادخانه باز شد / غمزه‏هاى چشم تير انداز شد
entries.english: I am the lover of that One to whom every that belongs: of a single pearl of His the bodyguard is Intellect and Spirit.

entries.farsi: من نالفم ور بالفم همچو آب / ‏نيست در آتش كشى‏ام اضطراب
entries.english: I do not boast, or if I boast, like water, I have no trouble in quenching fire.

entries.farsi: چون بدزدم چون حفيظ مخزن اوست / ‏چون نباشم سخت رو پشت من اوست
entries.english: How should I steal when He is the keeper of the treasury? How should not I be hard-faced? He is my support.

entries.farsi: هر كه از خورشيد باشد پشت گرم / ‏سخت رو باشد نه بيم او را نه شرم
entries.english: Every one whose back is warmed by the Sun will be hard-faced: he will have neither dread nor shame.

entries.farsi: همچو روى آفتاب بى‏حذر / گشت رويش خصم سوز و پرده در
entries.english: His face has become foe-burning and veil-rending, like the face of the peerless Sun.

entries.farsi: هر پيمبر سخت رو بد در جهان / ‏يك سواره كوفت بر جيش شهان
entries.english: Every prophet was hard-faced in this world, and beat single-handed against the army of the kings,

entries.farsi: رو نگردانيد از ترس و غمى / ‏يك تنه تنها بزد بر عالمى
entries.english: And did not avert his face from any fear or pain, single and alone dashed against a world.

entries.farsi: سنگ باشد سخت رو و چشم شوخ / ‏او نترسد از جهان پر كلوخ
entries.english: The rock is hard-faced and bold-eyed: it is not afraid of the world that is full of brickbats;

entries.farsi: كان كلوخ از خشت زن يك لخت شد / سنگ از صنع خدايى سخت شد
entries.english: For those brickbats were made solid by the brick-maker, the rock was hardened by Divine art.

entries.farsi: گوسفندان گر برونند از حساب / ‏ز انبهيشان كى بترسد آن قصاب
entries.english: If the sheep are beyond count, how should the butcher be afraid of their numerousness?

entries.farsi: كلكم راع نبى چون راعى است / ‏خلق مانند رمه او ساعى است
entries.english: ‘Each of you is a shepherd’: the prophet is as the shepherd. The people are like the flock; he is the overseer.

entries.farsi: از رمه چوپان نترسد در نبرد / ليكشان حافظ بود از گرم و سرد
entries.english: The shepherd is not afraid of the sheep in contention, but is their protector from hot and cold.

entries.farsi: گر زند بانگى ز قهر او بر رمه / ‏دان ز مهر است آن كه دارد بر همه
entries.english: If he cries out in wrath against the flock, know it is from the love which he has for them all.

entries.farsi: هر زمان گويد به گوشم بخت نو / كه ترا غمگين كنم غمگين مشو
entries.english: New Fortune says into my ear every moment, ‘I will make you sorrowful, be not sorrowful.

entries.farsi: من ترا غمگين و گريان ز آن كنم / ‏تا كت از چشم بدان پنهان كنم
entries.english: I will make you sorrowful and weeping, to the end that I may hide you from the eyes of the wicked.

entries.farsi: تلخ گردانم ز غمها خوى تو / تا بگردد چشم بد از روى تو
entries.english: I will cause your temper to be soured with sorrows, in order that the evil eye may be averted from your face.

entries.farsi: نه تو صيادى و جوياى منى / ‏بنده و افكنده‏ى راى منى
entries.english: You are not a hunter and seeker of Me; you are My slave and prostrate before My providence.

entries.farsi: حيله انديشى كه در من در رسى / ‏در فراق و جستن من بى‏كسى
entries.english: You are thinking of devices whereby you may attain unto Me: in quitting and in seeking Me you are helpless.

entries.farsi: چاره مى‏جويد پى من درد تو / مى‏شنودم دوش آه سرد تو
entries.english: Your anguish is seeking a means for Me: I was listening yesterday to your heavy sighs.

entries.farsi: من توانم هم كه بى‏اين انتظار / ره دهم بنمايمت راه گذار
entries.english: I am even able, without this waiting, to give access and show unto you the way of passage,

entries.farsi: تا از اين گرداب دوران وارهى / ‏بر سر گنج وصالم پا نهى
entries.english: That you may be delivered from this whirlpool of Time and may set your foot upon the treasure of union with Me;

entries.farsi: ليك شيرينى و لذات مقر / هست بر اندازه‏ى رنج سفر
entries.english: But the sweetness and delights of the resting-place are in proportion to the pain of the journey.

entries.farsi: آن گه از شهر و ز خويشان بر خورى / ‏كز غريبى رنج و محنتها برى
entries.english: Then will you enjoy your town and your kinsfolk when you suffer pains and tribulations from exile.’”

sections.title_fa: تمثيل گريختن مومن و بى‏صبرى او در بال به اضطراب و بى‏قرارى نخود و ديگر حوايج در جوش ديگ و بر دويدن تا بيرون جهند
sections.title_en: Comparison of the true believer’s fleeing and his impatience in affliction to the agitation and restlessness of chick-peas and other pot-herbs when boiling in the pot,
and to their running upwards in order to jump out.

entries.farsi: بنگر اندر نخودى در ديگ چون / ‏مى‏جهد بالا چو شد ز آتش زبون
entries.english: Look at a chickpea in the pot, how it leaps up when it is subjected to the fire.

entries.farsi: هر زمان نخود بر آيد وقت جوش / ‏بر سر ديگ و بر آرد صد خروش
entries.english: At the time of its being boiled, the chickpea comes up continually to the top of the pot and raises a hundred cries,

entries.farsi: كه چرا آتش به من در مى‏زنى / ‏چون خريدى چون نگونم مى‏كنى
entries.english: Saying, “Why are you setting the fire on me? Since you bought me, how are you turning me upside down?”

entries.farsi: مى‏زند كفليز كدبانو كه نى / ‏خوش بجوش و برمجه ز آتش كنى
entries.english: The housewife goes on hitting it with the ladle. “No!” says she: “boil nicely and don’t jump away from one who makes the fire.

entries.farsi: ز آن نجوشانم كه مكروه منى / ‏بلكه تا گيرى تو ذوق و چاشنى
entries.english: I do not boil you because you are hateful to me: nay, it is that you may get taste and savour,

entries.farsi: تا غذا گردى بياميزى به جان / ‏بهر خوارى نيستت اين امتحان
entries.english: So that you may become nutriment and mingle with the spirit: this affliction of yours is not on account of being despised.

entries.farsi: آب مى‏خوردى به بستان سبز و تر / بهر اين آتش بده‏ست آن آب خور
entries.english: You, when green and fresh, were drinking water in the garden: that water-drinking was for the sake of this fire.”

entries.farsi: رحمتش سابق بده‏ست از قهر ز آن / ‏تا ز رحمت گردد اهل امتحان
entries.english: His mercy is prior to His wrath, to the end that by mercy he may suffer affliction.

entries.farsi: رحمتش بر قهر از آن سابق شده‏ست / ‏تا كه سرمايه‏ى وجود آيد به دست
entries.english: His mercy preceded His wrath in order that the stock-in trade, existence, should come to hand;

entries.farsi: ز انكه بى‏لذت نرويد لحم و پوست / ‏چون نرويد چه گدازد عشق دوست
entries.english: For, without pleasure, flesh and skin do not grow; and unless they grow, what shall the love of the Friend consume?

entries.farsi: باز لطف آيد براى عذر او / كه بكردى غسل و برجستى ز جو
entries.english: If, because of that requirement, acts of wrath come to pass, to the end that you may give up that stock-in-trade,

entries.farsi: ز آن تقاضا گر بيايد قهرها / تا كنى ايثار آن سرمايه را
entries.english: Again the Grace will come in order to excuse it, saying, “you have washed yourself and have leaped forth from the river.”

entries.farsi: گويد اى نخود چريدى در بهار / رنج مهمان تو شد نيكوش دار
entries.english: She says, “O chickpea, you did feed in the springtime: Pain has become your guest: entertain him well,

entries.farsi: تا كه مهمان باز گردد شكر ساز / پيش شه گويد ز ايثار تو باز
entries.english: That the guest may return, giving thanks, and may relate your generosity in the presence of the King,

entries.farsi: تا به جاى نعمتت منعم رسد / جمله نعمتها برد بر تو حسد
entries.english: So that the Bestower of favour may come to you instead of the favour, and that all favours may envy you.

entries.farsi: من خليلم تو پسر پيش بچك / ‏سر بنه إني أراني أذبحك
entries.english: I am Khalíl, and you are my son: lay your head before the knife: lo, I see that I shall sacrifice you.

entries.farsi: سر به پيش قهر نه دل بر قرار / تا ببرم حلقت اسماعيل‏وار
entries.english: Lay your head before wrath, with heart unmoved, that I may cut your throat, like Ismail.

entries.farsi: سر ببرم ليك اين سر آن سرى است / ‏كز بريده گشتن و مردن برى است
entries.english: I will cut off your head, but this head is the head that is immune from being cut off and dying;

entries.farsi: ليك مقصود ازل تسليم تست / ‏اى مسلمان بايدت تسليم جست
entries.english: Yet your giving yourself up is the object of eternal purpose: O Moslem, you must seek to give yourself up.

entries.farsi: اى نخود مى‏جوش اندر ابتال / تا نه هستى و نه خود ماند ترا
entries.english: Continue, O chickpea, to boil in tribulation, that neither existence nor self may remain to you.

entries.farsi: اندر آن بستان اگر خنديده‏اى / ‏تو گل بستان جان و ديده‏اى
entries.english: If you have laughed in that garden, you are the rose of the garden of the spirit and the eye.

entries.farsi: شو غذا و قوت و انديشه‏ها / شير بودى شير شو در بيشه‏ها
entries.english: If you have been parted from the garden of water and earth, you have become food in the mouth and have entered into the living.

entries.farsi: گر جدا از باغ آب و گل شدى / ‏لقمه گشتى اندر احيا آمدى
entries.english: Become nutriment and strength and thoughts! You were milk: be a lion in the jungles!

entries.farsi: از صفاتش رسته‏اى و اللهَّ نخست / در صفاتش باز رو چالاك و چست
entries.english: By God, you grew from His attributes in the beginning: go back nimbly and fleetly into His attributes.

entries.farsi: ز ابر و خورشيد و ز گردون آمدى / ‏پس شدى اوصاف و گردون برشدى
entries.english: You came from the cloud and the sun and the sky; then did you become attributes and ascend to heaven.

entries.farsi: آمدى در صورت باران و تاب / ‏مى‏روى اندر صفات مستطاب
entries.english: You came in the form of rain and heat: you will go into the goodly attributes.

entries.farsi: جزو شيد و ابر و انجمها بدى / ‏نفس و فعل و قول و فكرت‏ها شدى
entries.english: You were a part of the sun and the cloud and the stars: you became soul and action and speech and thoughts.”

entries.farsi: هستى حيوان شد از مرگ نبات / ‏راست آمد اقتلوني يا ثقات
entries.english: The existence of the animal arose from the death of the plant: “slay me, O trusty friends” is right.

entries.farsi: چون چنين بردى است ما را بعد مات / ‏راست آمد إن في قتلي حيات
entries.english: Since there is such a victory for us after the checkmate, “truly, in my being slain there is a life” are true.

entries.farsi: فعل و قول و صدق شد قوت ملك / ‏تا بدين معراج شد سوى فلك
entries.english: Action and speech and sincerity became the food of the angel, so that by means of this ladder he mounted to heaven,

entries.farsi: آن چنان كان طعمه شد قوت بشر / از جمادى بر شد و شد جانور
entries.english: Just as that morsel became the food of Man, it mounted from inanimateness and became possessed of soul.

entries.farsi: اين سخن را ترجمه پهناورى / گفته آيد در مقام ديگرى‏
entries.english: As regards this topic, a wide explanation will be given in another place.

entries.farsi: كاروان دايم ز گردون مى‏رسد / تا جتارت مى‏كند وا مى‏رود
entries.english: “The caravan is incessantly arriving from heaven that they may traffic and go back again.

entries.farsi: پس برو شيرين و خوش با اختيار / نه به تلخى و كراهت دزدوار
entries.english: Go, then, sweetly and gladly with free-will, not with bitterness and loathing, like a thief.

entries.farsi: ز آن حديث تلخ مى‏گويم ترا / نا ز تلخيها فرو شومي ترا
entries.english: I am speaking bitter words to you, in order that I may wash you of bitterness.

entries.farsi: ز آب سرد انگور افسرده رهد / سردى و افسردگى بيرون نهد
entries.english: The frozen grape is thawed by cold water and lays aside its coldness and congealment.

entries.farsi: تو ز تلخى چون كه دل پر خون شوى / پس ز تلخيها همه بيرون روى‏
entries.english: When, from bitterness, your heart is filled with blood, and then you will escape from all bitterness.

sections.title_fa: تمثيل صابر شدن مومن چون بر سر و خير بال واقف شود
sections.title_en: A comparison showing how the true believer becomes patient when he understands the inward meaning and the beneficial nature of tribulation

entries.farsi: سگ شكارى نيست او را طوق نيست / خام و ناجوشيده جز بى‏ذوق نيست‏
entries.english: A dog is not for hunting, he has no collar: the raw and uncooked is nothing but the insipid.”

entries.farsi: گفت نخود چون چنين است اى ستى / خوش بجوشم يارمي ده راستى‏
entries.english: The chickpea said, “Since it is so, O lady, I will gladly boil: give me help in verity!

entries.farsi: تو در اين جوشش چو معمار منى / كفچليزم زن كه بس خوش مى‏زنى‏
entries.english: In this boiling you art, as it were, my architect: smite me with the skimming spoon, for you smite very delightfully.

entries.farsi: همچو پيلم بر سرم زن زخم و داغ / تا نبينم خواب هندستان و باغ‏
entries.english: I am as the elephant: beat and brand my head that I may not dream of India and gardens;

entries.farsi: تا كه خود را در دهم در جوش من / تا رهى يامب در آن آغوش من‏
entries.english: So that I may submit myself to the boiling, to the end that I may find a way to that embrace;

entries.farsi: ز انكه انسان در غنا طاغى شود / همچو پيل خواب بني ياغى شود
entries.english: Because Man, in independence, grows insolent and becomes hostile, like the dreaming elephant

entries.farsi: پيل چون در خواب بيند هند را / پيلبان را نشنود آرد دغا
entries.english: When the elephant dreams of India, he does not listen to the driver and displays viciousness.”

sections.title_fa: عذر گفتن كدبانو با نخود و حكمت در جوش داشتن كدبانو نخود را
sections.title_en: How the housewife made apologies to the chickpea, and the wise purpose in her keeping the chickpea on the boil.

entries.farsi: آن ستى گويد و را كه پيش از اين / من چو تو بودم ز اجزاى زمني‏
entries.english: The dame says to it, “Formerly I, like you, was a part of the earth.

entries.farsi: چون بنوشيدم جهاد آذرى / ‏پس پذيرا گشتم و اندر خورى
entries.english: After I had drunk a fiery self-mortification, then I became an acceptable and worthy one.

entries.farsi: مدتى جوشيده‏ام اندر زمن / ‏مدتى ديگر درون ديگ تن
entries.english: For a long while, I boiled in Time; for another long while, in the pot of the body.

entries.farsi: زين دو جوشش قوت حسها شدم / ‏روح گشتم پس ترا استا شدم
entries.english: By reason of this double boiling I became strength to the senses: I became spirit: then I became your teacher.

entries.farsi: در جمادى گفتمى ز آن مى‏دوى / ‏تا شوى علم و صفات معنوى
entries.english: in the inanimate state I used to say, ‘You are running to the end that you may become knowledge and spiritual qualities.’

entries.farsi: چون شدى تو روح پس بار دگر / جوش ديگر كن ز حيوانى گذر
entries.english: Since I have become spirit, now boil once more and pass beyond animality.”

entries.farsi: از خدا مى‏خواه تا زين نكته‏ها / در نلغزى و رسى در منتها
entries.english: Beseech God continually that you may not stumble over these deep sayings and that you may arrive at the end,

entries.farsi: ز انكه از قرآن بسى گمره شدند / ز آن رسن قومى درون چه شدند
entries.english: For many have been led astray by the Qur’an: by that rope a multitude have fallen into the well.

entries.farsi: مر رسن را نيست جرمى اى عنود / چون ترا سوداى سربالا نبود
entries.english: There is no fault in the rope, O perverse man, inasmuch as you had no desire for the top.

sections.title_fa: باقى قصه‏ى مهمان آن مسجد مهمان كش و ثبات و صدق او
sections.title_en: The remainder of the story of the guest of that guest-killing mosque, and his firmness and sincerity

entries.farsi: آن غريب شهر سربالا طلب / ‏گفت مى‏خسبم در اين مسجد به شب
entries.english: That high-aspiring stranger to the town said, “I will sleep in this mosque at night.

entries.farsi: مسجدا گر كربالى من شوى / ‏كعبه‏ى حاجت رواى من شوى
entries.english: O mosque, if you become my Karbala, you will be the Ka’ba that fulfils my need.

entries.farsi: هين مرا بگذار اى بگزيده دار / تا رسن بازى كنم منصوروار
entries.english: Listen, give me leave, O chosen house, that I may perform a rope-dance, like Mansur!

entries.farsi: گر شديد اندر نصيحت جبرئيل / ‏مى‏نخواهد غوث در آتش خليل
entries.english: If in counselling you have become Gabriel, Khalil will not crave succour in the fire’.

entries.farsi: جبرئيال رو كه من افروخته / ‏بهترم چون عود و عنبر سوخته
entries.english: Go, O Gabriel, for, having been kindled, I, like aloes-wood and ambergris, am better burnt.

entries.farsi: جبرئيال گر چه يارى مى‏كنى / ‏چون برادر پاسدارى مى‏كنى
entries.english: O Gabriel, although you are helping and guarding like a brother,

entries.farsi: اى برادر من بر آذر چابكم / ‏من نه آن جانم كه گردم بيش و كم
entries.english: O brother, I am eager for the fire: I am not that spirit that I should become more and less.”

entries.farsi: جان حيوانى فزايد از علف / ‏آتشى بود و چو هيزم شد تلف
entries.english: The animal spirit is increased by fodder: it was a fire and was consumed like firewood.

entries.farsi: گر نگشتى هيزم او مثمر بدى / ‏تا ابد معمور و هم عامر بدى
entries.english: Had it not become firewood, it would have been fruitful: it would have prospered unto everlasting and would have caused prosperity.

entries.farsi: باد سوزان است اين آتش بدان / ‏پرتو آتش بود نه عين آن
entries.english: Know that this fire is a burning wind: it is a ray of fire, not the essence thereof.

entries.farsi: عين آتش در اثير آمد يقين / ‏پرتو و سايه‏ى وى است اندر زمين
entries.english: Assuredly the essence of fire is in the ether: on the earth there is its ray and shadow.

entries.farsi: الجرم پرتو نپايد ز اضطراب / ‏سوى معدن باز مى‏گردد شتاب
entries.english: Of necessity, the ray, on account of quivering, does not endure: it is speedily returning to its source.

entries.farsi: قامت تو برقرار آمد به ساز / سايه‏ات كوته دمى يك دم دراز
entries.english: Your stature is normally invariable, your shadow is now short, now long.

entries.farsi: ز انكه در پرتو نيابد كس ثبات / ‏عكسها وا گشت سوى امهات
entries.english: Inasmuch as no one finds permanence in the ray, the reflections return to origins.

entries.farsi: هين دهان بر بند فتنه لب گشاد / خشك آر اللهَّ أعلم بالرشاد
entries.english: Listen, close your mouth: Mischief has opened its lips. Dry up! God best knows the right way.

sections.title_fa: ‏ذكر خيال بد انديشيدن قاصر فهمان
sections.title_en: Account of the conception of evil fancies by those deficient in understanding.

entries.farsi: پيش از آنك اين قصه تا مخلص رسد / دود گندى آمد از اهل حسد
entries.english: Before this tale reaches the conclusion, there comes from the envious a vapour of stench.

entries.farsi: من نمى‏رنجم از اين ليك اين لگد / خاطر ساده دلى را پى كند
entries.english: I am not pained by it, but this kick may break the nerve of a simple-hearted man’s mind.

entries.farsi: خوش بيان كرد آن حكيم غزنوى / ‏بهر محجوبان مثال معنوى
entries.english: Well did the Sage of Ghazna set forth the spiritual parable for the sake of those who are veiled,

entries.farsi: كه ز قرآن گر نبيند غير قال / ‏اين عجب نبود ز اصحاب ضالل
entries.english: That if one see in the Qur’an naught but words, this is not surprising on the part of them that have lost the way,

entries.farsi: كز شعاع آفتاب پر ز نور / غير گرمى مى‏نيابد چشم كور
entries.english: Since the eye of the blind is sensible of naught but heat from the beams of the luminous sun.

entries.farsi: خربطى ناگاه از خر خانه‏اى / ‏سر برون آورد چون طعانه‏اى
entries.english: Suddenly a great booby popped his head out of an ass-stable, like a railing woman,

entries.farsi: كاين سخن پست است يعنى مثنوى / ‏قصه‏ى پيغمبر است و پى روى
entries.english: That this discourse, namely, the Masnaví, is low; it is the story of the Prophet and imitation;

entries.farsi: نى ذكر بحث و اسرار بلند / كه دوانند اوليا آن سو سمند
entries.english: There is no mention of investigation and the sublime mysteries towards which the saints make their steeds gallop;

entries.farsi: از مقامات تبتل تا فنا / پايه پايه تا مالقات خدا
entries.english: from the stations of asceticism to the passing away, step by step up to union with God,

entries.farsi: شرح و حد هر مقام و منزلى / ‏كه به پر زو بر پرد صاحب دلى
entries.english: the explanation and definition of every station and stage, so that by means of the wings thereof a man of heart should soar.

entries.farsi: چون كتاب اللهَّ بيامد هم بر آن / ‏اين چنين طعنه زدند آن كافران
entries.english: When the Book of God came, the unbelievers railed likewise at it too,

entries.farsi: كه اساطير است و افسانه‏ى نژند / نيست تعميقى و تحقيقى بلند
entries.english: Saying, “It is legends and paltry tales; there is no profound inquiry and lofty speculation;

entries.farsi: كودكان خرد فهمش مى‏كنند / نيست جز امر پسند و ناپسند
entries.english: The little children understand it; it is naught but things approved and disapproved—

entries.farsi: ذكر يوسف ذكر زلف پر خمش / ‏ذكر يعقوب و زليخا و غمش
entries.english: The account of Joseph, the account of his curly locks, the account of Jacob and Zalikha and her passion.

entries.farsi: ظاهر است و هر كسى پى مى‏برد / كو بيان كه گم شود در وى خرد
entries.english: It is plain, and every one finds the way: where is the exposition in which the intellect becomes lost?”

entries.farsi: گفت اگر آسان نمايد اين به تو / اين چنين آسان يكى سوره بگو
entries.english: He said, “If this seems easy to you, say one Sura so ‘easy’ as this.

entries.farsi: جنتان و انستان و اهل كار / گو يكى آيت از اين آسان بيار
entries.english: Let the Jinn and mankind and the skilled among you produce a single verse of this ‘easy’ style.”

sections.title_fa: ‏تفسير اين خبر مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام كه للقرآن ظهر و بطن و لبطنه بطن إلى سبعة أبطن
sections.title_en: Commentary on the Tradition of Mustafa, on whom be peace, that the Quran has an exterior and an interior, and that its interior has an interior, to seven interior.

entries.farsi: حرف قرآن را بدان كه ظاهرى است / ‏زير ظاهر باطنى بس قاهرى است
entries.english: Know that the words of the Quran have an exterior, and under the exterior an interior, exceedingly overpowering;

entries.farsi: زير آن باطن يكى بطن سوم / ‏كه در او گردد خردها جمله گم
entries.english: And beneath that inward a third interior, wherein all intellects become lost.

entries.farsi: بطن چارم از نبى خود كس نديد / جز خداى بى‏نظير بى‏نديد
entries.english: The fourth interior of the Quran none has perceived at all, except God the peerless and incomparable.

entries.farsi: تو ز قرآن اى پسر ظاهر مبين / ‏ديو آدم را نبيند جز كه طين
entries.english: In the Quran do not you, O son, regard the exterior: the Devil regards Adam as nothing but clay.

entries.farsi: ظاهر قرآن چو شخص آدمى است / ‏كه نقوشش ظاهر و جانش خفى است
entries.english: The exterior of the Quran is like a man’s person, for his features are visible, while his spirit is hidden.

entries.farsi: مرد را صد سال عم و خال او / آن كه گويند اوليا در كه بوند
entries.english: A man’s paternal and maternal uncles for a hundred years and of his state not see the tip of a hair.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه رفتن انبيا و اوليا عليهم السلام به كوهها و غارها جهت پنهان كردن خويش نيست و جهت ‏خوف و تشويش خلق نيست بلكه جهت ارشاد خلق است و تحريض بر انقطاع از دنيا به قدر ممكن
sections.title_en: It is explained that the going of the prophets and the saints, on whom be peace, to mountains and caves,

is not for the purpose of hiding themselves and on account of their fear of being disturbed by the people, but for the purpose of guiding the people in the right way and inciting them to abandon this world as much as is possible.

entries.farsi: تا ز چشم مردمان پنهان شوند / پيش خلق ايشان فراز صد كه‏اند
entries.english: As for their saying that the saints are in the mountains in order that they may be hidden from the eyes of men,

entries.farsi: گام خود بر چرخ هفتم مى‏نهند / پس چرا پنهان شود كه جو بود
entries.english: In the sight of the people they are higher than a hundred mountains and plant their footsteps on the Seventh Heaven.

entries.farsi: كاو ز صد دريا و كه ز آن سو بود / حاجتش نبود به سوى كه گريخت
entries.english: Why, then, should he who is beyond a hundred seas and mountains become hidden and seek the mountains?

entries.farsi: ‏كز پيش كره‏ى فلك صد نعل ريخت / چرخ گرديد و نديد او گرد جان
entries.english: He has no need to flee to the mountains, he in pursuit of whom the colt, Heaven, has dropped a hundred horse-shoes.

entries.farsi: ‏تعزيت جامه بپوشيد آسمان / گر به ظاهر آن پرى پنهان بود
entries.english: The celestial sphere revolved and never saw the dust of the spirit; Heaven donned the garb of mourning.

entries.farsi: آدمى پنهان‏تر از پريان بود / نزد عاقل ز آن پرى كه مضمر است
entries.english: If, outwardly, the peri is hidden, Man is a hundred times more hidden than the peris.

entries.farsi: ‏آدمى صد بار خود پنهان‏تر است / آدمى نزديك عاقل چون خفى است
entries.english: In the view of the intelligent, Man is indeed a hundred times more hidden than the peri who is concealed.

entries.farsi: ‏چون بود آدم كه در غيب او صفى است
entries.english: Since, in the view of the intelligent, Man is hidden, how must be the Adam who is pure in the unseen world!

sections.title_fa: ‏تشبيه صورت اوليا و صورت كالم اوليا به صورت عصاى موسى و صورت افسون عيسى عليهما السلام
sections.title_en: Comparison of the form of the saints and the form of the speech of the saints to the form of the rod of Moses and to the form of the incantation of Jesus, peace be on them both!

entries.farsi: آدمى همچون عصاى موسى است / ‏آدمى همچون فسون عيسى است
entries.english: Man is like the rod of Moses; Man is like the incantation of Jesus.

entries.farsi: در كف حق بهر داد و بهر زين / ‏قلب مومن هست بين اصبعين
entries.english: For the sake of justice and for the sake of decorum, the true believer’s heart is in the hand of God, between two fingers.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش چوبى و ليكن پيش او / كون يك لقمه چو بگشايد گلو
entries.english: Its exterior is a piece of wood, but existence is one mouthful to it when it opens its throat.

entries.farsi: تو مبين ز افسون عيسى حرف و صوت / ‏آن ببين كز وى گريزان گشت موت
entries.english: In the incantation of Jesus do not regard the letter and the sound: regard the fact that Death turned and fled from it.

entries.farsi: تو مبين ز افسونش آن لهجات پست / ‏آن نگر كه مرده بر جست و نشست
entries.english: In his incantation do not regard the petty words: consider that the dead sprang up and sat down.

entries.farsi: تو مبين مر آن عصا را سهل يافت / ‏آن ببين كه بحر خضرا را شكافت
entries.english: In that rod, do not regard the easy getting: regard the fact that it cleft the green sea.

entries.farsi: تو ز دورى ديده‏اى چتر سياه / ‏يك قدم وا پيش نه بنگر سپاه
entries.english: You have seen from afar the black canopy: take a step forward and behold the army!

entries.farsi: تو ز دورى مى‏نبينى جز كه گرد / اندكى پيش آ ببين در گرد مرد
entries.english: From afar you see nothing but the dust: advance a little and see the man in the dust.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ها را گرد او روشن كند / كوهها را مردى او بر كند
entries.english: His dust makes eyes bright; his manliness uproots mountains.

entries.farsi: چون بر آمد موسى از اقصاى دشت / ‏كوه طور از مقدمش رقاص گشت
entries.english: When Moses came up from the remotest part of the desert, at his advent Mount Sinai began to dance.

sections.title_fa: َتفسير يا جِبالُ أَوِّبِي مَعَهُ وَ الطَّيْر
sections.title_en: Commentary on, O you mountains, repeat in accord with him, and the birds.

entries.farsi: روى داود از فرش تابان شده / ‏كوهها اندر پيش نالان شده
entries.english: The face of David shone with His glory: the mountains sang plaintively after him.

entries.farsi: كوه با داود گشته همرهى / ‏هر دو مطرب مست در عشق شهى
entries.english: The mountain became an accompanist to David: both the minstrels drunk in love for a King.

entries.farsi: يا جِبالُ أَوِّبِي امر آمده / ‏هر دو هم آواز و هم پرده شده
entries.english: The command came, “O you mountains repeat”: both joined their voices and kept the tune together.

entries.farsi: گفت داودا تو هجرت ديده‏اى / ‏بهر من از هم دمان ببريده‏اى
entries.english: He said, “O David, you have suffered separation: for My sake you have parted from your intimates.”

entries.farsi: اى غريب فرد بى‏مونس شده / ‏آتش شوق از دلت شعله زده
entries.english: O lonely stranger who have become friendless, from whose heart the fire of longing has flamed up,

entries.farsi: مطربان خواهى و قوال و نديم / ‏كوهها را پيشت آرد آن قديم
entries.english: You desire minstrels and singers and boon-companions: the Eternal One brings the mountains unto you.

entries.farsi: مطرب و قوال و سرنايى كند / كه به پيشت باد پيمايى كند
entries.english: He makes minstrels and singers and pipers: He makes the mountain blow in measure before you,

entries.farsi: تا بدانى ناله چون كه را رواست / ‏بى‏لب و دندان ولى را ناله‏هاست
entries.english: To the end that you may know that, since the mountain is permitted to sing, the saint has plaintive songs without lips or teeth.

entries.farsi: نغمه‏ى اجزاى آن صافى جسد / هر دمى در گوش حسش مى‏رسد
entries.english: The melody of the particles of that pure-bodied one is reaching his sensuous ear every moment.

entries.farsi: همنشينان نشنوند او بشنود / اى خنك جان كاو به غيبش بگرود
entries.english: His companions hear it not, he hears: oh, happy is the soul that believes in his hidden mystery.

entries.farsi: بنگرد در نفس خود صد گفت‏وگو / همنشين او نبرده هيچ بو
entries.english: He beholds a hundred discourses in himself, while his companion has gotten no scent.

entries.farsi: صد سؤال و صد جواب اندر دلت / ‏مى‏رسد از المكان تا منزلت
entries.english: Within your heart a hundred questions and a hundred answers are coming from non-spatiality to your dwelling-place.

entries.farsi: بشنوى تو نشنود ز آن گوشها / گر به نزديك تو آرد گوش را
entries.english: You hear; the ears do not hear, if he brings his ear near to you.

entries.farsi: گيرم اى كر خود تو آن را نشنوى / ‏چون مثالش ديده‏اى چون نگروى
entries.english: O deaf man, I grant that truly you hear them not; since you have seen their emblem, how will not you believe?

sections.title_fa: جواب طعنه زننده در مثنوى از قصور فهم خود
sections.title_en: Reply to him who rails at the Masnavi on account of his being deficient in understanding.

entries.farsi: اى سگ طاعن تو عوعو مى‏كنى / ‏طعن قرآن را برون شو مى‏كنى
entries.english: O railing cur, you are bow-wowing and practicing evasion for the purpose of railing at the Qur’an.

entries.farsi: اين نه آن شير است كز وى جان برى / ‏يا ز پنجه‏ى قهر او ايمان برى
entries.english: This is not such a lion that you will save your life from it or carry off your faith from the claws of its vengeance.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت مى‏زند قرآن ندا / اى گروهى جهل را گشته فدا
entries.english: The Qur’an is proclaiming till the Resurrection— “O people devoted to ignorance,

entries.farsi: كه مرا افسانه مى‏پنداشتيد / تخم طعن و كافرى مى‏كاشتيد
entries.english: Who were deeming me to be an idle tale and were sowing the seed of raillery and infidelity,

entries.farsi: خود بديديد آن كه طعنه مى‏زديت / ‏كه شما فانى و افسانه بديت
entries.english: You yourselves have seen what you were scoffing at, that you were perishable and an idle tale.

entries.farsi: من كالم حقم و قايم به ذات / ‏قوت جان جان و ياقوت زكات
entries.english: I am the Word of God and subsistent through the Essence; I am the Food of the soul of the soul. And the Jacinth of purity.

entries.farsi: نور خورشيدم فتاده بر شما / ليك از خورشيد ناگشته جدا
entries.english: I am the Sunlight that has fallen upon you, but I have not become separate from the Sun.

entries.farsi: نك منم ينبوع آن آب حيات / ‏تا رهانم عاشقان را از ممات
entries.english: Lo, I am the Fountain of the Water of Life that I may deliver the lovers from death.

entries.farsi: گر چنان گند آزتان ننگيختى / ‏جرعه‏اى بر گورتان حق ريختى
entries.english: If your greed had not raised such a stench, God would have poured a draught on your graves.”

entries.farsi: نه بگيرم گفت و پند آن حكيم / ‏دل نگردانم به هر طعنى سقيم
entries.english: Nay; I will accept the advice and counsel of the Sage: I will not let my heart be sickened by every taunt.

sections.title_fa: ‏مثل زدن در رميدن كره‏ى اسب از آب خوردن به سبب شخوليدن سايسان
sections.title_en: Parable of the foal’s refusing to drink the water because of the bawling of the grooms.

entries.farsi: آن كه فرمودست او اندر خطاب / ‏كره و مادر همى‏خوردند آب
entries.english: As he has said in his discourse, the foal and its mother were drinking the water.

entries.farsi: مى‏شخوليدند هر دم آن نفر / بهر اسبان كه هال هين آب خور
entries.english: Those persons were bawling incessantly at the horses, “Come on! Hey, drink!”

entries.farsi: آن شخوليدن به كره مى‏رسيد / سر همى‏برداشت و از خور مى‏رميد
entries.english: That bawling reached the foal: it was lifting its head and refusing to drink.

entries.farsi: مادرش پرسيد كاى كره چرا / مى‏رمى هر ساعتى زين استقا
entries.english: Its mother asked, “O foal, why are you always refusing to drink this water?”

entries.farsi: گفت كره مى‏شخولند اين گروه / ‏ز اتفاق بانگشان دارم شكوه
entries.english: The foal said, “These people are bawling: I am afraid of the occurrence of their shouts.

entries.farsi: پس دلم مى‏لرزد از جا مى‏رود / ز اتفاق نعره خوفم مى‏رسد
entries.english: Therefore my heart is trembling and jumping: dread of the occurrence of the outcry is coming on me.”

entries.farsi: گفت مادر تا جهان بوده‏ست از اين / ‏كار افزايان بدند اندر زمين
entries.english: The mother said, “Ever since the world existed, there have been busybodies of this sort on the earth.”

entries.farsi: هين تو كار خويش كن اى ارجمند / زود كايشان ريش خود بر مى‏كنند
entries.english: Listen, do your own business, O worthy man: soon will they tear their beards.

entries.farsi: وقت تنگ و مى‏رود آب فراخ / ‏پيش از آن كز هجر گردى شاخ شاخ
entries.english: The time is restricted, and the abundant water is flowing away: drink first, through being parted, you fall to pieces.

entries.farsi: آب خضر از جوى نطق اوليا / مى‏خوريم اى تشنه‏ى غافل بيا
entries.english: There is a famous conduit, full of the Water of Life: draw the Water, in order that verdure may grow up from you.

entries.farsi: شهره كاريزى است پر آب حيات / ‏آب كش تا بر دمد از تو نبات
entries.english: We are drinking the water of Khizr from the river of the speech of the saints: come, O heedless thirsty man!

entries.farsi: گر نبينى آب كورانه به فن / ‏سوى جو آور سبو در جوى زن
entries.english: If you do not see the water, artfully after the fashion of the blind bring the jug to the river, and dip it in the river.

entries.farsi: چون شنيدى كاندر اين جو آب هست / ‏كور را تقليد بايد كار بست
entries.english: Forasmuch as you have heard that there is water in this river-bed,: the blind man must practise conformity.

entries.farsi: جو فرو بر مشك آب انديش را / تا گران بينى تو مشك خويش را
entries.english: Carry down to the river the water-skin that has thoughts of the water, so that you may find your water-skin heavy.

entries.farsi: چون گران ديدى شوى تو مستدل / ‏رست از تقليد خشك آن گاه دل
entries.english: When you have found it heavy, you will be led to infer: at that moment your heart is delivered from dry conformity.

entries.farsi: گر نبيند كور آب جو عيان / ‏ليك داند چون سبو بيند گران
entries.english: If the blind man does not see the river-water visually, yet he knows, when he finds the jug heavy,

entries.farsi: كه ز جو اندر سبو آبى برفت / ‏كاين سبك بود و گران شد ز آب و زفت
entries.english: That some water has gone from the river into the jug; for this was light and it has become heavy and swollen with water;

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر بادى مرا درمى‏ربود / باد مى‏نربايدم ثقلم فزود
entries.english: “Because,”, “every wind used to sweep me away, the wind does not sweep me away: my weight has increased.”

entries.farsi: مر سفيهان را ربايد هر هوا / ‏ز انكه نبودشان گرانى قوى
entries.english: The foolish are swept away by every gust of desire, because they have no weight of faculties.

entries.farsi: كشتى بى‏لنگر آمد مرد شر / كه ز باد كژ نيابد او حذر
entries.english: The wicked man is an anchorless ship, for he finds no precaution against the perverse wind.

entries.farsi: لنگر عقل است عاقل را امان / ‏لنگرى دريوزه كن از عاقالن
entries.english: To the intelligent man the anchor of intelligence is security: beg an anchor from the intelligent.

entries.farsi: او مددهاى خرد چون در ربود / از خزينه‏ى در آن درياى جود
entries.english: Since he has borne away the succours of intelligence from the pearl-treasury of that Sea of Bounty,

entries.farsi: زين چنين امداد دل پر فن شود / بجهد از دل چشم هم روشن شود
entries.english: By such succours the heart is filled with knowledge: it shoots from the heart, and the eye too becomes illuminated,

entries.farsi: ز انكه نور از دل بر اين ديده نشست / ‏تا چو دل شد ديده‏ى تو عاطل است
entries.english: Because the light from the heart has settled upon this eye so that your eye, having become the heart, is inactive.

entries.farsi: دل چو بر انوار عقلى نيز زد / ز آن نصيبى هم به دو ديده دهد
entries.english: When the heart too has come into contact with the intellectual Lights, it bestows a portion thereof on the eyes also.

entries.farsi: پس بدان كآب مبارك ز آسمان / ‏وحى دلها باشد و صدق بيان
entries.english: Know, then, that the blessed Water from Heaven is the inspiration of hearts and the true explanation.

entries.farsi: ما چو آن كره هم آب جو خوريم / ‏سوى آن وسواس طاعن ننگريم
entries.english: Let us also, like that foal, drink the water of the stream; let us pay no regard to the evil suggestions of the scolder.

entries.farsi: آن خداوندان كه ره طى كرده‏اند / گوش با بانگ سگان كى كرده‏اند
entries.english: You are a follower of the prophets, tread the Way: deem all the railing of creatures to be a wind.

entries.farsi: پى رو پيغمبرانى ره سپر / طعنه‏ى خلقان همه بادى شمر
entries.english: When have the Masters who have traversed the Way lent ear to the clamour of curs?

sections.title_fa: ‏بقيه‏ى ذكر آن مهمان مسجد مهمان كش
sections.title_en: The remainder of the story of the guest in the guest-killing mosque

entries.farsi: باز گو كان پاك باز شير مرد / اندر آن مسجد چه بنمودش چه كرد
entries.english: Relate what appeared in the mosque to that self-sacrificing valiant man, and what he did.

entries.farsi: خفت در مسجد خود او را خواب كو / مرد غرقه گشته چون خسبد به جو
entries.english: He slept in the mosque, where in truth had he slept? How should a submerged man sleep in the river?

entries.farsi: خواب مرغ و ماهيان باشد همى / نيم شب آواز با هولى رسيد
entries.english: Always, for the lovers beneath the flood of a passion, there is the sleep of birds and fishes.

entries.farsi: كايم آيم بر سرت اى مستفيد / پنج كرت اين چنين آواز سخت
entries.english: At midnight came an awful voice, “I come, I come upon you, O you that seek advantage.”

entries.farsi: مى‏رسيد و دل همى‏شد لخت لخت
entries.english: Five times came such a terrible voice, and his heart was being rent piecemeal.

sections.title_fa: َتفسير آيت وَ أَجْ لِبْ عَ لَيْهِمْ بِخَيْلِكَ وَ رَجِلِك
sections.title_en: Commentary on the verse: “And raise the battle-cry against them with your horsemen and men on foot.”

entries.farsi: تو چو عزم دين كنى با اجتهاد / ديو بانگت بر زند اندر نهاد
entries.english: When you earnestly resolve to be religious, the Devil in your nature cries out at you,

entries.farsi: كه مرو ز آن سو بينديش اى غوى / ‏كه اسير رنج و درويشى شوى
entries.english: “Go not in that direction! Bethink you, O misguided one; for you will become captive to distress and poverty.

entries.farsi: بى‏نوا گردى ز ياران وابرى / ‏خوار گردى و پشيمانى خورى
entries.english: You will become destitute, you will be cut off from friends, you will be despised, and you will feel sorry.”

entries.farsi: تو ز بيم بانگ آن ديو لعين / ‏واگريزى در ضاللت از يقين
entries.english: From fear of the outcry of that accursed Devil you flee away from certain truth into error,

entries.farsi: كه هال فردا و پس فردا مراست / ‏راه دين پويم كه مهلت پيش ماست
entries.english: Saying, “Ho, to-morrow is mine and after to-morrow: I will run in the Way of religion, I have time.”

entries.farsi: مرگ بينى باز كاو از چپ و راست / ‏مى‏كشد همسايه را تا بانگ خاست
entries.english: Then again you see Death killing your neighbours on left and right, so that the cry is raised.

entries.farsi: باز عزم دين كنى از بيم جان / ‏مرد سازى خويشتن را يك زمان
entries.english: Now, in fear of life, you resolve to be religious: for a while, you make yourself a man;

entries.farsi: باز بانگى بر زند بر تو ز مكر / كه بترس و باز گرد از تيغ فقر
entries.english: So you put on the armour of knowledge and wisdom, saying, “I will not shrink from any danger.”

entries.farsi: پس سلح بر بندى از علم و حكم / ‏كه من از خوفى نيارم پاى كم
entries.english: Again he deceitfully cries out at you— “Be afraid and turn away from the sword of poverty!”

entries.farsi: باز بگريزى ز راه روشنى / ‏آن سالح علم و فن را بفكنى
entries.english: Once more you flee from the Way of Light and cast off that armour of knowledge and virtue.

entries.farsi: سالها او را به بانگى بنده‏اى / ‏در چنين ظلمت نمد افكنده‏اى
entries.english: Years, you are a slave to him because of a cry: you have laid down the blanket in such darkness as this!

entries.farsi: هيبت بانگ شياطين خلق را / بند كرده ست و گرفته حلق را
entries.english: Dread of the cry of the devils has bound the people and taken hold of their throats,

entries.farsi: تا چنان نوميد شد جانشان ز نور / كه روان كافران ز اهل قبور
entries.english: Till their souls have become as hopeless of the Light as the spirits of the infidels who dwell in the tombs.

entries.farsi: اين شكوه بانگ آن ملعون بود / هيبت بانگ خدايى چون بود
entries.english: Such is the terror of the cry of that accursed one: how must be the dread of the Divine cry!

entries.farsi: هيبت باز است بر كبك نجيب / ‏مر مگس را نيست ز آن هيبت نصيب
entries.english: Dread of the falcon is upon the noble partridge: the fly has no portion of that dread,

entries.farsi: ز انكه نبود باز صياد مگس / ‏عنكبوتان مى‏مگس گيرند و بس
entries.english: Because the falcon is not a hunter of flies: only spiders catch flies.

entries.farsi: عنكبوت ديو بر چون تو ذباب / ‏كر و فر دارد نه بر كبك و عقاب
entries.english: The spider, the Devil, has dominion over flies like you, not over the partridge and the eagle.

entries.farsi: بانگ ديوان گله بان اشقياست / ‏بانگ سلطان پاسبان اولياست
entries.english: The cry of the devils is the shepherd of the damned; the cry of the Lord is the guardian of the saints,

entries.farsi: تا نياميزد بدين دو بانگ دور / قطره‏اى از بحر خوش با بحر شور
entries.english: To the end that, by reason of these two cries far distant, not a drop of the sweet sea may mingle with the briny sea.

sections.title_fa: رسيدن بانگ طلسمى نيم شب مهمان مسجد را
sections.title_en: How the talismanic cry came at midnight to the guest in the mosque.

entries.farsi: بشنو اكنون قصه‏ى آن بانگ سخت / ‏كه بدان از جا نرفت آن نيك بخت
entries.english: Now hear the tale of the terrible cry, by which that good-fortuned man was not dismayed.

entries.farsi: گفت چون ترسم چو هست اين طبل عيد / تا دهل ترسد كه زخم او را رسيد
entries.english: He said, “How should I fear? For this is the drum of the Festival. Let the drum fear, since blows belong to it.

entries.farsi: اى دهلهاى تهى بى‏قلوب / ‏قسمتان از عيد جان شد زخم چوب
entries.english: O empty drums without hearts, your share in the festival of the spirit are blows of the stick.

entries.farsi: شد قيامت عيد و بى‏دينان دهل / ‏ما چو اهل عيد خندان همچو گل
entries.english: The Resurrection is the Festival, and the irreligious are the drum: we, like the festive folk, are laughing as the rose.”

entries.farsi: بشنو اكنون اين دهل چون بانگ زد / ديگ دولتبا چگونه مى‏پزد
entries.english: Now hear how, when this drum boomed, he cooks the pot containing the broth of felicity.

entries.farsi: چون كه بشنود آن دهل آن مرد ديد / گفت چون ترسد دلم از طبل عيد
entries.english: When that man of insight heard the drum, he said, “How should my heart be afraid of the drum of the Festival?”

entries.farsi: گفت با خود هين ملرزان دل كز اين / ‏مرد جان بد دالن بى‏يقين
entries.english: He said to himself, “Beware, do not let your heart tremble, for the souls of the faint-hearted who lack faith have died at this.

entries.farsi: وقت آن آمد كه حيدروار من / ‏ملك گيرم يا بپردازم بدن
entries.english: The time has come for me, like Haydar, to seize a kingdom, or to quit the body.”

entries.farsi: بر جهيد و بانگ بر زد كاى كيا / حاضرم اينك اگر مردى بيا
entries.english: He sprang up and shouted, “O prince, lo, here am I: if you are a man, come on!”

entries.farsi: در زمان بشكست ز آواز آن طلسم / ‏زر همى‏ريزيد هر سو قسم قسم
entries.english: At his voice that talisman instantly was shattered: the gold poured down, diverse sorts, in every direction.

entries.farsi: ريخت چندان زر كه ترسيد آن پسر / تا نگيرد زر ز پرى راه در
entries.english: So much gold poured down that the youth feared lest, from its abundance, it might block the doorway.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن برخاست آن شير عتيد / تا سحرگه زر به بيرون مى‏كشيد
entries.english: Afterwards that ready lion rose up, and till dawn he was carrying out the gold

entries.farsi: دفن مى‏كرد و همى‏آمد به زر / با جوال و توبره بار دگر
entries.english: And burying it and coming to it once more with sack and bag.

entries.farsi: گنجها بنهاد آن جان‏باز از آن / ‏كورى ترسانى واپس خزان
entries.english: That self-devoting one laid by stores thereof, to the confusion of the timidity of the backsliders.

entries.farsi: اين زر ظاهر به خاطر آمده‏ست / ‏در دل هر كور دور زر پرست
entries.english: This external gold has occurred to the mind of every blind, God-forsaken gold-worshipper.

entries.farsi: كودكان اسفالها را بشكنند / نام زر بنهند و در دامن كنند
entries.english: Children break potsherds, give the name of gold, and put them in their skirts.

entries.farsi: اندر آن بازى چو گويى نام زر / آن كند در خاطر كودك گذر
entries.english: When in that game you mention the name of gold that crosses the child’s mind.

entries.farsi: بل زر مضروب ضرب ايزدى / ‏كاو نگردد كاسد آمد سرمدى
entries.english: Nay, the gold stamped with the Divine stamp, which does not become obsolete, is everlasting;

entries.farsi: آن زرى كاين زر از آن زر تاب يافت / ‏گوهر و تا بندگى و آب يافت
entries.english: The gold from which this gold gained lustre and derived sheen and splendour and brilliance;

entries.farsi: آن زرى كه دل از او گردد غنى / ‏غالب آيد بر قمر در روشنى
entries.english: The gold whereby the heart is made rich: it surpasses the moon in brightness.

entries.farsi: شمع بود آن مسجد و پروانه او / خويشتن درباخت آن پروانه‏خو
entries.english: That mosque was the candle, and he was the moth: that man of moth-like nature gambled himself away.

entries.farsi: پر بسوخت او را و ليكن ساختش / ‏بس مبارك آمد آن انداختش
entries.english: It burnt his wings, but it complied with him: his throwing was very blessed.

entries.farsi: همچو موسى بود آن مسعود بخت / ‏كاتشى ديد او به سوى آن درخت
entries.english: That man of happy fortune was like Moses who beheld a fire in the direction of the tree.

entries.farsi: چون عنايتها بر او موفور بود / نار مى‏پنداشت و آن خود نور بود
entries.english: Since the favours were plenteously bestowed on him, he fancied it was fire, and really it was the Light.

entries.farsi: مرد حق را چون ببينى اى پسر / تو گمان دارى بر او نار بشر
entries.english: O son, when you see a man of God, you suppose in him the fire of human nature.

entries.farsi: تو ز خود مى‏آيى و آن در تو است / ‏نار و خار ظن باطل اين سو است
entries.english: You are coming from yourself, and that is in you: the fire and thorns of vain opinion are in this quarter.

entries.farsi: او درخت موسى است و پر ضيا / نور خوان نارش مخوان بارى بيا
entries.english: He is the tree of Moses and filled with radiance: come, now, call him the Light, do not call him fire.

entries.farsi: نه فطام اين جهان نارى نمود / سالكان رفتند و آن خود نور بود
entries.english: Did not the weaning from this world seem a fire? The pilgrims went, and that was really the Light.

entries.farsi: پس بدان كه شمع دين بر مى‏شود / اين نه همچون شمع آتشها بود
entries.english: Know, then, that the Candle of Religion is always mounting: this is not like the candle of flames.

entries.farsi: اين نمايد نور و سوزد يار را / و آن به صورت نار و گل زوار را
entries.english: This seems to be Light, it burns its friend, while that is fire in appearance, but is roses to visitors.

entries.farsi: اين چو سازنده ولى سوزنده‏اى / ‏و آن گه وصلت دل افروزنده‏اى
entries.english: The former is like a complaisant, but it is a burner, while that is an illuminator of the heart at the moment of union.

entries.farsi: شكل شعله‏ى نور پاك سازوار / حاضران را نور و دوران را چو نار
entries.english: To those present (with God) the appearance of the spark of pure and worthy Light is luminous, while to those far (from God) it is like fire.

sections.title_fa: ‏مالقات آن عاشق با صدر جهان
sections.title_en: The meeting of the lover with the Sadr-ı Jahan

entries.farsi: آن بخارى نيز خود بر شمع زد / گشته بود از عشقش آسان آن كبد
entries.english: The man of Bukhara also cast himself upon candle: because of his passion that suffering had become easy to him.

entries.farsi: آه سوزانش سوى گردون شده / ‏در دل صدر جهان مهر آمده
entries.english: His burning sighs went up to heaven: kindness (for him) came into the heart of the Sadr-i Jahan,

entries.farsi: گفته با خود در سحرگه كاى احد / حال آن آواره‏ى ما چون بود
entries.english: Said with himself at dawn, “O One, how fares that distraught wanderer of Ours?

entries.farsi: او گناهى كرد و ما ديديم ليك / ‏رحمت ما را نمى‏دانست نيك
entries.english: He committed a sin, and We saw, but he was not well acquainted with Our mercy.

entries.farsi: خاطر مجرم ز ما ترسان شود / ليك صد اوميد در ترسش بود
entries.english: The sinner’s heart becomes afraid of Us, but in his fear there are a hundred hopes.

entries.farsi: من بترسانم وقيح ياوه را / آن كه ترسد من چه ترسانم و را
entries.english: I frighten the impudent man who has lost the way: why should I frighten him who is afraid?

entries.farsi: بهر ديگ سرد آذر مى‏رود / نه بدان كز جوش از سر مى‏رود
entries.english: Fire is used for the cold pot, not for that which is boiling over.

entries.farsi: ايمنان را من بترسانم به علم / ‏خايفان را ترس بردارم به حلم
entries.english: I frighten the unafraid by knowledge; I take away the fear of the afraid by clemency.

entries.farsi: پاره دوزم پاره در موضع نهم / ‏هر كسى را شربت اندر خور دهم
entries.english: I am a tailor: I put the patch in place; I give drink to everyone in due measure.”

entries.farsi: هست سر مرد چون بيخ درخت / ‏ز آن برويد برگهاش از چوب سخت
entries.english: A man’s inmost consciousness is like the root of a tree; hence his leaves grow from the hard wood.

entries.farsi: در خور آن بيخ رسته برگها / ‏در درخت و در نفوس و در نهى
entries.english: The leaves grow according to the root, in the tree and in souls and in minds.

entries.farsi: بر فلك پرهاست ز اشجار وفا / أصلها ثابت و فرعه في السما
entries.english: From the trees of faithfulness there are wings to heaven its root is fast, and its branch is in the sky.

entries.farsi: چون برست از عشق پر بر آسمان / ‏چون نرويد در دل صدر جهان
entries.english: Since through love grew the wing to heaven, how should it not grow in the heart of the Sadr-i Jahan?

entries.farsi: موج مى‏زد در دلش عفو گنه / ‏كه ز هر دل تا دل آمد روزنه
entries.english: Forgiveness of the sin was surging in his heart, for as much as there is a window from each heart to heart;

entries.farsi: كه ز دل تا دل يقين روزن بود / نه جدا و دور چون دو تن بود
entries.english: For assuredly there is a window from heart to heart: they are not separate and far, like two bodies.

entries.farsi: متصل نبود سفال دو چراغ / ‏نورشان ممزوج باشد در مساغ
entries.english: The two clay lamps are not joined, but their light is mingled in passage.

entries.farsi: هيچ عاشق خود نباشد وصل جو / كه نه معشوقش بود جوياى او
entries.english: No lover, in truth, is seeking union without his loved one seeking him;

entries.farsi: ليك عشق عاشقان تن زه كند / عشق معشوقان خوش و فربه كند
entries.english: But the love of lovers makes the body a bowstring, the love of loved ones makes it comely and fat.

entries.farsi: چون در اين دل برق مهر دوست جست / ‏اندر آن دل دوستى مى‏دان كه هست
entries.english: When the lightning of love for the beloved has shot into this heart, know that there is love in that heart.

entries.farsi: در دل تو مهر حق چون شد دو تو / هست حق را بى‏گمانى مهر تو
entries.english: When love for God has been doubled in your heart, without any doubt God has love for you.

entries.farsi: هيچ بانگ كف زدن نايد به در / از يكى دست تو بى‏دستى دگر
entries.english: No sound of clapping comes forth from one hand of yours without the other hand.

entries.farsi: تشنه مى‏نالد كه اى آب گوار / آب هم نالد كه كو آن آب خوار
entries.english: The thirsty man is moaning, “O delicious water!” The water moans too, saying, “Where is the water-drinker?”

entries.farsi: جذب آب است اين عطش در جان ما / ما از آن او و او هم آن ما
entries.english: This thirst in our souls is the attraction exerted by the Water: we are Its, and It is ours.

entries.farsi: جمله اجزاى جهان ز آن حكم پيش / ‏جفت جفت و عاشقان جفت خويش
entries.english: The Wisdom of God in destiny and in decree made us lovers of one another.

entries.farsi: حكمت حق در قضا و در قدر / كرد ما را عاشقان همدگر
entries.english: Because of that fore-ordainment all the particles of the world repaired as mates and are in love with their own mate.

entries.farsi: هست هر جزوى ز عالم جفت خواه / ‏راست همچون كهربا و برگ كاه
entries.english: Every particle of the universe desires its mate, just like amber and the blade of straw.

entries.farsi: آسمان گويد زمين را مرحبا / با توام چون آهن و آهن ربا
entries.english: Heaven says to the earth, “Welcome! To you I am as the iron and the magnet.”

entries.farsi: آسمان مرد و زمين زن در خرد / هر چه آن انداخت اين مى‏پرورد
entries.english: In the intellect, heaven is man and the earth woman: whatever that casts forth this fosters

entries.farsi: چون نماند گرمى‏اش بفرستد او / چون نماند ترى و نم بدهد او
entries.english: When it has no heat remaining, it sends it; when no freshness and moisture remains, it bestows it.

entries.farsi: برج خاكى خاك ارضى را مدد / برج آبى ترّيش اندر دمد
entries.english: The terrene sign is replenishment to the dust of the earth; the aqueous sign produces freshness therein;

entries.farsi: برج بادى ابر سوى او برد / تا بخارات وخم را بر كشد
entries.english: The aerial sign wafts the clouds towards it, that they may sweep away the pestilential vapours;

entries.farsi: برج آتش گرمى خورشيد از او / همچو تابه‏ى سرخ ز آتش پشت و رو
entries.english: The fiery sign is the source of the sun’s heat, like a frying-pan red-hot, back and front, by fire.

entries.farsi: هست سر گردان فلك اندر زمن / ‏همچو مردان گرد مكسب بهر زن
entries.english: Heaven is turning giddily in Time, like men around gain for the wife’s sake;

entries.farsi: وين زمين كدبانويى‏ها مى‏كند / بر والدات و رضاعش مى‏تند
entries.english: And this earth nurtures: it attends to births and to suckling that.

entries.farsi: پس زمين و چرخ را دان هوشمند / چون كه كار هوشمندان مى‏كنند
entries.english: Therefore regard earth and heaven as endowed with intelligence, since they do the work of intelligent beings.

entries.farsi: گر نه از هم اين دو دل بر مى‏مزند / پس چرا چون جفت در هم مى‏خزند
entries.english: Unless these two sweethearts taste from one another, then why are they creeping together like mates?

entries.farsi: بى‏زمين كى گل برويد و ارغوان / ‏پس چه زايد ز آب و تاب آسمان
entries.english: Without the earth how should roses and arghawan-flowers grow? What, then, would be born of the water and heat of heaven?

entries.farsi: بهر آن ميل است در ماده به نر / تا بود تكميل كار همدگر
entries.english: The desire in the female for the male is to the end that they may perfect each other’s work.

entries.farsi: ميل اندر مرد و زن حق ز آن نهاد / تا بقا يابد جهان زين اتحاد
entries.english: God put desire in man and woman in order that the world should be preserved by this union.

entries.farsi: ميل هر جزوى به جزوى هم نهد / ز اتحاد هر دو توليدى زهد
entries.english: He also implants the desire of every part for another part: from the union of both an act of generation results.

entries.farsi: شب چنين با روز اندر اعتناق / ‏مختلف در صورت اما اتفاق
entries.english: Likewise night and day are in mutual embrace: different in appearance, but in agreement.

entries.farsi: روز و شب ظاهر دو ضد و دشمنند / ليك هر دو يك حقيقت مى‏تنند
entries.english: Day and night, outwardly, are two contraries and enemies, but they both attend on one truth—

entries.farsi: هر يكى خواهان دگر را همچو خويش / ‏از پى تكميل فعل و كار خويش
entries.english: Each desiring the other, like kinsfolk, for the sake of perfecting their action and work

entries.farsi: ز انكه بى‏شب دخل نبود طبع را / پس چه اندر خرج آرد روزها
entries.english: Because, without night, the man’s nature would receive no income: what, then, should the days expend?

sections.title_fa: ‏جذب هر عنصرى جنس خود را كه در تركيب آدمى محتبس شده است به غير جنس
sections.title_en: How each element attracts its congener that has been imprisoned in the human constitution by the non-homogeneous

entries.farsi: خاك گويد خاك تن را باز گرد / ترك جان كن سوى ما آ همچو گرد
entries.english: Earth says to the earth of the body, “Return! Take leave of the spirit; come to me like the dust.

entries.farsi: جنس مايى پيش ما اوليترى / ‏به كه ز آن تن وارهى و ز آن ترى
entries.english: You are my congener, you are more suited with me: it is better that you should escape from that body and that moisture.”

entries.farsi: گويد آرى ليك من پا بسته‏ام / ‏گر چه همچون تو ز هجران خسته‏ام
entries.english: It answers, “Yes; but I am fettered, although like you I am weary of separation.”

entries.farsi: ترى تن را بجويند آبها / كاى ترى باز آ ز غربت سوى ما
entries.english: The waters seek the moisture of the body, saying, “O moisture, and come back to us from exile.”

entries.farsi: گرمى تن را همى‏خواند اثير / كه ز نارى راه اصل خويش گير
entries.english: The ether is calling the heat of the body, saying, “You are of fire: take the way to your origin.”

entries.farsi: هست هفتاد و دو علت در بدن / ‏از كششهاى عناصر بى‏رسن
entries.english: There are two-and-seventy diseases in the body, by the elements pulling without cord.

entries.farsi: علت آيد تا بدن را بسكلد / تا عناصر همدگر را واهلد
entries.english: Disease comes to shatter the body, so that the elements may abandon each other.

entries.farsi: چار مرغند اين عناصر بسته پا / مرگ و رنجورى و علت پا گشا
entries.english: These elements are four birds with their legs tied: death and sickness and disease loosen their legs.

entries.farsi: پايشان از همدگر چون باز كرد / مرغ هر عنصر يقين پرواز كرد
entries.english: When it has released their legs from one another, assuredly every bird-element flies away.

entries.farsi: جذبه‏ى اين اصلها و فرعها / هر دمى رنجى نهد در جسم ما
entries.english: The pull between these originals and derivatives continually implants some pain in our bodies,

entries.farsi: تا كه اين تركيبها را بر درد / مرغ هر جزوى به اصل خود پرد
entries.english: In order that it may tear these coalitions asunder each part, like a bird, may fly to its home;

entries.farsi: حكمت حق مانع آيد زين عجل / ‏جمعشان دارد به صحت تا اجل
entries.english: Divine Providence hinders them from this hastening and keeps them together in health till the appointed term,

entries.farsi: گويد اى اجزا اجل مشهود نيست / ‏پر زدن پيش از اجلتان سود نيست
entries.english: And says, “O parts, the term is not certainly known: it is useless for you to take wing before the term.”

entries.farsi: چون كه هر جزوى بجويد ارتفاق / ‏چون بود جان غريب اندر فراق
entries.english: Inasmuch as every part seeks support, what must be the state of the soul, a stranger, in separation?

sections.title_fa: منجذب شدن جان نيز به عالم ارواح و تقاضاى او و ميل او به مقر خود و منقطع شدن از اجزاى اجسام كه كنده‏ى پاى باز روح‏اند
sections.title_en: How likewise the soul is drawn to the world of spirits, and how it craves and desires its home, and becomes severed from the bodily parts which are a fetter on the leg of the spiritual falcon.

entries.farsi: گويد اى اجزاى پست فرشى‏ام / غربت من تلخ‏تر من عرشى‏ام
entries.english: It says, “O my base earthly parts, my exile is bitterer: I am celestial.”

entries.farsi: ميل تن در سبزه و آب روان / ‏ز آن بود كه اصل او آمد از آن
entries.english: The desire of the body for green herbs and running water is because its origin is from those;

entries.farsi: ميل جان اندر حيات و در حى است / ‏ز انكه جان المكان اصل وى است
entries.english: The desire of the soul is for Life and for the Living One, because its origin is the Infinite Soul.

entries.farsi: ميل جان در حكمت است و در علوم / ‏ميل تن در باغ و راغ است و كروم
entries.english: The desire of the soul is for wisdom and the sciences; the desire of the body is for orchards and meadows and vines.

entries.farsi: ميل جان اندر ترقى و شرف / ‏ميل تن در كسب و اسباب علف
entries.english: The desire of the soul is for ascent and exaltedness; the desire of the body is for gain and the means of procuring fodder.

entries.farsi: ميل و عشق آن شرف هم سوى جان / ‏زين يحب را و يحبون را بدان
entries.english: That exaltedness too has desire and love towards the soul: from this understand He loves them and they love Him.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم شرح اين بى‏حد شود / مثنوى هشتاد تا كاغذ شود
entries.english: If I explain this, it will be endless: the Masnavi will amount to eighty volumes.

entries.farsi: حاصل آن كه هر كه او طالب بود / جان مطلوبش در او راغب بود
entries.english: The gist is that whenever anyone seeks, the soul of the object sought by him desires him.

entries.farsi: آدمى حيوان نباتى و جماد / هر مرادى عاشق هر بى‏مراد
entries.english: Man, animal, plant, or mineral, every object of desire is in love with everything that is without the object of desire.

entries.farsi: بى‏مرادان بر مرادى مى‏تنند / و آن مرادان جذب ايشان مى‏كنند
entries.english: Those who are without their objects of desire attach themselves to an object of desire, and those desired ones draw them;

entries.farsi: ليك ميل عاشقان الغر كند / ميل معشوقان خوش و خوش فر كند
entries.english: But the desire of the lovers makes them lean; the desire of the loved ones makes them fair and beauteous.

entries.farsi: عشق معشوقان دو رخ افروخته / ‏عشق عاشق جان او را سوخته
entries.english: The love of the loved ones illumines the cheeks; the love of the lover consumes his soul.

entries.farsi: كهربا عاشق به شكل بى‏نياز / كاه مى‏كوشد در آن راه دراز
entries.english: The amber loves with the appearance of wanting naught, the straw is making efforts on that long road.

entries.farsi: اين رها كن عشق آن تشنه دهان / ‏تافت اندر سينه‏ى صدر جهان
entries.english: Leave this. The love of that thirsty-mouthed man shone in the breast of the Sadr-i Jahan.

entries.farsi: دود آن عشق و غم آتش‏كده / ‏رفته در مخدوم او مشفق شده
entries.english: The smoke of the love and pain of the fire-temple entered his lord turned into compassion.

entries.farsi: ليكش از ناموس و بوش و آبرو / شرم مى‏آمد كه واجويد از او
entries.english: But on account of glory and pride and magnificence he was ashamed to inquire for him:

entries.farsi: رحمتش مشتاق آن مسكين شده / ‏سلطنت زين لطف مانع آمده
entries.english: His mercy had begun to yearn after that lowly man, his majesty hindered from this kindness.

entries.farsi: عقل حيران كاين عجب او را كشيد / يا كشش ز آن سو بدين جانب رسيد
entries.english: The intellect is bewildered, wondering whether this one attracted him, or whether the attraction came from that quarter to this side.

entries.farsi: ترك جلدى كن كز اين ناواقفى / ‏لب ببند اللهَّ أعلم بالخفى
entries.english: Abandon presumption, for you are ignorant of this. Close your lips: God best knows the secret.

entries.farsi: اين سخن را بعد از اين مدفون كنم / ‏آن كشنده مى‏كشد من چون كنم
entries.english: Henceforth I will bury this topic. That Drawer is drawing me: what can I do?

entries.farsi: كيست آن كت مى‏كشد اى معتنى / ‏آن كه مى‏نگذاردت كاين دم زنى
entries.english: Who is he that is drawing you, O solicitous one? He who does not allow you to utter this word.

entries.farsi: صد عزيمت مى‏كنى بهر سفر / مى‏كشاند مر ترا جاى دگر
entries.english: You make a hundred resolutions to journey: He draws you to some other place.

entries.farsi: اسب زيرك‏سار ز آن نيكو پى است / ‏كاو همى‏داند كه فارس بر وى است
entries.english: He turns the bridle in every direction in order that the untrained horse may gain knowledge of the rider.

entries.farsi: ز آن بگرداند بهر سو آن لگام / ‏تا خبر يابد ز فارس اسب خام
entries.english: The clever horse is well-paced because it knows that the rider is on it.

entries.farsi: او دلت را بر دو صد سودا ببست / ‏بى‏مرادت كرد پس دل را شكست
entries.english: He fixed your heart on a hundred passionate desires, disappointed you, and then broke your heart.

entries.farsi: چون شكست او بال آن راى نخست / ‏چون نشد هستى بال اشكن درست
entries.english: Inasmuch as He broke the wings of that first intention, how was not the existence of the Wing-breaker perfectly established?

entries.farsi: چون قضايش حبل تدبيرت سكست / ‏چون نشد بر تو قضاى آن درست
entries.english: Since His ordainment snapped the cord of your contrivance, how was not God’s ordainment perfectly established to you?

sections.title_fa: فسخ عزايم و نقضها جهت با خبر كردن آدمى را از آن كه مالك و قاهر اوست و گاه گاه عزم او را فسخ ناكردن و نافذ داشتن تا طمع او را بر عزم كردن دارد تا باز عزمش را بشكند تا تنبيه بر تنبيه بود
sections.title_en: The annulment and destruction of resolutions in order to let man know that He is the Lord and the Almighty; and His occasional non-annulment of his resolution and His carrying it into effect in order that hope may urge him to form a resolution, so that He again may destroy it,
to the end that warning may follow on warning.

entries.farsi: عزمها و قصدها در ماجرا / گاه گاهى راست مى‏آيد ترا
entries.english: In the course of events your resolutions and purposes now and then come right,
