entries.farsi: تا به طمع آن دلت نيت كند / بار ديگر نيتت را بشكند
entries.english: In order that, through hope of that, your heart may form an intention, and that He may once more destroy your intention.

entries.farsi: ور بكلى بى‏مرادت داشتى / ‏دل شدى نوميد امل كى كاشتى
entries.english: For if He were to keep you wholly unsuccessful, your heart would despair: how would it sow expectation?

entries.farsi: ور نكاريدى امل از عورى‏اش / ‏كى شدى پيدا بر او مقهورى‏اش
entries.english: And unless it sowed expectation, how from its barrenness would its subjection become apparent to it?

entries.farsi: عاشقان از بى‏مراديهاى خويش / ‏با خبر گشتند از موالى خويش
entries.english: By their failures the lovers are made aware of their Lord.

entries.farsi: بى‏مرادى شد قالووز بهشت / ‏حفت الجنة شنو اى خوش سرشت
entries.english: Failure is the guide to Paradise: listen, O man of goodly nature, to, “Paradise is encompassed.”

entries.farsi: كه مراداتت همه اشكسته پاست / ‏پس كسى باشد كه كام او رواست
entries.english: That all that you desire is broken-legged, then there is One whose pleasure is fulfilled.

entries.farsi: پس شدند اشكسته‏اش آن صادقان / ‏ليك كو خود آن شكست عاشقان
entries.english: Therefore the sincere have become broken before Him; but where indeed is the abasement of those who love?

entries.farsi: عاقالن اشكسته‏اش از اضطرار / عاقالنش بندگان بندى‏اند
entries.english: The intelligent are abased before Him from necessity; the lovers are abased with hundredfold free-will.

entries.farsi: عاشقانش شكرى و قندى‏اند / ائتيا كرها مهار عاقالن
entries.english: The intelligent are bond-slaves to Him; the lovers are like sugar and candy to Him.

entries.farsi: ‏ائتيا طوعا بهار بى‏دالن
entries.english: “Come against your will” is the toggle for the intelligent; “come willingly” is the spring-time of them that have lost their hearts.

sections.title_fa: نظر كردن پيغامبر عليه الصالة و السلام به اسيران و تبسم كردن و گفتن كه عجبت من قوم يجرون ‏إلى الجنة بالسالسل و األغالل
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom be peace, looked at the captives and smiled and said,
“I marvel at folk who are dragged to Paradise in chains and shackles.”

entries.farsi: ديد پيغمبر يكى جوقى اسير / كه همى‏بردند و ايشان در نفير
entries.english: The Prophet saw a troop of captives being taken along, and they were in loud lamentation.

entries.farsi: ديدشان در بند آن آگاه شير / مى نظر كردند در وى زير زير
entries.english: That wary Lion saw them in chains: looking askance at him,

entries.farsi: تا همى‏خاييد هر يك از غضب / ‏بر رسول صدق دندانها و لب
entries.english: So that each was gnashing his teeth and chewing his lips in anger against the veracious Prophet;

entries.farsi: زهره نه با آن غضب كه دم زنند / ز انكه در زنجير قهر ده منند
entries.english: Notwithstanding that anger, they dare not utter a word, because they are in the ten-kilogram chain of violence.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشاندشان موكل سوى شهر / مى‏برد از كافرستانشان به قهر
entries.english: Their custodian is marching them along to the city: he is taking them by force from the land of the infidels.

entries.farsi: نه فدايى مى‏ستاند نه زرى / ‏نه شفاعت مى‏رسد از سرورى
entries.english: “He will not accept any ransom or any gold: no intercession is coming from any prince.

entries.farsi: رحمت عالم همى‏گويند و او / عالمى را مى‏برد حلق و گلو
entries.english: He is called a mercy to the world, and he is cutting the throats and gullets of a world.”

entries.farsi: با هزار انكار مى‏رفتند راه / ‏زير لب طعنه زنان بر كار شاه
entries.english: With a thousand disbelief they marched along, railing under their breath at the actions of the king,

entries.farsi: چاره‏ها كرديم و اينجا چاره نيست / ‏خود دل اين مرد كم از خاره نيست
entries.english: “We remedied, but in this case there is no remedy: truly this man’s heart is not inferior to a rock.

entries.farsi: ما هزاران مرد شير الپ ارسالن / با دو سه عريان سست نيم جان
entries.english: We, thousands of men brave as lions, with two or three feeble and half-dead naked fellows,

entries.farsi: اين چنين درمانده‏ايم از كژروى است / ‏يا ز اخترهاست يا خود جادوى است
entries.english: Are left helpless like this: is it on account of wrong-doing or stars, or is it sorcery?

entries.farsi: بخت ما را بر دريد آن بخت او / تخت ما شد سر نگون از تخت او
entries.english: His fortune tore up our fortune; our throne was overturned by his throne.

entries.farsi: كار او از جادويى گر گشت زفت / ‏جادويى كرديم ما هم چون نرفت
entries.english: If his cause became mighty by sorcery, we too practiced sorcery: how did not it succeed?

sections.title_fa: تفسير اين آيت كه إِنْ تَسْ تَفْتِحُوا فَقَدْ جاءَكُمُ الْفَتْحُ‏اآلية، اى طاعنان مى‏گفتيد كه از ما و محمد عليه السلام آن كه حق است فتح و نصرتش ده و اين بدان مى‏گفتيد تا گمان آيد كه شما طالب حقيد بى‏غرض اكنون محمد را (ص) نصرت داديم تا صاحب حق را ببينيد

sections.title_en: Commentary on the verse, “If you ask for a decision, the decision has indeed come to you. O critics, you were saying, ‘Give the decision and victory to us or to Mohammed, whichever is in the right’; and you were saying this in order that it might be supposed that ye were seeking the right disinterestedly. Now We have given the victory to Mohammed, to the end that ye may see the champion of the right.”

entries.farsi: از بتان و از خدا درخواستيم / ‏كه بكن ما را اگر ناراستيم
entries.english: We besought the idols and God, saying, ‘Destroy us if we are untrue.

entries.farsi: آن كه حق و راست است از ما و او / نصرتش ده نصرت او را بجو
entries.english: Whichever is right and true, between us and him, give the victory to that one and desire him to be victorious.’

entries.farsi: اين دعا بسيار كرديم و صالت / ‏پيش الت و پيش عزى و منات
entries.english: Often we made this invocation and prayer before Lat and ‘Uzza and Manat,

entries.farsi: كه اگر حق است او پيداش كن / ‏ور نباشد حق زبون ماش كن
entries.english: Saying, ‘If he is in the right, make him manifest; if he is not in the right, make him subject to us.’

entries.farsi: چون كه وا ديديم او منصور بود / ما همه ظلمت بديم او نور بود
entries.english: When we recognised, he was the one to whom victory was given: we all were darkness, he was the light.

entries.farsi: اين جواب ماست كانچه خواستيد / گشت پيدا كه شما ناراستيد
entries.english: This is our answer what you desired; it has become evident that you are the untrue.’”

entries.farsi: باز اين انديشه را از فكر خويش / ‏كور مى‏كردند و دفع از ذكر خويش
entries.english: Then, again, they were blindfolding this thought from their reflective faculty and banishing it from their memory,

entries.farsi: كاين تفكرمان هم از ادبار رست / ‏كه صواب او شود در دل درست
entries.english: Saying, “This thought too has arisen from our ill-luck, that his being in the right should be perfectly established in our minds.

entries.farsi: خود چه شد گر غالب آمد چند بار / هر كسى را غالب آرد روزگار
entries.english: What, indeed, does it matter if he has prevailed several times? Time brings everyone to predominance.

entries.farsi: باز گفتندى كه گر چه او شكست / ‏چون شكست ما نبود آن زشت و پست
entries.english: We also were made successful by the Days, and at times became victorious over him.”

entries.farsi: ما هم از ايام بخت آور شديم / ‏بارها بر وى مظفر آمديم
entries.english: Again they were saying, “Although he was defeated, it was not disgraceful and vile like our defeat,”

entries.farsi: ز انكه بخت نيك او را در شكست / ‏داد صد شادى پنهان زير دست
entries.english: Because in defeat good fortune gave him underhand a hundred secret joys;

entries.farsi: كاو به اشكسته نمى‏مانست هيچ / ‏كه نه غم بودش در آن نه پيچ پيچ
entries.english: For he did not at all resemble one defeated, as he felt no sorrow or distress thereat,

entries.farsi: چون نشان مومنان مغلوبى است / ‏ليك در اشكست مومن خوبى است
entries.english: Since to be vanquished is the mark of the true believers; yet in the true believer’s defeat there is goodness.

entries.farsi: گر تو مشك و عنبرى را بشكنى / ور شكستى ناگهان سرگين خر
entries.english: If you crush some musk or ambergris, you will fill a world with the exhalation of sweet herbs;

entries.farsi: خانه‏ها پر گند گردد تا به سر / وقت واگشت حديبيه به ذل
entries.english: And if you suddenly crush the dung of an ass, the houses will be filled to the top with stench.

entries.farsi: ‏دولت إِنَّا فَتَحْ نا زد دهل
entries.english: At the moment of the ignominious return from Hudaybiya, the empire of Lo, We have opened victory proclaimed itself.

sections.title_fa: سر آن كه بى‏مراد باز گشتن رسول عليه الصالة و السلام از حديبيه حق تعالى لقب آن فتح كرد كه إِنَّا فَتَحْ ناكه به صورت غلق بود و به معنى فتح چنان كه شكستن مشك به ظاهر شكستن است و به معنى درست كردن است مشكى او را و تكميل فوايد اوست

sections.title_en: The hidden reason why God most High gave the title of “victory” to the return of the Prophet, on whom be peace, from Hudaybiya without having gained his purpose: as, “Lo, We have opened victory”; for it was a locking in appearance, and in reality an opening, just as the crushing of musk is apparently a crushing, but really the confirmation of its muskiness
and the exhibition of its virtues in their perfection.

entries.farsi: آمدش پيغام از دولت كه رو / تو ز منع اين ظفر غمگين مشو
entries.english: From the empire came to him the message, “Go, be not saddened by the withholding of this victory,

entries.farsi: بنگر آخر چون كه واگرديد تفت / ‏بر قريظه و بر نضير از وى چه رفت
entries.english: For in this present abasement of you there are victories: lo, such and such a fortress, such and such a town, are to you.”

entries.farsi: كاندر اين خوارى نقدت فتحهاست / ‏نك فالن قلعه فالن بقعه تراست
entries.english: Consider, after all, when he retreated in haste, what he did against Qurayza and Nadir.

entries.farsi: قلعه‏ها هم گرد آن دو بقعه‏ها / شد مسلم و ز غنايم نفعها
entries.english: The fortresses, also, round those two settlements submitted, and advantages of spoils.

entries.farsi: ور نباشد آن تو بنگر كاين فريق / ‏پر غم و رنجند و مفتون و عشيق
entries.english: And if that be not so, consider that this class are sorrowful and woeful and distraught and enamoured.

entries.farsi: زهر خوارى را چو شكر مى‏خورند / خار غمها را چو اشتر مى‏چرند
entries.english: They eat the poison of abasement, like sugar; they feed, like camels, on the thistle of sorrows.

entries.farsi: بهر عين غم نه از بهر فرج / ‏اين تسافل پيش ايشان چون درج
entries.english: for the sake of the sorrow itself, not for the sake of relief: in their eyes this lowliness is as a ladder.

entries.farsi: آن چنان شادند اندر قعر چاه / ‏كه همى‏ترسند از تخت و كاله
entries.english: So glad are they at the bottom of the pit that they are afraid of the throne and the tiara.

entries.farsi: هر كجا دل بر بود خود همنشين / ‏فوق گردون است نه زير زمين
entries.english: Every place where the Beloved himself is their companion is above the sky, not below the earth.

sections.title_fa: ‏تفسير اين خبر كه مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام فرمود ال تفضلوني على يونس بن متى
sections.title_en: Commentary on the Tradition that Mustafa, on whom be peace, said, “Do not declare me to be more excellent than Yúnus ibn Mattá.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه معراج مرا / نيست بر معراج يونس اجتبا
entries.english: The Prophet said, “No preference is to my ascension as being superior to the ascension of Yúnus.

entries.farsi: آن من بر چرخ و آن او نشيب / ‏ز انكه قرب حق برون است از حساب
entries.english: Mine was up to heaven, and his was down below, because nearness unto God is beyond calculation.”

entries.farsi: قرب نه بالا نه پستى رفتن است / ‏قرب حق از حبس هستى رستن است
entries.english: To be near is not to go up or down: to be near unto God is to escape from the prison of existence.

entries.farsi: نيست را چه جاى بالاى است و زير / نيست را نه زود و نه دورست و دير
entries.english: What room has non-existence for “up” and “down”? Non-existence has no “soon” or “far” or “late.”

entries.farsi: كارگاه و گنج حق در نيستى است / ‏غره‏ى هستى چه دانى نيست چيست
entries.english: The laboratory and treasure of God is in non-existence. You are deluded by existence: how should you know what non-existence is?

entries.farsi: آن چنان شادند در ذل و تلف / ‏همچو ما در وقت اقبال و شرف
entries.english: The sum of the matter this defeat of theirs, O sire, does not resemble our defeat at all.

entries.farsi: برگ بى‏برگى همه اقطاع اوست / ‏فقر و خواريش افتخار است و علوست
entries.english: They rejoice in being abased and destroyed, just as we in the hour of success and honour.

entries.farsi: حاصل اين اشكست ايشان اى كيا / مى‏نماند هيچ با اشكست ما
entries.english: The provision of lack is his entire domain: poverty and lowliness are his pride and glory.

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفت ار چنان است آن فريد / چون بخنديد او كه ما را بسته ديد
entries.english: One said, “If that adversary is such, how did he laugh when he saw us bound?

entries.farsi: چون كه او مبدل شده ست و شادى‏اش / ‏نيست زين زندان و زين آزادى‏اش
entries.english: Since he has been transmuted, and his joy is not caused by this prison and this freedom of his,

entries.farsi: پس به قهر دشمنان چون شاد شد / چون از اين فتح و ظفر پر باد شد
entries.english: How, then, did he rejoice at the subjection of enemies? How was he puffed up by this victory and conquest?

entries.farsi: شاد شد جانش كه بر شيران نر / يافت آسان نصرت و دست و ظفر
entries.english: His soul rejoiced because he easily gained the help and the upper hand and the victory over fierce lions.

entries.farsi: پس بدانستيم كاو آزاد نيست / ‏جز به دنيا دل خوش و دل شاد نيست
entries.english: Therefore we knew that he is not free, and that only on account of this world is he happy and glad at heart.

entries.farsi: ور نه چون خندد كه اهل آن جهان / ‏بر بد و نيكند مشفق مهربان
entries.english: Else, how should he laugh? For the otherworldly are compassionate and kind to the evil and the good.”

entries.farsi: اين بمنگيدند در زير زبان / ‏آن اسيران با هم اندر بحث آن
entries.english: Thus did those captives mutter to each other under their breath in discussing that,

entries.farsi: تا موكل نشنود بر ما جهد / خود سخن در گوش آن سلطان برد
entries.english: Lest the custodian hear and spring upon us and personally carry our words to the ear of that Sultan.”

sections.title_fa: آگاه شدن پيغامبر عليه السلام از طعن ايشان بر شماتت او
sections.title_en: How the Prophet, on whom be peace, became aware of their chiding him for his exultation.

entries.farsi: گر چه نشنيد آن موكل آن سخن / ‏رفت در گوشى كه آن بد من لدن
entries.english: Though the custodian did not hear those words, they entered into the ear that was from the presence.

entries.farsi: بوى پيراهان يوسف را نديد / آن كه حافظ بود و يعقوبش كشيد
entries.english: The scent of Joseph’s spirit was not perceived by its keeper, but Jacob inhaled it.

entries.farsi: آن شياطين بر عنان آسمان / ‏نشنوند آن سر لوح غيب دان
entries.english: The devils on the high front of Heaven do not hear the secret of the mystery-knowing Tablet;

entries.farsi: آن محمد خفته و تكيه زده / ‏آمده سِر گِرد او گردان شده
entries.english: Mohammed went to sleep and reclined, the secret came and circled round him.

entries.farsi: او خورد حلوا كه روزيش است باز / آن نه كانگشتان او باشد دراز
entries.english: He whose allotted portion is open eats the sweetmeat, not he whose fingers are long.

entries.farsi: نجم ثاقب گشته حارس ديور ان / ‏كه بهل دزدى ز احمد سر ستان
entries.english: The gleaming star became a watchman and drove the devils away, saying, “Abandon theft and receive the secret from Ahmad.”

entries.farsi: اى دو ديده سوى دكان از پگاه / ‏هين به مسجد رو بجو رزق اله
entries.english: O you, whose eyes from early are towards shop, listen, go to the mosque and seek the portion allotted by God.

entries.farsi: پس رسول آن گفتشان را فهم كرد / گفت آن خنده نبودم از نبرد
entries.english: The Prophet, then, apprehended their words and said, “That laughter of mine was not from hostility.

entries.farsi: مرده‏اند ايشان و پوسيده‏ى فنا / مرده كشتن نيست مردى پيش ما
entries.english: They are dead and rotted by decay: in my judgment it is not the part of a man to kill the dead.

entries.farsi: خود كى‏اند ايشان كه مه گردد شكاف / ‏چون كه من پا بفشرم اندر مصاف
entries.english: Who are they indeed? For the moon is split when I plant my foot on the battlefield.

entries.farsi: آن گهى كازاد بوديت و مكين / ‏مر شما را بسته مى‏ديدم چنين
entries.english: At the time when you were free and powerful, I was seeing you bound, like this.

entries.farsi: اى بنازيده به ملك و خاندان / ‏نزد عاقل اشترى بر ناودان
entries.english: O you that pride yourself on your possessions and household, in the view of the intelligent you are the camel on the water-spout.

entries.farsi: بنگرم در غوره مى‏بينم عيان / ‏بنگرم در نيست شى بينم عيان
entries.english: Since the bowl, the bodily form, fell from the roof, there has rolled before my eye ‘Everything that is to come shall come.’

entries.farsi: نقش تن را تا فتاد از بام طشت / ‏پيش چشمم كل آت آت گشت
entries.english: I look on the unripe grape, and I see the wine clearly; I look on nonentity, and I see the entity clearly.

entries.farsi: بنگرم سر عالمى بينم نهان / ‏آدم و حوا نرسته از جهان
entries.english: I look on the inmost consciousness, and I see a universe hidden, Adam and Eve not arisen from the world.

entries.farsi: ُمر شما را وقت ذرات أَ لَسْ ت / ‏ديده‏ام پا بسته و منكوس و پست
entries.english: You I have seen, fettered and overthrown and abject, at the time (when mankind was assembled in the shape) of ants on the Day of Alast.

entries.farsi: از حدوث آسمان بى‏عمد / آن چه دانسته بدم افزون نشد
entries.english: That which I had known was not increased by the coming into existence of the unsupported heaven.

entries.farsi: من شما را سر نگون مى‏ديده‏ام / ‏پيش از آن كز آب و گل باليده‏ام
entries.english: I have ever seen you headlong, before I grew from the water and the clay.

entries.farsi: بسته‏ى قهر خفى و آن گه چه قهر / قند مى‏خورديد و در وى درج زهر
entries.english: I did not see new, that I should rejoice thereat: I used to see this during your former prosperity.

entries.farsi: نو نديدم تا كنم شادى بدان / ‏اين همى‏ديدم در آن اقبالتان
entries.english: Bound in invisible Wrath—and then what Wrath! — you were eating sugar wherein poison was contained.

entries.farsi: اين چنين قندى پر از زهر ار عدو / خوش بنوشد چت حسد آيد بر او
entries.english: If your enemy delights in eating such a poisonous sugar, what envy of him would come to you?

entries.farsi: با نشاط آن زهر مى‏كرديد نوش / ‏مرگتان خفيه گرفته هر دو گوش
entries.english: You were eating that poison with glee, Death had secretly laid hold of both your ears.

entries.farsi: من نمى‏كردم غزا از بهر آن / ‏تا ظفر يابم فرو گيرم جهان
entries.english: I did not make war for the sake of gaining victory and conquering the world,

entries.farsi: كاين جهان جيفه ست و مردار و رخيص / ‏بر چنين مردار چون باشم حريص
entries.english: For this world is a carcass and carrion and vile: how should I be covetous of such carrion as this?

entries.farsi: سگ نيم تا پرچم مرده كنم / ‏عيسى‏ام آيم كه تا زنده‏ش كنم
entries.english: I am not a dog that I should tear off the top-knot of the dead; I am Jesus: I come to make him living.

entries.farsi: ز آن همى‏كردم صفوف جنگ چاك / ‏تا رهانم مر شما را از هالك
entries.english: I was cleaving the battle-ranks for the purpose that I might deliver you from destruction.

entries.farsi: ز آن نمى‏برم گلوهاى بشر / تا مرا باشد كر و فر و حشر
entries.english: I do not cut men’s throats in order that power and glory and followers may be mine,

entries.farsi: ز آن همى‏برم گلويى چند تا / ز آن گلوها عالمى يابد رها
entries.english: I cut some throats in order that a world may obtain deliverance from those throats,

entries.farsi: كه شما پروانه‏وار از جهل خويش / ‏پيش آتش مى‏كنيد اين حمله كيش
entries.english: For you in your ignorance make a habit of rushing thus, like moths, at the fire,

entries.farsi: من همى‏رانم شما را همچو مست / ‏از در افتادن در آتش با دو دست
entries.english: I, as a drunk man, drive you away with both hands from falling into the fire.

entries.farsi: آن كه خود را فتحها پنداشتيد / تخم منحوسى خود مى‏كاشتيد
entries.english: That which you deemed victories for yourselves you were sowing the seed of your damnation.

entries.farsi: قهر مى‏كرديد و اندر عين قهر / خود شما مقهور قهر شير دهر
entries.english: You were calling one another most earnestly; you were riding your horses towards the dragon.

entries.farsi: يك دگر را جد جد مى‏خوانديد / سوى اژدرها فرس مى‏رانديد
entries.english: You were overpowering, while in the very act of overpowering you yourselves were being overpowered by the lion Time.”

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه طاغى در عين قاهرى مقهور است و در عين منصورى مأسور
sections.title_en: Showing that the rebellious sinner in the very act of overpowering is overpowered, and in the very moment of victory is made captive

entries.farsi: دزد قهر خواجه كرد و زر كشيد / او بدان مشغول خود والى رسيد
entries.english: The robber overpowered the merchant and carried off the gold: he was just engaged in that, the magistrate arrived.

entries.farsi: گر ز خواجه آن زمان بگريختى / ‏كى بر او والى حشر انگيختى
entries.english: If at that time he had fled from the merchant, how should the magistrate have set the police on him?

entries.farsi: قاهرى دزد مقهوريش بود / ز انكه قهر او سر او را ربود
entries.english: The robber’s overpowering was his being overpowered, because his act of violence took away his head.

entries.farsi: غالبى بر خواجه دام او شود / تا رسد والى و بستاند قود
entries.english: Prevailing over the merchant becomes a trap for him, in order that the magistrate may arrive and take retaliation.

entries.farsi: اى كه تو بر خلق چيره گشته‏اى / ‏در نبرد و غالبى آغشته‏اى
entries.english: O you that have become mighty over the people and art steeped in warfare and victory,

entries.farsi: آن به قاصد منهزم كردستشان / ‏تا ترا در حلقه مى‏آرد كشان
entries.english: That One has purposely caused them to be routed, that all the while drawing you on He may bring you into the net.

entries.farsi: هين عنان در كش پى اين منهزم / ‏در مران تا تو نگردى منخزم
entries.english: Beware, draw rein! Do not push on in pursuit of this fugitive, lest you have your nostrils pierced with a nose-ring.

entries.farsi: چون كشانيدت بدين شيوه به دام / ‏حمله بينى بعد از آن اندر زحام
entries.english: When by this device He has drawn you into the trap, after that you will see the onset pressing in crowds.

entries.farsi: عقل از اين غالب شدن كى گشت شاد / چون در اين غالب شدن ديد او فساد
entries.english: When did the intellect rejoice in this victory, inasmuch as in this victory it saw ruin?

entries.farsi: تيز چشم آمد خرد بيناى پيش / ‏كه خدايش سرمه كرد از كحل خويش
entries.english: The intellect is keen-eyed, possessed of foresight, for God has powdered it with His own eye salve.

entries.farsi: از كمال حزم و سوء الظن خويش / ‏نه ز نقص و بد دلى و ضعف كيش
entries.english: The Prophet said that the folk of Paradise are feeble in quarrels, because of accomplishments—

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه هستند از فنون / ‏اهل جنت در خصومتها زبون
entries.english: Because of the perfection of their prudence and thinking ill, not from deficiency and cowardice and weakness of faith

entries.farsi: در فره‏دادن شنيده در كمون / ‏حكمت لَوْ ال رِجالٌ مؤمنون
entries.english: In giving the advantage they have listened in secret to the wisdom of, Had not there been true-believing men…

entries.farsi: دست‏كوتاهى ز كفار لعين / ‏فرض شد بهر خالص مومنين
entries.english: To keep their hands off the accursed infidels became a duty for the sake of delivering the true believers.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى عهد حديبيه بخوان / ‏كف أيديكم تمامت ز آن بدان
entries.english: Read the story of the covenant of Hudaybiya: He restrained your hands: from that perceive the whole

entries.farsi: نيز اندر غالبى هم خويش را / ديد او مغلوب دام كبريا
entries.english: Even in victory he deemed himself subdued by the snare of Divine Majesty.

entries.farsi: ز آن نمى‏خندم من از زنجيرتان / ‏كه بكردم ناگهان شبگيرتان
entries.english: “It is not because I suddenly marched against you before dawn that I laugh at your chains;

entries.farsi: ز آن همى‏خندم كه با زنجير و غل / ‏هى كشمتان سوى سروستان و گل
entries.english: I laugh because I am dragging you in chains and shackles to the cypress garden and the roses.

entries.farsi: اى عجب كز آتش بى‏زينهار / بسته مى‏آريمتان تا سبزه‏زار
entries.english: O wonder, that we are bringing you in bonds from the merciless fire to the place abounding in verdure;

entries.farsi: از سوى دوزخ به زنجير گران / ‏مى‏كشمتان تا بهشت جاودان
entries.english: With heavy chains I am dragging you from the direction of Hell to the everlasting Paradise.”

entries.farsi: هر مقلد را در اين هر نيك و بد / همچنان بسته به حضرت مى‏كشد
entries.english: Every blind follower in this Way, be he good or evil, He is dragging, bound like that, into His Presence.

entries.farsi: جمله در زنجير بيم و ابتال / مى‏روند اين ره بغير اوليا
entries.english: All go along this Way in the chains of fear and tribulation, except the saints.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشند اين راه را پيكاروار / جز كسانى واقف از اسرار كار
entries.english: They are dragged along this Way reluctantly, except those persons who are acquainted with the mysteries of the action.

entries.farsi: جهد كن تا نور تو رخشان شود / تا سلوك و خدمتت آسان شود
entries.english: Endeavour that your light become radiant, so that your travelling and service may be made easy.

entries.farsi: كودكان را مى‏برى مكتب به زور / ز انكه هستند از فوايد چشم كور
entries.english: You take children to school by force, because they are blind to the benefits;

entries.farsi: چون شود واقف به مكتب مى‏دود / جانش از رفتن شكفته مى‏شود
entries.english: When he becomes aware, he runs to school: his soul expands at going.

entries.farsi: مى‏رود كودك به مكتب پيچ پيچ / ‏چون نديد از مزد كار خويش هيچ
entries.english: A child goes to school in sore distress because he has seen nothing of the wages for his work;

entries.farsi: چون كند در كيسه دانگى دست‏مزد / آن گهان بى‏خواب گردد شب چو دزد
entries.english: When he puts in his purse a single coin (dang)earned by his handiwork, then he goes without sleep at night, like the thief.

entries.farsi: جهد كن تا مزد طاعت در رسد / بر مطيعان آن گهت آيد حسد
entries.english: Endeavour that the wages for obedience may arrive: then you will envy the obedient.

entries.farsi: ائتيا كرها مقلد گشته را / ائتيا طوعا صفا بسرشته را
entries.english: Come against your will is for him that has become a blind follower; come willingly is for him that is moulded of sincerity.

entries.farsi: اين محب حق ز بهر علتى / ‏و آن دگر را بى‏غرض خود خلتى
entries.english: The former loves God for the sake of some cause, while the other has indeed a pure disinterested love.

entries.farsi: اين محب دايه ليك از بهر شير / و آن دگر دل داده بهر اين ستير
entries.english: The former loves the Nurse, but for the sake of the milk, while the other has given his heart for the sake of this Veiled One.

entries.farsi: طفل را از حسن او آگاه نه / ‏غير شير او را از او دل خواه نه
entries.english: The child has no knowledge of Her beauty: he has no desire of Her in his heart except for milk,

entries.farsi: و آن دگر خود عاشق دايه بود / بى‏غرض در عشق يك رايه بود
entries.english: While the other is, truly, the lover of the Nurse: he is disinterested, single-minded in love.

entries.farsi: پس محب حق به اوميد و به ترس / ‏دفتر تقليد مى‏خواند به درس
entries.english: Hence he that loves God because of hope and fear reads studiously the book of blind conformity,

entries.farsi: و آن محب حق ز بهر حق كجاست / ‏كه ز اغراض و ز علتها جداست
entries.english: While he that loves God for God’s sake—where is he? For he is apart from self-interests and causes

entries.farsi: گر چنين و گر چنان چون طالب است / ‏جذب حق او را سوى حق جاذب است
entries.english: Whether he be like this or like that, inasmuch as he is a seeker, God’s attraction is drawing him towards God.

entries.farsi: گر محب حق بود لغيره / ‏كى ينال دايما من خيره
entries.english: Whether he love God for something other than He, that he may continually partake of His good,

entries.farsi: يا محب حق بود لعينه / ‏ال سواه خائفا من بينه
entries.english: Or whether he love God for His very Self, for naught besides Him, in fear of separation from Him—

entries.farsi: هر دو را اين جستجوها ز آن سرى است / ‏اين گرفتارى دل ز آن دلبرى است
entries.english: The quests and seeking of both are from that Source: this captivation of the heart is from that Heart-ravisher.

sections.title_fa: جذب معشوق عاشق را من حيث ال يعلمه العاشق و ال يرجوه و ال يخطر بباله و ال يظهر من ذلك ‏الجذب أثر في العاشق إال الخوف الممزوج باليأس مع دوام الطلب
sections.title_en: How the Beloved attracts the lover in such wise that the lover neither knows it nor hopes for it,
nor does it occur to his mind, nor does any trace of that attraction appear in the lover except the fear that is mingled with despair, though he still perseveres in the quest.

entries.farsi: آمديم اينجا كه در صدر جهان / ‏گر نبودى جذب آن عاشق نهان
entries.english: We came to this point, that if the attraction of that lover had not been hidden in the Sadr-i Jahan,

entries.farsi: ميل معشوقان نهان است و ستير / ميل عاشق با دو صد طبل و نفير
entries.english: How would he have been impatient of separation, and how would he have come running back to his home?

entries.farsi: ناشكيبا كى بدى او از فراق / ‏كى دوان باز آمدى سوى وثاق
entries.english: The desire of loved ones is hidden and veiled; the desire of the lover is with a hundred drums and trumpets.

entries.farsi: ترك آن كرديم كاو در جستجوست / ‏تا كه پيش از مرگ بيند روى دوست
entries.english: Here is a story of consideration, but the man of Bukhara has become desperate from waiting expectantly;

entries.farsi: يك حكايت هست اينجا ز اعتبار / ليك عاجز شد بخارى ز انتظار
entries.english: We omit it, for he is in search and seeking, that before death he may see the face of his beloved,

entries.farsi: تا رهد از مرگ تا يابد نجات / ‏ز انكه ديد دوست است آب حيات
entries.english: To the end that he may escape from death and gain deliverance, because the sight of the beloved is the Water of Life

entries.farsi: هر كه ديد او نباشد دفع مرگ / ‏دوست نبود كه نه ميوه‏ستش نه برگ
entries.english: Any one the sight of whom does not repel death is not the beloved, for he has neither fruit nor leaf.

entries.farsi: كار آن كار است اى مشتاق مست / ‏كاندر آن كار ار رسد مرگت خوش است
entries.english: The matter, O intoxicated longing lover, is that matter in which death, if it you, is sweet.

entries.farsi: شد نشان صدق ايمان اى جوان / ‏آن كه آيد خوش ترا مرگ اندر آن
entries.english: O youth, the token of sincerity of faith is that in which death comes sweet to you.

entries.farsi: گر نشد ايمان تو اى جان چنين / ‏نيست كامل رو بجو اكمال دين
entries.english: If your faith, O soul, is not like this, it is not perfect: go, seek to make religion perfect.

entries.farsi: هر كه اندر كار تو شد مرگ دوست / ‏بر دل تو بى‏كراهت دوست اوست
entries.english: Whoever in matter of yours has become death-loving without dislike to your heart, he is beloved.

entries.farsi: چون كراهت رفت آن خود مرگ نيست / صورت مرگ است و نقالن كردنى است
entries.english: When dislike is gone, truly it is not death: it is the semblance of death, and it is a migration.

entries.farsi: چون كراهت رفت مردن نفع شد / پس درست آيد كه مردن دفع شد
entries.english: When dislike is gone, dying becomes advantageous; hence it comes true that death is repelled.

entries.farsi: دوست حق است و كسى كش گفت او / كه تويى آن من و من آن تو
entries.english: The beloved is God and the person to whom He has said, “You are Mine and I am yours.”

entries.farsi: گوش دار اكنون كه عاشق مى‏رسد / بسته عشق او را بحبل من مسد
entries.english: Now listen, for the lover is coming whom Love bound with a cord of palm-fibre.

entries.farsi: چون بديد او چهره‏ى صدر جهان / ‏گوييا پريدش از تن مرغ جان
entries.english: When he beheld the countenance of the Sadr-i Jahan, you might say the bird, his spirit, flew out of his body.

entries.farsi: همچو چوب خشك افتاد آن تنش / ‏سرد شد از فرق جان تا ناخنش
entries.english: His body fell like dry wood: his vital spirit became cold from the crown of his head to his toes.

entries.farsi: هر چه كردند از بخور و از گالب / ‏نه بجنبيد و نه آمد در خطاب
entries.english: Whatever they applied of incense and rose-water, he neither stirred nor spoke.

entries.farsi: شاه چون ديد آن مزعفر روى او / پس فرود آمد ز مركب سوى او
entries.english: When the King saw his saffron-coloured face, he dismounted from his steed and came towards him.

entries.farsi: گفت عاشق دوست مى‏جويد به تفت / چون كه معشوق آمد آن عاشق برفت
entries.english: He said, “The lover hotly seeks the beloved: when the beloved comes, the lover is gone.”

entries.farsi: عاشق حقى و حق آن است كاو / چون بيايد نبود از تو تاى مو
entries.english: You are a lover of God, and God is such that when He comes there is not a single hair of you.

entries.farsi: صد چو تو فانى است پيش آن نظر / عاشقى بر نفى خود خواجه مگر
entries.english: At that look a hundred like you vanish away: I think, sir, you are in love with self-erasing.

entries.farsi: سايه‏اى و عاشقى بر آفتاب / ‏شمس آيد سايه ال گردد شتاب
entries.english: You are a shadow and in love with the sun: the sun comes, the shadow is speedily erased.

sections.title_fa: ‏داد خواستن پشه از باد به حضرت سليمان عليه السلام
sections.title_en: How, in the presence of Solomon, on whom be peace, the gnat appealed for justice against the Wind.

entries.farsi: پشه آمد از حديقه و ز گياه / ‏و ز سليمان گشت پشه داد خواه
entries.english: The gnat came from the garden and the grass, and the gnat began to demand justice from Solomon,

entries.farsi: كاى سليمان معدلت مى‏گسترى / ‏بر شياطين و آدمى زاد و پرى
entries.english: Saying, “O Solomon, you deal out justice to the devils and the children of men and the Jinn.

entries.farsi: مرغ و ماهى در پناه عدل تست / ‏كيست آن گم گشته كش فضلت نجست
entries.english: Bird and fish are under the protection of your justice: who is the lost one whom your bounty has not sought out?

entries.farsi: داد ده ما را كه بس زاريم ما / بى‏نصيب از باغ و گلزاريم ما
entries.english: Give justice to us, for we are very miserable: we are deprived of the orchard and the rose-garden.

entries.farsi: شهره ما در ضعف و اشكسته پرى / ‏شهره تو در لطف و مسكين پرورى
entries.english: The difficulties of every weakling are solved by you: the gnat in truth is the similitude for weakness.

entries.farsi: مشكالت هر ضعيفى از تو حل / ‏پشه باشد در ضعيفى خود مثل
entries.english: We are celebrated for weakness and frailty: you art celebrated for kindness and care of the lowly.

entries.farsi: اى تو در اطباق قدرت منتهى / ‏منتهى ما در كمى و بى‏رهى
entries.english: O you who have reached the limit in the stages of Power, we have reached the limit in failure and aberration,

entries.farsi: داد ده ما را از اين غم كن جدا / دست گير اى دست تو دست خدا
entries.english: Be just, relieve us from this sorrow, and take our hand, O you whose hand is the hand of God.”

entries.farsi: پس سليمان گفت اى انصاف جو / داد و انصاف از كه مى‏خواهى بگو
entries.english: Then Solomon said, “O seeker of equity, tell, against whom are you demanding justice and equity?

entries.farsi: كيست آن ظالم كه از باد و بروت / ‏ظلم كرده ست و خراشيده ست روت
entries.english: Who is the oppressor that in insolence has done you injury and scratched your face?

entries.farsi: اى عجب در عهد ما ظالم كجاست / ‏كاو نه اندر حبس و در زنجير ماست
entries.english: Oh, wonderful! Where, in Our epoch, is the oppressor that is not in Our prison and chains?

entries.farsi: چون كه ما زاديم ظلم آن روز مرد / پس به عهد ما كه ظلمى پيش برد
entries.english: When We were born, on that day Injustice died: who, then, has produced in Our epoch an act of injustice?

entries.farsi: چون بر آمد نور ظلمت نيست شد / ظلم را ظلمت بود اصل و عضد
entries.english: When the light dawned, the darkness vanished: darkness is the origin and support of injustice.

entries.farsi: نك شياطين كسب و خدمت مى‏كنند / ديگران بسته به اصفادند و بند
entries.english: Look, the devils are doing work and service; the others are bound in shackles and bonds.

entries.farsi: اصل ظلم ظالمان از ديو بود / ديو در بند است استم چون نمود
entries.english: The origin of the injustice of the oppressors was from the devil: the devil is in bondage: how did violence appear?

entries.farsi: ملك ز آن داده ست ما را كن فكان / ‏تا ننالد خلق سوى آسمان
entries.english: ‘Be, and it was’ has bestowed the kingdom on Us, that the people may not cry out in lament to Heaven;

entries.farsi: تا ببالا بر نيايد دودها / تا نگردد مضطرب چرخ و سها
entries.english: That burning sighs may not soar upward; that the sky and the stars may not be shaken;

entries.farsi: تا نلرزد عرش از ناله‏ى يتيم / ‏تا نگردد از ستم جانى سقيم
entries.english: That the empyrean may not tremble at the orphan’s wail; that no soul may be marred by violence.

entries.farsi: ز آن نهاديم از ممالك مذهبى / ‏تا نيايد بر فلك‏ها يا ربى
entries.english: We established a law throughout the kingdoms, to the end that no ‘O Lord!’ should go up to the skies.

entries.farsi: منگر اى مظلوم سوى آسمان / ‏كاسمانى شاه دارى در زمان
entries.english: O oppressed one; do not look to Heaven, for you have a heavenly king in the temporal world.”

entries.farsi: گفت پشه داد من از دست باد / كاو دو دست ظلم بر ما بر گشاد
entries.english: The gnat said, “My appeal is against the hand of the Wind, for he opened the two hands of oppression against us.

entries.farsi: ما به ظلم او به تنگى اندريم / با لب بسته از او خون مى‏خوريم
entries.english: Through his oppression we are in sore straits: with closed lips we are drinking blood from him.”

sections.title_fa: ‏امر كردن سليمان عليه السلام پشه‏ى متظلم را به احضار خصم به ديوان حكم
sections.title_en: How Solomon, on whom be peace, commanded the plaintiff gnat to bring its adversary to the court of judgment.

entries.farsi: حق به من گفته ست هان اى دادور / مشنو از خصمى تو بى‏خصمى دگر
entries.english: Then Solomon said, “O you with the pretty voice, it behooves you to listen with soul to the command of God.

entries.farsi: پس سليمان گفت اى زيبا دوى / ‏امر حق بايد كه از جان بشنوى
entries.english: God has said to me, ‘Beware, O Judge! Do not hear one litigant without the other litigant.

entries.farsi: تا نيايد هر دو خصم اندر حضور / حق نيايد پيش حاكم در ظهور
entries.english: Until both litigants come into the presence, the truth does not come to light before the judge.

entries.farsi: خصم تنها گر بر آرد صد نفير / هان و هان بى‏خصم قول او مگير
entries.english: If the litigant alone raises a hundred clamours, beware, beware! Do not accept his word without his adversary.’

entries.farsi: من نيارم رو ز فرمان تافتن / ‏خصم خود را رو بياور سوى من
entries.english: I dare not avert my face from the command. Go, bring your adversary before me.”

entries.farsi: گفت قول تست برهان و درست / ‏خصم من باد است و او در حكم تست
entries.english: It said, “Your words are an argument and sound. My adversary is the Wind, and he is in your jurisdiction.”

entries.farsi: بانگ زد آن شه كه اى باد صبا / پشه افغان كرد از ظلمت بيا
entries.english: The King shouted, “O East-wind, the gnat complains of your injustice: come!

entries.farsi: هين مقابل شو تو و خصم و بگو / پاسخ خصم و بكن دفع عدو
entries.english: Listen, come face to face with your adversary and reply to your adversary and rebut your opponent.”

entries.farsi: باد چون بشنيد آمد تيز تيز / پشه بگرفت آن زمان راه گريز
entries.english: When the Wind heard, he came very rapidly: the gnat at once took to flight.

entries.farsi: پس سليمان گفت اى پشه كجا / باش تا بر هر دو رانم من قضا
entries.english: Then Solomon said, “O gnat, where are you going? Stop, that I may pass judgment on both.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه مرگ من از بود اوست / ‏خود سياه اين روز من از دود اوست
entries.english: It answered, “O King, my death is from his being: truly, this day of mine is black from his smoke.

entries.farsi: او چو آمد من كجا يابم قرار / كاو بر آرد از نهاد من دمار
entries.english: Since he has come, where shall I find rest? For he wrings the breath out of my body”

entries.farsi: همچنين جوياى درگاه خدا / چون خدا آمد شود جوينده ال
entries.english: Even such is the seeker of the Court of God: when God comes, the seeker disappears.

entries.farsi: سايه‏هايى كه بود جوياى نور / نيست گردد چون كند نورش ظهور
entries.english: Although that union is immortality on immortality, yet at first that immortality (baqa) consists in dying to self (fana).

entries.farsi: گر چه آن وصلت بقا اندر بقاست / ‏ليك ز اول آن بقا اندر فناست
entries.english: The reflections that are seeking the Light disappear when His Light appears.

entries.farsi: عقل كى ماند چو باشد سر ده او / ‏كُلُّ شَيْ‏ءٍ هالِكٌ إِلاَّ وجهه
entries.english: How should the reason remain when He bids it go? Everything is perishing except His Face.

entries.farsi: هالك آيد پيش وجهش هست و نيست / ‏هستى اندر نيستى خود طرفه‏اى است
entries.english: Before His Face the existent and the non-existent perish: existence in nonexistence is in truth a marvellous thing!

entries.farsi: اندر اين محضر خردها شد ز دست / ‏چون قلم اينجا رسيده شد شكست
entries.english: In this place of presence minds are lost beyond control; when the pen reaches this point, it breaks.

sections.title_fa: نواختن معشوق عاشق بى‏هوش را تا به هوش باز آيد
sections.title_en: How the Beloved caressed the senseless lover that he might return to his senses.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشيد از بى‏هشى‏اش در بيان / ‏اندك اندك از كرم صدر جهان
entries.english: The Sadr-i Jahan, from kindness, was drawing him little by little from senselessness into clear expression.

entries.farsi: بانگ زد در گوش او شه كاى گدا / زر نثار آوردمت دامن گشا
entries.english: The Prince cried into his ear, “O beggar, I bring gold to scatter over you; spread out your skirt.

entries.farsi: جان تو كاندر فراقم مى‏طپيد / چون كه زنهارش رسيدم چون رميد
entries.english: Your spirit, which was quivering in separation from me— since I have come to protect it, how has it fled?

entries.farsi: اى بديده در فراقم گرم و سرد / با خود آ از بى‏خودى و باز گرد
entries.english: O you, who have suffered heat and cold in separation from me, come to yourself from selflessness and return!”

entries.farsi: مرغ خانه اشترى را بى‏خرد / رسم مهمانش به خانه مى‏برد
entries.english: The domestic fowl, in the manner of a host, foolishly brings a camel to her house.

entries.farsi: چون به خانه‏ى مرغ اشتر پا نهاد / خانه ويران گشت و سقف اندر فتاد
entries.english: When the camel set foot in the hen’s house, the house was destroyed and the roof fell in.

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى مرغ است هوش و عقل ما / هوش صالح طالب ناقه‏ى خدا
entries.english: The hen’s house is our intelligence and understanding the good intelligence is a seeker of God’s she-camel

entries.farsi: كرد فضل عشق انسان را فضول / ‏زين فزون‏جويى ظلوم است و جهول
entries.english: When the she-camel put her head into its water and clay, neither its clay remained there nor its soul and heart.

entries.farsi: ناقه چون سر كرد در آب و گلش / ‏نه گل آن جا ماند نه جان و دلش
entries.english: Pre-eminence in love made Man overbearing: because of this desire for excess he is very unjust and very ignorant.

entries.farsi: جاهل است و اندر اين مشكل شكار / مى‏كشد خرگوش شيرى در كنار
entries.english: He is ignorant, and in this difficult chase the hare is clasping a lion in his arms.

entries.farsi: كى كنار اندر كشيدى شير را / گر بدانستى و ديدى شير را
entries.english: How would he clasp the lion in his arms, if he knew and saw the lion?

entries.farsi: ظالم است او بر خود و بر جان خود / ظلم بين كز عدلها گو مى‏برد
entries.english: He is unjust to himself and to his own soul: behold an in-justice that bears away the ball from justices!

entries.farsi: جهل او مر علمها را اوستاد / ظلم او مر عدلها را شد رشاد
entries.english: His ignorance is the teacher to knowledge; his injustice has become the right way for justice.

entries.farsi: دست او بگرفت كاين رفته دمش / ‏آن گهى آيد كه من دم بخشمش
entries.english: He took his hand, saying, “This man whose breath has departed will then come alive when I give him breath.

entries.farsi: چون به من زنده شود اين مرده تن / ‏جان من باشد كه رو آرد به من
entries.english: When this man whose body is dead shall become living through Me, it will be My spirit that turns its face towards Me.

entries.farsi: جان نامحرم نبيند روى دوست / ‏جز همان جان كاصل او از كوى اوست
entries.english: By means of this spirit I make him possessed of high estate: the spirit that I give sees My bounty.

entries.farsi: من كنم او را از اين جان محتشم / ‏جان كه من بخشم ببيند بخششم
entries.english: The unfamiliar spirit does not see the face of the Beloved: except that spirit whose origin is from His dwelling-place.

entries.farsi: در دمم قصاب وار اين دوست را / تا هلد آن مغز نغزش پوست را
entries.english: Butcher-like, I breathe upon this dear friend, in order that his goodly inward part may leave the skin.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى جان رميده از بال / وصل ما را در گشاديم الصال
entries.english: He said, “O spirit that have fled from tribulation, We have opened the door to union with Us; welcome!

entries.farsi: اى خود ما بى‏خودى و مستى‏ات / ‏اى ز هست ما هماره هستى‏ات
entries.english: O you whose selflessness and intoxication is Our Self, O you whose being is incessantly from Our Being,

entries.farsi: با تو بى‏لب اين زمان من نو به نو / رازهاى كهنه گويم مى‏شنو
entries.english: Now, without lip, I tell you the old mysteries anew: listen!

entries.farsi: ز انكه آن لبها از اين دم مى‏رمد / بر لب جوى نهان بر مى‏دمد
entries.english: Because those lips are fleeing from this Breath; it is breathed forth on the lip of the hidden River.

entries.farsi: گوش بى‏گوشى در اين دم بر گشا / بهر راز يَفْعَلُ اللهَُّ ما يشاء
entries.english: At this moment open the ear of silence for the sake of the mystery of God does what He -wills.”

entries.farsi: چون صالى وصل بشنيدن گرفت / ‏اندك اندك مرده جنبيدن گرفت
entries.english: When he began to hear the call to union, little by little, the dead man began to stir

entries.farsi: نه كم از خاك است كز عشوه‏ى صبا / سبز پوشد سر بر آرد از فنا
entries.english: He is not less than the earth which at the zephyr’s blandishments puts on green and lifts up its head from death;

entries.farsi: كم ز آب نطفه نبود كز خطاب / ‏يوسفان زايند رخ چون آفتاب
entries.english: He is not less than the seminal water from which at the bidding there are born Josephs with faces like the sun;

entries.farsi: كم ز بادى نيست شد از امر كن / ‏در رحم طاوس و مرغ خوش سخن
entries.english: He is not less than a wind at the command “Be!” peacocks and sweet-voiced birds came to being in the womb

entries.farsi: كم ز كوه سنگ نبود كز والد / ناقه‏اى كان ناقه ناقه زاد زاد
entries.english: He is not less than the mountain of rock which by birthing brought forth the she-camel that brought forth a she-camel

entries.farsi: زين همه بگذر نه آن مايه‏ى عدم / ‏عالمى زاد و بزايد دم‏به‏دم
entries.english: Leave all this behind. Did not the substance of non-existence bring forth, and will it not bring forth continually, a Universe?

entries.farsi: بر جهيد و بر طپيد و شاد شاد / يك دو چرخى زد سجود اندر فتاد
entries.english: He sprang up and quivered and whirled once or twice joyously, joyously; fell to worship.

sections.title_fa: ‏با خويش آمدن عاشق بى‏هوش و روى آوردن به ثنا و شكر معشوق
sections.title_en: How the senseless lover came to himself and turned his face in praise and thanksgiving to the Beloved.

entries.farsi: گفت اى عنقاى حق جان را مطاف / ‏شكر كه باز آمدى ز آن كوه قاف
entries.english: He said, “O ‘Anqa of God, the place of the spirit’s circling flight, thanks that you have come back from yonder Qaf Mountain.

entries.farsi: اى سرافيل قيامت گاه عشق / ‏اى تو عشق عشق و اى دل خواه عشق
entries.english: O Seraphiel of Love’s resurrection place O Love of love and O Heart’s-desire of love,

entries.farsi: اولين خلعت كه خواهى دادنم / ‏گوش خواهم كه نهى بر روزنم
entries.english: I desire, as the first gift of honour you will give me, that you lay your ear on my window.

entries.farsi: گر چه مى‏دانى به صفوت حال من / ‏بنده پرور گوش كن اقوال من
entries.english: Although through purity you know my feelings, lend ear to my words, O cherisher of your slave.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران بار اى صدر فريد / ز آرزوى گوش تو هوشم پريد
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of times, O unique Prince, did my wits fly away in longing for your ear—

entries.farsi: آن سميعى تو و آن اصغاى تو / و آن تبسمهاى جان افزاى تو
entries.english: That hearing of your and that listening of your, and those life-quickening smiles of your;

entries.farsi: آن نيوشيدن كم و بيش مرا / عشوه‏ى جان بد انديش مرا
entries.english: That listening unto my lesser and greater, the beguilements of my evil-thinking soul.

entries.farsi: قلبهاى من كه آن معلوم تست / ‏بس پذيرفتى تو چون نقد درست
entries.english: Then my false coins, which are well-known to you, you did accept as genuine money;

entries.farsi: بهر گستاخى شوخ غره‏اى / ‏حلمها در پيش حلمت ذره‏اى
entries.english: For the sake of the boldness of one impudent and deluded, O you beside whose clemency all clemencies are a mote!

entries.farsi: اوال بشنو كه چون ماندم ز شست / ‏اول و آخر ز پيش من بجست
entries.english: Firstly, hear that when I abandoned net the first and the last shot away from before me;

entries.farsi: ثانيا بشنو تو اى صدر ودود / كه بسى جستم ترا ثانى نبود
entries.english: Secondly, hear, O loving Prince, that I sought long, there was no second to you;

entries.farsi: ثالثا تا از تو بيرون رفته‏ام / ‏گوييا ثالث ثالثه گفته‏ام
entries.english: Thirdly, since I have gone away from you, it is as though I have said, ‘the third of three’

entries.farsi: رابعا چون سوخت ما را مزرعه / ‏مى‏ندانم خامسه از رابعه
entries.english: Fourthly, forasmuch as my wheat field is burnt-up, I do not know the-fifth from the fourth

entries.farsi: هر كجا يابى تو خون بر خاكها / پى برى باشد يقين از چشم ما
entries.english: Wherever you find blood on the sods, you investigate; it will certainly be from mine eye.

entries.farsi: گفت من رعد است و اين بانگ و حنين / ‏ز ابر خواهد تا ببارد بر زمين
entries.english: My words are the thunder, and this noise and moaning demands of the cloud that it should rain upon the earth.

entries.farsi: من ميان گفت و گريه مى‏تنم / ‏يا بگريم يا بگويم چون كنم
entries.english: Between words and tears I continue whether I should weep or speak: how shall I do?

entries.farsi: گر بگويم فوت مى‏گردد بكا / ور بگريم چون كنم شكر و ثنا
entries.english: If I speak, the weeping will be lost; and if I weep, how shall I render thanks and praise?

entries.farsi: مى‏فتد از ديده خون دل شها / بين چه افتاده ست از ديده مرا
entries.english: Heart’s blood is falling from mine eye, O King: see what has befallen me from mine eye!”

entries.farsi: اين بگفت و گريه در شد آن نحيف / ‏كه بر او بگريست هم دون هم شريف
entries.english: The emaciated man said this and began to weep that both base and noble wept for him.

entries.farsi: از دلش چندان بر آمد هاى‏و هوى / ‏حلقه كرد اهل بخارا گرد اوى
entries.english: So many ecstatic cries flowed from his heart the people of Bukhara made a ring around him.

entries.farsi: خيره گويان خيره گريان خيره خند / مرد و زن خرد و كالن حيران شدند
entries.english: Speaking crazily, weeping crazily, and laughing crazily: men and women, small and great were bewildered.

entries.farsi: شهر هم هم رنگ او شد اشك ريز / مرد و زن درهم شده چون رستخيز
entries.english: The city, too, shed tears in conformity with him: men and women were gathered together as the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: آسمان مى‏گفت آن دم با زمين / ‏گر قيامت را نديدستى ببين
entries.english: At that moment the heaven was saying to the earth, “If you have never seen the Resurrection, behold it!”

entries.farsi: عقل حيران كه چه عشق است و چه حال / ‏تا فراق او عجبتر يا وصال
entries.english: The intellect bewildered, saying, “What is love and what is ecstasy? Whether separation from Him or union with Him is the more marvellous”

entries.farsi: چرخ بر خوانده قيامت نامه را / تا مجره بر دريده جامه را
entries.english: The sky read the letter of Resurrection it rent its garment up to the Milky Way.

entries.farsi: با دو عالم عشق را بيگانگى / ‏اندر او هفتاد و دو ديوانگى
entries.english: Love bath estrangement with the two worlds: in it are two-and-seventy types of madness.

entries.farsi: سخت پنهان است و پيدا حيرتش / ‏جان سلطانان جان در حسرتش
entries.english: It is exceedingly hidden, and its bewilderment is manifest: the soul of the spiritual sultans is pining for it

entries.farsi: غير هفتاد و دو ملت كيش او / تخت شاهان تخته بندى پيش او
entries.english: Its religion is other than the two-and-seventy sects: beside it the throne of Kings is a splint-bandage.

entries.farsi: مطرب عشق اين زند وقت سماع / ‏بندگى بند و خداوندى صداع
entries.english: At the time of the sama Love’s minstrel strikes up this: “Servitude is chains and lordship headache.”

entries.farsi: پس چه باشد عشق درياى عدم / ‏در شكسته عقل را آن جا قدم
entries.english: Then what is Love? The Sea of Not-being: there the foot of the intellect is shattered’

entries.farsi: بندگى و سلطنت معلوم شد / زين دو پرده عاشقى مكتوم شد
entries.english: Servitude and sovereignty are known: loverhood is concealed by these two veils.

entries.farsi: كاشكى هستى زبانى داشتى / تا ز هستان پرده‏ها برداشتى
entries.english: Would that Being had a tongue; that it might remove the veils from existent beings!

entries.farsi: هر چه گويى اى دم هستى از آن / ‏پرده‏ى ديگر بر او بستى بدان
entries.english: O breath of existence, whatsoever words you may utter, know that thereby you have bound another veil upon it. .

entries.farsi: آفت ادراك آن قال است و حال / ‏خون به خون شستن محال است و محال
entries.english: That utterance and state are the bane of perception: to wash away blood with blood is absurd, absurd.

entries.farsi: سخت مست و بى‏خود و آشفته‏اى / ‏دوش اى جان بر چه پهلو خفته‏اى
entries.english: Since I am familiar with His frenzied ones, day and night I am breathing forth in the cage.

entries.farsi: من چو با سوداييانش محرمم / ‏روز و شب اندر قفس در مى‏دمم
entries.english: You are very drunk and senseless and distraught: yesterday on which side have you slept, O soul?

entries.farsi: هان و هان هش دار بر نارى دمى / ‏اوال برجه طلب كن محرمى
entries.english: Beware, beware! Take heed lest you utter a breath! First spring up and seek a trusted friend.

entries.farsi: عاشق و مستى و بگشاده زبان / ‏اللهَّ اهلل اشترى بر ناودان
entries.english: You are a lover and intoxicated, and your tongue loosed! —God! God! You are the camel on the water-spout’!

entries.farsi: چون ز راز و ناز او گويد زبان / يا جميل الستر خواند آسمان
entries.english: When the tongue tells of His mystery and coquetry, Heaven chants, “O You that art goodly in covering!

entries.farsi: ستر چه در پشم و پنبه آذر است / ‏تا همى‏پوشيش او پيداتر است
entries.english: What covering? The fire is in the wool cotton whilst you are covering it up, it is more manifest.

entries.farsi: رغم انفم گيردم او هر دو گوش / ‏كاى مدمغ چونش مى‏پوشى بپوش
entries.english: When I endeavour to hide His secret, He lifts up His head, like a banner, saying, ‘Look, here am I!”

entries.farsi: چون بكوشم تا سرش پنهان كنم / ‏سر بر آرد چون علم كاينك منم
entries.english: Despite me He seizes both my ears, saying, “O scatter-brain, how will you cover it Cover it!”

entries.farsi: گويمش رو گر چه بر جوشيده‏اى / ‏همچو جان پيدايى و پوشيده‏اى
entries.english: I say to Him, “Go! Though you have bubbled up, you are manifest and concealed, like the soul.”

entries.farsi: گويد او محبوس خنب است اين تنم / ‏چون مى اندر بزم خنبك مى‏زنم
entries.english: He says, “This body of mine is imprisoned in the jar, like wine I am clapping hands at the banquet.”

entries.farsi: گويمش ز آن پيش كه گردى گرو / تا نيايد آفت مستى برو
entries.english: I say to Him, “Go before you are put in pawn, lest the bane of intoxication befall.”

entries.farsi: گويد از جام لطيف‏آشام من / ‏يار روزم تا نماز شام من
entries.english: He says, “I befriend the day with delicious cup until the evening-prayer.

entries.farsi: ز آن عرب بنهاد نام مى مدام / ‏ز انكه سيرى نيست مى خور را مدام
entries.english: When evening comes and steals my cup, I will say to it, ‘Give back, for my evening has not come.”

entries.farsi: چون بيايد شام و دزدد جام من / ‏گويمش واده كه نامد شام من
entries.english: Hence the Arabs applied the same mudam to wine, because the wine-drinker is never sated.

entries.farsi: عشق جوشد باده‏ى تحقيق را / او بود ساقى نهان صديق را
entries.english: Love makes the wine of realization to bubble: He is the cup- bearer to the true lover (siddiq) in secret.

entries.farsi: چون بجويى تو به توفيق حسن / ‏باده آب جان بود ابريق تن
entries.english: When you seek with good help, the water of the spirit is the wine, and the body is the flagon.

entries.farsi: چون بيفزايد مى توفيق را / قوت مى بشكند ابريق را
entries.english: When He increases the wine of His help, the potency of the wine bursts the flagon.

entries.farsi: آب گردد ساقى و هم مست آب / ‏چون مگو و اهلل أعلم بالصواب
entries.english: The water becomes the Cup-bearer, and the water also the drunkard. Tell not how! And God best knows the right.

entries.farsi: پرتو ساقى است كاندر شيره رفت / ‏شيره بر جوشيد و رقصان گشت و زفت
entries.english: It is the radiance of the Cup-bearer that entered into the must: the must bubbled up and began to dance and waxed strong.

entries.farsi: اندر اين معنى بپرس آن خيره را / كه چنين كى ديده بودى شيره را
entries.english: On this matter, ask the heedless, “When did you see must like this?”

entries.farsi: بى‏تفكر پيش هر داننده هست / ‏آن كه با شوريده شوراننده هست
entries.english: To everyone who has knowledge it is without reflection, that together with the person disturbed there is a Disturber.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت عاشقى دراز هجرانى و بسيار امتحانى
sections.title_en: Story of the lover who had been long separated and had suffered much tribulation

entries.farsi: يك جوانى بر زنى مجنون بده ست / ‏مى‏ندادش روزگار وصل دست
entries.english: A certain youth was madly enamoured of a woman: the fortune of union was not granted to him.

entries.farsi: بس شكنجه كرد عشقش بر زمين / ‏خود چرا دارد ز اول عشق كين
entries.english: Love tortured him exceedingly on the earth: why, in truth, does Love bear hatred from the first?

entries.farsi: عشق، از اول چرا خونى بود / تا گريزد آن كه بيرونى بود
entries.english: Why is Love murderous from the first, so that he who is an outsider runs away?

entries.farsi: چون فرستادى رسولى پيش زن / ‏آن رسول از رشك گشتى راه زن
entries.english: Whenever he sent a messenger to the woman, the messenger because of jealousy would become a highwayman;

entries.farsi: ور به سوى زن نبشتى كاتبش / ‏نامه را تصحيف خواندى نايبش
entries.english: And if his secretary wrote to the woman, his delegate would read the letter with tashíf;

entries.farsi: ور صبا را پيك كردى در وفا / از غبارى تيره گشتى آن صبا
entries.english: And if in good faith he made the zephyr his envoy, that zephyr would be darkened by a dust.

entries.farsi: رقعه گر بر پر مرغى دوختى / ‏پر مرغ از تف رقعه سوختى
entries.english: If he sewed the letter on the wing of a bird, the bird’s wing would be burnt by the ardour of the letter.

entries.farsi: راههاى چاره را غيرت ببست / ‏لشكر انديشه را رايت شكست
entries.english: The jealousy barred the ways of device and broke the banner of the army of cogitation.

entries.farsi: بود اول مونس غم انتظار / آخرش بشكست كى هم انتظار
entries.english: At first, expectation was the comforting friend of sorrow; at last, there broke him—who? Even expectation.

entries.farsi: گاه گفتى كين بالى بى‏دواست / ‏گاه گفتى نه حيات جان ماست
entries.english: Sometimes he would say, “This is an irremediable affliction”; sometimes he would say, “No, it is the life of my spirit.”

entries.farsi: گاه هستى زو بر آوردى سرى / ‏گاه او از نيستى خوردى برى
entries.english: Sometimes existence would lift up a head from him; sometimes he would eat of the fruit of non-existence.

entries.farsi: چون كه بر وى سرد گشتى اين نهاد / جوش كردى گرم چشمه‏ى اتحاد
entries.english: When this nature became cold to him, the fountain of union would boil hotly.

entries.farsi: چون كه با بى‏برگى غربت بساخت / ‏برگ بى‏برگى به سوى او بتاخت
entries.english: When he put up with the unprovidedness of exile, the provision of unprovidedness hastened towards him.

entries.farsi: خوشه‏هاى فكرتش بى‏كاه شد / شب روان را رهنما چون ماه شد
entries.english: The wheat-ears of his thought were purged of chaff: he became, like the moon, a guide to the night-travellers.

entries.farsi: اى بسا طوطى گوياى خمش / ‏اى بسا شيرين روان رو ترش
entries.english: Oh, there is many a parrot that speaks though it is mute; oh, there is many a sweet-spirited one whose face is sour.

entries.farsi: رو به گورستان دمى خامش نشين / ‏آن خموشان سخن‏گو را ببين
entries.english: Go to the graveyard, sit awhile in silence, and behold those eloquent silent ones;

entries.farsi: شحم و لحم زندگان يكسان بود / آن يكى غمگين دگر شادان بود
entries.english: But, if you see that their dust is of one colour, their active state is not uniform.

entries.farsi: ليك اگر يك رنگ بينى خاكشان / ‏نيست يكسان حالت چالاكشان
entries.english: The fat and flesh of living persons is uniform, one is sad, another glad.

entries.farsi: تو چه دانى تا ننوشى قالشان / ‏ز انكه پنهان است بر تو حالشان
entries.english: Until you hear their words, what should you know, inasmuch as their state is hidden from you?

entries.farsi: بشنوى از قال هاى و هوى را / كى ببينى حالت صد توى را
entries.english: You may hear words— háy, húy; how will you perceive the) state that has a hundred folds?

entries.farsi: نقش ما يكسان به ضدها متصف / ‏خاك هم يكسان روانشان مختلف
entries.english: Our figure is uniform, endued with contrary qualities: likewise their dust is uniform, their spirits are diverse.

entries.farsi: بانگ اسبان بشنوى اندر مصاف / ‏بانگ مرغان بشنوى اندر طواف
entries.english: Similarly, voices are uniform, one is sorrowful, and another full of charms.

entries.farsi: همچنين يكسان بود آوازها / آن يكى پر درد و آن پر نازها
entries.english: On the battle-field you may hear the cry of horses; in strolling round you may hear the cry of birds.

entries.farsi: آن يكى از حقد و ديگر ز ارتباط / آن يكى از رنج و ديگر از نشاط
entries.english: One from hate, and another from harmony; one from pain, and another from joy

entries.farsi: آن درختى جنبد از زخم تبر / و آن درخت ديگر از باد سحر
entries.english: Whoever is remote from their state, to him the voices are uniform.

entries.farsi: بس غلط گشتم ز ديگ مرده‏ريگ / ‏ز انكه سر پوشيده مى‏جوشيد ديگ
entries.english: One tree is moved by blows of the axe, another tree by the breeze of dawn.

entries.farsi: هر كه دور از حالت ايشان بود / پيشش آن آوازها يكسان بود
entries.english: Much error befell me from the worthless pot, because the pot was boiling covered by the lid.

entries.farsi: جوش و نوش هر كست گويد بيا / جوش صدق و جوش تزوير و ريا
entries.english: The fervour and savour of everyone says to you, “Come”— the fervour of sincerity and the fervour of imposture and hypocrisy.

entries.farsi: گر ندارى بو ز جان رو شناس / ‏رو دماغى دست آور بوشناس
entries.english: If you have not the scent from the soul that recognises the face, go, get for yourself a brain that recognises the scent.

entries.farsi: آن دماغى كه بر آن گلشن تند / چشم يعقوبان هم او روشن كند
entries.english: The brain that haunts yon Rose-garden— it is it that makes bright the eyes of Jacobs.

entries.farsi: هين بگو احوال آن خسته جگر / كز بخارى دور مانديم اى پسر
entries.english: Come now; relate what happened to that heart-sick, for we have left the man of Bukhara far behind, O son.

sections.title_fa: ُيافتن عاشق معشوق را و بيان آن كه جوينده يابنده بود كه فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْراً يَرَه
sections.title_en: How the lover found his beloved; and a discourse showing that the seeker is a finder, for he who shall do as much good as the weight of an ant shall see it.

entries.farsi: كان جوان در جست و جو بد هفت سال / ‏از خيال وصل گشته چون خيال
entries.english: That for seven years that youth was in search and seeking: from the phantasy of union he became like a phantom.

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى حق بر سر بنده بود / عاقبت جوينده يابنده بود
entries.english: The shadow of God is over the head of the servant, the seeker at last will be a finder.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه چون كوبى درى / ‏عاقبت ز آن در برون آيد سرى
entries.english: The Prophet said that when you knock at a door, at last a head will come forth from that door.

entries.farsi: چون نشينى بر سر كوى كسى / ‏عاقبت بينى تو هم روى كسى
entries.english: When you sit on the road of a certain person, at last you will see also the face of a certain person

entries.farsi: جمله دانند اين اگر تو نگروى / ‏هر چه مى‏كاريش روزى بدروى
entries.english: When, every day, you keep digging the earth from a pit, at last you will arrive at the pure water.

entries.farsi: چون ز چاهى مى‏كنى هر روز خاك / ‏عاقبت اندر رسى در آب پاك
entries.english: If you may not believe, all know this, one day you will reap whatever you are sowing.

entries.farsi: سنگ بر آهن زدى آتش نجست / ‏اين نباشد ور بباشد نادر است
entries.english: You struck the stone against the iron: the fire did not flash out! This may not be; or if it be, it is rare.

entries.farsi: آن كه روزى نيستش بخت و نجات / ‏ننگرد عقلش مگر در نادرات
entries.english: He to whom felicity and salvation are not apportioned— his mind regards nothing but the rarities

entries.farsi: كان فالن كس كشت كرد و بر نداشت / ‏و آن صدف برد و صدف گوهر نداشت
entries.english: That such and such a one sowed seed and had no crop, while that one bore away an oyster-shell, and the shell had no pearl.

entries.farsi: بلعم باعور و ابليس لعين / ‏سود نامدشان عبادتها و دين
entries.english: Balam son of Baur and the accursed Iblis, their acts of worship and their religion availed them not.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران انبيا و رهروان / ‏نايد اندر خاطر آن بد گمان
entries.english: The hundreds of thousands of prophets and travellers on the Way do not come into the mind of that evil-thinking man.

entries.farsi: اين دو را گيرد كه تاريكى دهد / در دلش ادبار جز اين كى نهد
entries.english: He takes these two which produce darkness: how should ill fate put aught but this in his heart?

entries.farsi: بس كسا كه نان خورد دل شاد او / مرگ او گردد بگيرد در گلو
entries.english: Oh, there is many a one that eats bread with a glad heart, and it becomes the death of him: it sticks in his gullet.

entries.farsi: پس تو اى ادبار رو هم نان مخور / تا نيفتى همچو او در شور و شر
entries.english: Go, then, O ill-fated man, do not eat bread at all, lest you fall like him into bale and woe!

entries.farsi: صد هزاران خلق نانها مى‏خورند / زور مى‏يابند و جان مى‏پرورند
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of folk are eating loaves of bread and gaining strength and nourishing the spirit.

entries.farsi: تو بدان نادر كجا افتاده‏اى / ‏گر نه محرومى و ابله زاده‏اى
entries.english: How have you fallen into that rare, unless you are deprived and are born a fool?

entries.farsi: اين جهان پر آفتاب و نور ماه / ‏او بهشته سر فرو برده به چاه
entries.english: He has forsaken this world full of sunshine and moonlight and has plunged his head into the pit,

entries.farsi: كه اگر حق است پس كو روشنى / ‏سر ز چه بردار و بنگر اى دنى
entries.english: Saying, “If it is true, then where is the radiance?” Lift up your head from the pit and look, O miserable wretch!

entries.farsi: جمله عالم شرق و غرب آن نور يافت / ‏تا تو در چاهى نخواهد بر تو تافت
entries.english: The whole world, east and west, obtained that light, whilst you are in the pit it will not shine upon you.

entries.farsi: هين مگو كاينك فالنى كشت كرد / در فالن سالى ملخ كشتش بخورد
entries.english: Leave the pit, go to the palace and the vineyards; do not wrangle here, know that quarrelling is unlucky.

entries.farsi: چه رها كن رو به ايوان و كروم / ‏كم ستيز اينجا بدان كاللج شوم
entries.english: Beware! Do not say, “Mark you, such and such a one sowed seed, and in such and such a year the locusts devoured what he had sown.

entries.farsi: پس چرا كارم كه اينجا خوف هست / ‏من چرا افشانم اين گندم ز دست
entries.english: Why, then, should I sow? For there is danger in this respect. Why should I scatter this wheat from my hand?”

entries.farsi: و انكه او نگذاشت كشت و كار را / پر كند كورى تو انبار را
entries.english: And he who did not neglect to sow and labour fills his barn, to your confusion.

entries.farsi: جست از بيم عسس شب او به باغ / ‏يار خود را يافت چون شمع و چراغ
entries.english: Since he was patiently knocking at a door, at last one day he obtained a meeting in private.

entries.farsi: چون درى مى‏كوفت او از سلوتى / ‏عاقبت دريافت روزى خلوتى
entries.english: From fear of the night-patrol he sprang by night into the orchard: he found his beloved, as candle and lamp.

entries.farsi: گفت سازنده‏ى سبب را آن نفس / ‏اى خدا تو رحمتى كن بر عسس
entries.english: At that moment he said to the Maker of the means, “O God, have mercy on the night-patrol!

entries.farsi: ناشناسا تو سببها كرده‏اى / ‏از در دوزخ بهشتم برده‏اى
entries.english: Unknown, You have created the means: from the gate of Hell You have brought me to Paradise.

entries.farsi: بهر آن كردى سبب اين كار را / تا ندارم خوار من يك خار را
entries.english: You have made this affair a means, to the end that I may not hold a single thorn in contempt.”

entries.farsi: در شكست پاى بخشد حق پرى / ‏هم ز قعر چاه بگشايد درى
entries.english: In the fracture of a leg God bestows a wing; likewise from the depths of the pit He opens a door.

entries.farsi: تو مبين كه بر درختى يا به چاه / ‏تو مرا بين كه منم مفتاح راه
entries.english: “Do not consider whether you are on a tree or in a pit: consider Me, for I am the Key of the Way.”

sections.title_fa: 4810گر تو خواهى باقى اين گفت‏وگو اى اخى در دفتر چارم بجو

entries.english: If you wish the rest of this tale, seek, O my brother, in the Fourth Book.
